Maharajah of Indore, April, 1910; specl. offr. for Delhi Durbar work in Central India, Aug., 1911; special duty in Central India, Feb., 1912; asst. resdt., Mewar, Oct., 1912; pol. offr. with the Maharajah Holker of Indore, April, 1913; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, and chief commr. of Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1913; asst. resdt., Mewar, Nov., 1913; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Dec., 1913; ditto, to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, April, 1915; pol. agent, Koweit, June, 1916; C.I.E., Aug., 1918; 1st asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Dec., 1918; finl. member, state council, Jodhpur, from March, 1919; ret'd., Mar., 1923.

BELL, SIR CHARLES ALFRED, K.C.I.E., C.M.G., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1898; mag. and collr., Feb. 1904; asst. pol. officer, Sikkim, May, 1904, to Nov., 1905; mag. and collr. and offg. pol. officer, Sikkim, April, 1908; pol. offr., ditto, from Oct., 1908; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa cadre, April, 1912; specl. duty with Tibet conference, Sept., 1913, to Aug., 1914; C.M.G., Jan., 1915; C.I.E., June, 1919; ret'd., March, 1919; K.C.I.E., Jan. 1922; author of a 'Manual of Colloquial Tibetan.'

BELL, CYRIL FRANCIS, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 23rd Feb., 1883).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1905 as asst. consvr., Central Provs.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1910; offg. consvr., March, 1921; transfed. to Burma as dep. consvr., Apl., 1923; consvr., Dec., 1925; re-transfcd. dep. consvr., Central Provs., Nov., 1926; offg. consvr., April, 1927; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. ch. consvr., Apl., 1933; and again, Apl., 1935; confd., Aug., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; ret'd., Feb., 1938.

BELL, SIR ERNEST ALBERT SEYMOUR, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., late Railway Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1888, and posted to state rlys.; lent to Assam-Bengal rly. co. from Jan., 1895, to Nov., 1902; exec. engr., July, 1893; asst. sec., public works dept., rly. branch, Dec.,

1903, to Jan., 1905; dep. manager, E. B. state rly., March, 1908; suptg. engr., N.W. rly., March, 1913; agent, E. Bengal rly., June, 1915; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; agent, N.-W. ry., Jan., 1919; member, rly. board, Jan., 1920; Kt., June, 1923; govt. dir. of Indian rly. cos., India Office, Oct., 1923; ret'd., Oct., 1926; member, Ind. staty. rly. bd. ctce., June, 1933.

BELL, FRANK OWEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd June, 1907).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Christ's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1931.

BELL, HERBERT JASPER, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 4th May, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Delhi, May, 1926; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

BELL, JAMES LOGIE, late Indian Forest Service (b. 18th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service, 14th Feb., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Feb., 1922; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

BELL, JOHN ALTON, Indian State Rlys. (agent, E.I. Rly.) (b. 27th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 29th Oct., 1910; in milty. employ from 5th Oct., 1915, to 18th Sept., 1919; services lent to Bengal-Nagpur Rly. Co. from 7th March, 1923; offg. exec. engr., E.I. Rly., April, 1927; offg. divl. supt., Feb., 1920; confd., June, 1933; offg. agent., E.I. rly., Oct., 1935; confd., Jan., 1936; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Dec., 1935.

BELL, JOHN MICHAEL GEOFFREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 17th Apl., 1913).—Educ. at Winchester, Marlborough Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Jan., 1938; arrived in India, Feb., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BELL, SIR ROBERT DUNCAN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th May, 1878).—Educ. at Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 12th Dec., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt., rev. and fin. depts., June, 1910, to Oct., 1912; asst. settlt. officer, Nov., 1913; sec. to the Indian Industrial Commn., June, 1916; controller of industrial intelligence, under Indian Munitions Board, March, 1917; temp. dir. of industries and controller of munitions, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1919; offg. sec. to govt. of Bombay, development dept., July, 1924; confd., June, 1925; offg. dir. of development, Apl., 1926; member of legisl. council, 1927, 1928, 1930, and 1931; offg. ch. secy. to govt., rev. dept., Mar., 1930; confd., July, 1930; C.S.I., Jan., 1932; commr., Jan., 1932; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, Jan. 1933; K.C.S.I., June, 1935; actg. govr. of Bombay, May, 1936; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

BELL, ROBERT WALLACE, late Indian Police (b. 6th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. on 28th Nov., 1912; confd., Nov., 1914; supt., Jan., 1925; retd., Jan., 1935.

BELL, ROGER CECIL SUTHERLAND, Indian Civil Service (sub. collr., Madras), (b. 4th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Fettes, Worcester Coll., Oxford, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub-collr., Mar., 1931; offg. under secty. to govt., Madras, public dept., June, 1934; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Apl., 1936.

BELL, ROLAND STANLEY, late Indian Police (b. 8th Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1903, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, Sept., 1907; supt., Oct., 1915; on military service, July, 1918, to Apl., 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1932; I.G. rly. police and police asst. to A.G.G. Rajputana; retd., Oct., 1937.

BELVALKAR, DR. SHRIPAD KRISHNA, M.A., Ph.D. (Harvard), late Indian Eductl. Service (b. 11th Dec., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appts. in eductl. dept., Bombay, from 5th Nov., 1906, to 10th Mar., 1915; prof., Deccan Coll., Poona, Mar., 1915; I.E.S. from 5th Feb., 1921; K-i-H medal, 2nd class, June, 1931; retd., Dec., 1936.

BEMROSE, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 21st June, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 23rd Nov., 1925; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1932; confd., Apl., 1938.

BENCE-JONES, PHILIP REGINALD, M.A., M.Inst.C.F., A.M.I.M.E. (b. 12th Jan., 1897).—Apptd. principl., Maclagan engrg. coll., 29th Mar., 1934.

BENEGAL, SANJIVA RAO (see Sanjiva Rao, B.)

BENEST, FRANCIS EDWARD, M.I.E.E., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 12th July, 1871).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' School; 1st asst., Bournemouth elec. works, Apl., 1895; station supt., Dover elec. works, July, 1895; sen. asst., Madrid elec. works, Sept., 1896 to 1898; chief asst., Bournemouth and Poole elec. works, Aug., 1899; apptd. asst. examr. of stores, India store depôt, 1st June, 1901; inspr. of scientific supplies, 1st March, 1905; dep. supt., India store depôt, Aug., 1917; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; supt., India store depôt, Aug., 1923; dir. of inspection, April, 1927; retd., July, 1936.

BENJAMIN, HERBERT DANIEL, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (secty., legis. dept., govt. of Bengal) (b. 4th March, 1899).—Educ. at Monmouth Gr. Sch., and Oxford (Jesus); joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; and sub. divl. officer, June, 1924; under-secty. to govt. of Bengal, finl., &c., dept., May 1927; offg. dep. secty. to ditto, March, 1929; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1930; confd. Mar., 1935; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, legisl. dept., Mar., 1935; attd. legisl. dept., govt. of India, May, 1936; special officer, fin. dept.,

govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1936; secty. legis. dept. govt. of Bengal, May, 1937.

BENN, RT. HON. W. WEDGWOOD, D.S.O., D.F.C., secty. of state for India, June, 1929, Aug., 1931.

BENNETT, SIR EDWARD, B.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (judge, Allahabad high ct.) (b. 26th Oct., 1880).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., May, 1914; sess. and sub. judge, Jan., 1917; dist. and sessions judge, Oct., 1922; offg. judge, high ct., Allahabad, April, 1928; and again, Apl., 1929; addl. judge, ditto, Oct., 1929; and again March, 1931; judge, ditto, Dec., 1932; knighted, Jan., 1939.

BENNETT, WILLIAM RYLAND RAINSFORD MOORE, M.C., late Indian State Railways (b. 22nd May, 1881).—Joined the dept., Oct., 1905; signal engr., E.B. Rly., Jan., 1920; dep. ch. entr., signals, Oct., 1922; retd., May, 1936.

BENNETT, CHRISTOPHER MACAULAY, B.Sc. Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Orissa) (b. 15th Feb., 1903).—Joined the service as exec. engr., Madras, 1st Oct., 1926; offg. exc. engr., Mar., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935; transf. to Orissa, Oct., 1936.

BENNETT, ERIC, M.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 20th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at Berkhamsted and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 7th March, 1921; arrived, 29th April, 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1923; spl. asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1928; ditto (subs.), Sept., 1935.

BENNETT, REV. FRANK, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estab. (United Provs.) (chaplain on probn.) (b. 6th Nov., 1901).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Feb., 1939.

BENNETT, HERBERT JOHN, Office of High Commr. for India (princpl. exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 30th June, 1881).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India Store Depôt, 6th April, 1901; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; supervisor, India Store Dept., April, 1921; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; princpl., ditto, May, 1938.

BENNETT, JOHN REGINALD WILLIAM, B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 18th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at King's Sch., Warwick, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., July, 1917; under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1919, to March, 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt., judl. dept., Oct., 1923; sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1927; and again, Nov., 1933; judl. secty. to govt., U.P., July, 1931.

BENNETT, JOHN THORNE MASEY, C.I.E., C.B.E., M.C., Indian Police (supt. Punjab) (b. 7th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1914; asst. supt., Jan., 1919; supt., July, 1924; C.B.E., June, 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl. variously from April, 1934; C.I.E., June, 1939.

BENNETT, JOSEPH WILLIAM, B.E., B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., June, 1923; confd., Sept., 1931; ret'd., Sept. 1935.

BENNISON, JOHN JENNINGS, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 15th June, 1892).—Educ. at Middlesbrough High School and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, Dec., 1916, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from July, 1918, to July, 1919; offg. dep. commr., and jt. regr., co-op. societies, Nov., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1925; confd., June, 1930; supt., census operations, Burma, Apl., 1930.

BENSKIN, ERNEST, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 7th July, 1888).—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1909, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Jan., 1915; offg. consvr., Bengal, April, 1928, to Dec. 1928; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1929; ditto, Oct., 1932; ret'd., Apl., 1933.

BENSON, WILFRED JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 27th July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

BENTINCK, ARTHUR HAROLD WALTER, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Dec., 1875).—Educ. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 6th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1906; mag. and collr., May, 1911; dep. commr., Assam, April, 1912; offg. commr., April, 1924; mbr. Assam leg. council; offg. chief sec. to govt., March, 1926; C.I.E., July, 1926; offg. commr., Dec., 1926; confd., May, 1927; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

BENTLEY, CHARLES ALBERT, C.I.E., M.B., D.P.H., late Dept. of Public Health, Bengal (b. 25th April, 1873).—Joined the service, 8th May, 1911, as special dep. sanitary commr. E. Bengal and Assam, after special employment in connection with Bombay malaria epidemic; special offr. for malaria research, Bengal, March, 1913; K.-i-H. medal, 1st class, Jan., 1915; san. commr., Bengal, May, 1917; dir. of public health, Bengal, Feb., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1929; ret'd., June, 1931.

BENTON, GORDON WILLIAM, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 25th March, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Nov., 1912; dist. supt., Sept., 1924; dep. dir. genl. police, Hyderabad State, Feb., 1929; and again, Nov., 1932; officer on spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., Aug., 1936; attd. for service with dir., intel. bureau, Oct., 1936; ditto with rly. bd., Oct., 1937; on special duty Kolhapur Residency, April to June, 1939.

BENTON, WILLIAM STEPHEN, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 21st Jan., 1893).—Asst. engr., 27th Dec., 1919; exec. engr. (prov.), Nov., 1935; confd., Mar., 1938.

BERG, CLEMENT LEONARD, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1898).—

Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1927; confd., Oct., 1930; deputed to mil. engrg. serv., Karachi, Oct., 1937 to Apl., 1938.

BERKELEY-HILL, OWEN ALFRED ROWLAND, M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 22nd Dec., 1879).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1907; in mil. employ up to 19th Oct., 1919; medl. supt., Ranchi European mental hosp., Nov., 1922; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

BERKELEY, GERALD EUSTACE, Indian State Rlys. (dep. traffic manager, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 24th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as traffic probnr., 7th Nov., 1914; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1918; divl. traffic manager, Aug., 1924; goods supt., Oct. 1924; asst. traffic manager, May, 1926; offd. variously as dep. traffic manager from May, 1926; confd., Dec., 1929.

BERRIE, JOHN GEORGE, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 1st June, 1876). Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1898, as asst. supt., telegraphs; supt., April, 1910, ditto, and dep. postmr.-gen., traffic, Feb., 1916; offg. dep. dir.-gen. telegraph traffic, Jan., 1923; confd., July, 1924; dir., tele. engrg., eastern circle, Nov., 1925; ditto, southern circle, Nov., 1927; ret'd. June, 1931.

BERRILL, REGINALD, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 20th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1902; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922, exec. engr., Oct., 1923; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

BERRINGTON, ARTHUR DAVIES, Lt.-Commdr., R.N.R. (Retd.), (Dep. Port Consvr., Madras) (b. 26th Nov., 1895).—Apptd. Harbour-master, 2nd Aug., 1921; dep. port consvr., Sept., 1930.

BERRY, WILLIAM, Public Works dept., Punjab (b. 30th Mar., 1889).—Appt. prof. Maclagan Engrg. coll., Lahore, 10th Oct., 1924.

BERTHOUD, EDWARD HENRY, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Sept., 1876).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 13th Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlmt. offcr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1910; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1912; mag. and collr., Dec., 1912; ditto and dep. commr., June, 1917; O.B.E., June, 1919; commr. of excise and inspr.-gen. of registration, etc., Oct., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; commr. Nov., 1928; ret'd., May, 1932.

BERTRAM, THOMAS, O.B.E., I.S.O. (b. 18th Sept., 1884).—Supt., viceregal estates, Feb., 1921; ret'd., Sept., 1939.

BERY, HARBANS LAL, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 9th Dec., 1912).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 30th Jan., 1936; confd., 1938.

BERY, KASH RAM, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th June, 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 5th Dec., 1901; exec. engr., Oct., 1909; suptg. engr., Feb., 1922; ret'd., June, 1935.

BEST, KENNETH HENRY, Indian Police (b. 20th Mar., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Dec., 1937.

BESTALL, FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 22nd July, 1910).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 3rd Dec., 1929.

BETHAM, GEOFFREY LAWRENCE, C.I.E., M.C., Lt.-Col. I.A., Indian Political Service (resdt., Mewar) (b. 8th Apl., 1889).—First commn., dated 20th Jan., 1909; asst. commr., May, 1919; asst. pol. agent, Mekran, Mar., 1924; pol. agent, Chagai, Jan., 1925; ditto, Sibi, July, 1927; ditto, Zhob, Mar., 1929; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Apl., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; resdt., Mewar, Mar., 1935, H.B.M.'s., minister, Nepal, Apl., 1938.

BETT, JOHN RAYMOND, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1901).—Educ. at Wintringham Secondary School, Grimsby, Univ. Coll., London, and St. Edmunds Hall, Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub. collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., variously from Apl., 1936.

BETTERTON, FREDERICK ARCHIBALD, late Indian Service of Engrs., Bihar (b. 29th Dec., 1882).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906; asst. engr., 21st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1925; confd., June, 1925; offg. ch. engr. and sec. to govt. P.W.D., April, 1931; confd., Jan., 1933; ret'd., Jan., 1938.

BEVAN, GILBERT JOHN BECKFORD, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent, Burma Rlys.) (b. 19th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (tempy.), 6th Jan., 1910; ditto (perm.), July, 1911; on milty. duty from 27th Sept., 1916, to 19th Sept., 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; dist. engr., Feb., 1921; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1933; confd., Mar., 1934.

BEVAN, WILLIAM JOHN, Indian State Rlys., Coal Dept (elec. and mech. engr.) (b. 28th Aug. 1893).—Joined service, April, 1928; mech. engr., April, 1929; elec. and mech. engr., Jan., 1937.

BEVERIDGE, ERNEST JOHN, Indian Police (b. 14th Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; asst. supt., Deoli deten. jail, Jan., 1935.

BEWERS, DUDLEY FRANCIS, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, education dept.) (b. 28th Sept., 1893).—Apptd. clerk to local adviser to Indian Students, London, 25th Aug., 1919; transf'd. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; clerical officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1925.

BEWOOR, SIR GURUNATH VENKATESH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dir.-genl., posts and tels.) (b. 20th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at schools in India, Bombay Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 27th Nov., 1912, and served in the Central Prov., as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., March, 1919; offg. postmr.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, July, 1922; confd. as postmr.-gen., July, 1924; dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1924; confd., June, 1925; dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., Nov., 1927; delegate to internl. Univ. Postal Congress, London, Mar., 1929; P.M.G., Bombay, Oct., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1932; dir.-genl., posts and tels., Mar., 1934; member, Council of State, Mar., 1936; Kt., June, 1939.

BEYNON, ALBERT CYRIL, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Aug., 1896).—Educ.

at Sandhurst, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1921; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1925, and again Jan., 1927; confd., Sept., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

BHADKAMKAR, BALAKRISHNA VASUDEO, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 28th Aug., 1893).—Educ. at Bombay and University Coll., London; arrived, 23rd March, 1919, and posted to Burma; transferred to United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., July, 1920; jt. mag., July, 1920; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1926; confd., May, 1932; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1932; excise commr., United Provs., May, 1938.

BHADRAN, CHAKAPANY AIVENGAR RAMA, M.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 24th Sept., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 15th Nov., 1932.

BHAGADE, SHRIDHAR MADHO RAO SAHIB, B.A., B.L., late Central Provs., C.S. (b. 1st Feb., 1879).—Joined the service as munsif, 14th Mar., 1903; sub-judge, Dec., 1914; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1932; ret'd., Apl., 1933.

BHAGAT SINGH, M.A., India Posts and Telegraphs (depy. postmr.-gen.) (b. 30 Dec., 1884).—Joined the service as proby. suptd., May, 1908; confd., Nov., 1909; depy. postmr.-gen., Jan., 1938.

BHAGAT, SURENDRA SINGH (see Surendra Singh, B.).

BHAGWAN DAS, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.V.O., late Indian Police (b. July, 1875).—Joined the service, 24th Dec., 1895; M.V.O., Mar., 1922; supy. supt., intell. bureau, govt. of India, home dept., May, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; ret'd., July, 1930.

BHAGWATI PRASAD SAKSENA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 17th July, 1899).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1923; apprentice engr., Sept., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1925; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1929; confd., Oct., 1934.

BHAGWATI PRASAD, VARMA, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., M.R.S.I., M.I.M.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 8th Aug., 1898; asst. engr., Punjab, Aug., 1899; exec. engr., Aug., 1910; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1920; confd., Dec., 1921; offg. ch. engr. and sec. to govt., irrigation branch, April, 1928; member, public services commn., Oct., 1930 to May, 1935; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

BHALJA, GOVARDHAN SHANKERLAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., local self govt. depts., Central Provs.) (b. 8th Aug., 1895).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1920; arrived 8th Jan., 1921, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925, and Nov., 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; dir. of industries and regr. of co-op. societies, Aug., 1933; secty. to local self govt. etc. depts., Apl., 1937.

BHALLA, T. P. (see Tejasvi Prasad Bhalla).

BHAN, CHANDAR (see Chandar, B.).

BHANDARKAR, KASHINATH YESHWANT (asst. solr.) (b. Oct., 1902).—2nd cl. sub-judge, variously from Nov., 1928 to Oct., 1936; P.A. to rembr. legal affairs, Bombay, Mar., 1933; asst. secy., Bombay Govt. legal dept., Oct., 1933; asst. solr., Govt. of India, legis. dept., Apl., 1938.

BHANDARKAR, VAMAN PRABHAKAR, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 18th Apl., 1892).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1914; dist. ditto., Dec., 1936.

BHANDARI, AMAR NATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Oxford Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 9th Dec., 1924, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., April, 1929; and again, Oct., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1933; confd., July, 1934; attd. govt. of India, legisl. dept., Nov., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Delhi, Sept., 1936.

BHANDARI, JAIGOPAL, C.I.E., M.A., late India Finance Dept. (b. 14th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service, 9th Feb., 1906; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Sept., 1906; asst. compt. Central Provs., Jan., 1909; asst. acct.-genl., U.P., Nov., 1912; ditto, Bengal, Aug., 1914; addl. asst. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1918; asst. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Calcutta, April, 1919; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Oct., 1922; ditto, central revenues, Jan., 1924; ditto, Punjab, Dec., 1924; offg. ditto, posts and tels., March, 1925; offg. acct.-genl., Punjab, March, 1927; dep. ditto, Nov., 1927; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, June, 1929; offg. acct.-genl., central revenues, Nov., 1929; ditto, Bengal, May, 1930; ditto, posts and tels., Mar., 1933; ditto, Punjab, Apl., 1934; acct.-genl., Punjab, Oct., 1934; retd., Mar., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1938.

BHANDARI, MADAN GOPAL, M.B., Lt-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bombay (supt.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1892).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ to 21st Oct., 1924; addl. supt., Oct., 1924; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Sept., 1927; supt., Borstal Institute, Lahore, April, 1928, to Dec., 1928; ditto, Central prison, Bombay, Apl., 1933; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bombay, Feb., 1936.

BHANDARI, RAM NATH, M.D., Ch.B., Major, I.M.S., Jail Dept., United Provinces (b. 15th Aug., 1892).—Apptd. supt., central prison, Bareilly, 5th Jan., 1929; ditto, Allahabad (Naini), Mar., 1937.

BHANDARKAR, GOPAL PRABHAKAR, M.B.E., B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Oct., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr., (provl. service), 16th May, 1918; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; confd., May, 1927; M.B.E. June, 1935; retd., Oct., 1937.

BHANOT, HARNAM DAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 9th Jan., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; dep. commr. (provl.), May, 1928; ditto (subs. perm.), Dec., 1931; senr. secty. to finl. commrs., and dept. secty. to govt., Punjab, dev. dept., July, 1934; secty. ditto fin. dept., Sept., 1937.

BHANSALI, MANSEN DAMODAR, M.A., LL.B., Barr., Indian C.S. (coll. and mag., Bombay, (b. 7th Nov., 1898).—Educ. at Bombay and Dulwich Coll., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. in 1923; arrived in India and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte. Dec., 1923; offg. dep. sec. to govt., finance dept., Jan., 1931; confd., Nov., 1931; member,

legisl. council, Bombay, 1932; offg. regr. of co-op societies, Bombay, July, 1936; regr. ditto, Dec., 1938; apptd. ex-officio jt. secty. to Govt. of Bombay, revenue dept., May, 1939.

BHARGAV, LACHHMAN PRASAD (see Lachhman, P.B.).

BHARGAVA, KALKAR PRASAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 15th July, 1908).—Educated at Government High School, Orai, Allahabad University and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service 6th October, 1933; arrived in India November, 1933, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Mar., 1937.

BHARGAVA, MURARI LAL, B.Sc., LL.B., A.S.A.A., Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 1st Aug., 1895).—Apptd. Sept., 1926:

BHARGAVA, RAMESHWAR PRASAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 27th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University and School of Oriental Studies, London. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived, in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1934.

BHARGAVA, VASHISTHA, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 5th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service 10th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1934.

BHARUCHA, JAMASJI CURSETJI, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (princpl., Medical sch., Agra) (b. 9th July 1887).—First commn., dated 27th Jan., 1912; in milty. employ until 3rd April, 1923; offg. dist. medl. officer, March, 1926; offg. surg., first dist., and supt., Rayapuram medl. school, &c., April, 1928; offg. supt., jail dept., Burma, Aug., 1929; confd., July, 1931; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Burma, Oct., 1933; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, May, 1934; princpl., medl. sch., Agra, Feb., 1935.

BHARUCHA, JEHANGIR SORAB, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 13th Oct., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 6th Aug., 1923; offg. supt. Feb., 1932; confd., Nov., 1937.

BHARUCHA, KAIKHUSRU BYRAMJI, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bihar (supt.) (b. 19th July, 1889).—First commn., dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ up to 15th March, 1924; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., Jan., 1928.

BHARUCHA, PHIROZSHAH BYRAMJI, D.S.O., O.B.E., M.D. (Lond.), F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt-Col., I.M.S. (medl. supt., Mayo hosp., Lahore) (b. 21st Feb., 1886).—First commn., 30th July, 1910; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; on mil. duty till April, 1921, when he was apptd. prof. of anatomy, King Edward Medical Coll., Lahore; offg. prof. of operative surgery, ditto, Oct., 1921; and again, March, 1926; medl. supt., Mayo hosp., Lahore, Nov., 1935; offg. princpl. King Edward Medl. Coll., Feb., 1936.

BHARUCHA, SORAB MANEKSHAH, B.A., late Bombay Civil Service (b. 28th Jan., 1875).—Apptd. dep. collr., Bombay; 5th April, 1897; ditto, and oriental translator to govt., July, 1913; collr., Thana, and pol. agent,

Javhar, May, 1916; collr. and mag., Kolaba, and pol. agent, Janjira, Feb., 1918; collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Jawhar, Nov., 1921; collr. and Salsette dev. officer, May, 1925; offg. secty. to govt., dev. dept., &c., May, 1929; retd., May, 1930.

BHASHYAM AYYANGAR, VALAVARAM, late Madras Civil Service. (b. Nov., 1876).—Joined the service 18th Aug., 1903; dist. munsif, Dec., 1904; sub. judge, Aug., 1920; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1928; offg. judge high ct., Madras, July, 1930; retd., Nov., 1931.

BHASKARA, RAO NAYUDU, TAMARAPAKAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 10th May, 1896).—Educ. at Presidency Coll., Madras; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-coll. and jt. mag., Feb., 1925; offg. collr. & dist. magte., Feb., 1930.

BHASKARAIYA, CHATTA, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 27th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 6th Mar., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Bombay, July, 1921; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Calcutta, Jan., 1930, dep. acct.-genl., central revs., Calcutta, Feb., 1933; ditto, Bengal, April, 1936; examiner outside audit dept., Bombay, Aug., 1938; ch. auditor rly. clearing accts., Delhi, Sept., 1938; financial adviser to the ch. commr., Delhi, April, 1939.

BHASKARAN, PUDIYA VITIL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Orissa) (b. 18th Nov., 1908).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 23rd Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

BHAT, MADHUSUDAN DAMODAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (municipal commr. for the City of Bombay) (b. 4th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Elphinstone Coll., Bombay, and Trinity Hall, Camb.; joined the service, 9th Nov., 1921; arrived, 27th Jan., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., offg. collr., dist. magte. and pol. agent, May, 1925, and again Aug., 1929; temp. under secty. to govt., fin. dept., Apl., 1929; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Jewhar; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Jath, Feb., 1933; Collr., Mar., 1936; secty. to govt. Sind, home, etc., depts.; member, Sind. rev. trib., June, 1938; municipal commr. for the City of Bombay, Dec., 1938.

BHATHENA, SORABJI JAMASJI, L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.F.F.S. (Glas.), L.M. (Dub.), Lieut.-Col. I.M.S., retd. (b. 24th Jan., 1879).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1907; and served in mill. dept. prior to 24th Oct., 1921; civil surg., Oct., 1921; retd., Jan., 1934.

BHATIA, KISHINCHAND MULCHAND, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Dec., 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 12th Aug., 1931.

BHATIA, KRISHNA BEHARI, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. U.P.) (b. 15th July, 1907).—Educ. at University of Allahabad; Royal College of Science, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived 7th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. coll. and mag., jt. mag., Apl. 1933; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1936; secty., tariff bd. Apl., 1937 to Apl., 1938.

BHATIA, RAM NARAYAN, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., B.Sc., M.B., F.R.C.S., late United Provs. medl. service (b. 23rd July, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. surg., 18th Feb., 1910; civil surg., Aug., 1919; offg. profir. of surg., King

George's medl. coll., Aug., 1924; retd., Nov., 1934.

BHATIA, SOHAN LAL, M.A., M.D., B.Ch. (Cantab.), L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S., F.C.P.S. (Bom.), M.C., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (princl., G.M. Coll., Bombay, and supt. J.J. group hosp.) (b. 5th Aug., 1891).—First commn. dated 15th May, 1920; served in milty. dept. prior to 22nd Aug., 1920; offg. profir. of physiology, &c., G.M. Coll., Sept., 1920; confd., Sept., 1923; princpl. G.M. Coll. and supt. J.J. group hosp., Oct., 1937.

BHATTACHARJI, DAKSHINA RANJAN, M.Sc., Ph.D., D.Sc., U.P. Edn. Serv. Cl. I. (b. 18th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as Prof. Muir Cent. Coll. Alld., 22nd Aug., 1910; on for. service with All. univ., from July, 1922.

BHATTACHARJI, DHARMADAS, B.A., Indian Police. (asst. supt., Bengal) (b. 1st Mar., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 12th June, 1927; confd., June, 1929; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1934; offg. supt., Jan., 1936.

BHATTACHARJI, DURGAGATI, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 1st July, 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st Feb., 1936; confd., 1938.

BHATTACHARJI, MAHENDRA KUMAR, B.Sc., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. Apl., 1889).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1919; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; confd. Jan., 1928.

BHATTACHARJI, NAND LAL, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 26th June, 1876).—Trained at T.C.E. Coll. and apptd. in India as apprentice engr., 30th June, 1899; asst. engr., June, 1900; exec. engr., Sept., 1908; retd., June, 1931.

BHATTACHARJI, PRAMATHA NATH, M.A., B.L., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 16th Jan., 1876).—Joined the service, 13th June, 1904; offg. munsif, Oct., 1906; confd. Feb., 1907; sub-judge, Sept., 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1931; retd., Oct., 1931.

BHATTACHARJI, TARAKESWAR, Ra Bahadur, B.A., Excise and Salt dept., Bengal (dep. commr.) (b. 25th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 10th Apl., 1906; sub-dep. collr., Dec., 1907; supt. of excise and salt, Apl., 1915; pers. asst. to commr. of excise, June, 1928; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1928; confd., Mar., 1930.

BHATTACHARYA, AMBARNATH (see Ambarnath Bhattacharya).

BHAUMIK, HARIPADA, O.B.E., B.A., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 10th June, 1882).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1905; asst. supt., Dec., 1908; divl. engr., tels., Nov., 1921; senr. elect. engr., May, 1930; offg. as elect. engr.-in-ch., Mar., 1931; O.B.E., Jan. 1934; offg. p.m.g., May, 1934; dir. tels., Aug., 1934; elec. engr. in ch., Sept., 1934; offg. p.m.g., Madras, Dec., 1935; confd. as p.m.g., Oct., 1936; retd., June, 1937.

BHAVE, DIVAN BAHADUR MAHADEV ANANT, B.A., LL.M., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 14th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th Apl., 1914; asst. judge, Feb., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge variously from Apl., 1934; retd. June, 1939; apptd. member public service commission, Bombay-Sind, June, 1939; Divan Bahadur, June, 1939.

BHAWANISHANKAR RAO, KHAMBADKONE, B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 5th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as offer., cl. II, May, 1924; junr. dep. fin. advr. mil. fin., Apl., 1935; dep. fin. advr. mil. fin., Oct., 1935.

BHIDE, MAHADEVA VISHNU, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 13th Feb., 1883).—Educ. at Poona, Bombay Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 1st Jan., 1908, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. censor, Bombay, Jan., 1915, to April, 1919; postal censor, Lahore, May, 1919; offg. legal membr. and sec. to govt., legislative dept., May, 1925; confd., April, 1926; actg. judge, Lahore High Ct., June-July, 1927, and again, Mar., 1933; addl. judge, ditto, variously from Nov., 1927; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1934; judge, high ct., Lahore, July, 1936.

BHIDE, MANOHAR RAMCHANDRA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Nagpur University, University College, London and Trinity College, Cambridge. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; regr. co-op. societies, June, 1938.

BHIDE, VITHAL SHIVARAM, B.A., LL.B., Barr.-at-Law, Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 23rd Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Miraj High School, Bombay Univ., and Cambridge (non-collegiate); apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; transf. to Bombay as asst. collr., April, 1919; asst. collr. and personal asst. to collr., Feb., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. magte., April, 1926; confd., Apl., 1935; offg. regr. co-op. societies, Oct., 1927; secty. to genl. and educational depts., Bombay, Nov., 1938, to May, 1939; secty. rev. dept., Bombay, May, 1939.

BHIKAJI RAGHUNATH MEHENDALE (see Mehendale, B. R.).

BHIM SEN, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 26th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1918; asst. engr., Sept., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1927; confd. Apl., 1929; on depn. to Orissa, Apl., 1936.

BHIMASENA RAU, HANUMANTA, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 30th April, 1876).—Joined the service, 6th Sept., 1899; asst. acct.-gen., Sept., 1901; asst. compt., Post Office, June, 1905; dep. ditto, Jan., 1906; asst. acct.-gen., Madras, July, 1906; ditto and examr. of local fund accts., Jan., 1908; dep. compt., Ind. treasuries, Oct., 1901; dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma, Oct., 1912; ditto, Madras, April, 1914; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Dec., 1915; dep. acctnt.-gen., ditto, March, 1916; offg. compt., Assam, June, 1921; dep. acctnt.-gen., Central Prov., May, 1922; offg. acctnt.-gen., United Prov., Jan., 1924; confd., Oct., 1925; dir. of audit, United Provs., April, 1926; acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1927; ditto, Madras; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

BHOLA, MATHURA PRASHAD, Rai Bahadur, O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 21st Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1902; dep. conserv., Aug., 1922; ret'd., Sept., 1936; O.B.E., Feb., 1937.

BHOOTHALINGAM, SUBRAHMANYA, B.A. Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and sub-collr., Madras) (b. 22nd Feb., 1909).—Educ. at Caius College, Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub collr. and jt. mag., May, 1933; spec. asst. settlement offer., Nov., 1934 and Nov., 1935.

BHORE, DAVID GORDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 28th Oct., 1915).—Educ. at Bromsgrove and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 12th Oct., 1937; confd., 15th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

BHORE, SIR JOSEPH WILLIAM, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th April, 1879).—Educ. at Bishop's High School, Poona, and Bombay Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 13th Dec., 1902; and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., May, 1910; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1910; diwan of Cochin, March, 1914; dep. dir. of civil supplies, Bezwada, April, 1919; C.B.E., Dec., 1919; sec. to high commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920, to 28th Nov., 1923; acting high commr. for India from 20th Oct., 1922, to 18th April, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1923; secy., govt. of India, dept. of education, health and lands, April, 1924; temp. member of gov. genl.'s council, Nov., 1926; secty., Indian Statutory Commission, Nov., 1927; member, govr. genl.'s exec. council, April, 1930; vice-presdt., ditto, Apl., 1935 to May, 1935; K.C.I.E., June, 1930; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1933; chairman Central Advisory Council for Railways, May, 1934; delegate of Govt. of India at Int. Lab. Conf., Geneva, June, 1935; ret'd., April, 1936.

BICKERS, MAURICE HENRY TOWNSHEND, India Posts and Telegraphs (Deputy Postmaster-General) (b. 9th June, 1891).—Joined the service as proby. supt., June, 1913; confd. June, 1915; Expedny. Force, Mesopotamia, Dec., 1917; depn. to Iraq Administration, March, 1921, to June, 1930; depy. Postmr.-General, Sept., 1937.

BICKFORD, WILLIAM REGINALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 14th Sept., 1911).—Educated at Cheltenham College and Wadham College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty., home dept., April, 1939.

BIGGIE, HENRY JAMES LESLIE, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 12th Mar., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., on 20th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1927; confd., Nov., 1936; comdt. and dist. supt., Port Blair, Apl., 1924.

BIGGIE, LAWRENCE PATRICK, Indian Police (b. 24th Apl., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Nov., 1936.

BIGG-WITHER, LIONEL, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 31st Dec., 1876).—Joined the service, 11th March, 1898; asst. loco. supt., July, 1902; dep. ditto, March, 1917; in milty. employ from 25th Oct., 1917; dep. ch. mech. engr., Dec., 1922; offg. ch. mech. engr., Feb., 1924; confd., Oct., 1930; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

BIGSBY, SYDNEY HERBERT, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Punjab) (b. 26th July, 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; on deputation to Jaipur State from 1 Aug., 1924; offg. supt. engr., Oct., 1926; confd., Mar., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to govt. p.w.d. irrign. bch., May, 1936; confd., Nov., 1937.

BIJAWAT, MUL CHAND (see Mul Chand Bijawat).

BIJAY, SIR CHAND MAHTAB (see Burdwan).

BILARAM, RUPCHAND (see Rupchand, B.).

BILL, CHARLES ESTWICK SYDNEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 15th Mar., 1914).—Educ. at Haileybury and Keble College, Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

BILL, VEN. SYDNEY ALFRED, M.A., Eccles. Dept., United Provs. (Bishop of Lucknow) (b. 21st Feb., 1884).—Apptd. a chaplain on probation, 15th Dec., 1911; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1914; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1924; archdeacon of Lucknow, Nov., 1925, to May, 1937; retd., Aug., 1938; Bishop of Lucknow.

BILLCLIFFE, LESLIE, India Office (asst. to infm. officer) (b. 25th Feb., 1893).—On mil. service Aug., 1914 to May, 1919; apptd. clerical officer., June, 1922; resdt. clerk, Oct., 1937 to Feb., 1938; asst. to information officer, Feb., 1938.

BILLIMORIA, KAIKHOSRU DHANJISHAH, B.Sc. (b. 21st Sept., 1902).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1924, as dep. supt. of police, Bombay; prom. to Indian Police, as supt., 1st Apl., 1937.

BILMORIA, RUSTORNJE SORABJI, M.A., LL.B., Barr., Bombay Judl. Dept. (offl. assignee).—Apptd. offl. assignee, high ct., Bombay, 1st Dec., 1935.

BINDRA BAN, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 7th July, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st May, 1913; asst. auditor genl., Jan., 1933; dept. acct.-genl., posts and tels., May, 1936; asst. auditor genl., Nov., 1936; ditto, personnel, Apl., 1938; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1939.

BINGHAM, Hon. HUGH TERENCE DE BURGH, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 31st Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. dep. dir. projects, rly. bd., May, 1924; offg. supt. of works, Aug., 1928; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1929; and again, May, 1933; confd., June, 1935; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., June, 1934; chief engr. (prov.), Aug., 1937.

BINNEY, ANTHONY LOCKHART, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Central Provs.) (b. 18th July, 1890).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1914, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; on mil. service from March, 1915, to Sept., 1919; dep. commr. (prov.), Aug., 1925;

confd., May, 1926; with resident, Hyderabad, from 3rd Sept., 1928; dir.-genl. and secty., rev. dept., Nizam's govt., Aug., 1931; offg. commr., June, 1934; finl. secty. to govt., C.P., Oct., 1935; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Central Provs., Feb., 1936; commr. (prov.), Nov., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1939; commr. confd., Feb., 1939.

BINNS, BERNARD OTTWELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of settlements, etc., Burma) (b. 16th July, 1898).—Educ. at Grammar Sch., Bury, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. commr., June, 1930; dep. secty., fin. dept., Dec., 1935; commr. of settlements and and record, Burma, Dec., 1936.

BION, ARNOLD EUSTACE, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bihar) (b. 2nd Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 1st Dec., 1910; supt., June, 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1934; and again, Apl., 1936; confd., Dec., 1937.

BION, CLARENCE RAYMOND FIELD, late Indian Police. (b. 31st, July, 1884).—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1903, as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa; transf. to Assam, Oct., 1906; supt. (prov.), Nov., 1910; confd. as supt., April, 1911; on mil. duty, Sept., 1918; to Feb., 1919; offg. inspr.-genl., Assam, June, 1932; retd., Mar., 1935.

BIRCH, Rev. Canon ORMONDE WINSTANLEY, M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 14th May, 1879).—Apptd. chaplain on prob. 7th Nov., 1911; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1914; senr. chaplain, March, 1925; retd., Sept., 1936.

BIRDWOOD, Baron (Sir WILLIAM RIDDELL BIRLWOOD), G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., C.I.E., D.S.O., LL.D., Field Marshal, I.A. (b. 13th Sept., 1865).—First commn., dated 9th May, 1885; div. prov. marshal, Tirah expedy. force, Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898; special service, South Africa, from Nov., 1899; D.A.A.G., South Africa, Oct., 1900, to June, 1902; mil. sec. to C.-in-C. in India, June, 1902; specially employed at Hqrs. of Army, July to Oct., 1902; A.M.S., and interpreter to C.-in-C., India, Nov., 1902; A.A.-G., Hqrs., India, Sept., 1904; mil. sec. to C.-in-C., India, Dec., 1905; A.D.C. to the King, Feb., 1906, to Oct., 1911; C.I.E., Jan., 1908; brig. commr., India, June, 1909; Q.M.G. in India, May, 1912; sec. to govt., Army Dept., Nov., 1912; war services: Hazara expdn., 1891, medal with clasp; Isazai expdn., 1892; N.W. Frontier of India, 1897-98, medal with two clasps; Tirah, 1897-98, clasp; S. African War, 1899-1902, Queen's medal with six clasps, King's medal with two clasps; N.W. Frontier of India, 1908; medal with clasp, D.S.O.; European War, 1914 (commanded Australian and New Zealand Army Corps, 1914-15; ditto, Mediterranean Expedy. Force, 1915-16; ditto, 5th Army, France, 1918-19); K.C.M.G., June, 1915; K.C.B., June, 1917; G.C.M.G., Jan., 1919; G.C.B., Jan., 1923; temp. member of govr.-gen.'s

exec. council, Aug. to Nov., 1924; C.-in-C. in India and member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Aug. 1925, to Nov., 1930; G.C.S.I., Jan., 1930; Col.-Commandant, 13 F.F. Rifles, Sept., 1930; apptd. master of Peterhouse, Cambridge, Mar., 1931; G.C.V.O., May, 1937; created Baron, Jan., 1938.

BIRT, A. G., B.Sc., late Dept. of Agriculture, Assam.—Apptd. dir. of agriculture, 1st Apl., 1931; retd., Feb., 1933.

BISCOE, DUDLEY HUGH, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 12th July, 1909).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1929; pers. asst. to A. to G.G., Gujarat States and retd. at Baroda, Dec., 1934; ditto, Cent. India, Mar., 1935; under trng. in U.P., June and Oct., 1935; asst. claims commr., Quetta, July, 1935; under secty. to A.G.G. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, June, 1937; confd., Feb., 1938; under secty. and pers. asst. to A.G.G., resdt. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Apl., 1938; special duty in Mysore, Oct., 1939.

BISCOE, WALTER TREWEEKE, Indian State Rlys. (divl. supdt. (junior)) (b. 8th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 11th July, 1910; on milty. duty from 6th Oct., 1914, to 28th Oct., 1918; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., June, 1928; resdt. manager, Indian rly. publicity bureau, New York, Dec., 1930; divl. comml. manager, N.W. Rly., Feb., 1936; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager May, 1936; divl. supdt. (junior) July, 1938.

BISHUMBHAR DAYAL, M.A., Indian Police (b. 29th July, 1907).—Joined the service as probey, asst. supt., United Provs., 10th Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1932.

BISHEN CHAND, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1st Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 21st Sept., 1904; Dep. chief auditor, N.W. Rly., Apl., 1933.

BISHNATH PRASAD, RAI SAHIB BABU, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., United Provs. (b. 13th Mar., 1875).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1901; dep. collr., May, 1913; asst. commr., March, 1923; retd., March, 1930.

BISHOP, JOHN EADIE, Indian Police (b. 15th Dec., 1917).—Asst. supt., Bihar, apptd. on probn., Dec., 1937; confd. April, 1939.

BISHOP, LAURENCE ARTHUR, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 3rd Feb., 1895).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1914; asst. supt., Jan., 1920; on milty. duty from 4th Aug., 1916, to 2nd Jan., 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1924; confd., Feb., 1930; on foreign service as commr. of police, Travancore, Aug., 1928; reverted to Brit. service, Nov., 1932.

BISHOP, ROBERT WILSON, late govt. printing and staty. dept., Burma (b. 24th Sept., 1876).—Rendered previous services in non-gazetted appts. from 24th April, 1902; dep. supt. govt. printing, India, 8th Dec., 1904; asst., ditto, Burma, Aug., 1905; retd. Jan., 1932.

BISHOP, THEODORE BENDYSH WATSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 13th

Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Rugby, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; pte. sec. to the lieut.-govr., United Provs., May to July, 1916; jt. mag., Dec., 1916; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1924; mag. and collr. (prov. sub.), Jan., 1927; confd., July, 1927; dep. commr., Nov., 1927; magte. and collr. again, Nov., 1932; commr., Aug., 1939.

BISHWA NATH LAHIRI, M.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 13th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 9th Aug., 1922; offg. supt., Dec., 1925; confd., Dec., 1932.

BISSET, ERNEST, M.B., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 1st Dec., 1876).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1902; in milty. employ to 28th May, 1911; on plague duty in U.P., June, 1911; dep. sany. commr. U.P., July, 1912; offg. dir. of public health, Burma, Jan., 1923; confd., July, 1923; retd., Apl., 1931.

BISSETT, ALLAN, India Audit Office (supig. exec. officer) (b. 4th Mar., 1895).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 5th Aug., 1914; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, April, 1926; suptg. exec. officer, Oct., 1934.

BISWAS, KSHITISH CHANDRA, M.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 11th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 4th April, 1909; asst. acct.-genl., Sept., 1910; dep. ditto, Punjab, April, 1925; ditto, Burma, Feb., 1926; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, April, 1927; dep. ditto, March, 1928; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1929; asst. auditor-genl., Mar., 1931; dep. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Aug., 1931; retd., Aug., 1934.

BISWAS, SARAT CHANDRA, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr. E.B. Rly.) (b. 1st June, 1897).—Joined service Jan., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1924; exec. engr. (prov.), Nov., 1938.

BISWAS, SATISH CHANDRA, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Indian State Rlys. (dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 12th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as dist. surg., 13th April, 1918; dist. medl. officer, Apl., 1929.

BISWAS, SATISH CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, M.B.E., (b. 23rd Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th July, 1904; supt. F. and P. Dept., Nov., 1927; asst. secty., ditto, May, 1935; asst. secty., political dept., Apl., 1937; M.B.E., June, 1939.

BIVAR, HUGH GODFREY STUART, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 28th Nov., 1897).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford (Classical Exhibitioner); joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; and sub-divl. officer, Aug., 1923; addl. presdy. mag., April, 1926; addl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1928; offg. ditto, Nov., 1931; confd., Feb., 1934.

BLACK, D. G. LYALL, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 4th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 9th July,

1920; offg. supt., April, 1923; confd., Aug., 1927.

BLACKBURN, JOHN INGAMELLS, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 24th Nov., 1910). Educ. at County High School and Trinity College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

BLACKER, HAROLD ALFRED CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 15th Aug., 1889).—Educ. at Charterhouse, Bedford Grammar Sch., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 30th Nov., 1914, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1917, to Dec., 1918; registrar, high ct., Punjab, Nov., 1922; dist. and sess. judge (prov.), July, 1925; ditto (perm.), Oct., 1925; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, Oct., 1934; regr., high ct., Lahore, Oct., 1935; actg. judge, ditto, June, 1936; and again, Apl., 1937; judge, ditto, Dec., 1937.

BLACKSTONE, THOMAS WEST, late Indian Police (b. 3rd July, 1881).—Joined the service, 10th Dec. 1901, as probir.; asst. supt., Jan., 1903; supt., Jan., 1910; asst. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1924; confd., Oct., 1926; commr. of police, Madras, June, 1931; retd., July, 1936.

BLACKWELL, SIR CECIL PATRICK, M.B.E. (milty. dn.), B.A. (judge, high ct., Bombay) (b. 8th Nov., 1881).—Joined the service as judge, high ct., Bombay, Nov., 1926; offg. ch. justice ditto, Apl., 1935; and again, Mar., 1937; knighted, Jan., 1938.

BLACKWOOD, JAMES FULTON, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 20th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1925; offg. dep. dir. programme, rly. board, Nov., 1926; secty., rly. bd., May, 1931; retd., June, 1935.

BLAIR, FRANCIS RITCHIE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1913; inspr. of schools, Apl., 1914; and again Oct., 1921; asst. dir. of public instrn., June, 1915; dep. ditto, Oct., 1927; special officer in charge, primary and girls' educ., Aug., 1930; prinpl., Patna training coll., July, 1931; dir. pub. instrn., Bihar, Apl., 1937; retd., Apl., 1938.

BLAIR, JAMES RICHARD, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (secty., communications and works dept., Bengal) (b. 20th Aug., 1890).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service from March, 1915, to Feb., 1919; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Feb., 1920; confd., March, 1921; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1922; mag. and collr., Nov., 1926; dep. secty., pol. and appt. dept., April, 1927; dep. secty. to govt. of India, army dept., June, 1930; offg. addl. secty., pol. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; secy., communications and works dept., Bengal, Oct., 1938.

BLAKE, ALEXANDER GRANVILLE, late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Aug., 1895).—Educ. at Rossall and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1920; arrived,

3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1922; offg. coll., Nov., 1926; offg. commr. f Coorg, May to Nov., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. magte., May, 1930; retd., Nov., 1937.

BLAKE, EDGAR VALENTINE MYLES, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 2nd Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 1st Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936.

BLAKE, REGINALD GILBERT, India Office (executive officer, accont.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 20th Feb., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 3rd Dec., 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; acting hr. exec. offr., July, 1939.

BLAKER, CHARLES EDWARD, O.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th June, 1881).—Joined the service, 18th Aug., 1905; asst. engr., Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; prinpl., govt. school of engrg., Rasul, April, 1918; offg. supt. engr., May, 1932; O.B.E., June, 1935; retd., June, 1936.

BLAKISTON, JOHN FRANCIS, C.I.E., late Archaeological Survey Dept. (b. 21st Mar., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Mar., 1911; I.A.R.O., Oct., 1915; supt., Jan., 1916; offg. dep. dir.-genl., Jan., 1925; offg. dir.-genl., Jan., 1933, and again, Dec., 1934; confd., June, 1935; C.I.E., May, 1937; retd., Sept., 1937.

BLANDY, EDMOND NICOLAS, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal) (b. 31st July, 1886).—Educ. at Clifton Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr. and mag.; transfed. to Bengal, April, 1912; offg. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., Sept., 1914; confd., Oct., 1914; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1917; sec. provl. recruiting board, Sept., 1917; under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., April, 1919; offg. commr. of income tax, Feb., 1922; mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; dep. commr., Dec., 1928; offg. secty., govt. of Bengal, fin., commerce and marine depts., Apl., 1930; confd., Aug., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; commr., Jan., 1934; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, Nov., 1934; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, fin. comm. and marine depts., Mar., 1936; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, Aug., 1936; spec. offcr. fin. dept., Oct., 1937; C.S.I., June, 1939.

BLANEY, BRYAN EDWARD, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 17th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1917; asst. engr., Oct., 1918; asst. exec. engr. and offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1919; confd., Aug., 1929.

BLANFORD, HARRY RICHARD, O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 15th June, 1884).—Joined, 15th Nov., 1905, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1910; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; consvr., Sept., 1925; ch. consvr., Burma, June, 1935; retd. Feb., 1939.

BLANK, ABRAHAM LEWIS, M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., legisl. dept., Assam) (b. 19th May, 1891).—Educ. at City of London School and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Nov., 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Nov., 1919; jt. mag. and dy. collr., Oct., 1920; offg. mag. and

collr., April, 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1925; confd., Nov., 1926; commr. for workmen's compn., March, 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Assam, legisl. dept., Mar., 1935; confd., Nov., 1935; secty., judicial dept. and legal remembrancer, Bengal, May, 1939.

BLASCHECK, ARTHUR DAVID, F.C.H., Oec. D., late Indian Forest Service (b. 16th Jan., 1879).—Joined the service 30th Nov., 1900, as asst. consr. of forests; dep. consr., Jan., 1907; instructor, Impl. Forest Coll., Dehra Dun, April, 1911, to April, 1914; on spl. duty in rev. and agric. dept., govt. of India, from June to Oct., 1915; consr., Dec., 1921; offg. chief consr., Punjab, Jan., 1924, and again, May, 1926; confd., Mar., 1929; inspr.-genl. of forests, Oct., 1930; ret'd., July, 1934.

BLEASE, Rev. RUPERT GORDON, B.Sc., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 12th Oct., 1896).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 11th Dec., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1929.

BLENCH, THOMAS, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Nov., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 15th Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936.

BLENKINSON, BERNARD ARTHUR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Assam) (b. 22nd Mar., 1879).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. engr., Bengal; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; ditto, Assam, April, 1912; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1922; offg. chief engr., Aug., 1925; suptg. engr., Oct., 1926; offg. secty. P.W. dept., Oct., 1927; chief engr., Sept., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

BLEWITT, ALBERT ERNEST, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 2nd Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 15th June, 1920; offg. supt., June, 1924; and again, Feb., 1930; confd., Aug., 1932.

BLOMFIELD, DOUGLAS JOHN, A.C.G.I., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 20th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908, as asst. engr.; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1927; confd., Feb., 1931; offg. ch. engr., Mar., 1934.

BLOOD, Sir BINDON, G.C.B., G.C.V.O., Genl., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—Apptd. to Bombay public works dept., 13th March, 1871; transf'd. to Bengal sappers and miners, Sept., 1871; on famine duty, 1874; exec. engr., March, 1874; took part in Jowaki expedn., 1877-78 (medal with clasp); on special duty, Calcutta torpedo defence, April 1878; served in S. African war (medal with clasp), and Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); rejoined Indian estabt., Feb., 1880, as offg. commandant, Bengal sappers and miners; took part in Egyptian expedn., 1882 (medal, star, and 4th class Osmanieh); supernumerary exec. engr., mil. works dept., Simla, April, 1885; second in command, Bengal sappers and miners, Aug., 1885; commandant, 1886-93; apptd. to mil. works dept. as suptg. engr., Sept., 1893; col. on staff, Rawal Pindi, Oct., 1894; chief staff officer, Chitral relief force, 1895; K.C.B., Jan., 1896; comdg. Bandelkhand dist., Jan., 1896;

commanded the Malakand Field Force, July, 1897; comdg. Meerut divn., Sept., 1898; served in South African war, 1899-1901; cmdg. Northern Army Corps, Nov., 1904; ret'd., Nov., 1907; G.C.B., June, 1909; G.C.V.O., June, 1932.

BLUMENTHAL, SIMON, India Audit Office (suptg. executive officer), (b. 2nd Dec., 1881).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India Audit Office, 25th April, 1901; minor staff clerk, Oct., 1919; staff clerk, Feb., 1922; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; suptg. ditto, Aug., 1933.

BLUNDEN, ALFRED CHARLES, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 18th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1912; supt., Aug., 1924; addl. asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Jan., 1931; ditto, spec. bch., Apl., 1936; cent. intell. offcr., U.P., Nov., 1937.

BLUNT, ALFRED DICKINSON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service, 8th Aug., 1903; in mil. employ from 26th May, 1916, to 31st Jan., 1920; asst. engr., U.P. engrg. service, Feb., 1920; I.S.E., 1st Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1927; ret'd. Nov., 1938.

BLUNT, BRIAN GAIRE, late Indian C.S. (b. 18th July, 1897).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Christchurch, New Zealand, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag.; under-sec. to govt., pol. and dept., appt. uly, 1924; actg. pte. sec. to govr. of Bihar and Orissa, April, 1925; settlement officer, June, 1927; ret'd., May, 1933.

BLUNT, SIR EDWARD ARTHUR HENRY, K.C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 14th March, 1877).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1906, to Oct., 1907; jt. mag., Oct., 1911; supt., census operations, from April, 1910; spl. duty, Nov. to Dec., 1912; spl. duty in fin. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1912, to April, 1913; dep. commr. (prov. subs.) and settlt. offr., Oct., 1915; dir. of civil supplies, Sept., 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; dir. of industries, United Provs., Oct., 1919; offg. fin. sec. to govt., Jan., 1920; confd., Nov., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; commr., Apl., 1931; member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Dec., 1931 to Apl., 1935; K.C.I.E., June, 1934; ret'd., Dec., 1936; lecturer on "Indian Social Welfare" to I.C.S. probationers at Cambridge Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, Nov., 1937.

BO, U, Indian Police (b. 13th Aug., 1911).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., 21st Jan., 1936.

BOAG, GEORGE TOWNSEND, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (second secty. to govt., Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1884).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived 24th Nov., 1908 and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. settlt. officer, Sept., 1912; spl. duty as asst. to controller of tan stuffs, Madras, April, 1918; sub-collr. and jt. mag., March, 1919; offg. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1923; supt. of census, Madras, April, 1920, to July, 1922;

commr., corpn. of Madras, Nov., 1923, to April, 1925; on special duty at Bangalore under govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., April to June, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte. and sec. to govt. of Madras, fin. dept., June, 1925; confd., Aug., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; on spl. duty in ch. secretariat, June, 1930; curator, Madras record office, Nov., 1930; member, Indian tariff board, Apl., 1931; offg. addl. secty. to govt., Madras, public dept., Oct., 1933; secty. to govt., law and educn. dept., Madras, Apl., 1935; actg. second secty. to govt., Madras, Apl., 1935; ditto (provl. subs.), Mar., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1936; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, July, 1936; again, July, 1938; secty. to the Governor, Apl., 1937; actg. govr. of Orissa, Aug.-Dec., 1938.

BOCQUET, GUY SUTTON, C.I.E., V.D., late Indian State Railways (b. 14th May, 1882).—Apptd. traffic inspr., 19th Jan., 1901; asst. traffic supt., June, 1902; dist. traffic supt., April, 1909; on mil. duty from May, 1917, to July, 1918; dep. traffic manager, April 1918; C.I.E., Aug., 1918; offg. traffic manager, Aug. 1925; traffic manager (provl.), Nov., 1928; confd., Feb., 1933; retd., Aug., 1936.

BODY, CHARLES STUART, late India Audit Office (b. 29th Dec., 1875).—Educ. at Beccles and Ashford Gr. Sch., clerk, 2nd class, 29th Jan., 1895; staff clerk, April, 1907; junior clerk, Feb., 1915; temply. transf. to mily. dept., May, 1917; senior clerk, Feb., 1922; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; retd., Mar., 1933.

BOEDDICKER, FRANCIS LEONARD FITZMAURICE, Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt., Burma Rlys.) (b. 18th Feb. 1884).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt. 7th July, 1910; dist. loco. supt., Mar., 1926; offg. dep. loco. supt., Apl., 1929.

BOKHARI, A. S., M.A. (Cantab.), All-India Radio (dep. contr. of broadcasting) (b. 1st Oct., 1898).—Apptd. station dir., All-India Radio, Mar., 1936; dep. contr. of broadcasting, June, 1936.

BOLAM, DOUGLAS HAIG, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 10th Oct., 1916).—Educ. at Whitgift Sch., Croydon, and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd. 7th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

BOLAM, JOHN WALLACE, Indian Police (b. 30th May, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 17th Nov., 1926; asst. to A.G.G. in C. India in criminal bch., Mar., 1933; supt. of police, Benares State, Feb., 1934.

BOLDEY, TIGRANE DAVID, P.W. Dept., Burma (div. elec. engr.) (b. 16th June, 1896).—Joined the service as temp. engr., Mar., 1927; div. elec. engr., Apl., 1937.

BOLTON, SIR HORATIO NORMAN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1875).—Educ. at Rossall and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 9th Dec., 1898, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to chief commr., Sept.,

1903; to May, 1904; pol. asst., Feb., 1906; pol. asst. and divl. judge, April, 1907; pol. asst. and dep. commr., March, 1909; pol. asst. and divl. and sess. judge, Dec., 1909; pol. agent and sec. to chief commr., N.W.F. Prov., May, to Dec., 1910; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Feb., 1911; dep. commr., Peshawar, Feb., 1912; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Oct., 1914; dep. commr., Peshawar, Jan., 1915; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; C.S.I., Jan., 1918; rev. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Decr., 1920; offg. chief commr., and agent to govr.-gen., N.W.F. Prov., July, 1923; confd. Dec., 1924; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1926; retd., Oct., 1931.

BOLTON, JOSEPH CLEMENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. fin. dept., Bombay) (b. 20th Mar., 1906).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar School and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Sind as asst. collr.; asst. rev. officer, Lloyd Barrage and Can. Constr., 1930-1; under-secty. to govt., home, etc., depts., Feb., 1933 to 1936; confd., July, 1933; offg. collr., Sept., 1936; dep. secty., fin. dept., May, 1937.

BOLTON, REGINALD RAINSBURY, M.O., B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Nov., 1896).—(exec. engr., Burma).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1927; confd. Oct., 1929.

BOMFORD, GUY, CAPT. R.E., Survey of India, (supt.) (b. 28th June, 1899). First commn. dated 28th Feb., 1917; in milty. employ to 24th Aug., 1921; proby. asst. supt., Aug. 1921; confd. Oct., 1925; supt., Aug., 1929.

BONAR, ROBERT, A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mech. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 10th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. works manager, 1st Apl., 1927; production engr., Sept., 1928; works manager, Oct., 1930; offg. divl. supt. mechl., E.I. Rly., Sept., 1935; dep. chief mechl. engr.; confd., Sept., 1937.

BONARJEE, NEIL BRUNIAT, Indian C.S. (jt. magte., United Provs.) (b. 10th March, 1901).—Educ. at Dulwich and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1925; arrived, 9th Dec., 1925; and served in the United Provinces as asst. commr.; jt. magte., Mar., 1927; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1934; officer on spec. duty Imp. Council Agric. Research, Nov., 1936; ditto, dept. of e.h. and l., Nov., 1937.

BOND, FRANCIS SELWYN, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 18th Feb., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct. 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; I.A.R.O. from 16th Aug., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., Sept., 1923; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. ch. engr., Dec., 1927; retd., Dec., 1930.

BONINGTON, MAX CARL CHRISTIAN, O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 26th Jan., 1874).—Joined the service, 23rd Aug., 1905; dep. consvr., March, 1920; retd., Dec., 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1934.

BONNAUD, REGINALD CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 8th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1916; asst. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; ret'd., July, 1933.

BÖNTÖFT, REV. REGINALD ARTHUR, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt.—Apptd. chaplain on probation, N.W.F.P., 9th Oct., 1930; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

BOON, GEORGE ALAN JAMES, Indian Police (b. 3rd Oct., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, United Provinces, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

BOOTH, EDGAR HICKMAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1929; asst. exec. engr., 31st Oct., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1938.

BOOTH, JOHN REGINALD TREVOR, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 14th August, 1883).—Educ. at Bromsgrove School and Worcester Coll., Oxford; appointed after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th November, 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Jan., 1916; spl. duty in posts and telegraphs dept., July, 1918; postmr.-gen., Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1919; ditto. central circle, Feb., 1920; offg. dep. dir.-gen. of post office, May, 1924; postmr.-gen., Punjab and N.W.F. circle, Feb., 1925; dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., May, 1928; P.M.G., Bengal and Assam, and offg. senr. dep. dir.-genl., Mar., 1930; confd., Feb., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; P.M.G., Bombay circle, Oct., 1936.

BOOTH, JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 14th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood College, Co. Kildare, Balliol College, Oxford, University College, London, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. minister, Khairpur state, May, 1935.

BOOTH-GRAVELY, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (counsellor to the govr., Burma) (b. 22nd May, 1882).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., and the Univ., Edinburgh, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd April, 1906, and served in Burma as asst. commr., asst. sec. to govt., March, 1909; offg. under-sec., May, 1910; asst. sec., rev. dept., Nov., 1910, to Nov., 1911; pte. sec. to Lt.-govr., Aug., 1912; under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1913; dep. sec. to ditto, ditto (s.p.t.), Oct., 1916, to April, 1918; dep. commr., April, 1918; offg. sec. to govt. of Burma, April, 1920; member of Burma legisl. council, June, 1920; rev. sec. to govt. of Burma, Jan., 1921; sec. to govt., fin. and rev. dept., Feb., 1924; offg. commr., Jan., 1928; ditto (provl. subs.), Nov., 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; ch. secty. to govt., Burma, Aug., 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1936; tempy. member, govr.'s exec. council, Burma, May, 1936; counsellor to the govr., Apl., 1937; K.C.M.G., Jan., 1939; acting govr. of Burma, July, 1939, to Sept. 1939.

BOR, NORMAN LOFTUS, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (forest botanist) (b. 4th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 20th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Aug., 1924; pol. offer., Balipara frontier tract, Dec., 1931; bot. for. offer. and silvicul., Assam, Nov., 1936; for. botanist, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1937.

BORLASE, REV. JOHN JENNINGS DINGLE, LL.D., M.B.E., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 10th Feb., 1878).—Apptd. chaplain, 15th Dec., 1916; rendered service during the war; M.B.E., June, 1919; senior chaplain, Dec., 1919; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

BOSE (see also Basu).

BOSE, AMBUJI NATH, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Bihar civil Medl. dept. (prof. Patna medl. coll.) (b. 21st Aug., 1890).—First commn. dated 13th Mar., 1916; in milty. employ prior to 1st Apl., 1926; offg. prof., Prince of Wales medl. coll., Patna, Apl., 1926; prof., medl. coll., Patna, Sept., 1927; bacteriologist to govt., Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1928; O.B.E., June, 1938.

BOSE, ARUN KUMAR, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., M.B.E., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 27th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service as offg. dep. magte. and dep. collr., 4th Sept., 1903; confd., May, 1904; M.B.E., June, 1918; offg. magte. and collr., Apl., 1930, and again, Apl., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

BOSE, BABU JATINDRA NATH, late Indian Forest Service (b. 12th Nov., 1881). Apptd. to I.F.S. as dep. consvr., 1st April, 1924; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

BOSE, BIRENDRA KUMAR, L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service as apprentice engr. (provl. service), 20th Feb., 1908; asst. engr., Aug., 1914; exec. engr., Feb., 1917; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

BOSE, CONSTANCE HOPE, Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, exec. grade, educn. dept.).—Apptd. educ. officer, 14th April, 1930.

BOSE, FANINDRA NATH, B.Sc., M.R.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 10th May, 1887).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1914 as junr. engr.; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; asst. exec. engr., E.I. Rly., Nov., 1927; exec. engr. (junr.), Mar., 1928; ditto (senr.), Oct., 1933.

BOSE, HEMENDRA KUMAR, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 10th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service as probnr., 11th Feb., 1916; asst. acct.-genl., Feb., 1918; dep. acct.-genl., Burma, May, 1929; ditto, Bengal, Sept., 1929; examr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Aug., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., June, 1932; offg. dep. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Feb., 1933; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing acct., Apl., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., United Provs., Jan., 1935; offg. dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Jan., 1935; dep. ch. accounts offr., E.B. Rly., Jan., 1937; dep. ch. accounts offr., E.I. rly., Jan., 1939.

BOSE, KUMUDINI KANTA, B.S. (Univ. of Illinois), Assoc. A.S.M.E. (contr. of purchase) (b. 1st Mar., 1887).—Joined the service 12th July, 1924, as asst. contr. of purchase, Indian Stores Dept.; contr. of purchase, Calcutta Circle, 9th July, 1937.

BOSE, NABA KUMAR, B.Sc. (Cal.) (Glas.) A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (asst. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1892); asst. dist. loco. supt., Feb., 1918; asst. supt. junior scale, Oct., 1926; asst. supt. senior scale, May, 1934; dep. ch. mech. engr. provl., Oct., 1939.

BOSE, PROBHAT CHUNDER, Rai Bahadur, Indian State Ry. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 12th Dec., 1890).—Asst. traffic supt., Aprl., 1922; asst. dist. traff. supt., junior scale, May, 1927; dist. traff. supt. (prov.), June, 1937.

BOSE, SUDHANSU KUMAR, B.Sc., A.R.S.M., Indian School of Mines (b. 7th Oct., 1900).—Apptd. prof. of mining and surveying, 7th Dec., 1927; offg. vice-prinpl., Indian sch. of mines, May, 1935.

BOSE, SUDHANSU MOHAN, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab), Bar.-at.-Law, apptd. Member, Public Service Commission, Bengal, 13th Aprl., 1937.

BOSE, VIVIAN, Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (Judge high ct., Nagpur).—Apptd. Judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936.

BOSTOCK, THOMAS MAURICE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Aug., 1877).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll., apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1900; attached to Punjab, Dec., 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1921; confd., Feb., 1922; offg. ch. engr., and secty. to govt., Punjab irrigation bch., July, 1931; confd., June, 1932; retd., Aug., 1932.

BOTHAM, ARTHUR WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1874).—Educ. at Newcastle-under-Lyme and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 21st Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1906; mag. and collr., April, 1911; dep. commr., commr. of excise, &c., Assam, April, 1912; second sec. to chief commr., Assam, Nov., 1913; dep. commr., and ditto, March, 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; chief sec. to govt., Assam, Dec., 1919; member of govt.'s exec. council, Assam, Dec., 1925; C.S.I., July, 1926; retd., Aprl., 1933.

BOTTOMLEY, JOHN EDWARD, Indian State Ry. (works manager, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 13th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 13th Jan., 1920; asst. works manager, G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1925; works manager, Oct., 1932.

BOTTOMLEY, JOHN MELLOR, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon), Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., Bengal) (b. 21st Mar., 1888).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's sch., Crosby and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service as prof., Dacca Coll., 12th Feb., 1911; insp. of schools, Nov., 1925; asst. dir. of public instrn., Aug., 1926; princpl., Chittagong Coll., Dec., 1926; asst. dir. of public instrn. for Muhammadan educn., Bengal, June, 1929; princpl., presy. coll., Calcutta, Aug., 1931; dir. of public instrn., Bengal, May, 1933; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, educn. dept., July, 1934; C.I.E., May, 1937.

BOTTOMS, ALVAN DOUGLAS, Indian Audit Office (higher executive officer) (b. 22nd Sept., 1899).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 8th Aug., 1919, after previous service from 25th Aprl., 1917; second class clerk, 29th Aprl., 1920; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; transfed. to India Audit Office, March, 1922; exec. officer, Aprl., 1922; higher exec. officer, Oct., 1934.

BOUCHIER, EDWARD WALLACE, Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1906).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School, and University College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag; sub-collr., Sept., 1932; under secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, June, 1934; ditto, home dept., Aprl., 1936; ditto public dept., Sept., 1937 to July, 1938; secty., Central Road Traffic Board, Madras, Nov., 1938.

BOUCHIER, OWEN PLATT, Office of High Commr. for India (exee. officer, accis. dept.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 14th Aprl., 1920; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Aprl., 1925.

BOULD, REGINALD PERRY, Indian State Ry. (b. 6th Jan., 1884).—Joined the service on N.W. Rly., on 16th Nov., 1905; services transferred to G.I.P. Rly. from 6th Aprl., 1912; asst. tel. supt., Aprl., 1912; dep. transptn. supt. (tels.), Oct., 1926.

BOULTON, Rev. WALTER, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 17th July, 1901).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Bengal, Jan., 1927; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1939.

BOURDILLON, Sir BERNARD HENRY, K.C.M.G., K.B.E., late Indian C.S.
