BUTLER, STANLEY GORDON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 15th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

BUTTER, JOHN HENRY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 20th April, 1916).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd. 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

BUTTERFIELD, EDWARD, D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Burma Commission. (b. 9th April, 1880).—Arrived in India, April, 1900, and rendered services in mil. dept.; asst. commr., May, 1906; on mil. duty from Dec., 1914, to Feb., 1917; comdt., mil. police, Feb., 1917; dep. commr., May, 1918; development officer in addn., Nov., 1923; ditto and land officer, Jan., 1924; sec., &c., to Rangoon dev. trust board, March, 1924; chairman, dev. trust, Rangoon, March, 1926; commr., town lands, Burma, Feb., 1927; offg. commr. variously since Feb., 1930; ret'd., June, 1935.

BUYERS, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Apl., 1876).—Joined the service as junr. asst. engr., 20th Nov., 1896; asst. engr., March, 1899; junr. engr., June, 1903; resdt. engr., July, 1907; dist. engr., Oct., 1914; in milty. employ from 25th May, 1918, to 8th Jan., 1919; dep. ch. engr., April, 1923; senr. govt. inspr., circle No. 5, Oct., 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

BYERS, JOSEPH AUSTEN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 1st Oct., 1895).—Educ. at King's Sch., Chester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 12th Dec., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr., and mag. sub. collr. and mag., Jan., 1924; addl. sessions judge, June, 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; confd., June, 1934; served temp. with govt. of Orissa, from Dec., 1936.

BYRNES, WILLIAM FRANCIS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th Oct., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

BYRON, FRANCIS HARVEY, Salt Rev. Dept., Burma (collr.) (b. 21st Sept., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1912; supt., Jan., 1912; offg. collr., June, 1929; confd., Nov., 1934; addl. commr., salt rev., Mar., 1937.

BYRON, RICHARD ROCHFORT, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 28th Mar., 1876).—Joined the service, 6th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Aug., 1908; exec. engr., Sept., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

BYU, U TUN, Barr., Burma Judl. dept. (asst. govt. advocate) (b. 22nd Aug., 1895).—Apptd. offg. asst. govt. advocate, 1st March, 1924; confd., Dec., 1926; offg. govt. advocate, Burma, May, 1936.

CABLE, WILLIAM ARTHUR, Eng. Lt. Comdr., R.I.N., ret'd. (late Marine Survey dept., Bombay) (b. 15th Jan., 1883).—Apptd. engr. and ship surveyor, Karachi, Feb., 1928; offg. princpl. ditto, Bombay, Jan., 1933; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

CADELL, Sir PATRICK ROBERT, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 7th Dec., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and administrator of Sachin state; 2nd asst., May, 1897; chairman, Karachi plague commn., July, 1898;

under-sec. to govt., rev., &c., depts., Sept., 1900, to April, 1901; 1st asst., April, 1902; collr. of customs, Bombay (imperial customs service), March, 1906; pte. sec. to govr., July, 1907; collr. of customs, Calcutta, March, 1908; senr. collr., Nov., 1912; munpl. commr., Bombay City, May, 1910; C.I.E., June, 1913; offg. commr. in Sind, July to Oct., 1913; chief sec. to govt., rev., finl. and septe. depts., Bombay, April, 1916; chief sec. in charge war purposes and septe. depts., and controller of prices (in addn.), July, 1918; C.S.I., June, 1919; offg. commr. in Sind and contr. of prices, Jan., 1920; offg. commr., Oct., 1920; confd., Mar., 1921; chairman, Bombay Port Trust, Mar., 1922; and again, Aug., 1924; temp. member of govr. exec. council, Bombay, Jan. to Aug., 1924; commr. in Sind, Sept., 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1926; knighted, Jan., 1935.

CADMAN, SAMUEL PARKES HUBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Adams' Grammar Sch., Newport, Shropshire, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1932; ret'd., Aug., 1938.

CAFFIN, ALBERT EDWARD, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 16th June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Mar., 1927, and again July, 1933; confd., Sept., 1933.

CALDECOTT, CHARLES D'ARCY, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Prov.) (b. 18th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 18th Feb., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1922.

CALDER, CHARLES CUMMING, B.Sc., F.L.S. (Supt. Royal Botanic Garden, Calcutta, and cinchona cultivation in Bengal) (b. 3rd Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 23rd March, 1912, as curator of the herbarium at Royal Botanic Garden, Calcutta; offg. supt. Royal Botanic Garden, Calcutta, and cinchona cultivation in Bengal, May, 1925.

CALDER, NORMAN DOUGLAS, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Jan., 1882).—Joined the service, 9th June, 1900; asst. traffic supt., June, 1905; dist. traffic supt., April, 1912; dep. traffic mgr., April, 1920; offg. dir. of estabt., rly. bd., Oct., 1929; on spl. duty, rly. bd., Oct., 1930; offg. dir. of traffic, rly. bd., Dec., 1932; offg. traffic manager, E.B. rly., Oct., 1934; confd., Aug., 1936; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

CALDER, WILLIAM BUCHANAN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 18th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1927; offg. exec. engr., Apr., 1931; confd., Oct., 1936.

CALDWELL, KENNETH SOMERVILLE, M.A., B.Sc., Ph.D., F.I.C., F.C.S., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Feb., 1880).—Joined the service, 17th Jan., 1910, as prof. of chemistry; offg. prinpl., science coll., Patna, July, 1927; confd., Jan., 1928; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

CALVERT, HUBERT, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th Nov., 1875).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 28th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as

asst. commr.; dep. commr., Oct., 1915; ditto, and registrar, co-operative credit societies, &c., Punjab, April, 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; off. commr., April, 1925; member, Royal Commn. on Agric. in India, 1926-27; commr., Oct., 1928; finl. commr., Mar., 1929; finl. commr. (dev.) and secty. to govt., Punjab, dev. dept., Mar., 1932; temp. member of gov.'s exec. council, Punjab, May, 1932; and again, Sept., 1932; C.S.I., June, 1933; retd., Nov., 1933.

CALVERT, WILLIAM HANDLEY, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 27th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 15th March, 1921; confd., Mar., 1923; supt., Apl., 1930; pol. officer. Sadiya Frontier tract, Mar., 1935.

CAMBRIDGE, SYDNEY JOHN PELHAM, V.D., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E., Indian State Ryls. (exec. engr., E.I. Ry.) (b. 11th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service 22nd Apl., 1912; asst. engr., Apl., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; exec. engr., July, 1938.

CAME, WILLIAM GERALD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bihar) (b. 8th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; suptg. engr. (temp.), Mar., 1934; confd., Apl., 1936.

CAMERON, BRUCE LACHLAN, Indian State Ryls. (dep. gen. manager N.W. Ry.) (b. 13th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service, 21st Mar., 1913; on milty. service from May, 1918, to Nov., 1920; offg. dist. traffic supt., Nov. 1920; asst. ditto, Jan., 1922; divl. transpn. supt., July, 1927; dep. agent, personnel, Jan., 1933; secty. rly. bd., May, 1937-May, 1938; dep. gen. manager, N.W. Ry., Oct., 1938.

CAMERON, DUNCAN CHARLES THOMAS, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 6th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. dist. supt., Mar., 1929, and again June, 1933; confd., Aug., 1935.

CAMERON, GILBERT STUART, Dept. of Mines in India (inspr.) (b. 22nd April, 1891).—Joined the service as junr. inspr., 25th April, 1921; offg. inspr., June, 1934; confd., Sept., 1936.

CAMERON, JOHN GORDON PATRICK, C.I.E., late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 31st Jan., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906; asst. supt., Oct., 1906; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; divl. engr., 1920; elec. engr.-in-chief, April, 1926; retd., March, 1932.

CAMERON, JOHN PHILIP, C.S.I., C.I.E., Col., I.M.S., retd., late Jail Dept., Assam (b. 22nd May, 1879).—Joined the service, 26th July, 1901; apptd. supt. of jails, Madras, 1st Dec., 1905; reverted to mil. duty, Nov., 1914; inspr. gen. of prisons, Madras, April, 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; transf. to Assam as inspr. genl. of civil hosps. and prisons, April, 1931; C.S.I., Jan., 1932; retd., Feb., 1935.

CAMERON KER, EDWARD, Indian Railway Accts. Service (dep. chief accts. offcr., E.I. Ry.). (b. 31st Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. auditor, E.I. rly., 29th Feb., 1908; served in I.A.R.O., from Oct., 1914, to Sept., 1916; accts. offcr., gr. I., E.I.R., Dec., 1925; confd. as dep. chief accts. offcr., E.I.R., Dec., 1926; dep. ditto, E.B. Rly., Apl., 1930, to Oct., 1932.

CAMMIADE, PAUL EUGENE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 17th Jan., 1874).—Educ. at Madras Univ., Caius Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., Dec., 1902; joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1906; mag. and collr., March, 1909; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1911; retransfd. to Bengal, April, 1912; actg. judge, Calcutta high ct., June to Aug., 1926; Nov., 1926; and again, Feb. to Aug., 1927; retd., Jan., 1931.

CAMPBELL, ALEXANDER, M.C., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instn., Burma) (b. 29th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as profir. of English on 24th March, 1915; on mil. service from 31st Aug., 1916, to 14th Feb., 1919; registrar Rangoon Univ., Dec., 1921; inspr. of schools, Burma, June, 1933; offg. dir. of public instn., Aug., 1933; and again, June, 1936; asst. dir. of public instn., Feb., 1935; dir. of public instn., April, 1939.

CAMPBELL, SIR ARCHIBALD YOUNG GIPPS, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th May, 1872).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt., Nov., 1903; private sec. to govr. of Madras, May, 1906; C.I.E., Jan., 1911; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1912; actg. presdt. of the Corporation of Madras, July to Oct., 1913; member, weights and measures cttee., govt. of India, Oct., 1913, to July, 1914; collr. and dist. mag., Madras, Oct., 1914; employd. in British Red Cross Society, June to Dec., 1916; dir. of industries, Madras, Nov., 1919, to Aug., 1921; on depn. to England, Sept., 1921, to Jan., 1922; employd. under govt. of India in connection with an enquiry into cost of administration of Coorg, April to June, 1922, and in connection with similar enquiry in respect of Central India, Rajputana, &c., Oct., 1923, to Jan., 1924; sec. to govt., rev. dept., July, 1914; C.B.E., June, 1921; chief sec. to govt. of Madras, March, 1925; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Aug., 1926; Aug., 1927; Mar., 1928; and again, June, 1929; confd., Jan., 1930; C.S.I., Jan., 1927; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., Jan., 1935.

CAMPBELL, CHARLES ROSS, D.S.O., M.V.O., Captain, R.I.M. (ret.) (b. 19th Nov., 1876).—Apptd. depy. conservator of the Port of Madras, May, 1922; M.V.O., Jan., 1922; presdy. port officer, Madras, May, 1924; retd., Feb., 1930.

CAMPBELL, DOUGLAS COLIN, Indian Railway Accts. Service (chief accts. offcr.) (b. 28th May, 1891).—Joined the service as dep. chief accts. offcr., Burma Ryls., 20th Mar., 1932, on abolition of appt. as dir. of comml. audit; confd. as chief accts. offcr., July, 1933; dir. rly. clearing accts. office, Oct., 1934; chief accts. offcr., N.W. Rly., Oct., 1937.

CAMPBELL, JAMES DONALD, D.S.O., Col., R.E., late Survey of India (b. 3rd May, 1884).—First commn. dated 21st Dec., 1901; joined the survey of India dept. as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1906; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to Oct., 1919; dep. supt., Feb., 1921; supt., May, 1928; offg. dir., Feb., 1929; confd., Aug., 1931; retd. May, 1939.

CAMPBELL, Sir JOHN, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., O.B.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., March, 1902; under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. and comm. dept., April, 1904, ditto, dept. of commerce and industry, Feb., 1905; sec., factory commn., Oct., 1907, to Jan. 1908; jt. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1908; chairman, Cawnpore Municipal Bd., Dec., 1908, to March, 1912; dep. commr., May, 1914; mag. and collr., April, 1915; O.B.E., June, 1918; temply. employed in rev. and stats. dept., India Office, from May, 1919, to July, 1921; ret'd., Oct., 1922; vice-chairman, Greek Refugee Settlt. Commn., 1923; C.S.I., June, 1923; Kt., June, 1925; economic and finl. adviser, C.O., Nov., 1929; K.C.M.G., June, 1933.

CAMPBELL, John HERBERT, Indian Service of Engineers (asst. exec. engr.), (b. 23rd May, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 20th Oct., 1930; offg. exec. engr., May, 1936.

CAMPBELL, Walter FENDALL, Major, (local Lt.-Col.), I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th May, 1894).—First commn., dated 14th Jan., 1914; joined the pol. dept., 21st Jan., 1921; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, June, 1921; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, May, 1926; asst. commr., Hangu, Nov., 1926; pol. agent, Kurram, Nov., 1927; dep. commr., D. I. Khan, Oct., 1928; ditto, Kohat, Oct., 1928; asst. commr., Thal, Nov., 1928; dep. ditto, Bannu, Nov., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., in the states of W.I., Oct., 1933; prime minister, Alwar, Mar., 1935, to Sept., 1936; pol. agent, Bundelkhan, Oct., 1937; resdt. in Waziristan, Feb., 1939.

CANDY, Ronald HERBERT, C.I.E., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (C.M.O. and civil surg., Dehli) (b. 13th June, 1886).—First commn. dated 27th Jan., 1912; offg. civil surg., Apl., 1923; confd., Aug., 1927; offg. surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Bombay, July, 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; C.M.O. and civ. surg., Delhi, Mar., 1938.

CANNING, Frederick, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 31st Dec., 1882).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1903, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1908; consvr., Apl., 1924; offg. ch. consvr., May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. inspr.-genl. of forests, Apl., 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

CANTIN, Pierre Antoine LEON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Jan., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. engr., United Provs.; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; suptg. engr., May, 1923; dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1928; ch. engr., Nov., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

CANTLIE, Keith, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 6th Feb., 1886).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll. (Aberdeen), Aberdeen Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. commr., June, 1911; ditto, Assam, April, 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), May, 1915, to April, 1919; presdt., Manipur

State Darbar, Sept., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1923; confd., May, 1927; offg. commr., Mar., 1937, to Feb., 1939; C.I.E., June, 1939.

CAPOOR, SHYAM BEHARI, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 15th Jan., 1907).—Educ. at Allahabad University and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service 10th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1938.

CARBERRY, MURLOUGH, M.A., B.Sc., M.C., Indian Agricultural Service (asst. dir. of agric., Bengal) (b. 24th July, 1890).—Joined the service as agric. chemist on 1st Dec., 1920; offg. asst. dir. of agric., Bengal, June, 1933; and again, Sept., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935.

CARDEN, Ven. Henry Craven, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 23rd Dec., 1882).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 23rd Feb., 1913; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1916; on mil. duty during 1918-19; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1926; archdeacon, Lahore, Oct., 1929; ret'd., Sept., 1936.

CARDEW, David, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 12th Aug., 1905; dist. loco. supt., Apl., 1913; divl. rolling stock offr. and offg. supt., mech. wkshops., May, 1929; offg. dep. loco. supt., May, 1931; offg. supt., mech. workshops, N.W. rly., Apl., 1932; confd., Dec., 1932; offg. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. rly., Jan., 1933; confd., May, 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1938.

CARDEW, John Horace, Indian State Rlys. (dep. loco. supt., Burma Rlys.) (b. 31st July, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 3rd July, 1913; on milty. duty from 13th Dec., 1914, to 8th May, 1920; offg. variously as dist. loco. supt. from July, 1920; offg. dep. loco. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., May, 1931.

CAREY-EVANS, Sir Thomas John, Kt., M.C., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 26th June, 1884).—Entered I.M.S., 1907; served in Egypt and Mesopotamia during European War, 1914-18; civil medl. officer, Baghdad; resdt. medl. officer, Mysore State; surgeon to H.E. the Viceroy of India; ret'd., July, 1926; apptd. examr. of surgical instruments, India store dept., 13th June, 1925.

CARGILL, Ian Peter MacGillivray, Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 29th Sept., 1915).—Educ. at Malvern and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd. 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

CARGILL, William M., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th Aug., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., 1st Aug., 1909; senr. asst. signal engr., March, 1914; signal engr., March, 1924; dep. ch. engr., signals, Oct., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

CARLESS, Henry Alfred, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 26th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 20th Nov., 1914; offg. supt., Feb., 1921; confd., Jan., 1926; princpl. police trng. school, Moradabad, Sept., 1930, to July, 1931.

CARLETON, Hadden Hamilton, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 25th July, 1904).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School, and Cambridge (Trinity Hall); joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec.,

1927, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub.-collr. and jt. magte., Jan., 1929; collr. and dist. mag. G. & M. station, Bangalore, May, 1939.

CARLIN, DANIEL, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 10th July, 1882).—Educ. at Glasgow Athenæum; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India store depot, 22nd Nov., 1901; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, May, 1922; ret'd., July, 1932.

CARMAN, FRANK, Indian Police (b. 15th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn. 26th Nov., 1936; confd. Jan., 1939.

CARNAGHAN, DOUGLAS WILLIAM BERESFORD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bombay) (b. 21st Dec., 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Bombay, Nov., 1932; confd., Apl., 1935.

CAROE, OLAF KIRKPATRICK, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 15th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at Winchester and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; city mag., Peshawar, Nov., 1923; dep. commr., Peshawar April, 1926; asst. dep. commr., Mardan, Oct., 1926; dep. commr., Hazara, Aug., 1927; ditto, Kohat, Nov., 1928; jt. dep. commr., Peshawar, April, 1930; dep. commr., ditto, July, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1933; dep. secty. to govt. of India, pol. dept., May, 1934; offg. resdt., Persian Gulf, Aug., 1937; offg. A.G.G., Resident and C.C. in Baluchistan, Nov., 1937; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Mar., 1938, temp. resdt. in Waziristan, July, 1938; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, March, 1939; secty. external affairs dept. Govt. of India, July, 1939.

CARPENTER, JOHN HOWARD, Indian State Rlys. (publicity officer, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Joined the service as 2nd asst. secty. to agent, 5th July, 1912; 1st asst. ditto. July, 1913; I.A.R.O. from 12th Nov., 1914, to 9th July, 1919; secty., June, 1920; offg. publicity officer, Nov., 1928; confd., Feb., 1930.

CARR, JOSEPH, late Indian Forest Service (b. 17th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1909, and posted to Central Provs. as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; offg. consvr., July, 1926, and again, Mar., 1927; confd., June, 1930; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

CARR, SIR WILLIAM, Kt., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Feb., 1872).—Educ. at Lancaster Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 14th Nov., 1893, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; judge, small cause court, Mandalay, Feb., 1905; also dist. judge, March, 1905; divl. judge, 1909; addl. judge, high ct., Rangoon Jan., 1923; judge, high ct., Rangoon, July, 1924; offg. ch. justice, July, 1930; Kt., June, 1931; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

CARREY, JOHN, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., B.Sc., Major I.M.S., Medl. Dept., Central Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 23rd July, 1894).—First commn., 20th Jan., 1923; offg. civil surgeon, Central Provs., Mar., 1932; confd., Mar., 1934.

CARRIER, PHILIP LESLIE CARO, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Rlys. (C.M.O., Burma rlys.) (b. 14th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service

as dist. medl. officer, Burma Rlys., 14th Jan., 1927; offg. C.M.O., ditto, Nov., 1933; and again, Mar., 1936; confd., May, 1936.

CARRITT, MICHAEL JOHN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Sedbergh and Queen's College, Oxford; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1932; offg. under-secty., pol. and appts. depts., Bengal; confd., Mar., 1936; ret'd., Feb., 1939.

CARROLL, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 21st July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 20th Dec., 1913; dep. consvr., Dec., 1918; offg. consvr., May, 1934; confd., June, 1937.

CARROTT, WILL JUDSON, Burma Civil Service (Cl. 1) (asst. commr.) (b. 18th Feb., 1916).—Educ. at Govt. English High Sch., Maymyo and Rangoon Univ.; apptd. on probn., 30th Sept., 1938.

CARSE, ROBERT, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 4th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service, Nov., 1923, and devoted to police training coll., Sardah, Bengal; proby. asst. supt., Oct., 1925; confd., May, 1926; offg. supt., Feb., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935; pol. offcr., Balipara fron. tract, July, 1938, to Nov., 1938.

CARSON, CHARLES FRED, M.C., Col. Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr.) (b. 10th Feb., 1886).—First commn. dated 26th June, 1908; asst. engr., July, 1913; on mil. duty from 23rd Oct., 1914, to 14th Sept., 1919; exec. engr., Dec., 1918; offg. divl. supt., June, 1930; offg. dep. agt., organisation, Apl., 1933; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), June, 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), N.W. rly., Sept., 1935; offg. agent, Mar., 1936; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Apl., 1936, confd. gen. manager, N.W. rly., May, 1939.

CARSON, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM CHARTERIS, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (b. 21st July, 1874).—Joined the service, 9th May, 1893, and served in acctnt.-gen.'s office, Bombay and Hyderabad; asst. comptr., Hyderabad, April, 1898; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Aug., 1898; ditto, Bengal, April, 1901; ditto, Burma, and inspr. of local fund accts., June, 1901; ditto, ditto, United Prov., Jan., 1903; asst. comptr., Central Prov., Nov., 1903; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1904; ditto and examr. of local fund accts., Jan., 1905; on deputation as acctnt.-gen., Jammu and Kashmir State, March, 1905, to June, 1912; offg. dep. comptr.-gen., India, Nov., 1913; confirmed, Nov., 1915; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, from May, 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; dep. contr. of currency, Bombay, Dec., 1919; acctnt.-gen., United Prov., May, 1922; ditto, Punjab, Nov., 1923; offg. contr. of civil accts., March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., July, 1929; Kt., June, 1939.

CARTER, ALFRED COLDRICK, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 6th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 28th Oct., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1931; asst. commr., Nov., 1933; confd. as dist. supt., Nov., 1937.

CARTER, ARTHUR CHARLES, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 27th May, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 24th March, 1922; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Feb., 1933; O.B.E., June, 1939.

CARTER, Rev. BRYAN BERNARD, M.A., late Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 15th Dec., 1876).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 16th Oct., 1909; junr. chaplain, 27th Oct., 1909; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1919; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

CARTER, HORACE LEONARD, M.I.R.S.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. signal engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 17th Dec., 1898).—Asst. signal engr., 15th Dec., 1922; dist. ditto, 5th Feb., 1935.

CARTER, MALCOLM OGILVY, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd July, 1898).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 15th Dec., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. and dep. collr., May, 1923; offg. magte. and collr., variously from Feb., 1929; offg. secty., bd. of rev., Bengal, Sept., 1934; mag. and collr., April, 1936; secty. land rev. commission, Bengal, Nov., 1938; dir. of land record and surveys, Bengal, Dec., 1938.

CARTER, REGINALD ROBERT LANGHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 16th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Bradfield and University College, Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., Mar., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932.

CARTER, THOMAS, M.B.E., Dept. of Printing and Stationery (manager G. of I. Press) (b. 22nd Nov., 1891).—Joined the service, 27th Mar., 1920; asst. manager, G. of I. press, Feb., 1927; offg. manager, ditto, Mar., 1927; confd., April, 1931; M.B.E., June, 1934.

CARTLAND, MURRAY A., late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 18th Apr., 1876).—Joined the service, 4th April, 1889; dist. opium officer, March, 1926; managing dir. opium factory, Ghazipur, Mar., 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

CARTLAND, WALTER CUTHBERT, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Nov., 1879).—Joined the service in subordinate estabts., 3rd Jan., 1907; block signalling works manager, July, 1919; asst. engr., Jan., 1922; dist. signal engr., June, 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr. (signals), Mar., 1933; confd., Aug., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

CARTWRIGHT, ADELBERT, Indian State Rlys. (transportation (power) supt. and asst. works manager, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1892).—Joined service, June, 1921; asst. works manager (junr. scale), Dec., 1924; asst. works manager (senr. scale), provl., Nov., 1938.

CARTWRIGHT, ALBERT, Indian Police (b. 27th Dec., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 21st Nov., 1931; confd., Apr., 1934.

CARVEY, EUSTACE MALTRAVERS, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 22nd July, 1893).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1915; asst. contr. of stores, Aug., 1919; offg. dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly. variously from Feb., 1921; confd., Dec., 1932.

CASE, ROBERT CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. contr. of standardisation (mech.)) (b. 19th Apr., 1893).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1919, as asst. loco. supt.; offg. dist. ditto, E.B. rly., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. dir., rly. bd., Nov., 1927; dep. ch. mechl. engr., G.I.P. rly., Nov., 1931; offg. dir., rly. bd., Sept., 1936; dep. ch. contr., standardisation (mechl.), Oct., 1936.

CASEMENT, EDGAR REGINALD, Indian State Rlys. (dtul. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 13th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 29th Oct., 1910; on milty. duty from June, 1915, to Oct., 1918; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1930; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., March, 1934; confd., Nov., 1935; dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1935.

CASSELLS, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th Dec., 1883).—Educ. at Kirkcaldy High Sch., St. Andrews Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., judl. poll. and appt. depts., Aug., 1913; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1916; mag. and collr., March, 1920; dep. sec., pol. and appt. depts., Feb., 1921; chief sec., govt. of Bengal, March, 1922, to Sept., 1923; offg. secty. to govt., finance, &c., depts., Nov., 1927; confd., Feb., 1928; offg. commr., Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

CASSELS, SIR ROBERT ARCHIBALD, G.C.B., C.S.I., D.S.O., p.s.c., General, Indian Army (commander-in-chief in India) (b. 15th Mar., 1876).—First commn. dated 22nd Jan., 1896; A.D.C. to Div. Comdr. India, 1906-07; brig.-maj., India, 1909-11; G.S.O.2 India, 1911-13; D.A.A.G. India, 1914-15; D.A.A.G., I.E.F. "D", April-Dec., 1915; G.S.O.1, 14th Div. and Cav. Div., I.E.F. "D" and Mes. E.F., 1915-17; Brig.-Genl. Gen. Staff, Mes. E.F., Aug.-Nov., 1917; Brig.-Comdr. and Div. Comdr., Mes. E.F. (18th Div. and Central Kurdistan Field Force), 1917-19; Div. Comdr., Egypt, 1919-20; Maj.-Gen. Cav., India, 1920-23; Dist. Comdr., Peshawar, India, 1923-27; A.G. in India, 1928-30; A.D.C. Gen. to the King, 1929-33; G.O.C. in C., N. Comdr., India, 1930-34; C. in C. in India and member Gov. Genl.'s exec. council, 29th Nov., 1935; G.C.B., 1933. War services, Iraq, 1915-19; Egypt, 1919-20. Mentioned in despatches nine times. Legion of Honour, 4th class; Order of the White Eagle, 4th class, with swords.

CASTENS, HERBERT ERNEST, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 10th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

CATER, SIR ALEXANDER NORMAN LEY, K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. and Indian Political Service (b. 15th June, 1880).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 5th Dec., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., pers. asst. to pol. agent, Pahluchistan States and Bahawalpur, April, 1907; pol. asst., Baluchistan, Jan., 1909; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Nov., 1909; asst. agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Oct., 1910; asst. pol. agent, Zhorb, Sept., 1911; asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Sept., 1914; asst. pol. agent, Kalat, Oct., 1914; second asst. to agent to govr.-gen., in Baluchistan, Oct., 1915; first asst. to ditto, Jan., 1916; first asst. resdt., Hyderabad, March, 1917; offg. dep. sec. (foreign), for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, April, 1919; confd., Oct., 1919; sec. to resdt. in Mysore, Feb., 1923; resident at Jaipur, Nov., 1925; also addl. charge of office of pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk from April, 1926; resdt., Jaipur, Sept., 1928; offg. agent to govr.-genl.

in Rajputana and C.C., Ajmer, Merwara, Apl., 1929; ditto, Madras States, Dec., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; judl. commr., Baluchistan, Nov., 1930; offg. A.G.G. & C.C., Baluchistan, Mar., 1931; confd., Dec., 1931; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1934; retd., Aug., 1938.

CAVE, LESLIE STORRER, Indian State Ryls. (divl. mech. engr., N.W. Ry.). (b. 3rd Mar., 1894).—Asst. loco. supt., junr. scale, 28th Feb., 1922; divl. mech. engr., senr. scale, Feb., 1937.

CHADHA, CHARANJIT LAL, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 21st Dec., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 19th Jan., 1923; dep. consvr., Jan., 1927; retd., May, 1935.

CHADWICK, Sir DAVID THOMAS, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 7th April, 1876).—Educ. at De Aston Sch., Market Rasen, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spcl. asst. settlm. offr., May, 1903, to Feb., 1907; spec. settl. offr. and spec. asst. collr., March, 1908; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1912; collr. and mag., Oct., 1912; dir. of agric., Oct., 1912; on deputn. to Russia, France and Italy, June, 1916; Indian trade commr. in England, Oct., 1917, to July, 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1921; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Oct., 1922; temp. member, gov.-genl.'s council, July-Dec., 1923; C.S.I., Jan., 1925; Kt., Jan., 1927; retd., March, 1929; K.C.M.G., May, 1937.

CHADWICK, FREDERICK JOHN, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1894).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1921; asst. supt., Sep., 1925; offg. dist. supt., July, 1932; confd., July, 1933.

CHAIN, U BA MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 24th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 1st Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

CHAINANI, HASHMATRAI KHUBCHAND, Indian C.S. (dep. secty. Legal Dept., Bombay) (b. 29th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. coll. and magte; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, Aug., 1933; offg. remembr. of legal affairs, June, 1935; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1935; offg. secty., legisl. council Bombay, March, 1936; dep. secty. leg. dept., Bombay, April, 1938; ex-officio sect. to Bombay Legislative Council, Apr., 1938.

CHAKKO, K. C., B.A., B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (princl., coll. of engrg.) (b. 24th Aug., 1892).—Apptd. profir. of civil engrg., coll. of engrg., Madras, 18th Nov., 1921; princpl., coll. of engrg., Guindy May, 1936.

CHAKRABATTI, JATINDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Indian Agricultural Service (b. 12th Jan., 1882).—Joined the service as agricl. supervisor, 4th Jan., 1908; offg. dep. dir. of agric., May, 1919; confd. Dec., 1920; offg. dir., Mar., 1933; retd., Sept., 1937.

CHAKRABATTI, REBATI MOHAN, Rai Bahadur, M.A., late Bengal Civil Service (b. 2nd July, 1879).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 18th July, 1901; dep. magte. and dep. collr., May, 1902; magte. and collr., Mar., 1929; retd., June, 1932.

CHAKRA DHAR JAYAL, RAI BAHADUR, B.A. (late Indian Police Service) b. 17th Jan., 1879).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 14th Aug., 1904, to 24th Dec., 1908; dep. supt., Dec., 1908; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1921; transferred to Tehri State from June, 1925; supt., Jan., 1926; retd., Sept., 1932.

CHAKRAVARTHI, SUBRAHMANYAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 30th Aug., 1910).—Educated at the Hindu High School, Triplicane, University of Madras, The School of Economics, London, and Trinity College, Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., Dec., 1937.

CHAKRAVARTI, A., M.A., late Indian Educational Service (b. 1st July, 1883).—Apptd. profir., presdy. coll., Madras, 23rd Feb., 1921; offg. principal, arts coll., Rajahmundry, 3rd July, 1929; confd., Feb., 1930; retd., July, 1938.

CHAKRAVARTY, ASHWINI KUMAR, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1890).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1914; Pers. asst. to contr. of currency, Dec., 1925; supt. Govt. of India fin. dept., Dec., 1928; asst. acct. genl., Bengal, Oct., 1936.

CHAKRAVARTI, PHANI BHUSAN, O.B.E., Rai Bahadur, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 16th June, 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Mar., 1906; asst. auditor genl., Dec., 1934; O.B.E., May, 1937; offer. on spec. duty in the fin. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1937.

CHAKRAVARTI, SURENDRA NATH, M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th June, 1892).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; municipal engr., Delhi, Dec., 1933 to April, 1939; M.B.E., Feb., 1937.

CHAKRAVARTI, TULSI DAS, B.A., Indian State Ry. (transptn. supt.) (b. 21st Aug., 1887).—Joined the service 24th Mar., 1913; asst. supt., Jan., 1916; transptn. supt. (traffic comml.), E.I. Rly., Nov., 1934.

CHAKRAVARTY, BIRENDRA NARAYAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 20th Dec., 1904).—Educ. at University of Calcutta, University C II., London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th October, 1929; arrived November, 1929, and served as asst. coll. and mag., Bengal; jt. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936.

CHAKRAVARTY, NARENDRA NARAIN, M.A. Income Tax dept., Bengal (asst. commr.) (b. Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1912; sub dep. collr., Dec., 1913; inc. tax officer, Aug., 1923; asst. commr., July, 1929.

CHALAM, BALAJIPAT SESHA, Indian State Ryls. (malariaologist, E.B. rly.) (b. 7th Nov., 1886).—Apptd. malariaologist, E.B. rly., on probn., 23rd June, 1928; confd., Sept., 1932.

CHALK, WALTER OLIVER, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 29th Nov., 1881).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 4th Dec., 1907; dist. ditto, June, 1914; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; pers.

asst. to ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1927; offg. dep. ch. opertg. supt., Nov., 1928; offg. supt., mech. workshops, Oct., 1920; and again, Oct., 1933; confd., May, 1934; dep. ch. operatg. supt., N.W. rly. (provl.), Mar., 1932; confd., Sept., 1934; offg. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. rly., Sept., 1935; retd., Nov., 1936.

CHALMERS, DAVID FERGUSON, late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 8th Feb., 1877).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 10th Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr., asst. supt., N. Shan states, and asst. settlmt. offr.; offg. dep. dir., land records dept., Nov., 1913; confd., Oct., 1914; dep. commr., April, 1915; offg. commr. of settlts. and land records, July to Oct., 1916; dir. of agric., Oct., 1918; commr. (s.p.t.), Oct., 1923; confd., 1925; retd., May, 1930.

CHAMAN LAL, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 6th Dec., 1879).—Joined the service, Dec., 1901; asst. acct.-genl., Central Revs., Oct., 1925; dept. acct.-genl., Punjab, Mar., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., United Provs., Nov., 1931; offg. acct.-genl., ditto, Mar., 1933; retd., Nov., 1934.

CHAMBERS, DAVID GOSTLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 19th Oct., 1907); educ. at Dulwich College and Caius College, Cambridge; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

CHAMBERS, HENRY ANGRAVE, A.M.I.O.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Jan., 1877).—Joined the service, 12th Jan., 1904; asst. engr., Nov., 1909; exec. engr., Jan., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1925; confd., Jan., 1929; retd., Jan., 1932.

CHAMBERS, JOHN, O.B.E., M.C., A.M.I.C.E. Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 17th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1919; exec. engr., Apl., 1926; O.B.E., June, 1935.

CHAMBERS, LESLIE RAPER, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 30th Apl., 1910).—Educated at Bradford Grammar School and Pembroke College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CHAMBERS, S. P., Bd. of Inland Revenue, on loan as Income-tax adviser to the Central Bd. of revenue from Dec., 1937.

CHAMIER, Sir EDWARD MAYNARD DESCHAMPS, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., Barr. (1887), late India Office (b. 4th June, 1866).—Entered the service as govt. advocate in the high court, N.W.P., 2nd Jan., 1896; 2nd addl. judl. commr. Oudh, Nov., 1901; 1st ditto, Nov., 1904; judl. commr., July, 1907; judge, high court, N.W.P., April, 1910; knighted, Jan., 1916; chief justice, high court, Patna, March, 1916, to Oct., 1917; legal adviser and solicitor to the sec. of state, Nov., 1917; K.C.I.E., June, 1924; retd., June, 1933; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1934.

CHAMIER, Miss ETHEL, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (inspectress of schools, Central Provs.) (b. 19th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 24th Sept., 1909; asst. inspectress, May, 1910; inspectress, Oct., 1915; O.B.E., June, 1939.

CHAMPA LAL, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 1st Jan., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932.

CHAMPHEKAR, MAHADEO LAXMAN, M.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 6th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 7th Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., June, 1925, March, 1926, March, 1927, and Nov., 1927; confd., Oct., 1931; dep. secty., govt. of Sind, p.w.d., May, 1938.

CHAMPION, FREDERICK WALTER, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 24th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

CHAMPION, HARRY GEORGE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 9th Dec., 1915; dep. consvr., July, 1919; asst. silviculturist, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1925; silviculturist, Jan., 1926.

CHAMPION, REGINALD STUART, O.B.E. (b. 21st Mar., 1895).—On military service, 1914-1917. Served in Occupied Enemy Territories Administration; appointed Deputy Governor Grade III, Hebron, Palestine, July, 1920; transferred to Beersheba, April, 1924; title changed to Administrative Officer, Jan., 1925; transferred to Jerusalem Division as asst. dist. commissioner, July, 1927; seconded for service as Protectorate Secretary, Aden, Oct., 1928; first asst. and protectorate secretary (now designated political sec.), Aden, March, 1932; offg. ch. commr., Aden, Apl., 1933; O.B.E., Jan., 1934.

CHAN TOON, JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 4th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Nov., 1931.

CHANCE, PERCIVAL VINCENT, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Central Provs.) (b. 12th June, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1929; confd., Sept., 1930; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to govt., C.P. and Berar; buildings and roads and irrig. bch., Apl., 1935; chief engr., Nov., 1937.

CHANCE, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian State Railways (ch. accts. officer) (b. 28th Feb., 1888).—Joined the service 15th Feb., 1912; senr. asst. auditor, Nov., 1926; dep. ch. accts. officer, Nov., 1930; dep. agent, Burma rly., Mar., 1931; ch. accts. officer, Mar., 1934.

CHAND, AJIT (see Ajit Chand).

CHAND, BISHEN (see Bishen Chand).

CHAND, AMIR (see Amir Chand).

CHAND, HARI (see Hari Chand).

CHAND, HUKAM (see Hukam Chand).

CHAND, KARM (see Karm Chand).

CHAND, KHUB, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 16th Dec., 1911).—Educated at Government High Schools, Delhi and Simla, University of Delhi and Oriel College, Oxford; joined the service, 4th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr; jt. magte., Mar., 1937.

CHAND MAL, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 7th April, 1911).—Educated at Govt. High School, Delhi, Delhi University, Thomason C.E. College, Roorkee, and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Jan., 1937.

CHAND, MUNISHWAR (see Munishwar Chand).

CHAND, NIHAL (see Nihal Chand).

CHAND, PREM (see Prem Chand).

CHAND, SAKHI (see Sakhi Chand).

CHAND, SUMER (see Sumer Chand).

CHAND, TEK (see Tek Chand).

CHANDA, APURVA KUMAR, Indian Educl. Service (princl., David Hare trng. coll.) (b. 12th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1920; vice-prin., Dacca Coll., June, 1922; prof., presdy. coll., Calcutta, Oct., 1926; offg. prin., Krishnagore Coll., Mar., 1930; confd., June, 1930; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, May, 1934; confd., Oct., 1934; princpl. David Hare trng. coll., Nov., 1936.

CHANDA, ASOKA KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 22nd Feb., 1902).—Joined the service, 3rd Mar., 1926; dept. acct.-genl., p. and t., Nagpur, June, 1936; examr. local fund accts. with Govt. of Madras, Nov., 1936.

CHANDAR BHAN, Rai Bahadur, A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 19th Oct., 1908; asst. engr., Oct., 1909; exec. engr. Oct., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1933.

CHANDAVARKAR, PROBHAKAR NARAYAN, B.A., LL.B. Impl. Customs Service (asst. collr., Madras) (b. 20th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as probr., 20th Jan., 1914; asst. collr., July, 1915; offg. collr., Sept., 1933.

CHANDIRAM KOTUMAL (see Kotumal, C.).

CHANDIRAMANI, SANTDAS BULCHAND,

B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. United Provs.) (b. 1st Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, King's Coll., London; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927; and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Mar., 1930; temp. sessions and sub-judge, Oct., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935.

CHANDRA, CHAUDHURI NANAK, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 10th Oct., 1896).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Mooltan, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; joined the service, 24th Nov., 1921; arrived 9th March, 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Punjab as asst. commr., April, 1923; dep. commr. (prov.), Oct., 1929; confd., Jan., 1934; junr. secty. to finl. commrs., Dec., 1931; dep. secty. to govt., rev. dept., Feb., 1934, to Oct., 1935.

CHANDRA, GANESH CHANDRA, A.I.I.A., Archaeological Survey Dept. (b. 1st Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 29th April, 1920; supt., Archl. Survey, Nov., 1927; supt., Southern Circle, July, 1937.

CHANDRA, HARISH (see Harish Chandra).

CHANDRA KISHOR, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1910; asst. engr., Sept., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1922.

CHANDRA, RAM (see Ram Chandra).

CHANDRA, ROMESH (see Romesh Chandra)

CHANDRASEKHARA AYYAR, NAGAPUDI, Judl. Dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 25th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as offg. city civil judge, Madras, 18th July, 1927; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935.

CHANDRASEKHARAN, VALIA VEETIL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 10th May, 1910).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 5th Feb., 1934; confd., Mar., 1936.

CHANDU LAL, DONALD, Indian Police (b. 28th June, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 29th Apl., 1927; confd., Apl., 1929; asst. I.G. rly. police, Apl., 1937.

CHANDWANI, PESUMAL BHAGWANDAS, M.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. pers. offcr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 1st Feb., 1888).—Joined the service, Mar., 1913; asst. audit offcr., July, 1927; Transfd. to Transpn. dept., Mar., 1930; divl. pers. offcr., May, 1935.

CHANING PEARCE, WILLIAM ALAN, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provinces, 1st Nov., 1931; confd., Apl., 1934.

CHANNER, FREDERICK FRANCIS RALPH, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 20th June, 1875).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1896 as asst. consr. of forests; dep. consr., Jan., 1906; O.B.E., June, 1919; consr., Jan., 1921; offg. chlef consr., United Provs., April, 1922; confd., Nov., 1926; C.I.E., June, 1930; ret'd., June, 1930.

CHAPMAN, ARTHUR FREDERIC, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Apl., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1927; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

CHAPMAN, EDWARD FREDERIC GILBERT, Indian Police (b. 2nd Aug., 1895).—(supt., United Provs.). Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Aug., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., July, 1930.

CHAPMAN, HAROLD VICTOR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 15th Apl., 1900).—Joined the service on 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

CHAPMAN, HENRY, Indian State Rlys. (supt. (Power), senr. scale, E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th Oct., 1883).—Asst. supt., junior scale, 1st Oct., 1928; supt. (Power), senr. scale, Jan., 1937.

CHAPMAN, JOHN ALEXANDER (b. 2nd Dec., 1875).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1905; princpl. govt. comml. institute, Calcutta, Jan., 1911; librarian, impl. library, Aug., 1911; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

CHAPMAN, LEONARD ALFRED, Indian C.S. (under secty. govt. of India, communications dept.) (b. 29th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Chigwell School, Essex, and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep.-collr., Nov., 1931; under secty. to govt., fin., comm. and marine depts., Bengal, Apl., 1935; offg. dep. secty., ditto, May, 1936; placed on spec. duty fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1937; under secty., govt. of India, communications dept., Dec., 1938.

CHAPMAN, R. C. G., Major R.E. (deputy master, Security Printing) (b. 18th May, 1899).—Under mil. dept. up to 31st Aug., 1934; deputy master, Security Printing, Sept., 1934.

CHAPPLE, FRANCIS GEORGE, Indian C.S. (under-secty., home dept., Madras) (b. 21st Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Grammar School, Wakefield, and University College, Oxford; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, December, 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub.-collr., Dec., 1932; under-secty., home dept., Madras, Sept., 1937.

CHARAGH, MAHBUB ILAHI (see Mahbub Ilahi Charagh).

CHARAN, ATMA, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, 7th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and coll.; temp. sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1934; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1936; under secty., Govt. of India ind. and lab. dept., Nov., 1936; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1938.

CHARI, PERUNGAVUR VARADA, M.B.E., A.C.G.I.E., M.I.E.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., A.A.I.E.E. (elec. inspr., Electricity Dept., Madras) (b. 11th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. elec. engr., 1st Dec., 1918; offg. elec. inspr. to govt., Feb., 1930; elec. inspr., Feb., 1939.

CHARI, RAJAGOPALA THIRUVENKATA, B.A. Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 30th Nov., 1913).—Educ. at Madras Univ.; joined the service, 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

CHARLTON, JAMES, M.Sc., F.I.C., Indian Agricl. Service (dir. of agric., Burma) (b. 11th June, 1894).—Joined the service as agricl. chemist, agricl. coll., Mandalay, 26th Jan., 1921; princpl. ditto in addn., Dec., 1922; offg. dir. of agricl., Burma, Sept., 1935; confd., Mar., 1936.

CHARSLEY, CLAUDE ROBERT, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 11th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service, 4th Nov., 1921, as asst. supt., on probn.; confd., July, 1924; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1928; confd., Mar., 1932; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924.

CHASE, ALFRED JAMES, O.B.E., late Indian Railway Board (b. 15th Sept., 1875).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1898, and apptd. asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., April, 1904; dep. loco. supt., April, 1911; on depn. as member of Mesopotamia Transport Commn., Nov., 1917, to March, 1918; on spl. duty, office of rly. bd., March, 1918; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; loco. and carriage supt., O. and R. rly., April, 1919; dir. mechanical engrg., April, 1924; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

CHATARJI, ABANIBHUSHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 13th Jan., 1907).—Educated at Patna University and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934.

CHATARJI, ANIL CHANDRA, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., (dir. of pub. health, Bengal) (b. 21st Dec., 1891).—Date of commn., 17th April, 1919; in mil. employ up to 18th May, 1934; asst. dir. of public health, Delhi Prov., to April, 1936; dir. ditto, Bengal, June, 1936.

CHATARJI, GANESH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof'r., govt. coll., Lahore)

(b. 4th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as prof'r. C.T. coll., Lahore, on 1st Dec., 1919, prof'r. govt. coll., Lahore, Sept., 1921.

CHATARJI, GOPENDRA BHUSHAN, BABU, M.A., LL.B., late United Provs. C.S. (b. 18th June, 1882).—Joined the service, 2nd May, 1911; sub-judge, May, 1925; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1935; ret'd., June, 1937.

CHATARJI, SIR NALINI RANJAN, Kt., M.A., B.L.—Apptd. acting judge, Calcutta high ct., Oct., 1910; confd., Dec., 1910; Kt., Jan., 1920; acting chief justice, high ct., Calcutta. 31st July to 27th Aug., 1925, 8th Jan. to 27th Feb., 1926, and from 2nd July to 26th Aug., 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1926.

CHATARJI, SUKUMAR, M.B.E., M.A., Bengal C.S. (inspr.-genl. of regn.) (b. 16th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1908; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1911; inspr.-genl. of regn., Bengal, May, 1936.

CHATARJI, SURENDRA NATH, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 31st Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 29th June, 1925; offg. supt., Mar., 1932; confd., Nov., 1937; King's Police Medal, 1937.

CHATARJI, SURESH CHANDRA, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 5th June, 1892).—Joined the service, 24th Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1917; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1925; confd., Jan., 1928.

CHATTARJI, HARI DAS, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., Bihar (b. 8th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., Dec., 1903; dep. magte. and dep. collr., April, 1905; supt. of excise and salt, Oct., 1916; supt. of inc. tax, Dec., 1921; asst. commr., Apl., 1924; commr., May, 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1936.

CHATTARJI, RANGA LAL, M.A., B.L., Bihar C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 9th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, 9th May, 1911; sub-judge, Aug., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1934; confd., Oct., 1934.

CHATTARJI, SHIBA PRIYA, Rai Bahadur, B.L., late Bihar C.S. (b. 1st Jan., 1882).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1910; asst. regr., high ct., Patna, July, 1921; sub-judge, July, 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1932; confd., Sept., 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

CHATTERJEE, AKHIL CHANDAR, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transportation officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1895).—Joined service, Feb., 1917; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1919; junior scale, Aug., 1920; divl. transportation (traffic-commercial) officer, senior scale, Dec., 1937.

CHATTERJEE, SIR ATUL CHANDRA, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Nov., 1874).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 10th Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W.P. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Jan., 1906; asst. commr., Nov., 1909, to Oct., 1910; regr., co-op. credit soc., June, 1912; ditto and dep. commr., May, 1914; ditto, and mag. and collr., March, 1915; temp. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Sept., 1918; offg. sec. to govt., April, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1919; chief sec. to govt., July, 1919; delegate of govt. of India to internatl. labour conference, Washington, Oct.-Nov., 1919; and Geneva, 1921, 1924, 1925, 1926, 1927, 1928, 1929 and 1933;

member, Indian munitions bd., Feb., 1920; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of industries, Feb., 1921; member of govr.-gen.'s council, 2nd Jan., 1923; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1925; retd. from I.C.S.I Jan., 1925; high commr. for India, 5th Jan. 1925 to 30th June, 1931; K.C.S.I., July, 1930; member of council of India, July, 1931 to July, 1936; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1933.

CHATTERJEE, BASANTA KUMAR, M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 14th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service, 11th June, 1913; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, May, 1914; ditto, Punjab, Jan., 1918; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, May, 1923; ditto, Calcutta, Jan., 1924; ditto, Punjab, Mar., 1925; ditto, Calcutta, April, 1926; dep. acct.-gen., Bengal, Jan., 1927; offg. dep. auditor-genl., June, 1930; dep. ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Feb., 1932; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Mar., 1933; comptr., Assam, Nov., 1933; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. acct.-genl., Burma, Mar., 1935; ditto, Central Provs., Mar., 1936.

CHATTERJEE, GOURI PATI, M.Sc. (Calcutta), Meteorological Dept. (suptg. meteorologist) (b. 25th Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as scientific asst. (temp.), 15th Apl., 1921; meteorologist, Feb., 1922; suptg. meteorologist, Oct., 1938.

CHATTERJEE, HRISHI KESH, Dept. of Mines (junr. inspr.) (b. 12th June, 1893).—Joined the service as junr. inspr. on probn., 29th Nov., 1925; confd., Aug., 1930.

CHATTERJEE, NRITYA GOPAL, Dept. of Mines (inspr. of mines) (b. 15th Jan., 1896).—Apptd. junior inspr. of mines on probn. 14th July, 1923; confd., July, 1925; inspr. of mines, Apl., 1938.

CHATTERJEE, SANTOSH KUMAR, B.A. (Cal. and Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 12th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at University of Calcutta and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. coll., Dec., 1929; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1935.

CHATTERJEE, SISIR CHANDRA, M.D., M.R.C.S., D.P.H., Indian State Ry. (C.M.O., E.B. rly.) (b. 4th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as dist. surg., G.I.P. Ry., 28th March, 1918; offg. dep. C.M.O., May, 1929; ditto, G.I.P. Ry., May, 1931; offg. C.M.O., E.B. rly., Apl., 1932; ditto, N.W. rly., Mar., 1933; ditto, G.I.P. rly., Sept., 1933; C.M.O., E.B. rly., Nov., 1934.

CHATTERJEE, SUBODH CHANDRA (judge, high ct., Patna).—Apptd. Sept., 1939.

CHATTERJEE, SURENDRA NATH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 10th Mar., 1910; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nagpur, Sept., 1930; asst. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Nov., 1931; dep. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Dec., 1932; ditto, Bengal, Jan., 1934; exr., local audit dept., Bengal, Dec., 1935.

CHATTERJI (see entries under Chatarji).

CHATTERTON, SIR ALFRED, C.I.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., late Indian Educ. Service.—Apptd. profir. of engrg., Mad. Engrg. Coll., 6th Aug., 1888; K.-i-H. medal, 1st class, May, 1900; dir. of industrial and technical inquiries, Madras, Aug., 1906; dir. of industries, Oct., 1908; on service under the Mysore Durbar, from

July, 1912; C.I.E., June, 1912; retd., Aug., 1918; Kt., June, 1919; member of the Indian industrial commn.; author of "Lift Irrigation."

CHATURVEDI, MANOHAR DAS (see Manohar, D. C.).

CHAUDHARI, KALYAN CHANDRA, M.A. (Cal.), Indian Railway Accts. Service (accts. officer) (b. 20th July, 1898).—Joined the service, Jan., 1929; accts. officer, Jan., 1930.

CHAUDHARY, KEWAL SINGH, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1915).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Jallanwala, Lyall Pur, Punjab Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 8th Sept., 1939; arrived India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

CHAUDHRI AKBAR HUSAIN (see Akbar Husain, Chaudhri).

CHAUDHURI, CHANDRA MOHAN, Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Bihar) (b. 26th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, on 19th Dec., 1922; divl. forest officer, Nov., 1929.

CHAUDHURI, HARI SADHAN GHOSH (see Ghosh Chaudhuri, H. S.).

CHAUDHURI, KUMUD RANJAN, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 2nd Oct., 1901).—Joined the service, 11th May, 1925; in training to April, 1927; asst. supt., May, 1927; offg. supt., Mar., 1932, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., 1937.

CHAUDHURI, KSHITISH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1st Feb., 1901).—Joined the service, 14th Mar., 1925; dep. acct. genl., B. and O., Apl., 1935; Dep. compr., Assam, Nov., 1936.

CHAUDHURI, MUHAMMAD KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., Assam C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 10th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Mar., 1909; dep. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1910; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1912; dep. commr., Jan., 1933; commr. of excise, and regr. jt. stk. coys., etc., Assam, Mar., 1935; offg. secty., transf. depts., Assam, in addn., Sept., 1936; dir. of land records and surveys, etc., March, 1939.

CHAUDHURI, NIRMALKANTI RAY, see Ray Chaudhuri, N.

CHAUDHURI, PRABHAT CHANDRA, Indian Agricultural Service (dep. dir. of sericulture, Bengal) (b. 9th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service, 10th Oct., 1913; dep. dir. of sericulture, Oct., 1922.

CHAUDHURI, PRAPHULLA CHANDRA, M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1916; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Apl., 1918, ditto, Bengal, Feb., 1924; asst. auditor, E.B. rly., Dec., 1925; ditto, N.W. rly., June, 1927; dep. auditor (constn. bch), N.W. rly., July, 1927; audit and accts. officer, Bombay dev. scheme, Apl., 1929; accts. officer, G.I.P. rly., Mar., 1930; ditto, E.B. rly., Apl., 1931; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts. office, Apl., 1933; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, E.B. rly., Jan., 1935; dep. ch. accts. offr., E.I. rly., May, 1936; comptr., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1937.

CHAUDHURI, PRASANTA CHANDRA, M.A. (Punjab), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 21st Oct., 1903).—Educ. at University of the Punjab, Sch. of Oriental Studies, London, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1928; arrived, 9th Nov.,

1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1936.

CHAUDHURI, RAMCHANDRA, Pandit, M.A., B.L., late Bihar Civil Service (b. 21st Aug., 1879).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1909; munisf., Aug., 1910; sub-judge, Dec., 1922; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1931; retd., July, 1936.

CHAUDHURI, SUKUMAR, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 14th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

CHAUDHURI, SURATH NATH (see Surath Nath, C.)

CHAUDHURI, TAJAL ISLAM MUHAMMAD NURANNABI (see Tajal I. M. N. Chaudhuri).

CHAUDHURI, TAPENDRA KUMAR GHOSH (see Ghosh Chaudhuri, T. K.).

CHAUNCY, FREDERICK CHARLES LESLIE, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Dec., 1904).—First commn., 27th Aug., 1924; in milty. employ until 12th Feb., 1930; under secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Feb., 1931; under secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf; vice-consul, Mohammedah, Nov., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Zhor, May, 1937; secty. to resdt. for Baroda and the Gujarat States, April, 1939.

CHAVE, ELMER HARGREAVES, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 10th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 14th April, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924.

CHAVES, HENRY JOSEPH, late Indian Posts and Telegraphs (b. 8th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1901; supt. tel. traffic, Feb., 1929; chief sup., Apl., 1937; retd., Oct., 1938.

CHEEMA, SARDAR GANDASING (see Gandasing, C. S.).

CHEESMAN, GEORGE MAURICE, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, accts. dept.) (b. 16th May, 1876).—Apptd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office, 13th Nov., 1914; on mil. service, Sept., 1916, to April, 1918; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India, April, 1921; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; M.B.E., Jan., 1939.

CHEIN, U. AUNG, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt. police, Burma, 21st Apl., 1928; confd., Apl., 1930; offg. dist. supt., Aug., 1934.

CHENEVIX-TRENCH, SIR RICHARD HENRY, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Col. I.A. (ret'd.), (b. 4th April, 1876).—First commn., dated 2nd Jan., 1896; apptd. to for. dept., Nov., 1901; pol. asst., Oct., 1902; first asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Feb., 1910; under sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., Dec., 1912; asst. sec., March, 1913; under sec., Nov., 1913; asst. sec., for. and pol. dept., March, 1915; dep. ditto, sec., Sept., 1915; asst. sec., ditto, April, 1916; first asst. and sec. to chief commr., Baluchistan, Feb., 1917; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi, Oct., 1918; 1st asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Sept., 1919; pol. agent, Zhor, July, 1920; C.I.E., June, 1923; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, April, 1925; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta, Pishin, Oct., 1926; on foreign service in Hyderabad State, Jan., 1927; Kt.,

Jan. 1930; adviser to delegate for Hyderabad. Indian round table conf., Nov., 1930-Jan. 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd Session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; retd., Apl., 1931.

CHENGAPPAH, KODANDERA APPIAH, late Indian Forest Service (b. 10th Feb., 1879).—Joined the service, 31st Mar., 1903; dep. consvr., June, 1922; retd. Feb., 1934.

CHERRY, HERBERT CECIL EDGAR, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suplg. engr., Burma) (b. 16th June, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; suptg. engr., April, 1939.

CHESHIRE, FRANCIS MORETON, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 14th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as profrr., Nagpur Coll., 3rd Feb., 1914; offg. princpl., July, 1926; confd., May, 1930; retd., June, 1933.

CHETPAT, PATTABHERAMA RAMASWAMI AYYAR (see Ramaswami Ayyar, Sir C. P.).

CHETTI, K.C.S. (see Sundaram Chetti, K.C.).

CHETTLE, GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 18th June, 1895) (dist. supt., Burma).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., on 19th Feb., 1921; offg. dist. supt., June, 1926; confd., July, 1927; awarded King's police medal, June, 1932.

CHETTUR, KRISHNA KRISHNA, M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 10th Mar., 1901).—Joined the service 5th Mar., 1925; currency officer, Rangoon, Oct., 1930; ditto, Calcutta, Apl., 1933; under secty. to Govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1937.

CHETTUR MADHAVAN NAYAR (see Nayar, C. Madhavan).

CHETTUR, SANKARA KRISHNA, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) b. 20th May, 1905).—Educ. at University of Madras and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th October, 1929; arrived, November, 1929, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub-collr., Sept., 1931; spec. settlement offcr., May to Sept., 1937.

CHETWODE, SIR PHILIP WALHOUSE, Bt., G.C.B., O.M., G.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., D.S.O., D.C.L., Field-Marshall (b. 21st Sept., 1869).—First commn. dated 20th Nov., 1889; asst. milty. secty. to G.O.C.-in-C., Aldershot, 1906-1908; brig.-comdr., Lond. dist., 1912-14; ditto, N. Comd., May-Aug., 1914; ditto, France, 1914-15; div. comdr., France, 1915-16; comdr., desert col. and sect. canal defences, E.E.F., 1916-17; comdr., eastern force, Egypt, Apl.-Aug., 1917; army corps comdr., E.E.F., 1917-1919; milty. secty., War Office, 1919-1920; adjt.-genl. to forces, W.O., 1922-1923; G.O.C.-in-C., Aldershot Comd., 1923-1927; A.D.C. Gen. to King, July, 1927; ch., genl. staff, India, Oct., 1928; C.-in-C. in India and member govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Nov., 1930 to Nov., 1935; G.C.S.I., June, 1934; O.M., Jan., 1936; War services, Burma, 1892-3; Chin Hills, S.A. War, 1899-1902; France and Belgium, 1914-16; E.E.F., 1916-18. Order of the Nile, 2nd class; Legion of Honour, 3rd class; French War Cross; Order of the Sacred Treasure, 1st class.

CHEYNE, GEORGE COLLIE, M.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., Public Works Dept., Burma (river training expert) (b. 1st Jan., 1887).—Joined the Indian Forest Service in Burma as asst. consvr., 16th Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916;

M.B.E., June, 1924; transferred to public wks. dep. as river training expert, 24th Oct., 1927.

CHHUTTAN LAL, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 11th Feb., 1882).—Joined the service, 29th Sept., 1904; asst. engr., Sept., 1905; exec. engr., Sept., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., July, 1922; confd., Oct., 1926; ch. engr., Apl., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

CHICK, ANDREW WILBERT, Legis. Dept., Govt. of India (asst. secty.) (b. 20th Aug., 1888).—Joined legis. dept., 1908; supt., Mar., 1932; asst. secty. Oct., 1932.

CHIDELL, JOHN WALLACE PHILIP, M.A., India Office (principl. public and judl. dept.) (b. 26th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. princpl., 21st Dec., 1933; resdnt. clk. Oct., 1935; pte. accty. to permnt. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937; acting princpl., Oct., 1938; confd. Feb., 1939.

CHILTON, ALFRED HENRY, A.M.I.E.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (b. 5th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as elec. engr., E.I. Rly., 1st Mar., 1927; ditto, N.W. Rly. and offg. as senr. elec. engr., Feb., 1928.

CHINMULGUND, PANDURANG JAIROA, B.A., Indian C.S., (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 20th Oct., 1913).—Educ. at New English Sch., Poona, Bombay Univ., and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937, confd., 12th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

CHIRRAVOORI BALKRISHNA, RAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 15th July, 1914).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn., 2nd Oct., 1937; confd., 29th Oct., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

CHISHOLM, DONALD MALCOLM FRANCIS, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 17th Sept., 1914; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Mar., 1930.

CHIT, U.BA, B.A., B.L., Burma Judl. Service (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 23rd Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1912; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1935.

CHITALE, P. K. (see Padmakar Krishan Chitale).

CHITHAM, Sir CHARLES CARTER, Kt., C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, Nov., 1906; asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1908; dist. supt., Jan., 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1926; confd., Aug., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1931; confd., June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; knighted, June, 1936; offg. member, Public Service Commn., Apl., 1937, to Sept., 1937.

CHITHAM, JOHN ROWLAND, Indian Police (b. 21st Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 24th Nov., 1935; confd., 1938.

CHITRE, ATMARAM ANANT, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 17th May, 1877).—Apptd. judge, small cause court, Bombay, 1st Apl., 1918; chief judge, ditto, Dec., 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, Apl., 1935; ret'd., May, 1937.

CHOPRA, AMAR NATH, M.B., B.S., D.T.M., D.P.H., Major, I.M.S. (asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S.) (b. 18th May, 1896).—First commn. dated 2nd Mar., 1930; in mil. employ to 7th Oct.,

1935; P.A. to surg. genl. with govt., Bombay, 8th Oct., 1935; asst. dir. genl. I.M.S., 1st Apl., 1938.

CHOPRA, AMAR NATH, B.A., A.M.I.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 15th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 4th Oct., 1915; asst. engr., Oct., 1916; on mil. duty with Indian D.F., 1st Aug. to 18th Oct., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

CHOPRA, GYAN CHAND, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 30th Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 4th Oct., 1904; asst. engr., Feb., 1909; exec. engr., Nov., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1926; ret'd. Feb., 1934.

CHOPRA, PINDI DAS, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., (supt., central jail, Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1888).—First commn. dated 16th July, 1915; supt., central jail, May, 1925; offg. dep. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Punjab, Feb., 1935.

CHOPRA, PRATAP CHANDRA, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. offer., N.W. Rly.) (b. 24th Feb., 1895).—Asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1915; divl. transpn. offer., May, 1935.

CHOPRA, RAM NATH, C.I.E., M.B., Bt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof'r., sch. of tropical medicine, Calcutta) (b. 17th Aug., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1908; in mil. employ up to 12th July, 1921; prof'r., sch. of tropical medicine, Calcutta, Aug., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; Hon. physician to the King, Dec., 1935.

CHOPRA WAZIR CHAND (see Wazir Chand Chopra).

CHOWDHRY, KHEM CHANDRA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 25th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at D.A.V. High Sch., Lahore, Punjab Univ., and London School of Economics; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CHOWDHURY, SATYA PRAKASH, B.A., A.M.I.T. (Lond.), Indian State Rlys. (supt. transportation, E.I. Rly.) (b. 29th Dec., 1892).—Joined service, March, 1913; asst. supt. E.I. Rly, Oct., 1918, junr. scale, April, 1921; transportation (traffic commercial) supt., senr. scale, provl., July, 1938.

CHOUDHURY, SHIAMA SHANKAR, B.Sc. (Cal. and (Glas.)), A.M.I.M.E. (Lond.), Indian State Rlys. (supt. transportation (power), E.I. Rly.) (b. 17th Dec., 1894).—Joined service, July, 1925; asst. works manager, E.I. Rly., Dec., 1927; supt. transportation (power), senr. scale, Jan., 1938.

CHRISTIE, WALTER HENRY JOHN, B.A. (Cantab), Indian C.S. (dep. pte. secty. to Viceroy) (b. 17th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; offg. under secty. to govt. of Bengal, poll. and appts. depts., Aug., 1931; confd., Feb., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1936; under-secty., govt. of India, dept. of communications, Dec., 1937; dep. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Sept., 1939.

CHRISTIE, WILLIAM M.C., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 29th Feb., 1896).—Educ. at St. Andrews Univ. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1920; arrived, 29th Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1924; asst. commr., March, 1925; offg. mag. and collr.,

April, 1926; offg. dep. commr., Delhi, April to Novr., 1928; secty. bd. of rev., U.P., Nov., 1929; offg. addl. dep. secty., govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Oct., 1932; dep. secty. to the Govt. of India, finance dept., Apl., 1933; mag. and collr. (prov. subs.), Apl., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; offg. commr., N.I. salt rev., Apl., 1936, to Oct., 1937; finance secty. United Provs., May, 1938.

CHRISTIE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER KYNOCH, B.Sc., Ph.D., late Geological Survey of India (b. 2nd Oct., 1882).—Apptd. chemist, geological survey of India, 18th Nov., 1906; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), March, 1915, to Aug., 1917; transf. temp. to fin. dept., govt. of India, as dep. assay master, Calcutta mint, April, 1918; retd., Oct., 1932.

CHRISTOPHERS, SIR SAMUEL RICHARD, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., Bt.-Col. I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 27th Nov., 1873).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1902; on spl. duty under dir.-gen., I.M.S., July, 1903, to May, 1904; supt. of the King Inst. of preventive medicine, and prof. of hygiene and bacteriology, Medl. Coll., Madras, May, 1904; on spl. duty under sany. commr. with govt. of India, for enquiry into blackwater fever in the Dooars, Dec., 1907, to Jan., 1909; asst. to dir., Central Research Inst., Kasauli, Jan., 1909; dir., Central Research Inst., Kasauli (s.p.t.), Dec., 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1915; on mily. duty, from Jan., 1916, to Oct., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1918; dir., Kala-azar Commission, Jan., 1923; dir., central research inst., retd. and re-employed from 27th Nov., 1930, Kasauli, June, 1925; K.H.P., Feb., 1927; retd., Nov., 1930; Kt., June, 1931.

CHUDASAMA, MANSINHJI MERUJI, LL.B., Indian Police (asst. supt., Sind) (b. 25th Apl., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 24th Dec., 1929; confd., Apl., 1931.

CHUNDER (see Chandra).

CHUNDER, KAMAL CHUNDER, B.A. (Calc.), B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Assam) (b. 4th Jan., 1895).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1919; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1919, and posted to Bengal; as asst. mag. and collr., services placed temp. under govt. of Assam, Dec., 1924; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Dec., 1924; offg. ditto, Bengal, April, 1927; confd., July, 1931.

CHUNI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., Punjab Civil Service, Judicial Bch. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 22nd June, 1883).—Joined the service as proby. extra asst. commr., 24th Jan., 1906; confd., Sept., 1910; sub-judge, Oct., 1912; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1921; confd., Oct., 1932.

CHUNI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 8th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th May, 1909; dep. supt., Dec., 1913; apptd. to I.P. as supt., April, 1933.

CHUNI LAL, HANDA, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 2nd Feb., 1907).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 21st Jan., 1929; proby. asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1933.

CHURCHER, JOHN EDWARD GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 29th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 18th Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Feb., 1933; confd. Aug., 1938.

CHURCHILL, EDWARD ARCHIBALD HICKLING, B.Sc., Indian Agricul. Service (dep. dir. of agric. Centr. Provs.) (b. 15th Dec., 1889).—Apptd. asst. dir. of agric., 31st Jan., 1922; dep. dir. of agric., Jan., 1930; offg. prin. agric. coll., Nagpur, Nov., 1935.

CINNA SAMUDRAM SESHAGIRI RAO HANUMANTHA RAO, RAO SAHIB, B.A. (inspr. of mun. cls., etc.) (b. 2nd May, 1884).—Apptd. dep. collr., 13th Aug., 1920; asst. secty., bd. of rev., Nov. to Dec., 1932; dep. inspr. of mun. councils and local bds., Sept., 1937, to Mar., 1938; inspr. ditto, Apl., 1938.

CLAGUE, JOHN C.M.G., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to sec. of state for Burma) (b. 29th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and as settl. offr.; offg. dep. dir., land records, Nov., 1916; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; on mil. duty, May, 1918, to Feb., 1919; dir. of agric., Burma, Nov., 1920; dep. commr., July, 1921; addl. sec. to govt., F.R. Dept. (in addn.), Nov., 1923; confd., July, 1924; offg. ch. secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; offg. commr., May, 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; retd., Mar., 1937; adviser to sec. of state for Burma, Apl., 1937; C.M.G., June, 1938.

CLARE, REV. HEBER, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 16th Feb., 1933.

CLARENCE SMITH, JOHN ASHTON, Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 8th March, 1917).—Educ. at Rugby and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

CLARK, ALLAN McDONALD, M.A., Indian C.S. (magt. and collr., Bengal) (b. 16th Oct., 1895).—Educ. at Keith Gr. Sch. and Aberdeen, and Oxford (Non-Coll.) Universities; apptd. after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Prov. of Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and sub-divl. officer, Nov., 1923; addl. mag. and collr., March, 1925; on spl. duty, rev. dept., Nov., 1925, to March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1927; confd., Nov., 1932; dep. sec. pol. and apppt. depts., Bengal, Mar., 1932.

CLARK, ARTHUR STIRLING, M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 3rd Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, and at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. junior clerk, store dept., 19th Feb., 1920; war service—European War, Oct., 1914, to April, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; services lent to Colonial Office, April to Dec., 1921; principal exec. officer, April, 1922; contr. (temp.), April, 1927.

CLARK, FRANK KYNOCH, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian Educ. Service (b. 19th Mar., 1885).—Apptd. headmaster, Patwardhan high school, Nagpur, June, 1917; offg. inspr. of schools, Jubbulpole Circle, Jan., 1927; confd., Nov., 1927; dep. dir., public instrn. and sec. to high school educn. board, Apr., 1931; educ. commr. with govt. of India (temp.), Mar., 1932 to June, 1933; offg. principl., Spencer training coll., Jubbulpole, June, 1933.

CLARK, GEORGE EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 6th Aug., 1912).—

Educated at Sandown (I. of W.) Secondary School and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.
