CLARK, THOMAS JOHN, late Indian Police (b. 26th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 10th Oct., 1921; offg. supt., July, 1927; confd., Mar., 1928; ret'd., June, 1938.

CLARK, THOMAS VINSON, Indian Police (b. 6th May, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, Nov., 1932.

CLARK, SIR WILLIAM HENRY, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I.—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; clerk, Board of Trade, 1899; sec. to special mission to Shanghai to negotiate a commercial treaty with China, 1901; acting second sec. in diplomatic service, 1902; sec. to royal commn. on supply of food, &c., in time of war, 1903-05; C.M.G., 1903; private sec. to presdt. of board of trade, 1906-08; ditto to chancellor of exchequer, 1908-10; member of council of gov'r.-gen. of India, commerce and industry dept., Nov., 1910, to April, 1916; C.S.I., Dec. 1911; K.C.S.I., June, 1915; compt'r.-gen., commercial intelligence, Board of Trade, 1916-17; compt'r.-gen., dept. of overseas trade, Sept., 1917; high commr. in Canada for H.M. Govt. since 1928; K.C.M.G., June, 1930; G.C.M.G., May, 1937.

CLARK, WILLIAM MORRISON, B.Sc., M.B.E., Indian Agricl. Service (b. 23rd May, 1895).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, on 3rd April, 1923; M.B.E., June, 1918; services transferred to Govt. of Bengal from Feb., 1938.

CLARKE, Rev. ARTHUR, Indian Eccles. Establishment (United Provinces) (junr. chaplain) (b. 13th March, 1901).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 9th Jan., 1937; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1939.

CLARKE, CHARLES JAMES, D.S.O., Col., R.E. (ret'd.), late Indian State Railways (b. 3rd June, 1875).—First commn., dated 27th Feb., 1894; joined the Public Works Dept. as asst. engr., state rlys., 1st June, 1896; exec. engr., June, 1907; on mil. duty from Oct., 1914, to May, 1919; suptg. engr., Dec., 1921; chief engr., Sept., 1924; ret'd., June, 1930.

CLARKE, E. W., Income Tax Dept. (commr., inc. tax, Madras) (b. 12th June, 1888).—Apptd. to income tax dept., central board of revenue, 7th March, 1919; asst. commr., Western range, Madras, March, 1924; offg. commr. variously from Dec., 1928; confd., Mar., 1937.

CLARKE, GEOFFREY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Oct., 1882).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1912, temp. suptg. engr., Nov., 1923; perm. ditto, Feb., 1927; offg. ch. engr., Burma, Sept., 1931; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

CLARKE, SIR GEOFFREY ROTHE, Kt., C.S.I., O.B.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Dublin Univ., apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 5th Dec., 1895, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; offd. as postmr.-genl., Madras, from Jan., 1904; confd., April, 1906; dep. dir.-gen., post office, April, 1907; post-master-gen., United Prov., Nov., 1909; dep. dir.-gen., post office, March, 1912; postmaster-

gen., Punjab, June, 1915; ditto, Bengal and Assam, April, 1917; O.B.E., Jan., 1918; offg. dir.-gen., posts and telegraphs, March, 1918; confd., Sept., 1918; C.S.I., Jan., 1921; on depn. as Indian delegate to Internl. Postal Congress, Stockholm, May, 1924; Kt., Jan., 1925; ret'd., Jan., 1926.

CLARKE, GEORGE, C.I.E., F.I.O., F.O.S., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 23rd Oct., 1878).—Apptd. agricl. chemist to govt., United Provs., Feb., 1907; offg. dir. of agric., United Provs., Oct., 1923; confd., Oct., 1926; C.I.E., July, 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

CLARKE, JOHN ARTHUR, Indian Forest Service (b. 4th Dec., 1897) (dep. consvr. Burma).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 27th Dec. 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

CLARKE, MATTHEW JOHN, Major, C.I.E., I.A. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 19th July, 1895).—First commn. dated 1st Dec., 1914; asst. commr., July, 1921; sec. to finl. commr., 11th Nov., 1926; offg. dep. commr., May, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1932; chairman, bd. of trustees for development of City of Rangoon, Aug., 1934; dep. commr., Nov., 1934.

CLARKE, Rev. MAURICE, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 21st July, 1893) (junr. chaplain, Madras).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, Madras, 15th Oct., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1929.

CLARKE, MAURICE NOBLE, Indian Civil Service (dist. and sess. judge, Central Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Uppingham School and Trinity Coll., Dublin; on mil. service from Jan., 1915; joined the service, 13th Feb., 1920, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 9th May, 1920; sub-judge, Dec., 1924; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1926; confd., May, 1929; actg. judge, high ct., Nagpur, June-Oct., 1938.

CLARKE, SIR REGINALD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Police Service.—Joined the service, Jan., 1900, and served as proby. asst. supt.; confd., Dec., 1905; supt., April, 1906; offg. persnl. asst. to inspr.-genl., March, 1911; confd., Jan., 1912; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, and Burma mily. police, April, 1912; offg. commr. of police, Calcutta, March, 1915; confd., May, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1919; knighted, June, 1922; ret'd., June, 1925.

CLARKE, Rev. RICHARD CHARLES BOLINGBROKE, M.A., late Eccles. dept., Central Provs. (b. 13th July, 1879).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 2nd March, 1912; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1912; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1922; ret'd., July, 1932.

CLARKE, WILLIAM GEORGE, M.B.E., late Indian Police Service.—Joined the service, 7th June, 1898; M.B.E., Jan., 1921; supt., Aug., 1923; awarded King's Police Medal, Jan., 1924; ret'd.

CLARKSON, WILLIAM, India Office (hr. clerical officer, econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 10th May, 1899).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1920 after previous service from Apl., 1917; hr. clerical officer, Sep., 1936.

CLAUSON, MILES JOHN, B.A., India Office (pte. secty. to secty. of state for India and for Burma) (b. 29th Mar., 1902).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; secty.'s office, G.P.O., Oct., 1925; to Dec., 1926; asst. prinpl., India Office, Jan.,

1927; resdt. clerk, 1st Oct., 1927; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state, Dec., 1928; actg. princpl., Jan., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; pte. secty. to secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937.

CLAY, Sir JOSEPH MILES, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to secty. of state for India) (b. 6th Sept., 1881).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1905, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., July, 1911; dep. commr., Garhwal, Apl., 1913; asst. mag. and collr., and forest settlt. offr. (in addn.), March, 1916; O.B.E., June, 1918; mag. and collr., Nov., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. commr., March, 1925; offg. sec. to govt., local self-govt. dept., April, 1927, and again, Oct., 1929; dep. commr., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Nov., 1931; confd., June, 1932; ty. member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Sept.-Dec., 1932; commr., Apl., 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Apl., 1935; K.C.I.E., June, 1936; retd. Aug., 1937; adviser to sec. of state for India, Aug., 1937.

CLAYDON, Rev. EVAN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 15th May, 1898) (junr. chaplain, Punjab).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 27th Mar., 1928; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1929.

CLAYTON, ALFRED HARE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 22nd Nov., 1873).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1904; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Dec., 1905; mag. and collr., Jan., 1908; re-transfd. to Bengal, April, 1912; chairman, Chittagong Port Trust, July, 1915; commr., Oct., 1924; retd., Dec., 1931.

CLAYTON, Sir HUGH BYARD, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Dec., 1877).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 16th Dec., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and railway mag.; asst. pol. agent, Nov., 1909; attached to mil. intelligence directorate, War Office, Sept., 1914, to Dec., 1918; offg. munpl. commr., city of Bombay, Dec., 1919; collr., Nov., 1920; munpl. commr., city of Bombay, Oct., 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; on spl. duty with govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., Nov., 1930, to Mar., 1931; commr., Jan., 1931; retd., Dec., 1936; chairman, public services commn. for provs. of Bombay and Sind, Apl., 1937; knighted, June, 1938.

CLAYTON, RICHARD HENRY MICHAEL, B.A., I.C.S. (asst. judge and asst. sessions judge, Bombay) (b. 11th Aug., 1907).—Educ. at Lancing College, Christ Church, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Jan., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge and asst. sessions judge, April, 1939.

CLAYTON, ROBERT VICKERS, M.B., B.S., D.P.H., Indian State Rlys. (P.M.O. G.I.P. rly.) (b. 18th Dec., 1887).—Apptd. dist. medl. officer, N.W. rly. (temp.), 26th Jan., 1928; ditto (permt.), B.B. & C.I. rly., May, 1929; offg. dep. C.M.O., N.W. rly., Nov., 1929; offg. C.M.O., N.W. rly., Dec., 1929; P.M.O. G.I.P. rly., July, 1932.

CLEAR, THOMAS, M.A., Indian Forest Service (consr., Madras) (b. 17th June, 1886).—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr., dist. forest officer, Sept., 1910; dep. consvr., Jan., 1914; on mil. service from Nov., 1917, to July, 1919; actg. consvr., Mar., 1924; confd., June, 1926.

CLEAR, THOMAS, late Indian Police Service (Bengal) (b. 25th Aug., 1876).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 25th March, 1896, to 14th July, 1909; asst. supt., July, 1909; supt., July, 1917; retd., Aug., 1931.

CLEARY, DENIS MACKROW, M.A., India Office (principal mil. dept.) (b. 20th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at St. Ignatius Coll., Stamford Hill, and St. Olave's and St. Saviour's Grammar Sch.; St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. principal, 19th Oct., 1931; resdt. clerk, Sept., 1934; pte. secty. to permt. under-secty. of state, Aug., 1936, to Oct., 1937; actg. principal, Oct., 1937; confd. Jan., 1939.

CLEARY, MICHAEL FRANCIS, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 4th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., on 4th Dec., 1914; confd., Nov., 1916; addl. supt., June, 1922; pers. assistant to dir. intell. bureau, govt. of India, Nov., 1923; supt., Dec., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; cent. intell. offer, Bengal and Assam, Nov., 1935; on depn. to Imp. Defence Coll., Jan., 1938; dep. dir. intell. bureau, Mar., 1939.

CLEBURNE, JEFFREY ST. HILL, late Indian Police (b. 16th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 4th Dec., 1911; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; retd., May, 1933.

CLEE, CHARLES BEAUPRE BELL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Secty. fin. dept., Govt. of Sind) (b. 5th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Fitzwilliam Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1919; arrived, 28th Nov., 1919, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; actg. dir. of information, March, 1924; sec., tariff bd., 1st June, 1925; offg. dep. secty. to govt., finance dept., Bombay, Apl., 1928; confd., Dec., 1928; offg. secty. to govt. of Bombay, home and eccl. depts., May, 1932; member, legisl. council, Oct., 1932; offg. secty. to govt. of Bombay, fin. dept., June, 1933, again Jan. 1936; collr. and mag., June, 1935; secty., fin. dept., Sind, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938.

CLEGG, EDWARD LESLIE GILBERT, B.Sc., Geological Survey of India (suptg. geologist) (b. 24th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1920; offg. supt., Apl., 1927; confd., June, 1932.

CLEMENTS, ERNEST, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and forest settlt. officer; 2nd asst., Aug., 1900; asst. agent for sardars in Deccan, Oct., 1902, to Sept., 1903; 1st asst., Aug., 1905; judge and sess. judge, Feb., 1907; retd., Nov., 1928; author of an "Introduction to the Study of Indian Music," and "The Ragas of Tanjore"; joint author of "The Ragas of Hindustan."

CLEMENTS, JOHN EDMUND, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Jail Dept., United Provs. (b. 25th Feb., 1875).—1st commn., 29th Jan., 1902; in mil. employ till April, 1904, when he was apptd. jail supt., Lucknow; on mil. duty, Oct. 1914, to May, 1919; supt., central prison,

Lucknow, Oct., 1916; offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons, United Provs., March, 1923; confd., July, 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

CLERICI, CHARLES JOHN EMILE, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Posts and Telegraphs Dept. (b. 22nd Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1900; asst. dir.-gen., posts and tels., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. dir.-gen., posts and tels., Calcutta; C.I.E., Aug., 1916; O.B.E., June, 1918; asst. dir.-gen., Calcutta, March, 1926; offg. postmaster-genl., Central Circle, July, 1927; confd., Sept., 1928; P.M.G., U.P., Oct., 1928; ditto, Bengal and Assam, Aprl., 1930; offg. senr. dep. dir. genl., Mar., 1931; ret'd., May, 1932.

CLERICI, MARIO CLAUDE, Indian Police (b. 8th Nov., 1914).—Asst. supt., Bihar, apptd. on probn., 11th Nov., 1935; confd. Sept., 1937.

CLERK, FREDERIC VIVIAN, late Burma Frontier Service (b. 20th April, 1879).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr., 1st Aprl., 1906; dep. commr., Dec., 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

CLIFF, ARNOLD PEARSE, B.A., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Bihar) (b. 3rd Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 5th Oct., 1920; secty., Indian Central Jute Ctee., Dec., 1936.

CLIFFORD, REGINALD CHARLES, D.S.O., M.C., F.R.C.S.E., M.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., United Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 17th Sept., 1884).—First commn., 30th July, 1917; civil surgeon, United Provs., July, 1930; ditto, Simla W., and New Delhi, Oct., 1935.

CLIFFORD, WILLIAM WALTER, Indian Police (Supt., Bombay) (b. 26th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1908; dep. supt., Aug., 1912; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Bombay, Nov., 1933.

CLIFFORD-HURST, LESLIE ERNEST, Indian State Rlys. (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 6th Mar., 1881).—Apptd. asst. supt., watch and ward, E.I. rly., 1st Sept., 1925; offg. dep. supt., Aprl., 1929; dist. supt., Nov., 1933.

CLIFT, FRANK CHARLES LECHMERE, A.C.H., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th Jan., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr., exec. engr., Oct., 1916; on mil. duty, Nov., 1917, to Mar., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., Aprl., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

CLIFT, GEORGE, B.Sc., Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 9th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 19th Aug., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Aprl., 1933; confd. Oct., 1938.

CLIFT, LAWRENCE, late Indian Police Service (b. 17th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1910; asst. supt., Nov., 1911; supt. (provl.), Jan., 1926; ret'd., May, 1930.

CLINTON-THOMAS, ROBERT ANTONY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 5th Aprl., 1913).—Educ. at Haileybury and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CLIVE, JOHN M'CLINTOCK, Major M. C., Mil. accts. dept. (b. 20th Oct., 1981).—First Commn., Aprl., 1915; joined service as an offcr., cl. II, Aug., 1930.

CLOSE, ERNEST REGINALD WARD, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 4th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1921; asst. supt., July, 1923; offg. dist. supt., May, 1928; confd., July, 1930.

CLOUGH, ARTHUR ROBERT, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1924.

CLOUGH, JOHN WILLIAM late India Office (b. 8th Jan., 1873).—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th July, 1892; staff clerk, Nov., 1914; on deputn. to India in connection with the accounts of British troops, April, 1918, to April, 1919; junior clerk, Oct., 1919; senior clerk, April, 1921; dep. chief acctnt. (acting), April, 1922; appt. confirmed from 1st Nov., 1922, designation changed to asst. acctnt.-gen., Jan., 1926; dep. acct.-gen., and clerk to Commrs. of Income Tax, March, 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

CLOW, SIR ANDREW GOURLAY, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., F.S.S., Indian C.S. (member, gov.-genl.'s exec. council) (b. 29th April, 1890).—Educ. at Merchiston Castle School and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd., after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1914, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. record offr. and asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1917; dep. commr. (provl.) and settlt. offr., Sept., 1918, to June, 1919; sec. to Disorders Enquiry Ctte., Feb., 1920; controller, labour bureau, May, 1920; under-sec. to Govt. of India, dept. of industries, April, 1923; dep. sec., ditto, Aug., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; sec. ditto (temp.), April, 1928; adviser and substitute delegate, internl. labour conference, Geneva, May-June, 1929; member of R.C. on labour in India, July, 1929; on spl. duty in dept. of indus. and labour, Oct., 1931; jt. secty., ditto, Nov., 1931; offg. secty., ditto, June, 1935; and again, Jan., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1935; confd. as secty. to govt. of India, indus. and lab. dept., July, 1936; member, council of state, Feb., 1936; secty. dept. of lab., Nov., 1937; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, May-Sept., 1938; confd. April, 1939; Kt., June, 1939.

CLUTTERBUCK, JACK ERNEST, Major R.E., Indian State Rlys. (ex engr. G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 8th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1918; asst. exec. engr. Khyber Rly. Survey and Construction, Aug., 1919; asst. exec. engr. E.I. Rly., April, 1921; provl. perm. exec. engr., G.I.P. Rly., May, 1939.

CLUTTERBUCK, SIR PETER HENRY, Kt., C.I.E., C.B.E., F.Z.S., F.R.G.S., V.D., late Indian Forest Service.—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1889, as asst. consr., Central Prov., transf. to N.W.P. and Oudh, Jan., 1896; dep. consr., July, 1896; re-transf. to Central Prov., July, 1905; K.-i-H. medal, 2nd class, Dec., 1911; consr., Nov., 1913; offg. chief consr., Central Prov., March to Oct., 1914; chief consr., United Prov., Oct., 1915; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; offg. inspr.-gen. of forests, govt. of India, May, 1919; confd., Oct., 1921; C.B.E., Dec., 1919; Kt., June, 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1926; timber adviser to High Commr. for India, Jan., 1927 to May, 1929.

CLYDE, DAVID, M.B., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., United Provs.) (b. 30th Mar., 1894).—First commn., dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ to 12th Feb., 1924; asst. dir. of public health, Feb., 1924; civil surg., Nov., 1929.

COATES, CHARLES LESLIE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar School and Wadham College, Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. regr. co-op. societies, Mar-May, 1937.

COATES, ERIC THOMAS, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (fin. adviser, milty. fin.) (b. 1st Oct., 1897).—Educ. at Heath Gr. Sch., Halifax, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1921, and posted to Bengal as asst. magte. and collr., and sub. divl. officer, April, 1923; on spl. duty in finl., commerce and marine depts., Nov., 1925, to March, 1926; offg. under-sec. in ditto, March, 1926; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Nov., 1928; ditto, central revenues, Apl., 1929; offg. acct. genl., Bombay, July, 1930; accts. officer, N.W.F.P., Oct., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, Nov., 1931; offg. dep. contr. of currency, Calcutta, Feb., 1932; offg. acct.-gen., Central Provs., Oct., 1933; ditto, Bombay, Apl., 1934; ditto, posts and tels., May, 1934; on spl. duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1935; jt. secty., fin. dept., Govt. of India, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1938; fin. adviser, milty. fin., April, 1939.

COATES, WILLIAM JOSEPH, B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 21st Aug., 1885).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd cl., Jan., 1910; dep. mil. acct. genl. (snr.), Apl., 1930; dep. fin. advr., mil. fin., Mar., 1931; confd., as Cl. I officer., Oct., 1935.

COATMAN, JOHN, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Police service (b. 5th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service, 21st Jan., 1911; asst. supt., May, 1912; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1916; supt., Sept., 1922; dir. of public information, Feb., 1926; C.I.E., June, 1929; secty. to Liberal delegation, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930 to Jan., 1931; 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931.

COBB, EVELYN HEY, O.B.E., Major, I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 15th Sept., 1899).—First commn., dated 15th Apl., 1919; in milty. employ prior to 15th June, 1921; pers. asst. to ch. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1924; asst. collr., Agra, Dec., 1925; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Nov., 1927; supy. asst. commr., Peshawar, June, 1929; city mag., ditto, May, 1930; asst. commr., Charsadda, Dec., 1931; ditto, Mardan, 1933; O.B.E., June, 1935; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1935; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Mar., 1937; serving in N.W.F.P., Nov., 1939.

COBBALD, ERNEST BARHAM, late Indian C.S. (b. 5th Aug., 1891).—Educ. at Ipswich Grammar School and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1922; offg. collr., May, 1925; ditto and dist. mgte., Nov., 1925; retd., Oct., 1934.

COBURN, MARMADUKE ROBERT, C.I.E., O.B.E., India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 9th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service as milty. acct., 11th March, 1910; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; dep. finl. adviser, May, 1920; offr. finl. adviser, July, 1925; offg. dir. of establishment, Nov., 1916; finl. advr., April, 1928; reverted to milty. accts. dept., Apl., 1933; dep. fin. advr. mil. fin., Oct., 1935; mil. acct. genl., Apl., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1939.

COCHRANE, Hon. Sir ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., D.S.O., Commander, R.N., retd. (govr. of Burma) (b. 8th Jan., 1885).—European War, 1914-1918 (desp. thrice, D.S.O. and bar); M.P. for East Fife, 1924-1929; M.P., Dumbartonshire, 1932-1936; K.C.S.I., Mar., 1936; govr. of Burma, May, 1936; G.C.M.G., Apl., 1937.

COCHRANE, GEORGE ALFRED DAVIES, B.Sc., A.M.I.Struct.E., Indian service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Central Provs., 26th Sept., 1924; exec. engr. and under-secy. to govt. p.w.d., Sept., 1933.

COCK, CHARLES MATTHEW, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. (traction) engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 27th Jan., 1895).—Apptd. traction engr., distribn., G.I.P. rly., 21st Oct., 1929.

COCKBURN, ARTHUR BALCOMBE, Indian Finance Dept. (b. 23rd Aug., 1885).—Joined the service, 6th Apl., 1905; asst. acct.-gen., May, 1914; examr. of local fund accts. Punjab, June, 1918; dep. acct.-gen., posts and tels., March, 1925; dep. acct.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1926; dep. dir. of audit, United Provs., Jan., 1927; offg. chief pay and accts. officer, April, 1927; dep. ch. accounting officer, United Provs., April, 1928, and again, Mar., 1930; ch. pay and accts. officer, U.P., Mar., 1929; dep. acct.-genl., U.P., Nov., 1931; audit officer, Lloyd barrage and canals constn., Mar., 1932; offg. comptr., N.W.F.P., Mar., 1933; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1935.

COCKBURN, GEORGE WILLIAM MACLOV, M.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I. (asst. commr.)) (b. 27th May, 1913).—Educ. at Fettes and St. Andrew's Univ.; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

COCKBURN, EDWIN DUNDAS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Feb., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E., Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1906; temp. suptg. engr., July, 1916; confd., Oct., 1918; retd., Feb., 1930.

COELHO, MANUEL ANTHONY TIMOTHY, B.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 24th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service, Nov., 1936; apptd. dist. and sess. judge, on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937.

COEN, TERENCE BERNARD CREAGH, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 31st March, 1903).—Educ. at Uppingham and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-

secty., local self-govt. dept., Dec., 1932; reg., high ct., Lahore, Nov., 1933; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Oct., 1935; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar Agency, Mar., 1936; secty. to the Resident at Hyderabad, Oct., 1937; confd. in the pol. service, Feb., 1938.

COFFEY, THOMAS MALO, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 4th May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., Mar., 1922.

COGHILL, DUNCAN JOSEPH KNIGHT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Provs.) (b. 25th April, 1903).—Educ. at Cheltenham and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs., as asst. commr.; jt. mag., June, 1929; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Nov., 1930; asst. pol. agent, Zohb, Nov., 1932; offg. under-secty. to govt. o. India, for. and pol. dept., Mar., 1934; offg. dep. secty., ditto, May, 1934, to Apl., 1935; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1935.

COGHLAN, JOHN ALOYSIUS, B.A., M.C., Indian Police Service (supt., Bombay) (b. 16th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Nov., 1927.

COHEN, WILLIAM CHARLES HENRY, India Office (higher clerical officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 28th Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital; apptd. second class clerk, 21st Jan., 1909, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

COHU, Rev. CLIFFORD JOHN, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 30th Dec., 1883).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 26th Feb., 1912; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1915; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1925; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

COLDMAN, Rev. ALFRED THEODORE, B.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 3rd July, 1884).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Madras, 5th Oct., 1926; senr. chaplain, July, 1933; ret'd. July, 1939.

COLDSTREAM, Sir JOHN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Dec., 1877).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 19th Nov., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1906, to Feb., 1910; asst. commr. and settlt. offr., April, 1910; temply. employed under War Office from Oct., 1914, to July, 1916; dep. commr., Oct., 1917; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1918; legal remembrancer and sec. to govt. of Punjab, legisl. dept., Oct., 1923; actg. judge, high ct., Lahore, May to July, 1925; ditto, April to July, 1926; addl. judge, ditto, Oct., 1926; judge, ditto, Sept., 1928; ret'd., Dec., 1937; Kt., Jan., 1938.

COLE, FRANCIS NORMAN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 29th July, 1906).—Educ. at Dulwich College, Trinity College, Oxford, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; pte. secty. to govr., Central Provs., Apl., 1934.

COLE, FREDERICK EUSTACE, V.D., late Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.) (b. 29th July, 1875).—Trained at T.E.C. Coll.; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1898; asst. engr., Oct., 1899; senr. asst. signal engr., June, 1909; signal engr., March, 1912; on mil. duty, Aug., 1917, to March, 1919; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct.,

1925; offg. technical officer, rly. bd., Nov., 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

COLE, GEORGE ASHMOND, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 1st Feb., 1902).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; asst. commr., Kohat, 8 Apl., 1931; asst. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Bahrain, Mar., 1935; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1936; pol. agent in Chagai, Oct., 1937; asst. pol. agent in Zohb, Dec., 1939.

COLE, JOHN SAMUEL, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 21st July, 1872).—Educ. at City of Westminster Sch., and City and Guilds' Technical Coll.; apptd. clerk for railway stores, 21st May, 1907; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; ret'd., July, 1932.

COLE, LESLIE PRICHARD, M.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 4th Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Madras, 20th Oct., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1938.

COLEBROOK, EDWARD HILDER, M.C., Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 16th Oct., 1898).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1921, as asst. supt. on probn.; confd. Apl., 1925; offg. spl. supt., Malabar spl. police, Mar., 1928; offg. dist. supt., Aug., 1929; confd., Nov., 1930; awarded King's police medal; Indian police medal, Jan., 1936.

COLERIDGE, Sir JOHN FRANCIS STANHOPE DUKE, K.C.B., C.M.G., D.S.O., A.D.C., Lieut.-General, I.A. (b. 25th April, 1878).—First commn., 20th July, 1898; 8th Gurkha Rifles, 1900; staff captain, France, Nov., 1914-Feb., 1915; G.S.O.3, Home forces and Med. E.F., Feb., 1915-Sept., 1915; G.S.O.2, Med. E.F., Sept., 1915-Apl., 1915; G.S.O.1, E.E.F., and France, Apl., 1916-Oct., 1917; Brig. Commdr., France, Oct., 1917-Nov., 1918; D.D. of Staff Duties, India, Nov., 1919-Oct., 1923; asst. comdt., staff coll., Quetta, Oct., 1923-Nov., 1925; milty. secty., A.H.Q., India, Feb., 1926-Jan., 1930; dist. comdr., India, Jan., 1930-Oct., 1933; K.C.B., June, 1933; milty. secty., India Office, Nov., 1933 to May, 1936; A.D.C. Gen. to the King, June, 1936; G.O.C. in C., Nthn. Cmd., June, 1936; colonel, 2/1st Punjab Regt., and 8th Gurkha Rifles; war services, Tibet, 1903-1904-Niani, Gyantsi, march to Lhasa, medal and clasp; Abor, 1911-1912, despatches; European War, 1914-1918, brevets of Lieut.-Col. and Colonel, D.S.O., with clasp, C.M.G., C.B., eight times mentioned in despatches; N.W. Frontier of India, 1930-1931, despatches; Clitral reliefs, 1932, despatches.

COLLET, ARTHUR LOWE, Judl. Dept., Bengal (regr. etc., high ct., Calcutta) (b. 9th Mar., 1888).—Apptd. offg. regr. in insolvency, high ct., Calcutta, 23rd Nov., 1931; confd. (provl.), May, 1932; offg. regr., original side, Sept., 1932.

COLLETT, ARTHUR PRESTON, late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Sept., 1880).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1911; jt. mag., May, 1914; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., Sept., 1915; on mily. service from May, 1916, to Jan., 1919; mag. and collr., Nov., 1920; offg. opium agent and commr. of inc. tax, U.P., Mar., 1928; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

COLLETT, HENRY ASHWOOD, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Jan., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 10th Aug., 1896, to 15th April, 1914; offg. asst. supt., April, 1914; confd., Oct., 1914; offg. dist. supt., May, 1921; dist. supt. (s.p.t.), July, 1923; supt., Feb., 1925; statistical officer, Nov., 1929, and again Apl., 1931; offg. dep. ch. optg. supt., Jan., 1931; Mar., 1932, and again, Oct., 1932; retd., Aug., 1936.

COLLIER, REV. CHARLES BERNARD GREY, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 17th July, 1877).—Joined, Feb., 1908; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1911; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1921; retd., Aug., 1933.

COLLIER, SIR GEORGE HERMAN, Kt., C.I.E., late Office of High Commr. for India.—Educ. at Uppingham Sch.; junr. clerk, India Office, 17th Feb., 1876; senr. clerk, Aug., 1896; dep. dir.-gen. of stores, July, 1912; dir.-gen. of stores, Nov., 1915; C.I.E., June, 1918; on deputation to India to attend Stores Cttee, Nov., 1919; knighted, Jan., 1921; retd., Aug., 1921.

COLLINGS, FRANCIS WILLIAM, late Indian Forest Service (b. 14th March, 1881).—Arrived, 28th Nov., 1903, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1917; on mil. service, May, 1918, to June, 1919; consvr., Sept., 1924; retd., Jan., 1932.

COLLINS, BERNARD ABDY, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Feb., 1880).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 14th Nov., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. offr.; priv. sec. to lieut.-govr. of Bengal, March to July, 1908; under-sec. to govt., rev., pol. and appt. depts., July, 1909, to March, 1910; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1911; transfid. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; under-sec. to govt., pol., appt. and educ. depts., April, 1912; regr., co-op. credit soc., May, 1913; temply. employed in rev. and stat. dept., India Office, from April, 1916, to May, 1917; mag. and collr., April, 1917; regr., co-operative credit societies, Sept., 1917; on spl. duty under Indian munitions board, Delhi, April, 1918; controller (industrial intell.), Simla, July, 1918, to Feb., 1919; offg. sec. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, finl. and munpl. depts., April, 1919; dir. of industries, April, 1920; also sec. to govt., educn. dept., in addition, from Dec., 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; on deputation as dir. of com. and industry, Hyderabad State, March, 1927; retd., Oct., 1933.

COLLINS, FREDERICK MICHAEL, M.A., M.B.M.Chir. (Camb.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Major I.M.S. (D.M.O., Madras) (b. 5th Sept., 1900).—First commn., 24th Jan., 1933; in mil. employ until 29th Oct., 1934; dist. med. offcr., Oct., 1934; principal, medical college, etc., Vizagapatam, April, 1937; surgeon, general hospital, Madras, June, 1939.

COLLINS, GODFREY FERDINANDO STRATFORD, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (secty., rev. dept., Sind) (b. 3rd Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on mily. duty from July, 1916, to Jan., 1919; O.B.E., Jan.,

1919; extra dep. dir. of civil supplies, Bombay, 1919; forest settlement officer (temp.), Sept., 1920; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1923; settlement officer, Oct., 1924; addl. dy. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte., April, 1926; confd., June, 1930; actg. regr., co-op. societies, June, 1926; secty. to govt. of Bombay, home and eccl. depts., Aug., 1929; member legisl. council, Bombay, 1929 and 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. pte. secty. to govr. of Bombay, Aug., 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. commr., Sind, Apl., 1935; secty. to govt. rev. dept.; member, Sind rev. trib., Nov., 1937.

COLLINS, JOHN LAVILLE, late Indian Police (b. 10th March, 1881).—Joined the service, 26th Aug., 1901; on mil. duty from 28th Sept., 1918, to 7th Feb., 1920; asst. supt., July, 1922; offg. supt., Nov., 1927; confd., June, 1931; retd., Mar., 1936.

COLLINS, TERENCE MOSTYN, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1913; on mil. duty from 10th Nov., 1917, to 21st Oct., 1919; dist. supt., Jan., 1925; prinpl. training school, Saugor, March, 1925; central intel. offcr., Central Provs., Nov., 1936.

COLLIS, MAURICE STEWART, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Rugby, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1912, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty, Dec., 1917, to May, 1919; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Jan. to Feb., 1920; offg. dep. sec. to govt., H. & P. dept., April, 1925; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1928; confd., Dec., 1930; retd., Jan., 1936.

COLLISTER, HAROLD JAMES, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. 14th Jan., 1885).—Educ. at South-Eastern Coll., Ramsgate, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; addl. sess. and sub-judge, Sept., 1917; jt. mag., Feb., 1918; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), July, 1918, to March, 1919; dist. and sessions judge, March, 1922; addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1933, and July, 1934; judge, ditto, July, 1935.

COLLISTER, JOHN ARTHUR, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th May, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. dist. loco. supt., 8th Dec., 1905; dist. ditto, July, 1919; supt., Feb., 1926; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., May, 1929, and again, Nov., 1932; retd., Jan., 1934.

COLQUHOUN, SAMUEL, Survey of India (manager, litho. bch.) (b. 23rd Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 18th Aug., 1905; manager, litho. bch., Nov., 1930.

COLSON, LIONEL HEWITT, C.I.E., Indian Police (commr. of police, Calcutta) (b. 24th May, 1887).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1906; asst. supt., April, 1908; supt., Aug., 1913; awarded King's police medal, 1916; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1928; offg. commr. of police, Calcutta, June, 1929; confd., Dec., 1931; dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1934.

COLTHUP, SIDNEY ROBERT, India Office (hr. executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 11th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Rochester Math. Sch.; served as sec. div. clerk, Local Govt. Bd., from Feb. to Sept., 1912; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 23rd Sept., 1912, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from Aug., 1915, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; hr. exec. officer, Sept., 1932; assessor of income tax, from Oct., 1935, to Dec., 1937.

COLVILLE, WILLIAM, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 12th April, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 8th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Mar., 1928; and again, Nov., 1933; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, Home dept., Apl., 1932; pers. asst. to dir., intelligence bureau, Mar., 1933; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Apl., 1936, to Apl., 1937; supt., Oct., 1937.

COLVIN, Sir CLEMENT PRESTON, Kt., O.B.E., V.D., late Indian State Ryis. (b. 12th July, 1879).—Joined the service, 21st March, 1900; asst. traffic supt., April, 1903; dist. ditto, Jan., 1905; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; dep. traffic manager, May, 1921; offg. dir. of traffic, rly. bd., Nov., 1928; confd., Apl., 1931; offg. member, rly. bd., Apl., 1931, May, 1932; and again, Nov., 1932; confd., Jan., 1933; knighted, June, 1935; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

COLVIN, Sir ELLIOT GRAHAM, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Charterhouse, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 4th Jan., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as personal asst. to chief commr., May, 1884; asst. pol. agent, Rajputana, Jan., 1885; private sec. to lieut.-govr. of Bengal, April, 1887; 1st asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan, April, 1889; offg. postmr.-gen., Bengal, July, 1890; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1891; mag. and collr., March, 1895; pol. agent, Ulwar, Nov., 1896; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, Sept., 1897; rev. and jud. commr., Baluchistan, April, 1900; resident in Kashmir, March, 1903; agent to govr.-gen'l., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, from Oct., 1905; C.S.I., June, 1906; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1911; transf. to Bihar and Orissa cadre, April, 1912; ret'd., Jan., 1918.

COLVIN, ELLIOT JAMES DOWELL, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th July, 1885).—First commn., 9th Jan., 1904; in mil. employ to 11th July, 1908, when he was apptd. personal asst. to resdt., Hyderabad; pol. officer on probation, 12th Feb., 1912; appt. to pol. dept. confirmed, July, 1914; agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, June, 1915; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, March, 1921; under-sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, April, 1922; on foreign service as adviser to H.H. the Maharaja of Rewa, Feb., 1924; offg. resdt. Gwalior, Apl., 1930; offg. pol. agent, E.R. States, April, 1930; confd., Sept., 1930; prime minister, Kashmir state, Mar., 1932, and again, Mar., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1933; resdt. for Baroda and Gujarat States, Jan., 1938.

COLVIN, Sir GEORGE LETHBRIDGE, Kt., C.B., C.M.G., D.S.O., V.D., late Indian State Ryis. (b. 1st April, 1878).—Agent, E.I. Rly., 30th Sept., 1921 to Mar., 1933; C.M.G., 1918; C.B., 1919; Kt., Jan., 1933.

COLYER, JOHN ALFRED, Indian State Ryis. (divl. mech. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1884).—Asst. works manager, junr. scale, 28th, July, 1927; ditto, senr. scale (provl.), July, 1937.

COMMISSARIAT, MANEKSHAH SORABSHAH, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Dec., 1881).—Apptd. profir. of history and pol. economy, Gujarat Coll., Ahmedabad, Feb., 1921; offg. prin., ditto, June, 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

COMPTON, JOSEPH NEILD, A.M.I. Loco.E., Indian State Ryis. (transpn. (power) supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 25th Sept., 1900).—Asst. mechl. draftsman, junr. scale, Sept., 1922; confd. supr., senr. scale, Nov., 1938.

COMRIE, MARTIN CAMERON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 6 Nov., 1886).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 18th Nov., 1911, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (provl.), July, 1922; confd., Nov. 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

CONDER, JOHN MARMADUKE, late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 10th May, 1889).—Educ. at Windermere Grammar School, and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived 10th Jan., 1914, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service, Jan., 1918, to Jan., 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; confd., Dec., 1924; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

CONDER, STANLEY JOHN, India Office (higher executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 28th June, 1892).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 10th June, 1913, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from July, 1915, to Dec., 1918; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, March, 1927; assessor of income tax, Oct., 1936.

CONDON, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 22nd Feb., 1886).—Joined the service 8th April, 1910; King's Police Medal, Jan., 1925; promoted to I.P. as supt., April, 1936.

CONES, HERBERT ALFRED MICHAEL, late Indian Police (b. 11th April, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 7th Dec., 1908; supt., Jan., 1923; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

CONLIN, JOHN FRANCIS, M.C., Indian Police (supt., Ajmer-Merwara) (b. 30th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 14th Oct., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1926, and again, Nov., 1932; asst. commr. in Andamans, Oct., 1926; supt., May, 1936; ditto, Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1936.

CONNAUGHT AND STRATHEARN (DUKE OF), H.R.H. ARTHUR WILLIAM PATRICK ALBERT, G.K., K.T., K.P., P.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., G.C.V.O., G.B.E., D.C.L., L.L.D., Field-Marshal.—First commn., 19th June, 1868; asst. adjutant-gen., Gibraltar, Oct., 1875; brigadier-gen., Aldershot, Sept., 1880; accompanied the expedy. force to Egypt, Aug., 1882 (medal with clasp, 2nd class Medjidie, and C.B.); commander-in-chief, and 2nd member of council, Bombay, Dec., 1886, to March, 1890; on return to England was apptd. to command of southern divn., Aug., 1890; commanding at Aldershot, Oct., 1893; commanding the forces in Ireland, Jan., 1900; and the third Army Corps, Oct.,

1901; presd. of Council of R. Patriotic Fund Corpn., 1903; inspr.-genl. of the forces, May, 1904; field-marshal commanding-in-chief, and high commr. in the Mediterranean, 1907, to July, 1909; govr.-gen. and C.-in-C., domn. of Canada, 1911-16.

CONNELL, CECIL BOURKE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 13th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1907, as asst. engr; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; ret'd., Jan., 1930.

CONNELL, EDMOND COLEMAN, late Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly.) (b. 11th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1896; storekeeper, March, 1916; dist. contr. of stores, July, 1918; contr. of stores, C.I.C. Rly. construction, Aug., 1924; offg. contr. of stores, Feb., 1926; confd., Apl., 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

CONNOLLY, JOHN STANLAY D'ARCY, late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 19th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

CONNOLLY, VINCENT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 17th Oct., 1878).—Educ. at Belvedere Coll., Dublin, Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1902, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addl. dist. mag., Delhi, Nov., 1913; senr. sec. to finl. commrs., Punjab, March, 1917; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; sec., home dept., Punjab, April, 1921; offg. commr., April, 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

CONNOR, FRANK ARTHUR, late Punjab Civil Service (b. 17th Nov., 1877).—Joined the service 21st June, 1896; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1910; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1928; confd., May, 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

CONNOR, Sir FRANK POWELL, Kt., D.S.O., F.R.C.S., Major-Gen., I.M.S. (surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Madras.) (b. 16th Sept., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1902; arrived, 5th March, 1904; on milty. duty to Nov., 1906, and Aug., 1914, to May, 1919; prof. of clinical and operative surgery and surgeon to med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, March, 1920; Kt., Jan., 1926; surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Madras, Nov., 1933; offg. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Oct., 1935; presd., medl. council of India, Oct., 1935.

CONNOR, GEORGE POWELL, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th Oct., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1906; on milty. service from 15th Nov., 1917, to 29th Jan., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Dec., 1921; ret'd., July, 1934.

CONNOR, JAMES ESSON CLEAL, Burma Civil Service (Cl. 1) (asst. commr.) (b. 14th March, 1917).—Educ. at Govt. English High Sch., Maymyo, and Rangoon Univ.; appt. on probn., 30th Sept., 1939.

CONNOR, JAMES POWELL, late Land Records Dept., Burma (b. 13th Oct., 1875).—Joined the service 1st April, 1899; supt., March, 1901; addl. asst. dir., Dec., 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

CONNOR, WILLIAM CASEY, B.Sc., late Indian Police (b. 29th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as proby. dep. supt., 24th May, 1909; confd., May, 1910; offg. supt., Oct., 1911; confd., May, 1931; ret'd. Oct., 1938.

CONSTABLE, ERNEST HENRY, India Office (higher clerical officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 8th July, 1883).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st March, 1922, after previous temporary service from Feb., 1920; higher clerical officer, 1st October, 1925.

CONSTANTINE, GEORGE BAXANDALL, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 22nd June, 1902).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar School, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Feb., 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Feb., 1936; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1936; regr., high ct., Bombay, June, 1937, to Dec., 1938; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1938.

CONTRACTOR, KHAN BAHADUR SHERLARJI DADABHAI, B.A., late Bombay Educl. Service (b. 5th Sept., 1878).—Joined the service 19th Nov., 1903; educl. inspr., Sind, Feb., 1922; asst. dir. of public instn., Bombay educl. service, Jan., 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

CONTRACTOR, SORAB CURSHEDJI, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., Jail dept., Madras (supl.) (b. 25th Jan., 1887).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ to 25th Sept., 1925; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confd., June, 1928.

COODE, JOHN, late Indian Forest Service (b. 7th Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Madras, May, 1931; ret'd., June, 1932.

COOK, BERNARD CHRISTOPHER ALLEN, Indian C.S. (under secty. fin. dept., govt. of India) (b. 20th July, 1906).—Educ. at Radley, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1929; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and coll.; under secty. to govt., U.P., genl. bch. (temp.), Apl., 1934; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1935; under secty. fin. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1938.

COOK, Sir EDWARD MITCHENER, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Uppingham Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Nov., 1909; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1911; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Jan., 1912; temply. employed in finl. dept., India Office, June to Nov., 1915; joined finance dept., govt. of India, as dep. acct.-gen., Bengal, Jan., 1916; addl. dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1916; addl. dep. acct.-gen., Bombay, July, 1916; offg. controller of currency, May, 1917; acct.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1917; offg. controller of currency, Calcutta, Sept., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1919; C.S.I., Jan., 1923; Kt., June, 1923; sec. to high commr. for India, 20th Nov., 1923, to 7th Sept., 1924; apptd. finl. adviser to govt. of Siam, 1925; ret'd., June, 1926.

COOK, HENRY PATULLO, M.B., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 13th Nov., 1882).—First commn., dated 1st Feb., 1906; in mil. employ to 11th Sept., 1913, and again from 9th Aug., 1914, to 30th Sept., 1918; civil surg., Oct. 1918; confd., April, 1921; ret'd., Feb., 1933.

COOK, JACK MERVYN GERARD, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 22nd Mar., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 24th Nov., 1930; confd., Jan., 1933.

COOK, JAMES HIGH ERNEST, Indian Posts and Telegraphs (P.M.G.) (b. 8th June, 1896).—Joined service as proby. supt., 26th April, 1907; confd., Jan., 1908; on deptn. to I.E.F., Aug., 1914, to May, 1915; dep. P.M.G., July, 1932; P.M.G., Jan., 1938.

COOK, LEWIS, C.I.E., M.B., B.S., F.R.C.S., Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Medical Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 24th Oct., 1876).—First commission, 26th July, 1902; in mil. employ till Aug., 1906, when he was apptd. offg. civil surgeon; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to Jan., 1921; civil surgeon, Jan., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1928; offg. inspr. genl., civil hosp., Bihar and Orissa, May, 1929; again Dec., 1930; confd., Aug., 1931; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

COOK, WILLIAM LOUIS CHARLES GERARD, Indian Police (asst. inspr. genl., Bengal) (b. 21st Feb., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 13th Nov., 1923; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1929; confd., Feb., 1936; asst. inspr. genl., Mar., 1938.

COOKE, CHRISTOPHER HERBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 29th May, 1899).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922; and served in United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; offg. dep. secty. to govt., United Provs. Jan. 1934; offg. secty., bd. of rev., United Provs., Oct., 1934; settlement offcr., Oct., 1936; magte. and collr., Dec., 1937.

COOKE, CONRAD REGINALD, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.E.E., India Posts and Telegraphs (divl. engr.) (b. 31st Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1925; divnl. engr., June, 1932.

COOKE, EDWARD BARNARD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th Nov., 1877).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1903; asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1904; dist. ditto, Jan., 1911; divl. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

COOKE, GEORGE HENRY, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. magte., Madras) (b. 24th June, 1895).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service 26th Oct., 1920; arrived 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; offg. under sec. to govt., dev. dept., June, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. magte., March, 1928; collr., C. & M Station, Bangalore Oct., 1928; addl. jt. regr. of co-op. soc. (temp.), Nov., 1935; to Dec., 1936; collr. (provl.), Apl., 1936; sec. to govt., educ. and public health dept., Madras, March, 1939.

COOKE, GEORGE HUMPHREY, Capt., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Jan., 1903).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1923; in milty. employ to 19th Jan., 1930; asst. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1931; senr. sub-judge, Hazara, Nov., 1933; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1935; asst. commr. Tank, Oct., 1938; dep. commr. D.I. Khan, Jan., 1939; asst. commr. Tank, Mar., 1939.

COOKE, ROBERT FRANCIS RICKARD, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 27th Dec., 1874).—Joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1897; asst. traffic supt., July, 1898; dist. ditto, Jan., 1906; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

COOKE, THOMAS FOTHERGILL, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 15th May, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1909; asst. supt., April, 1911; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; supt., Sept., 1921; ret'd., June, 1937.

COOKSEY, WILFRID, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 1st Jan., 1913; colliery manager, April, 1921; offg. and asst. colliery supt., Mar., 1932; ret'd. Sept., 1939.

COOKSON, DOUGLAS MACGREGOR, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 20th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; services lent to Jaipur Durbar as suptg. engr. from Dec., 1913, to Dec., 1915, and again from Nov., 1922, to Aug., 1924; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. dep. ch. engr., Aug., 1926; ret'd., Feb., 1931.

COOMBS, ERNEST EDWIN, O.B.E., late Printing and Stationery Dept. (b. 17th Dec., 1875).—Joined the service as supt., govt. central press, 1st Nov., 1902; supt., govt. printing, Bombay, July, 1914; O.B.E., June, 1925; contr., printing, stationery and stamps, July, 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

COOMBS, GEORGE OSWALD, Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 16th Nov., 1876).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 29th May, 1898; extra asst. consvr., May, 1905; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 2nd Class, June, 1915; dep. consvr., Mar., 1920; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

COOPER, ALLAN, late Indian State Railways (b. 12th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service, Oct., 1903; dist. traffic supt., April, 1914; divl. comml. officer, Nov., 1928; offg. dep. ch. operatg. supt., Apl., 1929; ditto (provl.), Sept., 1929; confd., Aug., 1933; dep. ch. comml. manager, Oct., 1932; offg. ch. ditto, Nov., 1934; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

COOPER, CHARLES ROBERT PLANT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th April, 1882).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to finl. commr., July, 1913, to March, 1914; on spl. duty in Co-operative Credit Societies Dept.; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; on mil. duty, June, 1918, to Feb., 1919; offg. regr., co-operative societies, Burma, Oct., 1919; also dir. of agric. from May to Sept., 1920; dep. commr., jt. regr. co-op. soc., Dec., 1921; offg. sec. to govt. (agri., excise and forest dept.), Sept., 1924; confd. Oct., 1925; secty. to govt., rev. dept., May, 1927; offg. commr., Aug., 1930; confd. Jan., 1934; ret'd., July, 1939.

COOPER, Sir DHANJISHAH BOMANJEE, Khan Bahadur.—Apptd. member, govr.'s. exec. council, Bombay, June, 1934, to March, 1937; knighted, Feb., 1937.

COOPER, DHANJISHAH DOSABHAI, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay C.S., Judl. Branch (b. 19th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 15th June, 1904, as sub-judge; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, Nov., 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, Sept., 1927; retd., Mar., 1930.

COOPER, HAROLD HINTON, Indian State Rlys. (divi. mechl. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 20th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th Dec., 1914; offg. dist. loco. supt. Oct., 1919; offg. works manager, loco. and carr., Nov., 1926; pers. asst. to ch. mechl. engr., Feb., 1927; works manager, loco., Sept., 1927; offg. supt., mechl. workshops, Aug., 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936; and again, Mar., 1937; provl. perm. Jan., 1939.

COOPER, HARRY LYSTER, late Indian Forest Service (b. 4th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1906, as asst. consr., Assam; dep. consr., Nov., 1915; served in United Provs., from April, 1924, to March, 1925; offg. consr., Punjab, Oct., 1925; ditto, United Provs., Nov., 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; retd., Oct., 1933.

COOPER, JOHN WALTER, Office of High Commr. for India (principal exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 22nd Dec., 1879).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Store Depot, 9th June, 1898; supervisor, ditto, Aug., 1919; transfd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; principal ditto (temp.), April, 1927; confd., Apl., 1929.

COOPER, ROBERT FRANCIS, M.A. (inspr. schools) Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th May, 1886).—Joined the service as prinl., G.B.B. Coll. Muzaffarpur, on 1st July, 1915; hd. master, Ranchi Zila School, Aug., 1923; inspr. of schools, Oct., 1927; profir., Patna training coll., Oct., 1932; headmaster, Zila sch., Ranchi, Mar., 1933; inspr. of schools, Chota Nagpur div., Nov., 1935.

COOREY, CLARENCE OSWOLD, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 28th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Colombo, Univ. of Ceylon and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., July, 1936.

COPPINGER, WALTER VALENTINE, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., B.Sc., F.R.C.S.I. (Dublin), Maj.-Gen., I.M.S., retd. (b. 1st Mar., 1875).—Joined the Service, 28th June, 1900; on mil. employ to May, 1903; offg. dep. sanitary commr., Calcutta, Aug., 1903; offg. civil surg., March, 1904; resdt., surgn., med. coll. hosp.; Manbuhm., May,

1908; resdt. surgn., med. coll., Calcutta, March 1913; profir. med. coll., Calcutta, April, 1914; services placed at disposal of govt. of Burma, Oct., 1918, to Feb., 1919; inspr.-genl., civil hosp., Cent. Provs., Mar., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; surg.-genl. with govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1930; officer of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, Dec., 1931; retd., Nov., 1933.

GORBAN-LUCAS, ARTHUR JOHN LIONEL, late Indian Police (b. 23rd Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 21st Nov., 1908; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; offg. dep. commr., Rangoon, Sept., 1926; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; confd., June, 1934; retd., Dec., 1936.

CORFIELD, CONRAD LAURENCE, C.I.E., M.C., M.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (Indian Political service) (b. 15th Aug., 1893).—Educ. at St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Feb., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. from 5th May, 1920; asst. pte. sec. to the Viceroy, May, 1921, to Dec., 1922; sec. to the agt. to the govr.-genl. in the states of Western India, Jan., 1925; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1926; secty. to agent to govr.-genl., Rajputana, Nov., 1928; pol. agent in Southern States of C.I. and in Malwa, June 1931; secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1931; vice-presdt., Rewa State council, Mar., 1932; adviser to delegate for Rewa, Indian round-table conf., 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; jt. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1934; offg. addl. ditto, Apl., 1935; confd., Aug., 1936; jt. secty., F. and P. Dept., Apl., 1935; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; jt. secty., Pol. dept., Apl., 1937; offg. secty. to H.E. the Crown Representative, May, 1937; jt. secty., Pol. dept., Oct., 1937; resdt. at Jaipur, Nov., 1938; resdt. Rajputana and the ch. commr. Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1939; resdt. Jaipur, Oct., 1939.

CORFIELD, WILLIAM FRANCIS, Office of the High Commissioner for India (hr. clerical officer, accs. dept.) (b. 24th April, 1882).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st June, 1923; actg. hr. ditto, April, 1927; confd., Mar., 1936.

CORIN, JOHN MADAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 9th May, 1907).—Educ. at St. Paul's, and Magdalene College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under-secty., home, pol. etc. depts., Bombay, Aug., 1935; passport officer, Bombay, Nov., 1936; secty. to govr., Sind, Apl., 1937.

CORMACK, FREDERICK EUSON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Assam) (b. 18th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service, Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Feb., 1914; I.A.R.O. from 27th June, 1916, to 7th June, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1922.

CORMACK, HENRY SLATER, M.C., M.B., F.R.C.S.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Burma Medl. Dept., (civil surg.) (b. 6th May, 1886).—First comm. dated 28th Jan., 1911; in mil. employ to 6th Nov., 1922; offg. ophthalmic surg., Rangoon, Nov., 1922; March, 1926, and again, May, 1929; civil surg., Nov., 1932.

CORNELIUS, ALVIN ROBERT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 1st May, 1903).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., and Oct., 1932; and again, May, 1933; confd., Nov., 1936.

CORNELIUS, EUSTACE HERBERT, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th May, 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr.; 1st Oct., 1902, and posted to United Provs.; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; offg. supt. engr., May, 1922; confd. Oct., 1926; ret'd., May, 1934.

CORNFORTH, ERNEST, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 4th May, 1931).—Joined the service, 29th Mar., 1909; asst. auditor, N.W. Rly., June, 1918; ditto, E.B. Rly., Jan., 1923; govt. examr. of accts., Burma Rlys., April, 1924; dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., March, 1927; govt. examr., B.B. & C.I. Rly., Apl., 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

CORNISH, ATHELSTAN, Indian State Rlys. (supt. mechl. workshops, N.W. Rly.) (b. 22nd July, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 7th Oct., 1913; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1926; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. rly., Mar., 1935; and again, Mar., 1936; offg. dep. ch. opertg. supt., N.W. rly., Nov., 1935; dep. ch. mechl. engr. (provl. perm.), Feb., 1937; supt. mechl. workshops, Nov., 1937.

CORNISH, HENRY DAUNCEY, Barr., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 7th Aug., 1877).—Apptd. admin. genl. and offl. trustee, Madras, Jan., 1921; actg. judge, high cr., July, 1927, and again, July, 1929; confd., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

CORNWELL, RICHARD BARRY, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 20th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1909; dep. consvr., Oct., 1922; on deputation to Forest Research Institute, Sept., 1924; dist. forest officer, Oct., 1933.

CORY, EDGAR MYRIE, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 6th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 14th Sept., 1906; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; in milty. employ from 22nd May, 1916, to 12th Feb., 1920; dist. engr., Nov., 1925; offg. divl. transpn. supt., Nov., 1930; dep. ch. engr., Sept., 1933; offg. ch. engr., variously from May, 1936; chief engr. (provl.), Nov., 1937.

COSGRAVE, SIR WILLIAM ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th April, 1879).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; vice-president, Manipur Darbar, May, 1907, to March, 1910; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1910; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; dep. commr., May, 1917; ditto, and pol. agent, Manipur, Dec., 1917, to March, 1920; offg. ch. secy. to govt. of Assam, April, 1930; confd., Oct., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; member, legisl. council, Assam, Nov., 1932; commr., Dec., 1933; offg. member, public service commn., Apl., 1934; ch. commr., Andaman and Nicobar

Islands, Feb., 1935, to Feb., 1938; knighted, June, 1938; ret'd., Nov., 1938.

COSTELLO, JOHN FRANCIS, M.A. late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Mar., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (tempy.), 28th May, 1909; resdt. engr., July, 1914; dist. engr., July, 1925; offg. principal, Bina Transportation School, Nov., 1926, and again, Dec., 1929; offg. dep. agent, May, 1929; Apl., 1930, and again May, 1931; confd. (provl.), July, 1932; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

COSTELLO, SIR LEONARD WILFRED JAMES, Kt., M.A., LL.B. (b. 25th Aug., 1881).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 30th Dec., 1926; knighted, June, 1935.

COSTER, GILBERT WALLACE, O.B.E. (b. 20th Apl., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 21st May, 1909; asst. contr. of printing, staty. and stamps, Calcutta, Sept., 1910; dep. ditto, May, 1924; dep. contr., central stamp stores, Bombay, Feb., 1928; dep. contr. of stamps, Apl., 1928; ret'd., Apl., 1935; O.B.E., June, 1935.

COTTAM, WILLIAM JOHN, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 23rd Mar., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 5th Dec., 1924; addl. supt., June, 1930; offg. supt., Mar., 1931; confd., Aug., 1937.

COTTER, EDWARD, M.B., B.Ch., D.P.H., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (public health commr. with the Govt. of India) (b. 6th Nov., 1892).—First commn., dated 8th Dec., 1915; in milty. employ prior to 1st July, 1930; asst. dir. pub. health, Burma, July, 1930; offg. dir., May, 1933; dep. public health commr. with the govt. of India, July, 1937; public health commr., Mar., 1939.

COTTER, GERALD DE PURCELL, B.A., D.Sc., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 8th Apl., 1881).—Joined the service 15th April, 1905, as asst. supt.; supt., Jan., 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

COTTERELL, CECIL BERNARD, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Dec., 1875).—Educ. at St. Peter's Sch., York, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 16th Nov. 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and agent to govt., Vizagapatam; dep. commr., salt, abkari, and customs dept., Feb., 1906; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; pte. sec. to govt., Madras, Jan., 1912, to Feb., 1915; C.I.E., June, 1915; collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1917; sec. to govt., local self-govt. dept., March, 1925; offg. third member, bd. of rev., Apr., 1928; offg. 2nd member, ditto, June, 1929; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, May, 1930; 2nd member, bd. of revenue, Madras, Oct., 1930; 1st member, ditto Sept., 1932; C.S.I., June, 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

COTTON, Rev. BEN, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 13th June, 1878).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 15th Feb., 1910; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1913; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1923; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

COTTON, Rev. HENRY WILMOT STAPLETON, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b.

22nd Feb., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 22nd Oct., 1925; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; offg. archdeacon of Lucknow, April, 1936; and again, May, 1937; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1937; bishop's commry., Apl., 1938.

COTTON, JOHN RICHARD, Capt., I.A., Indian Pol. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1909).—First commn., 31st Jan., 1929; pers. asst. to ch. commr. and resdt., and C. in C. at Aden, Nov., 1934; passport offcr. (in addn.), Jan., 1935; under trng. in the Punjab, Jan., 1936; under secty to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, July, 1937; cfd., on Mar., 1938; attd. officer, Rajputana Agency, Feb., 1939; under sec. to resdt., Rajputana, Feb., 1939; sec. to resdt., Rajputana, in addn. Mar., 1939; under sec. to resdt., Rajputana, April, 1939.

COUCHMAN, Sir HAROLD JOHN, D.S.O., M.C., Brig., R.E. (ret'd.), late Survey of India (b. 29th July, 1882).—First commn. dated 18th Aug., 1900; joined the Survey of India dept. as asst. supt. 30th Jan., 1906; temply. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to Nov., 1919; dep. supt., Sept., 1916; temp. dep. master, security printing, India, April, 1926; offg. master, security printing, Feb., 1929; dir. (temp.) May, 1929; confd., Aug., 1929; offg. surveyor-genl., Apl., 1933; confd., Oct., 1933; knighted, May, 1937; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

COUESLANT, D. C., B.Sc. (Lond.), Industries Dept., Bihar (b. 23rd July, 1877).—Apptd. princpl. Bihar Coll. of engrg., 3rd Apl., 1928; offg. dir. of industries, Feb., 1931, and again, Apl., 1931.

COULSON, ARTHUR LENNOX, M.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., Geological Survey of India (supfg. geologist) (b. 25th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on probn., 25th Nov., 1922; confd., Nov., 1924; offg. supt., Aug., 1930; confd., April, 1936.

COUPER, Sir THOMAS, Kt., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th March, 1878).—Educ. at Fettes Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. offr.; dep. dir., land records from Nov., 1911 to Oct., 1914; dep. commr., April, 1916; provl. subst. director of agric., April, 1917, to May, 1918; on mil. duty, May, 1918, to Jan., 1920; on spl. duty Cttee. Land rev. system from Dec., 1920; offg. commr. from March, 1923; offg. fin. commr., Oct., 1925; confd., May, 1928; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Aug., 1929; and again, Aug., 1930; confd., Oct., 1930; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; actg. govr. of Burma, Apl., 1935; knighted, June, 1935; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

COUPLAND, RICHARD ERNEST, late Indian Police Service (b. 1st Oct., 1875).—Educ. at New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Nov., 1896, and posted to United Provs.; supt. of police, July, 1906; empd. in Ajmer-Merwara from March, 1907; supt., govt. rly. police, Rajputana, April, 1914; spl. duty under dir.

criminal intell., India, May, 1917, to March, 1918; spl. duty, criminal investign. dept., United Provs., April, 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., United Provs., March, 1923; confd., Aug., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

COURTENAY, REGINALD HERBERT, late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Sept., 1885).—Educ. at Dover Coll., Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; appointed after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 15th Dec., 1910, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., Nov., 1915; addl. under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Nov., 1917, to Oct., 1918; regr., high ct., Madras, March, 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1922; confd., Dec., 1925; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

COUSENS, HENRY JAMES MORRISON, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th May, 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr., Bombay; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., Bombay, Oct., 1930; confd., Nov., 1934; ret'd., May, 1938.

COUSINS, EDMUND RICHARD JOHN RATCLIFFE, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Bihar) (b. 22nd April, 1888).—Educ. at Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1918; offg. dep. commr., March, 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1922; confd., July, 1925; dep. commr., Feb., 1926; offg. commr., Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1938; commr., Mar., 1937.

COUTTS, DUNCAN, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd., late Civil Med. Dept., Bihar (b. 7th Nov., 1882).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1906; in milty. employ up to 21st Nov., 1922; civil surg., Dec., 1922; princpl. medical coll., Patna, April, 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1936.

COVENTRY, JOHN PHILIP CHETWYND, Indian Police (b. 14th Dec., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Dec., 1937; services placed at the disposal of the Govt. of Cent. Provs. and Berar from Oct., 1938.

COWAN, ALAN CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 7th Sept., 1915).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd., on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

COWARD, DUNCAN CECIL, Indian State Rys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 16th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 25th June, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Ryys., Feb., 1932.

COWBURN, Rev. FRANK, B.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 11th May, 1896).—Appt.

chaplain on probation, 10th Apl., 1926; confd., Apl., 1928; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1938; ret'd. July, 1939.

COWGILL, JOHN FELIX, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. commr. of police, Calcutta) (b. 11th June, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 25th Nov., 1923; pers. asst. to dir., intelligence bureau, home dept., Mar., 1930; confd. as supt., July, 1936; O.B.E., June, 1935; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Mar., 1938.

COWGILL, JOHN HARRY CHRISTOPHER, Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 14th Nov., 1927; offg. addl. supt., Mar., 1934.

COWHAM, REV. ARTHUR GERARD, M.A., Indian Ecl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 27th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 25th Feb., 1929; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1931.

COWIE, JOHN GRANDISON, Dept. of Industries, Punjab (inspr. of industrial schools) (b. 12th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as vice-princl., Mayo sch. of arts, Sept., 1924; inspr. of industrial schools, May, 1928.

COWLEY, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th November, 1915).—Educ. at Middlesbrough High Sch., Jesus Coll. Cambridge, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

COX, ALFRED INNES, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Major, I.M.S. Medical Dept., Madras (dist. medl. officer) (b. 1st Nov., 1894).—First commn., 27th Sept., 1919; in milty. employ until 25th Jan., 1932; dist. medl. officer, Madras, May, 1934.

COX, CUTHBERT EUSTACE CONNOP, C.I.E., Indian Forest Service (ch. consr., Central Prov.) (b. 9th Mar., 1835).—Joined the service, 8th Nov., 1907, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Dec., 1912; offg. consvr. June, 1924, July, 1925, and again, May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1928; ch. commr., Nov., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1939.

COX, EDMUND LEONARD, Indian Police (asst. comdt., Malabar Special Police) (b. 29th Jan., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; asst. comdt., Malabar spec. police, Oct., 1936.

COX, EDWARD ORAM, B.A.I., M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th May, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; M.B.E., June, 1919; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Jan., 1935.

COX, HUBERT EUSTACE, Indian State Railways (dep. ch. engr., signals, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 14th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1915; signal and interlocking engr., G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., signals, July, 1935; confd., Dec., 1935.

COX, VERNON MAURICE HERVALWIL, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Aug., 1898).—First commn., 12th Sept., 1917; in milty. employ to 1st Nov., 1927; asst. for

Mekran to pol. agent in Kalat, Jan., 1930; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat and of Chagai, May, 1932; asst. commr., Peshawar, Feb., 1935; dep. commr., Peshawar, May, 1936; offer. on spec. duty, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1936; pol. agent, ditto, Mar., 1937; rev. and divi. commr., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1938; home secty., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1939.

COYAJEE, SIR JEHANGIR COOVERJEE, Kt., B.A. (Cantab.), LL.B. (Bom.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Sept., 1875).—Joined the service as profir., Presdy. Coll., on 17th March, 1911; member of Royal Commn. on Indian Tariff and Indian Currency; Kt., Jan., 1928; offg. prinpl. presdy coll., Oct., 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1931; sub-delegate of India at Assembly of League of Nations, Sept., 1932.

CRACKNELL, ARTHUR JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th May, 1885).—Joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1912; asst. loco. supt., July, 1914; in milty., employ from 6th July, 1915, to 21st April, 1916; asst. mechani. engr., Nov., 1922; offg. asst. works manager, May, 1925; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. dep. ch. mechani. engr., May, 1928, June, 1930, and again, Apl., 1931; confd. (provl.), Mar., 1932; confd., Feb., 1938; ret'd. Nov., 1938.

CRACKNELL, FREDERICK GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 12th Feb., 1910).—Educated at Leeds Grammar School and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., June, 1934.

CRAIG, MALCOLM THOMAS, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Prov.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th April, 1920; asst. exec. engr., May, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1926.

CRAIG, THOMAS JOSEPH ALEXANDER, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 3rd Dec., 1881).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1902; supt., Feb., 1911; prin. police training coll., July to Sept., 1913; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1924; confd., Sept., 1925; inspr.-genl., Sept., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

CRAIK, SIR HENRY DUFFIELD, Bart., K.C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (govr. of Punjab) (b. 2nd Jan., 1876).—Educ. at Eton Coll., Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; regr. of the chief court, March, 1905; asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1908; settlt. offr., Oct. 1910; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1915; dep. commr., and rev. sec. to govt., Feb., 1916; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1919; offg. addl. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1919; offg. chief sec. to Punjab Govt., April, 1922; confd., July, 1922; C.S.I., Jan., 1924; offg. finl. commr., Nov., 1927; commr. (provl.), Oct., 1929; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, Apl., 1930; confd., Aug., 1930; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1933; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, July, 1934; apptd. govr. of Punjab, Apl., 1938.

CRAKER, PHILLIP WILLIAM, O.B.E., Indian State Railways. (exec. engr. E.I. Rly.) (b. 31st

Aug., 1887).—Asst. Engr., 1st Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1920; Exec. engr., Aug., 1937. On depn. to Burma Govt., Apl., 1938.

CRAW, HENRY HEWAT, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (finl. commr., Burma) (b. 29th Oct., 1882).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., and Jena Coll., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty, Oct., 1917, to Jan., 1919; offg. depy. commr., May, 1921; dep. commr., Jan., 1923; offg. commr., May, 1931; confd., June, 1935; ch. secty. to govt., Burma, Sept., 1935; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; finl. commr., Feb., 1938.

CRAWFORD, CHARLES ANTHONY, Indian State Rlys. (dep. chief comml. mgr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 14th April, 1900).—Joined the service 14th Jan., 1916, and held non-gazetted appts.; asst. supt., Feb., 1923; asst. comml. manager, Mar., 1926; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, June, 1929; and again, Nov., 1935; confd. Dec., 1936.

CRAWFORD, COLIN GRANT, late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 3rd June, 1890).—Educ. at Clifton and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 7th Dec., 1914, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. service, March, 1915, to Dec., 1919; transfed. to Assam, Dec., 1919; presdnt. Manipur State Durbar from Sept., 1922; ret'd. Jan., 1930.

CRAWFORD, MAURICE NEWENHAM, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 2nd Sept., 1879).—Apptd. asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1902; ditto, and asst. postmr.-gen., traffic (tempy.), Apl., 1914; supt., engrg., Feb. 1917; asst. chief engrg., April, 1917; on field service (Afghan War), from May, 1919; dep. postmr.-gen., Sind and Baluchistan, July, 1922; dir., posts and tels., ditto, May, 1928; offg. dir., tel. engrg., Aug., 1929; offg. P.M.G., B. & O., 1929; offg. P.M.G., Burma, Mar., 1930; dir., tel. engrg., Aug., 1930; addl. dep. dir.-genl., tels. (tempy.), Oct., 1933; offg. P.M.G., central circle, Jan., 1934; ditto, Bengal and Assam, Apl., 1933; confd. as P.M.G., May, 1934; ret'd., Sept., 1934; War decorations:—South African medal, 2 clasps, Tibet medal with clasp.

CRAWFORD, WILLIAM GLASGOW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 28th April, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., May, 1931.

CRAWLEY, DESMOND JOHN CHETWODE, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 2nd June, 1917).—Educ. at King's School, Ely, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd. 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

CRAWLEY, GEORGE RICHARD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 22nd Apl., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1930; confd., Apl., 1933; A.D.C. to govt., Oct., 1936.

CREAGH-COEN (see Coen, T. B. C.).

CREANER, THOMAS AMBROSE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 24th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engrg., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1930.

CREED, CLARENCE JAMES, M.C. Indian Police (suplt., Bihar) (b. 19th July, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 29th March, 1920; supt., Sept., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1936.

CREEDY, HAROLD DOUGLAS, B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 15th June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. dep. agent, Mar., 1929, and again, Feb., 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

CREEK, ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 11th May, 1910).—Educated at the East Anglian School, Bury St. Edmunds, and St. John's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.
