CREIGHTON, THOMAS GORDON, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., M.I.L.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. mech. engrg., N.W. Rly.) (b. 29th Dec., 1888), asst. loco. supt., junr. scale, 11th Dec., 1922; divl. mech. engrg., senr. scale, July, 1937.

CRERAR, SIR JAMES, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Dec., 1877).—Educ. at Geo. Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, Edin. Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 15th Nov., 1901, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. collr. and manager, encumbered estates, May, 1906; asst. commr. in Sind and Sindhi translator to govt., Sept., 1907, to Jan., 1911; dep. munpl. commr., Bombay, Oct., 1911; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Jan., 1912; special duty in connection with Royal commn., 1912; munpl. commr., Bombay, July, 1913; offg. dep. sec. to govt., pol., judl. and spl. depts., Aug., 1914; pte. sec. to govt., Bombay, Oct., 1914; C.I.E., June, 1917; addl. sec. to govt., poll., judl. and spl. depts., July, 1918; sec. to govt. pol., &c., depts., Sept., 1919; C.S.I., Jan., 1922; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1922; confd., Jan., 1923; temp. member of Gov.-Gen.'s Council April, 1926; member of ditto, July, 1927, to Mar., 1932; K.C.S.I., June, 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

CREWE (MARQUESS OF), MOST HON. ROBERT OFFLEY ASHEURTON CREWE-MILNES, K.G., P.C., M.A., F.S.A., Earl of Madeley, in the county of Stafford, and Marquess of Crewe (cr. 1911). Sec. of State for India, 7th Nov., 1910, to March, 1911, and from May, 1911, to May, 1915; chairman, Ind. constit'n'l relations ctee., 1919.

CRICHTON, GERALD CHARLES LAWRENCE, Maj., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 30th July, 1900).—First commn. dated 14th July, 1921; prob. in pol. dept., May, 1926; settlement officer, Peshawar, Jan., 1927; asst. commr., Bannu, July, 1927; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana and C.C. Ajmer Merwara, June, 1928; pol. asst. to C.C. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1928; asst. commr., Tank, Nov., 1930; offg. asst. resdnt., Kolhapur, Oct., 1931; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1933; offg. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transfed. depts., Dec., 1933; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Oct., 1934; home secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1937; dep. secty., ext. dept., govt. of India, June, 1938; home sec., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1938; pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct. 1939.

CRICHTON, WALTER HUGH, Major, I.M.S. (b. 24th July, 1896).—First commn., 8th Dec., 1924; agency surg., Kurram, Feb., 1933; health officer, Simla, Oct., 1934; asst. dir. pub. health, Delhi, Oct., 1936; chief health officer, Delhi, Apl., 1938.

CRICHTON, The Ven. WALTER RICHARD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 4th Jan., 1884).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 31st Oct., 1917; junr. chaplain, 31st Oct., 1919; senr. chaplain, 31st Oct., 1929; archdeacon of Madras, Mar., 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

CRICKMAY, JOSEPH GEORGE, India Office (supig. executive officer, milty. dept.) (b. 23rd April, 1883).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 17th Jan., 1902; transf'd. to mil. dept., from Oct., 1914; minor staff clerk, March, 1917; staff clerk (actg.), Nov., 1919; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; representative of the India Office on the Navy, Army and Air Force Insurance Fund Advisory Ctee., Apl., 1922; actg. suptg. exec. officer, Mar., 1935; and again, Apl., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937.

CRITCHLY, VERNON FAIRBANK (dep. chief engr. Punjab Electricity) (b. 22nd Sept., 1893).—Joined the service 13th Oct., 1927, served as exec. and elec. engr. variously up to Dec., 1934; dep. chief engr., Jan., 1936.

CROAD, ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., Sept., 1923; secty. to ch. commr., Delhi, Dec., 1932; suptg. engr. (confd.) cent. p.w.d., Aug., 1938.

CROCKETT, WILLIAM GORDON, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 18th Dec., 1880).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 23rd Nov., 1900; junior clerk, Nov., 1914; M.B.E., Jan., 1918; transf'd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; personal asst. to high commr., Oct., 1920; senr. clerk, April, 1921; contr., store dept., April, 1922.

CROFT, WILLIAM DAWSON, C.I.E., C.V.O., B.A., India Office (asst. secty., econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 16th Aug., 1892).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, military dept., 28th May, 1919, after previous service as temporary junr. clerk since Nov., 1915; apptd. pte. sec. to the perm. under sec. of state, Jan., 1920; principal, April, 1921; establishment officer, July, 1921, to Jan., 1922; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; pte. secty. to secty. of state, Apl., 1931 to Oct., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; asst. secty., Aug., 1936; posted to econ. and overseas dept., Oct., 1937; C.V.O., Jan., 1938.

CROFTON, DENIS HAYES, Indian C.S. (under-secty. to govt. of India, dept. of lab.) (b. 14th Dec., 1908).—Educated at Tonbridge School and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India Dec., 1932, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty. to govt., pol. dept., Bihar, June, 1936; attached to

govt. of India, dept. of lab., April, 1939; under secty. to govt. of India, dept. of lab., June, 1939.

CROFTON, RICHARD MARSH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dir-genl., rev. dept., Hyderabad) (b. 6th April, 1891).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived 3rd Dec., 1915, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from Oct., 1917, to March, 1919; offg. dep. commr., July, 1922; dep. commr. (provl.) and finl. sec to govt., May, 1926; dep. commr., Dec., 1926; offg. rev. secty. to govt., Dec., 1927; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1931; excise and opium commr. in C. India, Nov., 1931; dir.-genl., rev. dept., Hyderabad, Feb., 1935.

CROFTS, FRANK NORMAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 23rd July, 1906).—Educ. at Rugby and Cairns Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived 1st Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. collr. and mag.; temp. sess. and sub-judge, May, 1935; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1936.

CROMBIE, ALAN DOUGLAS, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. magte., Madras) (b. 20th Jan., 1894).—Educ. at Wolverhampton Grammar Sch.; joined the service, 25th Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1923; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1925; on foreign service as agent under Rajah of Parlakimedi, Nov., 1926; dep. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Madras, Dec., 1929; pte. secty. to govt., Madras, June, 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; collr. (provl. subs.), Sept., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936; collr. and dist. magte., Sept., 1936.

CROMBIE, GEORGE EDMOND, M.A., India Office (principal, Pol. dept.) (b. 14th June, 1908).—Educ. at Aberdeen Grammar Sch. and Fettes Coll., Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. asst. principal, 19th Oct., 1931; actg. resdt. clk., July to Aug., 1932; resdt. clk., July, 1934 to Oct., 1934; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of State, Oct., 1935; pte. secty. to parly. under-secty. of State, Aug., 1936; actg. princpl., Apl., 1937; confd., Oct., 1938.

CROMBIE, PETER, M.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 2nd Nov., 1911).—Educ. at Fettes College, University of Aberdeen and Cairns College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1938.

CROOKSHANK, HENRY, B.A., B.A.I. (Dub.) Geological Survey of India (supig. geologist) (b. 13th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1920; offg. supt., Sept., 1929; and again Mar., Oct., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

CROOKSHANK, Sir SYDNEY D'AGUILAR, K.C.M.G., C.B., C.I.E., D.S.O., M.V.O., A.I.C.E., Col., late R.E., India Public Works Dept. (ret'd.).—First commn., dated 27th July, 1889; joined the Public Works Dept., Jan., 1892, and served on the state rlys.; in charge of Gohna Lake operations, 1894, and famine relief wks., 1896-97; under-sec. to govt., United Prov., 1905-07; in charge of construction of King George's Hospital, Lucknow, 1908-10; supt. of wks., Delhi Durbar, 1911; suptg. engr., impl. city wks., Delhi, 1913-14; offg. sec. to the govt. of India, public wks. dept., Nov., 1919; chief engr. (provl.), Nov., 1919; confd., Nov., 1920; reverted to Impl. estabt., June, 1925; ret'd., June, 1927. War services—Relief of Chitral, 1895 (medal and clasp); British Expedy. Force, 1914-19 (dir.-gen. of transportation, France, 1914-15 Star, British War and Victory medals); Honours—M.V.O., Dec., 1911; C.I.E., June, 1912; D.S.O., June, 1916; Bt.-Col., Jan., 1918; C.B., June, 1918; K.C.M.G., June, 1919; Commdr., Légion d'Honneur; ditto, Ordre de Leopold; Grande Croix, Ordre d'Aviz; Croix de Guerre Française (avec palme); Croix de Guerre Belge.

CROSSLEY, DONALD, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 22nd July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1929; confd., Mar., 1934; awarded Indian Police Medal, Mar., 1937.

CROSSLEY, THOMAS BIGHAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Feb., 1910).—Educated at Blackburn Grammar School and King's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

CROSTHWAIT, LELAND GEORGE, Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Survey of India (b. 29th Aug., 1881).—First commn., 23rd July, 1900; asst. supt. on probn., trigon. branch, Oct., 1905; confd., Oct., 1906; on mil. service in Mesopotamia, Dec., 1914, to June, 1919; dept. supt., May, 1916; supt., Jan., 1926; offg. dir., eastern circle, Oct., 1926; ditto, Burma circle, May, 1927; ditto, Southern circle, Oct., 1929; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

CROSTHWAIT, TIMOTHY LELAND, B.A., Indian C.S., (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 5th Aug., 1915).—Educ. at Wellington and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 19th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

CROSTHWAITE, Sir BERTRAM MAITLAND, Kt., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Oct., 1880.)—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt. 14th Dec., 1898; dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1905; dep. traffic manager, April, 1915; offg. traffic manager, June, 1924; confd., Sept., 1924; offg. agent, Dec., 1926; member of rly. bd., May-Nov., 1927; actg. agent, July, 1929; confd., Aug., 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1935; knighted, June, 1935.

CROSTHWAITE, CHARLES GILBERT, C.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 3rd June, 1878).—First commn. dated 4th Aug., 1897; in

mil. emp. to Jan., 1903; pol. asst., Dec., 1903; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1904; asst. commr., N.W.F. Prov., Feb., 1904; dep. commr., July, 1911; dist. judge, Peshawar, Oct., 1912; dep. commr., Kohat, Jan., 1915; addl. divl. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Nov., 1915; dep. commr., Bannu, April, 1916; pol. agent, Wana, April, 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; dep. commr. Peshawar, May, 1921; C.B.E., June, 1924; offg. pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, June, 1925; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, March, 1926; offg. resident at Gwalior, June, 1926; offg. agent to govr. genl., Madras States, April, 1928; offg. resdt., Baroda, Mar., 1931; confd., June, 1931; ret'd., June, 1933.

CROSTHWAITE, HUGH STUART, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (member, fed. public service commn.) (b. 20th Oct., 1879).—Educ. at Rugby Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1910-1912; chairman, Allahabad munpl. bd., Apl., 1913, to Oct., 1916; supt. Dehra Dun, from Mar., 1917; mag. and collr., Jan., 1921; sec. to govt. of United Prov., industries dept., April, 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; offg. finance secty. to govt., April, 1927; dep. commr., Nov., 1927; offg. commr., Mar., 1929; confd., May, 1932; offg. ch. commr., Delhi, Apl., 1934; member fed. public service commn., Feb., 1935.

CROTHERS, EDWARD MICHAEL, late Indian Forest Service (b. 6th Feb. 1880).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1901; dep. consvr., March, 1920; ret'd. Sept., 1933.

CROXFORD, ERNEST WILLIAM, India Audit Office (exec. officer) (b. 18th Jan., 1915).—Apptd. exec. officer, 2nd Oct., 1933.

CRUICKSHANK, FLETCHER CECIL MACDONALD, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Aug., 1882.)—Educ. at Rugby and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; special manager. Awah estate, Oct., 1912; jt. mag., Jan., 1916; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), July, 1915, to May, 1919; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, May, 1919; mag. and collr., Nov., 1923; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; confd., June, 1928; ret'd., July, 1934.

CRUICKSHANK, MARTIN MELVIN, M.D., B.Sc., Ch.M., D.O.M.S., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., Medical Dept., Madras (dist. medl. officer) (b. 22nd Mar. 1888).—First commn., 17th Mar., 1919; in milty. employ until 25th Feb., 1931; dist. medl. officer, Madras, May, 1934; prof. of surgery, Madras medl. coll., and surgeon, genl. hospital, Madras, Sept., 1934.

CRUMP, EDWIN SAMUEL, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th July, 1882.)—Joined the service as asst. engr., on 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Nov., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Punjab, Feb., 1926; confd., Feb., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1936; ret'd., July, 1937.

CRUMP, Sir HENRY ASHBROOKE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 10th Nov., 1885, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; commr. of excise, June, 1893; dep. commr., Aug., 1894; second sec. to chief commr., Nov., 1894, to Feb., 1895; chief sec. to ditto, April, 1901, to May, 1902, and from June, 1906; commr., Oct., 1907; offg. chief commr., Jan. to Feb., 1912; offg. finl. commr., Feb. to Aug., 1912; finl. commr., March, 1913; offg. chief commr., Jan. to April, 1914; C.S.I., June, 1914; retd., Nov., 1920; K.C.I.E., Jan. 1921.

CRUMP, Sir LOUIS CHARLES, Kt., late Indian C.S. —Educ. under private tuition and at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 10th Dec., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; 2nd asst., March, 1897; ditto, and asst. judge, Feb., 1898; ditto, and asst. to the legal remembrancer, March, 1899; under sec. to govt. pol., judl., and legisl. depts., March, 1899, to Jan., 1900; 1st asst., April, 1902; judge and sess. judge, Sept., 1904; remembrancer of legal affairs and sec. to govt., legal dept., Jan., 1909, to July, 1911; addl. jud. commr., Sind, Nov., 1918; temp. member govr.'s exec. council, June, 1919, to Oct., 1919; offg. judge, Bombay High Ct., from Nov., 1919; judl. commr., Sind, April, 1920; judge, Bombay High Ct., Jan., 1921; Kt., Jan., 1928; retd., Jan., 1929.

CUERDON, THOMAS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b. 28th Feb., 1876).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 9th Aug., 1899, to 31st Jan., 1920; asst. engr., Feb., 1920; asst. engr., March, 1922; exec. engr., June, 1923; retd., Feb., 1931.

CUMING, Sir ARTHUR HERBERT, Kt., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 24th Nov., 1871).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., London Univ., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.,; asst. commr., Assam, Jan., 1899; mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1906; dist. and sessions judge, March, 1908; re-transfd. to Bengal, April, 1912; offg. supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Bengal, April, 1914; acting judge, Calcutta high ct., June to Sept., 1916, March to April, 1917, and from April, 1919, to Sept., 1920, and from Feb., 1921; permanently apptd. as judge, high ct., Calcutta, 10th Nov., 1921; Kt., Jan., 1928; retd., Nov., 1931.

CUMMING, DONALD BRODBY, D.F.C., late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 8th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 6th Dec., 1911; supt., Sept., 1924; retd., Oct., 1932.

CUMMING, Sir JOHN GHEST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at High Sch., Glasgow, Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 24th Oct., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and settlt. officer; offg. under sec. to govt., July to Oct.,

1894; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1896; mag. and collr. June, 1900; sec. to govt., judl. and genl. depts., Jan., 1909; ditto, rev. and genl. depts., Sept., 1909; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; dir., land records, Dec., 1911; special duty. Bengal secretariat, Dec., 1911; offg. commr., May, 1912; offg. chief sec. to govt., May, 1913; confd., Jan., 1914; addl. member, govr.-genl.'s council, Dec., 1915; C.S.I., June, 1916; temps. member of govr.'s council, Bengal, Sept., 1917, to March, 1918; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bengal, April, 1918, to May, 1920; K.C.I.E., 1920; retd., Oct., 1921.

CUMMING, RALPH FLETCHER EDWARD, late Bombay C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 17th Dec., 1895; dep. collr., May, 1911; offg. asst. commr. of salt, etc., Apl., 1914; asst. supt. of stamps, etc., Dec., 1919; retd., Dec., 1933.

CUMMING, ROUALEYN CHARLES ROSSITER, Indian Police (inspr.-genl. police, and jt. secty., Assam) (b. 2nd Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 7th Dec., 1911; supt., Apl., 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1935; inspr.-genl., and jt. secty. (home dept.), Assam, Dec., 1937.

CUNLIFFE, Sir JOHN ROBERT ELLIS, Kt., Barr. (b. 4th Mar., 1886).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, 12th Nov., 1924; Kt., Jan., 1932; judge, high ct., Calcutta, Sept., 1934; retd., Nov., 1937.

CUNNINGHAM, Sir CHARLES BANKS, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian Police (b. 8th May, 1884).—Apptd. asst. supt. of police, Madras, 24th Dec., 1904; supt. and dep. commr. of police, Madras, Feb., 1913; commr. of police, Travancore, from Aug., 1915, to March, 1921; offg. dep. inspr. genl., Jan., 1928; actg. commr. of police, Madras, May, 1928; confd., Apl., 1930; awarded King's police medal, Mar., 1929; offg. inspr.-genl., May, 1930; confd., Oct., 1930; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; knighted, June, 1933; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937; retd., Jan., 1938.

CUNNINGHAM, Sir GEORGE, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (govr., N.W.F.P.) Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Mar., 1888).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1911, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.,; asst. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1914; personal asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Oct., 1915; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1917; asst. commr., Tank, Dec., 1918; pol. asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Feb., 1919; sub-divl. offr., Tank, April, 1919; pte. sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., May, 1919; spl. duty on staff of chief pol. offr. with N.W. Frontier Field Force, May, 1919; O.B.E., Aug., 1920; asst. commr., Thal, Nov., 1920; pol. agent, Tochi, April, 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; counsellor, British Legation, Kabul, Nov., 1925; pte. sec. to the Viceroy, July, 1926; C.S.I., June, 1931; member, exec. council govr. N.W.F.P., June, 1932, to May, 1936; actg. govr. N.W.F.P., Nov., 1932; offg. agent to govr.-genl., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1932; K.C.I.E., June, 1935; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1937; govr., N.W.F.P., Mar., 1937.

CUNNINGHAM, JOHN, C.I.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) late Medical Research Dept. (b. 9th July, 1881).—In mil. employ from 1st Feb., 1905 to 14th Oct. 1908, when apptd. to officiate as asst. to dir., Bombay bacteriological laboratory; asst. dir., central research inst., Kasauli, Jan., 1921; on spl. duty for dysentery enquiry under the Indian research fund assocn., from July, 1913; reverted to mil. employ, April, 1915; acting dir., central research inst., Kasauli, Jan., 1916; asst. dir., ditto, May, 1917; dir., King inst. of prev. medicine, Madras, March, 1919; dir., Pasteur institute, Kasauli, Nov., 1925; offg. dir., central research institute, Kasauli, April, 1928; C.I.E., June, 1928; ret'd., June, 1931.

CUNNINGHAM, JOHN RICHARD, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Assam) (b. 18th Feb., 1876).—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1907, and officiated as prof'r., Presdy. Coll., Calcutta, and asst. dir. of public instn., Bengal; inspr. of schools, Bengal, Jan., 1912; dir. of public instn., Assam, April, 1912; C.I.E., June, 1921; offg. member, public services commn., June, 1928; ret'd., July, 1931.

CURGENVEN, SIR ARTHUR JOSEPH, B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th July, 1876).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 16th Nov., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; 1st asst. commr. and dist. mag., Coorg, Sept., 1905; sub. collr. and jt. mag., April, 1911; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1919; offg. judge, high ct., June, 1926, Oct., 1926, July, 1927, and again July, 1928; confd., July, 1929; ret'd., July, 1936; knighted, Feb., 1937.

CURRIE, ALGERNON JAMES, late Finance Dept., Govt. of India (b. 18th Dec., 1883).—Joined the service, 14th June, 1905; offg. comptroller, Assam, Sept., 1925; confd., Oct., 1926; offg. acct.-gen., Bengal, Mar., 1929; confd., Oct., 1929; acct. genl., Bombay, Nov., 1930; ditto, Central Provs., Dec., 1930; ditto, Central Provs., Nov., 1931; offg. dir. of army audit, Feb., 1932; acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1932; ditto, Central Provs., Oct., 1934; ret'd., Sept., 1936.

CURRIE, DONALD McDONALD, B.Sc. (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 11th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922.

CURRIE, MARK MAINWARING LEE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 14th Apr., 1882).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settl. offcr.; addl. dist. mag., Delhi, Feb., 1917; dep. commr., Nov., 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1924; legal remembr. and secty., legisl. dept., Nov., 1927; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, variously from Mar., 1930; actg. judge, ditto, variously from May, 1932; offg. secty. to govt., legal dept., Punjab, Oct., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1938.

CURRY, JOHN COURT, late Indian Police Service (b. 31st Dec., 1887).—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1907, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, Dec., 1920; dy. commr.; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1923; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

CURRY, JOHN ELLIOT PRINGLE, late Marine Dept., Bombay (b. 25th July, 1876).—Joined the dept., 1st April, 1906; dep. shipping master, May, 1907; shipping master, July, 1918; ret'd., March, 1932.

CURRY, THOMAS ARCHIBALD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Dec., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; supt. engr., Dec., 1927; offg. ch. engr., May, 1933; confd., Sept., 1935.

CURSETJI, MANEKJI JEHANGIR MANEKJI, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.K.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 18th Sept., 1890).—Joined the provl. service as asst. engr., June, 1915; asst. engr. (impl. service), Jan., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., June, 1924.

CURTIS, GERALD COLVILLE SEYMOUR, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service, (b. 4th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Westminster, Christchurch Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 8th October, 1929; arrived Dec., 1929, and served as asst. commr. in the Punjab; pol. asst. and commandant, Border Mily. police, May, 1932; colonisation officer and asst. pol. agent, Nasirabad, May, 1933; and again, Dec., 1934; pol. agent, Sibi, Nov., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Oct., 1936; asst. commr. and city mag., Peshawar, Aug., 1938; dep. commr., Mardan, Oct., 1938; dep. commr., Hazara, Mar., 1939.

CURTIS, SYDNEY JOSEPH, M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 1st Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., Mar., 1922.

DABHOLKAR, ANANT YASHWANT, M.C., M.B., B.Sc. (Bom.), D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Public health dept., Bombay (dir.) (b. 30th June, 1888).—First commn., 17th Mar., 1919; in milty. employ prior to 11th Dec., 1929; asst. dir. of public health, Jan., 1930; dir., 1933.

DABOO, ERACH RUTTANJI, M.C., M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (b. 9th May, 1888).—Civil surgeon, Assam, Feb., 1933.

DADABHOY, SIR MANEKJI BYRAMJI, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E.—Apptd. pres., council of state, 21st Dec., 1932; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1936.

DADACHANJI, RATANJI SORABJI, B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 27th Nov., 1875).—Apptd. judge, small cause courts, 25th Oct., 1919; master and regtr. in equity, Aprl., 1929; ret'd., Aprl., 1935.

DAFF, THOMAS ALLEN, late Indian Police Service (b. 5th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1910; asst. supt., April, 1912; supt., Aug., 1919; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; ret'd., Aprl., 1932.

DAFTARY, GAVRISHANKAR DULLABJI, B.E. (Civil), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 23rd Jan., 1889).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., March, 1913; asst. engr., March, 1914; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., March, 1923.

DAGLISH, JULIAN, Burma Police (Class I) (b. 19th Sept., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1937.

DAIN, Sir JOHN RUTHERFORD, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (member, fed. public service commn., Orissa) (b. 13th Jan., 1883).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1914; jt. mag. and ditto, Nov., 1915; mag. and collr., Oct., 1922; sec. to govt. of Bihar and Orissa, rev. dept., Oct., 1923; dep. commr., Oct., 1926; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; offg. commr., Apl., 1929; confd., May, 1932; offg. member, bd. of rev., Bihar and Orissa, Aug., 1933; rev. commr., Orissa, Dec., 1936; knighted, June, 1938; member, fed. public service commn., May, 1939.

DAINTITH, TERENCE JOSEPH, M.C., India Posts and Tels. (dep. dir. gen.) (b. 23rd Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1907; supt., July, 1908; offg. dep. p.m.g., Madras, Dec., 1933; dep. p.m.g., United Provs., Mar., 1936; p.m.g., Aug., 1938; senior dep. dir. gen., April, 1939.

DALAL, Sir ARDESHIR RUSTAMJI, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 24th Apl., 1884).—Educ. at Fort High Sch., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived 14th Nov., 1908, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and supt. of land records; asst. collr. and dep. sec. to govt., rev. dept. (temp.) Nov., 1921; dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Aug., 1925; offg. sec. to govt., fin. dept., March, 1926; member of legisl. council, 1926; offg. secty. to govt. of India, educn., health and lands dept., July-Nov., 1927; collr. and munpl. commr. for city of Bombay, Dec., 1928; retd., July, 1931; knighted, Jan., 1939.

DALAL, BHICAJI PHIROZESHAH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 1st Dec., 1913).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Coll., Coonoor, Bombay Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 17th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

DALAL, Sir DADIBA MERWANJEE, Kt., C.I.E.—Member of comtee. on Indian exchange and currency, 1919; chairman, govt. securities rehabilitation comtee., Bombay, 1921; apptd. member of council of sec. of state for India, 19th Nov., 1921; delegate for India at Genoa Economic Conference, and representative for India at The Hague Economic Conference, 1922; member of Indian retrenchment comtee., 1922; high commr. for India, 19th April, 1923, to 26th Sept., 1924; delegate for India at Impl. Economic Conference, 1923; C.I.E. Jan., 1918; Kt., Jan., 1924.

DALAL, RATANJI DINSHAH, C.I.E., M.L.A., L.M. & S. (Bom.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.).—Apptd. dep. sany. commr., Southern Regtn. Dist., Bombay, 14th June, 1913; asst. dir. of public health, Southern Regtn. District, Oct., 1921; reed. K.-I.-H. medal (2nd class), June, 1923; dir. vaccine institute, Belgaum, Oct., 1923; retd., Oct., 1925; member,

Indian legisl. assembly, since 1930; C.I.E., June, 1936; author of "A Manual of Vaccination."

DALIP SINGH, KANWAR, B.A., Judl. Dept., Punjab (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 2nd June, 1885).—Apptd. asst. legal remembrancer, Nov., 1922; govt. advocate, Nov., 1924; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, Oct., 1925; judge, ditto, Feb., 1930.

DALIP SINGH, M.A., Income-tax Dept., Punjab (asst. commr.) (b. 29th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, Aug., 1920; held non-gaz. appts., Aug., 1920-1921; collr. of Income-tax, Sept., 1921; on special duty, Punjab Secretariat, May, 1930; Income-tax offcr., June, 1930; cnfd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, June, 1936.

DALJIT SINGH, RAJA, Sir, K.B.E., C.S.I.—Member of the legislative council, Punjab, 1913-1915; member govr.-gen.'s legislative council, 1913-1915; C.S.I., June, 1914; member of council of Sec. of State for India, March, 1915, to June, 1917; K.B.E., June, 1919; chief minister, Kashmir, to 1921.

DALLEY, RICHARD POMEROY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 10th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1913, as asst. consvr.; on milty. duty from 3rd Aug., 1916, to 13th Nov., 1918; dep. consvr., Mar., 1919; offg. consvr., Mar., 1935.

D'ALMEIDA, WILLIAM, M.B.E. (asst. secty., home dept., Govt. of India) (b. 21st Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1909; supt., home dept., June, 1922; supt., Indian staty. commn., Mar., 1928; offg. asst. secty., home dept., Govt. of India, June, 1930; confd., Mar., 1933; offg. under-secty., home dept., Govt. of India, Sept., 1934; asst. secty., ditto, Sept., 1934.

DALY, CLIVE KIRKPATRICK, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 3rd Apl., 1888).—First commn., 18th Dec., 1907; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; pol. agent, Bahrain, Nov., 1925; H.M. consul, Seistan and Kain, Mar., 1929; offg. consul-genl., Khorasan, June, 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar agency, June, 1938; dep. resdt. Rajkot, Mar. to June, 1939; dep. resdt., W.I. States Agency, Mar., 1939; resdt. for Baroda and the Gujarat States, July, 1939.

DALYELL OF THE BINNS, Lieut.-Col. G., C.I.E., name changed from LOCH, P. G. (q.v.).

DALZIEL, WALTER WATSON, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 5th Oct., 1900).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and Corpus Christi, Oxford, joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived 7th Dec., 1924, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settlement offr., Nov., 1927; offg. judl. commr., Apl., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1932; confd., Apl., 1934; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, legisl. dept., Apl. to Oct., 1935.

DAM, SARAT CHANDRA, B.Sc., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 13th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service, 19th April,

1922; asst. exec. engr., April, 1923; offg. exec. engr., June, 1929; confd., Apl., 1932.

DAMLE, KASHINATH RACHUNATH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 3rd March, 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University, King's College, London, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.

DANDEKER, NARAYAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. of customs, Madras) (b. 24th June, 1908).—Educ. at School of Oriental Studies, Lond.; joined the service 13th Oct., 1931; arrived in India Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and coll. ; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Apl., 1933; asst. collr. of customs, Madras, Jan., 1937.

DANE, Sir LOUIS WILLIAM, G.C.I.E., C.S.I., F.R.G.S., late Indian C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 14th Nov., 1876, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; private sec. to lieut.-govr., Oct., 1879, to April, 1882; on special duty, Lahore Rakhs, 1884-85; offg. registrar, chief court, June, 1886; settlt. officer, Gurdaspur, Oct., 1887; asst. commr., 1st grade, May, 1889, dep. commr. and settlt. collr., Peshawar, Dec., 1892; chief sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1898; resident in Kashmir, Nov., 1901; sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., March, 1903; C.S.I., June, 1904; on spl. duty with the Kabul mission from Oct., 1904, to April, 1905; K.C.I.E., May, 1905; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, May, 1908, to May, 1913; G.C.I.E., Dec., 1911; ret'd., May, 1913.

DANE, Sir RICHARD MORRIS, K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Kingstown Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 20th Nov., 1874, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; served under the foreign dept. of the govt. of India, 1878-79; offg. sec. to finl. commr., Punjab, Nov., 1880, to Jan., 1881; offg. under sec. to govt., Punjab, March to Aug., 1882; boundary settlt. officer, Central India, 1883-84; asst. commr., Ajmir, Jan., 1885; served subsequently as offg. dep. commr., Punjab; settlt. officer, Gurdaspur, Nov., 1885; offg. commr. of excise, inspr.-gen. of registrn., &c., 1890-92; acted as commr., N. India salt rev., May, 1892; on special duty in connection with opium commn., Sept., 1893; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., March, 1895; C.I.E., Jan., 1896; commr., N. India salt rev., Dec., 1898; inspr.-gen., excise and salt, Sept. 1907; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1909; ret'd., Oct., 1909.

DANIELS, MARK, O.B.E., B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 22nd Feb., 1892).—Educ. at Central Foundation School, London, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge (wrangler; scholar, 1910-14); apptd. temp. junr. clerk, finl. dept., 16th March, 1915; resdt. clerk (temp.), Sept., 1915, to Dec., 1916; junr. clerk, store dept., 1st Nov., 1915; on mil. service from Dec., 1916, to Feb., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920, as asst. controller, store dept.; senr. clerk, Dec.,

1921; contr., April, 1922; O.B.E., June, 1934; officer for defence planning and co-ordn. Sept., 1939.

DAR, JEEWAN NATH, RAI SAHIB, B.A., late Indian Posts and Telegraphs (b. 31st Jan., 1884).—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1906; asst. supt., Apl., 1910; supt., Dec., 1912; dep. P.M.G., Oct., 1937; ret'd. Jan., 1939.

DANTYAGI, VISHWESWAR DAYAL, M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Jan., 1899). Joined the service, 13th July, 1922; dep. acct. genl., central revenues, Apl., 1936; dep. acct. genl., P. & T., Delhi, Apl., 1937; asst. dir. of audit, defence services, W.C. and Lahore Dist., Oct., 1938.

DAR, SHYAM SUNDAR LAL, M.A. (Cantab), Barr, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Dec., 1895).—Educ. at Punjab Univ., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1920; arrived, 16th Mar., 1921, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and jt. mag.; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1925; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. excise commr., July, 1931; inspr.-genl., regns., United Provs., Apl., 1932; magte. and collr., Nov., 1933.

DARBY, GEORGE SCOTT, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 16th April, 1885).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 17th Mar., 1904; asst. traffic supt., May, 1905; on milty. duty from 30th Mar., 1915, to 20th Feb., 1919; dist. traffic supt., Sept., 1921; offg. dep. traffic manager, Apl., 1928, and again April, 1929; confd., Apl., 1931; offg. traffic manager, June, 1932; confd., Aug., 1932; ret'd. March, 1939.

DARGAN, PATRICK ARTHUR, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgeon, Punjab) (b. 1st Aug., 1888).—First commn., 1st Nov., 1920; civil surgeon, Punjab, Apl., 1931.

DARLEY, Sir BERNARD D'OLIER, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 24th Aug., 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1903, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; suptg. engr., March, 1921; offg. chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., March, 1924; confd., Oct., 1925; Kt., Dec., 1928; on spl. duty in P.W.D., Nov., 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1934; apptd. commr. for Superior Services (India) Family Pensions Fund, Oct., 1937.

DARLING, GEORGE KENNETH, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th May, 1879).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 1st Dec., 1903, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1913; mag. and collr., Feb., 1921; offg. commr., Jan., 1927; confd., July, 1932; C.I.E., June, 1936; member, bd. of rev., United Provs., Oct., 1936; ret'd. Dec., 1938.

DARLING, HENRY JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (ch. electrical engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 24th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. elec. engr., 13th Oct., 1926; dist. elec. engr., E.I. Rly., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. loco. supt., E.B. Rly., Aug., 1928; confd., Feb., 1930; ch. electrical engr., N.W. Rly., Nov., 1931; offg. ditto, E.I. rly., Oct., 1935; confd., Nov., 1936.

DARLING, Sir MALCOLM LYALL, K.C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (finl. commr., Punjab) (b. 10th Dec., 1880).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; tutor and guardian to H.H. the Raja of Devas, Jan., 1907; under sec. to govt. of Punjab, Feb., 1911, to July, 1913; dep. commr., and jt. registr., co-operative socs., Punjab, Jan., 1920; commr. of income tax, Oct., 1921; regr. co-op. societies, Oct., 1927; commr., July, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. finl. commr. (dev.) and secty. to govt., dev. dept., Jan., 1936; finl. commr., Dec., 1936; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1939.

DARRINGTON, VICTOR GEORGE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 25th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 12th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1924; senr. instructor, Madras Forest Coll., Dec., 1925; pers. asst. to ch. consvr., Mar., 1931, to Mar., 1934.

DARSHAN SINGH, SARDAR, F.R.H.S., Barr., Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric. on 23rd Dec. 1913.

DART, Rev. JOHN LOVERING CAMPBELL, M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Eccles. Estab. (b. 30th May, 1882).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 30th Sept., 1911; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1914; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1924; offg. Archdeacon of Bombay, June, 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

DARVELL, HAROLD F., late Marine dept., Bengal (b. 8th Apl., 1881).—Apptd. shipping master, Calcutta, 2nd Dec., 1924; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

DARWIN, JOHN HENRY, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Oct., 1884).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 25th Nov., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Sept., 1917; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Sept., 1918, to Jan., 1919; dep. dir. of civil supplies, United Prov., Jan., 1919; acting mag. and collr., May, 1921; confd., Feb., 1925; dep. secty. to govt., genl. branch, Sept., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1932; reforms commr., Oct., 1936; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

DAS, AMARENDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur O.B.E., M.A., Bihar C.S. (secty. bd. of rev.) (b. 16th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 28th Nov., 1905; dep. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1907; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1928; confd., July, 1935; offg. secty., rev. bd., June, 1933; secty. bd. of rev., Dec., 1935; O.B.E., Jan., 1939.

DAS, AMRITA LAL, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sindh) (b. 1st Feb., 1906).—Joined the service as student, 9th May, 1928; asst. exec. engr., May, 1929; offg. exec. engr., May, 1933; confd., May, 1938.

DAS, ARJAN (see Arjan Das).

DAS, ATULANANDA, late Indian Forest Service (b. 10th Dec., 1879).—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1900; asst. consvr., Aug., 1921; dep. consvr., Sept., 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

DAS, BHAGWAN (see Bhagwan Das).

DAS, BHUBAN MOHAN, B.E., Rai Bahadur, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th July, 1878).—Joined the service, 20th Dec., 1902;

asst. engr., Dec., 1917; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; confd., Sept., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

DAS, CEDRIC PERCY MAYA, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of agric., United Provs.) (b. 8th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric. on 1st Sept., 1917; prof., agric. coll., Cawnpore, Nov., 1921; prinpl. ditto, Dec., 1925; dep. dir. of agric., United Provs., May, 1934; confd. in sel. grade, Apl., 1936.

DAS, DEBES CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 1st July, 1911).—Educ. at Mitra Institution, Calcutta, University of Calcutta and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the Service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; under secty., govt. of Assam, Oct., 1938.

DAS, DHARM (see Dharm Das).

DAS, GOVINDA CHARAN, M.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. June, 1892).—Joined the service as prob., 4th Mar., 1915; asst. acct.-genl., Bombay, July, 1918; ditto, United Provs., Feb., 1921; ditto, Punjab, April, 1924; ditto, Bengal, Dec., 1926; dep. ditto, June, 1928; ditto, C.P., Sept., 1928; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

DAS, GURCHARN (see Gurcharn Das).

DAS, JYOTIS RANJAN, Barr., late Burma Judicial Dept. (b. 9th Mar., 1874).—Apptd. offg. judge, high ct., Rangoon, from 12th March, 1925; judge, high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1925; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

DAS, MATHRA (see Mathra Das).

DAS, NABAGOPAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 20th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at Calcutta University, the School of Economics, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934.

DAS, NARSINGH (see Narsingh Das).

DAS, NIRANJAN (see Niranjan Das).

DAS, PRAFULLA RANJAN, Barr., late Judl. Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 28th Apl., 1881).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, 12th Feb., 1919; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

DAS, PRIA (see Prie Das).

DAS, PULIN BEHARI, M.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 20th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 28th Feb., 1908; asst. acct.-gen., May, 1910; dep. acct.-gen., Jan., 1917; offg. dep. auditor-genl. (inspn.), Feb., 1928; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

DAS, RADHA CHARAN, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Orissa Civil Service (b. 23rd Jan., 1883).—Sub-dep. collr., Feb., 1906; dep. collr., June, 1915; magte. and collr., May, 1937; ret'd., Jan., 1938.

DAS, RAM (see Ram Das).

DAS, SARADA PRASANNA, M.A., late Indian Educ. Service (Bengal) (b. July, 1876).—Held appts. in the provl. service from 15th July, 1888; apptd. profr., Presdy. Coll., Calcutta, 23rd Feb., 1921; ret'd., July, 1931.

DAS, SUDHANSHU KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (regr., high ct., Patna) (b. 3rd Sept., 1898).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Sch. of Oriental

Studies, London Univ.; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 21st Dec., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. magte. and collr., Mar., 1929; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; registrar, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1939.

DAS, SUNDER, K. (see Sunder Das, K.).

DAS, SURENDRA NATH, B.A., Rai Bahadur, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 18th Apl., 1877).—Joined the service, 13th April, 1898; probysupt., Aug., 1901; supt., Feb., 1903; offg. dep. postmaster-genl., May, 1921; confd., Jan., 1923; offg. dep. dir.-genl., Mar., 1930; retd., Feb., 1931.

DAS GUPTA, KULADA CHARAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 3rd Jan., 1900).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and sub-divl. officer; jt. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1929, and again, Apl., 1933; addl. ditto, Oct., 1932; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1934.

DAS GUPTA, PRITHWISH CHANDRA, M.A., A.C.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 3rd Aug., 1897).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1925; offg. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., May, 1935; exr., outside audit dept., Bombay, Oct., 1935; again, Apl., 1937; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Mar., 1937; examiner, outside audit dept., Bombay, April, 1937; dep. govt. examiner of accts., B.B. & C.I. Rly., Oct., 1938.

DAS GUPTA, SACHINDRA CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 2nd Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1911, as profir., Dacca Coll.; asst. comptr., India treasuries, March, 1916; asst. acct.-gen., posts and tels., June, 1916; dep. acct.-gen., ditto, Central Provs., May, 1926; dep. dir. of audit, May, 1927; offg. ch. pay and accts. officer, United Provs., April, 1928; offg. dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, May, 1930; offg. dep. ch. auditor, N.W. Rly., Mar., 1931; offg. ch. accts. officer, E.B. Rly., Sept., 1932; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, Burma rlys., April, 1934; offg. acct. genl., Burma, June, 1935; offg. ch. auditor, N.W. rly., Mar., 1936; dep. ch. accts. officer, ditto, June, 1936; offg. acct.-genl., Bengal, Oct., 1936; chief accts. offcr., E.I. Rly., June, 1938.

DAS GUPTA, SURENDRA NATH, M.A., Ph.D., (Cantab.), Indian Educl. Service (prinpl. Sanskrit coll.) (b. Oct., 1887).—Apptd. profir., Presdy. Coll., Calcutta, 22nd March, 1924; prinpl. Sanskrit coll., Apr., 1931.

DASH, ARTHUR JULES, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal) (b. 24th Apl., 1887).—Educ. at Worcester Cathedral King's Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr. and mag.; transfld. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1916; I.A.R.O. from Aug., 1915, to Dec., 1918; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1921; dist. judge (tempy.), Jan., 1923; mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; secty. to govt., dept. of educn., Dec., 1928; offg. commr., Apl., 1932; confd., Mar., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

DASS, KANSHAL KUMAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 25th May, 1915).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

DASTUR, SIR HORMAZDIAR PHIROZ, Kt., B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay, Judicial Dept. (b. 20th Mar., 1877).—Joined the dept., 4th Aug., 1923; 5th presdy. mag., Aug., 1923; 4th ditto, Feb., 1924; 3rd ditto, Mar., 1925; chief presdy. magte., Apl., 1929; Kt. June, 1932; retd., Apl., 1938.

DASTUR, JEHANGIR FARDUNJI, M.Sc., D.I.C., Indian Agric. Service (mycologist, Central Provs.) (b. 12th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, 14th May, 1910; mycologist, Dec., 1923.

DATT, DEV (see Dev Datt).

DATT, GURU, SONDHI (see Guru Datt, S.).

DATTA (see also Dutt).

DATTA, ABINASH CHANDRA, Income tax dept., Assam (asst. commr.) (b. 30th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1915; income tax officer, Jan., 1927; asst. commr., June, 1934.

DATTA, AMBICA CHARAN, Rai Bahadur, late Bengal Civil Service (b. 14th July, 1879).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1901; dep. magte., Nov., 1902; magte. and collr., Nov., 1928; retd., July, 1934.

DATTA, DINES CHANDRA, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 1st July, 1904).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 15th May, 1928; confd., July, 1930; asst. to pol. officer, Sadiya frontier tract, Apl., 1932; offg. supt., Jan., 1936.

DATTA, GURU SADAY (see Dutt, G. S.).

DATTA, NARENDRA NATH, Babu, B.A., Bihar C.S. (dep. mag. and dep. collr.) (b. 16th April, 1883).—Joined the service, 6th Aug., 1904; dep. collr., Oct., 1906; offg. dep. commr., May, 1933.

DATTA, PARES CHANDRA, M.B., Ch.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Railways (dist. medl. officer) (b. 31st July, 1892).—Apptd. dist. medl. officer on probn., 1st May, 1925; confd., May, 1928.

DATTA, PRAPHULLA CHANDRA, Babu, B.L., late Bengal Judl. Dept.—Joined the service, 17th May, 1904; judge, Calcutta small cause ct., Dec., 1926; retd., June, 1930.

DATTA, PROMODE CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, C.I.E.—Apptd. member, govr's. exec. council, Assam, 31st Mar., 1934; vacated office, Mar., 1937; advocate-general, Assam, Aug., 1939.

DATTA, RABINDRA NATH, B.A. (Oxon.), M.Sc. (All.), Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Central Provs., 14th Oct., 1927; dep. consvr., Nov., 1931.

DATTA, SISIR KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 27th Nov., 1911).—Educ. at Govt. High School Silchar, Assam; Calcutta Univ., and London School of Economics; joined the service, 14th Jan., 1937; arrived in India, 19th Feb., 1937, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

DATTA, SUDHINDRA MOHAN, B.A., Indian Police (b. 18th Mar., 1910).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., 15th Feb., 1934; confd., Feb., 1936.

DATTATRAYA VAMAN REGE (see Rege, D. V.).

DAUBENY, RONALD GILES, Indian Police, Indian Political Service, (b. 19th July, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 21st Nov., 1926; offg. A.D.C. to Govt., May, 1929; pte. secy. to ditto, Aprl., 1932; A.D.C. to the Viceroy, Oct., 1933; under secy. to A.G.G., Eastern States, Aprl., 1936; asst. magte. and collr., Moradabad, Nov., 1936; under-secty. to resdt. for Eastern States, Nov., 1937; asst. pol. agent, Orissa States, Nov., 1938; confd. in the Indian Political Service, Feb., 1939.

DAUKES, SIR CLENDON TUBERVILLE, Kt., C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd. (b. 24th Dec., 1879).—First commn. dated 12th Aug., 1899; in mil. emp. to Dec., 1903; sup. asst. commr., Dec., 1903; pol. asst., Nov., 1904; pol. agent, Loralai, Dec., 1904; H.B.M.'s vice-consul for Seistan and Kain, March, 1906; H.B.M.'s consul, Tujbat-i-Haidari, Feb., 1907; pol. asst., Meshed, Oct., 1907; asst. to H.B.M.'s consul for Seistan and Kain, Nov., 1907; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent in Kalat, March, 1911; asst. pol. agent, Chilas, Oct., 1911; pol. agent, Gilgit, Nov., 1911; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, May, 1916; temply. reverted to mil. duty, July, 1918; on spl. duty under chief commr., Baluchistan, June, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1920; pol. agent, Loralai, Aprl., 1921; offg. British envoy at court of Nepal, Nov., 1929; confd., June, 1931; envoy extraordinary and minister plenipotentiary at Katmandu, July, 1934; ret'd., Aug., 1935; knighted, Jan., 1935.

DAULATRAM, DAYALMAL (see Dayalmal, D.).

DAVE, KARUNASHANKAR NANDLAL, B.A., LL.B., Income Tax Dept., Central Provs. (asst. commr.) (b. Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1914; income tax officer, May, 1925; offg. asst. commr., Jan., 1926; confd., Feb., 1932.

DAVID, ALAN MICHAEL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 18th Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 11th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

DAVIDSON, DAVID ALEXANDER GIBB, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 15th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 19th May, 1922; asst. consvr., 1st Nov., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

DAVIDSON, JOHN, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 31st Aug., 1878).—Joined the finance dept., govt. of India, 22nd Feb., 1902; asst. acct.-gen., Burma, Aprl., 1905; ditto, Bengal, March, 1907; ditto, and examr. of local funds accts., United Provs., May, 1908; ditto, ditto, Punjab, Oct., 1909; dep. compt., Assam, May, 1912; on mil. service, March, 1916, to Feb., 1919; offg. dep. compt.-gen., Feb., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; auditor, Govt. of India sanctions (mily.), Jan., 1922; services placed at disposal of govt. of Punjab from Sept., 1923; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1926; ditto, Burma, Aprl., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

DAVIDSON, SIR LIONEL, K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of

1886; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting asst. and under sec.; head asst., Feb., 1895; spl. asst. collr. and mag., Oct., 1895; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1896; sub-collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1899; sec. to land rev. commrs. Jan., 1900; commr. and dist. judge, Coorg, Nov., 1902; dist. and sess. judge, Madras, Oct., 1902; collr. and mag., March, 1906; offg. sec. to govt., rev. dept., March, 1912; C.S.I., Jan., 1913; sec. to govt., local-munpl. &c., depts., July, 1913; ditto, rev. dept., Oct., 1914; acting chief sec. to govt., Madras, Aprl., 1916; an addl. member, govr., genl.'s legisl. council; tempy. member of exec. council, Madras, Nov., 1918; confirmed, Feb., 1919; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1921; ret'd., Aug., 1924.

DAVIES, ANDREW EVAN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bombay) (b. 24th Mar., 1914).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Bombay, Nov., 1933; confd., Aprl., 1936.

DAVIES, ARTHUR CECIL, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th May, 1889).—Educ. at St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 13th Dec., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl. and munpl. depts., Oct., 1917; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aprl., 1926; confd., Sept., 1929; offg. secty. to govt., judl., dept., and supt. and remembr. legal affairs, Jan., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; ret'd., July, 1939.

DAVIES, MISS EDITH ELIZABETH, late Indian Educl. Service (b. 27th Feb., 1875).—Apptd. principal, Dow Hill girls' school, Darjeeling, 1st Aprl., 1922; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

DAVIES, EDWARD LLOYD, Office of High Commr. for India (executive officer, store dept.) (b. 18th Dec., 1883).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 18th Sept., 1906; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Aprl., 1922.

DAVIES, HAROLD ESCOTT, B.A., Burma Office (asst. princpl.) (b. 5th Nov., 1915).—Educ. at Aldenham School, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. princpl., 11th Oct., 1937; resident clerk, May, 1939.

DAVIES, HENRY MERSON, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 2nd June, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. dist. loco. supt., 2nd Nov., 1907; in milty. employ from 2nd July, 1915; mech. engr., July, 1925; pers. asst. to dep. ch. mech. engr., Nov., 1925; fuel economy officer, Nov., 1926; divl. rolling stock officer, June, 1930; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Nov., 1929; confd., Nov., 1933; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., power, E.I. Rly., Nov., 1933.

DAVIES, JOHN HUGH RATHBONE, Indian Police (b. 16th June, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Dec., 1937.

DAVIES, LAWRENCE STANLEY RICHARD GILBERT, late Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 19th June, 1894).—Held non-gaz. and sub-gaz. appts., May, 1912, to Nov., 1930; apptd. officr. cl. II, Nov., 1930; ret'd. June, 1939.

DAVIES, P. W. WILTON (see Wilton Davies).

DAVIES, POWYS, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 27th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.;

exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., July, 1917; confd., May, 1928; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

DAVIES, ROBERT WILLIAM HOME, B.A., Indian C.S. (judl. commr. for W.I. and for Ajmer-Merwara) (b. 10th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Jan., 1922; arrived, 18th March, 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr. and supt. of stamps, May, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1925; asst. magte. and collr., April, 1928; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1932; confd., Oct., 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Oct., 1934; ditto, Kathiawar, Sept., 1936; offg. judl. commr. for W.I., and for Ajmer-Merwara, June, 1938, to Oct., 1938; confd. May, 1939.

DAVIES, ROLAND GWYNNE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sindh) (b. 11th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at the Grammar School, Bradford, and Queen's College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

DAVIES, STUART THOMAS, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), major, I.M.S. Medical Dept., Madras (b. 10th Sept., 1901).—First commn. dated 15th Aug., 1928; in milty. employ until Dec., 1936; dist. med. offr., Dec., 1936.

DAVIES, VINCENT ELLIS, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 3rd June, 1903).—Educ. at St. John's School, Leatherhead, and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1926; arrived, 20th Dec., 1926, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1933.

DAVIES, WILLIAM FREDERICK MANT, Indian Police (b. 11th July, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 16th Nov., 1927; A.D.C. to govr., Central Provs., Mar. 1935.)

DAVIES-LEIGH (see under Leigh).

DAVIS, ARNOLD PERCIVAL, late Indian Forest Service (b. 18th July, 1887).—Arrived, 18th Dec., 1909, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; on mil. service, May, 1918, to June, 1919; offg. consvr., June, 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

DAVIS, CHARLES KENNETH late Burma Commn. (b. 2nd Aug., 1877).—Apptd. dep. commr. from 15th Dec., 1923, and remain seconded for service under Rangoon dev. trust; land officer, dev. trust, March, 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

DAVIS, DAVID, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 11th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1925; instructor, forest coll., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1930, to May, 1933.

DAVIS, GODFREY, Indian C.S. (judl. commr. Sindh) (b. 23rd Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr., asst. rembr. of legal affairs, Jan., 1920; asst. judge and addnl. sess. judge, Dec., 1922; secy. to cttee. on Bombay Stock Exchange, Sept., 1923, to Jan., 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1924; ditto, and agent for sardars in the Deccan, Aug., 1925; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1926; confd., Jan., 1929; secty. to govt.,

legal dept., Bombay, and remembr., legal affairs, Jan., 1932; member, legisl. council, Feb., 1932; judl. commr., Sindh., Dec., 1935.

DAVIS, HAROLD PERCY WHITTON, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 7th Dec., 1893).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1923; instr., forest research coll., June, 1926, to Apr., 1929; offg. consvr., June, 1935.

DAVIS, Percival William, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 19th July, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926; offg. princpl., Madras forest coll., June, 1933.

DAVIS, PERCY, Stationery and Printing Dept., Punjab (supt.) (b. 22nd Apr., 1890).—Joined the service, 3rd July, 1924, as works manager, central jail press, on probn.; confd., July, 1925; supt., Govt. printing, May, 1935.

DAVIS, REGINALD TREBY, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Veterinary Service (b. 10th June, 1883).—Joined the service, 31st Mar., 1922; vice-prin., B.V. coll., Nov., 1924; offg. dir., June, 1927; offg. princpl., B.V. coll., Apr., 1929; princpl. vety. coll., Patna, Apr., 1930; offg. dir., vety. services, Bihar and Orissa, Apr., 1934; ret'd., June, 1938.

DAVIS, WILLIAM JOHN, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1st Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1907, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; on mil. duty from Aug., 1916, to Jan., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Sept., 1933; confd., Feb., 1936; ret'd. Dec., 1939.

DAVY, ANTHONY EDWARD GAINS, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 10th Dec., 1908).—First Commn., 30th Aug., 1928; Pers. Asst. to A.G.G., Punjab States, October, 1935; Under Sec. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Feb., 1936; under trng. in Punjab, Aug. 1936; City Magistrate, Peshawar, Mar., 1938; Asst. Pol. Agent, Zhob, Apr., 1939; confd. in Indian Political Service, May 1939; Asst. Pol. Agent, Orissa States, Nov., 1939.

DAVY, HORACE FREDERICK, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Stores dept. (contr. of inspn.) (b. 19th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. laby. officer, 2nd Dec., 1913; dep. supt. of local manufactures, Dec., 1918; contr. of inspn. (temp.), May, 1923; confd., Jan., 1925; offg. dir. of inspn., June, 1928.

DAWKINS, CLINTON GEORGE EVELYN, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 16th June, 1882).—Arrived 19th Dec., 1908, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Mar., 1914; offg. consvr., July, 1925; confd., June, 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

DAWSON, MISS EUNICE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 23rd May, 1881).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1914; offg. inspectress of schools, Oct., 1918; confd. Oct., 1919; princpl., girls' high school, Bankipur Oct., 1921; ditto, Patna, Oct., 1924; dep. directress of public instrn., July, 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

DAWSON, GEOFFREY WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 20th Oct., 1899; 3rd asst. secty. G.I.P. Rly., March, 1914; publicity supt., E.I. Rly., April, 1920; publicity officer, ditto, April, 1927; ditto, E.B. Rly., Feb., 1928; asst. ch. publicity officer, Feb., 1928; on depn. to publicity bureau, Indian rlwys., London, Jan., 1931.

DAWSON, JAMES ALEXANDER, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th May, 1880).—Educ. at Gordon's Coll. and the Univ., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1911; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; dep. commr., Nov., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; commr. of excise, Mar., 1932; ch. secty. to govt., Assam, Nov., 1933; offc. as member rev. tribunal, May, 1937, to Jan., 1938; C.S.I., Jan., 1939; ret'd., Nov., 1939.

DAWSON, LIONEL EDWARD, B.A., M.A.I. Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1910; in mil. employ from 14th Feb., 1918, to 7th Feb., 1920; exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1918; pers. asst. to ch. engr., and under-sec. to govt., P.W. dept. (I.B.), April, 1927; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1931; confd., July, 1936.

DAWSON, STEPHEN, B.A., B.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th July, 1879).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1903; arrived Feb., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., Sept., 1923; confd., June, 1926; chief public works officer, F.S.S., March, 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

DAWSON, SIR VERNON, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S., India Office (asst. secty., public and judi. dept., seconded for spl. duty in reforms dept.) (b. 22nd Feb., 1881).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1905, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., rev. and appt. dept., Aug., 1909; under sec. to govt. of India, Home Dept., July, 1910; transf. to Bengal, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1913; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1916; on spl. duty, home dept., govt. of India, Sept. to Dec., 1916; on spl. duty, Calcutta high ct. Jan. to Mar., 1917; on spl. duty, legisl. dept., govt. of India, April to Aug., 1917; temply. empd. in rev. and stats. dept., India Office, from July, 1918, and in judl. and public dept. from Aug., 1918; sec., India Office Reforms Committee, Aug., 1918, to Dec., 1919; acting asst. sec. in judl. and public dept. from Dec., 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; resigned from Indian C.S., Jan., 1921; senr. clerk, India Office, March, 1921; principal, April, 1921; asst. secty., public and judl. dept., Apl., 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1934.

DAWSON, WILLIAM MAURICE GRANT, M.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 11th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; profir. civil engrg. Thomason Coll. Roorkee, Oct., 1935 to Oct., 1936.

DAY, BINGLEY WALDEMAR, Indian C.S. (under secty., public dept., Madras) (b. 2nd Feb., 1908).—Educ. at Stowe and Corpus Christi coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th October, 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Nov., 1932; under secty. public dept., Oct., 1937.

DAY, Rev. EDWARD RIDLEY, M.A., late Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 21st Aug., 1882).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 16th Aug., 1920; junr. chaplain, Aug., 1922; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1932; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

DAY, FRANCIS EDWARD India Audit Office (exec. officer) (b. 22nd July, 1915).—Apptd. exec. officer, 23rd Jan., 1936.

DAY, JOHN THOMAS, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transport supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 18th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 27th May, 1910; asst. traffic supt., Mar., 1919; dist. ditto, Jan., 1922; asst. traffic manager, G.I.P. rly., Aug., 1923; goods supt., G.I.P. rly., May, 1926; divl. transport supt., Dec., 1933.

DAYA KRISHNA, KHANNA, RAI SAHIB LALA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 19th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service, 24th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

DAYAL, BISHAMBHAR (see Bishambhar Dayal).

DAYAL DEVI (see Devi Dayal).

DAYAL, HARISHWAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 15th July, 1915).—Educ. at Govt. Intermediate Coll., Allahabad, and Allahabad Univ.; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

DAYAL, ISHWAR (see Ishwar, D.).

DAYAL, MADHU SUDAN (see Madhu Sudan, D.).

DAYAL, RAGHUBAR, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 26th Oct., 1900).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1925; arrived, 17th Nov., 1925, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag.; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Jan., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1933; confd., Sept., 1936.

DAYAL, RAJESHWAR (see Rajeshwar Dayal).

DAYAL, SHAMBHU (see Shambhu Dayal).

DAYAL, SHANKAR (see Shankar Dayal).

DAYARAM, THAWARDAS, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service, Judicial Branch (b. 8th Jan., 1875).—Joined the service 1st Dec., 1903; sub-judge, Mar., 1904; asst. judge, Nov., 1928; offg. judge and sess. judge, May, 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1930.

D'CUHNA, MARCEL DOMINIC, O.B.E., B.A., late Imp. Customs Service. (b. 2nd June, 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st May, 1903; offg. asst. collr., Bombay, Mar., 1917; confd., Apl., 1920; ditto, Madras, Oct., 1921; ditto, Bombay, Feb., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1936; ret'd., June, 1936.

DE, ANATH BANDHU, B.A., Rai Bahadur, late Bengal Civil Service (b. 30th Dec., 1876).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., May, 1897; offg. dep. magte. and dep. collr., Oct., 1897; confd., April, 1900; offg. magte. and collr., March, 1927; confd., Aug., 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

DE, BIRENDRA NATH, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Mar., 1882).—Educ. at Collegiate School, Midnapur, Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1906, and served in the United Provs. as asst.

commr. and settlt. offr.; excise commr., April, 1919; dep. commr., Dec., 1919; excise commr. and supt. of stamps, April, 1925; dep. commr. and rev. sec. to govt., Central Provs., Jan., 1926; finl. secty. to ditto, March, 1927; offg. commr., Nov., 1928; confd., Nov., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

DE, GOSHTO BIHARI, B.A., B.L., late Central Provs. C.S. (Judl.) (b. Mar., 1881).—Joined the service as munsif, 1st Sept., 1905; sub-judge, Nov., 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1931; confd., Jan., 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

DE, INDRA NATH, Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 20th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 24th Apl., 1925; offg. supt., Mar., 1933; confd., Jan., 1938.

DE, INDU BHUSAN, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 18th Jan., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, Dec., 1926; exec. engr., Dec., 1935.

DE, JIBAN KRISHNA, B.Sc., B.E. (Cal.), A.M.I.E., Indian P. and T. (div. engr.) (b. 31st Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. div. engr., 19th June, 1922; div. engr., Aug., 1930.

DE, JNANANKUR, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 21st Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and University Coll., London; apptd. to I.C.S. by nomination; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1917, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dy. collr., Jan., 1922, and sub. divl. officer, May, 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1927; confd., Dec., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1939.

DE, KALI CHURUN, B.Sc., Indian State Ryrs. (dist. contr. of stores, E.I. Rly.) (b. 17th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 8th Jan., 1920; asst. dir. (technical) rly. bd., Apl., 1924; dist. contr. of stores (temp.), July, 1927; offg. dep. contr. of stores, variously from Mar., 1929.

DE, MAKHANLAL, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 4th Dec., 1882).—Joined the dept., 1st July, 1907; asst. profr., Nagpur, July, 1909; profr., Oct., 1916; offg. princpl., coll. of science, Nagpur, July, 1934; confd., Apl., 1937; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

DE, PROBODH CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sessions judge, Bengal) (b. 12th Jan., 1884).—Educ. at Central Coll. Sch., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 11th Dec., 1908, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1915; dist. and sessions judge, Nov., 1922.

DE, RAJENDRA NATH, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. July, 1891).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1914; proby. extra asst. consvr., July, 1916; confd., July, 1917; apptd. to I.F.S. as dep. consvr., 3rd Nov., 1928.

DEAN, ARTHUR WILLIAM HENRY, B.Sc., M.O., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., central P.W.D.) (b. 7th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 3rd Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., April, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; transfid. to Indore, Jan., 1933; tempy. suptg. engr., aviation circle, Feb., 1935; offg. suptg. engr., construction circle, Apl., 1936.

DEAN, JOHN MARTIN, Indian Police (b. 9th July, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1936.

DEAN, VINCENT LEE, V.D., M.Inst.T., Indian State Ryrs. (divl. transp officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 21st Mar., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 8th Nov., 1914; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; offg. dist. traffic supt., Apl., 1925; divl. transp. officer, Apl., 1928.

DEANE, DONALD VICTOR, Captain, R.E. (b. 19th Oct., 1902).—Mil. employ up to 21st Dec., 1932; dep. mint master on probn., 30th Dec., 1932; confd., Oct., 1934; offg. mint master variously from Feb., 1934.

DEANE, LANCELOT VICTOR, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 14th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 22nd Nov., 1925; asst. commr., Port Blair, Jan., 1930.

DEANE, MAHOMED KHAIRUD, B.Sc., Factory Dept., Bombay (inspr.) (b. 16th Apr., 1891).—Apptd. inspr. of factories, Bombay, 20th Nov., 1923; offg. ch. inspr., Feb., 1931.

DEANE, NEVILLE BERNARD, O.B.E., Indian Finance Dept. (auditor-genl., Burma) (b. 12th June, 1885).—Joined the service, 3rd Jan., 1908; asst. acct. genl., June, 1909; dep. ditto, Jan., 1926; offg. acct. genl., Burma, Apl., 1930; O.B.E., Dec., 1930; dep. auditor-genl. (inspn.), Nov., 1930; acct. genl., Bombay, Nov., 1931; ditto Central Revs., Oct., 1934; ditto, Burma, Oct., 1936; auditor-genl., Burma, Apl., 1937 (on foreign service).

DEANS, HARRY SCOTT, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 29th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 15th Dec., 1922; on deputation to the Kashmir State from 4th March, 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

DEAS, LEONARD JOSEPH MONTAGUE, M.B., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 12th July, 1873).—First commn., 28th June, 1900; agency surg., Eastern Rajputana States, Jan., 1912; ditto, Bhopawor, April, 1912; resdy. surg. Jaipur, Oct., 1919; civil surg., Ajmer, April, 1920; agency surg., Kotah, Oct., 1921; resdy. surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, March, 1923; reverted to milty, employ, Jan., 1928; ret'd., July, 1930.

DEASE, RICHARD EDMUND, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 19th Feb., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 14th Oct., 1923; offg. supt., Sept., 1931; offg. addl. ditto, Mar., 1933; supt., Apl., 1938.

DEB, BIRENDRANATH, Office of the High Commr. for India (asst. to Indian trade publicity officer) (b. 13th Aug., 1881).—Apptd. to Office of H.C. for I., April, 1921; clerical officer, Feb., 1924; exec. officer, Feb., 1927; asst. to Indian trade publicity officer, Nov., 1929.

DEB, MUNINDRA NATH, B.A., B.E. late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 16th Feb., 1878).—Joined the service, 20th Jan., 1904; asst. engr., Jan., 1905; exec. engr., Jan., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1925; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

DEBENDRA NATH SEN GUPTA (see Sen Gupta, D. N.).

DEBIDAT PANDYA, D.P.H., L.R.C.P. & S., L.F.P. and S., Rai Bahadur (late Dept. of Public Health, United Provs. (b. 8th Aug., 1876).—Joined the service as addl. dep. san. commr., July, 1912; asst. dir. of public health, Nov., 1921; retd., Feb., 1933.

DEBI DATTA MAI, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as temp., engr. on 27th May, 1910; asst. engr., U.P. Engrg. service, March, 1922; I.S.E., Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; prof. civil engrg. coll., Feb., 1933; P.A. to ch. engr., and undersecy., p.w.d., I.B., Nov., 1935; again 1936; again Jan., 1937; retd. June, 1939.

DEBI LAL SAH, B.A., M.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 7th Mar., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. working plan officer on 26th Dec., 1925; asst. consvr., July, 1926; dep. consvr., Feb., 1933.

DE CHAZEL, JOHN ROBERT, Indian Police (b. 19th Feb., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 26th Nov., 1936.

DE GALE, HUGH OTWAY, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Punjab) (b. 9th Jan., 1891).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1911; asst. supt., May, 1913; on mil. duty from 15th Nov., 1917 to 28th Feb., 1919; King's police medal, Jan., 1921; supt., Jan., 1924; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1925; princl. police training school, Phillour, April, 1927; offg. dep. inspr. genl., Feb., 1933; ditto, (provl. subs.), June, 1935; offg. inspr. genl., Feb., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1938.

DEHEJIA, VENILAL TRIBHOVANDAS, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. coll., Sind) (b. 23rd July, 1908).—Educ. at Bombay University, Rl. College of Science, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. coll. and supt., salt and excise.

DEHLAVI, SAMIULLA KHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Sept., 1913).—Educ. at Anglo-Urdu High Scho., Poona, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 1st Oct., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DE KRETSEK, CLAUDE BERTRAM, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma), (b. 23rd Oct., 1892).—Educ. at University Coll., London, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, 17th Jan., 1918, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, offg. asst. collr. of customs, Jan., 1920, to July, 1921; services re-placed at disposal of govt. of Burma, July, 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1923; confd., Sept., 1927.

DE LA FOSSE, Sir CLAUDE FRASER, Kt., O.I.E., M.A., late Educ. Dept., United Provs.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. prof. of English literature and logic, Queen's Coll., Benares, 18th Feb., 1896; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1897; asst. dir. of public instn., Nov., 1902; dir., Nov., 1906; member of lieut.-govr.'s legisl. council; C.I.E., June, 1918; Vice-Chancellor of Allahabad University, April, 1922; knighted, June, 1922; retd., 1923.

DELAHAY, Ven. WILLIAM, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 25th Jan., 1884).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 13th Dec., 1919; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1921; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1929; archdeacon of Rangoon, Mar., 1937; retd. Jan., 1939.

DE LA HEY, Miss DOROTHY CISLEY, late Indian Educ. Service (b. 6th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as prinpl., coll. for women, Madras, 1st July, 1914; prinpl. Queen Mary's coll., Madras, May, 1923; retd., Feb., 1936; K.I.H. medal, 1st class, May, 1937.

DE LA NOUGEREDÉ, FREDERICK ADAIR, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 15th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 16th July, 1915; asst. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., July, 1918; offg. dist. contr. of stores variously from Jan., 1919; confd., Mar., 1931; offg. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., Feb., 1935.

DELL, PERCY BERTRAM, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. offic., store dept.) (b. 15th Nov., 1888).—Second class clerk, bd. of trade, Sept., 1908, to Sept., 1909; ditto, bd. of edn., Sept., 1909, to June, 1911; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 9th June, 1911, and attd. to registry and record dept.; transf. to store dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to July, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1926; higher exec. offic., May, 1938; M.B.E., June, 1938.

DE MONTE, FRANK THOMAS, C.I.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 1st Dec., 1879).—Trained at R.I.E. coll.; joined the telegraph service, 1st Oct., 1899 as asst. supt.; supt., Nov., 1910; dep. postmr.-gen. (traffic), April, 1914; asst. dir.-gen., telegraph traffic, April, 1919; offg. dep. dir.-gen.; postmr.-gen. and dir., telegraph traffic, Burma, June, 1923; dep. dir.-gen., telegraph traffic, March, 1925; offg. ch. engr., tels., April, 1928; P.M.G., Bengal and Assam, Dec., 1928; offg. ch. engr., posts and tels., April, 1930; confd., Oct., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; retd., Dec., 1934.

DE MONTMORENCY, Sir GEOFFREY FITZHERVEY, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., K.C.V.O., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Aug., 1876).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 6th Dec., 1900, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. colonization offr., Chenab Canal Colony, Oct., 1903; special duty for delimiting the River Ravi, Nov., 1903, to July, 1904; junr. sec. to finl. commr., April, 1911; on special duty in connection with transfer of capital to Delhi, Feb., 1912; transf. to Delhi as personal asst. to chief commr., Oct., 1912; C.I.E., June, 1915; acting dep. commr., Delhi, April, 1917; offg. chief commr., Delhi, Oct., 1917; dep. commr., Punjab, April, 1918; C.B.E., Dec., 1919; addl. dep. sec. to govt. of India, Foreign and Pol. Dept., Feb., 1920; pte. sec. to the Viceroy, March, 1922; K.C.I.E., July, 1926; member of govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, July, 1926; govr. of Punjab, Aug., 1928, to Apl., 1933; K.C.S.I., Oct., 1928; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1933; retd., Aug., 1935.

DENCH, WILLIAM GEORGE, B.A., B.A.I., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 1st Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921;

under-secty. to govt., irrigation branch, Punjab, April, 1931; under-secty. (North), Punjab irrigation sectt., Jan., 1933; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1933, and again, Feb., 1936.

DENNEHY, HAROLD GEORGE, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., educ. and local self govt. depts., Assam) (b. 18th Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Clifton and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 6th Dec., 1914, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug. 1915, to Mar., 1919; under sec. to govt. of Assam, April, 1923; offg. chief sec., ditto, Apl., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1925; confd., June, 1933; offg. secty. to govt. in transferred depts., Assam, June, 1929; confd., June, 1933; offg. ch. secty., Assam, Sept., 1936; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; secty., educ. and local self govt. depts., Feb., 1938.

DENNING, Sir HOWARD, Kt., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S.
