FYSON, PHILIP FURLEY, M.A., F.L.S., late Indian Educ. Service (b. 17th April, 1877).—Apptd. prof. of biology, presdy. coll., Madras, 11th July, 1904; prof. of botany, ditto, ditto, Dec., 1914; dist. educ. officer, Feb., 1923; offg. princpl., presdy. coll., July, 1925; ret'd., Apl., 1932.

GABRIEL, CECIL HAMILTON, C.V.O., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 16th Feb., 1879).—First commn. dated 7th Dec., 1901; was in mil. employ until 18th Sept., 1904, when he was employed on special duty under govt. of India, for. dept.; spl. asst. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, March, 1905; pol. asst., and supy. asst. commr., Jan., 1906; asst. commr., Delhi, March, 1906; H.B.M.'s consul, and asst. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, July, 1906; second asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1908; asst. pol. agent, Chilas, Oct., 1909; first asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Jan., 1913; ditto to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Jan., 1919; dist. judge, Kohat, Nov., 1920; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1921; confd., Aug., 1921; C.V.O., Feb., 1922; pol. agent, Bhopal, April, 1925; resident, Mewar, Feb., 1928; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1928; resident, Jaipur, May-Sept., 1928; ditto, Rajputana states, May, 1928; offg. resdt., Gwalior, Apl., 1929; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

GABRIEL, SIR EDMUND VIVIAN, C.S.I., C.M.G., C.V.O., C.B.E., V.D., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 7th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offd. as commr., Ajmer-Merwara, June-July, 1901; offd. as asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Central India, Nov., 1901; asst. to pol. A.D.C. to S. of S., India Office, for H.M.'s coronation, June to July, 1902; on deputation as attache, govt. of India, Nov., 1902, to Jan., 1903; asst. sec. in the foreign dept., Feb., 1903; offg. under sec., March, 1905; confd., Nov., 1905; C.V.O., Jan., 1906; pol. offr. with the Prime Minister of Nepal, Sept., 1908; resdt. in W.R. States, Sept., 1908; sec. to chief commr., N.W.F. Prov., Jan., 1909; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Oct., 1909; pol. agent and ditto, Feb., 1910; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; sec. to central cttee. for coronation Durbar, Delhi, Dec., 1910, to March, 1912; special duty, govt. of India for. dept., Jan., 1913, to March, 1914; on deputation to Bikaner State, March, 1914; on special duty under agent to the govr. gen. in the Rajputana, Sept., 1914; service, replaced with govt. of Bengal, March, 1915; on mil. duty, from March, 1915; finl. adviser, Occupied Enemy Territory, 1918-19; Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; Order of Crown of Italy (Officer); Order of St. Maurice and St. Lazarus (Officer); Italian War Cross; C.M.G., June, 1918; C.B.E., June, 1919; ret'd., Mar., 1920; knighted, May, 1937.

GADRE, NARAYAN BALKRISHNA, A.M.I.E. (Ind.), B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 3rd Dec., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 8th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Nov., 1931; confd., Oct., 1933.

GADSDEN, CECIL HOLROYD, C.I.E., late Indian Police, Madras (b. 18th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1907; asst. supt., May., 1909; supt., Dec., 1917; asst. inspr.-genl., May, 1929; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1930, June, 1933, and again, Jan., 1935; confd., Dec., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; ret'd., Oct. 1939.

GAGE, EDWARD TREGURTHA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (inspr. of schools, Burma) (b. 6th April, 1888).—Joined the service as prinpl., govt. high school, Rangoon, on 18th Nov., 1918; head master, Taunggyi, Nov., 1922; ditto, English high school, Maymyo, Apl., 1933; inspr. of schools, Burma, Apl., 1936.

GAINSFORD, DONALD, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 18th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 12th June, 1920; offg. supt., April, 1926; confd., Oct., 1931.

GAISFORD, PHILIP, Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Nov., 1891).—First commn. dated 18th Jan., 1911; in milty. employ to 24th June, 1919; asst. commr., June, 1919; sec. to agent to govt.-genl. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, April, 1925; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., March, 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; on foreign service as guardian to H.H. the Maharajah of Gwalior, Mar., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Nov., 1934; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Mar., 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; dep. secty., pol. dept., Apl., 1937, and again, Sept., 1937; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1937; resdt. at Gwalior and pol. agent for states of Rampur and Benares, Apl., 1938; pol. agent in Bhopal, Sept., 1939.

GAIT, SIR EDWARD ALBERT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 11th Dec., 1884, and served as asst. commr. in Assam; provl. supt. of census, 1890, and wrote the provl. report; acted as sec. to the chief commr., 1891-92 and 1895-96; dep. commr., Nov., 1892; dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1893; mag. and collr., Nov., 1897; supt. of census operations, Bengal, April, 1900; offg. census commr. of India, Jan., 1903; dep. commr., Dec., 1904; member of legisl. council, Bengal, June, 1905; chief sec. to govt. of Bengal, pol., appt. and rev. depts., Jan., 1908; C.I.E., Jan., 1907; commr., July, 1909; census commr. for India, Nov., 1909; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa as member, board of rev. April, 1912; C.S.I., June, 1912; member of exec. council, Aug., 1912; K.C.S.I., June, 1915; lieut.-govr., Bihar and Orissa, 20th Nov., 1915; ret'd., Dec., 1920; member of the Council of India, 20th June, 1922, to 19th June, 1927; author of 'Report on the Census of Assam, 1891, and other papers dealing with Assam govt., 'A History of Assam,' 'Bengal Census Report, 1901,' 'India Census Report, 1901,' and 'India Census Report, 1911.'

GALE, BERKELEY, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 5th Feb., 1884).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1909; in mil. employ to 1st Jan., 1923; civil surg., Jan., 1923; ret'd. Jan., 1939.

GALE, LUCY, India Office (hr. clerical officer) (b. 16th Jan., 1882).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st June, 1921; actg. hr. ditto, July, 1935; confd., Jan., 1936.

GALE, U. MAUNG (C1), Indian Police (b. 5th May, 1889).—Apptd. dist. supt., Burma, on 7th April, 1925; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1927; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1932.

GALE, U. MAUNG (A8), B.A., late Burma commission (b. 15th June, 1880).—Previous service from July, 1904; dep. commr., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec. to govt., March, 1924; sec. to govt., local govt. dept., May, 1927; ditto, judl. dept., Aug., 1929; ret'd., June, 1935.

GALES, SIR ROBERT RICHARD, Kt., F.C.H., M.Inst.C.E., M.A.S.C.E., late Railway Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886, and posted to state rlys.; exec. engr., Nov., 1896; suptg. engr., March, 1910; chief engr., March, 1915; knighted, June, 1915; chief engr. with the rly. board, July, 1915; offg. agent, N.W. Ry., May, 1917; temp. agent, ditto, July, 1917; ret'd., Oct., 1919.

GALLAGHER, WILLIAM NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 12th Jan., 1913).—Educ. at Royal Academical Institution, Belfast, and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 18th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay asst. as collr.

GALLANT, MAURICE NAHUM, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 24th Mar., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928; for. econ., Oct., 1934.

GALLETTI DI CADILHAC, A. M. A. C. (see Di Cadilhac, A. M. A. C. Galletti).

GALLETTI DI CADILHAC, R. (see Di Cadilhac, R.)

GALLOWAY, ARNOLD CRAWSHAW, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 5th Jan., 1901).—First commn., 24th Dec., 1920; in milty. employ to June, 1928; under-secty. to A.G.G. in the states of Western India, Mar., 1932; vice-consul, Zahidan, Jan., 1933; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Feb., 1924; secty., ditto, June, 1934; and again, Apl., 1937; secty., British legn., Kabul, Nov., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Bahrein, Nov., 1937; secty. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Feb., 1938; pol. agent, Kuwait, May, 1939.

GALVIN, JOHN SMART, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Bombay (civil surg.) (b. 4th Mar., 1892).—First commn., 15th Mar., 1920; civil surg., Mar., 1931; offg. presdy. surg., Bombay, Feb., 1935.

GALVIN, PERCY HAROLD, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 6th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1913 as asst. supt., E.I. rly.; transpn. supt. (traffic comml.) E.I. rly., May, 1934.

GANAPATI, CODANDA MADIAH, Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., Madras (dir. of public health) (b. 1st Sept., 1886).—Date of first commn., 15th Mar., 1920; asst. dir. genl., I.M.S., June, 1924; dep. ditto, May, 1933; dir. of public health, Madras, Oct., 1934.

GANAPATIPILLAI, NATARAJA, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. Oct., 1879).—Apptd. 1st Jan., 1928; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1928; confd., Jan., 1933; retd., Oct., 1934.

GANDASING CHEEMA, SARDAR, D.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (horticulturist to govt. of Bombay) (b. 2nd Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as horticulturist, 13th June, 1921.

GANGA NATH, BABU, B.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., United Provs. (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. 11th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 19th May, 1908; sub judge, June, 1923; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1933; addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, variously from Oct., 1934; judge, ditto, Apl., 1937.

GANGARAM KAULA (see Kaula, G.).

GANGARAM, V. (see Vadhumal Gangaram).

GANGULI, ANIL BEHARI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1933.

GANGULI, JOGENDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 11th July, 1875).—Joined the service, 15th June, 1897, and held non-gazetted appt. prior to 27th Feb., 1913; insp., Feb., 1913; dep. supt., July, 1921; apptd. to I.P.S., 1st June, 1928; supt., June, 1928; retd., Sept., 1930.

GANGULI, JYOTI PRAKASH, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Stores Dept. (b. 20th Feb., 1882).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Apl., 1900; offg. asst. secty., Indian munitions board, Jan., 1919; confd., Feb., 1919; off. under-secty., industries and labour dept., Aug., 1924; on spl. duty in posts and tels. dept., Nov., 1925; dep. dir. of admn. and intell., June, 1930; retd., June, 1937.

GANGULI, NIBARAN CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 13th Mar., 1875).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1901; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1924; exec. engr., Nov., 1924; retd., Mar., 1930.

GANGULI, SANAT KUMAR, B.L., Bengal Judl. Service (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Apptd. asst. and sess. judge, Feb., 1928.

GANGULI, SUSIL KUMAR, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (b. 16th July, 1884).—Joined the service, 20th June, 1905; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Apl., 1910; asst. regr. co-op. societies, Oct., 1922; offg. asst. secty., agric. and industries dept., Bengal, Dec., 1926; asst. secty. to Govt., Bengal, irrign. dept., Feb., 1929; regr., co-op. societies, Bengal, Jan., 1931; retd., Oct., 1934; re-employed, officer in charge, co-op. training and education, Bengal, Mar., 1937 to May, 1939.

GANGULY, KESAV LAL, B.E., B.S.C., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr. E.I. Rly.) (b.

20th Feb., 1898).—Joined service Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., E.B. Rly. Oct., 1923; provl. perm. exec. engr. E.I. Rly., April, 1938.

GANPAT RAI, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, B.A. Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 16th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service, 24th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under-secty. to govt., irrigation bch., Punjab, Oct., 1931.

GANY, HAFIZ MUHAMMAD ABDUL (see Hafiz, M. A. G.).

GARBETT, COLIN CAMPBELL, C.S.I., C.M.G., C.E.E., B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (fin. commr., Punjab) (b. 22nd May, 1881).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 28th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; settlt. offr., Mandi State, 1910; dist. judge, Simla, 1913; lent to mil. dept., 1915; asst. postal censor, Karachi, March, 1915; ditto, Bombay, Sept., 1916; on active service, Mesopotamia, 1916; revenue commr., Mesopotamia; Feb., 1917, and administrator, agricl. development (mil.), June, 1917; twice mentd. in despatches. C.I.E., Aug., 1917; temply. empld. in pol; dept., India Office, July, 1919, to Aug., 1920; civil sec. to high commr., Mesopotamia, Sept., 1920, to May, 1922; dep. commr., Dec., 1921; C.M.G., June, 1922; ch. sec. to govt., Punjab, April, 1931; commr., Multan, Mar., 1935; C.S.I., June, 1935; finl. commr. (devel.), Dec., 1937.

GARDINER, REGINALD THOMAS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr.) (b. 27th Apl., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1929; confd., Nov., 1930.

GARDINER, WILLIAM NORMAN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 1st June, 1882).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 13th Nov., 1901; on mily. service, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, March, 1926.

GARDNER, JAMES CLARK MOLESWORTH, Indian Forest Service (systematic entomologist, Dehra Dun) (b. 15th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 11th Dec., 1922; systematic entomologist, Aug., 1923; offg. forest entomologist, Feb., 1932.

GARDNER, JOHN WILLIAM BERTRAND, M.B.E. (b. 13 Aug., 1886).—Joined the service, Dec., 1904; officer supervisor, headquarters, R.A.F. in India, March, 1928; asst. secty. defence dept., Govt. of India, Oct., 1930; retired April, 1938.

GAREWAL, AJAB SINGH, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Major, I.M.S. Jail Dept. (supt., Central Jail) (b. 13th Jan., 1894).—First commn., 24th Apl., 1920; in mil. employ up to 16th Feb., 1931; supt., Central Jail, Jubbulpore, Apl., 1931; ditto, Hazaribagh (B. & O.), Dec., 1931; ditto, Central Provs., Mar., 1933.

GAREWAL, SARDAR JAGDEO SINGH, M.R.C.V.S., I.V.S. (b. 18th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as supt., C.V.D. (Punjab and N.W.F.P.), 30th Jan., 1918; confd., Jan., 1921.

GARLAND, EDWARD ANDREW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1922, as asst. consvr., Bombay, dep. consvr., Nov., 1925; p.a. to I.G. of forests, Dehra Dun, Aug., 1938.

GARNIER, CHARLES NEWDIGATE, O.B.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 5th Jan., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., on 1st Oct., 1906; O.B.E., June, 1918; asst. divl. engr., Nov., 1920; divl. engr., March, 1925; offg. dir., tel. engrg., Punjab and N.W.F. circle, Mar., 1931; confd. as dir. of tel. engrg., May, 1934; offg. P.M.G., Punjab, Apl., 1935; confd., Aug., 1936; ret'd., Apl., 1938.

GARRAD, ERNEST WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 9th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 12th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., July, 1929; addl. asst. inspr.-genl., Simla, June, 1935.

GARRARD, HAROLD SEPTIMUS, India Office (asst. suptd. of records) (b. 21st Aug., 1886).—Apptd. clerical officer, 17th Oct., 1921; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1927; asst. suptd. of records, April, 1939.

GARRETT, HERBERT LEONARD OFFLEY, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 16th June, 1881).—Educ. at Charterhouse and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Dec., 1912; profr., govt. coll., Lahore, 5th Jan., 1913; on milty. duty from 1st Oct., 1917, to 31st Dec., 1918; sec., publicity dept., June to Oct., 1919; keeper of the records of the govt. of Punjab since 1924; member of the Indian Records Comm. since 1926; prinpl., govt. coll., Lahore, Sept., 1927; ret'd., June, 1936; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; supervisor of I.C.S. probationers at Cambridge University, Oct., 1937. Publications:—“Cunningham's History of the Sikhs” (new ed.), “Muhammadan Period of Indian History,” and a number of historical textbooks; general editor of Punjab Govt. Record Office publications.

GARRETT, Sir JOSEPH HUGH, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd June, 1880).—Educ., at Highgate Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. settlt. offr., dep. commr. of salt and excise, northern divn., Dec., 1919; offg. collr. and dist. mag., and pol. agent, Jan., 1921; offg. collr. and Talukdari Settlement officer, June, 1923; and again, June, 1925; confd., Jan., 1926; offg. commr., March, 1925; Feb., 1926; Mar., 1929, and again, June, 1933; member legisl. council, July, 1929; again, Mar., 1931; C.S.I. Jan., 1931; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bombay, pol. and reforms dept., Apl., 1935, and again, Apl., 1936; commr., Ahmedabad, Nov., 1935; actg. govr. of Sind, Aug. to Dec., 1938; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1939; ret'd., Nov., 1939.

GARROD, EDGAR SAMUEL, D.S.O., M.C., D.C.M., Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 6th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 12th Oct., 1923; asst. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, Jan., 1925; offg. supt., May, 1933.

GARROD, REV. WILLIAM FRANCIS, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 23rd Mar., 1893).—Apptd. junior chaplain, 10th Feb., 1930.

GARROD, WILLIAM HENRY EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr. Bombay) (b. 10th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; cons. pub. health engr. to govt., Nov., 1937.

GARSON, WILLIAM AUBREY, M.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd May, 1895) Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. on 1st Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Bihar, Oct., 1927; ret'd., June, 1938.

GARSTIN, WILLIAM ARTHUR MACDONNELL, C.B.E., Lt.-Col. I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 14th May, 1882).—In milty. employ from 28th July, 1900; asst. resdt. Western States, Rajputana, Aug., 1907; supy. asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Feb., 1908; asst. commr., ditto, July, 1909; presl. asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Dec., 1910, to May, 1911, and from Sept., 1911, to May, 1914; district judge, Kohat, Dec., 1916; asst. sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., April, 1918; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1920; O.B.E., Jan., 1923; divl. and sess. judge Peshawar, May, 1925; pol. agent, Khyber, Feb., 1928; C.B.E., Jan., 1930; pol. agent, southern states of Central India and in Malwa, Mar., 1932; resdt., Gwalior, Nov., 1932; resdt., Mewar, and pol. agent, S. Rajputana states, Mar., 1933; offg. resdt., and A.G.G., Madras states, Feb., 1935; ret'd., May, 1937.

GARVIN, STEPHEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 2nd Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GASH, REV. IVER JOHN, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 26th May, 1890).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, Bengal, 9th Oct., 1928; transferred to Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1929; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1930; transf. to Central Provs., Oct., 1932.

GASKELL, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 14th Jan., 1874).—Educ. at Loughborough and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 21st Nov., 1898, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1902; under sec. to govt., April, 1904; asst. settlmnt. offr., Oct., 1905; dep. commr. and jt. sec. to board of rev., April, 1912; temply. employed in finl. dept., India Office, from Jan. to Dec., 1916; mag. and collr., March, 1918; commr. of income tax, Dec., 1920; opium agent, Ghazipur, March, 1924; C.I.E. June, 1925; offg. member, central bd. of revenue, Oct., 1928; ret'd., July, 1932.

GASKING, REV. CLEMENT AUGUSTINE, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 6th June, 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 12th Oct., 1926; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1928.

GASSON, LIONEL BELL, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 9th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service 24th Nov., 1910; asst. supt., July, 1912; supt., Jan., 1926; dep. commr., March, 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; asst. inspr.-

genl., Nov., 1930; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1934; supt., spl. branch, C.I.D., Apl., 1935; cent. intell. officer., Madras Prov., May, 1938.

GASTER, VIVIAN ISAAC, B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, principal exec. grade, education dept.) (b. 11th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; on mil. service Nov., 1914, to April, 1917; asst. principal (temp.), Home Office, Oct., 1918, to Feb., 1920; asst. to local adviser to Indian students, London, Feb., 1920; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India as asst. to joint secs., Indian students dept., Oct., 1920; principal exec. officer, educ. dept., April, 1925.

GASTRELL, EVERARD HUDDLESTON, O.B.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Apl., 1898).—First commn., dated 14th Nov., 1916; joined pol. dept., 8th March, 1922; Brit. Consul for Arabistan, July, 1922; vice-consul, Ahwaz, Nov., 1922; on settlement training in Punjab, Nov., 1926; services placed at disposal of Ch. Commr., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1927; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, March, 1927; vice-consul, Duzdap, Dec., 1930; secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Dec., 1931; and again, Nov., 1933; pol. agent, Bahrein, July, 1933; consul-genl. for Khorasan, Mar., 1934; claims commr., Baluchistan, July, 1935; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Mar., 1936; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Nov., 1936; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Dec., 1936; pol. agent i/c. of the Bolan Pass and Chagai dist., Feb., 1937; O.B.E., May, 1937; sec. to resdt., Central India, Mar. 1939.

GATELEY, TERENCE PATRICK (b. 16th Dec., 1898).—Elec. engr., Central Public Works Dept., Oct., 1933.

GATES, SIR FRANK CAMPBELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Barr. (1891), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 18th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; from Aug., 1884, served in Burma as asst. commr.; on special duty in Upper Burma, March, 1887; junr. sec. to chief commr., April, 1887; under sec., Oct., 1887; dep. commr., Aug., 1889; junr. sec., March, 1891; sec., March, 1893; rev. sec., Jan., 1895; sec. to govt., May, 1897; commr., Aug., 1901; chief sec. to govt., Sept., 1903; member of legisl. council, Burma, Sept., 1903, and Oct., 1905; finl. commr., April, 1907; C.S.I., Jan., 1906; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1913; ret'd., April, 1914.

GATFORD, HARRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 6th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service, 10th Apl., 1920; resdt. engr., E.I. rly., Apl., 1924; exec. engr. (junr.) E.I. rly., Apl., 1930; ditto (senr.), Nov., 1933.

GAUL, REV. ARTHUR CECIL, M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 20th Oct., 1880).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 22nd April, 1918; junior chaplain, April, 1920; senr. chaplain, May, 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

GAULD, WILLIAM, O.B.E., late India Office (b. 5th Mar., 1875).—Clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 28th Dec., 1895; transfid. to public works dept., Jan., 1902; resdt., clerk, March, 1914 to March, 1923; staff clerk, April, 1919; administrative officer, April, 1922; apptd. asst. govt. dir. of Indian rly. cos., Jan., 1925; O.B.E., Jan., 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

GAUNT, CLIVE HERBERT, LL.B., late Burma Judl. dept. (b. 7th June, 1874).—Apptd. offg. asst. govt. advocate, 16th April, 1914; confd., April, 1921; ret'd., June, 1934.

GAUNTLETT, SIR MAGER FREDERIC, K.C.I.E., K.B.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Oct., 1873).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. stilt. officer; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1897; dep. acct.-gen., N.W.P. and Oudh, March, 1898; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1901; transfid. to fin. dept., govt. of India, as dep. acctnt.-gen., United Provs., April, 1903; offg. acctnt.-gen., ditto, Nov., 1904; ditto, Burma, April, 1906, to Nov., 1907; acctnt.-gen., Burma, and commr. of paper currency, Nov., 1908; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1909; ditto, Burma, April, 1906, to Nov., 1907; acctnt.-gen., Burma, and commr. of paper currency, Nov., 1908; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1909; ditto, rys., April, 1910; offg. compt. and auditor-gen., March, 1912, to Oct., 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1915; on delegation under the Board of Admiralty, Aug., 1915 to Nov., 1917; again offg. compt. and auditor-gen., Jan., 1918; confd. July, 1918; C.B.E., Jan., 1918; K.B.E., Jan., 1923; K.C.I.E., June, 1929; ret'd., May, 1930.

GAURI, NATH, P.M.S., late Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 20th Sept., 1884).—Civil surg., Apl., 1927; ret'd. July, 1938.

GAVAGHAN, EDWARD LAWRENCE, late Finance Dept., Govt. of India (b. 18th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service, Apl., 1908, as asst. examr., P.W. Accts.; on milty. service from 7th Jan., 1916, to 22nd July, 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Bombay, Jan., 1921; dep. ditto, Bengal, Feb., 1921; ditto, United Provs., Dec., 1921; ditto, Central Provs., April, 1923; dep. dir. of audit, U.P., Oct., 1926; offg. dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, March, 1927; ditto, posts and tels., Calcutta Nov., 1927; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Nov., 1929; offg. dep. auditor-genl., Dec., 1930; offg. compt., Assam, Feb., 1931; ch. auditor, E.B. rly., and govt. exr. of accts., Assam rly., Jan., 1934; offg. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Apl., 1934; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

GAWAN-TAYLOR, EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Sind) (b. 29th Oct., 1889).—Educ. at Durham Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr., supt. land records, Southern Div., Jan., 1921; on depn. as settl. officer from Dec., 1921, to May, 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1925; ditto and Talukdari settlement offr., Feb., 1926; confd., Apl., 1931.

GAWNE, EWAN MOORE, Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. magte., Madras) (b. 26th March, 1889).—Educ. at Wellington and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 8th Dec., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr., and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1919, to Feb., 1921; temp. dep. sec. to govt. of Madras, April, 1924, to June, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., July, 1925; confd., Feb., 1932.

GAWTHORNE, ARTHUR RICHARDSON, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 12th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 15th Apl., 1920; exec. engr., Burma Rlys., July, 1933.

GEARING, ALFRED HENRY, late Indian Police (b. 23rd Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 18th Mar., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1933; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

GEILS, HENRY THOMAS, India Posts and Telegraphs (Chief Superintendent) (b. 19th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, Oct., 1900; Superintendent, Aug., 1932; Chief Superintendent, Nov., 1938.

GELSON, WILLIAM EDWARD, B.Sc., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.Mech.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (bridge engr. N.W. Rly.) (b. 5th Feb., 1899).—Asst. exec. engr. bridges, Nov., 1926; provl. perm. bridge engr., Oct., 1937.

GEMMELL, GEOFFREY WOODBURN, Major, I.A. Survey of India (supt.) (b. 26th Sept., 1897).—First commn., 15th Nov., 1915; asst. supt., geodetic bch., May, 1927; supt., Apl., 1930.

GENNINGS, JOHN FREDERICK, C.I.E., C.B.E., Barr. (b. 21st Sept., 1885) (dir. of information and commr. of lab., Bombay).—Joined the service as dir. of information (temp.) 26th Dec., 1920; confd., 10th Oct., 1924; member, legis. council, Bombay, 1932; commr. of labour, Bombay, Apl., 1933; delegate of Govt. of India to internatl. lab. conf., Geneva, 1933; presdt., appellate ct., Dec., 1933; C.B.E., June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1939.

GENTLE, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Judl. Dept., Madras (judge, high ct.).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, Jan., 1936.

GENTLE, JOSEPH ALFRED HECTOR ROBERTS, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 26th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 2nd Jan., 1925; dep. consvr., Jan., 1929.

GENTRY, EDGAR CHARLES (b. 10th Aug., 1893).—Architect, Central Public Works Dept., Oct., 1933.

GEORGE, FRANK, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to finl. commr., Burma) (b. 9th Feb., 1907).—Educ. at Priory School, Shrewsbury, and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; secty. finl. commr., Sept., 1938.

GEORGE, FRANK SEAVER SCOTNEY, late Indian Police Service (b. 16th Mar., 1876).—Joined the service as prob., Madras, 22nd Nov., 1896; asst. supt., July, 1898; supt., June, 1907; services lent to Travancore Durbar from Aug., 1910, to Aug., 1915; dep. inspr.-gen., July, 1919; awarded King's police medal; ret'd., May, 1930.

GEORGE, HUGH SHAW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provinces) (b. 29th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 6th Dec., 1915; on mil. duty from 9th Aug., 1917, to 8th March, 1919; dep. consvr., Nov., 1921; sylviculturist, C.P., Oct., 1932.

GEORGE, JAMES, B.A. (Cantab.) Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 17th May, 1905).—Educ. at Priory

County School, Shrewsbury, Queen's Coll., Cambridge, and School of Oriental Studies; London; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Apl., 1931; offg. under-secty. to govt., Bengal, pol; and appt. depts., Sept., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936.

GEORGESON, WILLIAM WARES, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and sub-collr., Madras) (b. 13th March, 1906).—Educ. at Aberdeen Grammar School, Aberdeen University, and Pembroke College, Oxford; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Jan., 1933.

GERAGHTY, MICHAEL JOSEPH, Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 29th Dec., 1929.

GERRARD, FREDERICK WERNHAM, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 25th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1908; serving in Army dept. as asst. censor, Oct., 1914, to April, 1915; I.A.R.O., April, 1915, to Jan., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1920; supt. of police, May, 1922; on foreign service in Shanghai, May, 1929 to Mar., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

GERRARD, MISS JESSIE MARGARET, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (prinpl., Lady Willingdon training coll., Madras) (b. 9th Jan., 1892).—Apptd. prof., Queen Mary's Coll., Madras, 8th Aug., 1921; prinpl., Lady Willingdon trng. coll., Dec., 1925; confd., Jan., 1927.

GHAFFUR, ABDUL (see Abdul Ghaffur).

GHANSHYAM, DAS, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 10th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1906; munisf., Mar., 1910; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1913; sub-judge, Apl., 1916; dist. and sess. judge, confd., Jan., 1934.

GHARPUREY, H. G. (see Hari Ganpat Rao Gharpurey).

GHARPUREY, KHANDU GANPAT RAO, B.A. (Cal.), L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.) L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 20th July, 1880).—Joined, 1st Feb., 1906, and served in mil. dept. prior to 12th July, 1911; on milty. duty from 14th Aug., 1914; to 18th Jan., 1920; civil surg., Dec., 1922; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Bombay, Jan., 1934; ret'd., July, 1935.

GHATAK LABANYA MOHAN, M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 2nd Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as an officer. Cl. II, May, 1923; dep. mil. acct. genl. (jnr.), Mar., 1935; ditto (senr.), Apl., 1936; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Apl., 1936.

GHATAK, NARENDRANATH, M.B.E., Barr., Judl. Dept., Bengal (b. Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as master and official referee, high ct., original side, Calcutta, 22nd Nov., 1922.

GHATE, BAL GANGADHAR, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 27th April, 1928.

GHATE, NAMDEV SITARAMPANT, Indian Forest Service (dep. constr., Madras) (b. 4th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

GHAZANFAR ALI KHAN, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st Mar., 1875).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., Christ's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 8th Dec., 1899, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Jan., 1915; O.B.E., June, 1918; excise commr., Nagpur, April, 1920; offg. commr., Jan., 1924, and Mar., 1925; confd., Aug., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., July, 1934.

GHAZANFAR ALI NAQVI, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 17th Apl., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 27th May, 1926; offg. supt., Mar., 1933.

GHOLAP, LAKSHMAN TRIMBAK, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 4th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr.; offg. pol. agent, Jawhar, Mar., 1933; collr. and dist. magte., Hyderabad.

GHOSAL, Sir JOSNA, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 13th June, 1871).—Educ. at Metropolitan Coll., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 25th Nov., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; junr. collr. and pol. agent, April, 1912; K-i-H. medal, 1st class, Jan., 1913; C.I.E., June, 1918; senr. collr., Jan., 1918; member of legisl. council, 1922, 1924; offg. commr. and commr. of excise, March, 1924; confd., Jan., 1926; commr., Jan., 1928; C.S.I., June, 1929; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, Mar., 1930; retd., Jan., 1931; knighted, Jan., 1936.

GHOSE, BIRENDRA NATH, Indian State Rlys. (Transpn. traffic-commcl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 12th Aug., 1900).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1919; asst. supt., June, 1922; senr. scale (provl.), Feb., 1938; confd., Nov., 1938.

GHOSE, Sir SARAI KUMAR, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd July, 1879).—Educ. at the Metrop. Instn. and the Univ., Calcutta, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1910; s.p.t., dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1914; confd. as dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1916; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, May—Sept., 1928, and again, May, 1929; confd., Sept., 1929; knighted, June, 1938; retd. July, 1939.

GHOSH, Ajoy KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Apl., 1908).—Educated at King's College and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Nov., 1932; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GHOSH CHAUDHURI, HARI SADHAN, B.Sc., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 11th Mar., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931.

GHOSH CHAUDHURI, TAPENDRA KUMAR, Rai Sahib, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as proby. dep. supt., Jan., 1911; confd., Jan., 1913; addl. supt., Dec., 1918; offg. supt., April, 1919; confd., May, 1927.

GHOSH, E. M. B., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th Feb., 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 5th Sept., 1932; transfed. to Central P.W.D., May, 1935; offg. exec. engr., May, 1936.

GHOSH, JOTISH CHANDRA, Indian Police (Assistant Superintendent, Bihar) (b. 28th Nov., 1912).—Appointed on probation, January, 1937, confirmed 15th Dec., 1937.

GHOSH, J. M., Barr. Judl. Dept., Bengal (regr. in insolvency, high court, Calcutta) (b. 16th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service 14th May, 1921; asst. regr., high ct., Calcutta, original side, Nov., 1921; dep., regr., ditto, Dec., 1931; offg. regr. in insolvency, original side, Nov., 1932.

GHOSH, MAHIM CHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab.), B.A. (Calc.), Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 20th Jan., 1880).—Educ. at Santosh Jahnavi Sch., Bengal, Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1911; retransfed. to Bengal, April, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1916; offg. sec. to govt., judl. dept., Nov., 1927; confd., March, 1928; offg. judge, Calcutta high ct., May, 1930; and again, Nov., 1931; confd., Nov., 1932.

GHOSH, NAGENDRA NATH, Inc. tax dept. (asst. commr.) (b. 8th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1912; inc. tax officer, Oct., 1927; asst. commr., Bihar, Nov., 1933.

GHOSH, NIBARAN CHANDRA, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 21st Dec., 1890).—Apptd. to the service, 20th Mar., 1913; asst. supt. E.I. rly., Jan., 1916; supt., Apl., 1928; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., variously from Apl., 1934; confd., Mar., 1938.

GHOSH, RADHAKANTA, B.L., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 23rd July, 1874).—Joined the service, 4th March, 1902; munsif, April, 1904; sub-judge, April, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; retd., June, 1930.

GHOSH, SACHINDRA MOHAN, B.A., Indian police (b. 1st Apl., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st Mar., 1935; confd., Mar., 1937.

GHOSH, SHOTTYENDRA KUMAR, Indian C.S. (contr. of emigrant labour, govt. of India) (b. 21st May, 1896).—Educ. at Scottish Churches Coll., Calcutta; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1927; addl. dist. mag., April, 1928; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1928; confd., Dec., 1933; contr. of emigrant labour, govt. of India, Nov., 1937.

GHOSH, SUKHENDRA NATH, B.A., B.Sc. (Glas.), M.Inst. C.E., F.R. San. I., M.I.E., (Ind.), Public Health Dept., Bengal (b. 16th Oct., 1888).—Apptd. asst. sanitary engineer, Bengal, Nov., 1918; executive engineer, public health dept., 1921; chief engr. public health dept., 1939.

GHOSH, TARAK NATH, Indian Police (Assistant Superintendent, Bihar) (b. 2nd Oct., 1914).—Appointed on probation Jan., 1937, confirmed 15th Dec., 1937.

GHOSHAL, UMESH KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 25th Aug.,

(1912).—Educ. at Hindu School, Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Lincoln College, Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1938.

GHULAM ABBAS, B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 17th May, 1902).—Joined the service, cl. II, Mar., 1925; asst. mil. acct. genl., Apl., 1933; secty., Quetta Rec. Cttee., Nov., 1935; dep. mil. acct. genl. (junr.), Oct., 1936.

GHULAM AHMAD, MIAN, M.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 12th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 29th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., June, 1928; and again, Oct., 1932; confd., Apl., 1934; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., variously from Feb., 1934; asst. dir. intell. bureau, home dept., June, 1939.

GHULAM, AHMAD KHAN, B.Sc., M.B., B.S., Major, I.M.S., Jail Dept., Punjab (supt.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1892).—First commr., 1st Nov., 1920; supt., Central Jail, Oct., 1930.

GHULAM AHMED, MADANI, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Faiz-I-Am High School, Meerut, and Aligarh Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

GHULAM, MUHAMMAD, M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (finance officer, Communications) (b. 29th Aug., 1895).—Joined the service, 7th March, 1920; asst. audit officer, O. and R. rly., Jan., 1921; govt. exr. of accts., B. & N.W. rlys., Jan., 1925; services placed at disposal of rly. board, Nov., 1925; on foreign service under Bhopal state, June, 1932 to Feb., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Mar., 1934; offg. dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., finance, May, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. finance officer, posts and tels., July, 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; fin. offcr., communications, Oct., 1937.

GIBBS, WALTER FELIX, Indian Police (b. 12th Apl., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Nov., 1938.

GIBLING, MORLEY THEODORE, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 12th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931; secty., cent. bd. of irrig., Simla, May, 1935 to April, 1939.

GIBSON, EDMUND CURREY, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 6th July, 1886).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London, St. John's Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; employed temp. under govt. of India; for. and pol. dept., from June, 1915, tutor and guardian to H.H. the Maharaja of Bharatpur; asst. pol. agent, E. Rajputana States (in addn.), Sept., 1917; addl. asst. master, Mayo Coll., March, 1918; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1918; dep. commr., Oct., 1923; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Jan., 1924; pol. agent, Eastern States of Rajputana, March, 1925; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1927; offg. resdt., and agent to govr.-genl., Eastern States, Apl., 1933, C.I.E., June, 1933; offg. resdt., Gwalior, Nov., 1934; resdt., cl. II, Aug., 1935; resdt. for States of W.I., Oct., 1937; confd. as resident, class 1, May, 1939.

GIBSON, JOHN CARRINGTON, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supdt.) (b. 28th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 8th March, 1912; dist. loco. supt., April, 1918; (supt., E.I. Rly., July, 1925, offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Feb., 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), E.I. rly., Jan., 1936; divl. supt., Oct., 1938.

GIBSON, JOHN PERCIVAL, M.A., India Office (principal, pol. dept.) (b. 2nd June, 1898).—Educ. at Royal Academ. Inst., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. asst. principal, mil. dept., 19th Oct., 1921; transf. to commerce and revenue dept., June, 1923, and to pol. dept., April, 1924; resident clerk, Oct., 1924; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1927; pte. secty. to perm. under-sec. of state, Dec., 1928; principal Apl., 1930.

GIBSON, RAYMOND EVELYN, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Oct., 1878).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1902, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of land records and registrn., Sind, Jan. to April, 1906, and from April, 1907; colonisation offr., Jamrao Canal, Dec., 1909, to Oct., 1910; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindhi translator to govt., Jan., 1911; pte. sec. to govr., Bombay, Oct., 1912, to March, 1914; collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1922; actg. commr. in Sind, April, 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; member of legisl. council, 1924; commr., Jan., 1932; rev. commr. for Sind, Apl., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1936; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

GIDNEY, CLAUDE HENRY, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Haileybury, Hertford Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; held various temp. appts. under govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., from Oct., 1914; pol. offr. (on probn.), and asst. pol. agent, Quetta, Jan., 1918; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent, Kalat, Sept., 1918; pol. agent, Sibi, Nov., 1919; second asst. to agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Feb., 1920; sec. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, April, 1921; dep. commr., Bannu, Dec., 1923; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1926; secty. to ch. commr. and agent to govr.-genl., N.W.F.P., May, 1928; dep. commr., Bannu, May, 1930; secty. to ch. commr. and agent to govr.-genl., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1930; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., June, 1932; mem. exec. council of govr., N.W.F.P., Apl. and Nov., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1932; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1934; member, govr.'s exec. council, N.W.F.P., May, 1936; resident, Hyderabad, Apl., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1938.

GIDWANI, VISHINO LAKHIMAL, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 4th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at N.J. High School, Karachi and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GILBERT, CHARLES FREDERICK, Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent, E.I. Rly.) b. 25th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Feb., 1923; offg. exec. engr., April, 1923; confd., Oct., 1923; offg. senr. supt., Nov., 1932; offg. dep. agent, E.I. rly., Mar., 1935; confd., Jan., 1936.

GILBERT, LEONARD BURGES, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (supt. engr., United Provs.) (b. 20th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1933; offg. consulting engr. to the Govt. of India (roads), July, 1936; supt. engr., June, 1939.

GILCHRIST, ROBERT NIVEN, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (reforms commr., Bengal) (b. 13th Apl., 1888).—Apptd. profr. Presy. Coll., Calcutta, 1911; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, 1914; principal, Krishnagar Coll., 1916; centr., labour bureau, govt. of India, 1921; labour intellige. officer, Bengal, and dep. sec., commerce dept., 1922; jt. secty. to govt., commerce and marine depts., July, 1930; labour commr., Bengal, Mar., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1934; reforms commr., Oct., 1934; author of 'Indian Nationality,' Principles of Political Science, 'Conciliation and Arbitration.'

GILDEA, ERIC PERCIVAL, Indian State Ryls. (divl. rolling stock officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 9th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Apl., 1916; temp. asst. loco. supt., Sept., 1916-Oct., 1919; confd., Nov., 1919; asst. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; offg. variously as divl. rolling stock officer from Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1927.

GILES, ALEXANDER GEORGE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 13th May, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, on 21st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

GILL, CLIFFORD ALLCHIN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Col. I.M.S., rettd. (b. 12th Aug., 1878).—First commn., 31st Aug., 1903; in mil. employ till Jan., 1906, when he was appointed asst. plague medical officer, Punjab; chief malaria medical officer, Punjab, Oct., 1913; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to Dec., 1918; asst. dir. of public health, Punjab, Nov., 1921; offg. dir. of public health, Punjab, June, 1924; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosps., May, 1930; ditto, Burma, July, 1932; confd., Feb., 1933; rettd., Aug., 1935.

GILL, FRANK FREDERICK PERCY, Indian Police (asst. to I.G., Orissa) (b. 2nd Apl., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 18th Nov., 1925; confd., Sept., 1929; asst. supt. India home intell., Apl., 1931 to Feb., 1933.

GILL, GURDIAL SINGH, M.B., Ch.B., Major, I.M.S., Jail Dept., Madras (supt.) (b. 21st Mar., 1896).—Date of first commn., 28th Aug., 1926; offg. supt., jail dept., Nov., 1931; confd., April, 1932.

GILL, HARRY ANDERSON CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (dep.-secty., fin. dept., Govt. of India) (b. 12th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at Bradfield and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1935; under-secty., finance dept., Govt. of India, Apl., 1937; dep. ditto, Apl., 1938.

GILLAN, GEORGE VAN BAERLE, C.I.E., Lt.-Col. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 3rd Sept., 1890).—First commn. dated 19th

Jan., 1912; in milty. employ to 31st Dec., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec. to ditto, Nov., 1924; H.B.M.'s consul-genl., Khasgar, July, 1925; 1st asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Nov., 1928; pol. agent, Gilgit, Sept. 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; secty. to A.G.G., Punjab states, Aug. 1935; resdt., W. Rajputana States, Apl., 1937; resdt., Jaipur (addtl. charge), Dec., 1937; resdt. Western Rajputana States, Mar., 1938; resdt. in Mewar and pol. agent, Southern Rajputana States (addl. charge) April, 1938; resdt. for Baroda and Gujarat States, April, 1939.

GILLAN, SIR ROBERT WOODBURN, K.C.S.I., B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1889), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Ayr Acad. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1893; offg. city mag. and wasika officer, Lucknow, March, 1894; asst. commr., May, 1896; settlt. offr., Oct., 1896; dep. commr., July, 1899; mag. and collr., Dec., 1901; joint sec., board of rev., April, 1902; sec., board of rev., Jan., 1903; offg. sec. to govt., Sept., 1907; comptr. and auditor-gen., and head commr. of paper currency, fin. dept., govt. of India, July, 1910; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1912; member, Royal commn. on Indian finance and currency, April, 1913; member, ry. board, April, 1914; temp. member of govr.-genl.'s council, April to Oct., 1914; presdt., ry. board, April, 1915; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1916; commr., United Provs., April, 1919; rettd., April, 1920.

GILLESPIE, JAMES ROSS, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 5th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Dunfermline High Sch., St. Andrews Univ., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938 and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

GILLIAT, ALGERNON EARLE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxf.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 7th Dec., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec., rev. sec.'s dept., April, 1913; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., April, 1916; offg. dep. commr., April, 1921; offg. sec. to govt., fin. and rev. dep., May, 1923; dep. commr., Sept., 1924; ditto and addl. sec., fin. and rev. dept., Dec., 1924; sec. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1928; offg. commr., May, 1932; confd., Jan., 1936.

GILLIES, JOHN ERNEST, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th April, 1889).—Educ. at Old Deer Public School, Aberdeenshire, Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and Aberdeen University; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 9th Dec., 1913, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service, July, 1918, to Feb., 1919; under-sec. to govt. of Burma, July, 1919; offg. dist. and sessions judge, April, 1924; confd., May, 1924; rettd., Feb., 1931.

GILLIES, PETER, L.D.S. (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 22nd May, 1897).—Apptd. dental surg. and supt., Punjab dental hosp., Lahore on probn., 19th Mar., 1930; confd., Sept., 1932.

GILLIGAN, WILLIAM BOYD, Indian C.S. (backward cl. officr.) (b. 3rd Apl., 1893).—Educ. at High School, Dublin, R.M.C. Sandhurst, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 20th Feb., 1922; arrived, 10th April, 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. collr. and dist. magte., May, 1926; offg. criminal tribes settlement officer, Bombay Presdy., Nov., 1927; Labour officer, Bombay, Oct., 1934; collr. and mag., July, 1935; backward class officer, Nov., 1937.

GILMORE, EDWARD FITZGERALD GELSTON, B.Sc. (Hons.), M.I.Mech.E. (b. 25th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 12th Feb., 1924, as physicist, Govt. Test House, Alipore; supt. Govt. Test House (provl.), Nov., 1934; subst. Mar., 1938.

GIMSON, CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 24th Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Oundle, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. commr.; transfed. to Assam, April, 1912; settlt. offr., July, 1914; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Feb., 1918, to Jan., 1919; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, Feb., 1919, to Nov., 1921; offg. depy. commr., March, 1923; confd., Apl., 1928; pol. agent, Manipur, Feb., 1933.

GITA RAM, GARG, Lala.—Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 8th Sept., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr. Apl., 1927; confd. Oct., 1928.

GLADDING, DONALD, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Aug., 1888).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch., Southwark, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty, May to June, 1918; under-sec., govt. of Bengal, fin. dept., Oct., 1918; ditto, pol. and appt. dept., Dec., 1918; industrial intelligence officer Bengal, July, 1920; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1923; labour intelligence officer, Bengal, Jan., 1925; offg. dep. sec., fin. dept., Nov., 1925; confd., Feb., 1927; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1928; magte. and collr., Bengal, July, 1928; dep. secty. to govt. of India, home dept., June, 1931; secty. to govt., finance, commerce and marine depts., Bengal, July, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1936; retd., July, 1939.

GLAISBY, ARTHUR GEOFFREY TINDALL, Indian State Rlys. (dep. con. stores, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 12th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 12th Sept., 1914; asst. engr., G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1917; resdt. engr., Sept., 1921; liaison officer, G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1934; offg. dep. contr. of stores, Nov., 1935; offg. dist. contr. of stores, Mar., 1936; dep. agent, styled dep. gen. manager (works), May, 1937; dep. con. stores, G.I.P. Rly., June, 1939.

GLANCY, Sir BERTRAND JAMES, K.C.I.E.; C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 31st Dec., 1882).—Educ. at Monmouth School and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and pol. asst.; temp. under-sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., March to Nov., 1913; asst. resident, Mewar, May, 1914; 1st asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Rajputana, March, 1915; 1st asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Dec., 1918; dep. sec. to Govt. of India, pol. dept., Oct., 1921, and again, April, 1927; employed under Kashmir durbar from Nov., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; offg. pol. secty. to govt. of India, June, 1928; offg. agent to gov. genl., Punjab states, Apl., 1929; in foreign service as president, council of state, Jaipur, Oct., 1929; offg. resdt., and A.G.G., Punjab states, Apl., 1932; offg. pol. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., July, 1932, and again, July, 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; resdt. and A.G.G. in C.I., June, 1933; member, council of state, variously from July, 1933; secty., Chamber of Princes, Oct., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1935; secty. to H.E. the Crown Representative, Apl., 1937; political adviser to H.E. the Crown Representative (temp.) Dec., 1938.

GLASCOTT, JOHN RICHARD DONOVAN, C.I.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th June, 1877).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 9th Jan., 1904; ditto (perm.), June, 1905; signal engr., Nov., 1908; dist. engr., Mar., 1915; dep. ch. engr., Sept., 1917; ch. engr., Nov., 1919; agent, Mar., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; retd., Apl., 1934.

GLASS, ERNEST LESLIE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Oct., 1879).—Trained at T.C.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Punjab, Nov., 1902; on deputn. to Gwalior State, Oct., 1908, to April, 1914; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; transfed. to Central Provs., Dec., 1914, and temply. to Bengal, Dec., 1917; suptg. engr., April, 1920; offg. ch. engr. and secty., irrgn. dept., Jan., 1928; confd., May, 1928; retd., Oct., 1934.

GLASS, FRANK CECIL, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service as tempy. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; asst. engr., Oct., 1925; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1927; exec. engr., Jan., 1932; retd., Aug., 1934.

GLASS, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian Audit Dept. (comptroller, p. and t. accts., Burma) (b. 29th Dec., 1887).—Held non-gazetted appt. from Aug., 1906 to Dec., 1918; offg. asst. accts. officer, posts and tels., Feb., 1919; confd., Dec., 1919; dep. acct. genrl., ditto, Aug., 1927; examr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Feb., 1935; dep. acct. genl., Bengal, Apl., 1935; attd. officer, office of the dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Madras, Feb., 1937; on for. service as comp. of posts and tele. accts., Burma, Apl., 1937.

GLASS, HARRY LISTER, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 23rd Sept., 1909; on spl. duty, recruiting, 4th Dec., 1916, to 12th Feb., 1917;

exec. engr., Feb., 1918; offg. dep. dir., projects, Rly. Bd., April, 1925; offg. dep. agent, April, 1928; offg. senr. ditto, Mar., 1930; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. divl. supt., Apl., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1932; retd., Feb., 1934.

GLASS, LESLIE CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 28th May, 1911); educated at Bradfield, Trinity College, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Dec., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. warden, Burma oilfields, Sept., 1937.

GLASSON, ALFRED KIRBY, late Indian Forest Service (b. 11th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 7th Dec., 1912; dep. consvr., Dec., 1917; employed under Oxford Imperial Forestry Institute, Dec., 1927, to July, 1929; ch. forest officer, Andamans, Aug., 1931; offg. consvr., Apl. 1933; retd., July, 1935.

GLEDHILL, ALAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 26th Oct., 1895).—Educ. at Rugby School, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; offg. ditto, March, 1928; confd. June, 1928.

GLEESON, HENRY JOSEPH, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1902; asst. engr. (provl. service), Jan., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; offg. under sec. to govt., P.W.D. (rds. and bldgs.), Jan., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; confd., Jan., 1928; retd., Oct., 1933.

GLENN, JOHN ARTHUR GOULD, Indian Police (b. 31st July, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provinces, on probn., Dec., 1937.

GLENNIE, EDWARD AUBREY, D.S.O., Lt.-Col., R.E., Survey of India (supt.), (b. 18th July, 1889).—First commn., 23rd July, 1910; asst. supt. (proby.), 20th Dec., 1919; confd., Dec., 1921; supt., May, 1928; offg. dir., Nov., 1936.

GLOVER, HAROLD MATTHEW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Punjab) (b. 29th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., April, 1912; offg. consvr., Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1929; served in United Provs., Oct., 1932; posted as consvr., Punjab, June, 1933.

GLOVER, JOHN WILLIAM THOMSON, C.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th July, 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, 10th April, 1914; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir for Leh, July, 1919; pol. agent, Wana, March, 1923; O.B.E., June, 1924; asst. commr., Thal, April, 1926; dep. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1926; pol. agent, Dir. Swat and Chitral, Oct., 1928; C.B.E., June, 1931; pol. agent, Khyber, Aug., 1931; consul-genl at Kashgar, Nov., 1933; resdt., Kashmir, Oct., 1937.

GOBIND NARAYAN PAREEKH, B.A., Indian Police (b. 10th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 21st Jan., 1929.

GOBIND SARUP MATHUR, BABU, B.A. (b. June, 1878).—Joined the service as offg. munsif, 23rd June, 1906; confd., May, 1908; sub-judge, Dec., 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1928; confd. Jan., 1932; retd., June, 1933.

GODBOLE, YESHWANT ANANT, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 4th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and at Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 24th Nov., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., April, 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; confd., Dec., 1928; regr. of co-op societies, Bihar and Orissa, Mar., 1934; offg. dir. of industries in addn., Mar., 1935.

GODFREY, REGINALD WILLIAM, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 10th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. 15th March, 1921; confd., March, 1923; offg. supt., Apl., 1930; confd., June, 1930; pol. officer, Sadiya fron. tract, Apl., 1938.

GODSON, CHARLES AUBREY, M.C., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 15th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1906; in milty. employ up to 27th Dec., 1909; offg. civil surg., Jan., 1910; on mil. duty from Aug., 1914, to Jan., 1920; civil surg., Oct., 1921; retd., Nov., 1935.

GODWIN, CHARLES, Office of High Commr. for India (executive officer, store dept.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, store depot, 2nd May, 1904; transf. to office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

GOFF, GEORGE HERBERT, Indian State Railways (dist. traffic supt., E.B. rly.) (b. 25th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1910; dist. traffic supt., E.B. rly., Mar., 1931.

GOGHARI, DULLABHAI WAGHJI, L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 31st May, 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Feb., 1905, to 3rd Aug., 1915; sub-engr., Aug., 1915; asst. engr. (provl. service), Jan., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., Jan., 1926; retd., May, 1930.

GOIL, DWARKA PRASAD, M.B., F.R.C.S. (Edin.), K.H.P., Maj.-Genl., I.M.S., retd. (b. 1sp Aug., 1879).—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1903; arrived, 21st July, 1903; in mil. employ to Apl., 1907; civil surgeon, Dec., 1908; offg. prin. medl. coll., Calcutta, Mar., 1930; insp.-genl. of civil hosps., Punjab, Apl., 1932; surg.-genl. with govr. of Bengal, Dec., 1933; retd., Mar., 1938.

GOKHALE, BHALCHANDRA KRISHNA, M.A., India C.S. (secy., finance dept., Bihar) (b. 23rd July, 1892).—Educ. at Bombay and Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived 15th Dec., 1915; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto and asst. settlmt. officer, Nov., 1919; offg. dep. commr., April, 1927; secty. to govt., educn. and dev. dept., Mar., 1929; magte. and collr. (provl. sub.), May, 1930; confd., Dec., 1931; secy., finance dept., Bihar, May, 1939.

GOKHALE, RAO BAHADUR GANESH HARI, B.A., LL.B., late Central Provs. C.S. (b. July, 1881).—Joined the service as munsif, 2nd Apl., 1906; sub-judge, Nov., 1923;

offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1933; confd., Apl., 1933; retd., Oct., 1936; member, jt. public serv. commn., Bihar, C.P., and Orissa, May, 1937.

GOKHALE, VINAYAK GOPAL, L.Ag. (Bom.), late Indian Agric. Service (b. 10th Mar., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Feb., 1905, to 4th Sept., 1915; extra dep. dir. of agric., Sept., 1915; dep. dir. of agric., June, 1924; prof. of agric., agric. coll., Poona, Jan., 1929; acting principl. ditto, Oct., 1932; confd., July, 1933; retd., Mar., 1938.

GOKUL CHAND BADHWAR, B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1898), late Indian C.S. (b. 6th Mar., 1875).—Educ. at the Punjab Univ., and Christ's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 18th Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. sess. judge, Nov., 1905; it. mag., Feb., 1910; asst. sess. judge, April, 1912, to Feb., 1913; sess. and sub. judge. March, 1916; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1916; retd., Nov., 1934.

GOLDING, GORDON HENRY ARCHIBALD, late Indian Forest Service (b. 6th Dec., 1887).—Arrived, 28th Dec., 1910, and served as asst. consvr; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; on mil. service, Feb., 1916, to March, 1920; retd., Dec., 1933.

GOLDNEY, ALFRED PEEL, late Indian State Ry.s. (b. 17th Jan., 1878).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1901; asst. traffic supt., April, 1902; dist. ditto, Aug., 1909; dep. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; offg. ch. traffic manager, March, 1927; confd., Dec., 1929 retd., Jan., 1935.

GOLDSMITH, ARTHUR OLIVER, India Audit Office (higher executive officer) (b. 26th Mar., 1886).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 28th Dec., 1904; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922.

GOLDSWORTHY, FRANK TRUSCOTT, India Office (exec. officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 11th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Bristol Grammar Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 25th Feb., 1913; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922.

GOLDSWORTHY, LEONARD JAMES, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service on 10th Oct., 1914; prof., Nagpur, Dec., 1914; on mil. service from 23rd Oct., 1917, to 23rd Mar., 1920; inspr. of schools, Central Provs., Mar., 1920; lecturer in chemistry, Rangoon, June, 1923; retd., Feb. 1938.

GOLLAN, DONALD WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 16th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; suptg. engr., Feb., 1939.

GONSALVES, JOSEPH NICHOLAS, B.Sc. Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 7th May, 1914).—Educ. at Montfort Boys' High Sch., Yercand, Madras Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938 and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

GOOD, JAMES FULLER, M.B., B.Ch., Col., I.M.S. (retd.), late Burma Medical Dept.—Arrived, 22nd Sept., 1900, and served in mil.

dept.; offg. civil surgn., May, 1903; civil surgn., Dec., 1908; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1919; reverted to milty. employ, July, 1928; retd., Aug., 1931.

GOODALL, J. W. D., Capt., M.D., Ch.B. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (Edin.), I.M.S. (civil surgeon, Bengal) (b. 10th Dec., 1900).—Date of first commission, Feb., 1934; civil surgeon, Sept., 1938.

GOODCHILD, WILLIAM BADEN, India Office (pte. secty. to parly.-under-secty. of state for India and for Burma) (b. 19th May, 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 22nd Mar., 1920; higher clerical officer, May, 1928; resdt. clerk, Dec., 1928, to July, 1934; asst. secty., Indian delegations to Assembly of League of Nations, 1928 to 1934; asst. secty., Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; actg. pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, July to Oct., 1935; asst. princpl., Nov., 1935; posted to Burma Office, April, 1937; pte. secty. to parly.-under-secty. of state for India and for Burma, Jan., 1939.

GOODERSON, RICHARD NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 3rd Mar., 1915).—Educ. at Northampton Town and County Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

GOODMAN, REGINALD TERENCE WYNNE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., N.W.F.P.) (b. 26th July, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 20th Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confd., Oct., 1931.

GOODWYN, HAROLD PETER, B.A., Indian C.S. (under secy., home dept., Bengal) (b. 19th July, 1913).—Educ. at Wellington College and Peterhouse, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1938; special officer, home dept., Aug., 1939; under secy., home dept., Sept., 1939.

GOPAL, NAND (see Nand Gopal).

GOPAL, RAM (see Ram Gopal).

GOPALASWAMI AYYANGAR, NARASIMHA, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., B.L., Diwan Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. 31st Mar., 1882).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 1st Apl., 1905; collr. and dist. magte., Dec., 1920; secty. to bd. of rev., land rev., April, 1923; secty. to govt., public works and lab., Madras; offg. second secty. to govt., Madras, Nov., 1932; 3rd member, bd. of rev., Madras, and offg. 2nd ditto, May, 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935; 2nd secty. to govt., Madras, Mar., 1936; offg. 1st member, bd. of rev., May, 1936; commr. of excise, Madras, Jan., 1937; retd., Mar., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; prime minister, Kashmir.

GOPALAKRISHNA RAO, BELTHANGADI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr. Bengal) (b. 1st June, 1909).—Educ. at Board High School, Puttur, University of Madras and St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1935.

GOPALAKRISHNAN, PANANGATTI ANANTHANARAYANAIYER, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 20th Dec., 1909).—Educated at Raja's High School, Kollengode, South Malabar, Madras University, University College, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933 and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1935.

GOPALASWAMI, RAMASWAMI AYYANGAR, Indian Civil Service (sub collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 18th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and magte.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Mar., 1930; jt. sec., board of rev., Madras, Feb., 1939.

GORDON, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr. and secty. to govt., P.W.D., Sind) (b. 15th June, 1880).—Joined as asst. engr., on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; suptg. engr. (subt. temp.), Jan., 1927; ditto (provl. perm.), Dec., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935; ch. engr., and secty. to govt. P.W.D., Sind, May, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

GORDON, ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 14th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 5th Dec., 1907; supt., Oct., 1915; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Nov., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. commr. of police, Calcutta, Apl., 1935.

GORDON, CHARLES HOWARD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Dec., 1916).—Educated at Oundle and Trinity Hall College, Cambridge; appointed on probation, 11th Oct., 1938; confirmed, 7th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939 and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GORDON, SIR EYRE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (chairman, fed. public services commn.) (b. 28th Feb., 1884).—Educ. at Rossal and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 24th Nov., 1908, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1912, to July, 1915; settlt. offr., Jan., 1916; dep. commr. (provl.), May, 1921; ditto, and rev. sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., June, 1921; commr. of settlts., dir. of land records, I.G. of reg., regr.-genl. and sec. to govt., April, 1926; offg. commr., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., Dec., 1929; confd., Aug., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Central Provs., May, 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1935; chairman, fed. public services commn., Oct., 1936. Knighted, Jan., 1939.

GORDON, FRANCIS WALDEN, M.C., M.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 10th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 8th Feb., 1921; dep. consvr., Jan., 1925; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

GORDON, GERALD WATSON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Assam) (b. 31st Oct., 1902).—Joined the service, 27th Oct., 1925, as asst. exec. engr.; under secty., P.W.D., Assam, June, 1937.

GORDON, JAMES, M.B.E., India Office (staff officer, services and general dept.) (b. 27th Dec., 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, 3rd Oct., 1921; M.B.E., June, 1934; actg. exec. officer, Nov., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935; staff officer for services questions, July, 1939.

GORDON, JOHN DE LA HAY, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Mar., 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; in mil. employ till Dec., 1911, when he was apptd. asst. pol. agent (on probn.); asst. resdt., Aden, April, 1913; mil. govr., Basra, Sept., 1916, to Sept., 1919; O.B.E. June, 1918; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar States, Jan., 1925; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Dec., 1927; 1st asst. resdt., Aden, Nov., 1931; pol. agent, eastern Kathiawaf agency, Mar., 1932; offg. A.G.G., Deccan States, Aug., 1935; offg. A.G.G., states of W.I., June, 1936; dep. A.G.G., states of W.I., Nov., 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; offg. resdt., Mysore and chief commr., Coorg, Dec., 1937; confd. as resdt. 1st class and resdt. in Mysore, Jan. 1939.

GORDON, NORMAN HORATIUS, B.A., late United Provs., C.S. (b. 27th Dec., 1832).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1907; dep. collr., Mar., 1911; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1925; confd., May, 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

GORDON, ROLAND GRAHAM, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th Oct., 1880).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; compiled admn. report, 1906-07; supt. of land records and registrn., Jan., 1908, to Aug., 1912; talukdari settlt. offr., April, 1915; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, April, 1920, and again, April, 1924; confd., Feb., 1925; on spl. duty in connection with talukdari estates, Dec., 1925, and again, Nov., 1926; ditto in rev. dept., March, 1926; pol. agent, Surgana, March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1934; author of a land survey manual.

GORDON, RONALD EAGLESON, M.C., Lt.-Col. R.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent (organisation) N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th Jan., 1889).—First commn. dated 23rd Dec., 1900; asst. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., June, 1921; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), N.W. rly., Dec., 1932; and again, Mar., 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), Nov., 1936; provl. perm. dep. ch. engr., May, 1939.

GORDON, SIDNEY VICTOR LEOPOLD DE MONTMORENCY, late Indian Police (b. 9th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1903, and posted to United Provs., asst. supt. of police, Nov., 1906; supt., Dec., 1915; ditto and A.D.C. to lieut.-govr., March, 1916; on mil. duty from Sept., 1917, to Feb., 1919; principal, police training sch., Moradabad, April, 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1929; confd., Apl., 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

GORMAN, JAMES HAMILTON, M.B., B.Ch., D.P.H., Major I.M.S. (Port health officer, Calcutta) (b. 29th Jan., 1904).—First Commission dated 4th Feb., 1930, in mil. employ to 6th Feb., 1939, when he joined the Port Health Department; port health officer, Calcutta, March, 1939.

GORRIE, Rev. LESLIE MANIFOLD, Indian Eccles. Estab. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 21st Mar., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th April, 1919; junr. chaplain, April, 1921; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1929.

GORRIE, ROBERT MACLAGAN, D.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 26th June, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th Dec., 1922 dep. consvr., Sept., 1930.

GORTON, WILLIAM HUGH, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 27th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; ret'd., Apl., 1932.

GORWALA, ASTAD DINSHAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. govt. of India, communications dept.) (b. 7th Dec., 1900).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Cambridge Univ. (Non-Coll.); joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 5th Dec., 1924, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr.; offg. collr. and dist. magte., May, 1928; offg. asst. rev. officer, Lloyd barr. and C.S., Nov., 1931; dep. secty., govt. of India, communications dept., Nov., 1937 and again, July, 1938; on spec. duty, Sind, May, 1938; offg. secty., govt. of India, communications dept., March, 1939; dep. secty., govt. of India, communications dept., May, 1939.

GOSCHEN, Viscount, Rt. Hon. GEORGE JOACHIM GOSCHEN, OF HAWKHURST, P.C., G.C.S.I. G.C.I.E., C.B.E..—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.P. for East Grinstead, 1895 to 1906; joint parly. sec. Board of Agriculture, 1918; govr. of Madras, 14th April, 1924, to 11th Nov., 1929; actg. govr. genl. of India; G.C.S.I., June, 1929; P.C., Feb., 1930.

GOSS, EDWARD SLADE, M.C., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., retired (b. 25th Sept., 1883).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1911; in military employ up to 31st Dec., 1920, and again from 6th Jan., 1923 to 30th Sept., 1929; offg. asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S. (stores), Jan., 1921, and again, Oct., 1929, nfd., Nov., 1929; offg. dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Oct., 1932; officer in charge, medl. store depot, Lahore, Aug., 1933; retired, Sept., 1938.

GOSS, JAMES SAMUEL, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 18th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 25th March, 1912; dist. loco. supt. (provl.), April, 1919; ditto (perm.), March, 1922; asst. agent, N.W. Rly., Oct., 1926; divl. personnel officer; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

GOSSET, HUGH HENRY EYRE, M.C., Major R.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1912; asst. engr., July, 1913; on milty. duty from 21st Oct., 1914; exec. engr., Jan., 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., Sept., 1932; provl. perm., April, 1938.

GOSWAMI, UPENDRA LAL, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (asst. commr.) (b. 5th Jan., 1912).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ.; joined the service, 24th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

GOULD, ALFRED JOHN, B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 28th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as lecturer in civil engrg., Univ. Coll., Rangoon, 29th Sept., 1914; ret'd., Jan., 1939.

GOULD, BASIL JOHN, C.M.G., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 29th Dec., 1883).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 29th Nov., 1907, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and pol. asst.; asst. to agent to govt.-gen. in Central India, May, 1909; asst. sec. to govt. of India for dept., June, 1910; special duty in for. dept. govt. of India, Sept., 1910; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., Oct., 1910; Br. trade agent, Gyantse, May, 1912; offg. pol. offr., Sikkim, Oct., 1913; asst. commr., Nowshera, Oct., 1914; ditto. and asst. commr., Charsada, May, 1915; dist. judge, Peshawar, Sept., 1916; asst. commr., Mardan, May, 1917; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, Sept., 1917; on spl. duty under orders of H.B.M.'s Consul, Seistan, from May, 1918; H.B.M.'s Consul for Seistan and Kain, Nov., 1918; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, April, 1920; C.I.E., Sept., 1921; first asst. to resident in Kashmir, Dec., 1925; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, June, 1926; and again, June, 1928; chargé d'affaires, Kabul, March, 1927; C.M.G., June, 1929; pol. agent, Kurram, Novr., 1929; ditto, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Jan., 1931; offg. resdt., Waziristan, Oct., 1931; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1933; and again, Dec., 1934; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Baluchistan, July, 1934; pol. officer, Sikkim, Dec., 1935.

GOULD, HERBERT ROSS, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th April, 1887).—Educ. at Clifton, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1911, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; on milty. duty, July, 1916, to Jan., 1919; depy. comr., U.S. Frontier, Jan., 1921; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, Dec., 1924; member of legisl. council, Aug., 1929; pte. secty. to govr. of Bombay, Aug., 1930; and again, Dec., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

GOVINDA MENON, A. V., Rao Bahadur, Barr., late Madras Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (b. 31st Jan., 1882).—Apptd. addl. sess. judge, 10th July, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1926; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

GOVINDAN NAYAR, ULLATHIL, B.A., B.L., late Judl. dept., Madras (b. 9th Nov., 1877).—Joined the service as offg. dist. munsif, 6th July, 1907; confd., Feb., 1908; sub-judge, Dec., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

GOVINDARAJA AYYANGAR, NARASIMHA, Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (dep. ch. engr., P.W.D., Madras) (b. 10th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 16th June, 1914; asst. engr., July, 1917; exec. engr., June, 1924; dep. ch. engr., bldgs. and roads, Dec., 1935.

GOVIND DINANATH MADGAONKAR (see Madgaonkar, G. D.).

GOVIND MAHADEV PANDIT (see PANDIT G. M.).

GOW, PETER FLEMING, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof. midwifery, Med. Coll., Calcutta) (b. 28th June, 1885).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1912, apptd. prof. midwifery, Med. Coll., Calcutta, Oct., 1932.

GOYDER, CECIL WILLIAM, B.Sc., A.C.G.I., All-India Radio (chief engineer) (b. 1st Apl., 1905).—Joined the service as chief engineer, ditto, 20th Aug., 1936.
