HANNA, CHARLES HUGH, India Audit Office (supgt. hr. executive officer) (b. 23rd Feb., 1894).—Educ. at Alleyne's Sch., Dulwich; apptd. second class clerk, 15th July, 1912; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; supgt. ditto., Sept., 1938.

HANNA, GEORGE BOYLE, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 20th Nov., 1887).—First commn. dated 30th May, 1916; in milty. employ up to 31st Aug., 1925; supt., central jail, Dacca, Sept., 1925; supt., presdy. jail, Calcutta, Dec., 1928; reverted to milty. duty.

HANNAH, JOHN STEWART, M.C., late Indian Police Service (Bengal) (b. 22nd Apl., 1893).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. on 12th Nov., 1913; confd., Dec., 1915; offg. dep. commr., port police, Jan., 1923; addl. supt., Dec., 1926; supt., Dec., 1927; ret'd., Apl., 1929.

HANNAY, Sir HUGH AUGUSTUS MACNISH, Kt., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th May, 1878).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Nov., 1906; dist. supt., Oct., 1914; dep. traffic manager, Oct., 1921; junr. dep. agent, Dec., 1921; offg. genl. traffic manager, June, 1923; confd., July, 1923; chief operating supt., Feb., 1925; member, rly. bd., Apl., 1929; offg. agent, E.B. rly., July, 1932; ditto, E.I. rly., Mar., 1933; confd., Apl., 1933; knighted, Jan., 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

HANSEN, HENRY ERIC, late Indian Police (b. 18th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 28th Nov., 1912; confd., May, 1914; addl. supt., Feb., 1925; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, June, 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; ret'd., July, 1936.

HAPPELL, ALEXANDER JOHN, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 13th June, 1887).—Joined the service, Nov., 1907; supt., Dec., 1917; dep. commr. of police, Madras City, Mar., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; O.B.E., June, 1925; supt., govt. railway police, Oct., 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1931, confd., Feb., 1936.

HAPPELL, ARTHUR COMYN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 20th Oct., 1891).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1921; arrived, 1st Dec., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1924; offg. dep. sec. to govt., finance dept., May, 1925; offg. under-sec. to govt., public dept., July, 1925; ditto, dev. dept., April, 1926; registrar, high ct., Oct., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1930; confd., Sept., 1932.

HAPPER, WILLIAM, M.B.Ch.B., M.R.C.P. (Edin.), Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., Madras (b. 4th Nov., 1901).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1932; in military employ to Dec., 1937; district medical officer, 30th Dec., 1937.

HAQ, S. M., B.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S. Judl. bch. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 13th Aug., 1895).—Joined the service, 8th July, 1932, as offg. dist. and sess. judge; confd., Jan., 1934.

HAQUE, KAZI ANWARUL, B.A., Indian Police (b. 8th Feb., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, Bengal on probn., 20th Feb., 1933; confd., May, 1935.

HAQUE, SHEIKH IKRAMUL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1915).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 15th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938 and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

HARBANS LAL, VEDHERA, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th Jan., 1901).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 23rd Oct., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1925; exec. engr., Oct., 1934.

HARDIE, ROBERT, M.A., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Burma) (b. 6th June, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 4th Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Nov., 1926; offg. commr. of police, Dec., 1932; confd., Sept., 1933; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1937.

HARDING, GEORGE TREVOR HAMILTON, C.I.E., Indian Police (supgt., Punjab) (b. 18th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 28th Nov., 1925; on milty. duty from 26th July, 1916, to 9th April, 1919; asst. supt., Dec., 1920; supt., Mar., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1931.

HARDING, Rev. JOHN AMBROSE, M.A., Burma Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Burma) (b. 12th Sept., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 7th May, 1926; junr. chaplain, May, 1928; transfed. to Burma, Feb., 1938.

HARDINGE, EUSTACE GILBERT, late Survey of India (b. 6th May, 1873).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1896; supt., July, 1925; ret'd., May, 1931.

HARDINGE OF PENSHURST, Baron, Right Hon. CHARLES HARDINGE, K.G., P.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., G.C.V.O., I.S.O.—Viceroy and govr. genl. of India, 23rd Nov., 1910, to 3rd April, 1916.

HARDINGHAM, HAROLD, late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 27th May, 1880).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1900; asst. acct. genl., Burma, Aug., 1929; retd., Nov., 1934.

HARDMAN, JAMES SNELSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (under secty. to Govt. of India, dept. of education, health and lands) (b. 7th June, 1905).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar School, and Trinity College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1929; arrived, 29th Nov., 1929, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and coll.; under-sectn. to govt., pol. and appts. dept., Bihar and Orissa, April, 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1936; attaché, dept. of education, health and lands, Nov., 1938; under secty., ditto, May, 1939.

HARDWICK, DONALD ROSS, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 26th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 18th Dec., 1914; offg. supt., May, 1924; confd., Dec., 1925; asst. inspr. genl., Jan., 1933.

HARDWICK, NOEL DE COURCY, O.B.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Dec., 1877).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 28th June, 1905; on milty. duty from 19th Dec., 1914, to 2nd June, 1920; O.B.E., June, 1919; dist. traffic supt., June, 1923; offg. dep. traffic manager, Dec., 1928; confd., July, 1931; retd., Sept., 1933.

HARDY, DONALD PEVERIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., U.P.) (b. 29th July, 1912).—Educated at High School, Middlesbrough, and Selwyn College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Nov., 1937.

HARDY, JAMES DOUGLAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 8th June, 1915).—Educ. at Penistone Grammar School and Caius College, Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HARDY, REGINALD HERBERT, A.C.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Oct., 1880).—Joined the service as 1st asst. auditor, 8th Apr., 1909; dep. ch. accts. offr., Dec., 1912; ch. accts. officer, Burma rlys., Nov., 1930; retd., Mar., 1934.

HARE, Rev. ARTHUR NEVILLE, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 28th Feb., 1886).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 12th April, 1918; junr. chaplain, Aprl., 1920; senr. chaplain, Aprl., 1930; retd., Feb., 1939.

HARE, REGINALD ARTHUR PROCTOR, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 13th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 10th Mar., 1921; supt., July, 1925.

HARE-DUKE, ARTHUR ROBERT AUBREY, M.A., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. contr. of stores, E.I. Rly.) (b. 13th July, 1885).—Joined the service as dep. contr. of stores (tempy.), 13th Dec., 1926; confd., Dec., 1923; offg. contr. of stores, variously from June, 1929.

HARGREAVE, HERBERT NEWTON, Indian Police (supt. Bihar) (b. 21st Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 8th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Feb., 1933; confd. Sept., 1937.

HARGREAVES, HAROLD, late Archaeological survey dept. (b. 29th May, 1876).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 16th Aprl., 1909; offg. supt., Nov., 1916; confd., Mar., 1914; offg. dir. genl. of archaeology, Oct., 1928; retd. May, 1931.

HARI CHAND, M.C., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 26th Aug., 1886).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; in milty. employ to 30th Jan., 1924; civil surg., Jan., 1924.

HARICHAND, D.E., P.H.E., D.Litt. (Paris), Indian Educl. Service (prof., Patna Coll.) (b. 26th Aprl., 1889).—Apptd. supt. of Sanskrit studies, Bihar and Orissa, 8th April, 1918; prof. of Sanskrit, Patna, April, 1929.

HARISH CHANDRA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1894).—Educ. at Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, and Oxford Univ. (Non-Coll.); apptd. proby. dep. collr., 4th March, 1918; asst. mag. and collr. 1st Mar., 1922; jt. mag., Oct., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1925; offg. sess. and sub. judge, Dec., 1926; confd., June, 1929; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1929; judicial secty., United Provs. and legal remembrancer, Nov., 1939.

HARISH CHANDRA, KHANNA, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 4th Nov., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Dec., 1928; on depn. to forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Aprl., 1929.

HARI SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, Sardar, B.A., Punjab C.S. (b. Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 26th June, 1902, and held non-gazetted appts. to 3rd Jan., 1916; extra asst. commr., July, 1917; dep. commr. for criminal tribes, April, 1923; ditto, and reclamation officer and insp.-genl. of reformatories, Punjab, Feb., 1931.

HARIVANSH LAL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 6th Dec., 1912).—Educ. at D.A.V. High School, Lahore, Punjab Univ., and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

HARLEY, ALEXANDER HAMILTON, M.A., late Indian Educ. Service (b. 11th June, 1882).—Joined the service, Jan., 1911, and apptd. prin. of Calcutta, Madrasah; ditto, Islamia Coll., Calcutta, July, 1926; retd., June, 1937.

HARLEY, THOMAS WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 10th Dec., 1876).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1902; D.M.O., supt. medl. school and medl. officer, dist. jail, Madura, Dec., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1926; retd., Dec., 1931.

HARLOW, CHRISTOPHER MILLWARD, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Cent. Provs.) (b. 5th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 23rd Dec., 1911; on milty. duty from 30th Sept., 1916, to 1st May, 1917; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; employed under Rewa Durbar, Nov., 1929; offg. consvr., May, 1931; confd., Aug., 1936.

HARNAM DAS BHANOT (see Bhanot, H.D.).

HARNETT, WALTER LIDWELL, C.I.E., M.B., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. ret'd. (b. 7th June, 1879).—Joined the service, 2nd Feb., 1907; in milty. employ up to 31st March, 1911, and again from 21st Feb., 1916, to 23rd Nov., 1921; civil surg., Oct., 1922; supt., Campbell medl. sch. and hosp., Calcutta, April, 1925; prof'r. of surg., medl. coll., Calcutta, Mar., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

HARPER, CUTHBERT SUTCLIFFE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 17th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. master, Daly Coll., Indore, 5th Oct., 1914; on milty. duty from 11th Nov., 1916, to 11th March, 1919; supt. of educn., Baluchistan, and princpl., Sandeman high sch., Quetta, Feb., 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

HARPER, GEORGE MILNE, M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 27th Aug., 1882).—Educ. at Royal High School, Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived 26th Nov., 1906, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., May, 1915; ditto, and asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1916; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Sept., 1918, to Feb., 1919; mag. and collr., Dec., 1922; offg. commr. from Aug. to Oct., 1923, and from Aug., 1925; secty. to govt., rev. and public wks. dept., Mar., 1931; offg. commr., Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1936.

HARPER, KENNETH GORDON, B.C.L., M.A., (Oxon.), late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1888).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Oct., 1917; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1918, to Jan., 1919; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, legisl. dept., April, 1925; sess. and sub-judge, Dec., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, 1st, 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; ret'd., May, 1931.

HARPER, REGINALD KIRMAN, M.C., Indian C.S. (commr. of income tax, Burma) (b. 31st Dec., 1895).—Educ. at King Edward VII Grammar School, Morpeth; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1927; offg. regr., co-op. societies, Burma, Mar., 1932; exr., local fund accts., Burma, June, 1936; commr. of income tax, Mar., 1938.

HARPER-NELSON, JOHN JOSEPH, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 12th Jan., 1882).—First commn., dated 1st Aug., 1908; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; prof'r., King Edward medl. coll., 24th Nov., 1919; princpl., ditto, Mar., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1936; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

HARRIES, SIR ARTHUR TREVOR, Judl. Dept., United Provs. (chief justice, high ct., Palma).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1934; chief justice, high ct., Patna, Sept., 1938; Knighted, Jan., 1939.

HARRIS, BRIAN ALFRED, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 11th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. examr. on 30th July, 1908; asst. acct.-genl., Jan., 1915; dep. acct.-genl., U.P., April, 1924; ditto, Central revenues, Oct., 1925; ditto, Madras, March, 1927; audit officer, Cauvery Mettur project, Dec., 1927; offg. comptr., Assam, May, 1933; ret'd. Feb., 1934.

HARRIS, DIGBY GORDON, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 2nd May, 1887).—Joined the service as proby. asst. opium agent, 2nd April, 1907; confd., April, 1909; 2nd asst. factory supt., April, 1913; 1st ditto, Dec., 1914; factory supt., Feb., 1924; ret'd., July, 1932.

HARRIS, DOUGLAS GORDON, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.I.E.(Ind.) late Dept. of Industries and Labour, Govt. of India (b. 19th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1907, as asst. engr., United Provs.; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; under sec. to govt. of Indi., public works dept., Oct., 1915; asst. inspr.-gen. of irrig. in India, Oct., 1920; sec., new capital construction cttee., Delhi, 1st Oct., 1922; dep. sec., public wks. branch, dept. of industries, etc., Nov., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. consulting engr. to govt. of India, Aug., 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; C.S.I., June, 1932; ret'd., May, 1934; offl. member, exec. council of Palestine, Jan., 1936.

HARRIS, GEORGE JAMES, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th Mar., 1874).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 9th March, 1893, to 4th Sept., 1906; offg. asst. supt., Sept., 1906; and again, Jan., 1908; asst. traffic manager, Aug., 1907; asst. supt., April, 1910; offg. dist. supt., April, 1916; confd., April, 1919; supt., Feb., 1925; tempy. dep. ch. operating supt., April, 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

HARRIS, HENRY WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (works manager (manufactg.) E.I. rly.) (b. 30th May, 1883).—Joined the service, Sept., 1920; asst. works manager, E.I. rly., Apl., 1921; offg. works manager (manufactg.), Feb., 1924; confd., Mar., 1928.

HARRIS, HUBERT ARNOLD, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 22nd July, 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1928; offg. under secty. to govt., Punjab, p.w.d., bldgs. and road bch., May, 1933; and again, Oct., 1936.

HARRIS, JOHN FREDERICK WILLOUGHBY, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 19th June, 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 1st Nov., 1931; confd., April, 1934; offg. supt., July, 1936.

HARRIS, JOHN RUSSELL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Sind) (b. 19th Mar., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 14th Nov., 1927; offg. supt., Sind, May, 1932.

HARRIS, REGINALD JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (asst. tel. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 29 Jan., 1901).—Asst. tel. supt., Jan., 1902; asst. transportation supt. tel. (junior), Nov., 1922; senior scale, Jan., 1939.

HARRIS, RONALD MONTAGUE JOSEPH, B.A., Burma Office (asst. princpl.) (b. 6th May, 1913).—Educ. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. princpl., India Office, 16th Nov., 1936; posted to Burma Office, April, 1937; services loaned to the Cabinet Offices, Jan., 1939.

HARRIS, WILLIAM, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Vety. Service, Assam (b. 4th Feb., 1879).—Joined the service, 25th April, 1906, for training in Bengal; supt., Assam, May, 1907; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

HARRISON, ALBERT FRANCIS LOUIS HELFRICH, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd Dec., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; I.A.R.O., Oct., 1916, to Feb., 1919; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1926; confd., July, 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

HARRISON, ARTHUR NEVILLE JOHN, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 15th Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1904; asst. examr., Jan., 1905; dep. examr., April, 1909; dep. chief auditor, B.B. and C.I. Rly., April, 1924; chief auditor, ditto, Dec., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1936.

HARRISON, ARTHUR SIDNEY, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; I.A.R.O., from 19th June, 1918, to 5th Feb., 1919; exec. engr., March, 1919; ret'd., June, 1936.

HARRISON, SIR CHARLTON SCOTT CHOLMELEY, Kt., C.I.E., M.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th May, 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1902; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; tempy. suptg. engr., Feb., 1921; offg. chief engr., Lloyd Barrage, July, 1923; chief engr., March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1928; Kt., Jan., 1932; ret'd., June, 1935.

HARRISON, CLIFFORD PEVENSEY MATTISON, M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Feb., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Jan., 1907, as asst. engr.; arrived, 11th Jan., 1907; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., June, 1913; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., May, 1920; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1927; confd., Sept., 1929; ch. engr., Nov., 1935; ret'd., Jan., 1939.

HARRISON, FRANCIS ANTHONY KITCHENER, B.A., India Office (asst. princpl., political dept.) (b. 28th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Winchester and New College, Oxford; apptd. asst. princpl., 1st Nov., 1937.

HARRISON, GEORGE FRANCIS, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 3rd July, 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 3rd Dec., 1928; confd., Jan., 1931.

HARRISON, MISS GLADYS, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 27th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as vice-principal, Queen Mary's Coll., Lahore, 15th Nov., 1919; offg. prinpl. ditto, April, 1921; confd., April, 1922; ret'd., June, 1936.

HARRISON, JAMES LEES, Indian Forest Engrg. Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. 3rd May, 1893).—Joined the service as forest engr., Coorg, 24th Nov., 1921; ditto, Assam, 4th Dec., 1927; dep. consvr., Oct., 1932; lecturer, Indian Forest College, Dehra Dun, Sept., 1938.

HARRISON, JOSEPH RICHARD, F.G.S., Indian State Rlys (ch. mining engr., rly. bd.) (b. 25th Feb., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. coal supt., 13th March, 1916; colliery supt., Kargali colliery, G.I.P. Rly., Feb., 1923; supt. of collieries, Oct., 1925; offg. chief mining engr., rly. board, variously from Apl., 1927; confd., June, 1935.

HARRISON, ROBERT TULLIS, C.S.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 18th Sept., 1876).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1899, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1908; ditto, and under sec. to govt., P.W.D., Bombay, May, 1910, to April, 1912; K.i-H. medal, 2nd class, Dec., 1911; suptg. engr., July, 1918; offg. chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., Dec., 1920; confd., July, 1921; sec. to govt., P.W.D., March, 1925; C.S.I., March, 1929; ret'd., July, 1931.

HARRISON, WILLIAM, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 22nd Jan., 1877).—Clerk 2nd class, 21st Oct., 1897; supervisor, Sept., 1914; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; prinpl. ditto, April, 1926; ret'd., May, 1938.

HART, FRANK ANTHONY ALBERT, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Orissa) (b. 30th Oct., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, on 29th Nov., 1921; agency forest officer, Nov., 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1936.

HART, FREDERIC JOHN ALBERT, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 15th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1922.

HARTLEY, ARTHUR COULTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 24th Mar., 1906).—Educ. at University of Manchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1929; arrived, November, 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1931.

HARTLEY, GEORGE EDWARD, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Apl., 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 24th Oct., 1930.

HARTLEY, JOHN MATHEWS, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 7th Mar., 1874).—Joined the service, 15th Jan., 1894, as asst. examr., p.w. accts.; dep. examr., Jan., 1898; examr., April, 1903; govt. examr. of accts., Aug., 1910; on depon. to mil. accts. dept., from Jan., 1918; offg. chief auditor, E.B. Rly., April, 1920; chief auditor, N.W. Rly., Oct., 1921; acctnt.-gen., rlys., Oct., 1925; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

HARTNOLL, ERIC STEWART, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 5th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. consr. on 21st Dec., 1915; dep. consr., Feb., 1924; offg. consvr., Apr., 1936.

HARTOG, SIR PHILIP JOSEPH, K.B.E., C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc. (b. 2nd Mar., 1864).—Educ. University Coll. Sch., Owens Coll., Manchester, and Universities of Paris and Heidelberg; C.I.E. Jan., 1917; member of commn. on Univ. of Calcutta, 1917-19; vice-chancellor of Univ. of Dacca since 1920; Kt., Jan., 1926; chairman of public services commn., Oct., 1926, to Apl., 1930; on depn. as chairman to educn. commee. of Indian statutory commn., June, 1928; retd., Apl., 1930; K.B.E., June, 1930.

HARTSHORNE, JOHN FRANCIS BYNG, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th July, 1884).—Educ. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxf.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 20th Nov. 1908, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., asst. settl. offr., and talukdari settl. offr.; actg. collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, Dec., 1922; actg. dep. commr. of salt and excise, Dec., 1923; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1924; pol. agent, Surat, May, 1927; collr., June, 1927; pres. civil and mily. examn. commn., April, 1931; retd., Nov., 1934.

HARTY, ARTHUR HENRY, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S.—Apptd. offg. supt., central mental hosp., Yeravada, Aug., 1925; confd., Sept., 1926; offg. civil-surg., Dec., 1929.

HARVEY, ALEXANDER FORDYCE, F.C.H., late Indian State Railways (b. 24th Mar., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll., joined the dept., Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; dep. dir. of stores, Apl., 1924; technical officer, rly. board (temp.), Sept., 1925; offg. eng. in ch., surveys and construction, April, 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., Jan., 1930; confd., Feb., 1932; offg. ch. engr. Mar., 1932; dir., rly. bd., Nov., 1932; offg. agent., E.B. rly., Mar., 1935; ch. engr., June, 1935; agent, E.B. rly., Oct., 1935; retd., March, 1939.

HARVEY, BERNARD ALEXANDER MACLEOD FORTESQUE, B.A., Indian Police (b. 19th Nov., 1916).—Appointed asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 20th Feb., 1939.

HARVEY, CECIL WALTER LEWERY, O.B.E., M.C., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Oct., 1897).—First commn. dated 12th Nov., 1914; in milty. employ to 30th Apl., 1924; asst. mag. and collr., Agra, May, 1924; on foreign service as adviser and pte. sec. to Raja of Poonch, Jan., 1925; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Jan., 1927; under-sec. to A.G.G. in Rajputana and C.C. in Ajmer Merwara, March, 1927; under-sec. to resident at Hyderabad, April, 1927; ditto to agent to gov.-genl. in Rajputana, May, 1928; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Mar., 1929; confd., Sept., 1929; on foreign service in Manipur State, Dec., 1930; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, Feb., 1933; secty. to A.G.G., Gujerat states, Apl., 1933; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar agency, Oct., 1934; secty., to A.G.G., states of Western India, Mar., 1936; O.B.E., Jan., 1938; Prime Minister, Alwar State, Sept., 1938.

HARVEY, Rev. Canon GEORGE FREDERICK, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 29th June, 1877).—Joined 23rd Apl., 1909; jun. chaplain, June, 1912; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1922; retd., Jan., 1934.

HARVEY, GODFREY ERIC, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th April, 1889).—Educ. at Exeter Sch., London Univ. (external student), and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1912, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., Sept., 1917; joint regr., co-operative societies dept., Sept., 1919; sub-divl. officer, March, 1925; offg. supt., N. Shan states, Oct., 1927; confd., Jan., 1929; on duty in connection with Wa states, Nov., 1931; offg. secty. local govt. dept., Burma, June, 1932; retd., Dec., 1934.

HARVEY, GUY JOSEPH, late Indian Police (b. 5th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 29th Nov., 1913; dist. supt., May, 1918; awarded King's police medal, June, 1932; retd., Aug., 1937.

HARVEY, THOMAS, M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 13th Nov., 1885).—Joined, 1st Oct., 1909, as asst. engr., Madras; exec. engr., Oct., 1919; supt. engr., housing and suburban circle, Bombay, Oct., 1925; on spl. duty in connection with Back Bay Reclamation Scheme, Nov., 1926, to Feb., 1929; retd., Feb., 1938.

HASAN, ABUL (see Abul, H.).

HASAN, ALI (see Ali Hasan).

HASAN, ASGHAR (see Asghar Hasan).

HASAN, CHAUDHRI SAIYID ABDUL (see Abdul, H. C. S.).

HASAN, MUNSHI MAHMUD (see Mahmud, H. M.).

HASAN, SAIYID WAZIR (see Wazir Hasan, S.).

HASAN, SHEIKH MAHMUD, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 14th June, 1908).—Educated at Government Jubilee High School, Lucknow, Lucknow University and Magdalen College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th October, 1933; arrived in India November, 1933, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Feb., 1936.

HASAN, SYED SIDDIQUE, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 4th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at University of Lucknow and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, 19th Nov., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1933; officer on special duty, co-op. societies.

HASAN, ZAFAR (see Zafar Hasan).

HASAN ZAHEER, SAIYID, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. to govt., United Provs.) (b. 16th Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Nov., 1923; arrived, 14th Dec., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., March, 1925; jt. mag., April, 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1928; and again, Sept., 1933; addl. dep. secty., genl. bch., United Provs. (temp.), Apl., 1935; dep. secty. to govt., U.P., Mar., 1937.

HASAN, ZIA-UL (see Zia-ul Hasan).

HASHIM RAZA, SAIYID, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 16th Feb., 1910); educated at Government High School, Hardoi, Lucknow Christian College, University of Lucknow and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the Servic. 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. commr.

HASHMI, ZAMANSHAH MAHABUBSHAH, Khan Bahadur, late Indian Police (b. 1st July, 1881).—Joined the service, 2nd July, 1906; dep. supt., Dec., 1920; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Dec., 1934; retd., July, 1936.

HASLAM, ROY HAVELOCK, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 30th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1913; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1918; supt. of police, April, 1925; ditto and dep. commr., Bombay, Dec., 1927; supt., Aden, Nov., 1928; dep. commr. of police for air raid precautions Bombay, July, 1939.

HASLEHUST, HAROLD MAITLAND M.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Bombay) (b. 30th Nov., 1888).—Joined 10th Dec., 1908; on milty. duty from May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; supt. of police, June, 1921; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1923; offg. dep. inspr. gen., Mar., 1936; confd., May, 1936.

HASNAIN, KHAN SAHIB S. G. (b. 13th Jan., 1891).—Apptd. asst. secty. legislative assembly dept., April, 1939.

HASSAN, AHMAD, Khan Bahadur, M.A., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 20th Jan., 1885) Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1908; extra asst. commr., July, 1912; dep. commr., subst. perm., July, 1933.

HASSAN, KHWAJA MUHAMMAD, Khan Bahadur, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (b. 20th Mar., 1891).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1914; dep. dir., estabt., rly. bd., May, 1932; supervisor of rly. lab., May, 1937.

HASSAN, SYED FIDA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 4th Dec., 1908).—Educated at D.S. High School, Lahore, Punjab University, the School of Oriental Studies, London, and Oriel College, Oxford; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offer., Apl., 1936.

HASTINGS, ARCHIBALD WILLIAMS (mil. accts. dept. (b. 18th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd cl., Feb., 1912; confd., cl. I offer., Nov., 1934.

HATCH-BARNWELL, ANTHONY WILLIAM CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 6th March, 1912).—Educated at Sherborne and Worcester College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1938.

HATCH-BARNWELL, STEPHEN BENEDICT, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 26th June, 1909).—Educated at Malvern College and Keble College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HATFIELD, MORGAN BESLEY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Jan., 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

HATT-COOK, ROWLAND, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 4th Nov., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; R.H.A. from 8th Mar., 1916, to 31st. Mar., 1919; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., May, 1933; and again, June, 1934; retd., Jan., 1937.

HATTIANGADI, SHRIDHAR SHIVARAO, B.A., (Bom.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 24th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at University of Bombay and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1935.

HATTYANGADI, SUMETRA ANANTAYYA, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (b. 26th July, 1879).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Apl., 1909, to 23rd July, 1912; sub-judge, July, 1912; ditto, class I., Nov., 1927; offg. judge, small causes ct., Poona; retd., July, 1934.

HAVELOCK, LIONEL ACTON; late Burma Excise Service (b. 5th May, 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 13th Jan., 1898; supt., Sept., 1906; offg. ch. supt., Apl., 1929; confd., July, 1929; retd., May 1933.

HAWES, CHARLES GEORGE, C.I.E., M.C., B.Sc. (Lon.), A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., M.R.San.I., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 7th Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1912; in milty. employ from 19th June, 1915, to 7th Feb., 1919; exec. engr., Dec., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1939.

HAWES, DERRICK GORDON HARRINGTON, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd May, 1907).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1927; in mil. employ up to 23rd Mar., 1934; p.a. to Resident, Kashmir, Mar., 1934; under training in the Punjab, Oct., 1934; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Mar., 1936; pol. agent, Bhopal, Sept., 1936; under-secty. to the A.G.G. in C.I., Nov., 1936; offg. dep. Opium Agent in Malwa, in addn., Nov., 1936; confd. in the Indian Pol. service, April 1937; on foreign service in Alwar State, Dec. 1937; under-secty., pol. dept. Mar., 1938; confd. Nov. 1938.

HAWES, RONALD NISBETT, Capt., R.E., retd., India Posts and Tels. (chief tele. commn. engr.) (b. 11th Nov., 1895).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1920; divl. engr., wireless, Oct., 1923; dir., Calcutta stn., Indian State broadcasting service, in addn., Apl. to Oct., 1933; chief tele., commn. engr., Burma, Apl., 1937.

HAWKER, REGINALD JAMES, Office of High commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 9th Dec., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class Clerk, India store depôt, 21st Sept., 1903; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, May, 1922; higher exec. officer, May, 1926.

HAWKES, FRANK ROXBURGH, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. comml. manager, N.W. Rly.) (b. 9th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1904; asst. traffic supt., Sept., 1906; dist. ditto, April, 1914; O.B.E., June, 1919; divl. comml. officer, Nov., 1924; offg. divl. supt., Nov., 1927; confd., Apl., 1930; offg. ch. comml. manager, Nov., 1932; confd., Dec., 1932.

HAWLEY, HERBERT, M.Sc., F.I.C., Medical Dept., Madras (b. 4th Nov., 1887).—Apptd. govt. analyst, King Inst., Guindy, 9th Jan., 1924.

HAWORTH, REV. CYRIL EVINGTON, late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 1st Feb., 1903).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, United Provs., 6th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; retd., Feb., 1936.

HAWORTH, EDWIN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Rlys. (dist. medl. officer, N.W. rly.) (b. 14th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1923; dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. rly., July, 1929; ditto, N.W. rly., Dec., 1935.

HAWORTH, SIR LIONEL BERKELEY HOLT K.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.).—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1893; in mil. employ to May, 1901; pol. asst., March, 1902; asst. to a.g.-g. in Baluchistan, March, 1902; asst. pol. agent, Lower Zhoab, March, 1903; asst. to a.g.-g. in Baluchistan, and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, March, 1904; consul, Kerman, May, 1905; ditto, Kermanshah, Oct., 1906; H.B.M.'s consul for Arabistan, and asst. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Jan., 1911, to May, 1914; pol. agent, and H.B.M.'s consul, Muscat, Nov., 1916; pol. agent, southern states of Central India, Dec., 1920; H.B.M.'s consul general and agent to govt. of India in Khorasan, March, 1924; offg. pol.-resident, Persian Gulf, Jan., 1927; confd., May, 1927; K.B.E., June, 1928; retd., May, 1929.

HAWTHORN, GEORGE THOMAS, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 20th June, 1892).—Apptd. to store dept., Nov., 1919; clerical officer, July, 1921; exec. officer, Jan., 1926.

HAY, ALLAN SELWOOD, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 21st July, 1888).—Asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1916; asst. exec. engr., 18th Jan., 1921; exec. engr. (prov.), Jan., 1936; confd., Mar., 1938.

HAY, BROUGHTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Orissa) (b. 2nd Nov., 1914).—Educ. at Radley and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in B. and O. as asst. mag. and collr.; posted to Orissa from May, 1938.

HAY, NORMAN TREVOR CARLTON, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 8th Feb., 1931.

HAY, ROBERT, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (dep. dir. genl., I.M.S.) (b. 8th Mar., 1889).—First commn. dated 23rd Jan., 1917; in milty. employ to 25th June, 1925; offg. agency surg., Bundelkhand, July, 1925; offg. medl. officer, Seistan consulate, Feb., 1926; agency surg., D. I. Khan, Nov., 1928; ditto, Kurram, April, 1930; resdy. surg., Kashmir, Apl., 1931; agency surgeon, Bundelkhand, Dec., 1931; civil surg., Quetta, Nov., 1933; ch. medl. officer, Baluchistan, Mar., 1935; resdy. surg., Mewar, Sept., 1935; ch. medl. officer, Rajputana, Dec., 1935; dep. dir. genl., I.M.S., Jan., 1939.

HAY, WILLIAM RUPERT, C.I.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 16th Dec., 1893).—First commn. dated 3rd July, 1915; joined pol. dept., 25th May, 1920; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, August, 1921; asst. commr., Bannu, Dec., 1923; ditto, Tonk,

March, 1924; pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Nov., 1925; asst. commr., Mardan, Dec., 1928; jt. dep. commr., Peshawar, Aug., 1930; spl. magte. in D.I. Khan dist., Aug., 1931; on special duty, Dokalim Boundary commn., June, 1932; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, July, 1932; and again, Jan., 1933; offg. ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; counsellor, Brit. legn., Kabul, Nov., 1934; offg. dep. secty., F. and P. dept., Govt. of India, Oct., 1936; confd., as dep. secty., External Affairs Dept., Apl., 1937; ditto, Nov., 1938.

HAYAT, SIR UMAR (see Umar Hayat Khan).

HAYDEN, ARTHUR BARTRUM, Lt., R.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 16th Nov., 1901).—First commn. dated 13th July, 1921; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1925; exec. engr., Nov., 1933.

HAYES, ARTHUR ANDERSON, late Indian Police (b. 26th July, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 26th Jan., 1920; offg. dist. supt., Nov., 1925; confd., May, 1926; retd., Oct., 1938.

HAYES, CYRIL BEAUCHAMP, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., Burma rly.) (b. 30th Apl., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 12th May, 1920; exec. engr., Burma rly., June, 1929; offg. supt. of stores, Feb., 1937.

HAYES, SIDNEY NUTTALL, F.R.C.S. (E.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof., King Edward medl. coll., Lahore, Punjab) (b. 19th July, 1891).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1921; in milty. employ to 1st Oct., 1922; civil surg., Oct., 1923; offg. prof. of midwifery, King Edward Medical Coll., Lahore, May, 1925; Lieut.-Col., Sept., 1937.

HAYFIELD, WILLIAM MONTAGUE, late Indian Service of Engrs. Burma (b. 16th Feb., 1883).—Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1904; asst. engr., Oct., 1905; exec. engr., Dec., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1928; retd., Feb., 1938.

HAYLEY, THOMAS THEODORE STEIGER, B.A., Indian C.S. (Asst. Commr., Assam) (b. 4th October, 1913).—Educated at Clifton College and Peterhouse College, Cambridge; appointed on probation, 29th Sept., 1937; confirmed, 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in Assam as Asst. Commr.

HAYMAN, ALBERT MELVILLE, O.B.E., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 21st Aug., 1882).—Joined the service, 20th March, 1900; dep. acctnt.-gen., April, 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; services placed at the disposal of railway bd. from 4th July, 1923; dep. dir., finance, rly. bd., Oct., 1924; dir. of finance, rly. bd., Aug., 1925; member, rly. bd., April, 1929; retd., Jan., 1933.

HAYNE, PERCY ALFRED, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 2nd July, 1876).—Joined the service as dist. medl. officer, 25th Oct., 1906; ch. medl. officer, Apl., 1927; retd., May, 1933.

HAYWARD, SIR MAURICE HENRY WESTON Kt., K.C.S.I., LL.B., Barr., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Harrow and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Aug., 1895; jud. asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1896; acting dist. judge, Karachi, Nov., 1899; confd., June, 1900; legal

remembrancer, Bombay, Nov., 1905, and sec. to govt. legal dept., July, 1907; extra addl. jud. commr. of Sind, March-April, 1908; dist. judge, Jan., 1909; addl. judl. commr., Sinda, Aug., 1910; judge, high ct., Bombay, May, 1918; member of govr.'s exec. council, Jan., 1921; Kt., June, 1923; K.C.S.I., June, 1925; retd., July, 1926.

HAYWARD, Rev. WILLIAM GEORGE, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (senr. chaplain) (b. 18th June, 1888).—Appdt. junr. chaplain, Madras, 5th March, 1926; senr. chaplain, Mar., 1936.

HAYWOOD, WILLIAM GERALD, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.) (b. 5th Sept., 1908).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1929; confd., Nov., 1931.

HAZARI, RAJENDRA NATH, B.A., LL.B. (b. 4th Nov., 1901) (Military Accounts Dept.).—Joined service as an officer in Class II, March, 1926; F.A. and secty., Quetta Reconstruction Committee, April, 1939.

HEAD, EDGAR ALFRED, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 7th April, 1876).—Educ. at King Edward's Gr. Sch., Bath; clerk, 2nd class, 30th May, 1895; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

HEAD, WILLIAM RALEIGH, late Burma Frontier Service (b. 13th June, 1880).—Joined the service as inspr. of police, Jan., 1901; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1906; asst. supt., B.F.S., Dec., 1922; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925; confd., Aug., 1925; retd., Oct., 1934.

HEADLEY, ROBERT HOLLOWELL, O.B.E., LL.B., late Indian Office (b. 9th Mar., 1876).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and King's Coll., London; clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 9th Mar., 1896; LL.B. (honours), London Univ., 1901; transf. to mil. dept., Nov., 1901; called to the bar (Middle Temple), 1903; appdt. member standing advisory cttee. on colours and distinctions, July, 1911; appdt. member, Navy and Army Ins. Fund Advisory Cttee., April, 1913; minor staff clerk, May, 1913; sec. to ordnance services cttee., 1914; resdt. clerk (temp.), Feb., 1915, to Aug., 1919; staff clerk (temp.), April, 1915; M.B.E., Jan., 1918; asst. to mil. sec. and to the staff offr., mil. dept., July, 1919; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; chief exec. officer, June, 1924; O.B.E., June, 1930; retd., Nov., 1937.

HEALD, Sir BENJAMIN HERBERT, Kt., V.D., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 16th Sept., 1874).—Educ. at Nottingham High Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; appdt. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlmnt. offr.; dist. judge, Dec., 1908; addl. judge, ct. of judl. commr., March, 1917; divl. judge, Nov., 1917; judge, chief ct., Lower Burma, June, 1921; judge, high ct., Rangoon, Dec., 1922; Kt., Jan., 1926; retd., Nov., 1933.

HEALE, DAVID HERBERT, Indian Police (b. 4th Feb., 1919).—Appdt. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Nov., 1938.

HEALE, ROBERT JOHN WINGFIELD, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., Lt.-Col., I.A. (retd.) (b. 24th Sept., 1876).—First commn., dated 23rd May, 1900; in mil. emp. to Jan., 1903; asst. commr., Punjab, Jan., 1903; pol. asst. N.W.F. Prov., Jan., 1904; asst. commr. and commdnt., B.M.P.,

Bannu, Oct., 1911; ditto, Peshawar, Nov., 1912; asst. commr., Charsadda, April, 1914; ditto, Thal sub-div., April, 1915; dep. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1915; ditto, D.I. Khan, Dec., 1915; on spl. duty as pol. offr. with Waziristan fd. force from May to July, 1917; pol. agent, Kurram, Oct., 1917; O.B.E., Dec., 1917; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1925; offg. resident, Gwalior, March, 1928; offg. ch. commr. and agent to govt. genl. N.W.F. Prov., May, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; agent to govr. genl. in C.I., June, 1930; retd., Jan., 1932.

HEALY, DANIEL, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 22nd Aug., 1884).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1904; supt. of police, Mar., 1914; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1923; actg. commr. of police, July, 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-gen. of police, April, 1926; again Nov., 30th, confirmed May, 1931; offg. commr. of police, Bombay, March, 1927; again, Apr., 1930; offg. inspr.-genl., June, 1934; inspr.-genl., Sind, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; retd., Aug., 1939.

HEALY, ROWLAND HENRY SAVAGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 13th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Earlsfort House School, 1905).—Educ. at Earlsfort House School, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., Rangoon, Aug., 1932; asst. dist. judge, Nov., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1936; confd., Oct., 1938.

HEANEY, GEORGE FREDERICK, Capt., R.E. Survey of India (supt.) (b. 1st June, 1897).—First commn., dated 26th May, 1916; in milty. employ to 10th Aug., 1921; asst. supt., Aug., 1921; offg. supt. Mar., 1929; confd., Aug., 1929.

HEAPE, ROBERT H., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Ry.s. (exec. engr.) (b. 8th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., 30th June, 1921; exec. engr., Apl., 1935.

HEARN, GEORGE WILLIAM, India Office (suptg. executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 23rd June, 1889).—Educ. at Central Foundation Sch., London; appdt. clerk, 2nd class, 4th Dec., 1907; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; acting staff clerk, April, 1921; higher exec. officer (acting), April, 1922; confd., Nov., 1922; also assessor of income tax; suptg. exec. officer, Sept., 1935.

HEARN, Sir GORDON RISLEY, Kt., C.I.E., D.S.O., A.M.I.C.E., Lt.-Col., R.E., late Railway Dept.—Appdt. asst. engr., N.W. Ry., 19th June, 1894; dep. consg. engr. for ry.s., Madras, Oct., 1901, to Nov., 1902; exec. engr., Jan., 1903; suptg. engr., May, 1918; on mil. duty from Oct., 1914, to Dec., 1918; chief engr., March, 1924; C.I.E., June, 1924; Kt., July, 1926; retd., Sept., 1926.

HEARN, JOHN WHITCOMBE, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 27th Nov., 1885).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; appdt. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Oct., 1920; dep. commr. (provl.), Oct., 1924; ditto (perm.), Oct., 1927; home secty. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1930; offg. commr., Feb., 1936; addl. secty. to govt., Punjab, Feb., 1936.

HEATH, CLEMENT JOHN, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 10th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1910; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; dep. commr. of police, Rangoon, Nov., 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1936.

HEATHCOTE, LAWRENCE HECTOR MARK, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 30th Oct., 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1930; and again, June, 1933; retd., Sept., 1936.

HECTOR, GEORGE PITTENDRIGH, M.A., D.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 20th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1908, as supy. botanist; apptd. economic botanist on probn., July, 1909; confd., Jan., 1910; offg. dir. of agric., Bengal, July, 1925; offg. dir. of agric., Feb., 1929; confd., Aug., 1932; retd., Oct., 1935.

HECTOR, JOHN ALEXANDER ERNEST, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 11th Feb., 1901).—Joined the service on 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; ditto, temp. aviation divn., central p.w.d., Rangoon, Feb., 1935.

HEDDON, HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 13th Oct., 1882).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., 16th Dec., 1902; transf. to store dept., Jan., 1917; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

HEENAN, JOHN LAWDER, Indian Police (b. 21st Apl., 1896) (dist. supt., Burma).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., on 7th Feb., 1921; offg. dist. supt., July, 1924; confd., May, 1929.

HEINIG, JOSEPH ERIS, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (asst. exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 27th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

HEJMADI, VASUDEO SUBRAO, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 26th Jan., 1900).—Educ. at St. Xavier's High School, Bombay, and Caius Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1923; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1923, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Jan., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Feb., 1931.

HELLIWELL, JAMES WILFRED, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 25th Aug., 1881).—Joined the service, 13th Jan., 1909; asst. loco. supt., 1st Jan., 1915; on military duty from 24th Oct., 1915; asst. works manager, Mar., 1927; offg. works manager, Apl., 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; retd., May, 1933.

HELME, CHARLES GEORGE GORDON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th March, 1885).—Educ. at Paisley Gr. Sch., Glasgow Univ., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 9th Dec., 1909, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Aug., 1911; ditto, Assam, April, 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1918, to Feb., 1919; offg. dep. commr., April, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), March, 1926; retd., Feb., 1934.

HEMEON, CLARENCE REID, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Central Provs.) (b. 30th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Cork Gr. Sch., and Univ. Coll., Cork; apptd. by nomination; joined the service,

1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 12th April, 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; sub-judge, June, 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1927; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. legal membr. and sec. legal dept., Central Provs., June, 1932; confd., Jan., 1935.

HEMINGWAY, CHARLES ERIC MONTGOMERIE, B.A., India Office (pte. sec. to permanent under-sec. of state) (b. 20th Dec., 1912).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. princpl., 26th Oct., 1936; resdt. clerk, Feb., 1938; pte. sec. to permanent under-sec. of state, May, 1939.

HEMMING, Rev. Canon CHARLES HENRY, late Indian Eccles. Estal. (Punjab) (b. 27th July, 1875).—Apptd. junior chaplain on 21st Dec., 1912; on milty. duty from 20th June, 1916, to 22nd Aug., 1917; senr. chaplain, Dec. 1922; retd. Feb., 1931.

HEMMINGS, LEONARD, Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th Apl., 1894).—Production engr., E.I. rly., Apl., 1927; confd., senr. scale, Sept., 1937.

HEMSWORTH, THOMAS GARRETT FREDERICK, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 2nd Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. on 1st Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

HENDERSON, ALAN GERALD RUSSELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 22nd March, 1886).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1915; I.A.R.O., Jan. to March, 1918; dist. and sessions, judge, March, 1923; offg. secty., judicial dept., and supt. and membr. of legal affairs, Apl., 1932; confd., July, 1932; offg. addl. judge, high ct., Calcutta, variously from Apl., 1933; actg. judge, ditto, Apl., 1934; confd., Mar., 1937.

HENDERSON, CHARLES ALEXANDER, C.S.I., V.D., Indian C.S. (member, board of revenue, Madras) (b. 2nd May, 1882).—Educ. at Haileybury, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. and spl. settlt. offr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1914; dir. of land records, June, 1916; on foreign service as Diwan, Jeypore Estate, April, 1920; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1921; sub-collr., Aug., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Apl., 1926; offg. secty. to govt., dev. dept., Madras, May, 1933; offg. second secty. to govt., Madras, Jan., 1935; and again Mar., 1936; offg. 2nd member, bd. of rev., May, 1936; 3rd member, ditto, Mar., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; 2nd member, ditto, Feb., 1938; agent of the Govt. of India in Burma, Sept., 1939 to May, 1939.

HENDERSON, CHARLES JOSEPH, C.E. (Glas.), A.M.I.C.E., A.I.E.E., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 3rd July, 1878).—Joined the service as hd. master, sch. of engrg., Dacca, 11th Mar., 1910; prin., Dacca engrg. sch., Dec., 1923; retd. May, 1933.

HENDERSON, GEOFFRY STEELE, N.D.A., N.D.D., late Impl. Dept. of Agric. (b. 23rd Apl., 1882).—Joined the service, 18th Jan., 1907, as asst. to the dep. dir. of agric., Bombay; 2nd dep. dir. of agric., July, 1907; dep. dir. of agric., Feb.,

1909; offg. impl. agriculturist, agricl. research inst., Pusa, June, 1916; confirmed, March, 1918; on deputn. as controller (agricl. requirements, Mesopotamia), under Indian munitions bd., July, 1918, to June, 1919; dir. of agric., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1929; ret'd., July, 1934.

HENDERSON, JOHN WILMOT, Indian State Rlys. (dir'l. transpn. officer, N.W. rly.) (b. 11th July, 1893).—Joined the service, 4th June, 1912; asst. transpn. traffic officer, Oct., 1924; offg. divl. personnel officer, N.W. rly., Mar., 1930; divl. transpn. officer, Mar., 1931.

HENDERSON, KENNETH HARVEY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 24th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Mill Hill, New College, Oxford, and King's College, London; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. asst. and comdt. border milty. police, June, 1933; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1935.

HENNESSY, JOSEPH MARTIN REEVES L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. Medical Dept., Central Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 7th May, 1886).—First commn. 31st Jan., 1914; civil surgeon, Jubbulpore, Sept., 1931; offg. in-pr.-genl. of civil hosps., C.P., May, 1935; and again, Feb., 1937.

HENNESSY, JOSSLEYN, B.A. Hons. (Oxon) (prinpl. information officer, home dept., Govt. of India) (b. 17th Nov., 1902).—Educ. at Charterhouse and New Coll., Oxford; chev. of legion of honr., 1937; apptd. dir. of public information, Sept., 1937.

HENNIKER-GOTLEY, GEORGE RAINALD, B.A., D.S.O., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 5th Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 18th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Aug., 1926.

HENRY, COLIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 23rd Feb., 1902).—Educ. at King Edward VII School, Sheffield, and Wadham College, Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, variously from June, 1931; confd., Nov., 1936; attd. govt. of India, legis. dept., Apl., 1938.

HENSHAW, LEWIS, A.M.I.Mech.E., A.M.I.E.E., A.M.I.Struct.E., M.I.E. (Ind.) (suptg. engr., Electricity Dept., Madras) (b. 2nd May, 1888).—Joined the service as elec. inspr. to govt., on 8th Nov., 1920; elec. engr., Mar., 1921; confd., Apl., 1924; suptg. engr., offg., Aug., 1935; confd., Aug., 1937.

HERAPATH, WILLIAM LANE KYNASTON, Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 24th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1912, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, Oct., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; asst. to inspr.-genl., Nov., 1937, to Mar., 1938.

HERBERT, CHARLES GORDON, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Feb., 1920; arrived, 11th May, 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., May, 1923; offg. collr. and dist. magte., March, 1928; ditto (provl. subs.), Jan., 1935; secty. to A.G.G., in Rajputana, Mar., 1936; jt. secty., pol. dept., May, 1938; confd. in the Indian Political Service, April 1939; C.I.E., June, 1939.

HERBERT, CHRISTOPHER ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 15th June, 1913).—Educ. at Mountjoy School, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

HERBERT, Lt.-Col. Sir JOHN ARTHUR, G.C.I.E., (b. 19th Nov., 1895).—A.D.C. to Viceroy, 1926-28; Governor of Bengal, November, 1939.

HERBERT, VAUGHAN AUGUSTUS, M.A., M.C., late Indian Forest Service (b. 13th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1909, as asst. consr.; on mil. service from June, 1915, to June, 1919; dep. consr., June, 1919; offg. consvr., March, 1927; confd., June, 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1938.

HERCHENRODER, MARIE FERDINAND PHILIPPE, LL.B., Barr., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 1st Jan., 1893).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Curepipe, Mauritius, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 4th Dec., 1916, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Aug., 1918; jt. mag., Nov., 1920; temp. addl. dep. sec. to govt., judl. dept., March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1926; confd., Oct. 1929; ret'd., July, 1931.

HERDON, GERALD CHARLES ROUTH, Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 30th July, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., Mar., 1925; confd., May, 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928.

HERON, ALEXANDER MACMILLAN, D.Sc., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 31st July, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Dec., 1906; offg. supt., Jan., 1920; supt., Sept., 1929; offg. dir., Apl., 1932, Oct., 1933, and Sept., 1935; confd., Nov., 1935; ret'd., July, 1939.

HERON, DAVIS, C.I.E., M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 8th Jan., 1878).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1903; in milty. employ to 25th Oct., 1912; M.O., Seistan Consulate, Dec., 1912; C.I.E., June, 1918; resdy. surg., Mewar, Jan., 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

HERON, FRANCIS ADENS THEODORE, O.B.E., late Indian Police Service (b. 14th June, 1890).—Joined the service 26th Nov., 1910; asst. supt., May, 1912; O.B.E., April, 1921; supt., April, 1922; ret'd., July, 1932.

HERVEY, ARTHUR CHARLES CONSTANTINE, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian Educl. Service (prinpl., Ludhiana Coll., Punjab) (b. 18th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as prof., Khalsa Coll., Amritsar, on 7th Oct., 1918; prinpl., Ludhiana Coll., 26th Sept., 1925.

HESTERLOW, ALFRED MAURICE VICTOR, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (dir. of public health, Assam) (b. 4th May, 1887).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ until 2nd Feb., 1924; asst. dir. of public health, Madras, Feb., 1924; prof., medl. coll., Madras, May, 1926; asst. dir. of public health, Madras, Aug., 1931; offg. dir., Mar., 1934; ditto, Bombay, Apl., 1935; dir., Assam, Jan., 1938.

HETHERINGTON, THEODORE JOHN HOSTE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Apl., 1914).—Educ. at Wellington and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 6th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec.,

1938 and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.; retd., April, 1939.

HEWETSON, CYRIL EDWARD, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr. Cent. Provs.) (b. 18th June, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 25th Dec., 1926 dep. consvr., Dec., 1930.

HEWETT, DECIMUS HENRY, Indian State Rly. (works manager, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 30th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service 12th Mar., 1926, as production engr., G.I.P. rly., Mar., 1926; offg. works manager, G.I.P. rly., May, 1928; confd., Oct., 1932; dep. ch. mech. engr. provl., Nov., 1938.

HEWETT, DOUGLAS PEARSE, late Indian Forest Service (b. 29th Sept., 1885).—Arrived, 11th Nov., 1907, and served as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Dec., 1912; on mil. service, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; offg. consvr., Aug. 1924; confd., Dec., 1926; retd., Sept., 1933.

HEWETT, Sir JOHN PRESCOTT, G.C.S.I., K.B.E., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 17th Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; in charge of provl. gazetteer, Aug., 1883; acted as asst. acctnt.-gen., Jan., 1884; junr. sec., board of rev., April, 1884; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1886; confd., Aug., 1887; offd. as private sec. to the viceroy in 1888 and 1892, and as sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1890; offg. census commr. for India, July, 1890; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1890; C.I.E., May, 1891; dep. commr., March, 1893; sec. of royal commn. on opium, Oct., 1893; mag. and collr., April, 1894; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1895; C.S.I., Dec., 1898; member of plague commn., Nov., 1898; offg. chief commr., Central Provs., Sept., 1902; confd., Nov., 1903; temp. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Aug. 1904; confd., Dec., 1904; lieut.-govr. of United Prov. (K.C.S.I.), Jan., 1907 to July, 1912; special duty in connection with Coronation Durbar from March, 1911; G.C.S.I., Dec., 1911; author of contributions to provl. gazetteer; knight of justice and bailiff of Egle of St. John of Jerusalem; retd., Sept., 1912; K.B.E., Dec., 1917; M.P. for Luton Divn., Nov., 1922, to Dec., 1923.

HEWITT, REV. GEORGE, late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 22nd Nov., 1875).—Joined, 27th June, 1908; jun. chaplain, June, 1911; senr. chaplain, June, 1921; retd., Nov., 1931.

HEWITT, JOHN ARCHIBALD, Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 23rd Dec., 1898).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1922; exec. engr., Nov., 1931.

HEWITT, RICHARD CLAUDE LIFFORD, B.A., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 10th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., variously from May, 1934.

HEWLETT, GEORGE GORDON, India Office (higher exec. officer, acctnt.-gens'. dept.) (b. 6th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 7th Jan., 1913, and attd. to library; transf. to acctnt.-gensl's. dept., Mar., 1913; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to Jan., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; asst. clk. to council, Feb., 1932 to Feb. 1935; act. hr exec. officer, Feb., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934.

HEYCOCK, WILLIAM BISSILL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 26th April, 1873).—Educ. at Oakham Sch., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1903; mag. and collr., Nov., 1909; dir. of agric., April, 1911; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, as mag. and collr., and dir. of agric., April, 1912; mag. and collr., March, 1914; ditto and dep. commr., June, 1919; offg. commr., Jan., 1920; commr., Oct., 1923; retd., Dec., 1931.

HEYSHAM, ERIC HENRY BERKELEY, Indian State rlys. (transpn. supt.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1896).—Joined the service, 28th Nov., 1919 as asst. supt. E.I. rly., transpn. supt. (traffic comml.), May, 1935.

HEZLETT, JAMES, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th June, 1875).—Educ. at Coletraine Academy, Queen's Coll., Galway, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, May, 1906; joint mag. and dep. coll., Sept., 1906; dep. commr., Assam, April, 1912; supt., Lushai Hills, Oct., 1912, to April, 1917; dep. commr., Sept., 1914; commr. of excise, April, 1921, to July, 1922; offg. commr., April, 1923, and again, May, 1926; confd., Oct., 1928. C.I.E., Jan., 1928; representative of govt. of Assam in legisl. assembly, Nov., 1932; retd., Nov., 1934; chairman, pub. serv. commn., Assam, April, 1937.

HIBBERT, RICHARD OSWALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th Dec., 1914).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HICKEY, PATRICK FRANCIS BURKE, D.S.O., Indian Service of Engineers (suptg. engr., United Provs.) (b. 4th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; in mil. employ. from 18th June, 1915, to 5th May, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1931; offg. ch. engr., variously from Mar., 1935.

HICKINBOTHAM, TOM, O.B.E., Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Apl., 1903).—First commn., 30th Aug., 1923; in military employ. until 15th Mar., 1930; offg. under secty. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Dec., 1932; civil secty. to ch. commr., Aden, Dec., 1933; asst. pol. agent, Bahrain, Apr., 1936; offg. pol. agent, Bahrain, May, 1936; secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Nov., 1937; civil secty., member of exec. cl., Colony of Aden and passport offcr., Aden, Mar., 1938; O.B.E., Jan., 1939; under sec. to pol. resdt. Persian Gulf, Feb. 1939; pol. agent, Muscat April 1939.

HICKS, GEORGE AUGUSTUS, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th Dec., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 3rd Sept., 1903; dist. engr., April, 1913; dep. ch. engr., Mar., 1919; ch. engr., April, 1921; offg. agent, Burma rlys., May, 1933; retd., Dec., 1936.

HICKS, HENRY ARTHUR HUGH GERARD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 31st Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

HICKS, VERNON WILLIAM FARQUHARSON, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 30th Jan., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 27th Dec., 1919; addl. supt., March, 1926; supt., Aug., 1929; O.B.E., Jan., 1935; on deptn. under Govt. of India (Rly. Bd.) for employment as Suptd., Watch and Ward Dept., E.B. RLY., May, 1936.

HIDAYATULLAH, KHAN BAHADUR SHAIKH, Sir GHULAM HUSAIN, K.C.S.I., B.A., LL.B.—Member of govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, June, 1928, to June, 1934; delegate to Indian round tabl conf., London, Nov., 1930—Jan., 1931; Second Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Reforms Office, Oct., 1931; K.C.S.I., June, 1933.

HIGGINS, JOHN COMYN, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (member, board of rev., Assam) (b. 21st May, 1882).—Educ. at Bradfield and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived 22nd Dec., 1906, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag., and collr.; vice-presd., Manipur Durbar, May, 1910; asst. commr., Assam, and ditto, April, 1912; C.I.E., June, 1912; dep. commr., March, 1921; pol. agent, Manipur, April, 1924; offg. commr., Mar., 1934; ditto (provl. subs.), Feb., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; member, rev. tribunal, April, 1939.

HIGGINS, RICHARD, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 19th April, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 15th March, 1921; addl. supt., Feb., 1925, and again March, 1927; offg. supt., March, 1926; offg. dep. comm. of police, Calcutta, March, 1928; confd., Jan., 1931.

HIGGS, MERVYN ARMSTRONG, M.B.E., M.A., late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 8th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1910; temp. engr., Sept., 1911; asst. engr., July, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1921; exec. engr., U.P., July, 1924; M.B.E., Jan., 1935; retd., July, 1938.

HIGHAM, BERNARD, C.I.E., M.B., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 14th Dec., 1880).—First commn., dated 1st Feb., 1906; served in mil. dept. until Jan., 1909, when apptd. prob. in chemical examr.'s dept., Bombay; chemical analyser for Sind and health officer of the port of Karachi, June, 1912; on mil. duty from Nov., 1914; to Dec., 1920; chemical analyser to govt. of Bombay, Dec., 1920; C.I.E., June, 1935; retd., Dec., 1935.

HIGHAM, Rev. PHILIP, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 21st Sept., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1919; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1921; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1931.

HIGHET, JAMES COCHRANE, F.C.H., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Railways (b. 19th May, 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; asst. sec., rly. bd., June, 1915; offg. junr. dep. agent, Jan., 1923; offg. senr. ditto, July, 1924; offg. secty., rly. board, Nov., 1926; dep. agent, N.W. rly., April, 1927; offg. dir. of estabt., rly. bed., Jan., 1928; confd. Oct., 1930; offg. agent, N.W. Rly., May, 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; offg. member, rly. bd., Mar., 1936; retd., May, 1939.

HIGMAN, BERTRAM FREDERICK, V.D.; late Indian State Ryis. (b. 27th Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 14th Feb., 1895; traffic inspr., Jan.,

1897; asst. dist. traffic supt., July, 1899; dist. ditto, Nov., 1909; divl. traffic manager, Oct., 1914; traffic manager, April, 1919, and again, April, 1921; chief supt., Jan., 1920; chief comml. manager, Feb., 1925; retd., Sept., 1933.

HIGMAN, WALTER FREDERICK BARON, A.M.Inst.T., Indian State Ryis. (transpn. traffic-commcl. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 18th May, 1902); joined the service, 29th July, 1921; asst. supt., Apl., 1922; senr. scale (provl.), Aug., 1937; confd., July, 1938.

HILEY, ARTHUR CLIFFORD, C.I.E., Indian Forest Service (chief consvr.) (b. 11th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service, Dec., 1910, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; offg. consvr., July, 1927; confd., Aug., 1928; chief consvr., Bombay, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1939.

HILL, Rev. EDWARD EUSTACE, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Bombay) (b. 2nd July, 1876).—Joined 11th May, 1906; junr. chaplain, May, 1909; senr. chaplain, May, 1919; on mil. duty from 10th June, 1918, to 7th April, 1919; retd., Apl., 1930.

HILL, JAMES HERBERT, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 31st Aug., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., July, 1926; retd., Nov., 1931.

HILL, KENNETH ARTHUR LULHAM, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., Bengal) (b. 22nd April, 1899).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. magte. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., variously from Mar., 1932; confd., Feb., 1936.

HILL, NEVILLE CHARLES, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 9th Sept., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, 26th Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

HILL, REGINALD JOHN JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) b. 1st July, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Edinburgh, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, 12th November, 1928, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1932.

HILL, ROBERT H., M.A., Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Central Provs.) (b. 5th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; economic botanist, Dec. 1925; dep. dir. of agric., Oct., 1926.

HILL, RONALD ARTHUR CHARLES, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 11th July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 15th Oct., 1921; offg. supt., Feb., 1926; confd., July, 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; O.B.E., May, 1937.

HILL, ROWLAND ALFRED KIRKHAM, M.B.E., late Indian Political Service (b. 26th Mar., 1884).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 16th Nov., 1903; supt. for. and pol. dept., Dec., 1927; asst. secty., ditto, Oct., 1931; offg. under-secty., ditto, Apl., 1935, to Nov., 1935; asst. secty. to govt. of India, external affairs dept., Apl., 1937; M.B.E., Jan., 1939; retd., Mar., 1939.

HILSON, GEORGE ROBERT, B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 19th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service, 26th Feb., 1910; dep. dir. of agric., Mar., 1910; cotton specialist, Coimbatore, June, 1920; offg. dir. of agric., Madras, April, 1925; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

HILTON, GEORGE GRIMMER, M.B.E., late India Office (b. 15th Sept., 1867).—Appointed clerical officer, 30th July, 1921; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1927; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

HILTON, GORDON CHARLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Oct., 1885).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; ditto, and pol. asst., and cmdt., B.M. Police, Oct., 1915, to Oct., 1916; offg. under-sec. to govt., finl. dept., May, 1918; ditto, rev. dept., June, 1918; under sec., rev. dept., Nov., 1918; dep. commr., Sept., 1923; provl. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1925; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1925; offg. legal remembr. and sec. to govt., legisl. dept., May, 1927; offg. judge, high ct., Lahore, April, 1929; addl. judge, ditto, variously from Mar., 1931; dist. and sess. judge (sub. provl.), July, 1936; ret'd., July, 1938.

HIND, NEVILLE GODFREY, M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A. (dep. secty., Govt. of India, dep. dept.) (b. 8th Jan., 1892). First commn., 6th Sept., 1911; in mil. employ until 18th May, 1938; dep. secty. to Govt. of India, dep. dept., May, 1938.

HINDE, REGINALD GRAHAM, Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 19th May, 1887).—Joined the service, 19th Jan., 1907; in milty. employ to June, 1920; pol. agent and consul, Muscat, Sept., 1923; and asst. to Resident in Kashmir, Feb., 1925; 1st asst. to ditto, June, 1926; sec., to A.G.G., Punjab States, April, 1927; pol. agent, S. Rajputana States, Feb., 1929; secy., to agent to govr.-genl., C.I., Oct., 1929; pol. agent in Bhopal, Oct., 1930; and again, Apl., 1931; on spl. duty under agent to govr.-genl., C.I., Nov., 1930; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Mar., 1931; pol. agent, Loralai, Dec., 1933; pol. agent, Chhattisgarh States, Nov., 1936; ret'd., April, 1939.

HINDLEY, SIR CLEMENT DANIEL MAGGS, K.C.I.E., late Indian Railway Board.—Held appt. as agent, East Indian rly.; chairman, commrs. of the Port of Calcutta, 1st Nov., 1921; apptd. chief commr. of rlys., 1st Nov., 1922; Kt., June, 1925; K.C.I.E., March, 1929; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

HINDLEY, NOEL LANYON, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 24th Dec., 1893).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Cambridge Univ.; apptd., after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1931; registrar, high ct., appellate side, Nov., 1931 to Mar., 1935.

HINDLEY, OLIVER WALTER, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1911; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1923; confd., June, 1927; ret'd. Aug. 1932.

HINGSTON, CLAYTON ALEXANDER FRANCIS, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 6th May, 1877).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1902; apptd. persl. asst. to surgn.-gen. with the govt., Madras, Nov., 1910; profir. of midwifery, medl. coll., Madras, Nov., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1925; prinpl. ditto, Jan., 1928; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Madras, Aug., 1929; ret'd., May, 1932.

HIRA SINGH, SARDAR SAHIB, late Punjab Med. Service (b. Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 26th Aug., 1905; variously apptd. as asst. surg., from Apl., 1907; civil surgeon (offg.), Nov., 1927; confd., Apl., 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

HIRST, REGINALD JOHN, C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.) late Indian Police (b. 11th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 13th Nov., 1901, as asst. supt. of police; supt., Aug., 1907; on deputn. as inspr.-gen. of police, Baroda, from Oct., 1916; dep. inspr.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Sept., 1922; offg. inspr.-gen., Mar., 1925; confd., Jan., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., June, 1934.

HISTED, SYDNEY EDWARD, late Indian Police (b. 1st Feb., 1880).—Held non-gazetted, appts. from 27th Nov., 1909, to 1st Feb., 1923; apptd. to I.P.S. as asst. supt., Feb., 1923; offg. supt., Sind Rlys., Mar., 1927; confd., April, 1932; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

HITCHCOCK, RONALD VICTOR, V.D., B.Sc. A.M.I.C.E. (late Indian State Rlys. (b. 22nd June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 5th Nov., 1910; resdt. engr., July, 1913; on milty. service from 3rd April, 1916, to 2nd April, 1919; divl. engr., Dec., 1926; offg. dep. agent (Works), G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1933; offg. dep. ch. engr. (maintenance), G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1934; confd. (provl.), Feb., 1935; ret'd., May, 1939.

HITCHCOCK, WILLIAM SURRIDGE, Indian Police (central intelligence officer, Bihar) (b. 14th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 30th Nov., 1910; supt., March, 1922; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; on deputation as dept. supt., watch and wards, E.I. Rly., Feb., 1927, to April, 1928; central intelligence officer, Bihar, March, 1939.

HNYIN, U THA, B.A., Barr., late Burma Commission (b. Oct., 1876).—Apptd. offg. dist. and sessions judge, March, 1923; confd., May, 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

HO, KWAY FOUNG, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 20th Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ.; joined the service 8th Sept., 1938; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1938, and served as asst. commr.

HOARE, CHARLES EDGAR, India Office (higher executive officer, mil. dept.) (b. 1st Jan., 1891).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 13th Nov., 1913; war services—European war, 1914-16; exec. officer, April, 1922; joint editor, India Office List, May, 1932, to Oct., 1937; actg. higher exec. officer, Mar., 1933; confd., Sept., 1935.

HOARE, The Right Hon. Sir SAMUEL, Bt., P.C., G.C.S.I., G.B.E., C.M.G., Hon. LL.D. (Cantab.), M.P.—Secty. of State for India, Aug., 1931, to June, 1935.

HOBART, ROBERT CHARLES ARTHUR STANLEY, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th July, 1881).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 29th Nov., 1905, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1914; mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; offg. commr., Apl., 1931; confd., Dec., 1934; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; ret'd., April, 1939.

HOBHOUSE, Rt. Hon. Sir CHARLES EDWARD HENRY, Bart.—Parly. under-sec. of state for India, Jan., 1907, to April, 1908; chairman of Royal Commn. on Indian decentralisation, 1907.

HOBSON, JAMES, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 24th Aug., 1895).—Joined the service, 16th Dec., 1922; offg. supt., Nov., 1925; confd., Nov., 1932; asst. to dep. inspr. gen. of police, C.I.D., Poona, Oct., 1938.

HODDER, WILFORD OWEN PENDEREL, Indian Police Service (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 10th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 30th Nov., 1922; offg. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, Mar., 1926; offg. supt., Sept., 1930; supt. (provl. subs.), June, 1935; offg. asst. dir., intel., Govt. of India, July, 1936.

HODDER, WILLIAM ST. LAWRENCE, Indian Police (b. 10th Aug., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 16th Nov., 1927.

HODGE, EDWARD HUMFREY VERE, C.I.E., Lt.-Col. ret'd., late I.M.S. (b. 28th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 31st July, 1909; apptd. prof'r. of med., Med. Coll., Calcutta, Mar., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1938; ret'd., Aug., 1938.

HODGE, JOHN DOUGLAS VERE, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Oct., 1887).—Educated at Durham Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. offg. addl. under sec., pol. dept., June, 1916; on spl. duty, pol. dept., Nov. to Dec., 1917; sec., sedition commee., Dec., 1917, to April, 1918; jt. mag. and collr., April, 1918; services placed at disposal of govt. of Bengal in pol. dept. from Oct., 1918; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1919; addl. under sec., ditto, April, 1920; regr., high ct., Calcutta, Nov., 1923; offg. addl. dep. sec., home dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1925; offg. dep. sec. from Mar.-Sept., 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; mag. and collr., Apl., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1929; offg. commr., July, 1932; secty. to govt., Bengal, agric. and ind. depts., Aug., 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1939.

HODGE, WILLIAM ERIC, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 2nd April, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 6th Oct., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1923.

HODGSON, ROBERT CECIL, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b. 14th Feb., 1876).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. engr.; prof. in C.E. Coll., Sibpur, from June to Oct., 1901; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; and under-sec., govt. of Bengal, P.W. dept., Oct., 1910, to Jan., 1914; suptg. engr., Oct., 1919; war decoration, Sikkim-Tibet mission, 1903-04; offg. cn. engr. and sec., P.W. dept. govt. of Bengal, June, 1926; ch. engr., Oct., 1928; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

HODSON, ERIC, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 19th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. on 2nd Dec., 1913; confd., June, 1915; addl. supt., April, 1922; awarded King's police medal, 1924; supt., July, 1925; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Feb., 1934.

HOEY, GEORGE McCAUSLAND, B.A., B.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1907; as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; suptg. engr., Nov., 1923; ret'd., Sept., 1938.

HOGG, Sir GILBERT PITCAIRN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Bengal) (b. 2nd Feb., 1884).—Educ. at Glasgow High Sch. and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 28th Nov., 1908, and served in E.
