Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1914; vice-chairman, Chittagong Port Commrs., July, 1915; on mil. duty, Oct., 1917, to Jan., 1918; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, May, 1918; on mil. duty, Aug., 1918, to Jan., 1919, and returned to Bengal: mag. and collr., April, 1921; commr. of excise and salt, Bengal, Sept., 1923; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, agric. and industries dept., and director of industries, April, 1926; secty., govt. of Bengal, agric. and industries and P.W. depts., Nov., 1928; offg. commr., July, 1931; confd., Dec., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1932; addl. secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. dept., Oct., 1932; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, Apl., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; temp. member, govr's exec. council, Bengal, Apl., 1936, and again, Feb., 1937; C.S.I., June, 1936; actg. govr. of Assam, June to Oct., 1938; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1939.

HOGG, Sir MALCOLM NICHOLSON, Kt.—Dep. chairman, Bombay Chamber of Commerce, and member of Bombay legis. council, 1915 to 1917; chairman, Bombay Chamber of Commerce, and member govrn.-gen.'s legve. council, 1917 to 1919; member of franchise cmtee., 1918-19; knighted, 1920; member of council of India, May, 1920, to April, 1925.

HOGG, WILLIAM PEAT, D.S.O., M.C., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (resdy. surg., Mewar) (b. 29th Feb., 1888).—First commn., 13th Mar., 1916; in milty. employ to 4th Oct., 1926; offg. civilsurg., Quetta, Oct., 1926; offg. surg., British Legation, Kabul, Apl., 1927; agency surg., Bhopal, Feb., 1930; ditto, Gilgit, Oct., 1931; ditto, N. Waziristan, May, 1933; resdy. surg., Mewar, Nov., 1934; offg. resdt., Mewar, Feb., 1935.

HOGGARTH, ROBERT ANDREW, A.R.C.A., Indian Educ. Service (Bombay) (b. 28th June, 1886).—Apptd. inspr. of drawing and craft work, Bombay Presdy., 6th Nov., 1914; ret'd., Mar., 1931.

HOGSHAW, FRANCIS HENRY, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 16th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 10th Oct., 1915; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. suptg. engr., Nov., 1934.

HOLBERTON, NELSON VAUGHAN, late Indian Forest Service (b. 27th May, 1885).—Arrived 29th Oct., 1906, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1911; employed under Indian munitions board, May, 1918, to Jan., 1920; asst. inspr.-genr. of forests, govt. of India, Feb., 1922; offg. consvr., Burma, March, 1926; confd., April, 1926; ret'd., Feb., 1938.

HOLDING, SAMUEL HENRY, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 7th April, 1882).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Dec., 1896, to 22nd March, 1917; with Mesopotamian field force from March, 1917, to Oct., 1920; asst. divl. engr., Aug., 1923; divl. engr., Oct., 1927; offg. dir., tels., Madras Circle, Apl., 1936; retd., Apl., 1937.

HOLDSWORTH, BENJAMIN GEORGE Indian C.S. (secty., rev. dept., Madras) (b. 31st July, 1892).—Educ. at Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 6th Dec., 1916, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from Nov., 1917, to May, 1920; sub-collr. Aug., 1920; special settlt. officer, Oct., 1923; secty. to bd. of revenue, land revenue, Aug., 1927; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; offg. collr., Mar., 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; secty., rev. dept., Madras, Oct., 1937.

HOLGATE, MAURICE JAMES, O.B.E., M.B., B.S. (Lon.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 15th May, 1884).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; offg. civil surg., June, 1924; confd., Dec., 1927; retd., May, 1939.

HOLLAND, ARCHER SELWYN, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bombay) (b. 23rd Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1907, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, April, 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

HOLLAND, EDGAR WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 28th April, 1899).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1923; arrived, 15th Dec., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. magte. and collr., Mar., 1929; confd., Dec., 1936.

HOLLAND, GEORGE REGINALD, late Salt Dept., Bombay (b. 30th Oct., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. (temp. and perm.), from 5th July, 1906, to 16th Sept., 1917; asst. collr. of salt and excise, Sept., 1917; on milty. duty from 20th March, 1917, to 9th Oct., 1919; supt. of salt and excise, Sept., 1920; asst. collr. of salt rev. (designation changed), Sept., 1926; retd., Aug., 1932.

HOLLAND, LAWRENCE BERTRAM, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Orissa) (b. 25th Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Tonbridge and Trinity coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1909, and served in the Punjab as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; offg. consvr., April, 1926; confd., Jan., 1932; transf. to Central Provs., Oct., 1934; transf. to Orissa, Jan., 1937.

HOLLAND, SIR ROBERT ERSKINE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.V.O., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th June, 1873).—Educ. at Winchester, and Ariel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 10th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Bombay, July, 1900; asst. collr., March, 1901; retransf. to Madras, Nov., 1902; dep. commr. of salt, &c., March, 1904; offg. asst. to the agent in Central India, June, 1904; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Sept., 1904; offg. dep. sec., Jan., 1908; ol. agent, Maskat, Nov., 1908; 1st asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1910; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, June, 1911;

ditto, and asst. resdt., Mewar, May, 1913; offg. dep. sec. for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, Feb., 1914; confirmed, April, 1914; on special duty, Persian Gulf, Sept., 1915, to March, 1916; spl. duty as jt. trade commr., Persian Gulf, Dec., 1916, to June, 1917; C.I.E., Jan., 1917; offg. pol. sec. to govt. of India, Feb., 1919; agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Dec., 1919; C.S.I., June, 1921; C.V.O., June, 1922; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1925; retd. from I.C.S., April, 1925; member of Council of India, 21st April, 1925 to July, 1931; apptd. judl. adviser to govt. of Siam, June, 1933; Order of Crown of Siam (1st cl.), Aug., 1936.

HOLLAND, SIR THOMAS HENRY, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., D.Sc., F.R.S., late Berkeley Fellow of the Owens Coll., late Geol. survey of India. —Joined the service 10th July, 1890, as asst. supt. and curator, geol. museum and laboratory; dep. supt., July, 1894; dir., Feb., 1903, to 1909; retd., Nov., 1910; chairman of trustees, Indian Museum, Calcutta, 1906-08; K.C.I.E., June, 1908; prof. of geology, Manchester Univ., 1909; presdt., Indian industrial commn., 1916; presdt., bd. of munitions, India, from 1917; K.C.S.I., June, 1918; temp. member of Vice-roy's council, Feb., 1919; member, govr.-genl.'s Council, April, 1920, to Sept., 1921; rector, impl. coll. of science and technology, 1922; pres. British Association, 1929; principal and vice-chancellor of Edinburgh Univ., 1929; has contributed to the survey publications.

HOLLIDAY, GEORGE DARGUE, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 14th May, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 17th Nov., 1926.

HOLLINS, SAMUEL THOMAS, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 6th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1902, and posted to the United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, Oct., 1907; supt., Aug., 1914; ditto, Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1915; inspr.-gen., Tonk State, April, 1916, to Nov., 1918; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1928; confd., Oct., 1930; offg. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1931; confd., July, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1931; retd., Oct., 1936.

HOLLIS, ARTHUR HENRY, M.Inst.T., Indian State Rlys. (supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 15th Jan., 1898).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1922; supt. (senr. scale), Mar., 1936.

HOLMAN, THOMAS GEORGE HOATHLEY, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th July, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 1st Jan., 1926; offg. addl. supt., variously from Mar., 1932; offg. princpl., police training coll., Sardah, Apl., 1933; supt. (temp.), June, 1936; confd., June, 1938.

HOLMAN-HUNT, HILARY LUSHINGTON, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th May, 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1901, and served in Burma as an asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1909; C.I.E., June, 1917; on mil. service, May, 1917, to Sept., 1919; suptg. engr., Dec., 1921; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., Nov., 1923; chief engr., Sept., 1926; retd., Feb., 1933.

HOLME, ALFRED SIEGFRIED, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., R.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 17th Mar., 1875).—First commn., dated 17th Aug., 1894; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1897, as asst. engr., and served in the

Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1906; reverted to mil. duty from Nov., 1912, to March, 1913; under-sec. to govt., March, 1913; suptg. engr., Feb., 1918; temp. reverted to mil. duty, from Oct., 1914, to June, 1919; O.B.E., June 1919; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

HOLMES, ANDREW GILMOUR, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 5th June, 1915).—Educ. at George Heriot's School, Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; joined the Service 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937 and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1939.

HOLMES, ARTHUR CLEMENT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th Nov., 1875).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1908; ditto, and settlt. offr., Jan., 1914; dep. commr. and settlt. offr., Dec., 1914; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1918, to Feb., 1919; mag. and collr. (s.p.t.), March, 1919; confd., Feb., 1922; offg. commr., May to Oct., 1924, and again, March, 1926; confd., Nov., 1928; offg. member, bd. of rev., United Provs., May, 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

HOLMES, ARTHUR MALCOLM, Indian Police (b. 21st July, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 24th Nov., 1928; A.D.C. to govr., C.P., Dec., 1933 to June, 1937; awarded King's Police Medal, July, 1938.

HOLMES, HENRY CHRISTOPHER, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 10th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service, as forest engr., Bombay, 24th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., July, 1928.

HOLMES, JAMES EDWARD, office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 30th Aug., 1897).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 2nd Feb., 1920; transferred to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; actg. exec. officer, May, 1930; confd., July, 1935.

HOLROYD, Lt.-Col. GILBERT, M.B., I.M.S., ret'd., Jail Dept., United Provs. (b. 17th Sept., 1880).—First commn., 2nd Feb., 1907; in milty. employ to 4th May, 1911; again, Oct., 1914, to Sept., 1920; supt., Sept., 1920; offg. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

HOLT, EDWARD HARLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collector, Sind) (b. 20 April, 1907).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar School and Queen's College Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India November, 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collector.

HOLT, JOHN WILLIAM CECIL, Indian State Rlys. (dep. dir. rly. bd.) (b. 22nd July, 1900).—Asst. supt., E.I. rly., Oct., 1925; supt. (senr.), Aug., 1937.

HOMAN, ARTHUR KNOX, Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt. (senior) N.W. rly.) (b. 17th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1908; asst. loco. supt., N.W. rly., Oct., 1908; dist. ditto, E.B. Rly., June, 1914; divl. supt., E.I. rly., July, 1925; divl. rolling stock officer, Nov., 1927; dep. ch. transpn. supt., E.I. rly., May, 1931; offg. divl. supt., senior, N.W. rly., Apl., 1934; divl. supt. (junr.), May, 1934; offg. divl. supt. (senior), Aug., 1933; confd., Sept., 1934.

HOMFRAY, CHARLES KENYON, Indian Forest Service (dep. consr., Bengal) (b. 19th April, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

HOMFRAY, JESTON, late Indian Forest Service (b. 12th Dec., 1885).—Arrived 4th Nov., 1907, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1919; offg. consvr., April, 1924; confd., Mar., 1929; ret'd. June, 1933.

HOOD, HAROLD HAYNES, late Impl. Customs Service (b. 23rd Nov., 1875).—Joined the service, 19th Oct., 1907, and served as asst. collr. in Bengal; apptd. to Bombay, July, 1911 to Madras, May, 1913; and to Sind, March, 1915; Rangoon, April, 1918; collr., Madras, Aug., 1919; ditto, Karachi, 1921; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

HOOD, HUGH MEGGISON, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (offg. chief secty. to govt., Madras) (b. 5th June, 1885).—Educ. at Middlesbrough High Sch., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; appt. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1909, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag. Sept., 1916; on milty. duty from Aug., 1916, to April, 1919; registrar, co-operative societies, April, 1923; and again, Nov., 1926; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1924; confd., Aug., 1929; offg. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Dec., 1925; chairman, Madras provl. banking enquiry commee., Dec., 1929; offg. secty. to govt., finance dept., Apl., 1931; confd. Dec., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. secty. to govt., Madras, law and educn. dept., Mar., 1936; secty. to govt., home dept., Apl., 1936; offg. second secty. to govt., Apl., 1936; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, June, 1936; Aug., 1938, and Aug., 1939; C.S.I., Jan., 1939.

HOOD, JAMES REANEY, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th May, 1888).—Educ. at Woodbridge Sch., Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. collr. and dist. magte. and pol. agent, July, 1923; offg. secty. to govt. of Bombay, home and eccl. depts., Oct., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

HOOD, ROBERT HOOD, Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 18th Dec., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1924.

HOOD, VISCOUNT, SAMUEL HOOD, B.A., India Office (asst. principal, services and general dept.) (b. Oct. 15, 1910).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed assistant principal, Feb. 19th, 1935; resdt. clk., Mar., 1936; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state for India, Sept., 1936; ditto for Burma, Apl., 1937; acting principal, May, 1939; services loaned to the Ministry of Information, Sept., 1939.

HOOD, WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr. (bridges), G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 5th Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. bridge engr., 23rd Jan., 1925; senr. asst. ditto, Aug., 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), Nov., 1932; confd., Sept., 1934; offg. dep. ch. contr. of standardisation, July, 1936.

HOOGWERF, GERALD JAMES, Indian Police (b. 8th Oct., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provinces, on probn., Nov., 1938.

HOOJA, MADAN MOHAN LALL., B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 5th Sept., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probation, March, 1939.

HOOLE, NORMAN PETER GLEDHILL, Indian Police (b. 9th May, 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., 6th Dec., 1935.

HOOLEY, SAMUEL PROSPER, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 12th Mar., 1876).—Joined the as 2nd asst. auditor, 22nd April, 1914; dep. agent, Apl., 1927; retd., Mar., 1933.

HOON, PRAN NATH, Indian State Ryls. (dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 5th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service, 12th Jan., 1915; asst. contr. of stores, Sept., 1923; dist. contr. of stores (junr.) E.B. rly., Sept., 1926; ditto (senr.), May, 1933.

HOOPER, ERNEST GEORGE GALE, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 14th Dec., 1899).—Apptd. clerical officer, 3rd Feb., 1924 after service at India Office from 21st May, 1920; exec. officer, 1st Nov., 1932.

HOOPER, GEOFFREY GRANT, B.A., M.C., late Indian Civil Service (b. 7th Dec., 1891).—Educ. at St. Paul's School, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. under Reconstruction Scheme; arrived, 2nd May, 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1925; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, legisl. dept., Jan., 1927; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1928; offg. secty. to govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., Oct., 1931, and again, Sept., 1932; confd. Oct., 1933; retd., Jan., 1938.

HOOTON, ALFRED, C.I.E., Major-Gen., I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 15th Mar., 1870).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1895; apptd. dep. sanitary commr., Bombay, April, 1903; civil surgn., March, 1904; on mil. duty from Oct., 1918; offg. surg.-genl. with the govt. of Bombay, May, 1923; confd., Jan., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; offg. D.G., I.M.S., Apl., 1927; retd., Feb., 1930.

HOPE, JOHN RUTHERFORD, Mil. Accts. Dept., (b. 14th May, 1894).—Held non-gaz. and sub-gaz. appts. up to Apl., 1929; apptd. to class II, Apl., 1929; dep. finl. advr., R.A.F., Oct., 1934.

HOPE, ROBERT CYPRIAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 25th Sept., 1915).—Educ. at St. Edward's Sch. Oxford, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 6th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

HOPKINS, ALAN CECIL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 30th Oct., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Sept., 1926.

HOPKINS, EDWARD ALFRED, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 4th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 29th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., May, 1928; awarded King's police medal, March, 1929.

HOPKINS, GEOFFREY MAYNE, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 5th July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consr., on 30th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1926.

HOPKINS, GERALD ADAMSON, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 9th Sept., 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., telegraphs, 1st Oct., 1901; supt., March, 1913; divl. engr., tels., Oct., 1920; offg. dep. chief. engr., tels., May, 1925; dir. tele. enrg. (prov.), Jan., 1930; confd., Mar., 1930; offg. P.M.G., B. and O. circle, Dec., 1930; P.M.G., Burma, Oct., 1932; retd., July, 1934.

HOPKINSON, ARTHUR JOHN, C.I.E., Indian Civil Service, Indian Political Service. (b. 28th

Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; arrived, 10th May, 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1924; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1924; British Trade Agent at Gyanthe and asst. to pol. offr. in Sikkim, Jan., 1927; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar States, Nov., 1928; dep. commr., Hazara, Nov., 1930; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transferred depts., Apl., 1932; chief secty., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; serv. in N.W.F.P., again from Oct., 1938; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar agency, March, 1939.

HOPKYNs, WILLIAM STENNING, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Feb., 1879).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 27th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1910; s.pt. mag. and collr., Nov., 1914; confd., April, 1917; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; offg. sec. to govt. of Bengal, revenue dept., Dec., 1920, to April, 1922, and from April, 1924; confd. as sec. rev. dept., March, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1928; offg. ch. secty. to govt. of Bengal, June, 1928; confd., May, 1930; commr., June, 1932; retd., June, 1933.

HOPWOOD, STEPHEN FRANCIS, M.C., late Indian Forest Service (b. 23rd June, 1880).—Arrived, 28th Nov., 1903, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1909; on mil. duty, Jan., 1916, to March, 1919; consvr., April, 1920; off. ch. consvr., May, 1927; confd., Sept., 1930; retd., June, 1935.

HORMASJI, JIVANJI, C.I.E., I.S.O., M.A., LL.B., Barr., late Burma Judl. Dept. (b. 11th Feb., 1871).—Appointed asst. regr., chf. ct., Lower Burma, Nov., 1900; 2nd dep. regr., Feb., 1911; 1st dep. regr., Nov., 1916; regr., high ct., Rangoon, May, 1924; offg. administrator-gen. and offl. trustee, April, 1925; confd., May, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1929; retd., Feb., 1931.

HORMAZDIAR PHIROZ DASTUR (see Dastur, H. P.).

HORN, WILLIAM ROWATT, D.Sc., late Indian State Ryls. (E.I.Rly.) (b. 25th Aug., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the state rys. as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr. (junr. govt. inspr.), Madras, Feb., 1912; spl. duty, ry. board, April, 1915; asst. sec., ry. board, Aug., 1915, to May, 1919; offg. dep. agent, E.B. Rly., Nov., 1924; offg. divl. supt., E.I. Rly., Oct., 1926; offg. contr. stores, E.I. Rly., March, 1928; retd., Oct., 1931.

HORNELL, SIR WILLIAM WOODWARD, Kt., C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon), late Indian Educatl. Service.—Educ. at Radley Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Jan., 1902; profr., Presy. Coll., Calcutta, Jan., 1902; inspr. of schools, Bengal, Sept., 1903; asst. dir. of public instn., Bengal, Oct., 1906; junr. examnr., bd. of edcn., England, 1908; asst. dir., ditto, 1910; dir., public instn., Bengal, May, 1913 to 1924; addl. member, Bengal legisl. council; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, Nov., 1917; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; vice-chancellor, Hong Kong Univ., 1924; Kt., Jan., 1931.

HORSFIELD, HERBERT ELLIS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Leeds Gr. School, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived

in India, 8th Dec., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1918; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from Oct., 1916; persl. asst. to dir., central intell., India, July to Oct., 1920; mag. and collr., May, 1925; sec., local self-govt. dept., July, 1925; commr., excise and salt, Oct., 1928; offg. commr., Nov., 1932; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

HORSFIELD, RODERICK, Indian State Ryls. (divl. trans. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 30th Apl., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. trans. supt., 18th April, 1924; offg. divl. trans. supt., Dec., 1926; confd., Sept., 1930.

HORTON, RALPH ALBERT, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 5th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 25th Nov., 1906; supt., Oct., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; confd., May, 1936; offg. inspr.-genl., June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1936.

HORWILL, LIONEL CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 19th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Royal Coll. of Science and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty, July, 1916, to Dec., 1919; sub-collr., Sept., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1925; confd., Feb., 1929; addl. judge, ct. of resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1935; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, variously from July, 1936; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1938.

HORWOOD, Rev. KENRIC COSENS, B.A., late Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (b. and Nov., 1880).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 8th Dec., 1909; junr., chaplain, March, 1912; senr. chaplain, Mar., 1922; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

HOSALI, SATYENDRANATH, B.A., Indian Police (b. 4th July, 1913) (asst. supt. Bombay) apptd. on probn., 3rd Feb., 1937; confd., March, 1939.

HOSE, Sir JOHN WALTER, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Dulwich, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., May, 1891; under sec. to govt., April, 1893; registrar, high court, March, 1894; dep. commr., March, 1898; mag. and collr., June, 1899; member of legisl. council from Oct., 1905; finl. sec. to govt., March, 1906; chief sec., April, 1910; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; commr., April, 1912; ret'd., Jan., 1916; employed temply. in the jud. and public dept., India Office, from Jan., 1916 to Oct., 1926; acting asst. sec., judl. and public dept., Oct., 1917 to May, 1918, and from Dec., 1918. Kt., July, 1926.

HOSIE, JAMES FINDLAY, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 22nd Aug., 1913).—Educ. at Kilsyth Academy, Glasgow Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937, confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

HOTSON, Sir JOHN ERNEST BUTTERY, K.C.S.I., O.B.E., M.A. (Oxon.) late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Mar., 1877).—Educ. at Edinburgh Coll. Sch., Edinburgh Acad., and Magdalen Coll.,

Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 24th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; supt., managed estates, Kathiawar, Nov., 1903; under sec. to govt., rev., fin., &c., depts., Jan., 1908; ditto, pol., judl., &c., depts., March, 1908, to Jan., 1911; temp. dep. sec., ditto, Oct., 1914; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.) from April, 1915; spl. duty in Baluchistan from March, 1916; O.B.E., June, 1918; sec. to govt., pol. dept., Nov., 1922; C.S.I., Jan., 1926; member of govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, April, 1926, to May, 1931; K.C.S.I., June, 1930; actg. govr. of Bombay Apl., 1931; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

HOULDEY, JOHN EMERIS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 19th June, 1879).—Educ. at Durham Sch. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 7th Dec., 1903, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Jan., 1917; on mil. duty, Jan. to March, 1918, and July, 1918, to April, 1919; prest. Rangoon municipal commee., April, 1920; offg. chairman, dev. trust, April, 1922; offg. commr., Nov., 1923; again offg. chairman, dev. trust, March, 1924; confd., July, 1924; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

HOULDING, GEOFFREY WILLIAM, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., late Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 20th Sept., 1896).—Joined the service as forest engr. on 19th Oct., 1921; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

HOULTON, JOHN WARDLE, C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., rev. dept., Bihar) (b. 28th Jan., 1892).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Feb., 1920; arrived, 12th May, 1920, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; magte. and collr. (provl. sub.), Mar., 1932; offg. secty. to govt., rev. dept., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1933; magte. and collr., July, 1935. C.I.E., Jan., 1939.

HOWARD, Sir ALBERT, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., A.R.C.S., F.C.S., F.L.S., late Dept. of Agriculture. —Joined the service, 5th May, 1905, as biological botanist, Agric. Coll., Pusa; imperial economic botanist, May, 1906; C.I.E., June, 1924; on foreign service under Indore Durbar, Oct., 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1928; knighted, June, 1934.

HOWARD, Sir HENRY FRASER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Aldenham Sch., and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; supt. of gaz. revision, March, 1904; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Feb., 1904; jt. mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, April, 1908; mag. and collr., March, 1909; asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta, July, 1909; collr., Dec., 1909; offg. sec., govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1912; C.I.E., June, 1913. offg. contr. of currency, Jan., 1914; confirmed, April, 1914; offg. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1916; addl. member, govr.-genl.'s legisl. council, April, 1916; offg. sec. to the govt. of India, comm. and industry dept., Dec., 1916; sec. to the govt. of India, finance dept., May, 1917; C.S.I., Jan., 1919; temp. member of govr.-gen.'s council, 29th May, 1919; on deputation as contr. of finance, India Office, 5th May, 1920, to 4th May, 1923; K.C.I.E., June,

1923; retd., Sept., 1925; author of 'Handbook of Criminal Procedure,' 'India and the Gold Standard,' and articles in Imperial Gazetteer.

HOWARD, Sir Henry George, Major, C.I.E., M.C., M.I.E.E., M.A.M.Soc.C.E., Electricity Dept., Madras, (b. 5th Oct., 1883).—Chief Engineer for Electricity on agreement from 6th Sept., 1927; Knighted, Jan., 1939.

HOWARD, Stanley Herbert, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service 23rd Nov., 1912, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Nov., 1916; silviculturist, forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1919 to Jan., 1926; offg. consvr., Dec., 1929; and again, Apl., 1933; confd., Apl., 1936.

HOWE, Henry, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th July, 1883).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 12th Nov., 1910; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; sub-divl. engr., Aug., 1925; offg. supt. (way and works), Feb., 1926; offg. dep. ch. engr. (signals), March, 1927; Mar., 1933; and again, Mar., 1934; confd., July, 1934; retd., June, 1939.

HOWE, Robert Douglas, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Bishop Foy Sch., Waterford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 12th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HOWELL, Alfred Edward, Indian State Rlys. (production engr. N.W. rly.) (b. 31st Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. works manager, N.W. rly., 6th April, 1927; production engr., N.W. rly., Oct. 1930.

HOWELL, David Arnold, O.B.E., A.M.I.C.E., Public Works Dept., Punjab (suptg. engr.) (b. 28th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., 25th Dec., 1920; exec. sany. engr., Mar., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Dec., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1939.

HOWELL, Sir Evelyn Berkeley, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Feb., 1877).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 8th Dec., 1900, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov., as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., May, 1904, to Sept., 1906; pol. asst., 1st Feb., 1906; dep. commr., May, 1907; dist. judge, Nov., 1907; asst. sec. for dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1909; dep. commr., Kohat Oct., 1910; durbar sec. to chief commr., Peshawar, July, 1911; asst. commr., Mardan, Aug., 1912; ditto, Peshawar, Dec., 1912; censor of Indian mails, Indian Expdny. Force, France, Sept., 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; pol. agent and H.B.M.'s consul at Muscat, June to Oct., 1916; dep. civil commr., Basrah, Oct., 1916; rev. s.ec. Mesopotamian administrn., Dec., 1918; C.S.I., March, 1919; dep. sec. to govt. of India, foreign and pol. dept., Feb., 1922; offg. resdt. in Waziristan, May, 1924; for. sec. to govt. of India (temp.), Oct., 1926; resident, Kashmir, April, 1927; for. sec. to govt. of India, May, 1930; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., Sept., 1934.

HOWES, John Baron, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Dec., 1907).—First Commn., 12th Sept., 1931; Pers. Asst. to

Pcl. Res., Persian Gulf, Oct., 1935; Under Sec. to Pol. Res., Persian Gulf (addl. charge), Feb., 1936; Asst. Pol. Agent, Bahrain, May, 1936; under trng. in U.P., Jan., 1937; on Special duty, Karachi, Apr., 1938; Asst. Pol. Agent, Bahrain, Sept., 1938; confd. in Indian Political Service, Feb., 1939, Pol. Officer, Trucial Coast, Oct., 1939.

HOWLETT, Cecil George, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd., late Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 30th May, 1882).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1910; in milty. service up to 16th Dec., 1922; civil surg., Dec., 1922; retd., Mar., 1934.

HOWLETT, Richard Ross, B.A., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 20th Nov., 1872).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge (scholar); junr. clerk, 16th April, 1895; senior clerk, April, 1909; dep. dir.-gen. of stores, Nov., 1915; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; dir. of purchase, store dept., April, 1927; retd. Nov., 1932.

HOWSON, Geoffrey, C.I.E., M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A., retd. For. and Pol. dept., govt. of India (asst. master, Mayo coll., Ajmer) (b. 14th Aug., 1883).—First commn. dated 21st Jan., 1903; C.I.E., June, 1919; in milty. employ to 24th April, 1927; asst. master, Mayo coll., Ajmer, April, 1927.

HOYLE, Arthur Lascelles, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Jan., 1887).—Educ. at Rugby, St. John's Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), July, 1916, to Nov., 1918; temply. transf. to Punjab, April to July, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), Oct., 1923; confd., April, 1925; excise commr., Central Provs., March, 1926; dep. commr., Sept., 1926; commr. N.I. salt rev., May, 1928; retd., Mar., 1937.

HOYLE, Leonard Arthur, Indian State Rlys. (asst. divl. supt. (traction), G.I.P. rly.) (b. 4th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1928, as traction engr., rolling stock, G.I.P. rly.; offg. depy. transport supt. (traction), June, 1930; asst. divl. supt. (traction), G.I.P. rly., May, 1933.

HTAY, Maung Ba, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 3rd June, 1908).—Educated at the Wesleyan High School, Monywa, Rangoon University and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, January, 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

HUBAN, John Patrick, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ch. medl. officer, Jaipur) (b. 13th Feb., 1891).—First commn. dated 23rd Sept., 1914; in milty. employ. to 16th Oct., 1922; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; civil surg., Oct., 1922; ch. medl. officer, Jaipur, Jan., 1932.

HUBBACK, Sir John Austen, K.C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (govr. of Orissa) (b. 28th Feb., 1878).—Educ. at Winchester and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 6th Dec., 1902; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlmt. offr.; settlmt. offr., May, 1909;

jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1910; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; special duty, bd. of rev., Dec., 1912; offg. sec., ditto, Sept., 1913, to May, 1914; temp. empd. in rev. and statistics dept., India Office, from May to Sept., 1915; mag. and collr., March, 1916; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from May to July, 1918; offg. sec. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, rev. and judl. depts., Feb., 1919; confirmed, April, 1919; dir. of land records and surveys, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1923; offg. commr., Feb., 1925; confd., Dec., 1928; offg. member, bd. of rev., May, 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; temp. member, govr's. exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Aug., 1933, and again, Oct., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1936; govr. of Orissa, Apl., 1936.

HUBBARD, ALEXANDER EGERTON, late Indian State Railways (b. 5th Nov., 1882.—Joined the service, 22nd May, 1920; resdt., engr., E.I. rly., May, 1922; exec. engr., Nov., 1931; ret., June, 1939).

HUBBARD, PHILIP MAITLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 9th Nov., 1910).—Educated at Elizabeth College, Guernsey and Jesus College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934 and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. colon. offcr., May, 1937, to Oct., 1937.

HUDDELESTON, Sir ERNEST WHITESIDE, C.I.E., C.B.E., Captain, R.I.N. (ret.), late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 18th Aug., 1874).—Entered R.I.M., 1895; served in Egyptian Campaign, 1895-6, China Expedition, 1901-2; and in Somaliland Expedition, 1902-4; staff officer, Bombay dockyard, 1911; senior marine transport officer, Bombay, 1914 to 1918; C.I.E., June, 1916; C.B.E., July, 1919; presidency port officer, Madras, 1920 to 1924; dep. director, R.I.M., from 1924; ret. from R.I.M., Dec., 1925; apptd. shipping surveyor and adviser, office of High Commr. for India, Nov., 1925; ret., Sept., 1938; Knighted, Jan., 1939.

HUDSON, Sir HAVELOCK, G.C.B., K.C.I.E., A.D.C., Gen., Indian Army (ret.) (b. 26th June, 1862).—First commission, 1881; Commandant, Cavalry Sch., Sangor, 1912; Brig.-Gen., General Staff, Northern Army, 1912-14; Adjutant-General, India, 1917-20; K.C.B., Jan., 1918; K.C.I.E., June, 1919; G.O.C.-in-chief, Eastern Army in India, 1920; A.D.C. General to H.M. the King, 1922; member of the Council of India, 1st Oct., 1924, to 30th Sept., 1929; vice-president, ditto, Nov., 1928; G.C.B., Jan., 1928; war services—Second Miranzai Expedition, 1901 (medal with clasp); N.W. Frontier, 1897 (medal and clasp); China, 1900 (despatches, medal, brevet of major); European War, 1914-18 (despatches, promoted Major-Gen.).

HUDSON, RICHARD JOHN HARRINGTON, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 17th May, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; ret., May, 1930.

HUDSON, Sir WALTER FRANK, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Aug., 1875).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 18th Dec., 1899, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindhi translr.

to govt., Feb., 1905; manager, Sind encumbered estates, Oct., 1907, to Oct., 1909; dep. commr., Thar and Parkar, Oct., 1911, to April, 1913; junr. collr. and collr. and pol. agent, Oct., 1916; collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, April, 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; on depn. as member of legisl. assembly, Sept. to Oct., 1922, and May to June, 1924; collr. and supt. of stamps, Nov., 1923; member of legisl. council, 1924-26; offg. commr., Oct., 1926; confd., June, 1927; commr. in Sind, July, 1927; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, Aug., 1929; ret., Oct., 1933; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1933.

HUGGINS, HENRY WALTER, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mechl. engr. N.W. Rly.) (b. 10th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 25th Jan., 1914; dist. loco. supt., Sept., 1925; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1927; offg. dep. ch. operatg. supt., Mar., 1936; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936; provl. perm., Nov., 1937.

HUGHES, ARTHUR, B.A. (Man.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at City and County School, Chester, Victoria Univ., Manchester, and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1933; offg. addl. dep. secty. to the Govt. of India, fin., comm. and marine depts., Nov., 1936.

HUGHES, DEDWYDD WILLIAM, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 7th Apl., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 19th Dec., 1912; dep. consvr., March, 1915.

HUGHES, HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Burma) (b. 18th June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; offg. supt. engr., July, 1924; confd., June, 1928; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1932; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1935; confd., May, 1938.

HUGHES, IAN DAVID ERSKINE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th Oct., 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1904, and posted to the Punjab irrign. branch; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1924; confd., July, 1931; ret., Aug., 1934.

HUGHES, THOMAS LEWIS, late Indian Civil Service (b. 27th July, 1897).—Educ. at Birkenhead Sch.; on mil. service from Sept., 1915, to Dec., 1922; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offices, Jan., 1925; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1932; ditto (provs. subs.), Mar., 1935; secty. to finl. commr., Burma, Dec., 1936; resigned, Aug., 1939.

HUGHES, VIVIAN ARTHUR, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 14th Nov., 1882).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Dec., 1898; on field service from 7th April to 1st Dec., 1917; supt., tele. traffic, Apl., 1921; dep. P.M.G., Lahore, Aug., 1925; ditto, Burma Circle, Oct., 1927; ch. supt., tele. traffic, Madras, Nov., 1930; contr., tele. traffic, Mar., 1931; ch. supt., Bombay, tele. office, Jan., 1932; ret., July, 1937.

HUGHES-HUGHES, EDWARD ESSEX, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 11th Aug., 1915).—Apptd. on probn., Nov., 1936; confd., Nov., 1938; asst. to the Political Officer, Sadiya Frontier Tract, Nov., 1938.

HUGHFF, REGINALD GEORGE, Indian State Ryis. (works manager, E.I. rly.) (b. 30th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1921; works manager E.I. rly., Sept., 1923; offg. pers. asst. to dep. ch. mechl. engr. senr. scale, June, 1925, and again, Feb., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. rly., Jan., 1936; confd., Dec., 1938.

HUGO, EDWARD VICTOR, C.M.G., V.H.S., M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 5th Jan., 1865).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1892; in mil. emp. until March, 1900, when he was apptd. to spl. plague duty, Punjab; offg. civil surgn., Punjab, Jan., 1901; confirmed as civil surgn., March, 1902; civil surgn., and prof. of midwifery, etc., Lahore medl. Coll., Aug. to Oct., 1906; prof. of surgery, Lahore medl. coll., June, 1909; on mil. duty from Dec., 1914; hon. surgn. to the Viceroy, May, 1916; ret'd., April, 1922; apptd. addl. member of medical board, India Office, June, 1925; member, ditto, Nov., 1928 to Jan., 1931.

HUKAM CHAND, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd Feb., 1880).—Joined the service, 11th Nov., 1903; asst. engr., Nov., 1904; exec. engr., Dec., 1912; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1925, Sept., 1926, and again, Aug., 1932; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

HUKKOO, RAJ BAHADUR (see Raj Bahadur H.).

HUKUM SINGH, THAKUR, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late United Provs. C.S. (b. March, 1884).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1907; dep. collr., June, 1911; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1930; confd., Sept., 1934; Rai Bahadur, June, 1935; ret'd., March, 1939.

HULKOTI, CHANAPPA CHANVIRAPPA, LL.B., Bombay Civil Service (Judl. Branch) (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 27th Dec., 1887).—Held various judicial appointments from 22nd June, 1928; judge and sess. judge 16th Jan., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1936.

HULL, GEORGE, Comdr., R.I.N., ret'd., late Marine Dept., Burma (b. 2nd July, 1879). 2nd engr. and ship surveyor, Bombay, Jan., 1920, to April, 1922; offg. engr. and ship surveyor, Karachi, Nov., 1924; confd., Feb., 1925; offg. prinpl. engr. and ship surveyor and suptg. engr. to govt. of Burma, Nov., 1928; offg. ch. engr. with govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Apr., 1933; ret'd., April, 1937.

HULLAND, WALTER GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collector, Bombay) (b. 28th Jan., 1908).—Educ. at the Grammar School, Barnstaple, and Jesus College, Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrvd. in India Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collector.

HUME, ANDREW PARKE, Indian Civil Service. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 24th Nov., 1904).—Educ. at University School, Hastings, Lausanne, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr.; jt. mag., Aug., 1929; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1932; offg. addl. dep. secty., to the govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, June, 1936; officer on spl. duty, ditto, Sept., 1936; chairman, Delhi improvement trust, Mar., 1937.

HUME, ROBERT, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. commr. Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1920; asst. supt., Aug., 1923; supt., May, 1930; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1925; O.B.E., June,

1931; dep. commr. for town of Madras, Feb., 1935; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1938.

HUMPHREY, CECIL ALBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 17th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived Dec., 1928, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apr., 1936.

HUMPHREY, WILFRED, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 11th June, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on probn., 5th Dec., 1924; confd., Oct., 1929; asst. dir. intell. G.I.R., Mar., 1937; ditto, under govt. of India, Peshawar, Apr., 1938.

HUMPHRYS, SIR FRANCIS HENRY, G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., K.B.E., C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 24th Apr., 1879).—First commn. dated 23rd May, 1900; in mil. emp. to Dec., 1903, when he was apptd. sup. asst. commr.; personal asst. to chief commr., N.W.F. Province, April, 1905; asst. commr., Hazara, Oct., 1907; asst. commr., Charsadda, April, 1910; dep. commr., Bannu, May, 1910; asst. commr., Charsadda, May, 1910; asst. commr., Thal, April, 1914; dep. commr., Kohat, Sept., 1914; asst. commr., Thal sub-divn., Oct., 1914; dep. commr., Bannu, April, 1915; pol. agent. Tochi, Nov., 1915; pol. offr. with Waziristan fd. force, in addn., from May to Nov., 1917; granted temp. commn., Royal Air Force, April, 1918; dep. commr., Bannu, April, 1919; pol. agent, Khyber, Oct., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1920; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., April, 1921; H.M.'s Minister at Kabul, Jan., 1922; K.B.E., June, 1924; G.C.V.O., March, 1928; K.C.M.G., June, 1929; ret'd. from I.A., Nov., 1929; high commr. in Iraq, Nov., 1929; G.C.M.G., Jan., 1932.

HUNT, EDWARD, V.D., late Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.) (b. 18th Feb., 1875).—Joined the service, March, 1904, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., April, 1911; dep. loco. supt., Feb., 1920; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; offg. divl. supt., Jan., 1925; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

HUNT, EDWIN WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 1st Feb., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 19th Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Nov., 1932, and again, Mar., 1933; awarded Indian police medal.

HUNT, GEOFFREY HOWARD, B.Sc., M.C., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; under secty. to govt., p.w.d., Punjab, Sept., 1933; ret'd. Oct., 1938.

HUNT, HENRY CARTER, Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Bengal) (b. 19th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 26th Nov., 1909; supt., June, 1920; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Feb., 1925; offg. dep. inspr. genl., Jan., 1933, and again, Oct., 1936; confd., July, 1937; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938.

HUNT, ROLAND CHARLES COLIN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 19th March, 1916).—Educated at Rugby and Queen's College, Oxford; appointed on probation 24th September, 1938; confirmed 14th September, 1939; arrived in India, October, 1939, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HUNTER, ALEXANDER MELEDORE, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th Apr., 1882).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 7th April, 1902, to 31st Dec., 1912; offg. asst. supt., Jan., 1913; confd., June, 1913; dist. supt., Feb., 1923; supt., Feb., 1925; offg. divl. supt., Sept., 1936; retd., Sept., 1937.

HUNTER, CHARLES FREDERICK, Indian Service of Engrs. (suprg. engr., United Provs.) (b. 12th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; in milty. employ from 28th July, 1918, to 31st Jan., 1919; exec. engr., May, 1919; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1933; suptg. engr., confd., Feb., 1937.

HUNTER, DOUGLAS CAMPBELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. 30th Aug., 1886).—Educ. at Felsted Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag., and collr., and asst. settl. offr.; jt. mag., Dec., 1916; on mil. duty, July, 1918, to March, 1919; offg. as dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1921; sess. and sub-judge, May, 1925; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1925; judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1939.

HUNTER, FREDERICK FRASER, D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., late India Survey Dept. (b. 7th Aug., 1876).—First commn., 10th Aug., 1898; in mil. emp. to Aug., 1905, when he was apptd. asst. supt., Survey of India; dep. supt., Sept., 1912; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Nov., 1914, to Nov., 1919; in charge of Mesopotamia survey party, Nov., 1919; offg. dir., map publ. office, June, 1927; dir., Dec., 1928; retd., Aug., 1931.

HUNTER, GUY GUNNING RATCLIFFE, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof., Morris coll., Nagpur) (b. 16th Apr., 1890).—Joined the service as prof., Jubbulpore coll., 28th Dec., 1912; prof., Morris coll., Nagpur, July, 1924; offg. prinpl., ditto, July, 1931; offg. dir. of publ. instr. and educ. secty., C.P. and Berar, June, 1938.

HUNTER, JAMES DE GRAAFF, C.I.E., D.Sc., M.A., late Survey of India (b. 11th Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 25th Oct., 1907, as mathematical expert on probn.; confirmed, Oct., 1910; mathematical adviser, trigl. survey office, Dehra Dun, April, 1912; depy. supt., May, 1920; supt., Jan., 1926; offg. dir. geodetic branch, July, 1927; dir. (temp.), ditto, Aug., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1933; retd., Mar., 1935.

HURLEY, THOMAS JOSEPH, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (prinpl. prof., of medicine, Madras Vety. Coll.) (b. 4th May, 1895).—Apptd. supt., I Circle, Civil Vety. dept., Vizagapatam, March, 1922; offg. as prinpl., Madras vety coll., June, 1926; and again, Sept., 1928; prof. of surg., ditto, Aug., 1928; offg. dir. of vety. services, Madras, variously from June, 1929, to Oct., 1935.

HURN, Rev. EDWARD LIDDELL ARTHUR, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (junr. chaplain) (b. 5th Sept., 1905).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 11th Nov., 1935; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1937.

HURRELL, IAN MURRAY, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.), Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs. on probn., 1st Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

HURST, THOMAS ANDERSON, late Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly.) (b. 21st Jan., 1875).—

Held non-gazetted appt. from 2nd April, 1897, to 31st Aug., 1917; asst. engr., Sept., 1917; exec. engr., Sept., 1922; retd., Jan., 1930.

HUSAIN, AKHTAR, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st March, 1902).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., &c., Oct., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Apr., 1932; confd. (subs. provl.), Mar., 1933; addl. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1935; junr. secty. to finl. commrs and dep. secty. to govt. revenue dept., May, 1937; dep. commr. (confd.), July, 1937, and settlement offcr., Gurgaon, Oct., 1938.

HUSAIN, AKHTAR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 10th Jan., 1907).—Educated at the Universities of Allahabad and Agra and at Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Sept., 1934; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Mar., 1937.

HUSAIN, BADR-UD-DIN TYABI (see Tyabi, H. B.).

HUSAIN, CHAUDHRI AKBAR (see Akbar Husain, C.).

HUSAIN, HAFAZAT (see Hafazat Husain).

HUSAIN, MAGBUL (see Maqbul Husain).

HUSAIN, MIAN MUHAMMAD TASDIQUE (see Mian M. T. H.).

HUSAIN, MUHAMMAD AZIM (see Muhammad Azim Hussain).

HUSAIN, NAJABAT (see Najabat Husain).

HUSAIN WAJAHAT (see Wajahat Husain).

HUSAIN, ZAHID (see Zahid Husain).

HUSAIN, ZAKIR (see Zakir Husain).

HUSBAND, JAMES, M.B., Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 6th Aug., 1876).—First commn. dated 27th June, 1901; in milty. employ to 24th Mar., 1903; offg. civil surg., D.I. Khan, March, 1913; ditto, Wano Agency, Sept., 1913; in milty. employ from 27th Oct., 1914; Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1915; offg. supt., dist. jail, Peshawar, March, 1921; civil surg., Abbottabad, July, 1923; ditto, Kumaon military, Dec., 1923; resdy. surg., Panagar, March, 1924; legation surg., Nepal, Jan., 1928; resdy. surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Apr., 1929; reverted to milty. employ, Sept., 1929; retd., Aug., 1933.

HUSSAIN, BUNYAD (see Bunyad Hussain).

HUSSAIN, HAFAZAT (see Hafazat Hussain).

HUSSAIN, IQBAL (see Iqbal Hussain).

HUSSAIN, MUHAMMAD AFZAL (see Muhammad, A. H.).

HUSSAIN, MUHAMMAD HADI, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 15th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Punjab University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Assam as asst. commissioner; under secty., under chief secty., govt. of Assam, May, 1937, to Mar., 1938.

HUSSAIN, MUZAFFAR (see Muzaffar Hussain).

HUSSAIN, SHAH SYED GHULAM (see Shah, S. G. H.).

HUSSEY, BERNARD EDWARD, I.C.S. (sub-coll., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1909).—Educated at King Edward VI. Sch., Stafford, and Downing Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam.

of 1931; arrived in India, Jan., 1933 and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Oct., 1935.

HUTCHESON, GEORGE, M.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Jail dept., Assam (b. 17th Dec., 1874).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1898; in mil. and temp. civil employ until Feb., 1906, when he was apptd. civil surgn. and supt. dist. jail, United Provs.; offg. principal, med. sch., Agra, May, 1918, to Aug., 1919; offg. inspr.-gen., civil hosp., United Provs., April, 1925; inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals and prisons, Feb., 1927; ret'd., Feb., 1931; war services—China Expedn., 1900 (medal).

HUTCHINGS, ROBERT HOWELL, C.I.E., Indian Civil Service (agent of the Govt. of India, in Burma) (b. 11th Mar., 1897).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and collr., April, 1922; under-sec. govt. of Bengal, pol. and appt. dept., Oct., 1924; offg. supt., Central jail, Alipore, Oct., 1926; addl. magte. and collr., March, 1928; addl. dep. secty., pol. dept., govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1929; offg. pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, Feb., 1930; offg. dep. secty. pol. dept., Bengal, April, 1931; member, mining board, Bengal, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Apl., 1933; and again, June, 1934; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935; mag. and collr., May, 1935; jt. secty., home dept., Bengal, Oct., 1937; addl. secy., home dept., Bengal, Feb., 1939; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Dept. of Edn., Health and Lands, Aug., 1939; agent of the Govt. of India in Burma, Sept., 1939.

HUTCHINSON, FREDERICK HEAP, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1914; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; agric. engr., Sept., 1929; under-secy. to govt., p.w.d., United Provs., Dec., 1931.

HUTCHINSON, WILLIAM GORDON, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 15th June, 1876).—First commn. dated 5th Aug., 1896; in mil. temp. to April, 1903; sup. asst. commr., Punjab, Oct., 1903; pol. asst., April, 1904; ditto and asst. to resdt. in Hyderabad, May, 1904; asst. commr., Merwara, Oct., 1906; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Oct., 1909; asst. resdt., W. Rajputana States, July, 1911; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1912; asst. pol. agent, E. Rajputana States, June, 1912; second asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Nov., 1913; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1914; second asst. to chief commr., Baluchistan, Feb., 1915; pol. agent, Chagai, April, 1915; also pol. adviser on Sarhad affairs, to G.O.C., Seistan, from June, 1916; O.B.E., June, 1918; pol. agent, Kalat, April, 1921; pol. agent in Southern States of Central India, Nov., 1922, and pol. agent, Malwa, March, 1924; on foreign service in Cooch Behar as vice-pres. of the council of regency, Nov., 1926; ret'd., June, 1931.

HUTCHISON, ALLAN MOIR, M.A. (b. 20th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. sec., Bengal legisl. council (s.p.t.), 16th Jan., 1908; confd., April, 1908; asst. sec., legisl. dept., April, 1914; dep. sec., ditto (temp.), March,

1922; 1st asst. sec., legis. dept., May, 1924, ret'd., Mar., 1933.

HUTCHISON, LIONEL PHILIP, late opium dept., United Provs. (b. 24th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. proby. asst. opium agent, 2nd April, 1904; confd., March, 1906; dist. opium officer (s.p.t.); confd., Dec., 1923; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

HUTTON, CHARLES INGLIS, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 31st Dec., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 14th May, 1903; dist. loco. supt., Mar., 1910; engine shop manager, Dec., 1914; dep. carr. and wagon supt., Nov., 1921; offg. loco. and carr. supt.; confd., June, 1929; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

HUTTON, JOHN HENRY, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th June, 1885).—Educ. at Chigwell Sch., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 27th Nov., 1909, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; offg. depy. commr., April, 1920; confd., July, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1920; services placed at the disposal of govt. of India as census commr., Sept., 1929; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Assam, Apl., 1935; ret'd., Oct., 1938.

HYDARI, M. S. A. (see Muhammad Saleh Akbar Hydari).

HYDE, EDGAR STANLEY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 6th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Sedbergh and St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928, and served in the Central Provs., as asst. commr.; admr., Baster state, Mar., 1934.

HYDE, HENRY ARMROID, C.I.E., M.C., Indian Service of Engineers (ch. engr., and secty., P.W.D. (B. and R.), Central Provs.) (b. 17th Jan., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. supt. engr., Feb., 1924; confd., June, 1929; ch. engr., and secty. to govt., P.W.D., Central Provs., Nov., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1936.

HYDER, LODHI KARM, C.I.E., Ph.D. Formerly prof. of economics, Aligarh Univ.; member, Taxation Enquiry comee., 1924-5; member, Banking Enquiry comee., 1930-1; substitute delegate to League of Nations, 1931; member, public services commn., Jan., 1932 to Jan., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1932; offg. chairman, public services commn., Aug.-Oct., 1936.

IBBOTSON, ARCHIE WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.B.E., M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Provs.) (b. 25th Jan., 1886).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; on mily. service (I.A.R.O.), from May, 1915 (Military Cross, Feb., 1917), to June, 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; jt. mag., Oct., 1916; offg. dep. commr., May, 1920; settlt. officer, Nov., 1923, to May, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1924; offg. dep. commr., April, 1925; confd. as mag. and collr., Aug., 1926; offg. commr., May, 1929; special commr., Alwar durbar, Jan., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1934; dep. commr., Oct., 1935.

IBRAHIM, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Ibrahim).

IDDON, COLWELL, M.B.E., India Office (administrative officer for services questions) (b. 1st Oct., 1893).—Apptd. clerical officer, 27th Mar., 1922; asst. to jt. editors, India Office List, 1927-29; acting higher clerical officer, June, 1930; confd., Sept., 1933; M.B.E., June, 1933; apptd. staff officer for services questions, Jan., 1936; apptd. administrative officer, July, 1939.

IDLE, JOSEPH BIRKENSHAW, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Vety. Service (b. 31st Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as supt., stock breeding, Burma, on 19th Feb., 1921; supt., central circle, Burma, Nov., 1926; dep. dir., July, 1929; ret'd., June, 1933.

IE NGAR, HARARRI VENKATANARASINGHA VERADA RAJ, Indian Civil Service (secty. to govt., fin. dept., Bombay) (b. 23rd Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Mysore Univ. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under sec. to govt., revenue dept., Bombay, March, 1931; confd., July, 1931; passport officer to govt., Bombay, Oct., 1933; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Bombay, June, 1934, and again, Oct., 1936; secty., ditto, May, 1937.

IFE, DOUGLAS WALTER STANTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 17th April, 1915); educ. at Watford G.S., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937, confd., 13th Dec., 1938; arrived in India, Jan., 1939, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

IJAZ ALI, MAULVI SAIVID, Khan Bahadur, B.A., M.B.E., late United Provs. Civil Service (b. 30th Jan., 1881).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 4th June, 1903; confd., June, 1905; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; offg. magte. and collr., Feb., 1929; confd., Aug., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

IKBAL, MUSHIR KIDWAI, SHAIKH, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd March, 1897).—Educ. at Clifton and Cambridge (Fitzwilliam Hall); served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., from 28th Feb., 1922; joint mag., Nov., 1924; asst. commr., Jan., 1927; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1929; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1935; removed, March, 1939.

IKRAM, SHEIKH MOHAMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 10th September, 1908).—Educ. at Government High School, Lyallpur, the Punjab University, and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 2nd October, 1933; arrived in India, November, 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; dep. reg. co-op. societies, Apl., 1937; offg. regr. ditto, June, 1938.

IKRAMULLAH, MOHAMMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. Indian trade commr.) (b. 15th Jan., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad and Nagpur, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, 13th Dec., 1927, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; off. dep. commr., 1931; special duty forest divisions, 1931-32; under secty. to govt., C.P., May, 1933; under secty. govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Oct., 1933; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Jan., 1936; and again, July and Oct., 1936; secty., coal mining committees, Nov., 1936, to April, 1937; dep. Indian trade commr., May, 1937; offg. Indian trade commr.,

May, 1938; adviser and secty., 24th and 25th Labour Conferences.

ILAHI, Nur (see Nur Ilahi).

ILLIUS, Henry WARWICK, C.I.E., F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 9th Oct., 1875).—First commn. 27th June, 1901; in mil. employ till Oct., 1907, when he was employed on plague duty; offg. civil surgeon and jail supt., April, 1909; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, till Sept., 1921; C.I.E., Feb., 1921; civil surgeon and jail supt., Sept., 1921; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

ILM-UD-DIN, MOHAMMAD, M.A., Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 14th Nov., 1895).—Joined the service, Mar., 1923. Asst. collr. of customs, Nov., 1935.

INAM-UL, MAJID (see Majid ndam-ul).

INAYATULLAH KHAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th Feb., 1907).—Educated at Allahabad University and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

INDARJIT RAI, TANDAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 10th Nov., 1905).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 19th Nov., 1927; proby. asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931.

INDER, ROBERT WILFRID, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 7th June, 1885).—Joined the service, Feb., 1911, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Feb., 1916; offg. consvr., July, 1927, and again, Feb., 1928; confd., Nov., 1929; ret'd., July, 1939.

INDRANARAYAN BRIJMOHANLAL, LL.B., Bombay Judicial dept. (judge, small causes ct., Bombay) (b. 31st July, 1885).—Officiated as addl. judge and as judge, small causes ct., Bombay, from 9th Feb., 1925, to 30th Sept., 1928; confd., Oct., 1928; offg. ch. judge, June, 1935.

INDRA MOHAN LAL, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 26th June, 1896).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; joined the service, 1st Sept., 1922; arrived, 2nd Oct., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1929; ditto, Nov., 1932; confd., July, 1934.

INDRAVADAN NARAYANBHAI MEHTA (see Mehta, I. N.).

INDUBHUSAN MUNDLE (see Mundle, I. B.).

INGLIS, CLAUDE CAVENDISH, C.I.E., B.A.I., (Trin. Coll., Dub.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (dir. central irrigation and hydrodynamic research station, Poona) (b. 3rd March, 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1927; confd., Nov., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; retired, April, 1939; dir. central irrigation and hydrodynamic research station, Poona.

INGLIS, HUGH ARBUTHNOT, Indian Police (supt. United Provs.) b. 28th June, 1890.—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1910; supt., July, 1923; prinpl. police training sch., Moradabad, Mar., 1931.

INGOLDBY, ERIC, Indian State Rlys. (ch. mechl. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 7th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1921; asst. mech. engr., Nov., 1922; works manager, May, 1935; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Mar., 1925; works manager, June, 1928; offg. dir., mech. engrg., Nov., 1928; on spl. duty, rly. bd., Nov., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Mar., 1930; confd., April, 1933; dep. ch. contr. of standardisation

(temp.), Dec., 1932; confd., Feb., 1935; offg., ch. ditto, Aprl., 1935; offg. dir., rly. bd., Oct., 1935; confd., Jan., 1936; ch. mechl. engr. G.I.P. rly (provl.), Feb., 1938.

INGRAM, Rev. JAMES WILLIAM, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (presy. senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland) (b. 10th Aprl., 1890).—Appdtd. chaplain-on-probation, 21st Aug., 1919; junr. chaplain, Aug., 1921; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1929; offg. presdy. senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Madras, Nov., 1930; and again, Feb., 1935; confd., Aug., 1937.

INGRAM, LESTER CHARLES, Indian Police (b. 7th Feb., 1914).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, United Provinces, Nov., 1933; confd., Aprl., 1936.

INKSON, NORMAN LEOPOLD, D.S.O., V.D., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transportation supt. G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 31st Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 11th March, 1914, and employed on Barsi Light Rly. as loco. supt.; offg. dist. loco. supt., March, 1914; in milty. employ from 22nd Oct., 1914, to 1st Jan., 1919; dist. loco. supt., Jan., 1919; asst. trans. supt., Nov., 1922; divl. ditto, Aug., 1926.

INNES, CHARLES, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 3rd Aprl., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1904; arrived in India, Jan., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; O.B.E., June, 1919; suptg. engr., Sept., 1923; temp. chief engr., July, 1926; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1929; ch. engr., Sept., 1931; rettd., June, 1935.

INNES, Sir CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Oct., 1874).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' School, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; appdtd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spel. asst. settlmt. offr., March, 1901, to July, 1905; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Feb., 1907, to March, 1910; head asst. collr. and mag., March, 1910; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; on spl. duty, dept. of industries, from Oct., 1916, to March, 1919; also controller of munitions, Madras circle, under Indian Munitions Board; collr. and dist. mag. (on spl. duty), July, 1917; jt. sec., rev. and agri. dept., govt. of India and foodstuffs commr., from Aprl., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1919; sec. to govt. of India, commerce dept., March, 1920; C.S.I., June, 1921; member, govr.-gen.'s council, Sept., 1921; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1924; govr. of Burma, Dec., 1927, to Dec., 1932; attended Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; rettd., Aug., 1933.

INNES, FERGUS MUNRO, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th May, 1903).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec., pol. dept., Punjab, Dec., 1927; registrar, high ct., Lahore, Dec., 1930; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1932; confd., Oct., 1936.

IQBAL AHMAD, B.A., LL.B., Judl. dept., United Provs. (judge, high ct., Allahabad).—Appt. addl. judge, Allahabad high ct., Oct., 1932; judge, ditto, July, 1933.

IQBAL HUSSAIN, KHAN SAHIB, MIAN, B.Sc. Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr.,

Punjab) (b. 9th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as sst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1912; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1933.

IRANI, MERWAN SORAB, L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.F.P. and S. (Glas.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S., rettd. (b. 17th Nov., 1878).—Joined 30th Jan., 1904, and served in mil. dept. prior to 31st May, 1909; civil surg., March, 1913; on mily. duty from 23rd Oct., 1914, to 30th March, 1922; rettd., Nov., 1933.

IRANI, RUSTOMJEE HORMUSJI, F.C.H., late Indian State Railways (b. 3rd June, 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; appdtd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; asst. dep. agent, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1918; asst. agent, ditto, June, 1919; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1923; offg. divl. supt., Jan., 1925; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1926; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. dir. of civil engrg., rly. bd., Sept., 1930; offg. ch. engr., and senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., May, 1932; confd., June, 1934; rettd., June, 1935.

IRELAND, FRANK WILLIAM, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 17th July, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; rettd., Nov., 1932.

IRVING, Sir MILES, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Aug., 1876).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; appdtd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 10th Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; senior sec. to fin. commrs., Punjab, Aprl., 1914; dep. commr., and ditto, Oct., 1914; on mil. duty from Feb., 1917, to Feb., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; fin. sec. to govt. of Punjab, Aprl., 1922; C.I.E., July, 1926; offg. commr., May, 1927, addl. secty. to govt., Punjab, reforms dept., Feb., 1928; commr., Feb., 1930; offg. finl. commr. (rev.), Sept., 1931; confd., Nov., 1931; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, Feb. and June, 1934; knighted, June, 1934; rettd., March, 1935.

IRWIN, BARON (see Halifax, Viscount).

IRWIN, Rev. Canon BENJAMIN CHRISTOPHER BULTEEL, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 7th Feb., 1874).—Appdtd. chaplain on probation, 18th Feb., 1908; junr. chaplain, Aprl., 1908; in milty. employ from 28th Oct., 1916, to 31st Aug., 1908; senr. chaplain, Aprl., 1918; rettd., Feb., 1930.

IRWIN, CYRIL JAMES, C.S.I., C.I.E., V.D., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Jan., 1881).—Educ. at Stonyhurst Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; appdtd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904 and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1909, to March, 1910; settlt. offr., Nov., 1913; dep. commr. (provl.), July, 1917; on mil. service, Jan., 1918, to March, 1919; dep. commr., Nagpur, Feb., 1920; C.I.E., June, 1922; rev. sec. to govt., Jan., 1924; offg. commr., May, 1925, and Aprl., 1926; commr. (provl.), July, 1929; confd., Aug., 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1930; rettd., Aug., 1936.

IRWIN, HERBERT WILLIAM, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 11th July, 1906).—Appdtd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 18th Feb., 1930.

IRWIN, JOSEPH BOYD, D.S.O., M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govr. of Bombay) (b. 6th March, 1895).—Educ. at Trinity Coll.,

Dublin; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1920; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1920, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; persnl. asst. to commr. in Sind and Sindhi translator to govt., Dec., 1924; asst. commr. in Sind and ditto, March, 1927; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Feb., 1929; and again, Mar., 1932; confd., Nov. 1935; offg. secty., home and eccl. deptts., Bombay, Feb., 1936; confd., May, 1936; secty. to govr., Bombay, Aug., 1938.

IRWIN, REGINALD DE VERE, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 5th April, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 3rd March, 1915; dist. loco. supt., March, 1924; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1934; confd., Feb., 1937.

ISAAC, PALATHUNKAL VARKI, B.A., M.Sc., D.I.C., Indian Agricul. Service (2nd entomologist) (b. 6th June, 1891).—Joined the service as 2nd entomologist, 26th June, 1922.

ISEMONGER, FREDERICK CHARLES, C.I.E., C.B.E., late Indian Police Service (b. 5th July, 1876).—Joined the service, 11th Jan., 1898; dist. supt. of police, Oct., 1905; princpl., police training sch., Phillour, Feb., 1916; on mil. service with I.A.R.O., July, 1917 to April, 1919; chief civil intell. offr., Punjab, April, 1919; principal, police training sch., Phillour, July, 1919; C.B.E., Jan., 1921; dep. inspr.-gen., Punjab, April, 1925; inspr.-gen., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1927; offg. dir., intell. bureau, home dept., govt. of India, April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., July, 1931.

ISHAQUB, HAFIZ SAYED MUHAMMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 6th August, 1906).—Educ. at Aligarh University, Lucknow University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 14th October, 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1933.

ISHWAR, DAYAL, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 16th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service, 18th Mar., 1921; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, July, 1923; divl. audit officer, O. and R. rly., Mar., 1925; accts. officer, N.W. rly., Apl., 1929; asst. contr. of rly. accts., Apl., 1932; asst. auditor-genl. (personnel), Mar., 1933; audit officer, Indian Stores Dept., Mar., 1936, and again, Jan., 1937; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nov., 1936; audit offr., Indian Stores Dept., Jan., 1937; comptr., Sind, Nov., 1938.

ISLAM AHMAD, Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 27th Jan., 1936.

ISLAM AHMAD KHAN, MUNSHI, B.A., late Indian Police Service (b. Oct., 1877).—Joined the service on 14th Jan., 1906; depy. supt., July, 1907; asst. supt., Feb., 1920; supt., April, 1920; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

ISMAIL, PARVEZ MUHAMMAD, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 27th May, 1908).—Educ. at Central Model School, Lahore, Punjab Univ. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th October, 1933; arrived in India, November, 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offcr., Nov., 1936.

ISMAIL, SHAH ABUL HASANAT MUHAMMAD (see Shah, A. H. M. I.).

ISVARAN, VENGUAYYAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 30th June, 1908).—Educated at Madras University and Balliol

College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

IVEY, RONALD CHURCHILL, Indian State Rlys. (dep. transpn. supt., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 18th June, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., G.I.P. Rly., 8th July, 1919; transpn. (traffic comml.) supt., May, 1929; offg. divl. transpn. supt., May, 1933; offg. dep. transpn. supt., Jan., 1935; confd., July, 1935.

IYENGAR, KOMBAR RAMASWAMI KRISHNASWAMI, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 5th July, 1883).—First commn. dated 4th July, 1920; bacteriological officer, central research institute, Kasauli, Nov., 1914; offg. dir., March, 1925; confd., July, 1926; dir. pasteur inst., Coonor, April, 1928; ret'd., July, 1938.

IYENGAR, MANDAYAM ANNADHORAI THIRUMALAI (see Thirumalai Iyengar, M.A.)

IYENGAR, SESHADRY AMARENDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 12th Feb., 1911).—Educ. at La Martinière Coll., Lucknow, Bishop Cotton's Boys' High Sch., Univ. of Madras and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London, joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., Apl., 1938.

IYENGAR, VANKIPURAM RANGASWAMI, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 29th Apl., 1884).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 15th Oct., 1900; asst. divl. engr., Dec., 1921; divl. engr., tels., May, 1930; offg. dir., tel. engr., July, 1932; confd., June, 1937; ret'd., March, 1939.

JACK, ALEXANDER PURVIS, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Nov., 1909; dist. supt., Jan., 1922; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

JACK, SIR ROBERT ERNEST, late Indian C.S. (b. 20th Dec., 1878).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Cork; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Apl., 1908; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1909; retransfd. to Bengal, April, 1912; s.p.d. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1914; confd., Nov. 1915; transf. to Assam, June, 1921; offg. judge, Calcutta high ct., April-Sept., 1928; confd., Nov., 1928; knighted May, 1937; ret'd., Dec., 1938.

JACKETS, CECIL HECTOR, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 19th Mar., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Nov., 1923; asst. commr. of police, Madras, July, 1930; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1931.

JACKSON, EUGENE WENTWORTH DISNEY, O.B.E., M.C., B.A., B.A.I., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 1st July, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; O.B.E., Feb., 1937.

JACKSON, Right Hon. SIR FRANCIS STANLEY, Lt.-Col., P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (b. 21st Nov., 1870).—Educ. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; finl. secty. to War Office 1922-3; chairman of Unionist Party, Mar., 1923; P.C., 1926; G.C.I.E., Feb., 1927; govr. of Bengal, Mar., 1927 to Mar., 1932. Knight of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, Dec., 1931; G.C.S.I., May, 1932.

JACKSON, SIR GILBERT HOLLINSHEAD BLOMFIELD, Kt., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Jan., 1875).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.,

Merton Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 5th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Mar., 1908; head asst. collr. and mag., Madras, Nov., 1909; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1917; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, Jan., 1924; ditto, June, 1926; confd., Dec., 1927; knighted, June, 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

JACKSON, GORDON NOEL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Punjab) (b. 25th Dec., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Punjab, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935; apptd. to Indian Political Service subs. on probn., May, 1939; personal asst. to resdt. Rajputana, May, 1939; under trng. in Ajmer, Oct., 1939.

JACKSON, HARRY, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Aug., 1881).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1909; asst. loco. supt., July, 1914; dist. ditto, Feb., 1916; on milty. duty from 4th Oct., 1917, to 1st June, 1919; sec. to commee. for standardisation of locomotive, Feb., 1924, to May, 1924; ditto, wagon and coaching stock, Dec., 1924; offg. dep. dir., stores, Oct., 1925; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Nov., 1927, and again, Nov., 1932; confd. (provl.), May, 1934; offg. ch. ditto, Mar., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

JACKSON, HERBERT GUY, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 30th April, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Madras, 1st Oct., 1915; asst. exec. engr., July, 1922; offg. exec. engr., Nov., 1924; confd., Aug., 1926.

JACKSON, JAMES DUNCAN, late Indian Service of Engrs., Punjab (b. 25th Aug., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1907; on mil. duty from 5th Sept., 1916, to 3rd Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. supt. engr., May, 1928, and again, Nov., 1932; ch. d., Jan., 1934; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

JACKSON, JAMES WOTHERSPOON CLARKE, late Indian Police (b. 5th July, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 29th Nov., 1912; supt., June, 1923; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

JACKSON, LAURENCE HUNGERFORD, Major, I.A., Survey of India (dir.) (b. 27th Sept., 1889).—First commn. dated 8th Sept., 1909; asst. supt. (proby.), 7th Sept., 1914; in milty. employ from 18th Sept., 1914, to 18th Dec., 1919; asst. supt., May, 1921; supt., Dec., 1926; offg. dir., June, 1934; dir., Sept., 1938.

JACKSON, LAURENCE SEPTIMUS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Dept. (junr. chaplain)—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 11th Dec., 1933.

JACKSON, LESLIE FREDRICK, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 2nd Nov., 1904; exec. engr., Nov., 1912; offg. supt. engr. and engr.-in-chief, June, 1925; offg. dep. ch. engr., April, 1928; confd., Nov., 1929; engr.-in-ch., Bombay-Sind construction rlwys. survey, Sept., 1930; offg. ch. engr., E.B. rly., Nov., 1932; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlvs., Oct., 1934; ch. engr., May, 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

JACKSON, ROBERT JOHN, B.A. (Oxon), Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, R.U.I., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 9th Dec., 1907, and served in the Central
