Prov. as asst. commr.; sub-judge, Aug., 1914; addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1915; regr. judl. commr.'s ct., Dec., 1915; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from Nov., 1918, to Feb., 1919; dist. and sessions judge, Dec., 1921; ditto and legal remembr. and sec. to govt., legal dept., Jan., 1923; addl. judl. commr., May, 1929; offg. judl. commr., June, 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

JACOB, SIR CLAUD WILLIAM, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., Field-Marshal, I.A. (b. 21st Nov., 1863).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch. and Royal Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; first commn., 1882; G.S.O., 1st grade, 1912; brig.-gen. commdg. Dehra Dun Brigade, 1915; ditto, Meerut Divn., Sept., 1915; commdg. 2nd Army Corps, 1916-19; K.C.M.G., 1919; chief of the general staff, India, 1920-24; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1924; G.O.C.-in-chief, Northern Command, India, 1924-25; C.-in-C. in India, 1925; sec. mil. dept., India Office, April, 1926, to May, 1930. War Services.—Zhob Valley, 1890; N.W. Frontier, 1901-2; European War, 1914-18 (C.B., wounded, mentd. in despatches ten times, promoted major-gen., lieut.-gen.) Order of St. Vladimir with swords (Russia); Grand Officier de l'ordre de la Couronne (Belgium); Grand Officier Legion d'Honneur, Croix de Guerre (France); G.C.B., July, 1926; Field Marshal, Nov., 1926; Col. 2/10th Baluch. regt., Dec., 1928; G.C.S.I., June, 1930.

JACOB, KORATHU GEORGE, M.A., Imp. Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 9th May, 1900).—Joined the service, 14th April, 1924, asst. collr., Bombay, April, 1926; second secty., central bd. of revenue, Oct., 1938.

JACOB, MAZHUANCHARRY CHAKKA, B.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 8th May, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 9th Nov., 1930.

JACOB, WILLOUGHBY ROSS LE GRAND, late Indian Forest Service (Assam) (b. 27th May, 1881).—Joined the service, 29th Nov., 1903, as asst. consvr. of forests, Assam; dep. consvr., Oct., 1913; offg. consvr., May, 1923; consvr., Nov., 1924; ret'd., Feb., 1931.

JACOBSON, ERIC JOHN HART, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 23rd Mar., 1894).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1913; transpn. supt., E.I. rly., Mar., 1926; M.B.E., Jan., 1939.

JAFAR, MOHAMMAD, M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S., D.T.M. & H., D.P.H., Major, I.M.S. (Airport health officer, Karachi) (b. 5th July, 1901).—First Commission dated 23rd Oct., 1930; in mil. employ to June, 1935; in the Medical Research Department to Jan., 1937, when he joined the port health department; Airport health officer, Karachi, Nov., 1937.

JAGAN NATH, BHAGAT, Rai Bahadur, M.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S., Judl. bch. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 1st Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th Jan., 1911; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1913; sub-judge, Sept., 1919; dist. and sess. judge, confd. July, 1934.

JAGAN NATH, JUNEJA, LALA, A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 27th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 5th Oct., 1911; asst. engr., Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921.

JAGANNATHAN, RAGAVIENGAR, M.A., B.L., mil. accts. dept. (b. 26th Feb., 1898).—Joined the service as offic. in cl. II, Aug., 1933

JAGANNATHAN, SARRUKAI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 18th May, 1914).—Educ. at Christian College School, Madras, Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India Oct., 1936, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

JAGAN NATH KUKAR, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1914; asst. engr., Oct., 1915; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; ret'd., June, 1934.

JAGAT NARAYAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 14th Feb., 1911).—Educ. at Government High School, Sitapur, Queen's Intermediate Coll., Benares, Thomason Civil Engineering Coll., Roorkee, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Mar., 1936.

JAGAT PRASAD, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 16th May, 1879).—Apptd. to Indian Finance Dept., 16th June, 1902; asst. acct.-gen., Bombay, April, 1905; dep. compt., Post Office, Nov., 1908; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, July, 1909; asst. compt.-gen., ditto, Oct., 1909; asst. acct.-gen., Hyderabad State, April, 1910, to April, 1911; dep. acct.-gen., posts and telegraphs, India, April, 1913; asst. compt.-gen., June, 1914; again dep. acct.-gen., posts and telegraphs, Aug., 1914; dep. acct.-gen., Punjab, Oct., 1916; dep. acct.-gen., United Provs., June, 1917; ditto, Punjab, April, 1918; ditto, United Provs., May, 1918; ditto, Bombay, May, 1918; ditto, Punjab, July, 1919; asst. auditor-gen., June, 1922; dept. contr. of currency, Northern India (temp.), March, 1924; offg. acct.-gen., Calcutta, Oct., 1925; acct.-genl., posts and tels., Delhi, April, 1928; addl. dep. auditor-gen., Feb., 1933; offg. ditto, Apl., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; ret'd., May, 1934.

JAGDEO SINGH, GAREWAL, SARDAR, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (supt., civil vety. dept., Central Provs.) (b. 18th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1918; supt., May, 1919; offg. dir. of civil vety. dept., B. and O., Apl. to Nov., 1930; offg. princpl., Punjab vety. coll., Apl., 1935; offg. dir. vety. services, C.P. June, 1937.

JAGDISH PRASAD, KUNWAR, SIR, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council) (b. 17th June, 1880).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 11th Dec., 1903, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1913; O.B.E., June, 1919; mag. and collr., Feb., 1921; and sec. to govt. (addl.); C.I.E., Jan., 1923; ch. secty. to govt., United Provs., July, 1927; confid., Jan., 1928; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; commr., June, 1932; ret'd., Apl., 1933; member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Apl., 1933; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, April, 1935; member, council of state, Apl., 1935; knighted, June, 1935; K.C.S.I., May, 1937.

JAGDISHWAR NIGAM, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. United Provs.) (b. 7th April, 1902).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1925; arrived, 28th Nov., 1925, and served in the United Provs. as asst. magte.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1928, and again, Jan., 1934; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1932; dir. of indus., April, 1937.

JAGTAP, BALKRISHNA PIRAJIRAO, RAO BAHADUR, L.C.E., M.R.San.I. and C., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 30th June, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Feb., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. exec. engr., May, 1928; confid., Oct., 1933.

JAI LAL, Sir, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Punjab (b. 17th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. legal remembrancer, 1st Jan., 1920; govt. advocate, Punjab, Mar., 1922; addl. judge, high ct., Nov., 1924; judge, ditto, Jan., 1930; knighted, June, 1938. ret'd., Aug., 1938.

JAI RAJ SINGHA, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 19th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., United Provs., on 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1929.

JAI RAM, LALA, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1914; asst. engr., Dec., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

JAIN, LAKSHMI CHANDRA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 30th June, 1910).—Educ. at Christ Church College, Cawnpore, Allahabad Univ., Imperial College of Science, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th October, 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1936.

JAKEMAN, ARTHUR REGINALD, Indian Police (b. 1st June, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 18th Nov., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; commr. of police and cmdt. of state forces, Pudukkottai, Aug., 1932; reverted to Brit. serv., May, 1933; offg. dist. supt., Mar., 1934; offg. asst. cmdt. of police, Aden, June, 1936; reverted, Dec., 1936.

JAKUB, SHAH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 2nd Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1920; asst. auditor, O. and R. Rly., Oct., 1924; divl. audit officer, N.W. rly., Jan., 1926; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Jan., 1929; offg. ch. accts. officer, N.W. rly., Sept., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, Nov., 1933.

JAMAL-UD-DIN, M.B., Ch.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 22nd May, 1891).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1914; civil surg., May, 1925.

JAMES, ANTHONY SAMUEL, M.C., India Posts and Tels. (b. 28th Apl., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 18th Dec., 1919; asst. divl. engr., Feb., 1921; divl. engr., March, 1927; dir., posts and tels, Sind and Baluchistan circle, May, 1933; offg. dir. tel. engrg., Bombay, Mar., 1934 ditto, Punjab, Apl., 1935.

JAMES, ARTHUR HULBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) b. 12th Mar., 1893).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 26th June, 1912, and attd. to stores dept.; on mil. service from Oct., 1915, to Nov., 1917; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1926.

JAMES, BASIL REGINALD, B.Sc., Indian Civil Service (jt. mag. United Provs.) (b. 1st Aug., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad and Selwyn Col., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Jan., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936.

JAMES, CLAUDE HUBERT, Indian Fin. Dept. (b. 3rd Oct., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. examr., p.w. accts., 17th Aug., 1903; dep. examr., Jan., 1907; dept. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, Sept., 1914; asst. acctnt.-gen., ditto, Nov., 1916; on depn. to mily. accts. dept., April, 1917, to March, 1919; offg. audit offr., Nushki Extension Rly., Aug., 1919; govt. examiner of accts., G.I.P. and B.B. and C.I. Rly., March, 1923; dep. auditor, E.B. rly., Nov., 1927; offg. dep. acct.-genl., rlys. (inspection), Feb., 1928; offg. audit officer, Burma Rlys., Jan., 1929; offg. dep. ch. auditor, E.I. Rly., Apl., 1930; dep. govt. examr. of accts., B.N. Rly., Nov., 1930; offg. ch. auditor, G.I.P. Rly., Apl., 1931.

JAMES, Sir JOHN FRANCIS WILLIAM, M.A., (Oxon.), Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd June, 1879).—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. offr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1910; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1918; offg. regr., Patna high ct., Feb., 1920, to Feb., 1921; regr., high ct., Patna, April, 1924; offg. judge, high ct., May, 1927; addl. ditto, Dec., 1928; judge, ditto, Mar., 1931; knighted, June, 1938; retd., June, 1939.

JAMES, JOSEPH FRAIN, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 1st Nov., 1882).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1906; in mil. employ up to 5th Feb., 1910; offg. civil surg., Feb., 1910; confd., April, 1923; reverted to milty. employ, May, 1930; retd., Apl., 1937.

JAMES, LEWIS EDWARD, Legis. Dept., Govt. of India (asst. secty.) (b. 3rd May, 1900).—Joined legis. dept., Nov., 1918; supt., Apl., 1932; asst. secty., Jan., 1938.

JAMES, Rev. RHYS, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Madras) (b. 10th Oct., 1889).—Apptd. tempy. chaplain, 1st July, 1918; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1921; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1931.

JAMES, SIDNEY HAMILTON, Dept. of Industries, Burma (chief insp. of factories expl. and mines) (b. 14th Dec., 1891).—Apptd. asst. inspr. of factories, 19th Oct., 1920; inspr., Oct., 1924; offg. ch. inspr., variously from Feb., 1925; confd., April, 1936, chief inspr. fac., expl., and mines, Apl., 1937.

JAMESON, ALEXANDER KEITH, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 26th Dec., 1883).—

Educ. at Edinburgh Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1912; settlt. offr., April, 1913; mag. and collr., April, 1918; sec. to board of rev., Oct., 1921; dir. of land records, Bengal, Feb., 1923; offg. commr., Sept., 1927; retd., Aug., 1930.

JAMESON, GERALD GREY, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 4th July, 1898).—Joined the service, 19th July, 1916; in mil. employ from 19th July, 1916, to 14th Nov., 1921; asst. supt., Nov., 1921; offg. dist. offr., frontier constabulary, Jan., 1925; supt., Jan., 1927.

JAMESON, THOMAS BLANDFORD, M.C., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 5th Jan., 1894).—Educ. at Royal Gram. Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne, and Queens' Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1921; arrived, 26th Nov., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1926; offg. ditto, May, 1928; confd., Feby., 1931; offg. presdt., special tribunal, Alipore, Aug., 1933; offg. commr. for workmen's compensation, Bengal, June, 1935.

JAMESON, WILLIAM JAMES, Indian Police (b. 29th Oct., 1915).—(asst. supt., Bengal), apptd. on probn., 21st Nov., 1935; confd., Jan., 1939.

JAMUAR, BRAHMDEV PRASAD, Bihar Civil Service (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 22nd Sept., 1898); offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1936; confd. April, 1937.

JANAKIRAM SINGH, BANAPHAR, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 15th Oct., 1899).—Joined the service, 5th, Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

JANARDHANA RAO, KARASAGOD PATNA SHETTI, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Police (dep. commr., crimes branch) (b. 15th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service, 4th June, 1915; apptd. to I.P. as dist. supt., Madras, Aug., 1932; offg. asst. inspr.-genl., April, 1936; dep. commr., crimes branch, Nov., 1936.

JANES, RONALD ARTHUR, India Office (asst. acct.-genl.) (b. 28th Sept., 1888).—Educ. at Thanet Coll., Margate, and Taunton Sch.; apptd., 2nd class clerk, 4th July, 1907; on mil. service, from Dec., 1915, to Feb., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; acting staff clerk, April, 1921; acting higher exec. officer, April, 1922; confd., July, 1922; suptg. exec. officer, Sept., 1932; actg. asst., acct. genl., Nov., 1937; assessor of inc. tax, Dec., 1937; confd. as asst. acct.-genl., Jan., 1938.

JANG, RANA LOKE SHAMSHER (see Rana L. S. J.).

JANGALWALA, NANABHAI TEMULJI, LL.B., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 24th March, 1879).—Apptd. presdy. magte., 15th Apl., 1929; retd., Mar., 1934.

JAKI NATH, ATAL, Pandit, late Punjab C.S. (b. 16th Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 12th June, 1908; extra asst. commr., Dec., 1912; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1928; confd., Dec., 1937; offg. dir., land records, Apl., 1931; retd., Sept., 1936.

JANVRIN, JULIAS VYVIAN BREE, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 16th July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 7th Dec., 1922; offg. addl. supt., Mar., 1929; offg. supt., Jan., 1930; awarded King's police medal, 1932; confd., Feb., 1934.

JAIQUES, COLIN CAMPBELL, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 11th June, 1905).—Joined the service 23rd Nov., 1925; in training to Oct., 1927; proby. asst. supt. Oct., 1927; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. supt., rly. police, Feb., 1937; supt. of police, confd., 1938.

JARBO, WILLIAM GEORGE, late Survey of India (b. 10th Mar., 1876).—Joined the service as proby. sub-assst. supt., 17th Nov., 1897; confd., Nov., 1898; extra asst. supt., Aug., 1906; dep. supt., Aug., 1925; supt., Jan., 1926; ret'd., May, 1931.

JARDINE, LIONEL WESTROPP, C.I.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 15th Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1921; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in United Prov. as asst. commr.; city mag., Nov., 1923; pol. officer on probn., Jan., 1925; asst. commr., Nowshera, Nov., 1926; ditto, Mardan, Aug., 1927; offg. dep. commr., Peshawar, May, 1928; pol. agent, Khyber, Nov., 1928; sec. to ch. commr., N.W.F.P., May, 1930; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1930; 1st asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, May, 1931; minister, Kashmir, Feb., 1932; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Apl., 1934; offcr. on spec. duty, Peshawar, Feb., 1936; dep. commr., Peshawar, Mar., 1936; offg. rev., and divl. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1936; again dep. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1936; rev. and divl. commr. N.W.F.P., Nov., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1939.

JASBIR SINGH, KUNWAR, BARR., United Provs. C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 16th June, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st. Aug., 1913; under secty. to govt., United Provs., Apl., 1921; mag. and collr., Oct., 1935.

JASWANT RAI, TANEJA, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 10th Aug., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 28th Jan., 1900; munisif, May, 1911; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1913; sub-judge, Jan., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

JATAR, K.S. (see Kashinath Shriram Jatar).

JATAR, NILKANTH SHIRAM, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt. Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Central Provs. (inspr.-genl.) (b. 26th May, 1887).—In milty. employ prior to 21st Jan., 1922; supt., central jail, Nagpur, Jan., 1922; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Feb., 1930; confd., Feb., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1938.

JATHAR, GANESH BHASKAR, B.A. (Cantab.), M.A. (Edin.), Indian Educl. Service (prof., Karnatak coll.) (b. 10th July, 1887).—Joined the service, 21st June, 1920; prof. of history, Elph. coll., Bombay, June, 1920; ditto, Deccan coll., Dec., 1924; offg. princpl., Karnatak coll., Dharwar, June, 1931.

JAVAD HUSSAIN KHAN SAHID, Khan Bahadur, Madras C.S. (sub. collr.) (b. 11th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1916; dep. collr., Apl., 1929; sub. collr., Nov., 1930; offg. collr., Nov., 1932; commr. to Madras corpn., Jan., 1934.

JAYAKAR, MUKUND RAMRAO, P.C., M.A., LL.B., Member Bombay legis. cl., 1923; member legis. assembly, 1926; delegate Indian R.T.C., 1930-1933; judge, federal court of India, Oct., 1937, to Feb., 1939; P.C., Feb., 1939; member of judl. comtee. of Privy Cl., Feb., 1939.

JAYAL, CHAKRA DHAR (see Chakra, D. J.).

JAYARAJAN, P. M. R. (see Paul, M. J. R.).

JAYARATNAM, THOMAS COOKE SAMUEL, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Oct., 1893).—Educ. at London Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 20th Feb., 1919; under-secy., govt. of Central Provs. in addition, July, 1923; under-secy., govt. of India, home dept., June, 1924; dep. commr., Aug., 1927; jt. secty., Indian franchise comee., Jan., 1932; offg. dep. sec., dept. of industries and lab., Govt. of India, July, 1932, and again Apl., 1933; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. of land records, etc., Central Provs., Oct., 1933; confd., June, 1934.

JAYAVANT, ANANT RAMKRISHNA, Indian Police (b. 20th June, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 20th Feb., 1932; confd., Nov., 1933.

JAYKAR, MOTABHAI SUNDARRAO, C.I.E., M.A., Bombay C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 1st Dec., 1884).—Apptd. dep. collr., March, 1909; actg. collr. of income tax, Dec., 1914; personal asst. to collr., March, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, May, 1926; collr., Oct., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1936.

JEEJEEBHOY, F., Chief Justice, Court of Small Causes, Rangoon.—Offg., May, 1937; confirmed, January, 1938.

JEFFERIS, CHARLES EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 1st Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; under sec. to govt., P.W.D. (irrigation), Jan., 1926; offg. suptg. engr., variously from Mar., 1929; confd., May, 1937; on depn. to Orissa, Apl., 1936 to Nov., 1937.

JEFFERYS, REGINALD CASTELMAN, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 15th Aug., 1922).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1912; asst. supt., May, 1914; on mil. duty from 6th Nov., 1917, to 2nd March, 1919; supt., Feb., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1936.

JEHANGIR, SIR COWASJI, Bart., K.C.I.E., O.B.E.—Tempy. member of govr.'s exec. council, 8th Dec., 1912, to 12th July, 1922; confd., 23rd June, 1923; O.B.E., Jan., 1918; K.C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., June, 1928; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930; Jan., 1931; 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd session, Nov., 1932; member, legisl. assembly, Jan., 1931; member of Ind. delegation to monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; member, Ind. res. bank ctee., July, 1931.

JENKIN, JAMES LESLIE, Office of High Commissioner for India (principal exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 23rd Aug., 1888).—Educ. at King Edward VI.'s Grammar Sch., Grantham; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 10th June, 1907; transf. to office of high commissioner for India, Oct., 1920; asst. controller, store dept., April, 1921; principal exec. officer, 1922.

JENKIN, REGINALD TREVOR, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 25th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1912; asst. postal censor, Bombay, from 27th April, 1915; dep. consvr., Sept., 1919; offg. consvr., variously from May, 1931.

JENKIN, WILLIAM NORMAN PRENTICE, C.I.E., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 27th Dec., 1919; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1925; offg. supt., April, 1927; confd., May, 1930; C.I.E., June, 1931; cent. intell. offcr., Punjab, Delhi and Sind, Nov., 1935.

JENKINS, EVAN MEREDITH, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (ch. commr., Delhi) (b. 2nd Feb., 1896).—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1925; ditto (provl.), Oct., 1927; ditto (perm.), Jan., 1932; dep. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Nov., 1933; offg. jt. secty. ditto, June, 1935, and again, Jan., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1936; ch. commr., Delhi, Mar., 1937.

JENKINS, JAMES BARROW, late Indian Police Service (Bombay) (b. 14th Nov., 1878).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1899; supt. of police, May, 1908; princpl. police training tch., Nasik, June, 1914, to Aug., 1916; asst. so inspr.-gen., April, 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1921; confd., Oct., 1924; ret'd., June, 1931.

JENKINS, JOHN LEWIS, Indian Police (asst. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 8th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 27th Jan., 1920; addl. supt., April, 1926; supt., Nov., 1929; asst. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1934.

JENKINS, WALTER ALLEN, D.Sc. (Sheffield), Indian Educ. Service (prin., David Hare training coll.) (b. 1st Apl., 1891).—Joined the service as prof. of Dacca Coll. on 2nd March, 1916; services placed at disposal of Univ. of Lacca from July, 1921 to July, 1926; offg. inspr. of schools, Dec., 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; offg. asst. dir. public instrn., Jany., 1931; prin., Rajshahi Coll., Mar., 1932; offg. special officer for primary educn., Mar., 1933; prin., David Hare training coll., Nov., 1934.

JENKINS, WILLIAM JOHN, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dir. of agric., Poona) (b. 27th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; dep. secty., Indian Central Cotton Comee.; offg. dir. of institute of Plant Industry, Indore, May to Novr., 1927; dep. dir. of agric., Sind, June, 1930; offg. dep. dir., Bombay, Nov., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1939; dir. of agric., Poona, July, 1939.

JENNER, ERIC JOHN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Sind) (b. 11th Feb., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, 25th Nov., 1930; confd., July, 1932.

JENNETT, STANLEY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr.) (b. 6th Aug., 1904).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1928; confd., Sept., 1930.

JENNINGS, ALFRED KICKWEEDE, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 30th Mar., 1890).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 2nd Feb., 1914; on mil. service from June, 1915, to June, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, May, 1926.

JENNINGS, EDGAR, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 18th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 19th Feb., 1921; dist. supt., Mar., 1929.

JEPHSON, MAURICE KEARLEY, India Office (higher executive officer, mil. dept.) (b. 2nd Apl., 1893).—Apptd. second class clerk, 26th Feb., 1912, and attd. to registry and record dept.; transf. to rev. and statistics dept., Jan., 1917; on mily. service from March, 1917, to Sept., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; actg. higher exec. officer, June, 1924, to June, 1925; confd., Aug., 1927.

JERRUM, JOHN HENLEY GRIMLE, M.V.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (dir. vety. services, Sind) (b. 6th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as supt. civil veterinary dept., Sind, March, 1921; offg. dir., vety. services, Bombay, Apl., 1933; dir., vety. services, Sind, Apl., 1936.

JESSON, ARTHUR WILLIAM MOSLEY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supt. engr., Punjab) (b. 12th Aug., 1884).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1927; confd., Apl., 1932.

JETLEY, BRIJ BHUSHAN SHARAN (see Brij. B. S. J.).

JEVONS, HERBERT STANLEY, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educ. Service (Burma).—Joined the service as lecturer in economics, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, 10th Aug., 1923; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

JHA, ADITYA NATH, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., U.P.) (b. 18th Aug., 1911).—Educ. at Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Oct., 1936.

JHA, AMAR NATH, M.A., U.P., Edn. Serv., Cl. I (b. 25th Feb., 1897).—Joined the service as prof., Muir Cent. Coll., Alld., 1st Aug., 1917; on for. serv. with Alld. Univ., from July, 1922; apptd. vice-chancellor, Allahabad Univ., Dec., 1938.

JHA, BHOLA NATH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1902).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, 27th Nov., 1926, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., 27th Oct., 1929; secty. bd. of rev., Dec., 1937.

JHA, CHANDRA MOHAN, Indian Police (Superintendent, Bihar) (b. 11th Feb., 1898).—Apptd. depy. supt. of pol., 1st Aug., 1922; to selection grade of depy. supt. of pol., 1st July, 1937; offg. supt., 12th June, 1938; confd., 11th Nov., 1938.

JHA, CHANDRA SHEKHAR, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (under secty., fin. dept., Bihar) (b. 20th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at the Watson High English School, Madhubani, Patna Univ. and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty., fin. dept., Bihar, Oct., 1935.

JHA, GANGA NATH (see Ganga N., J.).

JHA, LAKSHMI KANT, B.A., Indian C.S., (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 22nd Nov., 1913).—Educ. at Barari H.E. School, Bhagalpur, Benares Univ. and Trinity College, Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937 and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

JHANJI KANWARLAL, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1908; asst. engr., July, 1910; exec. engr., Sept., 1917; retd., Apl., 1937.

JHAVERI, CHIMANLAL MANEKLAL, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial dept., Bombay (b. 17th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1908; sub-judge, June, 1910; judge, small cause ct., Ahmedabad, June, 1930; retd., Aug., 1933.

JHUNNA SINGH, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr. Punjab) (b. 31st July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 2nd Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

JIA RAM, Indian Police (b. 21st Nov., 1912).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Feb., 1937.

JIVA NAND PANDE, B.A., Indian Police, (b. 19th Dec., 1883).—Joined the service as dep. supt., Jan., 1908; supt., March, Nov., 1925; retd., Dec., 1938.

JIWAN AMRIK SINGH, B.A., Dip. of Forestry, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 10th July, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

JOG, DATTATRAYA PARASHURAM (see Parashuram Jog, D.)

JOHN, JORDAN CONSTANTINE, O.B.E., M.B., Major, I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (civil surg.) (b. 22nd Sept., 1886).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1912; O.B.E., June, 1919; in mil. employ up to 30th April, 1923; civil surg., Sept., 1924.

JOHN, RICHARD STANLEY THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Feb., 1909).—Educ. at Haverfordwest Grammar School, University Coll., Cardiff, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, December, 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1934; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Dec., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Feb., 1937; under-secty. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, June, 1938; vice-consul, Bushire, Feb., 1939.

JOHN, WILLIAM REES, late Indian Police (b. 21st Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1912; asst. supt., Nov., 1913; service with mil. dept. from 24th Oct., 1915, to 7th March, 1916; I.A.R.O. from 20th June, 1917; supt. in charge of agency operations, Apl., 1923; offg. supt., Aug., 1924; prinpl. police training school, Vellore, June, 1928; pers.

asst. to dep. inspr.-genl. of police (rlwys. and C.I.D.), Madras, Apl., 1930; awarded King's police medal; retd., July, 1933.

JOHNSON, FRANK, India Office (higher clerical officer, milty. dept.) (b. 10th Dec., 1883).—Apptd. clerical officer, 17th Oct., 1921; acting higher ditto, Sept., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934.

JOHNSON, JAMES RUSSELL, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 15th May, 1891).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 22nd May, 1911; dep. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1913; proby. asst. supt. of police, March, 1920; confd., June, 1921; asst. supt., May, 1920; addl. supt., April, 1921; offg. supt., April, 1924; confd., Aug., 1926.

JOHNSON, Sir JOHN NESBITT GORDON, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Rossall, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; under-sec. to govt., May, 1915, to April, 1916; jt. mag., April, 1917; regr., high ct., Allahabad, March, 1918; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1918, to Feb., 1919; again regr., high ct., Allahabad, March, 1919; mag. and collr., Dec., 1924; again regr., high ct., Allahabad, Nov., 1920; dep. commr., Delhi, Nov., 1924; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, industries and labour dept., May, 1925; dep. commr., Delhi, Sept., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; offg. ch. commr., Delhi, March, 1928; confd., March, 1932; C.S.I., June, 1936; vacated appt. of chief commr., Mar., 1937; knighted, May, 1937; resigned, July, 1939.

JOHNSON, LESLIE JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 8th Sept., 1914).—Educ. at St. George's College, Mussoorie, Punjab University, and New College, Oxford; appointed on probn., 3rd Oct., 1938; confd., 8th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, October, 1939, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

JOHNSON, NORMAN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. carr. and wagon supt., Burmarlys.) (b. 31st July, 1894).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1920; asst. rolling stock officer, Dec., 1924; asst. works manager, Nov., 1927; asst. loco. supt., Nov., 1932; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Mar., 1933; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934.

JOHNSON, STANLEY MERRY, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (bridge engr.) (b. 5th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1923; asst. bridge engr., July, 1928; exec. engr. (bridges), N.W. Rly., Mar., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), N.W. rly., Mar., 1936.

JOHNSON, The Ven. WILFRID HARRY COWPER, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 24th Feb., 1879).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 24th Feb., 1909; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1912; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1922; retd., Oct., 1932.

JOHNSTON, ALEXANDER, India Office (higher clerical officer, acctn.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 6th Dec., 1882).—Apptd. clerical officer, 17th Oct., 1921; higher clerical officer, 6th April, 1926.

JOHNSTON, DAVID PERCIVAL, A.R.C.Sc.X., Indian Agricl. Service (asst. dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. 29th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 1st Dec., 1920; offg. profir. of agric., Punjab agricl. coll., July, 1927; confd., May, 1933; offg. asst. dir. of agric., Mar., 1930; confd., Oct., 1933.

JOHNSTON, DAVID SETON, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., R.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bombay) (b. 7th Aug., 1886).—Joined as asst. engr., 21st Sept., 1909; on milty. duty from 25th Oct., 1914, to 10th Sept., 1919; exec. engr., Feb., 1917; on deputation as chairman and ch. engr., Aden Port Trust, &c., Oct., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; chairman, Karachi Port Trust, May, 1934; suptg. engr., Dec., 1934.

JOHNSTON, ERIC FURKER, Lt.-Col., R.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Aug., 1891).—First commn., dated 20th July, 1911; joined Indian State Rlys., as asst. exec. engr., 18th Nov., 1920; exec. engr., Jan., 1923; ret'd., July, 1937.

JOHNSTON, SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Nov., 1872).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 17th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. agent, N.W. Frontier Prov., Dec., 1901; sec. to chief commr., Oct., 1909; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, fin. dept., Dec., 1911, confd., March, 1912; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; C.S.I., June, 1913; spec. duty, fin. dept., Oct. to Nov., 1913; member, committee on co-operation in India, from Nov., 1914, to June, 1915; temply. employed in Ministry of Munitions, London, from August, 1915, to June, 1917; on spl. duty under the agent to the govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, from April, 1918; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Feb., 1919; spl. duty in sectt., fin. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1919; resdt., Baluchistan, and rev. and judl. commr., Sept., 1919; resdt. agent to the govr.-genl., and chief commr. in Baluchistan, Sept., 1923; K.C.I.E., June, 1927; offg. pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Nov., 1928; ret'd., Mar., 1931.

JOHNSTON, Rev. Canon GEORGE FREDERICK, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 17th Apl., 1884).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 29th Oct., 1919; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1921; chaplain, Quetta, Oct., 1927; senr. chaplain, Sind, Feb., 1928; ret'd., June, 1938.

JOHNSTON, JAMES HALCRO, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 8th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1914; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923.

JOHNSTON, JOHN, M.A., Indian Civil Service (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 13th July, 1904).—Educ. at Anderson Educational Institute, Lerwick, Univ. of Edinburgh, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Mar., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1933.

JOHNSTON, JOHN PIM WATSON, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 20th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th March 1921; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., June, 1938; O.B.E., June, 1938.

JOHNSTON, MALCOLM KENNETH, Indian Police (b. 22nd April, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 17th Nov., 1926; offg. supt., Jan., 1933, and again, May, 1933; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1936; awarded King's police medal, 1938; asst. dir., intell. bureau, home dept., Mar., 1938.

JOHNSTON, ROBERT LENG, late Indian Police (b. 4th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1907; dist. supt., Feb., 1910; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1933; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938 and Indian police medal, Jan., 1938; ret'd., July, 1939.

JOHNSTON, RONALD HILARY GRAVDS, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 26th Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch.; joined the service, 4th Sept., 1923; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. magte. and collr., Mar., 1929; services placed at disposal of govt. of Bengal, Apl., 1935; mag. and collr., Feb., 1938.

JOHNSTON, WILFRID, M.C., B.A., Burma Office (acting asst. secty.) (b. 9th Oct., 1892).—Educ. at Methodist Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (scholar); apptd. junr. clerk, 20th Jan., 1920, and attd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept., India Office; asst. principal, April, 1921; acting principal clerk, Jan. to March, 1922; resdt. cler., March, 1922; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state for India, Sept., 1924; actg. principal, Aug., 1927; confd., June, 1929; acting asst. secty., Burma Office, Feb., 1939; war service—served in R.F.A., Nov., 1915, to April, 1919 (M.C., Sept., 1917).

JOHNSTON, WILLIAM, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th May, 1888).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Dunganon, St. Andrews' Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1921; confd., Aug., 1925; commr. of income tax, March, 1926; transfed. to Bengal, May, 1932; ret'd., Aug., 1938.

JOHNSTONE, DONALD, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Dec., 1884).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edin., and King's Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 17th Nov., 1908, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; transfed. to Delhi as city mag., Oct., 1912; addl. dist. mag., May, 1913; city mag., July, 1913; asst. commr., Punjab, April, 1914; on spl. duty, Delhi, Jan., 1917; persl. asst. to chief commr., Delhi, from April, 1917; addl. dist. mag., Delhi, Oct., 1917; under-sec. to govt., pol. dept., Punjab, Oct., 1917, to April, 1919; sec., jail commtee from April, 1919, to Sept., 1920; offg. dist. and sessions judge, March, 1922; confd., April, 1925; actg. judge, Lahore high ct., June, 1927, and again, April, 1928; addl. ditto, Apl., 1929; and again Oct., 1930 to May, 1931; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

JOHNSTONE, THOMAS WHITE, O.B.E., A.R.C.Sc. D.I.C., Factory Dept., Bombay (ch. inspr.) (b. 31st Aug. 1887).—Joined the service as inspr. of factories, Bombay, 28th Mar., 1913; offg. ch. inspr., June, 1920; confd., May, 1923; O.B.E., Jan., 1936.

JOLLY, EVELYN HUGH PARKER, B.A., Bar-at-Law, late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Apl., 1886).—Educ. at Framlingham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; appt. after exam. of 1908; arrived 22nd Nov., 1909, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. settl. officer and asst. judge; on mily. duty from July, 1916, to Aug., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., legal dept. (temp.), Jan., 1923; judl. asst. to the agent to the govr. in Kathiawar, Sept., 1923; offg. judge

and sess. judge, Jan., 1926; confd., Sept., 1927; judi. commr. N.W.F.P., Mar., 1929; ditto, States of W. India, Oct., 1930; retd., Nov., 1934.

JOLLY, GORDON GRAY, C.I.E., M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., D.T.M., and H., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S. (dir.-genl., I.M.S.) (b. 6th Apl., 1886).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1908; C.I.E., June, 1919; in mil. employ to 7th March, 1922; asst. dir. of public health, Burma, March, 1922; offg. dir. of public health, March, 1927; confd., Feb., 1928; dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Dec., 1933; offg. public health commr. with the Govt. of India, Nov., 1935; dir. pub. health, Burma, July, 1937; insp. genl., civil hosps., Punjab, Nov., 1937; dir.-genl., I.M.S., Sept., 1939.

JOLLYE, HUMPHREY CYPRIAN BEVERLY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Orissa) (b. 26th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 5th Dec., 1912; with army dept. from 24th Sept., 1916, to 6th March, 1919; dep. consvr., Jan., 1920; employed under Govt. of India, rly. dept. as timber advisory officer, Oct., 1931.

JONES, ALFRED JOSEPH, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 25th July, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 27th Nov., 1922; offg. dist. supt., July, 1932; confd., Feb., 1937.

JONES, ARTHUR DOUGLAS, Indian Police (b. 23rd Feb., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Dec., 1937.

JONES, ASHLEY NORMAN POSTANCE, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 5th Apl., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., on 2nd July, 1923; offg. dist. supt., March, 1927; confd., July, 1939.

JONES, CHARLES EVAN WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 9th July, 1879).—Apptd. principal, Morris Coll., Nagpur 4th Aug., 1906; offg. dir. of public instrn., N.-W.F. Prov., April to Oct., 1913, and from July to Dec., 1915; ditto, Central Provs., from Jan. to Nov., 1916; dir. of public instrn., N.-W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1917; offg. as dir. of public instrn., Central Provs., from Nov., 1921; ditto and sec. to govt. of Central Provs., education dept., Aug., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1924; retd., July, 1934.

JONES, CYRIL EDGAR, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept.) (b. 29th Dec. 1891).—Educ. at Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. and spl. asst. settlt. offr.; on mily. duty from Oct., 1917, to Jan., 1919; sub.collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1918; special settlt. officer, Nov., 1920; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Nov., 1929; offg. addl. secty., ditto, Apl., 1932; ditto, public dept., Apl., 1933; collr. (prov. subs.), Aug., 1933; secty. to govt., finance dept., Madras, July, 1934; collr., Oct., 1934; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., April 1939.

JONES, EDMUND BRYAN, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 3rd June, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1909; supt., Dec., 1919; asst. to inspr.-genl., Nov., 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1932; awarded Ind. Police Medal, 1933; ditto (prov. subs.), July, 1934; confd., July, 1938; C.I.E., June, 1939.

JONES, EDMUND DOUGLAS JOHN, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Nov., 1911; dist. supt., July, 1924; employed as dep. supt., watch and ward depot, E.I. Rly., April, 1928.

JONES, EDWARD ELLIS, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd May, 1883).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 5th May, 1915, to 31st March, 1919; asst. bridge engr., April, 1919; bridge engr., E.B. Rly., July, 1928; retd., Nov., 1938.

JONES, FREDERICK THEODORE, C.I.E., M.V.O., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., central public wks. dept.) (b. 30th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 18th Sept., 1907; asst. engr., Sept., 1908; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; exec. engr., May, 1917; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. suptg. engr. variously since May, 1925; confd., Aug., 1933; offg. ch. engr., Nov., 1932; and again, Apl., 1936; K.-i-H. medal, 1st class, Jan., 1936; confd. as ch. engr., Aug., 1938.

JONES, Rev. GRYFFYDD WATCYN, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 2nd May, 1889).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 12th May, 1921; junr. chaplain, May, 1923; senr. ditto, Sept., 1929.

JONES, HAROLD GORDON, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr. E.I. rly.) (b. 5th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 4th Apl., 1920; asst. rolling stock officer, Feb., 1925; transptn. (power) supt., E.I. rly., Aug., 1934; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Apl., 1936; confd., Jan., 1938.

JONES, HERBERT ARNOLD, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 22nd Apl., 1886).—Joined the service, 11th April, 1901; dist. opium officer, June, 1922; retd., Aug., 1934.

JONES, HUBERT CECIL, A.R.S.M., A.R.C.S., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 2nd Dec., 1881).—Joined the service 2nd Dec., 1906, as asst. supt.; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Sept., 1917; supt., March, 1923; retd., June, 1933.

JONES, Rev. HUGH, M.A., B.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 13th May, 1878).—Joined the service, 27th April, 1910; junr. chaplain, April, 1913; senr. chaplain, April, 1923; retd., May, 1933.

JONES, IVAN ELLIS, Indian Civil Service (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 26th June, 1903).—Educ. at The High School, Harcourt Street, Dublin, and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., home dept., Nov., 1929; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1931; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1934; chairman, Lahore improvement trust, Nov., 1935, variously to Oct., 1937.

JONES, IVOR DAVENPORT, M.D. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.C.P.S. (Bom.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. retd. (b. 9th Sept., 1879).—Joined, 30th Jan., 1904, and served in mil. dept. prior to Jan., 1910; civil surg., Aug., 1913; on mil. duty from 4th Oct., 1914, to 28th Mar., 1920; retd., Sept., 1934.

JONES, MONTAGU EDWARD NIGEL, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 7th June, 1876).—Trained at R.I.E., Coll.; joined the telegraph service, 1st Oct., 1898, as asst.

supt.; supt., Aug., 1910; transf. to field service, Waziristan fd. force, from June to Aug., 1917; dir. tel. engrg., May, 1922; dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1928; dir., tel. engrg., Madras circle, Apr., 1929; offg. P.M.G., Madras, Jan., 1930; retd., June, 1931.

JONES, PHILIP NORTON, Indian Police (supt. and dep. commr., Bengal) (b. 27th Mar., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 7th Dec., 1922; offg. addl. supt., Jan., 1929; awarded King's police medal, Oct., 1933; supt., Mar., 1933; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, June, 1935.

JONES, REGINALD TREVOR, M.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 19th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service, Oct., 1912, as asst. engr.; I.A.R.O. from 16th June, 1915, to 29th March, 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; under-secty. to govt., public wks. dept., Nov., 1927; offg. supt. engr., variously from July, 1932.

JONES, THOMAS HENRY BERTRAM, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mech. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Oct., 1900).—Asst. loco. supt., junr. scale, 11th Dec., 1922; divl. mech. engr. (prov.), senr. scale, Feb., 1937; confd., Jan., 1939.

JONES, THOMAS KIRKHAM, M.C., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 11th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as forest engr., Punjab, on 27th Nov., 1921; under-secty. to govt., pub. wks. dept. (elect.), Punjab, Jan., 1930; apptd. to I.S.E. irrig. bch., Punjab, Jan., 1935; prinpl., govt. sch. of engrg., Rasul, Apr., 1936.

JONES, WILLIAM JOHNSTONE, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 2nd Apr., 1884).—Joined the service, 28th March, 1904; asst. acctnt.-gen., May, 1913; dep. acctnt.-gen., Central Provs., May, 1924; ditto, posts and tels., April, 1925; dep. contr., Assam, Oct., 1926; audit officer, N.W.F.P., Apr., 1929; offg. pay and accts. officer, N.W.F.P., Aug., 1929; dep. govt. exr., B.N. Rly., Jan., 1932; offg. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Apr., 1933; dep. govt. exr. of accts., B.N. rly., Apr., 1934; and again, Oct., 1934; offg. acct.-genl., Central Provs., July, 1934; ditto, United Provs., Mar., 1935; offg. ch. accts. officer, E.B. rly., Oct., 1935; acct. genl., Bengal, Apr., 1937; retd., April, 1939.

JORDAN, HENRY PAUL, M.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 30th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 30th Oct., 1910, and apptd. prof. of mechanical engrg., Thomason Coll., Roorkee; in mil. employ from May to Oct., 1915; prof., Queen's Coll., Benares, Oct., 1922; actg. prof. of structural design, coll. of engrg., Poona, Nov., 1922; confd., Aug., 1924; retd., Jan., 1933.

JOSCELYNE, ALAN HOPE, V.D. late Indian State Railways (b. 6th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1901, as asst. loco. supt., state rlys.; dist. loco. supt., April, 1906; on mil. duty with Indian expedy. force 'B,' from Sept., 1914, to Jan., 1915; dep. loco. supt., June, 1917; offg. loco. supt., May, 1926; confd., Jan., 1929; ch. mechl. engr., Jan., 1932; retd., July, 1935.

JOSCELYNE, HAROLD AVELING, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. engr.) (b. 9th Mar., 1886).—Joined the service as asst.

engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr. Apr., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1930; offg. contr. of stores, Feb., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1932; confd., May, 1934; offg. ch. engr., Sept., 1932; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., Sept., 1934; ch. engr., Dec., 1935.

JOSEPH, NADUVATHUSERI JOSEPH, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 22nd Apr., 1897).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 19th June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., June, 1920; offg. exec. engr., April, 1927; confd., June, 1929.

JOSEPH, PALAKUNNATH MATHAI, M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 24th Apr., 1897).—Joined the service, 4th Apr., 1921; asst. acct.-genl., Bombay, Aug., 1922; asst. dir. of audit, United Provs., Apr., 1929; dep. acct.-genl., Burma, June, 1930; asst. dir., army audit, western cmd., May, 1931; dep. contr., Assam, Aug., 1932; dep. dir. of comml. intell. and statistics, commerce dept., govt. of India, June, 1934; reverted to Indian Audit dept. from Apr., 1937 and posted to Madras as dep. acct. genl.; comptr., Orissa, Jan., 1939.

JOSHI, DATTATRAYA SHRIDHAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (under secty. home pol. depts., Bombay) (b. 11th October, 1908).—Educ. at the New English School, Poona, Bombay Univ., the School of Economics, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th October, 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; under secty., home pol. depts. and passport offcr., Dec., 1937.

JOSHI, GAJANAN KRISHNA, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 27th Oct., 1899).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Oxford Univ. (non-coll.); joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 5th Dec., 1924, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr., dist. magte. and pol. agent, Nov., 1925; under-secty. to govt., rev. dept., March, 1928; collr., Nov., 1936.

JOSHI, JAGANNATH VISHWANATH, M.A. (Cantab.), Commerce Dept., Govt. of India (dep. dir. of statistical research) (b. 21st May, 1898).—Apptd. asst. secty. to the Govt. of Bombay, finance dept., on probn., Jan., 1924; confd., Jan., 1925; dep. dir. of comml. intelligence and statistics, on probn., Dec. 1928; confd., Dec., 1929; apptd. dep. dir. of statistical research, Jan., 1933.

JOSHI, KESHO DAT (see Kesho Dat Joshi).

JOSHI, Sir MOROPANT VISHWANATH, K.C.I.E.—Member of govr.'s exec. council, Central Provs., 17th Dec., 1920, to 16th Dec., 1925; Kt., June, 1923; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1926.

JOSHI, VINAYAK MAHADEO, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 10th July, 1914).—Educ. at New English School, Poona, Bombay Univ., and Caus College, Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

JOTISH, CHANDRA GHOSH, Indian Police (b. 28th Nov., 1912).—Apptd. asst. supt. Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Jan., 1937.

JOYCE, ALEC HOUGHTON, O.B.E., India Office (information officer). (b. 5th Mar., 1894).—On mil. service from Dec., 1914, to Sept., 1919; apptd. clerical officer, Feb., 1922; higher clerical officer, Aug., 1924; asst. to information officer, Mar., 1930; jt. publicity officer, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd Session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; ditto, Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; pers. asst. to prime minister, monetary and econ. conf., London, June-Aug., 1933; officer on spl. duty under Govt. of India, home dept., Apl.-Nov., 1935; asst. information officer, Nov., 1935; again on spl. duty with the Govt. of India, home dept. from Oct., 1936 to Nov., 1937; information officer, Dec., 1937; O.B.E., June, 1938.

JOYCE, ALFRED ERNEST, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 21st Mar., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; suptg. engr., Dec., 1924; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to govt., June, 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1933.

JOYCE, CLIVE EARDLEY, Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.). (b. 18th Feb., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 14th June, 1920; addl. supt., Sept., 1924; offg. supt., Feb., 1927; confd., May, 1929; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1934; senior asst. dir. intell., Quetta, Oct., 1937.

JOYCE, GARRETT JOSEPH, Major, I.M.S. (b. 5th Mar., 1904).—Date of first commn., 22nd Dec., 1923. Civil surgeon and supt. dist. jail, Punjab, Apl., 1937.

JUDGE, DIGBY THOMAS BENJAMIN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bombay) (b. 9th July, 1891).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 13th April, 1910, to 19th July, 1915, and from 7th Dec., 1920, to 3rd Dec., 1924; on milty. duty from Sept., 1915, to June, 1920; asst. supt., Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Feb., 1932, and again, Apl., 1933.

JUDGE, HARRY FREDERICK PERCEVAL, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 16th Mar., 1880).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1907, as asst. examr.; dep. examr., June, 1906; asst. auditor, N.W. Rly., March, 1922; dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. ch. account officer, E.I. Rly., April, 1926; offg. dep. auditor, clearing house accounts, Jan., 1927; offg. ch. auditor, N.W. Rly., July, 1927; offg. audit officer, rly. clearing accts., Oct., 1927; offg. dep. auditor, ditto, Feb., 1928; offg. ch. auditor, E.I. Rly., Aug., 1928; ditto, E.B. Rly., Nov., 1928; services placed temporarily at disposal of commr., rlwys., 1st May, 1930; ch. auditor, G.I.P. rly. and govt. exr. of accts., B.B. and C.I. rly., Nov., 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

JUDGES, FREDERICK CYRIL, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1914; offg. exec. engr., July, 1923; confd., Oct., 1923; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

JUGAL KISHORE, THAKUR, B.A., LL.B., Indian Police Service (b. 23rd Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 1st May, 1926; offg. dist. supt., May, 1932.

JUKES, JOHN EDWIN CLAPHAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Nov., 1878).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst.

collr. and mag. and supt. of land records; under sec. to govt., fin. and rev. depts., March, 1908; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., March, 1909, to Jan., 1912; talukdari settlt. offr., May, 1913; temp. dep. sec. to govt. pol., jud., &c. depts., Bombay, April, 1915; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., Dec., 1918, to April, 1919; jt. sec., fin. dept., Dec., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1921; on special duty in fin. dept. from Nov., 1923, and again from Oct., 1925; actg. auditor general, June, 1925; offg. sec. to govt. of India finance dept., Mar. 1926; offg. auditor-genl., Sept., 1926; contr. of civil accts., Sept., 1927; C.S.I., June, 1930; temp. member, govt.-genl.'s council, Oct., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

JUPP, STANLEY DERRINGTON, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 2nd May, 1909).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 6th Dec., 1928; confd., Dec., 1930.

JWALA PRASAD, Raja, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 22nd Nov., 1876).—Trained at T.C.E. Coll., and apptd. as asst. engr., 13th Oct., 1901; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1922; confd. as suptg. engr., May, 1923; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., Feb., 1925; confd., April, 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

JYOTSANATH GHOSAL (see Ghosal, J.).

KABIR, ALAMGIR MAHINUDDIN AMIRE, B.A., Indian Police (b. 25th Nov., 1911). Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st Mar., 1935; confd. 1937.

KABRAJI, KAEKHUSHRO JEHANGIR, M.Sc., Ph.D., Meteorological dept. (Meteorologist) (b. 28th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as meteorologist on probn., 15th Feb., 1927; confd., Feb., 1929.

KADRI, MAHBUB MIAN IMAM BAKSH, O.B.E., B.A., LL.B., late Bombay C.S.—Served in non-gazetted appts. from 11th Jan., 1893, to 14th June, 1898; actg. sub-judge to Dec., 1905; confd., 17th Dec., 1905; asst. judge and jt. judge, Feb., 1923; O.B.E., June, 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, July, 1924; confd., Nov., 1924; ret'd., Nov., 1928; author of a Gujarati legal work, 'The Conciliators' Guide,' and also 'A Sketch of the Life of Sri Saiyid Ahmedkhan.'

KAHLON, GYAN SINGH, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 13th Mar., 1913). educ. at Govt. Inter. Coll., Ludhiana and Punjab Univ.; joined the Service, 12th Dec., 1938; arrived in India, Jan., 1939, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

KAIWAR, SETHU RAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (under secty., educ., etc., dept., Madras) (b. 2nd July, 1911).—Educ. at the Hindu High Sch., Triplicane, Univ. of Madras, Univ. Coll., London, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1937; under secty., educ. and pub. health dept., Jan., 1938.

KAJI, HIRALAL LALLUBHAI, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (prof., coll. of commerce, Bombay) (b. 8th Apl., 1886).—Held non-gazetted appts. in Rajkot from 11th April, 1910, to 2nd Aug., 1916; lecturer in geography, Bombay, 3rd Aug., 1916; prof'r. ditto, Feb., 1919; I.E.S. from 5th Feb., 1921; prof'r. of

statistics, Feb., 1921; K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd class, June, 1930.

KALE, CHINTAMAN GOVIND, B.A., B.Sc., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 5th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service, 25th Aug., 1917; asst. engr. (prov. service), Aug., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; offg. exec. engr., July, 1923; confd., Aug., 1927.

KALI CHARAN, M.A., Indian Audit Department (b. 28th Oct., 1900).—Joined the service, 24th Feb., 1926; asst. dir. of audit, def. services, Northern Command, May, 1936; asst. acctnt. genl., Punjab, May, 1938.

KALIA, GUR SARAN DAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 22nd Feb., 1915).—Educ. at D.A.V. High School, Lahore, Punjab University and Oriel College, Oxford; appointed on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; conf., 6th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

KALUMAL PAHLUMAL, B.A., LL.B. (b. 18th April, 1877).—Officiated as judge, small cause ct., Karachi, from 3rd Dec., 1927, to 25th Mar., 1929; confd., Mar., 1929; retd., Dec., 1935.

KALYANARAMAN, AYYASWAMI, B.S., Indian Audit Department (b. 18th Jan., 1903). Joined the service, 22nd Mar., 1926; depy. chief auditor, G.I.P. Rly., Apl., 1934; depy. govt. examiner of accts., B. and N.W. Rly., May, 1936; asst. acctnt. gen., United Provs., Aug., 1937; dep. acctnt. gen., United Provs., Dec., 1937; depy. acctnt. genl., Madras, Mar., 1939.

KALYANARAMAN, CHELLAMIER, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 21st Sept., 1906).—Educ. at School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. magte. and collr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., fin. dept., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1935; under-secty. to govt., Orissa, Apl., 1936 to Mar., 1937.

KAMA KAKA, BURJORJI HORMASJI, M.C., Lt.-Col., retd., late I.M.S. (b. 24th Aug., 1883).—First commn., 25th Feb., 1921; civil surg., Dera Imail Khan, May, 1930; agency surg., S. Waziristan, Mar., 1933; civil surg., Kurram, Oct., 1933; C.M.O., Baluchistan, Mar., 1937; retd., Aug., 1938.

KAMALA PATI ROY (see Roy, K. P.).

KAMAL-UD-DIN AHMAD, SHAMS-UL-ULAMA, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 20th Jan., 1882).—Apptd. prin. Chittagong Madrasah, 1st Apl., 1915; offg. prinpl., Calcutta Madrasah, Dec., 1926; ditto, Krishnagar coll., July, 1928; inspr. of schools, Nov., 1932; prin., Krishnagar coll., Dec., 1934; retd., Jan., 1937.

KAMAT DWARKANATH DHARMAJI, B.A., L.M. and S. (Bom.), L.R.C.P. and S. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glas.), Lt.-Col. I.M.S., retd. (b. 29th Feb., 1880).—Joined 2nd Feb., 1907, and served in mil. dept. prior to 5th Feb., 1914; on mil. duty from 11th Aug., 1914, to 12th Aug., 1920; civil surg., Oct., 1918; retd., Mar., 1935.

KAMAT, GAJANAN RAOJI, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 26th Dec., 1908).—Educ. at Bombay University, R.I. College of Science and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in

India, Nov., 1931, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Assam, in the depts. under the chief secty., Nov., 1934; confd., Mar., 1935; headquarters asst. to collr. of salt rev., Madras, May, 1937; on spec. duty, govt. of India, fin. dept., Apl., 1938; asst. finl. advr., mil. fin. Sect., 1938.

KAMATH, HUNDI SRINIVASA, B.A. (Mad.) Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras, University Coll. and Sch. of Economics, London, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, 16th Nov., 1928, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1935.

KAMATH, HUNDI VISHNU, late Indian C.S. (b. 15th July, 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ., London Sch. of Economics and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Central Provs., as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1936; under secty. to govt., Central Provs., Oct., 1936; retd., Apl., 1938.

KAMLAKAR DUBE, Pandit, Rai Bahadur, M.A., late United Provs. C.S. (b. 10th Feb., 1881).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1904; dep. collr., Dec., 1906; mag. and collr., July, 1931; retd., May, 1935.

KAMTA PRASHAD, M.Sc., B.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof. of physics, science coll., Patna) (b. 11th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service 9th Dec., 1919; prof. of physics, Patna, July, 1920; prof., Ravenshaw Coll., March, 1922.

KAMTE, NARAYANRAV MARUTIRAV, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 11th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 18th Aug., 1923; offg. supt. variously from May, 1929; confd., May, 1936; dep. commr., Bombay (crimes) to Apl., 1938.

KANCHI PRASAD, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly. (b. 17th Nov., 1890)).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 13th Sept., 1914; exec. engr., Oct., 1923.

KANETKAR, V. G., Indian Police.—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 30th Jan., 1934; confd. June, 1935.

KANGA, DARAB DINSHAH, A.I.C., M.A., A.I.I.Sc., F.C.S., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th Nov., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1923; prof. of chemistry, Gujarat Coll., June, 1924; retd., Nov., 1934.

KANGA, Sir JAMSHEDJI BEHRAMJI, Kt., M.A., LL.B., Barr., late Juddi Dept., Bombay.—Advocate-general, 16th April, 1922, to Feb., 1935; Kt., June, 1928; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Oct., 1932.

KANIA, HARILAL JERISONDA, LL.B., Judicial dept., Bombay (judge, high ct., Bombay).—Apptd. offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, 17th June, 1930; actg. addl. judge, Oct., 1932; judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1933; chairman, disturbances enquiry comtee, Bombay, Jan., 1939.

KANJILAL, PROPHULLA C. (see Prophulla, C. K.).

KANWAR SAIN, LALA RAI SAHIB, Indian Service of Engrs. (under-secty. (Haveli), Punjab) (b. 2nd Jan., 1899).—Joined the service, 9th Oct., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; under-secty. (Haveli), May, 1937. Author of "A Guide for the Young Men" (in Urdu) regarding social reforms.

KAPADIA, DINSHAH DORABJI, M.A., B.Sc. late Indian Educl. Service (Bombay) (22nd July, 1875).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1919; prof. of maths., Deccan Coll., Sept., 1919; offg. prin., ditto, June, 1927; retd., July, 1930.

KAPADIA, JAMSHED DINSHAH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 15th Feb., 1908).—Educ. at Cambridge University (Non-Collegiate); joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

KAPADIA, MANEKSHA PHIROZSHAH, B.A., Income Tax Dept., Bombay (asst. commr. of income tax) (b. 12th Oct., 1887).—Held temp. and permanent non-gazetted appts. from 21st Dec., 1908, to 7th Feb., 1924; income tax officer, Bombay City, Feb., 1924; asst. commr. of income tax, April, 1925; on spec. duty with Central Bd. of Rev., June, 1937 to Feb., 1938; asst. commr. of inc. tax, Mar., 1938.

KAPOOR, NANAK CHAND, Indian State Ry. (supt., transportation, E.I. Ry.) (b. 12th Mar., 1902).—Joined service on E.B. Ry., Jan., 1926; asst. dist. supt., Jan., 1928; transportation (traffic commercial) supt., senior scale provl., Jan., 1939.

KAPUR, BALKISHAN (see Balkishan Kapur).

KAPUR, BALRAJ KRISHNA, Capt. I.A., Indian Pol. Serv. (b. 12th Jan., 1910).—First Commn., 30th Jan., 1930; under trng. in the Punjab, Nov., 1934; asst. commr., Bannu, Apl., 1936; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Jan., 1938; confd., Sept., 1938.

KAPUR, RAGHU PATI, B.A., B.Sc. Econ., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 2nd Sept., 1914).—Educ. at D.A.V. High School, Lahore, Punjab University, University College, London, and London School of Oriental Studies; appointed on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; conf. 8th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

KAPUR SINGH, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 2nd March, 1909).—Educ. at the Khalsa High School, Lyallpur, the Punjab Univ. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offcr., May, 1936 to May, 1938.

KARAKA, FRAMJI JAHANGIR DOSABHAI FRAMJI, B.A., late Impl. Customs Service (b. 14th Aug., 1882).—Held non-gaz. appts. from 1st Oct., 1907, to 14th May, 1913; asst. collr., May, 1913; offg. collr., June, 1934; retd., Aug., 1937.

KARAMATULLAH, MUHAMMAD, Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 1st May, 1904).—Educ. at University of Madras, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub. collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1930; under-secty., dev. dept., Madras, Apl., 1935 to July, 1936.

KARANDIKAR, SHANKAR KESHAV, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 6th April, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 9th Feb., 1926; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1930; confd., Feb., 1935.

KARAN SINGH, RAI BAHADUR THAKUR, B.A., LL.B. late Indian Police (b. 26th July, 1882).—Joined the service as dep. supt., 6th July, 1906; supt., Oct., 1922; retd., July, 1937.

KARIM, A. (see Abdul Karim).

KARIM, FAZL AHMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 5th Dec., 1907).—Educated at Allahabad University and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934.

KARIM, MUHAMMAD FAZLUL, see MUHAMMAD, F. K.

KARM CHAND, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 8th Feb., 1882).—Joined the service, 21st Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Sept., 1906; exec. engr., Sept., 1914; off. dep. chief engr., Aug., 1925, Nov., 1927, Jan., 1929, and again Sept., 1930; confd., Nov., 1930; retd., Feb., 1937.

KARTAR SINGH, BHAI, Sardar Bahadur, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 2nd Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1912; asst. consvr., Jan., 1923; dep. consvr., Dec., 1925; on depn. to Chamba State, Dec., 1930.

KARUNAKARAN NAYAR, CHETTUR RAO BAHADUR, B.A., Bar-at-Law, Indian Police (b. 22nd June, 1889).—Joined the service, 22nd Feb., 1912; apptd. to the Indian Police as dist. supt., Madras, May, 1937.

KASHI RAM BERY (see Bery, K. R.).

KATHPALIA, K. N. (see Krishna Narayan K.).

KAUL, H. K. (see Hari Kishan Kaul).

KAUL, MAHESHWAR NATH, M.A. (Cantab), Barr. (b. 16th Sept., 1901).—Apptd. dep. secty., Legis. Assembly dept., Sept., 1937.

KAUL, PRADYUMNA KRISHNA, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 14th Jan., 1900).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1923; arrived, 10th Dec., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Apl., 1931; confd., Mar., 1935.

KAUL, TRILOKI NATH, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 27th Dec., 1912).—Educ. at S.P. High School, Srinagar, Kashmir, Punjab Univ., Allahabad Univ., and King's College, London; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

KAULA, GANGARAM, C.I.E., B.A., late India Finance Dept. (b. 9th May, 1877).—Joined the service, 3rd Jan., 1896, as asst. examr., p.w. accts.; dep. examr., Feb., 1900; examr., Jan., 1906; dept. compt., Central Prov., April, 1909; dep. acctnt.-gen., United Prov., April, 1912; ditto, Punjab, Oct., 1914; on spl. duty in connection with revision of P.W. Code, from April, 1918, to March, 1920; acctnt.-gen., May, 1923; chief auditor, O.R. Ry., Dec., 1923; offg. acctnt.-gen., Central Provs., June, 1924; acctnt.-gen., central revenues, May, 1925; acct.-gen., ry.ys., Feb., 1929; dir. of rly. audit, rly. bd., Apl., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1930; offg. contr. of civil accts., Apl., 1930; retd., Oct., 1932.

KAYANI, MALIK RUSTUM, Indian Civil Service (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 18th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1935; confd., Nov., 1936.

KAYE, JOHN FREDERIC BARKER, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 29th Aug., 1895).—Joined the service, 22nd April, 1919; asst. supt., April, 1921; offg. dist. supt., March, 1924; confd., May, 1930; pers. asst. to inspr.-genl. of rlwys., and C.I.D., May, 1931; prin. police training sch., June, 1934.

KAZIJ, ABDEALI MUHAMMAD ALI (see Abdeali M. A. Kaziji).

KAZIM RAZA, SAIVID, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 25th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 18th Feb., 1921; offg. supt., April, 1924, and again April, 1927; confd., Nov., 1932; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., April, 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930.

KEALY, SIR EDWARD HERBERT, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th June, 1873).—Educ. at Felsted, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. asst. commr., Merwara, March, 1902; ditto, Ajmer, April, 1904; 1st asst. to govr.-genl's agent in Central India, Sept., 1904; asst. sec., foreign dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1905; on special duty in foreign dept., Dec., 1905; pol. asst., Jan., 1907; asst. commr., Ajmer, May, 1907; pol. agent, N.W.F. Prov., July, 1908; divl. and sess. judge, Feb., 1909; supt., census operations, Rajputana and Ajmer-Merwara, from May, 1910; transfed. to Bihar, April, 1912; addl. divl. and sess. judge, Derajat, Oct., 1914; ditto, Peshawar, Feb., 1915; sec. to chief commr., N.-W. Frontier Prov., May, 1915; pol. agent in Baghelkhand, March, 1922; and resdt. at Gwalior, May, 1922; resdt. at Baroda, Aug., 1924; C.I.E., July, 1926; offg. agent to govt. gen. in C.I., March, 1927; ditto, in States of Western India, Oct., 1927; confd., April, 1928; Kt., June, 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

KEARNS, CYRIL SEYMOUR VERE, late Indian Police (b. 28th Apr., 1893).—Joined the service, 4th Nov., 1913; asst. supt., Dec., 1914; offg. supt., June, 1926; confd., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

KEARSEY, STEPHEN HENRY, late Indian Police (b. 6th May, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 12th Nov., 1921; offg. dist. supt., March, 1926; confd., March, 1928; supt., rly., police, eastern secn., Feb., 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

KEDAR NATH AGARWAL, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 30th June, 1890).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 1st Nov., 1921; asst. engr., Aug., 1923; apptd. to I.S.E. (United Provs.), as asst. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1930; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

KEEGAN, DENIS FRANK, Impl. Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 2nd Dec., 1896).—Apptd. prob., Impl. Customs Service, 18th Feb., 1924; asst. collr., Madras, Feb., 1926; secty., tariff bd., cotton textile industry enquiry, July, 1926; asst. collr., Bombay, Jan., 1928; and Aug., 1934; ditto, Rangoon, Mar., 1928; and again, Dec., 1932; ditto, Karachi, June, 1931; ditto, Bombay, Aug., 1934; offg. collr., Karachi, Oct., 1936; transfed. to Madras, Feb., 1939.

KEELAN, CYRIL JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 8th June, 1885).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 5th Nov.,

1908; asst. engr., July, 1912; resdt. engr., July, 1916; divl. engr., June, 1926.

KEELAN, DOUGLAS HUGH, V.D., late Indian State Railways (b. 27th Feb., 1879).—Joined the service, 17th June, 1901, as asst. traffic supt.; dist. traffic supt., April, 1910; dep. traffic manager, O. & K. Rly., Oct., 1918; offg. chief operating supt., March, 1925; dep. ch. comml. manager, E.I. Rly., July, 1925; offg. ch. comml. manager, April, 1927; ret'd., June, 1934.

KEELAN, ESMOND HARDING, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 26th Oct., 1903; dist. carr. and wagon supt., Feb., 1916; offg. dep. ditto, Feb., 1925; divl. rolling stock officer and offg. dep. ch. operating supt., Oct., 1927; dep. ch. mechl. engr., Mar., 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

KEELING, SIR HUGH TROWBRIDGE, Kt., C.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Oct., 1887; exec. engr., Oct., 1898; suptg. engr. March, 1912; on deputn. as chief engr. and sec. to the chief commr. in the P.W.D., Delhi, Nov., 1912; member, Impl. Delhi Cttee., 1913; C.S.I., June, 1915; ret'd., April, 1920; re-employed as chief engr. for five years from 14th April, 1920; Kt., June, 1923.

KEENE, HUGH, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 22nd Nov., 1897).—Joined the service, 7th Oct., 1920; confd. asst. supt., July, 1922; offg. dist. supt., Apr., 1928; confd., Oct., 1930; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; asst. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1935; awarded Indian Police medal, Jan., 1939; cent. intell. offr., Madras, March, 1939.

KEILY HENRY GERALD, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1874).—Joined the service as draughtsman, 21st Feb., 1896; asst. loco. supt., March, 1898; dist. ditto, July, 1901; dep. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; offg. ch. transportation supt., March, 1926; confd., June, 1927; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

KEITH, ROBERT FARQUHARSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 22nd June, 1912).—Educ. at Fettes College, Gonville and Caius College, Cambridge, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; appointed after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Jan., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

KEKRE, BALKRISHNA BHASKAR, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., Central Provs. (b. 5th Feb., 1878).—Held acting and permanent non-gazetted appts. from 1st Oct., 1901, to 1st Sept., 1902; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1909; asst. commr. of income tax, March, 1922; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

KELAVKAR, MADHAV KRISHNA, M.B.E., M.B., B.S., D.T.M. & H., M.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S.) (b. 30th Apr., 1893).—First commn. dated 26th Aug., 1926; in mil. employ prior to 31st May, 1933, when he joined medl. store dept., asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Oct., 1938.

KELLAY, HERBERT FRANK, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 7th Feb., 1883).—Apptd. clerk; 2nd class, India store dept., 27th Dec., 1901; on mil. service, Oct., 1917, to Feb., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

KELLY, CHARLES SPENCER, Indian C.S. (dep. secty., defence dept., Burma) (b. 15th June, 1907).—Educ. at King Edward's School, Stafford and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served as asst. commr. in Burma; asst. supt., Shan States, Aug., 1935; dep. secty., defence dept., April, 1939.

KELLY, JAMES, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 31st Aug., 1880).—Appdt. asst. principl., Aitchison Coll., Lahore, 18th March, 1904; principl., ditto, Oct., 1917; retd., Aug., 1935.

KELLY, JOHN WILLIAM, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (contr. of currency, Bombay) (b. 23th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1910; asst. acctnt.-gen., United Prov., May, 1912; ditto, Madras, Dec., 1913; asst. acctnt.-gen., United Prov., May, 1915; ditto, Central Prov., May, 1921; currency officer, Bombay, April, 1922; offg. dep. contr. of currency, Calcutta, Sept., 1925; ditto, Bombay, Oct., 1926; offg. contr. of the currency, Aprl., 1931; June, 1932, and again, Feb., 1933; confd., Feb., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1934; dir., res. bank of India, Nov., 1935.

KELLY, PATRICK JOSEPH, M.B., Ch.B. (N.U.I.), L.M. (Dub.), Major, I.M.S. (D.M.O., Madras) (b. 7th Aprl., 1902).—First commn., 7th Mar., 1927; in mil. employ until 4th July, 1934; dist. med. officer, July, 1934.

KELLY, Sir PATRICK ALOYSIUS, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 14th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1902, and posted to Bombay; supt. of police, Sept., 1912; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1921; commr. of police, Sept., 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; Kt., Jan., 1930; bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1931; presy.-magte., Bombay, Jan., 1932; retd., Aprl., 1935.

KELLY, PHILIP DESMOND LORNE, Indian Police (dep. commr. Bengal) (b. 13th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 22nd Dec., 1919; offg. supt., July, 1924; confd., Aprl., 1930; dep. commr., Feb., 1939.

KELSALL, ROBERT, D.S.O., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Burma Medl. Dept. (b. 18th Oct., 1875).—First commn. dated 31st Aug., 1903; in mil. employ to 3rd Mar., 1905; spl. plague officer, Rangoon, March, 1905; offg. civil surg., April, 1909; on mil. service from 3rd Dec., 1914, to 28th April, 1919; civil surg., May, 1919; offg. medl. supt., gen. hosp., Rangoon, Nov., 1919; confd., Feb., 1921; physician, gen. hosp., Rangoon, June, 1926; retd., Oct., 1930.

KEMM, ARCHIBALD HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th June, 1891).—Educ. at Radley Coll., and Merton Coll., Oxford; appdt. after exam. of 1914; arrived, Dec., 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and settlt. officer; appdt. to the I.A.R.O., August, 1916, to March, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1926; confd., Dec., 1927; addl. dep. secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. dept., Feb., 1928; retd., July, 1933.

KEMP, ARTHUR HUGH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bihar) (b. 28th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Portsmouth Grammar Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct.,

1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. coll. and mag.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1935.

KEMP, CYRIL KENNETH, India Police (supt.) (b. 2nd April, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 2nd Dec., 1922; awarded King's medal, Jan., 1932; offg. supt., Nov., 1933; confd., Mar., 1934.

KEMP, Sir KENNETH MCINTYRE (legal advr. and solr. to the sec. of state) (b. 13th Dec., 1883).—Joined the service as profr., govt. law school, 1st June, 1919; editor, Indian law reports, June, 1921; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1928; retd., May, 1931; appdt. advocate genl., Bombay, Feb., 1935; knighted, May, 1937; retd., Dec., 1937; appdt. legal advr. and solr. to the sec. of state, Dec., 1938.

KENNEDY, ALWYNE JOSEPH, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 13th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Nov., 1920; offg. supt., May, 1924; confd., April, 1929.

KENNEDY, ROBERT STEWART, India Office (suptg. exec. officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 29th Dec., 1879).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Madras Coll., St. Andrews; appdt. 2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., 10th Feb., 1904; transfid. to finl. dept., April, 1905; temp. asst. to resdt. clerks, Aug., 1914; temp. addl. resdt. clerk, Oct., 1914, to Sept., 1915; on mil. service, Sept., 1915, to Oct., 1919; transfid. to Indo-European Tel. Dept., 1920; transfid. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., Feb., 1922; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; also assessor of income tax; suptg. exec. officer, Jan. 1928.

KENNEDY, WILLIAM GEORGE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 29th Jan., 1909).—Educ. at Calday Grange Grammar School, West Kirby, Cheshire, and Jesus College, Cambridge; appdt. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Aprl., 1936.

KENNEDY-MINARDS, W. I., B.Sc., A.M.I. Chem.E., Major, late His Majesty's Mint, Bombay (b. 20th Oct., 1887).—Appdt. dep. assay master (on probn.), Jan., 1920; confd., July, 1920; offg. assay master, Jan., 1922; confd., April, 1922; retd., Feb., 1934.

KENNY, DICK EDWARD COURTENAY, Lt.-Col., I.A., late Burma Commn.
