LAURIE, MALCOLM VVVYAN, Indian Forest Service (silviculturist, Dehra Dun) (b. 30th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1925; forest economist, April, 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930; offg. silviculturist, forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1935; confd., Oct., 1936.

LAW, HENRY DUNCAN GRAVES, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at Westminster School, Edinburgh Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 4th Dec., 1906, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; 2nd asst. resdt., Hyderabad, June, 1912; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1914; 2nd asst. to agent to gov.-gen. in Rajputana, March, 1915; asst. sec., for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1915; under-sec., ditto, April, 1916; spl. asst. to resident in Kashmir, Nov., 1916; first asst. agent of gov.-gen. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1919; H.M.'s consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, Sept., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1927; consul-genl. and agent to gov. genl. in Khorasan, April, 1929; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

LAW, HENRY RUSSEL MACKAY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 16th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

LAWIE, WILLIAM, Major, I.M.S., Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 22nd Jan., 1895).—Date of first commn., 29th Nov., 1923; civil surgeon, 3rd July, 1933.

LAWLEY, WILFRED, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 2nd June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1934.

LAWRENCE, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 8th June, 1900).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 27th Oct., 1919; clerical officer, Jan., 1920; transferred to office of high commr., Oct., 1920; actg. exec. officer, Feb., 1927; confd., June, 1929.

LAWRENCE, Sir HENRY STAVELEY, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Sept., 1896; under sec. to govt. rev. and finl. depts., May, 1898; 1st asst., Sept., 1900; K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd class, May, 1900; offd. as sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Nov., 1901, and as dir. of land records and agric. inspr.-genl. of registrn., &c., Oct., 1901, and March, 1902; confd. in latter appt., Oct., 1902; junr. collr., Dec., 1906; dir. of agric. and co-operative credit societies, June, 1907 to April, 1908; supt. of stamps, &c., Oct., 1911; senr. collr., March, 1912; commr., May, 1916; commr. in Sind, July, 1916; C.S.I., June, 1918; member of gov.'s exec. council, Bombay, 17th April, 1921, to 19th March, 1926; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1926; actg. govr. of Bombay, 20th March, 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1926; member of Royal Commn. on Agric. in India, 1926-7.

LAWRENCE, JAMES TAYLOR, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Jan., 1888).—Educ. at King Edward's Gr. Sch., Five Ways, Birmingham, Aberdeen Univ., Trinity Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte., and asst. judge; actg. judge and sess. judge, May, 1922; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept. (temp.), Oct., 1924; on spl. duty in fin. dept., Dec., 1925, and again, Feb., 1926; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Jan., 1926; offg. judge and sess. judge, and agent for sardars in the Deccan, June, 1926; offg. asst. pol. resdt., Aden, and addl. sess. judge, Aden, April, 1927; judge and sess. judge doing duty as judl. asst. to pol. resdt., Aden, Sept., 1927; ret'd., June, 1932.

LAWRENCE, Sir WALTER ROPER, Bart., G.C.I.E., G.C.V.O., C.B., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 5th Dec., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and in Ajmir and Merwara as pol. asst. and mag.; under sec. to govt., Punjab, 1885 and 1886; under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., Sept., 1886; offd. as sec. in 1888; settlt. officer, Kashmir, April, 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1891; ret'd., Mar., 1897; author of 'The Valley of Kashmir,' 1895; private sec. to the Viceroy (Lord Curzon), Jan., 1899, to Oct., 1903; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1903; G.C.I.E., May, 1906; Bart., July, 1906; member of the Council of India, Oct., 1907, to March, 1909; member of committee on Indian rly. fin. and administration, 1907-8; C.B., Jan., 1917; G.C.V.O., June, 1918.

LAWSON, REGINALD GEORGE BATEMAN, Indian Police (commr., Rangoon city police) (b. 22nd Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 15th Dec., 1914; dist. supt., May, 1918; asst. inspr.-genl., Rangoon, Mar., 1935, and again, Oct., 1936; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., to Oct., 1936; asst. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936; commr., Rangoon city police, Jan., 1938.

LAWSON, WILFRED CHARLES, Indian Police (b. 7th June, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1938.

LAWTHER, BARRY CHARLES ALFRED, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 25th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1908; asst. supt., March, 1910; supt., Feb., 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Punjab, May, 1929; C.I.E., June, 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

LAWTON, HAROLD, F.C.H., late Indian State Railways (b. 22nd Jan., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; asst. agent, E. Bengal Ry., Dec., 1913; suptg. engr., June, 1924; offg. chief engr., Nov., 1924; confd., Feb., 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

LAYARD, AUSTEN HAVELOCK, C.I.E., M.A., Barr.-at-Law, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Rugby and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 29th Dec., 1920, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; dep. commr., Delhi, Mar., 1932; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

LAYTON, GEORGE EDWARD, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 12th Oct., 1879).—Apptd., 1st July, 1925, in connection with the G.I.P. Rly. Co.

LAZARUS, MISS THEODORA, B.A., Indian Educ. Service (inspectress of girls' schools, Madras) (b. 9th Oct., 1884).—Apptd. supt., govt. secondary sch. for women, Royapetta, 28th June, 1924; inspectress of girls' schools, May, 1926.

LEACH, Sir ALFRED HENRY LIONEL (ch. justice, high ct., Madras).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, 15th Feb., 1933; ch. justice, high ct., Madras, July, 1937; knighted, Jan., 1938.

LEACH, ARTHUR GORDON, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 16th March, 1885).—Educ. at Bradfield and New College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 24th Nov., 1909, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. and spl. settlt. offr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; on mil. duty, 1917-19; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1920; ditto, June, 1922; ditto, March, 1925; confd., Jan., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., P.W.D. and labour, April, 1929; C.I.E., June, 1933; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

LEACH, FRANK BURTON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st August, 1881).—Educ. at Eton, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 10th Dec., 1905, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Nov., 1911, to April, 1912, and from Sept., 1912, to April, 1913; dep. commr. s.p.t. and dep. commr., Port Blair, April, 1916; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; on mil. duty, Dec., 1917, to April, 1919; offg. commr., March, 1924; ch. secty. to govt., Burma, Aug., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

LEAKE, JONES INCHIQUIN, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 18th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service, 8th April, 1924; asst. supt., Jan., 1925.

LE BAILLY, WILLIAM FRANCIS GRAHAME, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Wellington and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1932, and Oct., 1933; ditto (subs. provl.), Mar., 1935.

LE BROCO, EDWARD HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 25th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 6th Jan., 1923; offg. supt., April, 1926; confd., Aug., 1934.

LE FEVRE, JOHN REGINALD, Indian Police (b. 15th Aug., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., Nov., 1938.

LECK, JOHN STEVENSON, Indian State Rlys. (elec. and mechl. engr.).—Apptd. electrical and mechl. engr. (temp.), 16th Oct., 1926; ditto (perm.) Jan., 1930.

LECKY-THOMPSON, THOMAS EDWARD, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 7th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, June, 1926; offg. dist. supt., June, 1934.

LECKY-THOMPSON, WILLIAM, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 4th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 10th Sept., 1922; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., Feb., 1936; supt. of police supplies Mar., 1938.

LEDGER, LLOYD KIRKWOOD, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil and agency surg.) (b. 27th May, 1895).—First commn. dated 15th Mar., 1920; in milty. employ up to 30th June, 1925; offg. agency surg., Peshawar, July, 1925; offg. medl. officer, Seistan; agency surg., Bundelkhand, May, 1929; ditto, Gilgit, Oct.,

1932; civil surg., Peshawar, Oct., 1936; O.B.E., June, 1938.

LEDWIDGE, WILLIAM BERNARD JOHN, B.A., Indian Office (asst. principal, political dept.) (b. 9th Nov., 1915).—Educ. at Cardinal Vaughan School and King's College, Cambridge; held Commonwealth Fund Fellowship at Princeton University, U.S.A., Sept., 1937 to June, 1939; apptd. asst. principal, 2nd Oct., 1939.

LEE, Rev. ARTHUR OLDFIELD NORRIS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 6th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, 30th Nov., 1921; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1931.

LEE, DIONYS JOHN NORRIS, B.A., Indian Civil Service (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 9th Oct., 1892).—Educ. at King's School, Canterbury, and Wadham, Coll. Oxford; on mil. service from April, 1915; joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1919, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 2nd Dec., 1919; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924; dep. commr. (prov.), Dec., 1926; confd., March, 1927.

LEE, EDWARD OWEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of excise, etc., Bihar) (b. 3rd Nov., 1891).—Educ. at Malvern and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1915, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1922; services replaced at disposal of govt. of Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1925; mgte. and collr. (prov. sub.), Jan., 1929; confd., May, 1930; offg. dep. commr., Delhi, April, 1929; chairman, Assam labour bd., Apl., 1933; commr. of emigrant lab. in Assam, Oct., 1933; commr. of excise, etc., Bihar, Oct., 1936.

LEE, GEORGE HENRY, M.C., Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 19th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 18th Oct., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Mar., 1931; off. dep. supt. Watch and Ward dept., E.I. rly., April, 1933; dist. supt., June, 1939.

LEE, JOSEPH STANDISH SEARCHFIELD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs., ret'd. (b. 8th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; ret'd. Oct., 1938.

LEE, Rev. Canon PERCY ERSKINE, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 9th Feb., 1882).—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1919; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1921; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

LEE, REGINALD HENRY, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd., late Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 19th May, 1880).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1906; civil surg., Oct., 1921; ret'd., May, 1935.

LEE, Rev. ROBERT EWING, M.A., B.D., M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 26th Sept., 1883).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 23rd Oct., 1923; on mil. service from 13th June, 1916, to 22nd Sept., 1919; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1916; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1925; services placed at disposal of Army Dept., from 28th March, 1924; ditto, Bombay Govt., from 10th Jan., 1927; offg. presy. senr. chaplain, church of Scotland, Bombay, Dec., 1927; ditto, Madras, Nov., 1928; confd., Feb., 1931; offg. presy. senr. chaplain, Bengal, Oct., 1933; confd., Mar., 1935; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

LEEPER, WILLIAM CLUFF, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Nov., 1899).—First commn., 15th Apl., 1919; in milty. employ to 21st Aug., 1928; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1929; pers. asst. to ch. commr. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., May, 1930; asst. commr., Peshawar, May, 1931; ditto, Kohat, Mar., 1933; ditto, Nowshera, June, 1933; ditto, Charsadda, Oct., 1933; ditto, Mardan, Feb., 1935; asst. commr., Manshera, May, 1937; pol. agent, Kurram, Oct., 1937.

LEES, LEONARD MARTYN, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 2nd Dec., 1898).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 21st Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt. (Edn.), June, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1927; dep. commr., p.s., Apl., 1937.

LEES, ROGER EDWARD ROWLEY, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 14th Aug., 1914).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 22nd Nov., 1934.

LEETE, Capt. M.B.T.S., Civil Aviation Directorate (b. 8th Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as addl. technical officer (temp.), Apl., 1936.

LEGGETT, STEPHEN, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Punjab (b. 4th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Oct., 1907; supt., Aug., 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

LEIGH, Rev. ARTHUR GEORGE DAVIES, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senior chaplain, Dec.) (b. 3rd Apl., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 26th Dec., 1926; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1928; senior chaplain, Dec., 1938.

LEIGHTON, ERIC, Indian Police (b. 11th May, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provinces, 7th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1928.

LE MESURIER, ALGERNON PAUL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. coll., Bombay) (b. 28th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Rugby, Lausanne, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr., June, 1936.

LEMON, GUY TALBOT, O.B.E., V.D., B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Riys. (b. 24th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 16th April, 1910; in milty. employ from 28th Nov., 1914, to 20th March, 1920; O.B.E., 1919; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1926; confd., Nov., 1926; pers. asst. to engr. in-ch., Nov., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

LEMON, ROBERT ERNEST, B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 2nd June, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; ret'd., Apr., 1930.

LENNAN, AIDAN WILLIAM, late Indian Police (b. 12th Aug., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Madras, 26th Nov., 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

LENNOX, GILBERT, Capt. I.A., ret'd., late Survey of India (b. 19th Feb., 1894).—First commn. dated 22nd Jan., 1913; asst. supt. (proby.), June, 1920; confd., June, 1922; supt., Dec., 1928; ret'd., May, 1932.

LESLIE, ANDREW SMITH, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. ret'd. (b. 24th June, 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1906; D.M.O., Kurnool, Dec.,

1923; offg. prof. of medicine, medl. coll., Vizagapatam, Feb., 1926; offg. civil surg., Sept., 1926; confd., June, 1929; offg. surg. to govr. of Madras, June, 1929; retd., June, 1937.

LESSLIE, DAVID FINDLAY, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 31st Mar., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Jan., 1920; offg. supt., March, 1925; confd., Aug., 1920; asst. to dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936.

LESTER-GARLAND, Rev. GEORGE HENRY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 9th May, 1895).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, N.W.F.P., 26th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1926; senr. chaplain, April, 1936.

L'ESTRANGE, ALBERT PERCIVAL PORTER, Land Records Dept., Burma (asst. dir.) (b. 13th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as probnr., 22nd Sept., 1903; supt., Aug., 1907; asst. dir., Aug., 1930.

LETHBRIDGE, MONTAGU HENRY BOWATER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 22nd March, 1889).—Educ. at Westminster, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1915; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1918; dist. and sess. judge and commr. for workmen's compensation, Nov., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Assam, legisl. dept., Oct., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; dist. and sess. judge, Bengal, Nov., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, selection grade, confd., Jan., 1939.

LETT, HARRY NELSON, D.F.C., M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 21st Oct., 1896).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Southampton, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 21st Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1925; confd., Mar., 1935; secty. to finl. commr., Rangoon, Aug., 1933.

LETTON, CHARLES THOMAS, C.I.E., M.B.E., late Printing and Stationery Dept. (b. 25th Mar., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 8th April, 1907; offg. supt. govt. printing, India, Calcutta, May, 1911; supt., govt. central branch press, Simla, April, 1914; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; offg. manager, govt. of India press, Calcutta, March, 1925; confd., Oct., 1926; offg. contr. of ptg., staty. and stamps, Feb., 1927; confd., Oct., 1930; retd., Mar., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935.

LEVINGE, SIR EDWARD VERE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 28th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., jud., &c., depts., Nov., 1893; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1896; mag. and collr., July, 1898; temp. addl. commr., Feb., 1908; offg. chief sec. to govt., April, 1910; commr., April, 1911; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa as member, bd. of rev., April, 1912; member, exec. council, Aug., 1912, to Nov., 1917; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1916; actg. lieut.-govr., Bihar and Orissa, from April, 1918; retd., Aug., 1918.

LEWIS, ARTHUR LLEWELYN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 24th

Jan., 1915).—Educ. at the County Intermediate Sch., Barmouth and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn. 29th Sept., 1937; confd. 13th Dec., 1938; arrived in India, Jan., 1939; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

LEWIS, CLINTON GRESHAM, Brig., O.B.E., R.E., Survey of India (surveyor-genl.) (b. 25th Nov., 1885).—First commn. dated 21st Dec., 1904; apptd. asst. supt. trigl. survey, 7th Nov., 1907; in charge Miri mission survey detachment, Oct., 1911, to Apl., 1912; temply. reverted to milly. duty Oct., 1914, to July, 1918; dep. supt., Aug., 1922; on foreign service with Turco-Iraq frontier delimitation commn., from Feb., 1927; supt., April, 1928; O.B.E., June, 1928; offg. dir., Apl., 1933; confd., June, 1934; surveyor-genl., Sept., 1937.

LEWIS, ELWYN BYRON, Cent. excises and salt dept., Northern India (chief mining engr., Khewra) (b. 3rd Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 5th Nov., 1935; chief mining engr., Khewra, Nov., 1935.

LEWIS, LUCIEN ALEXANDER, M.I.E.E. Office of High Commr. for India (dep. dir. of inspection, store dept.) (b. 3rd May, 1882).—Educ. at Brighton Gr. Sch., and City and Guilds Inst., Finsbury Coll.; apptd. asst. inspr. of scientific supplies, Store Depot, 19th Nov., 1906; electrical engr., ditto, Aug., 1917; dep. dir. of inspection, store dept., July, 1936.

LEWIS, Rev. OLIVER GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estab. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, 5th Oct., 1929; junior chaplain, Oct., 1931.

LEWIS, SYDNEY ALLAN MAYBURY, M.R.San.I., Indian Service of Engrs. (supg. engr., Sind) (b. 23rd Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1933; suptg. engr. (provl. perm.), May, 1936; confd., Dec., 1937.

LEWIS, WALTER ALAN SCOTT, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st July, 1907).—Educ. at Winchester and Lincoln College, Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1933.

LEWIS, SIR WILLIAM HAWTHORNE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (reforms commr.) (b. 29th June, 1888).—Educ. at Oundle, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on censor duty, Bombay, from Apl., 1915, to March, 1916; under-sec. to govt., rev. and jud. depts., Bihar and Orissa, June, 1918; jt. mag., March, 1921; offg. dep. commr., March, 1923; offg. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Oct., 1925; confd. and magte. collr., Oct. 1926; on spl. duty, home dept., govt. of India, April, 1927; jt. secty., reforms office, May, 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf. London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; jt. secty., reforms office, Jan., 1932; offg. reforms commr., Govt. of India, May, 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; addl. secty., reforms, govt. of India, Feb., 1935; reforms commr., July, 1936; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

LEWIS, WILLIAM HOWARD, Indian Police (b. 7th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 14th Nov., 1927; offg. supt., Feb., 1934; offg. dep. commr., Calcutta, Feb., 1936.

LEWISOHN, LEON HORNBV, Indian Police (b. 24th Dec., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, Nov., 1932; confd., Mar., 1935; a.d.c. to govr., Orissa, Feb., 1938.

LEWTON-BRAIN, GERALD EVAN, Indian Police (b. 24th June, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 17th Nov., 1926.

LEWYS-LLOYD, IDRIS WYN, B.A., Indian Civil Service (jt. magte., United Provs.) (b. 7th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Colwyn County Sch., Univ. of Wales and Paris; joined the service, 10th Dec., 1928; arrived, 12th Jan., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Mar., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1934; settlement offcr., Nov., 1937.

LIAQAT HAYAT KHAN, SIR, NAWAB, KHAN BAHADUR, K.B.E., late Indian Police Service (Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 22nd May, 1909; asst. supt., March, 1919; supt., Dec., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1923; home sec., Patiala State, Oct., 1923; ret'd., June, 1931; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd Session, Nov., 1932; knighted, Jan., 1933; K.B.E., Jan., 1939.

LIDDELL, HENRY CRAWFORD, C.I.E., M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bengal), (b. 17th Dec., 1878).—Educ. at Geo. Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 5th Dec., 1901, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, April, 1906; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1910; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1914; ditto, Assam, Feb., 1916; re-transfd. to Bengal as dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1922; supt. and legal remembr. and sec., judicial dept., govt. of Bengal, June, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

LIDIERTH, JAMES EUGENE, I.S.O., M.B.E. (offr. of pol. dept.) (b. 12th Oct., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st June, 1908, to 15th Oct., 1919; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; extra asst. commr., April, 1921; offg. under-sec. to agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1922, and again, Aug., 1929; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal (2nd class) in 1925; registrar, A.G.G.'s office, Quetta, April, 1929; asst. secty. to A.G.G. and C.C. in Baluchistan, Aug., 1929; offg. under secty., ditto, Dec., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Apl., 1934; offg. under secty. to A.G., Baluchistan; July, 1935; I.S.O., May, 1937.

LIGHTFOOT, GORDON SHELLEY, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 18th Aug., 1897).—Served in the Indian Army from 12th May, 1915, to 18th May, 1923; proby. asst. supt., 19th May, 1923; confd., May, 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; supt., May, 1930; pol. officer, Balipara frontier tract, Oct., 1934.

LIGHTWLER, IVOR HARRISON, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores) (b. 21st Dec., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 20th May, 1912; asst. contr. of stores, April, 1919; dist. ditto, Mar., 1931.

LILEY, WALTER HEMMING, A.M.I.M.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 7th June, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. signal and interlocking engr., 31st May, 1907; signal and interlocking engr., June, 1917; dep. ch. engr., signals, G.I.P. rly., Aug., 1926; ret'd., Dec., 1935.

LILLEY, HUGH MICHAEL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Burma) (b. 2nd Mar., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., 5th Dec., 1934; confd., Dec., 1936.

LILLIE, CECIL JAMES WINGATE, Indian C.S. (dep. secty. Govt. of India, Home Dept.) (b. 6th July, 1897).—Educ. at Clifton, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1921; arrived, 1st Dec., 1921; and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925; under secty. to govt., Dec., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1932; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. of land records, etc., May, 1933; offg. secty., rev. dept., Central Provs., Nov., 1936; dep. commr., Mar., 1937; dep. secty. Govt. of India, Home Dept., Nov., 1938.

LILLIE, HORACE, Indian Police (b. 2nd Apl., 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1906; asst. supt., Nov., 1907; supt., Aug., 1914; comdt., frontier constaby., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1933.

LIMPUS, CHARLES GEORGE, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 20th Apl., 1871).—Joined the service 18th Jan., 1889; asst. dist. loco. supt., July, 1919; asst. trans. supt., Nov., 1922; divl. trans. supt., Aug., 1926; offg. dep. ditto, Feb., 1928; ret'd., May, 1932.

LINCKE, SYDNEY HOWARD PHIPPS, Indian state Rlys. (dep. contr. of stores, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 7th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 8th Nov., 1912; dist. contr. of stores, Nov., 1926; dep. ditto, Nov., 1928; offg. contr. of stores, variously from Nov., 1929; confd., June, 1939.

LINCOLN, EDWARD HENNING, V.D., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 16th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service, 14th Aug., 1905; extra asst. commr., Mar., 1934; dep. commr., Nov., 1933; offg. secty., elec. and indus. depts., Punjab, Apl., to July, 1936.

LINCOLN, HENRY HENNING, M.B.E., late Dept. of Educn., Health and Lands, Govt. of India (b. 25th Mar., 1882).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1898; supt., dept. of educn., health and lands, Govt. of India, Apl., 1923; offg. regr., ditto, Oct., 1926; asst. secty., ditto, Jan., 1928; M.B.E., Jan., 1935; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

LINDOP, KENNETH JAMES HULME, M.C., Indian C.S. (commr. of settlements and land records, Burma) (b. 13th July, 1898).—Educ. at Denston Coll., Staffs, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. settlt. officer, March, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1928; confd., Jan., 1936; commr. of settlements and land records, July, 1939.

LINDSAY, Sir HARRY ALEXANDER FANSHAW, K.C.I.E.,—C.B.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Mar., 1881).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 28th Nov., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., rev. and genl. depts., Mar., 1910; transf. to Bihar, April, 1912; under sec. to govt., rev. dept., April, 1912; under sec. to govt. of India, comm. and ind. dept., from Nov., 1912; dir.-gen., comml. intelligence, India, from Jan. 1916; C.B.E., June, 1919; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Sept., 1921; Indian trade commr., London, from 1st Feb., 1923, to Sept., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; sec. and adviser to Indian del., Impl. econ. conf., May to Sept., 1932; adviser to Indian delegation, monetary and economic conf., June, 1933; deputed to Indian lac cess ctee., U.S.A., Nov., 1933; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1934; commr., Dec., 1935; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

LINDSAY, JAMES HAMILTON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 13th March, 1882).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived 4th Dec., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1912; mag. and collr., April, 1918; sec. to govt. of Bengal, educn. dept., May, 1925; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

LINDSAY, ROBERT WILLIAM (b. 20th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service 16th July, 1909; held non-gaz. appt. in mil. dept.; services placed at disp. of govt. of Burma, P.W. dept., from Sept., 1921, as asst. engr.; employed as exec. engr., Port Blair, variously from Apl., 1923 and from Oct., 1929; again asst. engr. (B.E.S.) Burma, Dec., 1928 to Sept., 1929.

LINDSAY, REV. W. T., M.A., Ind. Eccles. Estabt. (b. 2nd Jan., 1897).—Apptd. junior chaplain, Bombay, on probn., 8th Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

LINES, ALFRED, F.C.H., late Indian State Railways (b. 15th Oct., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1908; suptg. engr., Aug., 1923; chief engr., May, 1925; offg. dir., civil engrg., rly. board, April, 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

LINES, ROY NICHOLAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 11th Sept., 1914).—Educ. at Royal Latin Sch., Buckingham, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

LINGWOOD, FREDERICK, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 1st Mar., 1901).—Apptd. clerical officer, 3rd Feb., 1924; actg. exec. officer, Feb., 1929; confd., Feb., 1936.

LINLITHGOW, Most Hon. Marquess of, VICTOR ALEXANDER JOHN HOPE, P.C., K.T., G.M.S.I., G.M.I.E., O.B.E., T.D. (viceroy and gov.-genl. of India) (b. 24th Sept., 1887).—Educ. at Eton; served European War, 1914-1918 (despatches); civil Lord of the Admy., 1922-24; presdt. navy league, 1924-31; chairman, departl. ctee. on distribn. and prices of agricl. produce, 1923; chairman, Rl. commn. on Indian agriculture, 1926-28; chairman, jt. select committee on

Indian constitl. reform, 1933; G.C.S.I., Apl., 1936; viceroy and gov.-genl. of India, Apl., 1936; Kt. of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, June, 1936.

LISTER, JOHN NAPIER, M.C., M.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; on mil. service from 21st Nov., 1915, to 1st Nov., 1920; exec. engr., Nov., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1928; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

LISTER, Rev. JOHN GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 8th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, 2nd Feb., 1914; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1927.

LISTER, Tom, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th May, 1887).—Educated at Wheelwright Gr. Schools, Dewsbury, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1911, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; priv. sec. to lieut.-govr., April, 1917; services placed temporarily at disposal of govt. of United Provs., Jan., 1918; priv. sec. to lieut.-govr., United Prov., Feb., 1918; depd. to Federated Malay States on special duty, Aug., 1919, to Jan., 1920; asst. commr. and sec. to devl. commr., Burma, Jan., 1921; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1922; and sec. to finl. commr. (addl.), Nov., 1924; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), Sept., 1927; offg. secty. to govt., rev. dept., May, 1929; confd., Sept., 1929; offg. secty. to govt., reforms office, June, 1930; delegate to Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1932; ret'd., May, 1937.

LISTER-JACKSON, ARTHUR, late Indian State Rly. (b. 18th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1914; on milty. service from Sept., 1915, to Nov., 1919; exec. engr. (junr.), Oct., 1923; ditto (senr.), July, 1934; ret'd. Nov., 1938.

LITTLE, EDWARD PYBUS, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 28th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1935.

LITTLE, MALCOLM, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th Dec., 1877).—Joined the service, 21st Nov., 1905; asst. engr., Nov., 1908; exec. engr., Nov., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1926; confd., Sept., 1928; offg. secy., P.W.D., Assam, June, 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; ch. engr., Oct., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

LITTLEHAILES, RICHARD, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 14th Feb., 1878).—Joined the service, 13th July, 1903, as prof. of maths., Presdy. Coll., Madras; inspr. of schools, Oct., 1908; prof. of physics, Presdy. Coll., June, 1913; meteorologist and dep. dir., Madras Observatory, June to Nov., 1913; prof. of mathematics, Presdy. Coll., Aug., 1913; dir. of public instrn., Madras, Feb., 1919; offg. eduel. commr. with govt. of India, Feb., 1925, and again, Apl., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., Feb., 1933.

LITTLEWOOD, ALFRED MALCOLM CECIL, late Indian Forest Service (b. 5th Oct., 1879).—Joined the service, 22nd July, 1899; dep. consvr., Mar., 1920; actg. consvr., Apl., 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

LITTLEWOOD, GLADYS ELIZABETH, M.B.E., Indian Educl. Service (inspectress of girls' schools, N.W.F.P.) (b. 1st Nov., 1886).—Joined

the service, 16th June, 1918; inspectress of schools, Ambala, Nov., 1919; ditto, N.W.F.P., May, 1920; K.-i-H. Medal, 1st Cl., June, 1934.
LITTLEWOOD, ROWLAND WHITELAW, Indian Agric. Service (b. 19th June, 1888).—Appdt. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, Jan., 1921.

LITTLEWORTH, MICHAEL CHARLES GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 19th May, 1919).—Appdt. asst. supt., United Provs. on probn., Nov., 1938.

LLBVELLYN, JOHN LISTER, B.A. (Cantab.) Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 10th April, 1906).—Educ. at Nottingham High School, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1929; arrived, November, 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931; off. mag. and collr., July, 1935.

LLOYD, Sir ALAN HUBERT, C.S.I., C.I.E. B.A., Indian C.S., Burma, attd. Impl. Customs Service (member, central board of revenue) (b. 30th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; appdt. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; off. asst. collr. of customs, Impl. Customs Service, Rangoon, March, 1910; confd., Nov., 1911; transf. to Bengal as asst. collr. of customs, June, 1916; on deput. under commercial intelligence dept., India, Dec., 1916, to June, 1917; off. collr. of customs, June, 1917; confd., Dec., 1919; appdt. first member of central board of rev., govt. of India, Dec., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; temp. member, govt.-genl's. exec. council, June, 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; member, council of state, Apl., 1935; knighted Jan., 1939; temp. additional secty. to Govt. of India, commerce dept., May, 1939.

LLOYD, ARTHUR HENRY, M.C., B.A. late Indian Forest Service (b. 15th June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., May, 1923; dir., Pyinmana forest school, Dec., 1925; retd., Aug., 1930.

LLOYD, Baron, Rt. Hon. GEORGE AMBROSE LLOYD OF DOLOBRAN, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., D.S.O., F.R.G.S.—Govr. of Bombay from 16th Dec., 1918, to 9th Dec., 1923.

LLOYD, Sir IDWAL GEOFFREY, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Jan., 1878).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; appdt. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., July-Aug., 1905; regr., chief court, Lower Burma, Dec., 1906; under sec. to govt. of India, Home Dept., Feb., 1910; sec. to finl. commr., Burma, Jan., 1913, to May, 1919; dep. commr., April, 1916; off. commr., Oct., 1924; commr. (provl.), April, 1926; ch. secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., Nov., 1929; finl. commr. (reserved subjects), Aug., 1930; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Burma, Oct., 1930; confd., June, 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; finl. commr., July, 1933; member govr.'s exec. council, Burma (temp.), Apl., 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; knighted, May, 1937; retd., Dec., 1937.

LLOYD, REGINALD BROUGHTON, M.B., Lt. Col., I.M.S. retd. (b. 31st Aug., 1881).—First commn., dated 1st Aug., 1908; in milty. employ up to 25th Sept., 1912; probnr., chemical examr.'s dept., Calcutta, Sept., 1912; in milty. employ. from Oct., 1914, to April, 1919;

offg. impl. serologist, May, 1919; confd., July, 1920; retd., Apl., 1936.

LLOYD, STEPHEN, late Indian C.S. (b. 5th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at Marlborough and Lincoln College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th October, 1930; arrived in India, November, 1930, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; retd., May, 1935.

LLOYD-JONES, CYRIL MICHAEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 1st Nov., 1914).—Educ. at Oundle and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; appdt. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 22nd Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

LOBB, JOHN CHALICE, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1897).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1919; supt., Nov., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; offg. dep. insp.-genl., variously from Apl., 1935.

LOBO, CHARLES MANUEL, LL.B., Judl. Dept., Bombay (addl. judl. commr., Sind) (b. 11th Aug., 1884).—Appdt. Govt. pleader and public prosecutor for Sind, 1st Apl., 1929; offg. addl. commr. for Sind, July, 1929; and again, Jan., 1936; confd., Mar., 1936; addl. judl. commr., Sind, May, 1938.

LOBO, PASCHAL CONSTANTINE, Diwan Bahadur, B.A. (Cantab.), Barr., late Madras Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (b. 17th May, 1878).—Joined the service as dist. munsi, 4th Aug., 1910; sub. judge, June, 1917; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1922; retd., May, 1933.

LOBO-PRABHU, JOSEPH MATHIAS, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 26th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the United Provs. as asst. coll. and mag.

LOCH, PERCY GORDON (name changed to DALYELL of the BINNS), C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., (ret'd.), late Indian Political Service (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—First commn., 5th Aug., 1905; in mil. employ till Feb., 1913; appdt. pol. officer on probation, March, 1913, and employed as asst. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf; on mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to Oct., 1916; pol. agent, Bahrein, Nov., 1916; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1921; pol. agent, Gilgit, Sept., 1924; secty. to resdt., Mysore, Feb., 1929; offg. resdt., Mysore and C.C. Coorg, May, 1930; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar Agency, Mar., 1931; on foreign service as presdt. of Rewa Council; offg. resdt., Persian Gulf, Apl., and July, 1933; and again, July, 1934; pol. agent, Bahrein, May, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1935; offg. resdt., Persian Gulf, July, 1935; pol. agent, Bahrein, Oct., 1936; retd., May, 1938.

LOCKE, JAMES EDWIN, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 28th Sept., 1887).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Feb., 1907; asst. supt., Madras, May, 1919; supt., Madras, July, 1919; ditto, Lahore, Sept., 1919; asst. to dep. dir.-genl., Nov., 1921; supt., C.T.O., Rangoon, Aug., 1924; asst. dir. genl. traffic, Dec., 1926; dep. P.M.G., traffic, Calcutta, Mar., 1927; ch. supt., Calcutta tel. office, June, 1930; dep. p.m.g. traffic, Burma, Oct., 1932; retd., Feb., 1934.

LOCKWOOD, HENRY FRANCIS, late Indian State Railways (b. 26th Sept., 1879).—Joined the service, 3rd Aug., 1907; asst. traffic supt., E. Ben. Rly., Aug., 1907; ditto, N.W. Rly., Feb., 1912; dist. traffic supt., ditto, Oct., 1912; offg. divl. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Oct., 1925; offg. ch. operatg. supt., Feb., 1930; and again, Apl., 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; retd., Sept., 1934.

LODGE, RONALD FRANCIS, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 8th April, 1889).—Educ. at Liverpool Institute High Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1925; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Apl., 1935, and again, Aug., 1936; regr. high ct., 1937.

LOGAN, FRANCIS HUGH, late Indian C.S. (b. 1st July, 1904).—Educ. at Haileybury, Corpu; Christi Coll., Cambridge, Sorbonne, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Mar., 1930; jt. magte., Nov., 1931; retd., June, 1938.

LOGAN, ROBERT ANCEL, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.) Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Central Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 13th Nov., 1889).—First commn., 10th Oct., 1919; civil surgeon, Apl., 1932.

LOKUR, NARAYAN SWAMIRAO, B.A., LL.B., Bombay Civil Service (judge and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 21st Apl., 1887).—Held various judl. appts. from 22nd Oct., 1918, to 31st Dec., 1925; judge and sess. judge, Bombay, Jan., 1926.

LONG, CECIL EUSTACE, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 28th Feb., 1882).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 11th Aug., 1902 to 18th Apl., 1907; 2nd asst. supt. of stores, Apl., 1907; 1st ditto, Oct., 1922; dep. supt. of stores, Oct., 1925; offg. supt. of stores, Mar., 1928, and again, Mar., 1932; confd., Oct., 1935; retd. Feb., 1939.

LONGHURST, ALBERT HENRY, late Archaeological Survey dept. (b. 2nd Nov., 1876).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Nov., 1906; supt., Sept., 1910; retd. Nov., 1931.

LONGHURST, HENRY RICHARD, Indian Police (b. 20th Dec., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, Nov., 1932; confd. July, 1935; asst. comdt., Malabar special police, Oct., 1936.

LONGRIGG, JOHN HEMSLEY, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 6th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1913; dep. consvr., Dec., 1918; offg. consvr., Aug., 1928; princp. for. coll., Coimbatore, Jan., 1937.

LONSDALE, CHRISTOPHER EDWIN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 18th Aug., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, 19th Nov., 1931; confd., Jan., 1934.

LORD, HUGH PROBYN MALET, Lt.-Col., I.A., retd., late Burma Commn. (b. 26th Aug., 1878).—Arrived in India, March, 1898, and served in mil. dept.; asst. commr., Burma, May, 1906; on mil. duty, Dec., 1914, to Sept., 1917; dep. commr., Jan., 1922; retd. June., 1933.

LORIMER, ALEXANDER PATRICK GORDON, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 9th Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1906; D.M.O., Anantapur, Dec., 1923; offg. D.M.O. and supt., mental hosp., Calicut, Nov., 1924; surg., 1st dist., June, 1929; retd., Aug., 1935.

LORT-WILLIAMS, SIR JOHN ROLLESTON, Kr., Judicial dept., Bengal (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 14th Sept., 1881).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and London Univ.; called to Bar, 1904; member of L.C.C. from 1907 to 1910; M.P. for Rotherhithe, 1918 to 1923; judge, high ct., Calcutta, 28th Dec., 1927; knighted, June, 1936.

LORY, FREDERICK BURTON PENDARVES, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educatl. Service (b. 23rd Mar., 1875).—Educ. at Marlborough Sch. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 27th Sept., 1901, as princpl., Elphinstone High Sch.; tutor and guardian to young Chiefs of Jamkhandi, Jath, Miraj (Junior), and Mudhol, May, 1902; headmaster, Poona High Sch., April, 1903; asst. to dir. of public instr., Oct., 1903; educatl. inspr., April, 1906; on mily. duty from July, 1916; dir. of public instrn., Bombay, July, 1923; retd., May, 1930; C.I.E., May, 1930.

LOTHIAN, ARTHUR CUNNINGHAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 27th June, 1887).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen University, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. mag. and asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1915; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1917; temply. offg. as asst. to pol. agent, Bunkelkhand, from Nov., 1915; first asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Dec., 1920; sec. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Apl., 1924, and again, Nov., 1927; dep. sec. govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., July, 1926; secty. to resdt., Mysore, May, 1928; resdt., Jaipur, Apl., 1929; on spl. duty, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1931; resdt., Baroda, July, 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states, Mar., 1933; resdt., Jaipur, June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Rajputana, Apl., 1934; addl. secty. to the govt. of India, f. and p. dept., Apl., 1935; employed as spl. representative of the Viceroy, Oct., 1936; addtl. secty. to G. of I., f. and p. dept., Feb., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; resdt. for Rajputana, and C.C. in Ajmer Merwara, Oct., 1937.

LOTHIAN, MARQUIS OF (cr. 1701), Most Hon. Philip Henry Kerr, C.H.—Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for India, Nov., 1931, to Sept., 1932.

LOVATT, ARTHUR WILLIAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 1st Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Dunfermline High Sch., St. Andrew's Univ., Vienna Univ., and London School of Economics; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

LOVELAND, FRANK, Indian State Ryis. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 14th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 23rd July, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Ryis., Aug., 1932.

LOVELLE, JAMES HENRY, F.C.H., late Railway Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1895, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1906; sec., Ganges bridge

commn., 1907; deputy asst. ut. N.W. Rly., 1914
17; attd. for. and pol. dept. for Gondal Por-
bander advisory tribunal, July and Aug., 1917;
retd., Jan., 1921; asst. govt. dir. of Indian rly.
cos., Jan., 1921 to Dec., 1924.

LOVELUCK, EDWARD BURTON, late Indian
Police Service (Madras) (b. 6th May, 1882).—
Joined the Madras police as prob., 30th Nov.,
1902; asst. supt., July, 1904; supt., May, 1911;
retd., Oct., 1930.

LOVETT, Sir HARRINGTON VERNEY,
K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Sher-
borne, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd.
after exam. of 1882; arrived, 20th Nov.,
1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh
as asst. commr.; joint mag., Dec., 1893; dep.
commr., Feb., 1896; mag. and collr., Dec.,
1896; K.-i.-H. Medal, Nov., 1901; addl.
commr., Dec., 1906, to Feb., 1907; commr.,
Nov., 1908; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; member of the
Bengal District Administration Cttee., Nov.,
1913, to May, 1914, and part author of the
Cttee.'s report; K.C.S.I., Jan. 1916; an addl.
member, govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Feb., 1917;
member, bd. of rev., United Provs., April, 1918;
retd., Oct., 1919.

LOW, ALASTAIR PETRIE, Indian C.S. (b. 30th
Jan., 1908).—Educ. at George Watson's College,
Edinburgh University and Balliol College,
Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930;
arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the
Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt.
pol. and home depts., Punjab, Mar., 1934;
under-secty. to A.G.G., states of W.I., Nov.,
1935; secty. to A.G.G., Gujerat states, May,
1936; asst. commr., Charsadda, Apl., 1938;
serving in the N.W.F.P., Nov., 1939.

LOW, Sir CHARLES ERNEST, K.C.I.E., B.A.,
late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Rugby, and Wadham
Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892;
arrived, 13th Dec., 1893, and served in the
Central Provs. as asst. commr., and offg. under
sec.; settlt. offr., Oct., 1898; K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd
class, Jan., 1901; dep. commr., Oct., 1902;
regr. of co-operative credit societies, Jan., 1905,
to Dec., 1905; also dir. of agric., July to Dec.,
1905; dir. of agric. and regr., co-operative credit
societies, Aug., 1907; on special duty, Nov.,
1907; C.I.E., Jan., 1909; dir. of agric., Oct.,
1910; dir. of agric. and industries, Oct., 1913;
member, district administration commee.,
Calcutta, Nov., 1913, to May, 1914; dir. of agric.
and industries, Central Provs., May, 1914; on
spl. duty in comm. and industry dept., govt. of
India, Jan., 1915; sec. to govt. of India, comm.
and industry dept., and addl. member, govr.-
genl.'s council, Feb., 1915; member, Indian
industrial commn., Nov., 1916, to May, 1917,
and from Nov., 1917; member, Indian munitions
bd., May, 1919; K.C.I.E., June, 1919; offg.
president, Indian munitions bd., June, 1919,
chief contr. surplus stores, Oct., 1920; commr.,
Dec., 1920; retd. Aug., 1922.

LOW, PETER DUNSTAN, O.B.E., A.M.I.E.E.,
Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Oct., 1891).—Joined
the service as dist. elec. engr., E.I. Rly., 7th Apl.,
1927.

LOW, THOMAS RALPH, M.C., B.Sc., Indian
Agric. Service (dir., inst. of plant industry,
Indore) (b. 21st Dec., 1893).—Joined the service
as dep. dir. of agric., 4th Dec., 1920; principl.
agric. coll., Cawnpore, May, 1934; offg. dir. of

agric., United Provs., Oct., 1935; dir., inst. of
plant industry, Indore, July, 1936.

LOWE, Miss IRENE H., M.Sc., Indian Educl.
Service (dep. directress of public instrn., Madras)
(b. 4th May, 1890).—Apptd. inspectress of
schools, 29th Mar., 1922; dep. directress of
public instrn., Aug., 1926.

LOWIS, Ross HENRY DONALD, M.B.E.,
Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1905).—Joined the
service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1924; asst. pol.
agent, Sth. Waziristan, Feb., 1936; under
trng., Punjab, Dec., 1937; M.B.E., Jan., 1938;
serving in the N.W.F.P., Nov., 1939.

LOWNDES, Sir GEORGE RIVERS, K.C.S.I.,
K.C., Barr.—Member of the govr.-genl.'s
council, 20th Dec., 1915, to Dec., 1920; was
formerly a leader at the Bombay Bar, and in
practice before the judl. cttee. of the Privy
Council; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1918; apptd. member
of judl. cttee. of Privy Council, March, 1929.

LOWNDS, EDWIN ALBERT, late India
Office (b. 24th Apl., 1878).—Clerk, 2nd class
26th June, 1899; higher clerical officer, April
1922; retd. April, 1939.

LOWSLEY, CONRAD OFFA, late Indian
Service of Engrs. (b. 27th Feb., 1882).—
Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr.,
1st Oct., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; offg.
supt. engr., April, 1925; supt. engr.,
(provl. perm.), Mar., 1931; confd., July, 1931;
offg. ch. engr., April, 1931; retd., Nov., 1934.

LOWTHER, ERNEST HERBERT NEWTON,
Indian State Rlys. (supt., E.I. Rly.). (b. 19th
May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt.,
10th June, 1911; dist. supt. (s.p.t.), July,
1923; supt. (s.p.t.) Feb., 1925; confd., July,
1925; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., July, 1932;
offg. dep. agent, personnel, E.I. rly., Mar., 1936.

LUBY, THOMAS, M.A., V.D., Indian C.S.
(dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 12th Nov.,
1882).—Educ. at Clifton, and Hertford Coll.,
Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived
28th Nov., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst.
mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa,
April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Sept.,
1913; dist. and sessions judge, Aug., 1921;
offg. judge, high ct., Patna, July, 1934; and
again, July, 1935.

LUCAS, E. S. S., Captain, I.M.S. (civ. surg.)
(b. 1st Nov., 1897).—Date of first commn.,
20th Aug., 1928; civil surgeon, United Provs.,
Nov., 1934.

LUCAS, HENRY CHARLES EDWIN, Indian
Police (b. 27th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service
as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 25th Nov., 1925;
offg. dist. supt., June, 1934.

LUCAS, LIONEL JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt.
mag. and dep. coll., Orissa) (b. 5th Feb., 1905).
—Educ. at St. Xavier's Coll., Calcutta, and
St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service,
8th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928,
and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.,
jt. mag., Dec., 1928; offg. dist. and sess. judge;
May, 1935; dep. secty., education, etc., dept.,
Bihar, April, 1939.

LUCE, GORDON HANNINGTON, M.A. (Cantab.),
Indian Educl. Service (prof., Univ. of Rangoon)
(b. 20th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as
Prof., Rangoon Coll., 1st Nov., 1912; on mil.
service from 24th July, 1918, to 9th Feb., 1919;
lecturer in English, May, 1922; lecturer in
Burmese and Far Eastern history, Sept., 1929.

LUCK, CHARLES PYNE, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 22nd Nov., 1915; I.A.R.O. from Aug., 1918, to Aug., 1919; offg. supt., March, 1920; pers. asst. to insprg. of railway police and police asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Oct., 1921; offg. supt., Nov., 1923; confd., Nov., 1926; supt., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1928, to Oct., 1932.

LUCKMAN, Rev. SYDNEY, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estant. (United Provs.) (senr. chaplain) (b. 3rd Nov., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 1st May, 1921; junr. chaplain, May, 1923; senr. chaplain, May, 1933.

LUFFMAN, MAURICE LIEWELYN ALBERT, late Indian Police Service (Assam) (b. 1881).—Joined the service, 22nd Jan., 1902, as asst. supt.; supt., Oct., 1908; offg. inspr.-gen., Assam, April, 1926; ret'd., July, 1931.

LUMBY, ARTHUR FREDERICK RAWSON, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col. I.A., ret'd. (b. 13th Aug., 1890).—First commn., 5th Sept., 1911; in milty. employ until 17th Mar., 1928, when he was apptd. offg. asst. secy. to the Govt. of India, Army Dept.; confd., June, 1928; offg. dep. ditto, July, 1929; confd., Mar., 1934; member, council of state, April, 1935; ret'd. Mar., 1937; pers. asst. to secty., milty. dept., India Office, May, 1936, to Oct., 1938.

LUMBY, ESMOND WALTER RAWSON, M.A., Indian Office (princl. econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 27th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; Gladstone Student at St. Deniol's Library, Harwarden, 1931-32; asst. princpl., Customs and Excise Dept., 23rd Oct., 1933, to 14th Oct., 1934; assistant principal, India Office, 15th Oct., 1934; pte. secty. to parly. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937; acting princpl., Oct., 1938; confd., May, 1939.

LUMLEY, Sir (LAWRENCE) ROGER, G.C.I.E., J.P., D.L., (b. 27th July, 1896) (Governor of Bombay):—Appointed Governor of Bombay, Sept., 1937.

LUNHAM, JOHN LUMSDEN, M.B., B.Ch. (R.U.I.), D.T.M. (Cantab.), F.R.C.S.I., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 10th Mar., 1879).—Joined 1st Sept., 1902, and served in mil. dept. prior to 5th Jan., 1910; civil surg., June, 1913; on milty. duty from 27th Sept., 1914, to 9th Nov., 1921; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

LWIN, MAUNG SAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 17th Dec., 1911).—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service 18th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

LYALL, JOHN HENRY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 16th Aug., 1885).—Joined the service, 16th Dec., 1910; asst. consvr., Dec., 1910; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; offg. consvr., June, 1929; ditto, United Provs., May, 1930; ditto, Bengal, Mar., 1931; ditto, Punjab, Oct., 1933.

LYDALL, EDWARD FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (b. 4th Aug., 1907).—Educ. at Marlborough and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. and pers. asst. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, Nov., 1935; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, May, 1936; offcr. on spec. duty, Kalat agency, Aug., 1936; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Mar., 1938; asst. commr. Peshawar, Feb. 1938; sec. Kabul Legation, Nov., 1939.

LYDE, LIONEL GILDEA, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 13th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Rugby, Wadham Coll., Oxford, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived, 30th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Mar., 1932.*

LYLE, ARTHUR NEVIN (late R.E.), O.B.E., B.A., B.A.I., A.M.I.C.E. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; confd., Oct., 1925.

LYLE, THOMAS McELDERRY, C.I.E., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr. and jt. secty. to govt. (irrgn. bch), United Provs.) (b. 24th May, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Nov., 1917; C.I.E., Dec., 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933; ch. engr. and jt. secty. to govt., U.P. irrgn. bch. (tempy.), Aug., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937.

LYNE, HOWARD WILLIAM, M.C., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 8th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch., and Merton Coll., Oxford; on mil. duty from Sept., 1914; joined the service, 3rd Feb., 1920; arrived, 8th Feb., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. mag., Feb., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1925, and again, Feb., 1927; addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1926; ret'd., June, 1931.

LYNN, GREGORY, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 13th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service, Nov., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1922; offg. exec. engr., June, 1928; confd., Mar., 1938.

LYSTER, ANTHONY ST. GEORGE, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 13th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Oct., 1911; on mil. duty from 12th March, 1915, to 10th March, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1919; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1933; on depn. to Bihar, Apl., 1936.

LYTTON, EARL OF, Rt. Hon. VICTOR ALEXANDER GEORGE ROBERT LYTTON, K.G., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., P.C.—Parly. under-sec. of state for India, 1st Oct., 1920; govr. of Bengal from Mar., 1922, to Mar., 1927; viceroy and actg. govr.-gen. of India, 10th Apl. to 5th Aug., 1925.

MACALPINE, ROBERT IAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th March, 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

MACANN, ARTHUR ERNEST HENRY, Major I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 19th Feb., 1898).—First commn. dated 18th June, 1917; in milty. employ prior to 24th Oct., 1924; asst. magte. and collr., Moradabad, Oct., 1924; vice-consul, Duzdap, March, 1926; sec. British Legation, Kabul, Mar., 1930; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transf. depts., Apl., 1934; dir. of agric. and attd. depts. (in addn.), Oct., 1935; H.M.'s charge d'affaires, Kabul, July, 1937; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Oct., 1936; and again Nov., 1937.

MACARTHUR, DONALD FREDERICK SWAN, Indian service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 7th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service 1st May, 1913; on mil. service from 17th Oct., 1918, to 28th Feb., 1920; asst. engr., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., June, 1921; offg. exec. engr., June, 1921; confd., June, 1928.

MACAULAY, DONALD, Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt.) (b. 4th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 11th May, 1920, as asst. loco. supt.; offg. works manager, E.B. rly., Dec., 1930; dist. loco. supt., E.B. rly. (prov.), Aug., 1934.

MACBRIDE, J. B., O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E., Bengal Factories Service (b. 9th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1923; inspr. of factories Sept., 1924; chief inspr. factories, Bengal, Sept., 1931; O.B.E., June, 1939.

MCBRIEN, CHARLES HUGH, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 28th Apr., 1881).—Joined the service, 25th Mar., 1903; asst. opium agent, Oct., 1912; dist. opium officer, Feb., 1923; ret'd., Apr., 1936.

MCALL, ANTHONY GILCHRIST, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 7th Jan., 1895).—Educ. at Cheltenham College, and R.M.A., Woolwich; joined the service, 5th Aug., 1921; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1930; dep. commr., Oct., 1935.

MCALLUM, DUGALD LESLIE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 13th July, 1910).—Educated at Oundle, Edinburgh University, Lincoln College, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; appointed after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Dec., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

MCALLUM, JAMES LESLIE, late Indian C.S. (b. 14th May, 1879).—Educ. at Carlisle Gr. Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 11th Dec., 1903, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. offr.; dep. commr., April, 1916; offg. commr., May, 1925; confd., May, 1929; finl. commr. (transfd. subjects), May, 1931; ret'd., July, 1933.

MCCARTHY, DENIS ARTHUR MACKAURA, Indian Police (b. 11th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 5th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., April, 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; comdt. and dist. supt., Port Blair, Dec., 1936.

MCCAUL, Rev. MATTHEW WILSON, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 19th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1914; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1917; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

MCCELLAND, ALEXANDER ALLEN, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Mar., 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

MCCLENNAGHAN, HERBERT ERIC ST. GEORGE, Indian C.S. (collr. salt rev., Bombay) (b. 5th Aug., 1896).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 14th Nov., 1922; arrived, 8th Jan., 1923, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., April, 1926; Mar., 1931, and again, April, 1933; confd., Mar., 1934; collr. salt rev., Bombay, April, 1939.

MACCOLL, ALEXANDER MALCOLM, Indian Police (supt., river divn.) (b. 28th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 26th Jan., 1920; dist. supt., Nov., 1924; supt., river divn., Nov., 1936.

MACCOLL, HUGH HERBERT, M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 7th Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 28th Nov., 1921; offg. dep. consvr., June, 1923; confd., April, 1927.

MCCONAGHY, CHRISTOPHER BIRDWOOD, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 25th Mar., 1877).—First commn., 27th June, 1901; agency surgeon, Baghdad, June, 1904; ditto, Bushire, and residency surgeon, Persian Gulf, Nov., 1909; agency surgeon, Bhopal, Sept., 1914; on mil. duty, May, 1916, to May, 1918; agency surgeon, Eastern States of Rajputana, June, 1918; agency surgeon, Bhopal, Jan., 1919; legation surgeon, Nepal, March, 1926; ret'd., May, 1930.

MCCORKELL, DAVID ROBERT ANDREW, Indian Police (b. 22nd Apr., 1916).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1936.

MCCRACKEN, ANDREW, M.A., Indian C.S. (secty., home dept., Burma) (b. 21st Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Ayr Academy and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1919; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept.; asst. rice commr., Rangoon, Feb., 1920; under sec. to govt., May, 1921; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, reforms office, June, 1930 to Jan., 1932; secty., home dept., govt. of Burma, Feb., 1938.

MCCREA, KENNETH IAN, Indian Police (b. 15th Dec., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 16th Nov., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

MCCULLOCH, RUPERT LEAROYD, late Indian Police Service (b. 4th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1900; supt. of police, July, 1908; recd. King's police medal, Jan., 1920; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Oct., 1920; actg. commr. of police, April, 1921; actg. inspr.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1923; ret'd., Apr., 1932.

MACDERMOT, PATRICK JOSEPH, India Office (higher clerical officer, telegraph and mails branch) (b. 2nd Feb., 1885).—Apptd. clerical officer, 23rd Aug., 1921; higher clerical officer, Oct., 1931.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER, M.A. (Glas.), B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 13th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service as prof., Dacca Coll., 27th Jan., 1911; prin., B.E. Coll., Sibpur, Mar., 1924; asst. dir. of public instrn., Dec., 1926; prinpl. Bengal engrg. coll., Nov., 1928; ret'd., Nov., 1938.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER MACLEOD, Indian Police (b. 10th Oct.—Apptd., 1915). asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 11th Nov., 1935.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER RANALD HUME, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 3rd Feb., 1909).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under secty. to govt., Assam, Mar., 1934; confd., Aug., 1934; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1934; ditto, S.

Waziristan, Nov., 1935; presdt., Manipur State Darbar, Feb., 1937.

MACDONALD, Rev. ALISDAIR JOHN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 12th June, 1900).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Church of Scotland, Oct., 1933; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1935; chaplain, atttd. to 2nd Btn. Argyll and Sutherland Hrs., Secunderabrad.

MACDONALD, ANGUS ALEXANDER, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Nov., 1904).—Educ. at University of Edinburgh, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928, and arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1932.

MACDONALD, Rev. DONALD, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 22nd Sept., 1891).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, 27th Feb., 1927; apptd. senr. chaplain, Feb., 1937.

McDONALD, DONALD CHARLES, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Prov.) (b. 28th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as proby. extra asst. consvr., 7th May, 1912; confd., Oct., 1916; in milty. employ from 29th Mar., 1918, to 20th Feb., 1919; apptd. to I.F.S., 25th June, 1929; dep. consvr., June, 1929; employed under Rewa Durbar, Feb., 1933.

MACDONALD, HECTOR IAN, O.B.E., Military Accounts Dept. (dep. secty. (revision) Govt. of India, defence dept.) (b. 10th Aug., 1885).—Joined service Jan., 1905; asst. financial adviser, military finance, Jan., 1921; confd. Mar., 1921; officer in class II, office of C.M.A., Lahore Dist., April, 1926; dep. financial adviser, military finance, Nov., 1929; officer on special duty, Army Dept., Nov., 1929; dir. regulations and forms, April, 1930; officer on special duty, finance dept., Jan., 1932; O.B.E., Jan., 1933; dep. secty. (revision) Govt. of India, defence dept., Aug., 1937.

MACDONALD, IAN HAY, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr. Orissa) (b. 19th July, 1915).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 16th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938 and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.; transferred to Orissa, May, 1939.

MACDONALD, RODERICK WILLIAM, C.I.E., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., late Indian Police Service (b. 22nd Apl., 1881).—Served in mil. dept. from 28th July, 1900; asst. commdt., Burma mil. police, Dec., 1904; asst. supt., Feb., 1908; dept. supt., Dec., 1910; on mil. service, Dec., 1914, to June, 1919; dep. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1921; offg. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1923; confd. as inspr.-gen., Oct., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1927; retd., Aug., 1932.

McDONALD, THOMAS, B.A. (Cantab).—Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., central prov.) (b. 16th Jan., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., central provs., 11th Oct., 1929; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934.

MACDONELL, OLIVER STEPHEN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 2nd May, 1878).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 18th Jan., 1906; dist. engr., Sept., 1917; exec. engr., Lashio-Muse survey, Oct., 1927; retd., Apl., 1935.

McDOUGALL, JAMES CURRIE, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agricl. Service (dir. agric., Central Prov.) (b. 5th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service

as asst. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1925; offg. prinpl., agric. coll., Nagpur, Nov., 1928; confd., Jan., 1934; dir. of agricl. (prov.) C.P., Oct., 1935.

McDOUGALL, RAIBEART MacINTYRE, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 30th April, 1892).—Educ. at Greenock Academy and Glasgow University; apptd. after exam. of 1914, and posted to Burma; on mil. service, from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919; under sec. to govt., Sept., 1920; regr., high ct., Rangoon (offg.), March, 1923; confd., Nov., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1926; confd., June, 1930; secty., local govt. dept., Mar., 1931; secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma, Mar., 1934; and again, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1935.

McDOWALL, ROGER GORDON, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to governor, Burma) (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at Dumfries Academy, Glasgow, and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1911, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. asst. sec. to govt., Sept., 1916; regr., chief court, Lower Burma, July, 1920; regr., high ct., Rangoon, Dec., 1922; offg. dep. commr., from Nov., 1923; dep. commr. (prov. sub.), July, 1927; offg. secty., fin. dept., Mar., 1929; confd., Sept., 1929; secty., reforms office, Nov., 1932; secty. to govr., Burma, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1935.

McDOWELL, ARNOLD EDWARD JOSEPH CONNELL, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Police (b. 13th March, 1883).—Joined the service 8th May, 1901; in mil. employ until 21st March, 1908, when apptd. asst. supt. of police; supt., Nov., 1909; on mil. service, 1914-19; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1923; confd., July, 1925; offg. inspr.-gen., Bihar, Apl., 1933; confd., June, 1934; C.I.E., May, 1937; retd., Mar., 1938.

McDOWELL, KENNETH MACLEAN, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 16th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

McDOWELL, LENNOX McLEOD, late Indian Police Service (b. 20th Aug., 1876).—Joined the service, 14th May, 1899; asst. dist. supt., Jan., 1901; asst. supt., April, 1906; supt., Jan., 1911; retd., Aug., 1931.

MacEDWARD, Rev. LACHLAN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland) (b. 25th Apl., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 15th Feb., 1926; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1928; senr. ditto, Feb., 1938; chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Bangalore.

McELHINNY, GEOFFREY WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Sind) (b. 16th Dec., 1900).—Educ. at Rugby and Corpus Christi, Oxford; joined the service 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 5th Dec., 1924, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1928; under secty. to govt., home, etc., depts., Sept., 1929; member, legisl. council, Feb., 1932; offg. collr., Sept., 1933; temp. addl. dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1934; collr., Sind, Nov., 1937.

McELLEGOTT, FRANCIS, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 17th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 7th Feb., 1920; dist. supt., Oct., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930.

MACEWEN, ALEXANDER ROBERT, C.I.E., M.C., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 8th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1919; arrived 3rd Jan., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1922; offg. under-sec., rev. dept., June, 1924; spl. asst. settlt. offir., Aug., 1924; spl. settlt. offir., July, 1925; secty. to bd. of rev., Oct., 1930; collr. (prov. subs.), Jan., 1936; confd., Mar., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938; secty. to govt., home dept., Madras, Dec., 1938.

MACEWEN, ROBERT BEGG, late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd July, 1898).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1924; under-secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, Oct., 1929; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Sept., 1932; and again, July, 1933; ret'd., Apr., 1937.

MACFARLANE, DONALD, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Aug., 1882).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Punjab, Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; ditto, and under-sec. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1916; on rly. duty, Mesopotamia, April, 1917, to Aug., 1918; services placed at disposal of Govt. of Sudan from Nov., 1923; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1927; confd., Mar., 1930; secty. to govt., P.W.D. (bldgs. and roads bch.), and offg. ch. engr., July, 1932; confd., Apr., 1933; C.I.E., May, 1937; ret'd.

MACFARQUHAR, ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Royal Academy, Inverness, Univ. of Aberdeen, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (subs. provl.) Feb., 1933; confd., Sept., 1936.

MCGILLIGAN, MICHAEL, late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Sept., 1887).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Coll., Univ. Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 7th Dec., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. censor, Madras, Oct., 1914; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; asst. collr., Nov., 1919; sub-collr., Nov., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1923; confd., March, 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

MCGOWAN, PERCIVAL BRYAN, late Indian State Railways (b. 17th Mar., 1880).—Joined the service, Feb., 1903; dist. traffic supt., April, 1914; ret'd., May, 1933.

MACGREGOR, ANDREW DAWSON, F.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (prin., veterinary coll., Bengal) (b. 5th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1914; I.A.R.O. from 19th Feb., 1916; offg. supt., civil vety. dept., Jan., 1919; confd. July, 1921; princpl., vety. coll., Bengal, Apr., 1922; also veterinary adviser to Govt. of Bengal (in addition) from Mar., 1938.

MACGREGOR, ROBERT ANDERSON, M.I.Mech.E., M.I.S.I., M.I.E.I., F.R.S.A., M.M.G.I. (metallurg. inspr.) (b. 9th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service as officer on special duty, 25th Feb., 1937; offg. metallurgical inspr., Apr., 1937; confd., June, 1937.

MACGREGOR, ROBERT FORRESTER DOUGLAS, C.I.E., M.C., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (resy. surg., Hyderabad) (b. 5th Jan., 1885).—First

commn. dated 29th Jan., 1910; M.O., Seistan Consulate, Dec., 1920; offg. agency surg., Bhopal, Sept., 1925; offg. resdy. surg., Indore, Sept., 1926; resdy. surg., Bushire, May, 1927; agency surg., Gilgit, Oct., 1927; resdy. surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Sept., 1929; ditto, in C.I., Apr., 1931; resdy. surg., Hyderabad, Mar., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1939.

MACGREGOR, WILLIAM DUNCAN, C.I.E., M.I.E.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 20th Oct., 1878).—Apptd. 27th Sept., 1900, and served as asst. supt., telegraphs; supt., Oct., 1911; ditto, engrg., April, 1914; asst. chief engr., March, 1920; dep. chief engr., March, 1921; offg. postmr.-gen., Central Circle, April, 1922, to Sept., 1923; dep. chief engr., telephones, Oct., 1923; offg. postmr.-gen., Central Circle, Oct., 1924; offg. postmr.-gen., Burma, Feb., 1925; dir., tele. engrg., Feb., 1925; dep. dir. genl., tels. and telephones, Nov., 1927; offg. P.M.G. & dir. tele. engrg., June, 1928; P.M.G., Burma, Sept., 1928; offg. ch. engr., posts and tels., July, 1931; P.M.G., Bengal and Assam Circle, Mar., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

MCGREGOR, WILLIAM MALCOLM, Indian State Rlys. (contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1889).—Joined the service 26th Oct., 1908; dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., Aug., 1919; offg. dep. ditto, Oct., 1928; offg. contr., Mar., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931.

MCGUIGAN, HUGH SUTHERLAND, Indian Police (b. 28th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 23rd Nov., 1936; confd. Sept., 1938.

MCGUIRE, ROBERT ELY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 22nd Aug., 1901).—Educ. at the High School, Dublin, and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dep., Nov., 1930; warden, oil fields, Sept., 1932; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1932; provl., Jan., 1937.

MCINERNY, AUGUSTINE, late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 29th Aug., 1875).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 28th March, 1918; resdt. engr., April, 1920; dist. engr., Oct., 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

MCINERNY, EDWARD FOLLET, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 5th May, 1910).—Educ. at Swansea Grammar School and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Mar., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1938.

MCINTOSH, ERIC PRIDEAUX, M.C., late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 15th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1911; supt., Dec., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; ret'd., Apr., 1930.

MCINTOSH, ROBERT, M.B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 23rd May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; M.B.E., Feb., 1937.

MACINTYRE, JOHN MACLACHLAN, M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1928; asst. exec. engr., Baluchistan, Aug., 1935; M.B.E., Jan., 1939.

MCINTYRE, THOMAS, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Ry's. (bridge engr. E.B. Rly.) (b. 4th Mar., 1898).—Asst. exec. engr. bridges, Dec., 1926; bridge engr. E.B. Rly., Nov., 1938.

MCIVER, ARCHIBALD DUNCAN CAMPBELL, late Northern India Salt Revenue Dept. (b. 1st Jan., 1877).—Joined the service as offg. asst. supt., 16th March, 1897; proby. ditto, April, 1897; confid., Dec., 1898; supt., May, 1902; asst. commr., April, 1923; genl. manager, Rajputana salt sources, March, 1925; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

MCIVER, COLIN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 21st Oct., 1884).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1912; in mil. employ to 10th Feb., 1924; civil surg., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

MACIVER, LEWIS JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (secty., local admin. dept., Madras) (b. 9th March, 1895).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 4th Feb., 1920; arrived 11th May, 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1922; under secty. to govt., Madras, local self-govt. dept., Jan., 1923; dep. dir. of commercial intelligee., Calcutta; June, 1923; offg. dir.-genl., ditto, Apl., 1924; Feb., 1926, and Jan., 1928; offg. Ind. trade commr., Aug., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte., July, 1930; commr. of Coorg, Mar., 1931; collr., Bangalore, Apl., 1934; secty., local admin. dept., Aug., 1937.

MACK, EDMUND ELMAR, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 2nd May, 1896).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived, 5th Jan., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1929; and again, Oct., 1932; confid., Jan., 1933.

MACKARNESS, CUTHBERT GEORGE MILFORD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. 15th April, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 7th Dec., 1912; in mil. employ from 23rd Nov., 1915, to 14th March, 1919; dep. consvr., June, 1919; pers. asst. to consvr., Assam., Apl., 1932; offg. consvr., Feb., 1937.

MACKAY, CHARLES GORDON, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 16th April, 1877).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jointmag., May, 1910; regr., high ct., Oct., 1913, to March, 1919; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1922; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1928; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

MACKAY, HENRY FISHER, late India Office (b. 18th Oct., 1875).—Educ. at Robert Gordons Coll., Aberdeen; clerk, 2nd class, 2nd Oct., 1895; joint editor, India Office List, from Nov., 1914, to Nov., 1920; staff clerk, March, 1919; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; suptg. exec. offr., April, 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

MACKAY, THOMAS IAN SUTHERLAND B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 25th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921.

MCKEEVER, RONALD FRASER, Indian Police (b. 20th Aug., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Central Provs., on probn., 14th Nov., 1935.

MACKEITH, ALEXANDER ADAM, Indian Police (b. 19th Mar., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Dec., 1937.

MCKELVIE, GEORGE MAURICE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr. Central Provs.) (b. 21st Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 8th Dec., 1920; offg. exec. engr. and under-secty. to govt., July 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1928; under-secty., p.w.d., Apl., 1937; on spec. duty with govt. of India, Nov., 1937; exec. engr., cent. p.w.d., Apl., 1938.

MCKELVIE, JOHN NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Malvern and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confid., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

MCKELVIE, Rev. ROBERT FRITZ STANLEY, M.A., D.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 14th Jan., 1882).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 2nd Nov., 1915; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1916; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1926; ret'd., Jan., 1935.
