MCKENNA, CORNELIUS HUGH, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 17th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1928.

MACKENNA, Sir JAMES, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 15th Aug., 1872).—Educ. at Dumfries Acad., Edinburgh Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and settlt. officer; dir. of land records and agric., May, 1904; dep. dir., Jan., 1905; dir. of agric., Oct., 1906; ditto, and jt. regr., co-op. credit soc., Nov., 1909; offg. agric. adviser to govt. of India, and dir., agric. research inst., Pusa, March, 1913, to March, 1914; on special duty in dept. of rev. and agric., govt. of India, Jan., 1915; agricl. adviser to govt. of India, and dir., agricl. research inst., Pusa, April, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1917; commr., Burma, May, 1920; member of Burma legisl. council, June, 1920; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Burma, April, 1925; Kt., June, 1925; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., from 24th Sept., 1926; member of Royal Commn. on Agric. in India, 1926-8; ret'd., May, 1930.

MACKENZIE, Rev. DONALD FRANCIS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (presdy. senr. chaplain, Church of Scotland, Bengal) (b. 31st Mar., 1885).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 30th Sept., 1919; junr. chaplain, Church of Scotland, Sept., 1921; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1927; presdy. senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Bengal, Aug., 1937.

MACKENZIE, Rev. DONALD STEWART, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senior chaplain) (b. 2nd Mar., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 24th April, 1919; junr. chaplain, April, 1921; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1930.

MACKENZIE, Sir DUNCAN GEORGE, K.C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 1st July, 1883).—Educ. at Winchester, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 29th Nov., 1907, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, June, 1912; asst. commr., Ajmer, March, 1913; city mag., Peshawar, Nov., 1914; asst. commr., N.W.F. Prov., Dec.,

1915; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, Nov., 1918; first asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1919; sec. to the agent to the govr.-gen., Punjab States, Nov., 1921; pol. agent, Southern Rajputana States, Nov., 1926; on foreign service in Bharatpur State, Feb., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1930; pol. agent in Bhopal, Nov., 1930; resdt., Jaipur, Apl., 1931; ditto and in Western States of Rajputana, Jan., 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states, Mar., 1933; resdt., Hyderabad, July, 1933; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

MACKENZIE, HARRY MALCOLM, C.I.E., M.B., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 14th May, 1875).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1900; in mil. emp. to July, 1906; health offr., Simla, April, 1907; prof., medl. coll. hosp., Lahore, Nov., 1908; health offr. and dist. plague medl. offr., March, 1909; medl. coll. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1910; ditto, Lahore, Sept., 1911; medl. offr., Bahawalpur States, Jan., 1912; dep. sanitary commr., Punjab, Sept., 1914; serving in the mil. dept., from 1915; prof. of pathology and hygiene, King Edward Medl. Coll., Lahore, and dep. sany. commr., Punjab (in addn.), Aug., 1918; bacteriologist to govt. of Punjab, Oct., 1922; offg. inspr. genl. of civil hosp., Sept., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1932; ret'd., May, 1932.

MACKENZIE, JAMES MACAULAY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 20th June, 1887).—Joined the service, 28th Dec., 1907; asst. supt., April, 1910; supt., Nov., 1915; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1929.

MACKENZIE, JOHN MITCHELL DOUGLAS, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 8th June, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th Jan. 1912; dep. consvr., Jan., 1917; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

MACKENZIE, MALCOLM AYERS, M.B.E., late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 27th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Nov., 1910; supt., May, 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

MACKENZIE, STEPHEN NAIRNE, late Indian C.S. (b. 17th May, 1880).—Educ. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 11th Dec., 1903; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; settld. offr., Dec., 1910; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1910; ditto and settld. offr., Dec., 1910; asst. commr. and settld. offr., Assam, April, 1912; offg. pol. offr., N.E. Frontier, Jan. to Oct., 1914; dep. commr., March, 1916; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1918, to Jan., 1919; commr. of excise, inspr.-gen. of registrn., &c., Jan., 1919; supt., Lushai Hills, April, 1921; offg. commr., March, 1925; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

MACKEOWN, JOHN AINSLIE, Indian C.S. (addl. dep. secty., Govt. of India, legislative dept.) (b. 27th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Radley and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1925; arrived, 6th Dec., 1925, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; deputed to Bahawalpur State, Nov., 1929; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1932; dep. secty., Govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Feb., 1936; offg. jt. secty., ditto, July, 1936, and again Oct., 1936; addl. dep. secty., legisl. dept., govt. of India, Mar., 1937; dep. secty., dept. of indus. and lab. Apl. to July, 1937; addl.

dep. secty., legislative dept. Aug. to Oct., 1937; dep. secty. communications dept., Nov. to Dec., 1937; addl. dep. secty. legislative dept. Jan., to March, 1938; dep. secty. communications dept. May to July, 1938; addl. dep. secty. legislative dept., July, 1938.

MCKERNAN, ROBERT LUCIAN, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Jan., 1891).—Educ. at St. Colomb's Coll., Derry, and University Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 12th Dec., 1914, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty as asst. recruiting offr., from Feb., 1918, to Jan., 1919; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1919; dep. acctnt.-gen., Calcutta, Nov., 1921; ditto, Bombay, Sept., 1922; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Sept., 1923; dep. acctnt.-gen., Calcutta, March, 1925; offg. acctnt.-gen., June, 1925; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1925; offg. acctnt.-gen. and dep. controller of currency, Madras, April, 1926; dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma, Nov., 1926; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1929; offg. acct.-genl., Bombay, March, 1928; on spl. duty, Nov., 1931; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

MCKERRAL, ANDREW, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agr. Service (b. 3rd Sept., 1876).—Arrived in India, May, 1907, and served as dep. dir. of agric.; asst. inspr.-gen. of agric. in India, June, 1910; dep. dir. of agric., Burma, June, 1911; offg. dir. of agric., Burma, May, 1922; confd., Nov., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1931; ret'd., March, 1936.

MACKERTICH, MERVYN, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 15th Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1899; dist. opium officer, April, 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

MACKIE, ALFRED WILLIAM WHITE, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 20th Oct., 1877).—Educ. at Dundee High Sch., St. Andrew's Univ., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 29th Nov., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of land records and registrn., Nov., 1905, to Jan., 1910; collr., Nov., 1920; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Feb., 1921; collr. and pol. agent, Bhor, Jan., 1923; on spl. duty in rev. dept., Feb., 1925; offg. settld. commr., etc., April, 1925; offg. sec. to govt., dev. dept., dep. dir. of dev., and commr., Bom. suburban div., and land manager, dev. directorate, April, 1926; pol. agent, Surat, Nov., 1926; ditto, Rewa Kantha, March, 1928; member of legisl. council, 1929, 1931 and 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; commr., Apl., 1931; ret'd., June, 1936.

MACKIE, FREDERICK PERCIVAL, C.S.I., O.B.E., M.D., Ch.B., M.Sc. (Bristol), F.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), Bt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 19th Feb., 1875).—Joined 1st Sept., 1902, and served in mil. dept. to 24th March, 1923; O.B.E., June, 1918; offg. dir., bacteriological laby. and consulting bacteriologist, G.M. Coll., Bombay, March, 1923; confd., Sept., 1924; dir., Haffkine Inst., Bombay, Nov., 1926; offg. public health commr. with govt. of India, March, 1928, to Jan., 1929; offg. dir., Pasteur Inst., Shillong, May, 1931; ret'd., Feb., 1932; C.S.I., June, 1932.

MACKIE, JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 1st Jan., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 10th Feb., 1928; exec. engr., Feb., 1937.

MACKIE, RONALD HASTINGS, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 3rd Dec., 1881).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 25th Apl., 1907; dist. loco. supt., Nov., 1921; offg. dep. carr. & wagon supt., Nov., 1928; confd., Feb., 1930; offg. loco. and carr. supt., Nov., 1932; and again, Mar., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

MCKIE, PERCY WILLIAM, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 4th May 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st July, 1896; proby. supt., Dec., 1919; confd., May, 1920; dep. P.M.G., traffic, June, 1930; ch. supt., Bombay tel. office, July, 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

MACKINNON, JOHN, C.I.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 30th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; on mil. duty from 4th May, 1916 to 17th May, 1919; exec. engr., October, 1917; offg. dep. agent, N.W. Rly., May, 1926; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1929, and again, Oct., 1933; offg. dep. agent, Mar., 1931, and again, Oct., 1933; confd., Feb., 1934; offg. ch. engr., N.W. rly., May, 1935; dep. ch. engr. (provl.), June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., May, 1936; chief engr. (provl.), Nov., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1939.

MACKINTOSH, CHARLES CALDER, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., p.w.d., Burma) (b. 28th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. engr., Mar., 1936; addl. ch. engr., P.W.D. (irrign. bch.), Burma, Feb., 1937.

MACKINTOSH, Rev. KENNETH, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 10th Jan., 1903) (junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, ch. of Scotland, 27th April, 1928; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1930; transfed. to defence dept., Nov., 1938.

McKINTY, FRANCIS PATRICK, late Indian Police (b. 1st Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Nov., 1910; supt., Dec., 1923; ret'd., Feb., 1936.

MACKLIN, ALBERT SORTAIN ROMER, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 4th March, 1890).—Educ. at Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 1st Dec., 1913, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; judge and asst. sess. judge, April, 1922; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, June, 1923; offg. judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; regr. high ct., appellate side, Nov., 1926; judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1929; judl. asst. and addl. sess. judge, Aden, June, 1929; offg. secy. to govt., legal dept., June, 1931; judl. commr. in the states of Western India, July, 1932; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1934; confd., June, 1935.

MACKNEY, HERBERT HODDY, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Rangoon) (b. 31st May, 1886).—Educ. at Liverpool Institute High Sch., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr., on mil. service from June to July, 1918; dist. and sessions judge, Dec., 1922; acting judge, high ct., Rangoon, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1933.

MACLACHLAN, DUNCAN, Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 9th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Mountjoy Sch., Dublin, and

Univ. Coll., Galway; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 21st Nov., 1913, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; on mil. duty from Aug., 1916, to Nov., 1919; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindhi translator to govt., April, 1920; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept. (tempy.), May, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1924; offg. collr. & dist. magte., Sept., 1928; confd., Jan., 1931; pol. agent, Bhor, April, 1932; offg. commr., Apl., 1935.

MACLACHLAN, ROBERT BOYD, C.I.E., B.A., B.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th Sept., 1880).—Joined 5th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Aug., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; suptg. engr. (sub. tempy.), variously from Oct., 1923; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

MACLAGAN, Sir EDWARD DOUGLAS, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and offg. under sec.; supt. of census operations in the Punjab, April, 1890; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Aug., 1890; confd., April, 1893; retransfd. to the Punjab, as asst. commr., March, 1895; dep. commr., Oct., 1896; settlement commr., Oct., 1896; offg. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and rev., April to Oct., 1902; offg. judl. and gen. sec., Punjab, Sept. to Oct., 1903; offg. chief sec., April to July, 1904; and again from Jan., 1906; confd., Dec., 1906; offg. sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Dec., 1906, to April, 1907; C.S.I., Jan., 1909; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric., July, 1910; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1913; tempy. member of govr.-gen.'s council, April to Oct., 1913; chief sec. to govt., Punjab, June, 1914; president, cttee. on co-operation in India, Oct., 1914, to June, 1915; sec. to govt. of India, educn. dept., Dec., 1915; also addl. member, govr.-gen.'s legisl. council; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, 26th May, 1919; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1921; govr. of Punjab, 3rd Jan., 1921, to May, 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1924.

MACLEAN, CHARLES ALLAN, M.B.E., M.C. M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Bihar) (b. 22nd May, 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 5th Dec., 1920; offg. dir., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1935.

MACLEAN, EVAN VICTOR, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Apl., 1887).—Joined the service, 6th Nov., 1919; asst. supt., July, 1925; offg. dep. dir., traffic and statistics, Dec., 1930; transptn. supt. (traffic comml.), E.I.Rly., Dec., 1934; ret'd., July, 1938.

MCLEAN, ANGUS, B.Sc., Indian Agricul. Service (dep. dir., Burma) (b. 27th Apl., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir., 16th Dec., 1921.

MCLEAN, ARTHUR ERROL JOHN, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (inspr. of schools) (b. 28th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service as head master, govt. high school, Maymyo, 30th Jan., 1922; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Aug., 1933.

MCLEAN, Rev. LAUGHLAN, M.A., B.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 18th Feb., 1886).—Served in the milty. dept. from 28th Feb., 1921, to 6th Sept., 1925; junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Sept., 1925; served under govt. of

India, army dept., from 15th Mar., 1929, to 5th Dec., 1929; and again from 1st Dec., 1930; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1931; offg. presy. senr. chaplain, Bengal, May, 1932; ditto, Madras, Oct., 1933; ditto, Bombay, confd., Mar., 1935; ret'd. Mar., 1939.

MCLEAN, SIR ROBERT, Kt., B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; offg. sec., rly. board, May, 1919; dep. agent, March, 1920; agent, April, 1922; Kt., 1926; ret'd., July, 1930.

MCLELLAN, REV. DUNCAN TAIT HUTCHISON, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (offg. presdy., senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Madras) b. 29th Nov., 1893).—Appdtd. chaplain on probn., church of Scotland, 11th Feb., 1922; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1923; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1933; offg. presdy. senr. chaplain, Madras, April, 1938.

MACLEOD, ALLAN, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th Mar., 1887).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy, and Merton Coll., Oxford; appdtd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1911, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., Nov., 1916; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1921; dep. commr. (s.p.t.), July, 1922; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Sept., 1922; member of frontier civil forces commee., March, 1926; offg. finl. adviser, milty. finance, July, 1926; addl. ditto (tempy), Aug., 1928; finl. adviser, ditto, Aug., 1929 C.I.E., Jan., 1933; ret'd., July, 1937.

MACLEOD, SIR NORMAN CRANSTOWN, Kt., B.A., Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept.—Official assignee, insolvent debtor's court, Bombay; appdtd. addl. member of govr.-gen.'s legis. council, 21st Oct., 1908; judge, Bombay High Ct., June, 1910; chief justice, ditto, June, 1919; knighted, June, 1919; ret'd., June, 1926.

MACLEOD, RODERICK DONALD WILLIAM DUNNING, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th May, 1886).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; appdtd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag.; jt. mag., s.p.t., Sept., 1916; asst. commr., Dec., 1918, to April, 1919; confd., as jt. mag., Jan., 1923; offg. mag. and collr. from Jan., 1923; confd., July, 1926; dep. secty. to govt., U.P., general branch, Oct., 1928; ret'd., Nov., 1935.

MCLEOD WILLIAM NORMAN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1926; suptg. engr. (provl. perm.), July, 1935; confd. as suptg. ngr., Oct., 1936.

MCINTIC, JOHN FRANCIS, Indian Police (b. 29th Aug., 1916).—Appdtd. asst. suptg., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1936.

MCMAHON, SIR ARTHUR HENRY, G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Col. Indian Army, ret'd., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 10th March, 1883; in mil. emp. to June, 1887, when he was appdtd. asst. commr., Punjab; employed in Zhob from Oct., 1890; dep. commr., Thal, and pol. agent, Loralai, April, 1892; pol. agent in Zhob, April, 1893;

accompanied mission to Kabul, Aug., 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; acting rev. commr., Baluchistan, Feb., 1894; British joint commr., Afghan-Baluch boundary commn., April, 1894, to June, 1895, and from January to June, 1896; C.S.I., June, 1897; pol. agent, Gilgit, Aug., 1897; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1898; pol. agent, Dir. Swat and Chitral, Mar., 1899; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, April, 1901; on depn. with the Seistan Mission from Nov., 1902; agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Jan., 1905; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1906; sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., Feb., 1911; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Feb., 1911; G.C.V.O., Dec., 1911; on special duty as British Plenipotentiary, Tibet Conference, Sept., 1913, to July, 1914; appdtd. H.M.'s High Commr., Egypt, Dec., 1914; ret'd. from Indian Army, March, 1915; G.C.M.G., 1916; author of works on the tribes and fauna of Gilgit, Chitral, &c., and on volcanic and other rocks on the Baluchistan Afghan frontier.

MACMILLAN, ARCHIBALD MORVEN, C.I.B., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th Apl., 1880).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ., Glasgow; appdtd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 21st Nov., 1903, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., mag., chief inspr. of factories, Mar., 1907, to Jan., 1908; manager, Sind encumbered estates, May, 1914, to April, 1917; collr., Feb., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. commr. of excise, Bombay presy., and inspr.-genl. of registrn., Mar., 1932; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

MACMILLAN, JOHN McCALLUM ANDERSON, M.A., M.D., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 14th Dec., 1876).—First commn. 31st Aug., 1900; in mil. employ to Jan., 1907, when he was appdtd. civil surgeon, Central Provs.; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to March, 1921; civil surgeon, Simla East, March, 1925; ret'd. Sept., 1930.

MACMILLAN, JOHN NIVEN, Indian State Rlys. (dist. signal engr., E.I. Rly. (b. 20th Oct., 1901).—Asst. signal engr., Oct., 1936; dist. ditto, Nov., 1935.

MACMILLAN, NEIL BOYD (commercial offer., Electricity Brch., Punjab) (b. 14th Jan., 1880).—Joined the service, 23rd Apl., 1928, as sales manager; asst. comml. manager, Sept., 1931; comml. manager, Apl., 1933; comml. offer., Jan., 1934.

MACNABB, ARCHIBALD CORRIE, M.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; appdtd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; city mag., Delhi, April, 1914; sec., municipalcttee., Delhi, Sept., 1915; again city mag., Delhi, Oct., 1916; spl. duty on staff of lieut.-govr., Punjab, from Oct., 1917, to March, 1918; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1923; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1924; senr. secty. to finl. commrs., Punjab, Nov., 1928; offg. dep. secty. to govt., Punjab, dev. dept., Apl., 1933; offg. commr., May, 1934; admin. Lahore municipality and chairman, Lahore impr. trust, Apl., 1937.

MCNAIR, GEORGE DOUGLAS, Judl. Dept., Bengal (judge, high ct., Calcutta).—Appdtd. acting judge, high ct., Calcutta, 28th April, 1933; confd., Apl., 1934.

MACNAIR, Sir ROBERT HILL, Kt., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Dec., 1877).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 4th Dec., 1900, and serve in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr. and dist. and sess. judge; served in fin. dept., govt. of India, from May, 1908; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1908; ditto, Bombay, Oct., 1909; ditto, Bengal, Nov., 1909; dep. acctnt.-genl., United Prov., May, 1910; asst. commr., Central Provs., Nov., 1910; ditto, and addl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1912; dep. commr., and divl. and sess. judge, Jan., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1917; addl. judl. commr., Jan., 1927; offg. judl. commr., Feb., 1929; confd., Jan., 1932; Kt., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

MENAMARA, FRANCIS STEUART, late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 21st April, 1876).—Joined the service, 19th July, 1901; supt. of police, Nov., 1910; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1923; confd., July, 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

MACNEE, EUSTACE ALBERIC, M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service as prof'r. of English Morris Coll., Nagpur, 25th Oct., 1908; inspr. of schools, Central Provs., Feb., 1912; principal, training coll., Jubbulpore, Mar., 1922; offg. dir. public instrn., Central Provs., Mar., 1928; and again, Apl., 1932; also offg. secty., educu. dept.; confd., July, 1934; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

MCNEIL, JOHN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (judge) (b. 2nd Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ. and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., genl. dept., Rangoon, Apl., 1936, to Apl., 1937; judge, Oct., 1937.

MCNEILL, KENNETH JOHN, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 13th July, 1890).—Joined the service as traffic probationer, 17th Feb., 1911; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1914; dist. traffic supt., June, 1918; divl. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; offg. supt., watch and ward, June, 1930; offg. dep. traffic manager, variously from May, 1933; divl. transpn. supt., Dec., 1934.

MACONACHIE, GORDON WILLIAM, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Jail Dept., Madras (b. 13th Nov., 1881).—First commn., 31st Aug., 1903; in mil. employ. to Jan., 1912, when he was apptd. jail supt.; supt., central jail, Sept., 1914; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to Dec., 1919; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Madras, May, 1921; supt. of prisons, July, 1925; offg. inspr.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1927; offg. inspr.-genl., Madras, Mar., 1930; confd., Apl., 1931; ret'd., July, 1936.

MACONACHIE, Sir RICHARD ROY, K.B.E., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Sept., 1885).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. commr., Peshawar, April, 1914; personal assistant to chief commr., N.-W. Frontier Prov., May, 1914;

asst. commr., Bannu, Feb., 1915; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1916; undersec. to govt. of India, foreign and pol. dept., March, 1917; on mily. service from Oct., 1917, to Oct., 1919; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Nov., 1919; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1921; Counsellor, H.M.'s Legation at Kabul, Feb., 1922; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Dec., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; dep. commr., Hazara, April, 1926; on spl. duty in for. and pol. dept., 1927; pol. agent, Kurram, Jan., 1928; H.M.'s Minister at Kabul, Mar., 1930, to Mar., 1935; K.B.E., June, 1931; ret'd., July, 1937.

MACPHERSON, COLIN JESSE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st Sept., 1914).—Educ. at Stowe and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 29th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

MACPHERSON, DONALD, M.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of excise and salt, Bengal) (b. 22nd Mar., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Rl. High Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, 19th Dec., 1917, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., May, 1918, to Jan., 1919; addl. dist. mag., Nov., 1923; and settl. officer, July, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1926; confd., Mar., 1929; offg. secty., bd. of rev., March, 1929; confd., Sept., 1930; offg. commr. of excise and salt, Bengal, June, 1935; confd., June, 1937.

MCPHERSON, Sir HUGH, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Paisley Gr. Sch., Glasgow Univ., London Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 28th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. offr., joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1897; mag. and collr., Nov., 1902; dir. of land records, July, 1907; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912, and apptd. dir. of land records; sec., rev. dept., Nov., 1912; offg. chief sec. to govt., Feb. to Sept., 1914, and again from April, 1915; confd. as chief sec., Nov., 1915; C.S.I., Jan., 1919; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1919, to Dec., 1920; member of exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, April, 1921, to Dec., 1925; K.C.I.E., June, 1924; actg. govr. of Bihar and Orissa from 27th March to 25th July, 1925; ret'd., Nov., 1926.

MCPHERSON, JAMES, C.I.E., M.A., M.B., F.R.C.S., M.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 16th Oct., 1876).—First commn. dated 26th July, 1902; agency surg., Bushire, Dec., 1915; ditto, Eastern Rajputana States, July, 1920; civil medl. duties of Peshawar Dist. and Khyber agency, July, 1923; resdy. surg., Mysore, July, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1933; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

MCPHERSON, Rev. KENNETH CECIL, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junior chaplain) (b. 8th July, 1890).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bombay, 10th Oct., 1931; confd., July, 1932.

MCPHERSON, Sir THOMAS STEWART, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 21st Aug., 1876).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ., and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.

and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1907; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1913; ditto, and judl. commr., Chota Nagpur, April, 1916; addl. supt. and membr. of legal affairs, Dec., 1916; supt. and membr. of legal affairs, and sec. to legisl. council, Oct., 1917; registrar, Patna high ct., Oct., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; offg. judge, high ct., Patna, March to August, 1923, and from June, 1924; actg. judge, Patna; high ct., March, 1926, and again, Nov., 1926; addl. judge, ditto, Jan., 1927; judge, Dec., 1927; Kt., Jan., 1933; retd., Aug., 1936.

MACQUEEN, PERCY, Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 13th Nov., 1883).—Educ. at Malvern and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 11th Dec., 1907, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1915; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1919; ditto, Jan., 1920; ditto, March, 1922; confd., Oct., 1927; curator, Madras record office, Apl., 1930 to May, 1935.

M CRAE, HENRY WARREN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 13th July, 1884).—Educ. at City of London Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 19th Aug., 1902; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, March, 1926.

M CRAE, IAN MACPHERSON, C.B., C.I.E., O.B.E., M.B., Major-Genl., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 2nd Oct., 1882).—Joined the service 1st Sept., 1904; in mil. employ, March, 1905, to July, 1908; jail supt., Dec., 1911; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to Aug., 1920; O.B.E., June, 1919; supt., central prison, Bareilly, Sept., 1920; ditto, Lucknow, March, 1923; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; asst. dir., medl. service, Bombay, Nov., 1933; apptd. King's hon. physician, Jan., 1936; C.B., June, 1939.

M CRAE, WILLIAM, Lt.-Col., R.E., late Indian State Railways (b. 28th May, 1880).—First commn. dated 9th Aug., 1899; apptd. asst. engr., state rlys., Feb., 1907; exec. engr., March, 1910; ditto, and junr. govt. inspr. of rlys., Sept., 1912; on mil. duty from Oct., 1914, to Feb., 1920; dep. chief engr., Nov., 1926; ch. engr., July, 1931; retd., May, 1935.

M CRAE, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A., D.Sc., F.L.S., late Indian Agricultural Service (b. 26th May, 1878).—Joined the service, 28th March, 1908, as supy. mycologist; agricl. mycologist, Coimbatore, Jan., 1910; imperial mycologist, Pusa, Dec., 1921; offg. agricl. expert, Impl. council of agricl. research, Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1934; retd., June, 1934.

M CROBERT, GEORGE REID, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Madras (prof. of medicine, medl. coll., Madras) (b. 21st Jan., 1895).—Date of first commn., 15th Mar., 1920; apptd. prof. of medicine, medl. coll., Madras, Oct., 1934.

MCSWEENY, DENIS LEON, M.C., Office of High Commr. for India (suptg. exec. officer, accounts dept.) (b. 26th Jan., 1883).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, store depôt, 14th March, 1904; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to May, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct.,

1920; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1922; suptg. exec. officer, Nov., 1934.

M CURK, JOHN, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (asst. commr.) (b. 26th July, 1914).—Educ. at Stowe and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

MACWATT, SIR ROBERT CHARLES, Kt., C.I.E., M.B., Major-Gen., late I.M.S.—First commn., 1st Oct., 1887; agency surgeon, Haraoti and Tonk, Ajmer and Kotah, and Jhallowar to June, 1907, when he was apptd. resdy.-surgeon, Western Rajputana States; agency-surgn., Eastern Rajputana States, Nov., 1913; chief med. offr. in Rajputana, and civil surgn., Ajmer, Sept., 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; inspr.-gen. of civil hospss., Punjab, and chief plague and malaria med. officer, in addn., Aug., 1918; dir.-gen. Indian Medical Service, Jan., 1923; Kt., Jan., 1925; retd., Sept., 1926.

M WATTERS, SIR ARTHUR CECIL, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1880).—Educ. at Clifton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1909; ditto, govt. of India, comm. and ind. dept., Feb., 1910, to March, 1913; jt. mag., Nov., 1913; asst. wheat commr. for India, March, 1915; wheat commr. for India, Oct., 1915; controller of hides and wool, Indian munitions board (in addn.), April, 1917; C.I.E., June, 1918; spl. duty in England, Jan. to March, 1919; sec. to secretariat procedure cmtee., Sept., 1919; employed in foreign and pol. dept., govt. of India, from Jan., 1920; offg. contr. of currency, Dec., 1920; confd., May, 1921; offg. sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., March, 1923; confd., Sept., 1923; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of industries and labour, Oct., 1926; temp. member of gov.-gen.'s council, Oct., 1927; April, 1928, Oct.-Dec., 1929, and again, Sept., 1930; Kt., June, 1929; secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1929; attended Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; retd., Dec., 1931; apptd. commr. for I.C.S. Family Pensions Fund, Oct., 1937.

M WATTERS, MATHew ROBERT CECIL, M.B., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 4th Jan., 1878).—First commn. 1st Feb., 1905; in mil. employ. till July, 1908, and from Dec., 1908, to May, 1913; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to Sept., 1919; prof. of surgery, King George's Medl. Coll., Lucknow, Sept., 1919; civil surgeon, United Provs., July, 1920; retd., Jan., 1933.

M WILLIAM, PETER NEISH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 21st Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Tottenham County School and East London College and Downing College, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931.

MADAN, FRANCIS FALCONER, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Oct., 1886).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.

and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; on mil. duty, Aug., 1918, to May, 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1919; offg. dist. and sessions judge, Oct., 1923; and again, Aug., 1926; offg. registrar, high ct., March, 1926; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1926; confd., April, 1928; addl. judge high ct., Patna, July, 1936; retd. Oct., 1939.

MADAN, FRAMROZ RUSTONJI, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Madras) (b. 3rd Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; offg. consvr., Apl., 1926; confd., Oct., 1931.

MADAN GOPAL SARDHANA, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., United Provs.) (b. 29th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1908; asst. engr., Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

MADAN, JANARDAN ATMARAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., Bombay, rev. dept.) (b. 12th Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Elphinstone High Sch., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and Oxford (Non-Collegiate); apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 19th Nov., 1909, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and asst. settl. offr.; asst. regr., co-op. societies, Oct., 1919; actg. collr. and regr., co-op. societies, Jan., 1920; member of legl. council, 1925; jt. sec. Royal Commn. on Agric. in India, 1926-8; collr. and dist. mgte., Oct., 1927; C.I.E., March, 1929; offg. dir. of lab. intell. and regr. of trade unions, May, 1930; offg. secty. to govt. rev. dept., Bombay, Jan., 1934; confd., June, 1934; commr., Dec., 1936; C.S.I., Jan., 1939.

MADAN LAL, PURI, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 13th Apl., 1885).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1908; in mil. employ to 11th Dec., 1921; civil surg., Oct., 1922.

MADAN MOHAN SINHA, RAI BAHADUR, M.B.E. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 26th Apl., 1913, as dep. collr.; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; asst. sec. to govt., March, 1921; sec., U.P. Commee. for the British Empire Exhibition, Feb., 1923; dep. dir. of industries, Dec., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933; confd., May, 1933.

MADANI, GHULAM AHMED (see Ghulam Ahmed Madani).

MADELEY, WILLIAM YORKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 26th Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Woodbridge Gr. Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., July, 1917; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1918, to Jan., 1919; actg. mag. and collr., May, 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1922; confd., Feb., 1927.

MADGAONKAR, SIR GOVIND DINANATH, Kt., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 21st May, 1871).—Educ. at St. Xavier's and Elphinstone Colls., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; transf. to Bombay, Mar., 1897; 2nd asst., July, 1897; 1st asst., Dec., 1903; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1905; actg. addl. judl. commr. of Sind, Aug., 1920;

actg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1925; judge, high ct., Bombay, Aug., 1925; offg. ch. justice, ditto, June, 1930; retd., April, 1931; Kt., June, 1931.

MADGE, DAVID BRISTO CULLEY, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., May, 1923; supt. (prov. sub.), Feb., 1929; confd., May, 1929.

MADHU SUDAN DAYAL, MITHAL, Lala, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 17th Feb., 1899).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Mar., 1935.

MAFFEY, SIR JOHN LOADER, G.C.M.G., K.C.B., K.C.V.O., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Rugby Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr., Jan., 1905; pol. asst., Oct., 1906; asst. sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1907; asst. pol. officer, Mohmand Field Force, May to June, 1908; sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1909; pol. agent, Khyber, Jan., 1909, to April, 1912; asst. commr., Mardan, Nov., 1913; dep. commr., Peshawar, April, 1914; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Jan., 1915; pte. sec. to Viceroy, from April, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1916; on spl. duty as chief pol. offr., N.W. Frontier Force, from May, 1919; C.S.I., June, 1920; chief sec. to H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught for Indian tour, 1920-1921; K.C.V.O., Feb., 1921; chief commr. and agent to govr.-gen., N.W. Frontier Prov., March, 1921; retd., Nov., 1925; apptd. govr.-gen., Sudan, Oct., 1926; K.C.M.G., June, 1931; perm. under sec. of state, Colonial Office, Nov., 1933; K.C.B., June, 1934; G.C.M.G., June, 1935.

MAFLIN, ALFRED JOHN GOODWIN, M.I.C.E., M.R.San.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b. 8th June, 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908, as asst. engr.; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; on mil. service, May, 1917, to March, 1919; retd., May, 1930.

MAFLIN, PERCY HAMILTON, O.B.E., M.C., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th Nov., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; on milty. duty from 1st April, 1916, to 2nd March, 1919, and again from 15th July, 1919, to 17th March, 1920; O.B.E., Aug., 1920; dep. dir. (projects), rly. board, April, 1924; offg. secty., rly. board, May, 1924; divl. supt., N.W. Rly., Oct., 1927; on spl. duty, rly. bd., Oct., 1927; offg. secty., rly. bd., Aug., 1928; offg. agent, E.B. rly., Apl., 1929; offg. member, rly. bd., Sept., 1930; retd., Apl., 1933.

MAGNIAC, SIR CHARLES LANE, Kt., C.M.G., C.B.E., Brig.-Gen., R.E. (ret'd.), late Railway Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., famine relief works, 7th Feb., 1897; ditto, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1897; dist. traffic supt., July, 1903; dep. traffic manager, June, 1913; on mil. duty, 1914; asst. dir. of rly. transport, May, 1915; C.M.G., 1916; C.B.E., 1919; dir. of movements, May, 1919; liaison offr. with the Rly. Bd., June, 1919; retd., March, 1922; Kt., June, 1923.

MAGRATH, WILLIAM CHRISTOPHER McLEAN, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 25th July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 12th Sept., 1921; offg. supt., Jan., 1924; confd., Apl., 1932; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937.

MAGUB, GEORGE DOUGLAS, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., C.P.) (b. 11th Oct., 1916).—Educ. at Dover County School and Caius College, Cambridge; appointed on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; conf., 11th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in C.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

MAGUIRE, ANGUS DENYS, Indian Police, (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 2nd Oct., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 17th Nov., 1927; offg. supt., June, 1934.

MAHABIR PRASAD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 7th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 19th Sept., 1911; asst. engr., Sept., 1912; exec. engr., Sep., 1921; offg. dep. dir. of industries, Jan., 1931; prof. of civil engrg., Thomason Coll., Dec., 1934, to Apl. 1937.

MAHADEVA AYYAR, CANDAMANGALAM SITARAMA, B.A., B.L., late Judicial dept., Madras (b. 31st July, 1876).—Joined the service as offg. dist. munsif, 24th July, 1905; confd., Apl., 1906; sub-judge, Sept., 1923; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1928; retd., July, 1931.

MAHADEVAN, VENKATASUBBA, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (dist. medl. officer, Madras) (b. 1st Feb., 1888).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; in milty. employ until 27th May, 1923; dist. medl. officer, May, 1923; surg., 1st dist., Nov., 1932; surgeon, general hospital, Madras, June, 1938.

MAHALANABIS, PRASANTA CHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (prof., presy. coll., Calcutta) (b. 29th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1917; prof., Dacca coll., May, 1919; prof. presy. coll., July, 1920.

MAHAMADI, RASHID AHMED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 24th July, 1909).—Educ. at Madrasa-i-Aliya, Hyderabad, Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th October, 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

MAHANTY, DIBAKAR (see Dibakar, M.).

MAHARAJ SINGH, KUNWAR, Sir, C.I.E., M.A., Barr., late United Provs., C.S. (b. 17th May, 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1904, as dep. collr., United Provs.; asst. regr., co-op. credit societies, July, 1908; asst. sec., dept. of education, April, 1911; senr. asst. sec., ditto, April, 1915; C.I.E., June, 1915; offg. dep. commr., July, 1917; mag. and collr., Sept., 1919; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of educn. and health, Nov., 1920, to March, 1923; on depn. to Mauritius and to B. Guiana to report on questions connected with Indian emigration to those colonies, 1925; offg. commr., May, 1927, and again March, 1928; confd., Apl., 1931; member, E. Africa deputation, Oct., 1927; on foreign service as vice-presd. of Jodhpur State Council; agent of Govt. of India in the Union of South Africa, Aug., 1932; Kt., Jan., 1933; retd., May, 1933; member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Mar., 1935, to Mar., 1937.

MAHBUB ALI KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR SHAIKH, O.B.E., Indian Political Service (b. Mar., 1894).—Joined the service, Mar., 1915; asst. commr., Peshawar, May, 1930; ditto, Mansehra, Mar., 1935; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, May, 1936; asst. commr., Thal, Mar., 1937; dep. commr., Kohat, Mar., 1938.

MAHBUB, ILAHI CHARAGH, SHAIKH, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 14th Mar., 1904).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 30th Oct., 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. eng., Oct. 1935.

MAHBUB, MIAN IMAM BAKSH (see Kadri M. M. I. B.).

MAHER, JOHN EDWARD, Indian C.S. (sub.-collr., Madras) (b. 5th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Cheltenham, and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub.-collr., Apl., 1931.

MAHIDA, UDAYSINH NARSENHBAVA, B.E., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 20th April, 1904).—Appointed assistant executive engineer, Bombay, 1st Oct., 1929; dep. secty., govt. Bombay (p.w.d.), June, 1938.

MAHONY, FRANCIS VINCENT, Indian State Rlys. (dep. contr. of stores, N.W. rly.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1886).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 22nd Dec., 1902, to 6th June, 1909; dist. contr. of stores, N.W. rly., May, 1919; offg. dep. contr., May, 1933; and again, Feb., 1934; confd., May, 1934; offg. contr., Aug., 1936.

MAHONY, GEORGE HENRY, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof. of midwifery, Patna) (b. 31st Dec., 1888).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; in mil. employ to 1st April, 1925; offg. civil surg., Central Provs., April, 1925; confd., May, 1928; reverted to milty. service, June, 1931; prof. of midwifery, Prince of Wales medl. coll., Patna, Oct., 1935.

MAHTAB, Sir BIJAY CHAND, see BURDWAN.

MAIN, THOMAS FORSTER, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 23rd Jan., 1882).—Appd. asst. inspr.-gen. of agric., Central Provs., 18th Aug., 1905; dep. dir. of agric., Bombay, Sept., 1909; ditto, Sind, Sept., 1915; offg. dir. of agric., April, 1924; dep. dir. of agric., Poona, June, 1925; O.B.E., June, 1927; dir. of agric., Oct., 1927; retd., July, 1933.

MAINPRICE, FREDERICK PAUL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., C.P.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1915).—Educ. at Haileybury and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 6th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in the C.P. as asst. commr.

MAINWARING, ALBERT JAMES, C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 30th Nov., 1891).—Educ. at King's School, Worcester, and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 4th Dec., 1915, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty from Aug., 1916, to June, 1919; ditto, and asst. settlmnt. officer, Nov., 1919; offg. under-sec. to govt., pol. and appti. dept., Feb., 1923; offg. asst. pol. agent, Orissa Feudatory States, Nov., 1924; offg. pte. sec. to govr., March, 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; offg. magte. and collr., Oct., 1928; ditto (prov. sub.), Nov., 1930; confd., Aug., 1933; C.I.E., June 1934; secty. to govr., Apl., 1937.

MAIR, ROBERT, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E. (Ind.) (dep. chief engr.) (b. 16th July, 1890).—Joined the service as junr. engr., June, 1914; in milty. employ from 26th Dec., 1914, to 21st Jan., 1919; asst. engr., June, 1919; asst. engr., June, 1920; exec. engr., Nov., 1925 on deputation to Calcutta Chord Rly. construction from Jan., 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr., E.I. rly., June, 1934; offg. dep. agent (organisation), Oct., 1934; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Apl., 1936; confd., May, 1939.

MAITLAND, VICTOR KENNARD, M.A., M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 12th June, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 16th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1924; ditto, and dir. of forest school, Balaghat, Mar., 1937.

MAITRA, SANKAR NATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 7th June, 1912).—Educated at Mitra Institution, Bhowanipur, Calcutta, University of Calcutta, King's College, London, and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935 and served in Assam as asst. commr.

MAITRA, SURENDRA NATH, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Sept., 1877).—Apptd. prin., Rajshahi Coll., 4th May, 1925; prinpl. Deccater coll., Nov., 1926; retd., Sept., 1932.

MAJEED, Abdul, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 26th Sept., 1914).—Educated at Government Intermediate College, Allahabad, Allahabad University, and Downing College, Cambridge; appointed on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; conf., 6th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

MAJID, A. (see Abdul Majid).

MAJID, HAFIZ ABDUL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Punjab University and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

MAJID, INAM-UL, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. Indian Govt. Trade Commn., Alexandria (b. 19th May, 1895).—Educ. at Perse Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Dec., 1921; arrived, 5th Jan., 1922, and served in Assam as asst. commr., under-secty. to govt., Apl., 1929; offg. dir. of indus., etc., Nov., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1935; secty., public dept., office of High Commr., Oct., 1937; actg. dep. high commr. for India, Mar.-May, 1938; confd. as dep. commr., Aug., 1938; Indian Govt. trade commr., Alexandria, Jan., 1939.

MAJIDULLAH, SARDAR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 14th Oct., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

MAJOR, JOHN, India Office (hr. clerical officer) (b. 13th Mar., 1888).—Apptd. clerical officer, 28th Sept., 1921; actg. higher ditto, Jan., 1935; confd., July, 1935.

MAJUMDAR, DWIJENDRA LAL, M.A. (Cal.), B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at University of Calcutta and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov.,

1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936; commr. of workmen's compensation, Bengal, April, 1938; secty., jute, paddy and rice enquiry comtee., Sept., 1938; special officer, finance dept. (in addition), June, 1939.

MAJUMDAR, NRIPENDRA NATH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. Feb., 1897).—Joined the service, 1st Mar., 1922; asst. acct.-genl., Bengal, Sept., 1923; ditto, posts and tels., Nov., 1928; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Jan., 1931; asst. dir., army audit, W. Cmd., Jan., 1932; attd. officer, office of the ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Mar., 1936; dep. govt. exr., B.N. rly., Apl., 1936; ch. auditor, E.B. rly., Feb., 1938; dep. acct. gen., Burma, Mar., 1939; comptr., posts and tels. accts., Burma, April, 1939; dep. acct. gen., Burma, June, 1939; contr. of mily. accts., Burma, June, 1939.

MAJUMDAR, SATIS CHANDRA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Bengal) (b. 22nd May, 1887).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1910, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. supt. engr., April, 1931; confd., Sept., 1935.

MAKAND, SAMUEL NORMAN, M.B., Ch. B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Central Provs. (dir. of public health) (b. 10th July, 1886).—Date of first commn., 25th Feb., 1921; in milty. employ until 20th Feb., 1930; medl. officer of health, Simla, Feb., 1931; offg. dir. of public health, central provs., Oct., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937.

MAL, DEBI DATTA (see Debi Datta Mal).

MALABARI, PHIROZSHAH BEHRAMJI, KHAN BAHADUR, Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 2nd Nov., 1876).—Joined the dept., April, 1903; prothonotary and regr., June, 1919; offg. offl. assignee, Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1928; retd., Dec., 1935.

MALCOLM, AUSTIN KENNETH, Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1917).—Apptd., asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Dec., 1937.

MALCOLM, CHARLES ADOLPH, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 27th Aug., 1879).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1902, as asst. consvr. of forests; dep. consvr., Dec., 1908; served under Indian munitions bd., as offg. dep. controller (timber supplies), Bombay, June to Aug., 1918; offg. consvr., Oct., 1920; consvr., Oct., 1923; chief consvr., Apl., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1931; retd., Aug., 1934.

MALCOLM, HUGH WAUDBY, India Office (exec. officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 21st May, 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, 23rd Apl., 1920; actg. exec. officer, Feb., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934.

MALCOLM, KENNETH ROBERT, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 17th Dec., 1908).—Educ. at Eton and New College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Apl., 1935.

MALCOLM, ROBERT CONACHER, Survey of India (asst. supt., mathl. instrument office) (b. 19th June, 1891).—Joined the service, 4th Apl., 1921; asst. supt., mathl. instrument office, Mar., 1926; offg. supt., ditto, May, 1929.

MALCOLMSON, GREER EDMUND Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 30th Sept., 1878).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1906; resigned, Sept., 1913; re-admitted, Feb., 1915; in mil. employ until

7th Aug., 1920; dist. medl. and sanitary officer, Madras, Sept., 1920; prof., medl. coll., and physician gen. hosp., Madras, Dec., 1923; retd., Sept., 1933.

MALET, GERALD RION LILLINGTON, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 14th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; on milty. duty from 21st May, 1916, to 24th Sept., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1922.

MALHAN, FAQIR CHAND, Rai Bahadur, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 28th May, 1883).—Held non-gaz. appts. from 2nd May, 1901; served under agent, N.W. Rly.; apptd. compilation offic., statistics, June, 1923; asst. dir. of statistics, Rly. Bd., Jan., 1925; senr. asst. dir., rly. clg. accts. office, Dec., 1926; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1927; accts. offic., gr. I., Apl., 1929; offic. on spec. duty, rates register experiment, Jan., 1930; dep. chief accts. offic., N.W. Rly. (provl.), July, 1933; retd., May 1938.

MALIK, ABDULLA KHALID, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 7th July, 1910).—Educ. at Government High School, Gujrat, Government Intermediate College, Gujrat, University of the Punjab and Magdalene College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offic., May, 1937.

MALIK, HARDIT SINGH, O.B.E., B.A., (Oxon), Indian C.S. (trade commr., New York) (b. 23rd Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Eastbourne Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; arrived in India, 6th Jan., 1922; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1926; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1927; dep. trade commr., London, Jan., 1931, to May, 1934; actg. trade commr., May-Sept., 1932; and again, Nov., 1933; dep. secty. to govt. of India, commerce dept., June, 1934; O.B.E., Jan., 1938; trade commr., New York, July, 1938.

MALIK, JASVANTSINGH, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 13th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 21st Jan., 1921; offg. exec. engr., July, 1923; confd., Oct., 1929; in foreign service as munpl. engr., Ahmedabad from Feb., 1930, to May, 1938.

MALIK, TEJA SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central P.W.D.) (b. 1st Sept., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 4th Nov., 1910; exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; exec. engr., central P.W.D., Apl., 1930; offg. supt. engr., Sept., 1931, and again, Jan., 1933.

MALLAM, GEORGE LESLIE, Barr., Major I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 13th Dec., 1895).—First commn. dated 14th Nov., 1916; joined pol. dept., 14th March, 1921; asst. commr., Bannu, June, 1921; under-sec. to pol. agent, Persian Gulf, Jan., 1923; dist. judge, Abbottabad, Dec., 1926; asst. comr., Tonk, June, 1927; ditto, Mardan, May, 1928; under-secty. to A.G.G. and C.C., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1929; census supt., N.W.F.P., April, 1930; counsellor, Brit. legn., Kabul, Apl., 1930; dep. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1934; senior sub. judge, Peshawar, Dec. 1938; addl. fin. secy. to govt. N.W.F.P., Mar., 1939; fin. secy. to govt. N.W.F.P. and jun. adviser to the local admn. N.W.F.P.; April, 1939.

MALLET, FREDERICK JOHN, late Public Works Dept., United Provs. (b. 21st Jan., 1878).—Joined the service as mechanical engr. for waterworks, 25th Dec., 1920; retd., Jan., 1933.

MALLIK, see MULLICK.

MALLIK, BHOLA NATH, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt., Orissa) (b. 9th Apl., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 13th April, 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; transferred to Orissa, May, 1939.

MALLIK, HARENDRA KRISTO, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 16th Mar., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Mar., 1910; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Burma circle, May, 1930; ditto, Bombay circle, Nov., 1931; asst. acct.-genl., United Provs., June, 1932; dep. ditto, Aug., 1934; attd. officr., office of comp., Orissa, Oct., 1936; dep. acct.-genl., Central revenues, Apl., 1937; exr., outside audit dept., Apl., 1938; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, June, 1938; dep. acct. gen., Posts and Tels., Nagpur, Feb., 1939.

MALLIK, SATYENDRA CHANDRA, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th Feb. 1874).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 8th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1906; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1911; presdt., spl. tribunal, Rungpur, Jan. to April, 1918; actg. judge, Calcutta high ct., July, 1926; ditto, Jan., 1927; ditto, April to Aug., 1927; ditto, Jan., 1928; confd., July, 1928; retd., May, 1934.

MALLYA, BANTWAL GANAPATHI, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bengal (civil surgeon, Bengal) (b. 4th April, 1890).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1914; in milty. employ to 22nd June, 1923; offg. supt., July, 1923; confd., Oct., 1925; offg. civil surgeon, May, 1933.

MAMA, JAMSHED BYRAMJI, D.P.H. (Oxon), D.T.M. (Liver.) late Public Health Dept., Bombay (b. 4th Jan., 1876).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st April, 1911, to 31st Aug., 1913; analyst to sany. board, Sept., 1913, to July, 1920; officer in charge, public health laby., Poona, Jan., 1921; dir., vaccine institute, Belgaum, April 1927; retd., Jan., 1931.

MAN MOHAN, RAI BAHADUR, M.A., Punjab Educl. Service (asst. dir., pub. instrn.) (b. 27th July, 1888).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1911; asst. prof. of econ., govt. coll., Lahore, Jan., 1913; lecturer, ditto, Oct., 1921; principal, Khalsa Coll., June, 1924; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1931; asst. dir., pub. instrn., Nov., 1936.

MAN SINGH, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 9th Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on probation, 7th Dec., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

MAN SINGH, RAI BAHADUR, C.B.E., B.A., Indian Police (member, pub. serv. commn., United Provs.) (b. 3rd July, 1883).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1906, as dept. supt.; supt., Sept., 1918; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1920; C.B.E., June, 1932; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1933. retd., Apl., 1937; member, United Provs. public service commission, Apl., 1937.

MANAVEDAN RAJA, KISHAKKA COVILAGATH. Rao Bahadur, Madras C.S. (sub-collr.) (b. 1st

Oct., 1884).—Joined the service 13th Jan., 1915; dep. collr., Oct., 1917; sub-collr., Nov., 1932; offg. collr., Nov., 1932.

MANDAL, see MUNDLE.

PATANJALI SASTRI MANDUKULATHUR. Appointed Judge, High Court, Madras, March, 1939.

MANGALMURTI KESHAO TUKARAM, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Central Prov. s.) (b. 7th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 4th Dec., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1927; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1929; and again, June, 1932; confd., Jan., 1936.

MANGAL SEN, DHODY, RAI BAHADUR LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd July, 1876).—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1899; asst. engr., July, 1900; exec. engr. Oct., 1910; supt. engr., Aug., 1922 retd., July, 1931.

MANGAT RAI, EDWARD NIRMAL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 30th Mar., 1915).—Educ. at R.I.M. Coll., Dehra Dun, Delhi Univ. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 10th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

MANI, NATESA SUBRAHMANYAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 11th July, 1913).—Educ. at Banadurai High Sch., Kumbakonam, Madras Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; joined the service 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

MANI, RANGIAH SUBRA, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 14th August, 1907). Educ. at Madras University, Imperial College of Science and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; under-secty. to the Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Aug., 1937.

MANI, TIRUCHENDURAI MUTHUKRISHNAN SUBRA, M.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty., rev. dept., Madras) (b. 1st Aug., 1908).—Educ. at University College, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Apl., 1933; under secty., rev. dept., Madras, Apl., 1936.

MANICO, EDWARD LESLIE, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. operating supt. (power)) (b. 12th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as assistant carriage and wagon supt., 25th Jan., 1911; dist. ditto, April, 1918; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1927; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Burma rlys., Oct., 1931; dist. loco. supt., N.W. rly., Nov., 1932; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. rly., Mar., 1933; offg. dep. ch. opertg. supt., N.W. rly., Oct., 1933; and again, June, 1934; dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936; dep. ch. operating supt. (power), Jan., 1939.

MANINDRA LAL SEN GUPTA (see Sen Gupta, M. L.).

MANKAR, WAMAN KESHAVJI, M.A., LL.B. (b. 23rd July, 1884).—Joined the service, 10th July, 1922; non-gaz. appt. in Bombay city

police from July, 1922 to October, 1934; police prosecutor from Nov., 1934; offd. as presdy. mag., May, 1936, to Mar., 1938; confd., April, 1938.

MANLEY, EDWARD LOVEL, M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th June, 1886).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; on milty. duty from 8th Feb., 1918, to 17th Dec., 1919; offg. govt. inspr. of rlys., Dec., 1923; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1929; confd., June, 1934; offg. dep. agent, organisation, E.B. rly., Mar., 1934; offg. agent, E.B. rly., Apl., 1935; and again, May, 1937; offg. ch. engr., E.B. rly., Oct. 1935, prov. confd., Dec., 1935; confd., Aug., 1937; retd., Oct. 1939.

MANMATHA NATH MITRA, L.M.S. (Punj.), L.M. (Dublin), D.P.H. (London), Public Health Dept., United Provs. (b. 23rd May, 1885).—Joined the service as health officer on probn., 1st Mar., 1914; confd., Mar., 1915; asst. dir. of public health, 12th Mar., 1930.

MANNING, ARTHUR PITCHER, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th Nov., 1882).—Joined the telegraph dept., India Posts and Telegraphs, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. supt.; ditto, engrg., April, 1914; deputed to fd. service, E. Africa, July, 1915 (awarded the Military Cross, June, 1917; mentioned in despatch); O.B.E., June, 1918; C.I.E., June, 1919; divl. engr., tels., Dec., 1920; tel. supt., N.W. Rly., Oct., 1927; retd., Nov., 1932.

MANNING, DONALD EDMUND BELFORD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 9th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Mar., 1928.

MANNOOCH, GEOFFREY HERBERT, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Nov., 1910; supt., Feb., 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., variously from 1932.

MANOHAR DAS CHATURVEDI, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr. United Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 28th Dec., 1923; silviculturist, March, 1927; dep. consvr., Dec., 1931.

MANOHAR LAL (judge, high ct., Patna).—Apptd. June, 1939.

MANOHAR LAL SOOD, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 28th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 19th Nov., 1927; engr. student, Nov., 1928; proby. asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1928; confd., Jan., 1930; exec. engr., Oct., 1937.

MANOHAR LAL ZUTSHI, PANDIT, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. July, 1876).—Joined the service, 21st Sept., 1902; inspr. of schools, Feb., 1921; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn. and under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1923; prinpl., jubilee intermediate coll., Lucknow, April, 1924; ditto, govt. coll., Sept., 1927; retd., July, 1931.

MANSER, THOMAS JAMES STEDMAN, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 24th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1899; asst. engr., tels., Oct., 1924; asst. divl. engr., tels., May, 1927; divl. engr., tels., June, 1933; retd., Oct., 1938.

MANSFIELD, PHILIP THEODORE, C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Orissa) (b. 1st Feb., 1892).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 11th Dec., 1915, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty, Oct., 1918, to Jan., 1919; ditto, and addl. pol. agent, Feudatory States, Oct., 1919; jt. mag., Jan., 1923; offg. dir. of land records and surveys, March, 1928; confd., Jan., 1929; mag. and collr. (prov. sub.), Jan., 1929; confd., Nov., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., rev. dept., Aprl., 1931; dir. of land records and surveys, Aprl., 1933; secty. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, reforms dept., Jan., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; ch. secty. to govt., Orissa, Aprl., 1936.

MANSON, FREDERICK PERCY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Ryis. (b. 4th Dec., 1883).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 12th Sept., 1914; asst. engr., Mar., 1918; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; exec. engr., Jan., 1929; retd., Aprl., 1939.

MANSON, JOHN ALEXANDER SHAND, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 19th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. Bombay, 1st Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confd., Oct., 1933.

MANSON, SAMUEL DUNCAN, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 10th Jan., 1885).—Joined the dept. as asst. traffic supt., 26th Oct., 1908; dist. traffic supt., Aprl., 1915; dir. of traffic, Aprl., 1924; offg. dir. of traffic, May, 1924; member, rly. rates advisory committee, Aprl., 1926; offg. dep. ch. coml. manager, Oct., 1928; confd., Jan., 1929; retd., Aug., 1933.

MANT, SIR REGINALD ARTHUR, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Sept., 1870).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Mar., 1895; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1899; under-sec. to govt. of India, finl. dept., March, 1901; dep. ditto, Aprl., 1904, to June, 1905; dep. commr., June, 1905; dept. offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, finl. dept., Nov. 1907; offg. joint sec., mily. fin. branch, July, 1908; dep. commr., Punjab, 1909; finl. sec. to govt., Nov., 1910, to June, 1915; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric., Jan., 1916, to June, 1920; addl. member, govr.-genl.'s legisl. council, Jan., 1916; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s council, Aprl., 1919; C.S.I., June, 1919; member of Indian Fiscal Commission, Oct., 1921, to July, 1922; commr., Aprl., 1923; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1924; retd., Sept., 1924; member of council of India, 1st Oct., 1924, to Oct., 1934; member, Royal Commn. on Indian Currency and Finance, 1925; member, East African Commn., Nov., 1927; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1933; member, Ind. staty. rly. bd. ctee., June, 1933; vice-chairman, Ind. res. bank ctee., July, 1933.

MAQBUL HUSAIN, SHAIKH, KHAN BAHADUR, C.I.E., B.A., Barr., late United Provs., C.S. (b. 26th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1903, as dep. collr.; served under govt. of India, home dept., as asst. dir. of criminal intelligence, from July, 1909, to Nov., 1919; rev. minister, Kashmir, Nov., 1909; C.I.E.,

Jan., 1916; jt. regr., co-op. credit societies, United Provs., Jan., 1918; temp. regr., ditto, June, 1918; confd., Sept., 1921; dep. commr., Nov., 1924; mag. and collr., Sept., 1921; retd., Nov., 1931.

MAQBUL SHAH, KHAN BAHADUR, SAYAD, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. Feb., 1875).—Joined the service, 14th Dec., 1896; inspr. of schools, March, 1920; ditto of vernacular education, Feb., 1921; retd., Dec., 1931.

MAQSUD ALI KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR, Munshi, B.A., late United Provs. C.S. (b. 15th Jan., 1878).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 14th June, 1901; confd., Dec., 1903; magte. and collr., Dec., 1928; retd., Jan., 1933.

MARAR, KOTTIL WALAPPIL PARAMESWARA, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 7th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Aprl., 1933; offg. under secty. to govt., Assam, in depts. under ch. secty., Oct., 1933; awarded K.-i.-H., gold medal, Jan., 1935; apptd. as provincial supt. of census operations, Assam, Dec., 1939.

MARCHINTON, WILLIAM FRANKLIN, late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 14th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of industries, Ranchi, 29th Oct., 1920; industrial engr., Nov., 1925; offg. electrical engr., Aprl., 1926; confd., Aprl., 1927; surveyor of inland steam vessels, Patna, Nov., 1932; retired, Oct., 1939.

MARDHEKAR, VAMAN BHIVRAM, M.A., late Bombay C.S. (b. 16th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service, 18th June, 1905; dep. collr., Oct., 1907; actg. supt., land records, Aug., 1916; confd., Aug., 1917; offg. collr., Feb., 1922; confd., May, 1930; retd., Nov., 1935.

MARIETTE, ERNEST EDGAR, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 13th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1914; asst. engr., March, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1923.

MARJORIBANKS, ALAN GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 28th Oct., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 11th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Dec., 1928, Jan., 1933, and July, 1933.

MARLOW, THOMAS, late Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 7th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as timber asst., 17th April, 1919; fcrest engr., Dec., 1922; retd., Aprl., 1933.

MARLOW, WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (supvg. executive officer, general dept.) (b. 8th July, 1889).—Educ. at Brentwood Gr. Sch., Monoux Sch., Walthamstow, and East London Coll.; apptd. sec. div. clerk board of educ., 29th Aprl., 1907; 2nd class clerk, India Office, 29th Oct., 1907; on mil. service from Dec., 1916; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; attd. to Indian del. to League of Nations, 1st Assembly, 1920; personal asst. to high commr., Aprl., 1921; higher exec. officer, June, 1927; on special duty in connec-

tion with the building of India House, Mar., 1929, to Aug., 1930; services lent to Indian del. Impl. Economic Conf., May to Sept., 1932 asst. sec., ditto, July, 1932; suptg. exec. officr., Oct., 1938.

MARRIOTT, ERIC LLEWELLYN, C.I.E., late Indian Police, Bihar (b. 28th May, 1888).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1907; asst. supt., Apl., 1912; supt., July, 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., May, 1931, and again, Feb., 1933; confd., June, 1934; C.I.E., June, 1934; ret'd., Aug., 1938.

MARRIOTT, ROBERT ECKLIN, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (ch. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 15th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Dec., 1924; offg. divl. supt., Apl., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1932; ditto (provl. perm.), Apl., 1936; confd., Aug., 1937; chief engr., Mar., 1938.

MARRIOTT, ROBIN GEORGE, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 3rd Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1911, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; services placed at disposal of Railway Board, Simla, from April, 1925, to Oct., 1927; offg. consvr. variously from Feb., 1932; confd., Sept., 1936; ret'd., Jan., 1939.

MARRIS, SIR WILLIAM SINCLAIR, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Oct., 1873).—Educ. at Canterbury Coll., New Zealand, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1899, to July, 1901; under-sec. to govt. of India, Home dept., Nov., 1901; dep. ditto, Nov., 1904; lent to govt. of the Transvaal, Oct., 1906, to 1908; dep. commr., May, 1911; C.I.E., Jan., 1914; mag. and collr., May, 1914; temply. employed in judl. and public dept., India Office, July, 1915, and from Sept. to Dec., 1915; inspr.-gen. of police (temp.), United Provs., April, 1916; jt. sec. (temp.), home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1917; on deputn. to England with the sec. of state for India, April to Oct., 1918; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1919; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1919; on spl. duty as reforms commr., 1919; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1921; govr. of Assam, 3rd March, 1921; govr. of United Provs., 24th Dec., 1922; member of Council of India, May, 1928—Sept., 1929; author of a summary of Lord Curzon's administration in the Home dept., 1899-1905, and of the report of the commn. of inquiry into the organization and making of the Central South African Rlys., 1907-8 (in part).

MARSDEN, EUSTACE HOWARD, Capt., R.I.M., ret'd., late Marine dept., Madras (b. 27th Oct., 1882).—Apptd. offg. presdy., port officer, 19th Feb., 1929; confd., Apl., 1929; prinpl. port officer, Madras dist., May, 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1933.

MARSDEN, PERCY, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab), (b. 26th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Cheltenham, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; personal asst. to pol. agent, Phulkian

States, Dec., 1914; sec., municipal cttee., Amritsar, March, 1935, to May, 1917; on mil. duty from July, 1918, to April, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), July, 1925; offg. sec. to govt. trans. depts., Punjab, Dec., 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. commr., Oct., 1935.

MARSH, PERCY WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A. Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 14th Oct., 1881).—Educ. at Wellington, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 26th Nov., 1905, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1914; temply. employed under War Office from April, 1916, to Dec., 1918; mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1929; offg. commr., Mar., 1931; commr. (provl. subs.), May, 1933; confd., Nov., 1934; member, bd. of rev., United Provs., Dec., 1938; C.S.I., Jan., 1939.

MARSH, WILFRED, Indian Police (dep. commr. of police, Rangoon) (b. 20th July, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 25th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1934; dep. commr. of police, Rangoon, March, 1939.

MARSH-SMITH, REGINALD NORMAN, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 24th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1911; asst. dep. inspr.-gen., C.I.D., Sept., 1920; supt., Aug., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1926; inspr.-genl., Gwalior Darbar, May, 1936.

MARSHALL, FREDERICK, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Burma) (b. 14th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; on mil. service from 19th Aug., 1916, to 21st Nov., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1931; ditto (provl. subs.), May, 1933; confd., July, 1933.

MARSHALL, GEORGE WARD, Indian State Rlys. (manager, coke plant colliery dept.) (b. 5th Sept., 1883).—Apptd. manager, coke plant colliery dept., E.I. rly., 7th Nov., 1911.

MARSAHL, JOHN BROWN, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Sept., 1884).—Educ. at Perth Academy and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on spl. duty, offg. registrar co-operative societies; C.I.E., June, 1920; dep. commr., March, 1925; offg. excise commr., Dec., 1925; confd., April, 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

MARSHALL, SIR JOHN HUBERT, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., Litt.D., F.S.A., Hon. A.R.I.B.A. (b. 19th Mar., 1876).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge (Porson prize), Prendergast and Craven fellowships; student of British Sch. at Athens, 1898-99; apptd. dir.-gen. of archaeology, 22nd Feb., 1902; C.I.E., June, 1910; knighted, Jan., 1915; on spl. duty, Sept., 1928; ret'd., Mar., 1931; officr. on spl. duty, Mar., 1931, to Sept., 1936.

MARSHALL, REV. NORMAN EDWYN, M.A. Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 31st Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, Punjab, 2nd Jan., 1915; senr. chaplain, 6th June, 1927.

MARSLAND, HAROLD, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Aug., 1881).—Trained in R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1904; arrived Nov., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; on mil. duty from April to Sept., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., July, 1924; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

MARTEN, Sir ALFRED AMBERSON BARRINGTON, Kt., M.A., LL.D., (Barr., 1895) (b. 8th Dec., 1870).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. judge, high court, Bombay, Dec., 1916; Kt., Jan., 1924; chief justice, Bombay high court, June, 1926; ret'd., June, 1930.

MARTEN, ERIC CHARLES, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 14th Feb., 1899).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; joined the service, 17th Sept., 1922; arrived, 21st Nov., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause court, Simla, April, 1927; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1930; Oct., 1932, and again May, 1933; addl. ditto, Sept., 1933; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1935; on spec. duty, legal remembrancer office, May, 1938.

MARTEN, FRANK RICHARD, O.B.E., late India Office (b. 9th Oct., 1872).—Solicitor to the Supreme Court, 1895; apptd. clerk, legal adviser's dept., 13th Aug., 1900; asst. solicitor, Dec., 1913; apptd. a commr. for oaths, July, 1916; O.B.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

MARTIN, ARCHDALL CHERRY, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 1st Apl., 1893).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1913; asst. supt., May, 1915; offg. supt., May, 1921; confd., Sept., 1929.

MARTIN, CHARLES DE CARTERET, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1888).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1914; in milty. employ prior to July, 1925; asst. dir., Pasteur inst., July, 1925; offg. asst. dir., central research institute, Kasauli, Jan., 1929; pathologist, genl. hosp., Rangoon, June, 1930; civil surgeon (tempy.), May, 1933.

MARTIN, FREDERICK GEORGE STEPHEN, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (contr. of stores, E.I. Rly.) (b. 26th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. works manager, G.I.P. Rly., 14th Dec., 1923; offg. works manager, May, 1925; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Feb., 1926; on spl. duty under Railway Board, 19th March, 1927; dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. Rly., Feb., 1928; offg. contr. of stores, Sept., 1930; confd., Oct., 1931.

MARTIN, The Ven. FREDERICK WILLIAM, M.A., Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (archdeacon) (b. 16th Jan., 1886).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 10th April, 1918; junr. chaplain, May, 1919; senr. chaplain, April, 1930; archdeacon, Sept., 1932.

MARTIN, GEOFFREY NOEL, late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Dec., 1893).—Educ. at Magdalen Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 5th Mar., 1921; arrived, 20th Apl., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; and sub-divl. officer, Dec., 1921; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1925; offg. dep. secty., fin. dep., Burma, Aug., 1929; confd., May, 1931;

secty. to govt., Burma, forest dept., Apl., 1934; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

MARTIN, GEORGE WILLIAM PERCY, India Office (b. 16th Dec., 1877).—Educ. at Allyn's Sch., Dulwich; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 30th Jan., 1899, and attached to the registry and record dept.; minor staff clerk, Dec., 1911; transferred to the finl. dept., Jan., 1917; staff clerk (temp.), Aug., 1918; sec. to Indian delegation to internl. fin. conference, Brussels, 1920; administrative officer, April, 1922; administrative officer for currency duties, Apl., 1933, ret'd., Dec., 1937.

MARTIN, HARRY WALTER, Office of High Commr. for India (dep. ch. accountg. officer) (b. 26th May, 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 13th Sept., 1902; minor staff clerk, Oct., 1919; staff clerk (actg.), April, 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from April, 1921; higher exec. officer, 1st April, 1922; suptg. exec. officer, 21st April, 1922; asst. ch. acctg. officer, Nov., 1934; dep. ditto, Nov., 1937.

MARTIN, HUGH BELLASIS, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 6th Oct., 1915).—Educ. at Harrow and Oriel College, Oxford; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; conf., 17th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

MARTIN, JAMES REA, C.I.E., B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 2nd Aug., 1877).—Educ. at Methodist Coll., and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 29th Nov., 1901, and served in Bombay and Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; manager, Sind encumbered estates, Nov., 1909; dep. commr., Upper Sind Frontier, Sept., 1912; temp. dep. sec. to govt., Bombay, rev. and finl. depts., Dec., 1916; on spl. duty in sectt., Jan. to Sept., 1917; compiled genl. admn. report, 1916-17; collr., Feb., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1923; dep. director of development, 1923; offg. director of development, July, 1924; member of council of state, 1924; member of legisl. council, 1924; sec. to govt., pol. dept., Aug., 1926; chief sec. to ditto, Jan., 1927; tempy. member of exec. council, Bombay, June, 1928; member legisl. council, 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1932; member, Indian States enquiry comte., Jan., 1932.

MARTIN, OLAUS MACLEOD, C.I.E., Indian C.S.
