SINHA, ALAKH KUMAR, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Police (depy. inspr. gen., Bihar) (b. 29th June, 1884).—Joined the service, 21st Feb., 1908; asst. supt., Jan., 1920; supt., Apl., 1924; O.B.E., June, 1926; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1936; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1937; depy. inspr. genl., Aug., 1938.

SINHA, AVADH KISHOR PRASHAD, Rai Bahadur, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 16th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1921; promoted to I.P. as supt., Feb., 1935.

SINHA, BABU NIRSU NARAYAN, M.A., B.L.—Apptd. presdt., Bihar and Orissa legisl. council, 21st Jan., 1931; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1934; to Mar., 1937; actg. vice-presdt., ditto, Oct., 1934.

SINHA, HARNANDAN PRASAD, Indian Serv. of Engrs. (b. 3rd Jan., 1906).—Apptd. as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., Jan., 1929; transf. to the central public works dept., 1932.

SINHA, KALI PRASHAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 1st March, 1897).—Educ. at Calcutta University and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Feb., 1924; arrived, 1st Feb., 1924, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1933; magte. and collr. (confd.), 1933.

SINHA, MADAN MOHAN (see Madan M. S.).

SINHA, MITHILESH KUMAR, B.A., Indian Police (b. 30th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 23rd April, 1928.

SINHA, RAMA AKHANDALESHVAR PRASHAD, Indian Police (b. 29th Jan., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar, on probn., 27th Jan., 1936.

SINHA, RAMANI MOHAN, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 19th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 25th Jan., 1910, and held non-gazetted appt. prior to 1st Feb., 1920; asst. engr., Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; exec. engr., Jan., 1933.

SINHA, ROMANI MOHAN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr. (Signals)) (b. 22nd Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., 1st Nov., 1919; dist. signal engr., E.I. Rly., Mar., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr. (signals), E.I. Rly., Nov., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935.

SINHA, SUDHIR KUMAR, Indian Police (b. Jan., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 1st Mar., 1932, confd.; Dec., 1935.

SINHA, Honble. SUSHIL KUMAR, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th June, 1895).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 19th Jan., 1920, and posted to Bengal; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1924; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1927; confd., June, 1931; offg. ch. pres. mag., Calcutta, June, 1931; confd., Sept., 1933; retd., Apl., 1937.

SINHA, Hon. TARU, B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 9th Feb., 1899).—Joined the service as officr. in cl. II, Oct., 1922.

SINTON, JOHN ALEXANDER, V.C., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 2nd Dec., 1884).—First commn., 29th July, 1911; in milty. employ until 8th Feb., 1921, when apptd. bacteriological officer, Pasteur inst., Kasauli; dir., malaria survey of India, April, 1932; retd., Aug., 1938.

SIRAJUDDIN AHMAD, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 1st Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 29th June, 1925; offg. supt., May, 1929, and again, Mar., 1933.

SIRCAR, SIR NRIPENDRA NATH, K.C.S.I., Barr., Bengal Judicial Dept.—Apptd. advocate genl., Bengal, Dec., 1928; Kt., June, 1931; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; member, govt. genl.'s exec. council, May, 1934, to April, 1939; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1936.

SIRKAR, SATYA CHARAN, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transportation officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 1st Dec., 1897).—Joined service, Nov., 1919; asst. dist. traffic supt., junior scale, Nov., 1921; divl. transportation (traffic-commercial) officer, senior scale (provl.), Jan., 1939.

SITAL PRASAD, B.Sc., Inc. tax Dept. (asst. commr.) (b. 7th May, 1892).—Apptd. asst. commr., United Provs., June, 1934.

SITAL SINGH, M.A., Sardar Bahadur, Indian Finance Dept. (b. 10th Mar., 1886).—Joined the service, 21st Nov., 1910, and held non-gazetted appt.; offg. supt., Jan., 1918; revereted to non-gazetted appt. from 2nd Mar., 1918; offg. supt., Jan., 1920; confd., Mar., 1922; asst. sec., Oct., 1922; offg. under secty., Apl., 1929; dep. acct. genl., central revs., Dec., 1932; ditto, Punjab (works), Apl., 1936.

SITZLER, EDWARD ALBERT—Name changed by Deed Poll to SEATON, q.v.

SITZLER, HENRY FERDINAND, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 4th March, 1883).—Educ. at Malvern, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1918; offg. excise commr., June, 1925; offg. secty. to govt., judl. dept., Dec., 1928; retd., June, 1930; ditto Punjab, (Works) April, 1936.

SIVARAMA KRISHNAN, RAMANATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 19th Oct., 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., July, 1927; sub-collr., Jan., 1928; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1937; confd., Nov., 1937.

SIVARAMA KRISHNAN, SESHIAH, B.S., Indian Audit Department (b. 16th April, 1895).—Joined the service, 7th Aug., 1916; asst. acct. genl., Bengal, Sept., 1936; special telephone rev. acctg. officer, Jan., 1937; asst. acct. genl., Central Provs., Aug., 1938; depy. acct. genl., Central Provs., Nagpur, Mar., 1939; Rao Bahadur, June, 1939.

SIVARAMAN, BALARAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 10th Jan., 1912).—Educ. at Christian College School, Madras, University of Madras and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; posted to Orissa, Aug., 1937.

SIVA RAMAN, MANAKKAL SUNDARALINGAM IYER, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 9th May, 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in

India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Sept., 1932; spec. settlement officer., Apl., 1937; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Aug., 1937.

SIVASANKAR, TUMKAR, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 14th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at University of Mysore, Victoria Univ., Manchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., July, 1929; offg. dep. commr. of excise, Madras, Apl., 1934; secty., cent. road traffic bd., Apl., 1938.

SIVASANKARA MENON, KUMAR PADMA, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Oct., 1898).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Christ Church Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to resdt., Hyderabad, Feb., 1925; under sec. to C.C. and A.G.G. in N.W.F.P., May, 1927; dist. judge, Peshawar, Apl., 1929; agent to the govt. of India in Ceylon, Oct., 1929; dist. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Aug., 1934; offg. addl. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Sept., 1935; addl. dep. secty. to the govt. of India for. and pol. dept., Apl., 1936; ditto, external affairs dept., Apl., 1937; relief commr. for Baluchistan in addn., Apl., 1936; pol. agent, Zhob, Nov., 1937.

SIVASWAMI AIYAR, Sir PAZHAMANHERI SUNDARAM AIYAR, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., B.L.—Vakil, High Court of Judicature, Madras, 9th Oct., 1907; advocate-gen., Madras, Jan., 1908; C.I.E., Jan., 1908; member of exec. council, Madras, Feb., 1912, to Feb., 1917; C.S.I., June, 1912; K.C.S.I., June, 1915; vice-chancellor of Madras Univ., 1916-18; vice-chancellor of Hindu Univ., Benares, 1918-19; elected member of Indian legisl. assembly, 1920.

SKEMP, FRANK WHITTINGHAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 13th Dec., 1880).—Educ. at Owens Coll., Manchester; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 10th Dec., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1923; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, March, 1927; actg. ditto., April, 1927; addl. ditto, from Nov., 1928; confd., Oct., 1933.

SKINNER, ARTHUR FREDERICK, Office of High Commr. for India (higher executive officer, store dept. (b. 28th May, 1887).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 22nd Sept., 1906; on mil. service from Dec., 1916, to Feb., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; actg. higher exec. officer, June, 1929; confd., Nov., 1932.

SKINNER, JAMES MACGREGOR, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 14th Apl., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1906; surg., 1st Dist., Madras, and supt., Rayapuram medl. school, Feb., 1927; offg. 2nd physician, gen. hosp., Madras, Feb., 1926; confd., June, 1929; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

SKINNER, PHILIP ERNEST FREEMAN, late Indian C.S. (b. 6th Mar., 1904).—Educ. at Charterhouse and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1927; arrived, 12th Dec., 1927, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under secty. to govt., C.P., Oct., 1931; offg. dep. commr., May, 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

SKRINE, CLARMONT PERCIVAL, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Feb., 1888).—Educ. at Winchester, New College, Oxford, and University College, London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 25th Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, Oct., 1915; H.B.M.'s vice-consul at Kerman, Oct., 1916; H.B.M.'s consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, Dec., 1917; prob., pol. dept., Jan., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Jan., 1920; pol. agent, Quetta, April, 1921; H.M.'s consul-general, Kashgar, July, 1922; sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Punjab States, July, 1926; H.B.M.'s Consul for Sistan and Kain, April, 1927; pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi, Mar., 1929; pol. agent, Kalat, Mar., 1932; and again, Dec., 1934; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, Aug., 1934; O.B.E., Nov., 1935; offg. resdt., Madras States, Nov., 1936; confd. as 2nd class resdt., Nov., 1938.

SLADE, MEAD, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr. of customs) (b. 25th Jan., 1894).—Educ. at Ilminster Sch., and University Coll., London; on mil. service from Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1923; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; and asst. to commr. of settlmt. and land records, May, 1925; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1927; confd., July, 1932; asst. collr., customs, Calcutta, April, 1928; offg. collr., ditto, May, 1930; ditto, Rangoon. Nov., 1932; ditto, Bombay, Feb., 1934; offg. jt. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of commerce, June, 1936; temp. jt. secty., ditto, Mar., 1937; member, council of state, Mar., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938; member, central bd. of rev. and ex-officio jt. secty. to the govt. of India, finance dept. (central revs.), Oct., 1938.

SLADEN, JOSEPH MAURICE, Indian C.S. (secty., home dept., Bombay) (b. 22nd Feb., 1896).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1920; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1920, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. asst. pte. sec. to govr., May to Nov., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. magte., and pol. agent, March, 1925; asst. commr. in Sind and Sindhi translator to govt., March, 1928; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; minister; Khairpur state, May, 1932; collr., Mar., 1936; secty., home dept., Bombay, Mar., 1938.

SLADEN, REGINALD JOHN LAMBERT, F.R.C.S., D.P.H., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 24th July, 1877).—Joined the service as dist. surg., G.I.P. Rly., 2nd May, 1902; offg. principal medl. and health officer, May, 1923, and again, Aug., 1925; services transferred to E.B. Rly., March, 1926; offg. ch. medl. officer, March, 1926; offg. prinpl. medl. and health officer, G.I.P. Rly., March, 1928; confd., July, 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

SLATER, ALFRED, M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 25th June, 1887).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd cl., Dec., 1909; D.M.A.G. (junr.), Mar., 1923; ditto (senr.), Feb., 1924; and again, Mar., 1929; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Feb., 1925; secty., army retrenchment ette., Dec., 1931; confd., as cl. I officer., Dec., 1929; confd. as cmd. contr., Nov., 1934.

SLATER, ALFRED FRANK MURRAY, Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Police Service (Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1882).—First commn. dated 4th May, 1901; in mil. employ prior to Aug., 1909; asst. supt., Aug., 1909; dist. supt., Nov., 1911; on mil. service from 20th Dec., 1914, to 15th March, 1916, and from 12th Nov., 1918, to 12th April, 1919; dep. inspr.-gen., Jan., 1924; ret'd., June, 1931.

SLATER, OWEN, M.C., Col. R.E., Survey of India (dir.) (b. 19th June, 1890).—First commn. dated 23rd July, 1910; asst. supt. (proby), 20th Apl., 1920; confd., Oct., 1922; supt., May, 1928; dir., May, 1939.

SLATER, RICHARD MERCER KEENE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 27th May, 1915).—Educ. at Eton, Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 13th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

SLATER, SAMUEL HENRY, C.M.G., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Jan., 1880).—Educ. at King Edward VII Gr. Sch., Louth, and Oxford Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., local and munpl. dept., May, 1910; ditto, pol. dept., May, 1910; under-sec. to govt. of India, dept. of comm. and ind., April, 1913; on special duty to S. Africa under comm. and ind. dep., Dec., 1913, to April, 1914; temply. employed in rev. and stat. dept., India Office, from May to Oct., 1916; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1916; temp. dep. sec., comm. and ind. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1916; spl. duty, comm. and industry dept., Dec., 1916, to June, 1917; on mil. duty from Feb., 1918, to March, 1919; finl. sec., Mesopotamian administration, March, 1919, to May, 1922; C.I.E., June, 1919; on foreign service under Iraq civil administration, Sept., 1922, to Feb., 1925; C.M.G., June, 1923; collr. and dist. mag., March, 1925; offg. sec. to govt., pub. wks. dept., May, 1926; commr. of labour, July, 1927; collr. and dist. magte., Apl., 1930; secty. to govt., Madras, dev. dept., Feb., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

SLATTERY, JOHN, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 26th June, 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1906; asst. supt., Nov., 1907; supt., Sept., 1914; asst. inspr.-gen., rly. police, Jan., 1924; O.B.E., June, 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Mar., 1928; officer. on spl. duty, central bd. of rev., May, 1929; dep.-inspr.-genl., Punjab (provl. subs), July, 1932; confd., June, 1933; offg. inspr.-genl., N.W.F.P., June, 1933; ret'd., May, 1937; chairman, pub. serv. commn., Punjab and N.W.F. Prov., May, 1937.

SLEEM, M., Barr. (advocate-general, Punjab) (b. 14th Jan., 1892).—Apptd. advocate-general, 11th Feb., 1938.

SLESSER, WILLIAM DUNCAN VIVIAN, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 28th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1908; asst. supt., Oct., 1909; on mil. duty from 24th Aug., 1916, to 24th March, 1919; supt., March, 1919.

SLOAN, TENNANT, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (b. 9th Nov., 1884).—Educ. at Glasgow Academy and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 25th Nov., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; under-sec. to govt. Jan., 1915; asst. supt., Dehra Dun. April, 1917; city mag., Lucknow, July, 1917; temp. addl. under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Sept., 1917; spl. duty, home dept., Nov., 1917; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., May, 1918; on deputn. to England, May to Oct., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., United Provs., March, 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1924; jt. sec., ditto, April, 1925; secty. to govt., rev. and public wks. dept. (B. and R.), U.P., March, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Home dept., May, 1932; jt. secty. to the Govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1933; offg. secty., ditto, June, 1935; C.S.I., Jan., 1936; settlement commr., Oct., 1936, to July, 1939; commr., Aug., 1939.

SLOCOMBE, CHARLES, late India Office (b. 16th Sept., 1872).—Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt. genl.'s dept., 22nd July, 1892; transfed. to Indo-European Telegraph Dept., June, 1900; staff clerk, May, 1917; clerk for special duties, March, 1920; re-transferred to acct.-genl.'s dept., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

SLOSS, DUNCAN JOHN, C.B.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 20th June, 1881).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1920; prof., Oct., 1920; princpl., University Coll., Rangoon, June, 1925; C.B.E., June, 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

SMALL, GERALD AGNEW, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., Assam) (b. 31st Aug., 1886).—Joined the service, 21st April, 1911; inspr. of schools, July, 1911; on mil. service from April, 1916, to May, 1920; offg. dir. public instrn., June, 1928; confd., Oct., 1931.

SMALLWOOD, ROBERT, Major, Indian Army (b. 25th Aug., 1893) (milty. accts. dept.).—Service in the Indian Army up to 25th July, 1935; joined milty. accts. dept. as an officer in Class II, July, 1935.

SMART, COLIN N. B., Indian State Railways (exec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 18th Feb., 1885).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1921; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1926; statistical officer, Mar., 1932.

SMART, ROBERT BROUGHTON, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 9th Apl., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 17th Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1932.

SMART, WILFRED WILMOT, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Jan., 1876).—Educ. at Dulwich Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 16th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. offr., settlmt. offr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1905, to April, 1907; collr. and dist. mag., Feb., 1920; actg. commr. in Sind and controller of prices in Sind, Sept., 1920; supt. of stamps,

Karachi, Feb., 1921; offg. commr., April, 1928; confd., Jan., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1935; retd., Nov., 1935.

SMELLIE, BENJAMIN ALEXANDER, B.A., LL.B., Central Provs. C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 26th Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr. on probn., 18th May, 1914; confd., July, 1915; ditto and under-secty. to Govt., May, 1928; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1930; confd., Feb., 1933.

SMITH, ARCHIBALD CLIFFORD, Indian Police (princl., p.t. school, Burma) (b. 9th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 7th Feb., 1921; dist. supt., June, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1937 princl., p.t. school, Dec., 1936.

SMITH, BERTRAM GILBERT, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 18th May, 1908; dist. traffic supt., Sept., 1917; on mil. service from 28th Feb., 1916, to 9th Oct., 1919; O.B.E., 3rd June, 1918; officer on sp. duty, Rly. Board, July, 1925; temp. dep. agent, E.I. Rly., Feb., 1926; offg. divl. supt., Mar., 1928; confd., Apl., 1929; retd., Mar., 1938.

SMITH, CECIL CURTIS HAROLD, Major, I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Aug., 1898).—First commn. dated 26th Nov., 1915; in milty. employ up to 5th April, 1925; asst. pol. agent, Chilas, July, 1926; asst. commr., Thal, Sept., 1930; asst. commr., Thal sub-divn., Apl., 1931; dep. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1932; pol. agent, Kurru, June, 1935; dep. commr., Peshawar, Mar., 1938.

SMITH, CHARLES HAROLD, O.B.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 19th June, 1883).—First commn., 31st July, 1909; O.B.E., June, 1919; offg. agency surg. on deputation to Rewa State as supt. medl. dept., from 18th Apl., 1922; agency surg., Bundelkhand, Jan., 1928; legation surg., Nepal, Apl., 1929; retd., Mar., 1938.

SMITH, DANIEL RICHARD, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Jan., 1897).—First commn. dated 4th June, 1915; in milty. employ prior to May, 1923; asst. pol. agent, Bahrein, June, 1924; vice-consul, Duzdap, Nov., 1924; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent in Kalat, Feb., 1927; pol. agent, Chagai, May, 1929; British trade agent at Gyantse and asst. to pol. offr. in Sikkim, Sept., 1929; under-secy. to agent to govr.-genl., Rajputana, March, 1930; officer on special duty, Mirpir, June, 1932; pte. secty. to Maharajah of Rewa, Oct., 1932; pol. agent, Harasti and Tonk, Oct., 1933; asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Mar., 1934; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha Agency, Oct., 1937; pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi, Nov., 1938.

SMITH, EDWIN CHARLES ALLAN, L.R.F.P.S., L.R.C.P. & S., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Bombay) (b. 16th Nov., 1887).—First commn., 1st July, 1921; offg. asst. dir. of public health, Rangoon, Aug., 1926; offg. supt., central mental hosp., Yeravda, Nov., 1928; confd., Aug., 1929.

SMITH, EDWIN DENSHAM, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1906; supt., June, 1920; offg. asst. inspr.-genl., rly. police, June 1931.

SMITH, EGERTON, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 7th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 25th Jan., 1910; prof., Dacca Coll., Jan., 1910; prin., Krishnagar Coll. Oct., 1923; retd., July, 1930.

SMITH, ERIC CONRAN, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 3rd Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Corpus Christi, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; temp. empd. in judl. and public dept., India Office, from April, to June, 1919; pte. sec. to govr. of Madras, April, 1921; C.I.E., June, 1924; on foreign service as commr. for Corp. of Madras, Oct., 1928; secty., local self-govt. dept., Madras, Oct., 1931; collr. (provl. subs.), Feb., 1933; addl. jt. secty. to govt. of India in the reforms office, Oct., 1934; collr., Dec., 1934; offg. pte. secty. to Viceroy, June, 1935; member, council of state, Apl., to May, 1936; jt. secty., govt. of India, reforms office, Mar., 1937.

SMITH, FRANCIS ARTHUR, Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supdt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 12th Jan., 1903).—Asst. loco. supdt., Jan., 1926; dist. loco. supdt., July, 1938.

SMITH, FRANCIS HERBERT, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 31st July, 1876).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 25th March, 1910; junr. chaplain, March, 1913, senr. chaplain, March, 1923; retd., June, 1930.

SMITH, FRANCIS WILLIAM HEAD, C.I.E., India Office (sec., services and general dept.) (b. 18th Aug., 1886).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. junior clerk, India Office, 2nd Nov., 1909, and attached to fin. dept.; resdt. clerk, Dec., 1911, to Dec., 1912; asst. sec. to Rl. Commn. on Indian fin. and currency, May, 1913, to Feb., 1914; reading clerk to council, July, 1913, to April, 1914; transf. to public works dept., April, 1914; senr. clerk, May, 1917; services temporarily lent to military dept., Dec., 1914; actg. asst. sec., public works dept., Jan., 1920; confd., March, 1921; actg. joint sec., military dept., May, 1923; dep. clerk of the Council, April, 1924; sec. to Rl. Commn. on Agric. in India, 1926-28; actg. secty., services and general dept., 1st Dec., 1928; confd., June, 1929; C.I.E., March, 1929.

SMITH, FRANK LOVELL, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Perse School, Cambridge, and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. supt. of census opers., U.P., Mar., 1931; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935.

SMITH, FREDERICK WILLIAM JAMES, Officer of High Commr. for India (executive officer, store dept.) (b. 25th Dec., 1893).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Store Depot, 6th Aug., 1914; on mil. service from Jan., 1916, to Feb., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

SMITH, G. CORLEY, M.B.E., Commerce Dept., Govt. of India (asst. secty.) (b. 12th June 1895).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1913; supt., commerce dept., Nov., 1928; offg. asst. secty., commerce dept., Sept., 1930; confd., June, 1932; offg. admin. officer, central p.w. d., Apl., 1933; and again, Apl., 1935; asst. secty.,

comm. dept., Nov., 1935; officer on special duty in Bombay Customs House, Dec., 1938, to April, 1939.

SMITH, GEOFFREY WILLIAM JOHN, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 30th March, 1913).—Joined the service as proxy, asst. supt., Assam, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; asst. to dep. commr., Naga Hills, Sept., 1937, to March, 1939.

SMITH, Ven. GEORGE CECIL AUGUSTUS, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estab. (b. 15th Dec., 1876).—Joined the service as chaplain, 26th Dec., 1905; senr. chaplain, 26th Dec., 1918; archdeacon, Feb., 1928; ret'd., July, 1931.

SMITH, GEORGE McIVER CAMPBELL, C.M.G., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 13th May, 1869). First commn. dated 27th July, 1892; apptd. med. adviser, Patiala State, June, 1903; offg. civil surg'n., Punjab, Feb., 1904; confd., June, 1909; temply. reverted to mil. duty, Jan., 1916; C.M.G., June, 1919; ret'd., Sept., 1921; addl. member, medl. bd., India Office, Dec., 1928; member, ditto, Jan., 1931 to May, 1934.

SMITH, HARRY PERCIVAL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. 26th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 22nd March, 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1923; on deputation to Andamans from Jan., 1926, to May, 1929; p.a. to consvr. of forests, Assam, Oct., 1935.

SMITH, Sir HENRY MONCRIEFF, Kt., C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Dec., 1873).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., April, 1907; addl. sess. judge, July, 1908; sess. and sub-judge, June, 1913; offg. sec. to govt., April, 1914; attaché, legislative dept., govt. of India, from Sept., 1915; dep. sec. to govt. of India, legislative dept., Oct., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, and ditto, Jan., 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; joint sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Jan., 1920; sec. to ditto, Jan., 1921; Kt., Jan., 1923; president, council of state, Dec., 1924, to Dec., 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

SMITH, Sir HENRY SCOTT, Kt., Indian C.S. (ret'd.).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Stratford-on-Avon, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 4th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. judge, June, 1894; divl. judge, March, 1902; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1913; temp. addl. judge, chief ct., Punjab, Jan., 1914; judge, do., April, 1915; judge, high ct., Punjab, April, 1919; Kt., June, 1924; actg. chief justice, high ct., Punjab, June, 1924; ret'd., May, 1925.

SMITH, HENRY WRAY SANDES, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th April, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. supt. engr., Sept., 1926; confd., July, 1929; ret'd., April, 1939.

SMITH, HERBERT CECIL, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 27th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st Dec., 1915; dep. consvr., Jan., 1922; offg. consvr., Feb., 1936.

SMITH, HERBERT ST. CLAIR, M.B.E., late Bombay C.S. (b. 9th June, 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 5th Sept., 1898, to 22nd Aug., 1908; dep. collr., Aug., 1908; offg. dep.

sec. to govt., dev. dept., etc., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. sec. to govt., dev. dept., May, 1925; ret'd., June, 1935; M.B.E., Jan., 1936.

SMITH, IAN WILLIAM KNIGHTON, B.A., B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Oct., 1882).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 4th Sept., 1909; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdt. engr., Dec., 1923; offg. supt., way and works, May, 1925; offg. dep. ch. engr., March, 1927; dep. ch. engr. (provl.), July, 1929; confd., Nov., 1931; offg. ch. engr., June, 1935; confd., August, 1935; ret'd., Sept., 1939.

SMITH, Sir JAMES COWLISHAW, Kt., C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 14th April, 1873).—Educ. at Derby, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896; and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settmt. officer; joint mag., Nov., 1905; dep. commr., Dec., 1913; mag. and collr., Nov., 1914; C.I.E., June, 1920; offg. commr., June, 1922; confd., Dec., 1924; offg. member, bd. of rev., Dec., 1928; confd. July, 1930; temp. member, govt.'s exec. council, United Provs., Oct., 1930, and again, July, 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1931; Kt., Jan., 1932.

SMITH, JOHN EDWARD, Indian Police (b. 14th Oct., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 14th Dec., 1927; offg. supt., May, 1934.

SMITH, Sir JOSEPH BENJAMIN GEORGE, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Feb., 1878).—Joined the service, 18th Aug., 1898; asst. engr. (provl. service), Aug., 1899; exec. engr., July, 1910; supt. engr., Nov., 1920; chief engr. and sec. to govt., P.W.D., irrigation branch, March, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1927; Kt., June, 1932; ret'd., Feb., 1933.

SMITH, LESLIE EVAN, late Indian Forest Service (b. 28th July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Dec., 1929; ret'd., Feb., 1936.

SMITH, NORMAN PERCIVAL ARTHUR, Indian Police (dep. commr., Bombay) (b. 30th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service, Nov., 1912; supt. of police, June, 1924; offg. dep. dir., intel. bureau, June, 1933; offg. dep. inspr. genl., Nov., 1935; cent. intell. offcr., Bombay Presdy., Mar., 1936; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938; dep. commr. police, Bombay, Aug., 1938.

SMITH, RALPH JEANRENAUD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 7th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1923, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

SMITH, RICHARD HUGH, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 22nd July, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

SMITH, Rev. RICHARD MARMADUKE LANGDALE, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 29th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1915; junr. chaplain, Madras, 1st Apl., 1918; senr. chaplain, 1st Apl., 1928; ret'd., Sept., 1938.

SMITH, ROBERT GEORGE, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th July, 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 8th Feb., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933.

SMITH, SAMUEL McLEOD, late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 16th Jan., 1879).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1903; dep. mag. and collr.,

Apl., 1907; diwan, Gangpur State, Apl., 1921; ditto, Mayurbhanj State, Jan., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1929; magte. and collr., Mar., 1931; retd. Jan., 1934.

SMITH, VICTOR WILLIAM, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 6th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, Nov., 1911; asst. supt., May, 1913; O.B.E., Jan., 1921; supt., Nov., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; on spl. duty, Home Dept., from Nov., 1925; pers. asst. to genl., inspr.-Punjab, Nov., 1926; retd. Aug., 1933.

SMITH, WALTER ROBERT GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian Police (commr. of police, Bombay) (b. 5th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1908, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, Mar., 1921; dep. commr. of police, Bombay, Feb., 1932; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1932; offg. commr. of police, Bombay, July, 1933; confd., Apl., 1935; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1933; C.I.E., May, 1937.

SMITH, WILFRED ARTHUR, O.B.E., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 30th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 11th May, 1905; confd., Jan., 1907; on field postal service on N.W. frontier, May, 1919, to Aug., 1920; offg. dep. P.M.G., July, 1928; confd., Feb., 1929; dep. P.M.G., Madras, Apl., 1933; retd., July, 1937.

SMITH, WILLIAM ERLAM, M.A. (Oxon), Indian Educl. Service (prof. of chemistry, presdy. coll., Madras) (b. 16th July, 1881).—Apptd. acting prof. of natural sciences, Rangoon Coll., 3rd Sept., 1906; prof. of chemistry, presdy. coll., Madras, Oct., 1907; on mil. service, from Sept., 1915, to March, 1920; offg. dir. of public instrn., Madras, Jan., 1930, and again, Nov., 1932.

SMITH, WILLIAM FREDERICK, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 19th Jan., 1903; asst. engr., Jan., 1923; apptd. to the I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1927; exec. engr., Jan., 1932; retd., May, 1934.

SMITH, WILLIAM ROSE, B.Sc., Indian State Railways (exec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 19th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 17th Sept., 1909; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Mar., 1923; exec. engr., May, 1932.

SMITH-PEARSE, THOMAS LAWRENCE HOWARTH, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (principal, Rajkumar Coll., Raipur) (b. 15th July, 1893).—Educ. at Marlborough and St. John's Coll., Oxford; vice-principal, Rajkumar Coll., Raipur, Feb., 1921; prinpl., ditto, July, 1931.

SMITHERMAN, FRANK, Indian Police (asst. supt., Burma) (b. 13th Oct., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Burma, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

SMYTH, ARCHIBALD ADAMS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 12th June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Madras, Sept., 1925; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1931; confd., Sept., 1934; awarded K.-i.-H. medal, cl. II, Jan., 1936.

SMYTH, DAVID ALEXANDER, C.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Central Provs.) (b. 24th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, Dec., 1907; asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Feb., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan.,

1925; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., June, 1926; confd., June, 1928; offg. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1933; and May, 1935; C.B.E., June, 1935.

SMYTH, JOHN WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th May, 1880).—Educ. at Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904 and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. commr., Upper Sind frontier, July, 1911; asst. commr. Sind, and Sindh translator to govt., Sept., 1912; dep. commr., Upper Sind Frontier, July, 1916; offg. collr. and district mag., Oct., 1921; ditto and pol. agent, Feb., 1922; collr. and dist. mag., March, 1926; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Oct., 1926; member, legisl. council, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ch. commr., Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Dec., 1931; commr., Poona, June, 1936; C.S.I., May, 1937; retd., Nov., 1939.

SMYTH, Sir SAMUEL ANDREW, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 18th March, 1877).—Educ. at London-derry Sch., and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 10th Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Nov., 1905, to Nov., 1908; dep. commr., Feb., 1915; on spl. duty in sectt.; offg. sec. to govt., Sept., 1917; offg. chief sec. to govt., Nov. to Dec., 1917; offg. commr., May, 1919; C.S.I., Jan., 1924; commr. (s.p.t.), Aug., 1923; confd., May, 1924; offg. finl. commr., March, 1925; confd., April, 1926; member, exec. council, Burma, May, 1928; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1931; retd., March, 1931; delegate to Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931.

SMYTH, SAMUEL, Major, I.M.S. (b. 10th June, 1899).—Date of first commn., 4th Aug., 1927; apptd. civil surgeon (offg.), Apl., 1934; O.S.D., Mayo Hosp., Lahore, Mar., 1937.

SMYTHIES, EVELYN ARTHUR, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (chief consvr., United Provs.) (b. 19th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1908, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Jan., 1914; offg. consr., April, 1926; confd., April, 1929; offg. chief consvr., Dec., 1937; confd., Feb., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1939.

SNADDEN, WILLIAM GEORGE, late Indian Police (b. 23rd Apl., 1889).—Apptd. dist. supt., Burma, 1st Dec., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; retd., June, 1936.

SNELL, OF PLUMSTEAD, Baron, HENRY SNELL, C.B.E. Parly. under-secty. of State for India, Mar.-Aug., 1931; delegate to Indian round table conf., 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931.

SNELSON, EDWARD ALEC ABBOTT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Central Provinces) (b. 31st Oct., 1904).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School, Caius Coll., Cambridge, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 9th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; sub-judge, Dec., 1931.

SNOW, CHARLES ALFRED, M.A. (Oxon) late Indian Educational Service (Burma) (b. 31st July, 1879).—Arrived in India, 1st Feb., 1905; on mil. service, April, 1915, to May, 1919; asst. dir. of public instruction, May, 1921; dir. of public instrn., Aug., 1923; retd. July, 1931.

SOAMES, GEOFFREY EWART, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Jan., 1881).—Educ. at Eastbourne Coll., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1905, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., fin. and munpl. dept., Nov., 1909; under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., May to Nov., 1911; under-sec. to chief commr., Assam, April, 1912; asst. commr., Assam, Aug., 1915; dep. commr., Jan., 1921; second sec. to govt. of Assam, April, 1923; offg. chief sec. to govt. of Assam, Feb., 1925; confd., Oct., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1927; ch. secty. to govt. of Assam, Oct., 1931; ret'd., June, 1933.

SOARES, ALEXANDER DUARANTE, M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Rlys. (dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 25th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service, 8th June, 1923; dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1929.

SOBHAN, KONDKAR FAZLE (see Khondkar Fazle Sobhan).

SODHI, HARBHAJAN SINGH, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 24th May, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

SODHI, NARENDRA SINGH, M.C., L.R.C.P., D.T.M., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 9th Aug., 1882).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1905; in mil. employ till June, 1910, when he was apptd. asst. plague med. offr., Punjab; dist. plague med. offr., July, 1910; plague med. offr., June, 1911; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914, to July, 1918; civil surgeon, Punjab, 1921; dep. inspr. of civil hosp., Nov., 1925; offg. inspr.-genl., Punjab, Nov., 1933; ditto, Burma, April, 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; ret'd., Aug., 1939.

SOE, MAUNG AUNG, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 27th Mar., 1906).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty., fin. dept., Burma, April, 1936; dep. commr., Mar., 1938.

SOHONI, SHRIDDHAR VASUDEV, B.A., Indian C.S. (assist. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 18th May, 1914); educ. at Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 8th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

SOHONI, VISHVANATH VISHNU, B.A., M.Sc. (Bombay), Meteorological Dept. (meteorologist) (b. 6th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service as meteorologist, 12th Mar., 1922.

SOKHEY, SAHISBINGH, M.A., B.Sc., M.D. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 15th Dec., 1887).—Joined, 26th July, 1913; in milty. employ to 7th Aug., 1925; apptd. supernumerary offr., Haffkine Inst., Bombay, Aug., 1925; offg. asst. dir., ditto, Feb., 1926; offg. dir., ditto, March, 1928; and again, Oct., 1932.

SOLOMAN, SAMUEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 20th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Clifton College, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1933; offg. publicity officer, Bihar and Orissa Mar., 1935.

SOLOMON, WILLIAM EWART GLADSTONE, late Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th Mar., 1880).—Apptd. princpl. and decorative artist and archtl. sculptor, Sir J. J. School of Art and Industries, Bombay, 25th Nov., 1919; designation changed to dir., ditto, Apl., 1929; 1st class K-i-H. medal, Jan., 1924; ret'd., Mar., 1937.

SOM NATH, KAPUR LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 10th Oct., 1908).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 17th July, 1931; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., July, 1932.

SOMAYRA, BODUGURU.—Apptd. Judge High Court, Madras, Feb., 1939.

SOMAYYA, SRI BODUGURU, Judge of the High Court, Madras.—Apptd., Jan., 1939.

SOMJEE, MOHOMEDBHOY ALLADINBHOY, (judge, high ct., Bombay).—Apptd. addtl. judge, Mar., 1937; judge, Dec., 1938.

SONAM WANGFEL LADEN LA, Sardar Bahadur (see Lal Lal S. W.).

SONDHI, GURU DATT, B.A. (Cantab), M.A. (Pb.), Barr., Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th Dec., 1890).—Apptd. prof. of economics, govt. coll., Lahore, 17th Sept., 1917.

SONDHI, RAM LALL (see Ram Lall, S.).

SONDHI, SUNDAR DAS, M.C., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Punjab (supr.) (b. 25th Apl., 1890).—First commn. dated 9th Nov., 1917; in milty. employ prior to 25th March, 1927; supr., April, 1927.

SOOD, MANOHAR LAL (see Manohar Lal Sood).

SOOFI, MAHMOOD ALI, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 20th Mar., 1901).—Educ. at Punjab Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, variously from May, 1932; confd., Nov., 1936.

SORBY, WILLIAM JOHN WOODCRAFT, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mech. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Apl., 1893).—Asst. loco. supr., junr. scale, 2nd Oct., 1920; divl. mech. engr., snrr. scale, Oct., 1936.

SORLEY, HERBERT TOWER, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., genl. and educn. depts., Bombay) (b. 12th April, 1892).—Educ. at Grammar School, Aberdeen, Aberdeen University, Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 9th Dec., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dep. commr. of salt and excise, June, 1923; criminal tribes settlt. officer, Bombay Presy. (offg.), March, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1926; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; offg. collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Feb., 1929; offg. prov. supr. of census, Bombay presyd.; collr., Apl., 1933; secty. to govt., genl. and educn. depts., Bombay, Mar., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1939.

SOTHERS, DONALD BEVAN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Sind) (b. 11th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1912, as asst. consvr., Bombay; on duty as asst. censor from 2nd Dec., 1914, to 11th Jan., 1919; I.A.R.O., from 23rd April, 1915; asst. contr., famine fodder, Jan. to April, 1919; dep. consvr., April, 1919; offg. consvr., May, 1928; and again, March, 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; offg. ch. consvr., Bombay, June, 1935; tranfd. to Sind, Nov., 1938.

SOUSA, AUGUSTUS, F.R.C.S., F.S.Sc. D.P.H., late Public Health dept., United Provs. (b. 3rd Oct., 1875).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1914; dep. santy. commr., Lucknow, Feb., 1917; dir. of epidemiology, Oct., 1921; asst. dir. of public health, Aug., 1922; Kaisar-i-Hind Medal, 1st Class, Jan., 1924; retd., Apl., 1931.

SOUTER, SIR CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th June, 1877).—Educ. at Arbroad High Sch., Edinburgh Univ., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 25th Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. sec. to govt., May, 1906; under-sec., rev. dept., March, 1909; sub-collr. and jt. mag., May, 1910; offg. commr., Coorg, June to Dec., 1916; commr. of Coorg, Dec., 1918, to Dec., 1923; collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1924; offg. secty. to govt., public wks. dept., March, 1928; 3rd member, bd. of rev. (provl. perm.), Mar., 1930; offg. 2nd ditto, May, 1930; confd., Aug., 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; 1st member, bd. of rev. (provl. subs.), Oct., 1933; member govrs. exec. council, Madras, Jan., 1935; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; retd., Apl., 1937.

SOUTHERN, REV. GERALD HOLTE BRACE-BRIDGE, M.A., late Indian Eccles Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 1st Nov., 1889).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 11th May, 1922; junr. chaplain, May, 1924; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1932; retd., Feb., 1938.

SOUTHORN, ANDREW HERBERT, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 2nd Feb., 1909).—Educated at St. Edwards School, Oxford, and Hertford College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Sept., 1934.

SPACKMAN, WILLIAM COLLIS, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Bombay) (b. 23rd Sept., 1889).—First commn. 26th July, 1913; offg. civil surg., March, 1924; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. prof., G.M. Coll., and supt., Bai Motlibai and Sir D. M. Petit hospitals, Bombay, July, 1927, Mar., 1930, and again, Nov., 1932.

SPARGO, FREDERICK WILSON, B.A., B.Sc., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sessions judge, Burma) (b. 30th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at William Ellis Sch., Gospel Oak, London, London Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 10th Dec., 1912, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service, Nov., 1917, to Oct., 1919; offg. dist. and sessions judge, March, 1923; confd., Dec., 1923; offg. judge, high ct. Rangoon, Mar. 1936; and again, Nov., 1936.

SPARKE, HARRY CHARLES, late Indian State Railways (b. 6th Sept., 1877).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1897; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1899; dist. supt., April, 1907; on special duty with the rly. board, Aug., 1914, to Jan., 1915; offg. dep. agent, N.W. rly., April, 1916; on milty. duty with I.A.R.O. from Feb., 1917, to March, 1918 (temp. lt.-col., Feb., 1917); dep. traffic manager, N.W. rly., Aug., 1917; temp. junr. dep. agent, ditto, April, 1918; offg. traffic manager, E. Ben. Ry., March, 1919; confd., Nov., 1924; offg. dir. traffic, rly. board, April, 1926; member, rly. rates advisory comtee., May, 1928; retd., Feb., 1933.

SPARKE, WALTER, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 14th Sept., 1899; asst. engr., Jan., 1901; dist. engr., May, 1911; retd., Nov., 1934.

SPEECHLY, ALBERT GEORGE, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 10th Jan., 1881).—Held non-gazetted posts from 1st Feb., 1898; on depn. with E. Persian Cordon F.F., Sept., 1918, to May, 1920; ch. supt., Calcutta tel. office, Oct., 1932; retd., Jan., 1936.

SPEECHLEY, EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th July, 1880).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 16th April, 1910; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdt. engr., Feb., 1920; exec. engr., March, 1926; retd., Apl., 1936.

SPENCE, SIR GEORGE HEMMING, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec., govt. of India, legisl. dept.) (b. 6th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Oxford, apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1917; addl. dist. mag., Delhi, Jan. to Nov., 1918; temp. under-sec. to govt., May, 1919; spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, from June, 1919; under-sec. to govt. of India, reforms office, Dec., 1919; addl. dep. sec. to govt. of India, legislative dept., Dec., 1920; offg. dep. sec., ditto, April, 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; secty., council of state, Nov., 1933; and Jan., 1935; offg. secty. to the govt. of India, legisl. dept., June, 1934, and again, Nov., 1935; confd., Apl., 1936; secty. to the Govr.-genl.'s exec. council, in addn., Aug., 1936; C.S.I., Feb., 1937; Kt., June, 1939.

SPENCER, ARDESHIR BARJORJI, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Nov., 1879).—Joined the service, 30th April, 1903; asst. engr., April, 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., Aug., 1926, and July, 1927; suptg. engr. (provl. perm.), Dec., 1931; confd., Nov., 1932; retd., Nov., 1934.

SPENCER, FRANK, M.B.E. (revision offcr., govt. of India, def. dept.) (b. 26th Sept., 1888).—Served with reg. army from 18th Nov., 1908, to 11th Mar., 1924; held minis. appts. at A.H.Q. up to Dec., 1930; offcr. supervisor, general staff branch, A.H.Q. (offg.), Jan., 1931; confd., Mar., 1931; M.B.E., Jan., 1932; apptd. revision offcr., def. dept., Apl., 1938.

SPENCER, JOHN EDWARD, late Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., 4th Dec., 191; confd., May, 1914; supt., Sept., 1923; offg. asst. to inspr.-genl., March, 1927; confd., Nov., 1927; retd., Aug., 1934.

SPILLER, THOMAS REUBEN, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 9th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1920; prinpl., New Coll., Patna, Oct., 1923; headmaster, Zila Sch., June, 1927; offg. prinpl., Patna training coll., variously from May, 1933; inspr. of sch., Bihar and Orissa, variously from Apl., 1933; headmaster, Ranchi Zila Sch., again, Oct., 1933; principal, Patna training coll., April, 1937.

SPILSBURY, LIONEL BRUCE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Sept., 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. engr., United Provs.; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; offg. suptg. engr., Nov., 1921; confd., Aug., 1923; retd., July, 1933.

SPITTELER, ARTHUR ERNEST, Indian Police (b. 29th Oct., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supi., Madras, on probn., 24th Jan., 1936.

SPRATT, NOEL NEWTON, India Office (higher clerical officer, mil. dept.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1891).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 9th March, 1914; on mil. service from Nov., 1916, to July, 1919; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

SPRAWSON, Sir CUTHBERT ALLEN, Kt., C.I.E., M.D., M.R.C.P., Maj.-Genl., I.M.S., retd., (b. 1st Mar., 1877).—First commn. dated 28th June, 1900; prof. of physiology, King George's Medl. Coll., Lucknow, July, 1911; prof. of medicine, ditto, July, 1913; on mil. duty from June, 1917; C.I.E., March, 1919; principal, King George's Medl. Coll., Lucknow, Nov., 1921; prof., ditto, Oct., 1925; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Mar., 1929; surg.-genl. with govt. of Madras, Apl., 1930; apptd. hon. physician to the King, Feb., 1933; offg. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Oct., 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; presdt., medl. council of India, Feb., 1934; member, council of state, Apl., 1936; served on N.W. Frontier of India, Waziristan, 1901-02, medal and clasp; knighted, June, 1936; retd., Mar., 1937.

SPRAY, WILLIAM ALFRED, Indian State Ry's. (supt. of collieries) (b. 27th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 13th Aug., 1919; offg. variously as colliery manager from Oct., 1919; confd., Sept., 1926; supt. of collieries, July, 1938.

SPRINGFIELD, ERNEST, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 3rd Nov., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 7th Dec., 1922; offg. supt., May, 1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; supt., July, 1934.

SPURGEON, CHRISTOPHER EDWARD, late Indian State Railways (b. 24th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1904, as asst. loco. supt., state ry's.; dist. loco. supt., Apl., 1911; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; offg. dep. chief mechanical engr., Feb., 1926; offg., supt., mechanical workshop, N.W. Ry., Oct., 1926; offg. ch. mechanical engr., Nov., 1927; confd., Feb., 1929; retd., May, 1934.

SQUIRE, GILES FREDERICK, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 12th Oct., 1894).—Educ. at Marlborough and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, on probation as first asst. to resdt. of Baroda, April, 1924; confd., March, 1925; sec. to agent to gov.-genl. in Central India, April, 1926; under-sec. to agent to gov.-genl. in Central India, Nov., 1926; secty. to agent to gov.-genl., Punjab States, Dec., 1927; ditto, W.I. States, Oct., 1929; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Nov., 1933; pol. agent, Kalat, Apl., 1934; ditto, Sibi, Dec., 1935; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Mar., 1936; consul genl. for Khorasan, Nov., 1936.

SQUIRES, HOWARD JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 3rd Sept., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 21st Oct., 1928; exec. engr., Oct., 1937.

SREEKANTESWARAN, KOLAR VENKATSWAMIER, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th Mar., 1903).—Joined the service, 8th Mar., 1926; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Apl., 1929; curr. offic., Bombay, Dec., 1933; dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, June, 1935; accts.

offcr., E.I. rly., Apl., 1936; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. Rly., Nov., 1938; examiner, outside audit dept., Bombay, March, 1939.

SRIHARI RAO NAYUDU, BANDI VENKATA, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.C., Registration Dept., Madras (inspr.-genl.) (b. 19th Feb., 1887).—Held non-gazetted appt., offg. dep. collr., Feb., 1920; confd., May, 1923; offg. 3rd presdy. magte., Apl., 1928; offg. 4th ditto, July, 1928; offg. asst. secty., bd. of rev., May, 1929; 3rd presdy. magte., Mar., 1930; 2nd ditto, Oct., 1930; inspr.-genl. of registration, Feb., 1931.

SRINARAYAN, RAI BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 3rd Mar., 1894).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1916; asst. engr., Sept., 1917; personal asst. to ch. engr. and under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1925; exec. engr., Jan., 1926; services placed at disposal of inspr.-gen. of police, from Apl., 1926, to July, 1929; on depn. to Improvement Trust Cawnpore, as ch. engr., Mar., 1932; as exec. officer, Jan., 1936, to Feb., 1939; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1934.

SRINIVASA RAO, CADAPANATHAM, RAO BAHADUR, M.A., late India Posts and Tels. (Madras) (b. 1875).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 1st Aug., 1898; supt. Feb., 1904; dep. postmaster-genl., Aug., 1920; retd., July, 1930.

SRINIVASA RAO, CHIKBALLAPUR VENKATA, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service, 17th March, 1914; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Nov., 1915; asst. compt., Assam, Dec., 1915; asst. compt., India treasuries, Nov., 1916; currency officer, Rangoon, May, 1919; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, July, 1919; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Madras, Jan., 1924; on spl. duty in fin. dept., March, 1925; offg. dep. contr. of rly. accts., Mar., 1933; addl dep. dir. (fin.) rly. bd., Jan., 1935; offg. finl. adviser, posts and tels., May, 1935; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, Burma ry's., Mar., 1936; offg. ch. ditto, Apl., 1936; on for. service as acct.-genl., Burma, Apl., 1937.

SRINAVASAN, KALYANA CHENNAKESAVA, M.A., F.C.S., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 26th Apl., 1896).—Joined the service, 24th March, 1921; Asst. Auditor, E.B. Rly., Aug., 1922; on depn. with Govt. of India in connection with accounts for dairy farms, Feb., 1923; Secty., rly. rates advisory ctee., May, 1926; divl. audit officer, Karachi, June, 1928; Govt. exr. of accts., B.N. Ry., Mar., 1929; dy. ch. auditor, G.I.P. Ry., May, 1930; dy. govt. exr., B.B. and C.I. Ry., Apl., 1931; on spl. duty under contr. of rly. accts., 1936; dep. ch. accts. offr., Jan., 1939; dep. dir. of finance, April, 1939; offr. on special duty, Rly. Board, June, 1939.

SRINIVASAN, KUPPUSWAMI, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 1st May, 1907).—Educ. at University Coll., London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Jan., 1934.

SRINAVASAN, MANDAYAM MADUMBAL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 15th Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 10th Nov., 1931.

SRINIVASAN, SUBRAMANIA IYER, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th June, 1903).—Joined the service, 15th Mar., 1926; currency

officer, Karachi, Sept., 1932; asst. acct. genl., Madras, May, 1935; examiner, local fund accts., Central Provs., Mar., 1937; dept. acct. genl., Central Provs., July, 1938; accts. officer, G.I.P. Rly., Mar., 1938.

SRIRAMULU NAYUDU, BUNDY, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 4th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1907; promoted to I.P. as dist. supt., Mar., 1932.

SRIVASTAV, JASODA NAND, Rai Sahib, P.M.S., Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. Dec., 1884).—Civil surg., Apl., 1928.

SRIVASTAVA, RAM CHANDRA, O.B.E., B.Sc., Industries Dept., United Provs. (dep. dir.) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Apptd. dep. dir. of industries, United Provs., 1st Sept., 1928; services placed at disposal of Impl. Council of Agricultural Research, for employment as sugar technologist, July, 1930; dir., Impl. Inst. of sugar technology, Cawnpore, Oct., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1938.

SRIVASTAVA, SHYAM MOHAN, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (it. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 13th July, 1910).—Educ. at K.P. Intermediate College, Allahabad, University of Allahabad and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1934; arrived in India Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr., jt. magte., Nov., 1936.

STACE, RALPH EDWARD, Lt.-Col., R.E. (ret'd.) (b. 9th Dec., 1881).—Apptd. dep. mint master, April, 1919; offg. mint master, Calcutta, April to Nov., 1920, and Nov., 1921, to April, 1922 junior mint master, Bombay, May, 1923; mint master, ditto, July, 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

STAFFORD, PAUL, Indian Police (b. 12th Dec., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on proba., Nov., 1938.

STAGG, MONTAGU, Col., R.E., O.B.E., (mint master, Calcutta) (b. 4th Nov., 1885).—Apptd. dep. mint master, Bombay, 11th May, 1920; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; dep. mint master, Calcutta, March, 1921; offg. mint master, Calcutta, from April, 1921; confd., Feb., 1926.

STAGGS, MALCOLM JAMES ALLAN (asst. secty. to govt. of India, defence dept.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1900); joined the service, June, 1919; apptd. asst. secty. to the govt. of India, defence dept. (offg.), Mar., 1939; confd., July, 1939.

STAIG, BERTIE MUNRO, C.S.I., Indian C.S., attd. Indian Finance Dept. (finl. commr. rlys.) (b. 14th Aug., 1892).—Educ. at St. Andrews, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, 18th Dec., 1917, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., May, 1918, to January, 1919; offg. under-sec., govt. of Bengal, pol. and appts. dept., March, 1921; ditto, fin. dept., Nov., 1921; transfd. to Indian audit and accts. list, Nov., 1922; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, May, 1924; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1924; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1925; finl. advr., P.W.D., and jt. sec. to govt. of Punjab, fin. dept., March, 1926; secty., hydro-electric branch enquiry commee., Oct., 1929; secty. to govt., P.W.D. (electricity), Punjab, Apl., 1933; addl. finl. adviser, milty. finance, July, 1934; finl. adviser, milty. finance, Mar., 1935; also relief commr., Baluchistan and jt. secty., govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1935; C.S.I., June, 1936; on spl. duty in for.

and pol. dept., Sept., 1936; member, frontier watch and ward cttee., Nov., 1936; finl. commr. rlys., Apl., 1937.

STAINTON, VERNON BRUCE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., home dept., govt. of Punjab) (b. 31st Mar., 1904).—Educ. at Harrow, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1932, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., Nov., 1936; dep. secty. to govt., home dept., Nov., 1937.

STALEY, HENRY SAMBLE, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (inspr. of schools, Central Provs.) (b. 20th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 27th March, 1915; inspr. of schools, April, 1916; offg. dep. dir. of public instrn., April, 1926; offg. prinpl., Spence training coll., Jubbulpore, July, 1934.

STALLARD, PHILIP LECHMERE, M.A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 20th May, 1880).—Joined the service as dist. surg., 14th May, 1909; offg. principal medl. and health officer, March, 1927; dist. medl. officer, April, 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

STALLARD, ROBERT HUMPHRY, O.B.E., Major, R.E. (ret'd.), late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., July, 1910; on milty. duty from 22nd Oct., 1914, to 29th Nov., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; exec. engr., Dec., 1919; offg. dep. agent, E.B. Rly., Oct., 1926; on foreign service with M. & S. M. rly. as dep. agent, April, 1929; etd., June, 1930.

STALLARD, WILLIAM LEONARD O'BRIAN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 29th Aug., 1908).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 28th Dec., 1928; confd. Apl., 1930; A.D.C. to govr., Orissa, Apl., 1936.

STAMPE, SIR WILLIAM LEONARD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 31st Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; profir., civil engrg. coll., Roorkee, Nov., 1920, to Oct., 1924; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1925; confd., Apl., 1926; K.I.H., medal, 1st Class, Jan., 1926; offg. ch. engr. and jt. sec. to govt., P.W. dept., I.B., Apl., 1927; and again, Mar., 1931; confd., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; Knighted, Jan., 1935; ret'd. Oct. 1937.

STAMPER, THOMAS HENRY GILBORN, C.I.E., F.S.I., M.C. (b. 27th Oct., 1884).—Apptd. asst. consultg. surveyor to govt., Bombay, Apl., 1920; offg. consulting surveyor, Feb., 1926; confd., June, 1930; member, legisl. council, Oct., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1938.

STANDEN, SIR BERTRAM PRIOR, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Uppingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 5th Dec., 1888, and served in Burma and the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, Nagpur, March, 1891, to March, 1892; settlt. officer, Dec., 1894; dep. commr., Aug., 1897; C.I.E., Jan., 1901; commr. of settlmt. and agric., Sept., 1904; dir. of agric., and regr., co-operative credit societies, April, 1908; chief sec. to chief commr., Nov., 1908, to Jan., 1911; commr., April, 1918; C.S.I., June, 1920; member of

exec. council of govr. of Central Provs., Dec., 1920, to Dec., 1925; K.C.I.E., June, 1925; retd., June, 1926.

STANFORD, JOHN KEITH, O.B.E., M.A., M.C., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Apl., 1892).—Educ. at Rugby, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Nov., 1914; apptd., 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 24th Dec., 1919, and served in Burma; dep. dir. of comml. intellge., Calcutta, Jan., 1922; services replaced at disposal of govt. of Burma, June, 1923; under-sec., home and pol. dept., Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., June, 1924; regr., high ct., Rangoon, June, 1925; offg. dep. commr., June, 1927; confd., Sept., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1932; secty., rev. dept., Burma, June, 1932; retd., Mar., 1938.

STANGER-LEATHES, HUGH ELLIS, Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 1st Nov., 1878).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1904; in milty. employ up to 28th Feb., 1922; health officer, Delhi, March, 1922; dep. dir., public health, New Delhi, April, 1926; offg. dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., March, 1928; confd., Juy, 1929; hon. surg. to the King, Sept., 1932; A.D.M.S., Madras Dist., Aug., 1933; retd., Aug., 1935.

STANIFORTH, WALTER HARLAND, India Office (suptg. executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 21st June, 1886).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 15th Aug., 1907; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; actg. staff clerk, April, 1921; acting higher exec. officer, April, 1922; confd., Oct., 1925; also assessor of income tax; suptg. exec. officer Oct., 1936.

STANSFIELD, Rev. HERBERT RIGG, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 22nd Sept., 1897).—Apptd. junior chaplain, 25th Dec., 1931; name changed by deed poll to RIGG-STANSFIELD (q.v.).

STANTIALL, WILLIAM, C.I.E., late India Office (b. 13th June, 1865).—Educ. at the City of London Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 25th Sept., 1882; junior clerk, July, 1898; senior clerk, Oct., 1903; sec., cttee. on Indian railway finance and administration, 1907-08; asst. sec. P.W.D., May, 1911; sec., ditto, Nov., 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; rly. adviser and govt. dir., Indian rly. cos., Oct., 1926; retd., June, 1931.

STANTON, WILLIAM THOMAS, Indian State Rlys. (colliery manager) (b. 16th May, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 15th Aug., 1919; colliery manager, Sept., 1931.

STAPLES, FRANCIS HAMMOND, M.A. (Oxon), Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st July, 1884).—Educ. at Downside Coll., Bath, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; sub-judge, July, 1914; dist. judge, March, 1916; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1916; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1919; addl. judl. commr., Dec., 1928; retd., July, 1936.

STAPLETON, HENRY ERNEST, D.Litt., B.Sc. (Oxon), late Indian Educatl. Service (b. 3rd May, 1878).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1900, as prof., presdy. coll., Calcutta; inspr. of schools, Oct., 1905; on mil. duty from May, 1915, to Dec., 1919; principal, Presy. Coll., Calcutta, July, 1924; offg. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, June, 1926; confd., July, 1930; retd., May, 1933.

STAPYLTON, JOHN MILES, late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Lancing Coll.; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1923; arrived, 16th Nov., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1927; dep. commr., etc., Port Blair, Apl., 1932; dep. commr. (subst.), Aug., 1936; retd., Sept., 1938.

STARTE, HORACE WILDERSPIN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Bombay) (b. 13th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 1st Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; offg. consvr., Feb., 1929, and again, Sept., 1932; confd., May, 1935.

STARTE, OLIVER HAROLD BAPTIST, C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Jan., 1882).—Educ. at Perse Grammar School, Cambridge, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1906, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on spl. duty in connection with settlt. of criminal tribes in the Bijapur dist., from Nov., 1909; K.-I.-H. medal, 2nd class Dec., 1911; member, jail commn., 1919-1920; criminal tribes settlement officer, June, 1920; collr., Aug., 1926; C.B.E., June, 1932; retd., Apl., 1936.

STATHAM, RANDULPH MEVEREL, C.I.E., B.A. (Hons.), Indian Educl. Service (dir. pub. instrn., Madras) (b. 4th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 13th July, 1913; dep. dir. of public instrn., Madras, May, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1930; dist. educ. officer, Sept., 1930; offg. princpl. teachers' coll., Saidapet, Sept., 1930; princpl. govt. training coll., Rajahmandry, Apl., 1932; princpl., govt. Muhammadan coll., Madras, Oct., 1933; offg. educ. commr. with the Govt. of India, May, 1935; dir. of public instrn., April 1936.

STEAD, Sir CHARLES, Kt. C.B.E., M.V.O., late Indian Police Service (b. 30th Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1898; supt. of police, July, 1906; M.V.O., Jan., 1912; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1915; personal asst. to inspr.-gen., Sept., 1915; O.B.E., Jan., 1921; asst. inspr.-gen., govt. rly. police, Punjab, March, 1922; C.B.E., June, 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1924; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. inspr.-genl. of police, N.W.F.P., May, 1927; ditto, Punjab, Feb., 1928; confd., May, 1930; Kt., Jan., 1931; retd., Aug., 1932.

STEADMAN, JOHN WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (asst. chief acctg. offcr.) (b. 3rd July, 1885).—Clerk to Rl. commn. on Indian decentralisation, June, 1908, to March, 1909; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 28th March, 1909, and attached to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; clerk to royal commn. on public services, May, 1913, to Sept., 1915; asst. sec. to Jails Commee., from July, 1919, to Sept., 1920; minor staff clerk, Nov., 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from April, 1921; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; suptg. exec. offcr., June, 1927; asst. chief acctg. offcr., Nov., 1937.

STEAVERSON, FREDERICK HENRY, late Indian C.S. (b. 19th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Malvern, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 18th Dec., 1909, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec., rev. sec.'s dept., April, 1913, to Dec., 1916; jt. regr., co-op. societies, Nov., 1920;

dep. commr., and ditto, March, 1925; offg. secty. to govt., forest dept., May, 1928; offg. dep. secty., home and pol. dept., Dec., 1928; ditto, edn. dept., Mar., 1929; offg. commr., July, 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

STEEL, RICHARD FRANCIS, F.R.C.S., M.B., B.Ch., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 28th Apr., 1878).—First commn., 31st Aug., 1903; apptd. civil surgn., Bombay, June, 1909; obstet. phys., J. J. Hospl., prof. of midwifry., G. M. Coll., April, 1923; supt., B. J. Med. School, Poona, Oct., 1925; ret'd., Apr., 1933.

STEELE, GEOFFREY LANYON, late Salt Dept., Bombay (b. 23rd Dec., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts., salt and customs depts., Bombay, from 15th Feb., 1900; asst. collr. of salt rev., Bombay, May, 1910; supt. of salt rev., Mar., 1924; asst. collr., Sept., 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

STEEN, HUGH BARKLEY, M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (addl. member, medl. bd., India Office) (b. 23rd Nov., 1875).—Joined the service, 20th Jan., 1902; arrived, 19th Apr., 1902; in mil. employ to Jan., 1906, when employed as civil surgn., Bengal; on mil. service, Sept., 1914, to Dec., 1915; prof., medical coll., Calcutta, Dec., 1916; ret'd., Nov., 1930; apptd. examr. of surgical instruments, India Store Dept., and addl. member, medl. bd., India Office, May, 1934.

STEIGER-HAYLEY, THOMAS THEODORE, see Hayley, T. T. S.

STEIN, ARCHIBALD HYNDMAN, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 11th Jan., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st Feb., 1924; dep. consvr., Feb., 1928.

STEIN, Sir (MARC) AUREL, K.C.I.E., Ph.D., D.Litt., D.Sc., F.B.A., late Indian Archaeological Survey (b. 26th Nov., 1872).—Educ. at Budapest and Univs. of Vienna and Tubingen; principal, Oriental Coll., Lahore, and regr. of the Punjab Univ. from 1888 to May, 1899, when he was apptd. principal of the Calcutta Madrasah; proceeded on archl. tour with Buner Field Force, 1898; in 1900-01 deputed on special duty for archl. exploration in E. Turkestan; inspr. of schools, Punjab, Nov., 1901; inspr. genl. of educn. and archl. surveyor, N.W. Frontier Prov. and Baluchistan, Jan., 1904; proceeded on a second archaeological and geographical expdn., Turkestan and West China, April, 1906; supt., archaeological survey, Eastern Circle, Oct., 1909; transferred to Frontier Circle, May, 1910; proceeded on spl. duty for explorations in E. Turkestan, W. China, Pamirs and Persia, Aug., 1913, to Feb., 1916; C.I.E., June, 1910; K.C.I.E., June, 1912; ret'd. June, 1929; author of 'Kalhanas Chronicle of Kashmir,' ed. and translated (1892-1901); 'Sand-buried Ruins of Khotan' (1903); 'Ancient Khotan' (1907); 'Ruins of Desert Cathay' (1912); 'Serindia' (1922); various reports on his other explorations, &c.

STEIN, GEORGE LESLIE HAMILTON, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 4th March, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1909; supt., Oct., 1920.

STEIN, JOHN ALAN, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 31st Oct., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1931; and again, Apr., 1933; confd., Mar., 1934.

STENT, PERCY JOHN HODSOLI, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., Wimbledon, and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 28th Nov., 1913, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., May, 1916; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from Sept., 1916, to Aug., 1919; under-sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., Dec., 1920; offg. dep. commr. and dep. regr., co-operative societies, March, 1922; dep. commr., Dec., 1925; rev. secty. to govt., United Provs., Oct., 1933; offg. commr., Dec., 1933, confd., Mar., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; ret'd., Feb., 1939.

STEPHEN, Sir HARRY LUSHINGTON, Bt., Barr. (1885).—Apptd. judge, high court, Calcutta, 18th Nov., 1901; knighted June, 1913; ret'd., Nov., 1914.

STEPHENS, CHARLES VIVIAN, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 28th Apr., 1887).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 25th June, 1910; perm. in provl. service, 1st Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr. in I.S.E., 1st Oct., 1925; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., Nov., 1931.

STEPHENS, JAMES, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 6th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

STEPHENS, JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 22nd July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt., 3rd Apr., 1920; asst. transpn. supt., Apr., 1923; transpn. supt., July, 1930.

STEPHENS, STANLEY DONALD, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 31st Aug., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 3rd Dec., 1931; confd., Jan., 1934.

STEPHENSON, Sir HUGH LANSDOWN, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to sec. of state for Burma) (b. 8th April, 1871).—Educ. at Westminster, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 14th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, judl., etc., dept., Feb., 1901, to Nov., 1903; joint mag. and dep. coll., May, 1904; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., Nov., 1903, to May, 1904, and from Nov., 1904, to April, 1905; mag. and collr., Nov., 1907; junr. sec., board of rev., Oct., 1910; sec., ditto, depts., Jan., 1912; duty with public services commn., Nov., 1912, to Feb., 1913; sec. to govt., finl. dept., Feb., 1913; C.I.E., June, 1913; services temp. lent to the War Office, 1916; services placed at disposal of govt. of India in the finl. dept., April, 1917; addl. sec., govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1917; on depn. to England in connection with Reforms Committee, Nov., 1918; C.S.I., June, 1919; chief sec., govt. of Bengal, May, 1920; member of exec. council of govr. of Bengal, March, 1922; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1924; actg. govr. of Bengal,

June to Oct., 1926, and again, June-Sept., 1930; K.C.S.I., March, 1927; govr. of Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1927 to Apl., 1932; govr. of Burma, Dec., 1932, to May, 1936; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; adviser to sec. of state for Burma, April, 1937.

STEPHENSON, HUGH SOUTHERN, Indian C.S. (under secty., home dept., Govt. of India) (b. 29th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Winchester and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the U.P. as asst. magte. and collr; addl. under secty., fin. dept., Govt. of India, (temp.), Jan., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; asst. secty. Niemeyer finl. enquiry, Jan., 1936; addl. under secty., home dept., Govt. of India, Nov., 1936; officer on spec. duty, ditto, Dec., 1936; under secty., ditto, April, 1937.

STEPHENSON, Rev. WILLIAM, B.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 4th Mar., 1898) (junr. chaplain, Punjab).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 15th Oct., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1929.

STEVENS, HAROLD SAMUEL EATON, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., agric., etc., depts., Bengal) (b. 29th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 23rd Aug., 1920; arrived, 7th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. mag., Nov., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1926; dep. secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and apptt. depts., Mar., 1930; mag. and collr., June, 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, agric. and indus., P.W. and irrig. depts., July, 1935; confd., Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1938.

STEVENSON, ARCHIBALD McCORKELL, Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 11th Aug., 1897).—Joined the service as officer, Cl. II, Feb., 1921; A.M.A.G., July, 1929.

STEVENSON, EDWARD THOMAS HOWARD, late Indian Police (b. 7th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1899, as prob. r.; asst. supt., July, 1902; supt., Dec., 1908; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1920; dep. inspr.-gen., Apl., 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

STEVENSON, FREDERICK, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retired (b. 1st June, 1884).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1908; offg. civil surg., D.I. Khan, Sept., 1920; agency surg., Gilgit, Nov., 1924; resy. surg., Kashmir, Oct., 1927; legation surg., Nepal, Oct., 1930; resy. surg., Kashmir, Apl., 1932; ditto, and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Feb., 1933; legn. surg., Nepal, Nov., 1935; resdy. surg., Kashmir, Apl., 1937; retired, Oct., 1938.

STEVENSON, Rev. GEORGE, M.A., Ind. Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Burma, Dec., 1932.

STEVENSON, HENRY LOUIS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 5th Dec., 1874).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived,

3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and asst. settlt. offr.; dep. commr., Dec., 1913; commr., Jan., 1923; offg. dev. commr. and addl. finl. commr. from June to Oct., 1924; chairman, Rangoon dev. trust, June, 1930; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

STEVENSON-MOORE, Sir CHARLES JAMES, K.C.I.E., C.V.O., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Felsted Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 10th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1894; also asst. settlt. officer, Dec., 1894; settlt. officer, Feb., 1896; mag. and collr., Jan., 1898; inspr.-genl. of police, Oct., 1904; offg. dir. criml. intell., June, 1907; chief sec. to govt., Bengal, Nov., 1910; C.V.O., Jan., 1912; member, board of rev., Jan., 1914; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1920; temp. member of govr.'s council, May, 1920; ret'd., Sept., 1921.

STEWART, ANDREW CHARLES, Capt. I. A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Apl., 1907).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1927; in mil. employ prior to 6th Apl., 1933; under secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Apl., 1933; offg. secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, July, 1933; under secty. to the pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Nov., 1933; under training in the Punjab, Jan., 1934; vice-consul, Meshed, Aug., 1935; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Oct., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Bahrain (temp.), Dec., 1936; pol. officer, Kuwait, Apl., 1937; vice-consul, Khorramshahr, May-Oct., 1937; under secty. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Nov., 1938; sec. to the pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Nov., 1939.

STEWART, ALEXANDER DRON, C.I.E., M.B., F.R.C.S.E., Lt.-Col. (ret'd.), late I.M.S. (b. 22nd June, 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1906; in mil. employ from 1st March, 1907, to 31st Jan., 1914; health offr., Simla, from 1st Feb. to 31st Oct., 1914; in mil. employ from Nov., 1914, to Nov., 1919; health offr., Delhi, Nov., 1919; dir. of public health laboratory, Calcutta, Dec., 1921; prof., ditto, Nov., 1926; offg. prof. of hygiene, sch. of tropical medicine, Calcutta, Jan., 1927; dir. public health institute, Calcutta, Oct., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1938.

STEWART, ALFRED JOSEPH, late India Office (b. 21st Feb., 1874).—Clerk, 2nd class, pol. dept., 27th Sept., 1892; transfd. to registry and record dept., Jan., 1896; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; transfd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., May, 1922; joint editor, 'India Office List,' May, 1926 to Apl., 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

STEWART, CLYNE GARDEN, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Burma) (b. 6th July, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 4th Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1926; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1932; offg. asst. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1933; to Mar., 1936; dir., defence bureau, govt. of Burma, Apl., 1937; dep. inspr.-genl. (confd.), Nov., 1937.

STEWART, DONALD, O.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 14th March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 28th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Apl., 1925; O.B.E., May, 1937.

STEWART, Sir DOUGLAS LAW, Bart., late Indian Police Service (b. 1st June, 1878).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 15th Sept., 1913; confd., Sept., 1915; offg. supt., Jan., 1919; confd., April, 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

STEWART, ERNEST HENRY JAMES, M.C., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 5th June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; offg. exec. engr., March, 1925; confd., Oct., 1925.

STEWART, FRANCIS WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.C., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 26th Feb., 1885)—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 24th Nov., 1909, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1916; on mil. duty from Jan., 1916, to Nov., 1919; sub-collr. and asst. commr., Oriya agency, May, 1922; offg. variously as collr. and dist. mag., April, 1924, to April, 1928; collr. and dist. magte. (prov.), Oct., 1928; confd., Aug., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; 3rd member, bd. of rev., Jan., 1938.

STEWART, GEORGE HERBERT, O.B.E., M.B., B.C.H. (Edin.), D.T.M. and H. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Cantab.), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Burma Medical Dept.
