(b. 8th Feb., 1875).—Arrived, 7th Oct., 1899, and served in mil. dept.; offg. civil surg., May, 1903; civil surg., Dec., 1907; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, April, 1915, to Jan., 1920; O.B.E., June, 1919; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

STEWART, GERALD PAKENHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 5th March, 1906).—Educ. at Portora Royal School, Enniskillen, and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; president, Manipur State Durbar, Feb., 1933, to Feb., 1936.

STEWART, HERBERT RAY, C.I.E., F.R.C.Sc.I., D.I.C., N.D.A., Dept. of Agric., Punjab (dir. of agric.) (b. 10th July, 1890).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; prof. of agric., Punjab Agric. Coll., Dec., 1923; actg. prin., ditto, March, 1927; offg. dir. of agric., May, 1927; on spl. duty, dept. of agric., Dec., 1927; asst. dir. of agric., Nov., 1928; offg. dir. of agric., April, 1931; confd., Dec., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1939.

STEWART, JOHN ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.C., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at the Grammar Sch. and Univ., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 10th Dec., 1905, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from May, 1915, to April, 1919, and from June, 1919, to Oct., 1919; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; offg. commr. March, 1927; confd., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; ret'd., Dec., 1935.

STEWART, PETER MCARTHUR, Barr., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 21st Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1907, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, Oct., 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., May, 1933; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934.

STEWART, Sir SAMUEL FINDLATER, G.C.B., G.C.I.E., C.S.I., LL.D., India Office

and Burma Office (perm. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma) (b. 22nd Dec., 1879).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. junior clerk, 19th Oct., 1903, and served in mil. dept. to Jan., 1910; reading clerk to Council, May, 1906; pte. sec. to; permanent under-sec. of state, Jan., 1910; senr. clerk, fin. dept., May, 1911; on duty in mil. dept. from Nov., 1911, to Feb., 1912, and from Nov., 1914; asst. sec., mil. dept., June, 1918; C.I.E., June, 1919; dep. sec., mil. dept., April, 1919; joint sec., mil. dept., July, 1920; sec. to royal commission on the public services in India, May, 1923, to March, 1924; C.S.I., June, 1924; asst. under-sec. of state and clerk of the council, Sept., 1924; sec., Indian Statutory Commission, Nov., 1927; K.C.I.E., June, 1930; perm. under-secty. of state for India, 11th July, 1930; K.C.B., Jan., 1932; G.C.I.E., June, 1935; perm. under-secty. of state for Burma, Apl., 1937; G.C.B., June, 1939; dir.-genl. of the Ministry of Information, Sept., 1939, to Nov., 1939.

STEWART, Sir THOMAS ALEXANDER, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (govr. of Bihar) (b. 26th Feb., 1888).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and coll and asst. commr.; on mil. service from July, 1918; actg. asst. collr., Imperial customs service, Jan., 1919; dep. commr. of rice, Rangoon, Jan., 1920; rice commr., July, 1921; offg. collr. of customs, Rangoon, Dec., 1923; collr. of customs, Madras, Oct., 1925; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1928; collr. of salt revs., May, 1932; offg. addl. secty., commerce dept., Oct., 1932; offg. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Mar., 1933; confd., Mar., 1934; C.S.I., June, 1935; member, council of state, Apl., 1936; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Apl., 1937; chairman, cent. adv. cl. for rlys., Nov., 1937; actg. govr., Bihar, May to Sept., 1938; K.C.S.I., June, 1939; govr., Bihar, Aug., 1939.

STEWART - WILSON, Sir CHARLES, K.C.I.E., B.A., Barr. (1886), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh High Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1890; under sec. to govt., Aug., 1891; from April, 1892, served as offg. postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Bengal (1893-94), and Bombay (1894); offg. dep. dir. of post office, Oct., 1894, and again April, 1897; postmr.-gen., Calcutta, April, 1896; ditto, Punjab, Jan., 1899; offg. dir. gen. of post office, Sept. to Dec., 1903, and from April, 1906; confd., Oct., 1906; dir.-gen., posts and telegraphs, April, 1912; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1911 ret'd., Aug., 1913.

STIBBS, JAMES CHARLES, O.B.E., India Office (dep. acctnt.-gen., acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 5th Sept., 1878).—Educ. at Plymouth Corp. Gr. Sch., and Penzance Coll. Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 5th Nov., 1897; staff clerk, March, 1919; junior clerk, April, 1921; suptg. exec. officer (acting), April, 1922; confd., Nov., 1922; also assessor of income tax; asst. acctnt.-genl., March, 1927; O.B.E., Jan., 1933; dep. acct.-genl., and clk. to the commrs. of inc. tax, Dec., 1934.

STIFFE, NORMAN CECIL, O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Nov., 1872).—Apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 16th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; city mag., Lucknow, Dec., 1903; joint mag., June, 1906; asst. commr., July, 1908; forest settlt. offr., Oct., 1911; dep. commr., and ditto, Nov., 1913; mag. and collr., and ditto, Nov., 1914; mag. and collr., Oct., 1915; O.B.E., June, 1919; chairman, Cawnpore Improvement Trust, Dec., 1919; commr., Aug., 1924; retd., Dec., 1931.

STILEMAN, DONALD FENWICK, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 19th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1922; retd., March, 1930.

STIRLING, ALEXANDER GILCHRIST, India Office (exec. officer, milty. dept.) (b. 19th May, 1916).—Apptd. exec. officer, 10th Feb., 1936.

STIRLING, REV. JAMES CLERK PAUL, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estabt. Apptd. junr. chaplain Church of Scotland, May, 1931.

STOCK, THOMAS DAVID, D.I.C., A.R.C.S., B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 19th June, 1891).—Joined the service as economic botanist, 24th Dec., 1919; dep. dir., Sept., 1923; offg. dir., Apl., 1926, and again, July, 1932; retd., Jan., 1936.

STOCKER, CHARLES JAMES, M.C., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (rettd.) (b. 24th June, 1886).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1911; offg. agency surg., Mohmand frontier constabulary, March, 1923; agency surg., Meshed, April, 1926; offg. civil surg., Central Provs., Nov., 1927; retd., Aug., 1931.

STOCKWELL, FREDERICK RANDOLPH, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs. on probn., 1st Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

STODART, JAMES CARLYLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 18th Feb., 1880).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1912; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1919; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1924; addl. judge, ct. of resdt. of Hyderabad, Nov., 1932; offg. judge, high ct., Madras July, 1935; confd., July, 1936.

STOKES, SIR HOPETOUN GABRIEL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Aug., 1873).—Educ. at Clifton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, Home Dept., July, 1903; confd., Jan., 1904; reverted to Madras, July, 1906; head asst., Nov., 1906; dep. sec. to govt. of India, Home Dept., Oct., 1909; ditto, fin. dept., May, 1911; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; finl. member, Impl. Delhi cttee., April, 1913, to July, 1914; acting private sec. to govr., Madras, March to Nov., 1915; collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1915; sec. to Lord Hunter's cttee., 1919; offg. sec. to govt. of Madras, development dept., March, 1921, to Nov., 1922; confd. as third member, board of rev., Madras, June, 1924;

second member, ditto, Aug., 1926; offg. as 1st. member, ditto, and ch. secty. to govt. variously from Aug., 1927; C.S.I., June, 1928; member, govr.'s exec. council, Madras, March, 1930; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1934; retd., Mar., 1935.

STOKES, RICHARD CROSBY, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 20th April, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 26th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; offg. supt., Sept., 1933.

STONE, BERNARD SIDNEY, Office of High Commr. for India (executive officer, store dept.) (b. 23rd Jan., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 30th July, 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

STONE, SIR GILBERT, (ch. justice, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 6th June, 1886).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, 30th Nov., 1930; ch. justice, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; Kt., Jan., 1936.

STONE, JULAND CHETWYN TALBOT WATKINS, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 17th June, 1886).—Joined the service, 5th June, 1911; asst. engr., Nov., 1915; exec. engr., July, 1924.

STONE, WILFRED JOHN, Indian State Railways (bridge engr., Burma rlys.) (b. 5th May, 1884).—Joined the service, 28th Aug., 1925; bridge engr., Jan., 1928.

STONEY, RICHARD FRANCIS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 17th Apl., 1876).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1907; suptg. engr. (offg.), April, 1919; suptg. engr., March, 1924; offg. ch. engr., April, 1925; confd., Feb., 1929; retd., Apl., 1931.

STOREY, CHARLES AMBROSE, M.A., late India Office (b. 21st Aug., 1888).—Educ. at Rossall and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (Porson Prize, Tyrwhitt Schol., Allen Schol.); prof. of Arabic, Muhammadan Coll., Aligarh, 1914, to 1919; asst. librarian, India Office, 1st June, 1919; librarian, ditto, Sept., 1927; has edited 'The Fakhir of al-Mufaddal'; retd., Aug., 1933.

STORK, HERBERT CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 28th June, 1890).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; regr., high ct., Calcutta, appellate side, Jan., 1925; offg. first land acquisn. officer, Calcutta, Nov., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, Assam Valleys Districts, Mar., 1937; remembrancer of legal affairs, etc., Assam, April, 1939.

STORR, NORMAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Holgate Grammar School, Barnsley, and Keble College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, November, 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; regr., high ct., Allahabad, Feb., 1939.

STORKS-FOX, REV. EDWIN AUBREY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 27th Jan., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th March, 1916; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1919; senr. ditto, Oct., 1920.

STOTT, HUGH, O.B.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (insp. genl. civil hosp., Bihar) (b. 18th July, 1884).—First commn., dated 1st Aug., 1908; apptd. surg. to H.E. the Govr. of Madras, 5th Feb., 1913; on mil. duty from Nov., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1918; prof., King George's medical coll., Lucknow, Nov., 1922; offg. prinpl., ditto, Aug., 1925, and again, Aug., 1927; employed under Lucknow Univ. from Nov., 1927; insp. genl. civil hosp., Bihar, Nov., 1937.

STOTT, RAYMOND, Home dept., govt. of India (govt. examr. of questioned documents) (b. 26th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 31st May, 1915; on milty. duty up to 17th Sept., 1920; asst. examr. of questioned documents, Sept., 1920; offg. govt. examr. of questioned documents, Mar., 1923; confd., June, 1926.

STOW, VINCENT AUBREY STEWART, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (principal, Mayo Coll.) (b. 27th July, 1883).—Educ. at Winchester, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; asst. master, Marlborough Coll., 1906; asst. master, Daly Coll., Indore, Feb., 1907; services placed at the disposal of the Ch. Commr., C.P., from 24th Aug., 1912; principal, Rajkumar Coll., Raipur, Aug., 1912; on milty. service, 1918-19; prinpl., Mayo Coll., Ajmer, July, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934.

STRACEY, HUGH AUBREY ST. CLAIR, Indian Police (asst. supt.).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., 27th Jan., 1934; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938.

STRACEY, PATRICK DONALD, Indian Forest Service (b. 31st Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 9th Nov., 1930.

STRACEY, RALPH AUGUSTINE ST. CLAIR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 30th Aug., 1909).—Educated at St. Joseph's College, Bangalore, Madras University, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th October, 1933; arrived in India, November, 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. pte. secty. to govr., Bengal, Feb. 1936.

STRAKOSCH, SIR HENRY, G.B.E., India Office (adviser to the sec. of state for India) (b. 9th May, 1871). Member of R.C. on Indian currency and finance, 1925; G.B.E., 1927; member of Council of India, Oct., 1930; member of Ind. del. at Imp. Econ. conf., July, 1932; ditto, monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; ditto, Ind. res. bank ctee., July, 1933; adviser to the sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937.

STRAND-JONES, Rev. JOHN, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 2nd Dec., 1877).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 21st Nov., 1909; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1912; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1922; retd., Apl., 1931.

STRANGE, FRANK HORACE LUMSDEN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th Aug., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 26th Dec., 1910; on milty. duty, 20th Jan., 1916; dist. loco. supt., April, 1918; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; pers. asst. to C.M.E., N.W. Rly., Feb., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Feb., 1932; confd., Oct., 1936; offg. ch. mech. engr., Aug., 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; dep. ch. operating supt., Nov., 1936; retd. Aug., 1939.

STRANGE, WILLIAM HOOD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 24th Nov., 1878).—Held non-gauged appt. from 14th Feb., 1914, to 19th Aug., 1918; offg. dist. carr. and wagon supt., Aug., 1918; confd., Oct., 1920; supt., Feb., 1925; retd., Jan., 1932.

STRANGMAN, Sir THOMAS JOSEPH, Kt., B.A., LL.B., Barr.—Apptd. advocate-genl., Bombay, 23rd Oct., 1908; addl. member of govr.'s legisl. council, Oct., 1908; resigned, April, 1915; reappointed advocate-genl., Dec., 1916; knighted, Jan., 1920; retd., May, 1922.

STRATFORD, Miss LENNA, B.A., M.B.E., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 9th Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1903; senr. inspectress, July, 1910; chief inspectress, Oct., 1913; M.B.E., June, 1919; dep. directress of public instrn., Punjab, Oct., 1923; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1927; retd., Aug., 1933.

STRATHIE, DAVID NORMAN, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 31st Oct., 1886).—Educ. at Glasgow Academy, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 22nd Jan., 1911, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., July, 1917; under-sec. to govt. of India, comm. and ind. dept., 1918; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras, May, 1919; commr. of income tax, Madras, June, 1923; offg. collr. and secty. to govt., rev. dept., June, 1928; offg. regr., co-operative societies, Dec., 1929; collr., Mar., 1930; confd., Oct., 1931; inspr. mun. cls. and local boards, May, 1937; commr. of labour, 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1939.

STRATTON, Rev. BASIL, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (Junr. Chaplain) (b. 27th Apl., 1906).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 15th Sept., 1935; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1937.

STREATFEILD, Rev. SIDNEY FREMLYN B.A., Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th Oct., 1887).—Apptd. garrison chaplain on probation, 25th June, 1930; junr. chaplain, June, 1932.

STREATHFIELD, TERENCE ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 18th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Sutton Valence School, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under secty. to govt., rev. dept., Bombay, Jan., 1934; confd., June, 1934; offg. collr., June, 1936.

STREET, WALTER, O.B.E., late Burma Commn. (b. 19th May, 1877).—Held various appts. in India prior to appt. as asst. commr. on 12th April, 1907; on mil. service, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; O.B.E., July, 1920; depy. commr., Sept., 1921; retd., May, 1931.

STRELLEY, RICHARD COLLIN, Indian Police (b. 30th June, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 16th Nov., 1925; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1932, and again, May, 1933.

STRICKLAND, CLAUDE FRANCIS, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 19th Dec., 1881).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; supt., Mandi State, Dec., 1913.

to Feb., 1914; dep. commr., Dec., 1919; offg. commr., N.I. salt rev., April, 1921; dep. regr., co-op. societies, Punjab, Nov., 1922; regr., ditto, June, 1926; with govt. of F.M.S., from Jan., 1929; ret'd., June, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; lecturer on "Indian Social Welfare" to I.C.S. probationers at Oxford Univ., Nov., 1937.

STRINGER, JOHN ALFRED, late India Audit Office (b. 1st Nov., 1877).—Educ. at Macclesfield Gr. Sch.; clerk, second class, 26th March, 1897; staff clerk, Oct., 1915; junior clerk, Feb., 1922; higher exec. officer, April 1922; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

STRONG, FREDERICK WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Sept., 1873).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., Selwyn Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr. and sub-divl. offr., May, 1899; under-sec. to chief commr., April, 1904, to Nov., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1906; mag. and collr., March, 1909; re-transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; mag. and dep. commr., Dec., 1916; offg. commr. of income tax, Bengal, March, 1926; confd., Nov., 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

STUART, DONALD NEIL, Lt.-Col., I.A., (ret'd.), late Burma Commn. (b. 8th Oct., 1881).—Arrived in India, Nov., 1902, and served in mil. dept.; asst. commr., May, 1906; on mil. service from Dec., 1914, to Feb., 1922; dep. commr., Oct., 1921; offg. commr., March, 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

STUART, Miss Helen Gordon, M.A., O.B.E., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 1st Sept., 1877).—Joined the service as chief inspectress of girls' schools, 26th Oct., 1908; O.B.E., June, 1925; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

STUART, JAMES DOUGLAS, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 3rd Oct., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. engr.; transf. to Burma, Nov., 1903; exec. engr., July, 1907; asst. to chf. engr. and under-sec. to govt. P.W.D., Burma, Oct., 1914; suptg. engr., Oct., 1919; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., May, 1923; confd., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

STUART, JOHN MATTHEW BLACKWOOD, C.I.E., B.A., B.A.I., T.C.D., A.M.I.C.E. (b. 14th Mar., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; on milty. service, May, 1915, to March, 1920; asst. to ch. engr., Irrign. branch, and undersecty. to govt. of Burma, Nov., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Nov., 1923; confd., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1930; confd., Sept., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1934; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

STUART, Sir Louis, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 12th March, 1870).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 17th Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., March, 1897; asst. supt. and judge, small cause court, April, 1898; joint mag., Feb., 1901; dist. and sessions judge,

Jan., 1906; mag. and collr., June, 1910; sec. to govt., June, 1910; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1912; first addl. judl. commr., Oudh, May, 1914; offd. as judge, high ct., Allahabad, Oct. to Nov., 1916, April to Aug., 1919, and from Feb., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1919; apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, Oct., 1922; chief judge, Oudh chief ct., Nov., 1925; Kt., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

STUART, MALCOLM MONCRIEFF, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 21st May, 1903).—Educ. at Sedbergh, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Jan., 1927; arrived, Feb., 1927, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., variously from April, 1934.

STUART, Sir ROBERT CHARLES OCHIL TREE, K.C.S.I., Major-Gen., R.A. (ret'd.).—First commn., 19th May, 1880; served in Burma 1885-87; ordce. dept. in India from Nov., 1889 asst. dir.-genl. of ordce. in India, Jan., 1898; dep. dir.-genl., June, 1904; ordce. consg. offr. for India, India Office, and July, 1906; dir. gen. of ordce. in India, May, 1911; C.S.I. June, 1915; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1917; member of Indian munitions bd., 1917-19; ret'd., Aug., 1919.

STUART, ULLICK CHARLES (b. 25th Feb., 1882).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 4th March, 1901; offg. supt., govt. of India, home dept., 30th April, 1917, to April, 1918, and from June, 1918, to Oct., 1919; confd., Oct., 1919; asst. sec., home dept., Oct., 1920; supt., ditto, April, 1921; asst. sec., ditto, March, 1923; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

STUART-HORNER, BERNARD, Capt., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Political Dept. (b. 5th Dec., 1889).—First commn. dated 5th May, 1915; on mil. duty up to 13th April, 1920; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1920; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, March, 1921; under-sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, April, 1923; sec. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Nov., 1924; supy. asst. commr., Hazara, Nov., 1928; dep. commr., ditto, Mar., 1929; divl. and sess. judge, Derajat, Dec., 1929; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

STUBBS, JOHN LAWRENCE COMMERE, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 23rd May, 1908).—Educ. at Radley and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

STUBBS, LAWRENCE MORLEY, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd March, 1874).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 6th Dec., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., July, 1907; asst. commr., Oct., 1911; joint mag., April, 1913; dep. commr., Oct., 1914; mag. and collr., May, 1918; C.I.E., June, 1922; offg. commr., March to Nov., 1922, and from April, 1924; confd., Aug., 1926; C.S.I., June, 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1933; author of a monograph on ivory-carving.

STUBBS, STANLEY SEVERS, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. offcr., N.W. rly.) (b. 12th Oct., 1891).—Asst. traffic supt., Dec., 1919; divl. transpn. offcr., May, 1935; on loan to I.R.C.A. as dep. genl. secty., from Nov., 1936.

STUBBS, SHIRLEY GRAHAM, O.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr. and secty., p.w.d. (b.r.), Punjab) (b. 14th Sept., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., June, 1917; on mil. duty from 27th July, 1918, to 19th Apl., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; offg. supt. engr., May, 1932; confd., Apl., 1933; offg. ch. engr., and secty. to govy. (blds. and roads bch.), Punjab, Apl., 1935; confd., Apl., 1937.

STUDDERT-KENNEDY, REV. HENRY GERALD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn. Punjab, 7th Mar., 1932; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1934.

STUDDY, HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 20th Apl., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 22nd Dec., 1914; supt., Oct., 1923; persl. asst. to inspr.-gen., Mar., 1925, to Oct., 1926.

STURDY, WILLIAM ARTHUR, C.B.E., late India Audit Office (b. 6th Mar., 1877).—Educ. at Leeds Gr. Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 27th Dec., 1894; junr. clerk, July, 1905; senior clerk, Nov., 1913; deputed to India in connection with compilation of war accounts, March to June, 1915; asst. to auditor, Oct., 1915; auditor, Dec., 1921; C.B.E., June, 1928; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

STURGIS, GERALD CLARE, late Indian Police (b. 4th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 28th Nov., 1912; confd., July, 1917; supt., May, 1925; prinpl. police training coll., Sardah, Mar., 1929; ret'd., Apl., 1938.

SUBBA RAO, MYSORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 25th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at School of Economics, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

SUBBARAYAN, VIRARAGHAVA, Indian Police (b. 28th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 12th April, 1926; offg. dist. supt., April, 1931.

SUBBAYANAYUDU, RAMISETTI, Diwan Bahadur, Madras C.S. (sub. collr.) (b. Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 30th Apl., 1905; dep. collr., Oct., 1918; sub-collr., Aug., 1932; offg. collr., June, 1931; joint secty., local self-govt. dept., Madras, Sept., 1934; commr., Corporation of Madras, Jan., 1937.

SUBERWAL, BANKE LAL, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 14th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Dec., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1933; confd., Jan., 1936.

SUBHEDAR, GANPAT LAXMAN, B.A., late Judicial Dept., Central Provs. (b. 26th Apl., 1876).—Apptd. offg. judl. commr., Feb., 1929; confd., May, 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

SUBHEDAR, LAKSHMIKANT SADASHEO, B.A., LL.B., late Income-tax Dept., Central Prov. (b. 2th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, Mar., 1913, E.A.C., 1913-1920, income-tax officer, June, 1922; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, July, 1936; ret'd., July, 1938.

SUBHEDAR, TRIMBAK RAGHUNATH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 22nd Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 29th Jan., 1931; confd., Apl., 1933.

SUBRAMANYA AYYAR, TRIPUNITTURA SUBRAMANI, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th June, 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1922; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Oct., 1926; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Mar., 1927; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. rly., June, 1930; asst. ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Nov., 1930; dep. govt. exr., B. and N.W. and R.K. rlys., July, 1933; ditto, S.I. rly., Oct., 1937; dep. acct.-genl., Madras, Apl., 1936; dep. govt. examiner of accts., S.I. Rly., Nov., 1937; govt. examiner of accts., M. & S.M. Rly., Feb., 1938; dep. acct. gen., Madras, Sept., 1938.

SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, CHANDRASEKHARA, B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 8th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. examr., 10th Aug., 1907; govt. examr. of accts., June, 1918; dept. auditor, E.B. Rly., July, 1921; dep. acct.-genl., Madras, Oct., 1921; senr. ditto, Madras, June, 1923; dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., March, 1925; ditto, E.B. Rly., July, 1926; ditto, E.I. Rly., May, 1928; offg. dep. dir. of rly., audit (insp.), Ap., 1929; offg. govt. examr. of accts., M. & S.M. Rly., Apl., 1930; ch. auditor, E.B. and Burmariys. and govt. exr. of accts., Assam Bengal rly., Feb., 1932; ch. auditor, G.I.P. rly., and govt. exr. of accts., B.B. and C.I. rly., Jan., 1935.

SUBRAHMANYAM, ATHAMANAKOTTAI, M.A., (b. 26th June, 1901) (Military Accounts Dept.).—Joined service as an officer in Class II April, 1926.

SUBRAHMANYAM, NARAYANASWAMY, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 20th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Municipal High School, Mayavaram, and Madras Univ.; joined the service 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., March, 1939.

SUBRAHMANYAN, MANGUDY, B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 5th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service 1st Nov., 1910; asst. acctnt.-gen., May, 1911; dep. ditto, July, 1924; offg. dep. auditor-genl., Mar., 1929; acct.-genl., Mar., 1929; acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1932; ditto, Bengal, Mar., 1933; ditto, C.P., Apl., 1938; acct. gen., Cent. Revs., April, 1938; acct. gen., Posts and Tels., Oct., 1938.

SUBRAHMANYAN, NATESA, B.A., Indian C.S. (name changed to MANI, N. S. (q.v.))

SUBRAMANIAN, KAVASSERI NARAYANA IYER, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provinces) (b. 4th June, 1909).—Educated at Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 11th October, 1932, arrived in India, November, 1932, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under-secty., July, 1938.

SUBRAMANYAN, MINAKHISUNDARAM VENKATA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 8th July, 1900). Educ. at Madras Univ., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr., Aug., 1926; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1931.

SUBRAMANIAN, VAITHINATHIER VENKATA, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 17th May, 1908).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., July, 1932.

SUD, AMAR NATH, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th July, 1890).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 9th Sept., 1913; asst. engr., March, 1915 exec. engr., 9th March, 1924.

SUDBURY, RONALD CEDRIC LOUIS, Burma Civil Service (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 4th May, 1916).—Educ. at Royal Grammar School, Worcester and Merton College, Oxford; apptd. on probn., 5th October, 1939.

SUDHANSU KUMAR HALDRA (see Haldar S. K.).

SUDHINDRA KUMAR HALDAR (see Haldar, S. K.).

SUFI, MUHAMMAD IBRAHIM (see Muhammad, I. S.).

SUFTI, MOHAMMED HUSAIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. Bombay) (b. 13th Feb., 1914).—Educated at Government High School, Pindi Bhatian, Punjab University and St. John's College Cambridge; appointed on probation 24th Sept., 1938; confirmed 6th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Oct., 1939, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

SUHRAWARDY, SIR HASSAN, O.B.E., M.D. (Adviser to the Secretary of State for India) (b. 4th Jan., 1884).—Served for 7 years 5½ months as gazetted medl. offr. under Government of Bengal; appointed as dist. medl. offr. E.I. Rly. April, 1916; O.B.E., June, 1927; ch. medl. and health offr. Indian State Rlys., 1928-1937; Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st Class, June, 1930; vice chancellor, Calcutta Univ., 1930-1934; Kt., Feb., 1932; LL.D. (Hon. Lond.), 1933; D.Sc. (Hon. Calcutta), 1934; member, public services commn., Bengal, April, 1937; adviser to the Secretary of State for India, July, 1939.

SUHRAWARDY, SIR ZAHHADUR RAHIM ZAHID, Kt., Barr.-at-law, late Judicial dept., Bengal (b. 27th Nov., 1870).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1917, and apptd. judge, Calcutta small cause court; judge, high ct., Calcutta, 1921; Kt., June, 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1931; presd't., rly. rates advisory ctee., May, 1933.

SUKH, DAYAL, late Punjab Med. Service (b. 16th Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 4th Feb., 1908; apptd. asst. sug., Feb., 1910; civil surg., Aug., 1934; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

SUKHDEO PRASAD, TANDON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1914; asst. engr., Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1923; confd., Sept., 1924.

SUKTHANKAR, YESHWANT NARAYAN, B.A., LL.D., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., commerce dept. govt. of India) (b. 24th Aug., 1897).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and at Cambridge Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 16th Dec., 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., April, 1927; under secty. to govt., C.P., Oct., 1932; offg. rev. secty. to

govt., C.P., June, 1933; dep. India trade commr., July, 1934; actg. Ind. trade commr., Oct., 1934; and again, Sept., 1935; dep. secty., commerce dept., Govt. of India, July, 1937; dep. commr. (confd.), Aug., 1937.

SULAIMAN, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Sulaiman).

SULAIMAN, SIR SHAH MUHAMMAD (see Shah Muhammad Sulaiman).

SULE, RAMCHANDRA GOVIND, L.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supt. engr., Bombay) (b. 17th May, 1888).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., 1st April, 1912; asst. engr., April, 1913; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., April, 1922; supt. engr., April, 1939.

SULLEN, ARTHUR ERNEST BUCKNER, O.B.E., late Indian State Railways (b. 10th June, 1878).—Joined the service, 29th June, 1907; asst. engr., N.W. Rly., March, 1908; exec. engr., ditto, Jan., 1909; O.B.E., June, 1921; dep. ch. engr., March, 1927; offg. dep. agent, E.B. Rly., Nov., 1930; ret'd., June, 1933.

SULLIVAN, BASIL MARTIN, C.I.E., O.B.E., F.R.I.B.A., A.M.T.P.I. (b. 30th Aug., 1882).—Apptd. consulting architect to the Punjab govt., 19th Jan., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1924; suptg. architect, Aug., 1928; C.I.E., May, 1937; ret'd. 1st Sept., 1938.

SULLIVAN, EDMOND HERVEY, late Indian Police (b. 21st Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, Dec., 1906; asst. supt., Sept., 1907; supt., Aug., 1915; awarded King's police medal; commr. of police, Bangalore, Oct., 1931; ret'd., July, 1937.

SULLIVAN, MICHAEL JOHN KENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., C.P.) (b. 22nd Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the C.P. as asst. commr.

SULTAN ALI, NOON, MALIK, Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. 23rd Mar., 1895).—Joined the service as supt. dep. dir. of agric., 2nd Sept., 1918; dep. dir. of agric., June, 1924.

SUMER CHAND, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 14th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 9th Sept., 1918; asst. engr. (provl. service), Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1923; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., Oct., 1930.

SUNDAR DAS, KHANGAR, Lala, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 24th Nov., 1897).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930.

SUNDAR SINGH, BABA, M.I.E.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 12th Mar., 1876).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1898; supt., April, 1910; divl. engr., Nov., 1920; dir. tel. engrg., Dec., 1921; dep. ch. engr., March, 1923; dir., tele. engrg., U.P. circle, June, 1928; ditto, Lahore, July, 1929; offg. P.M.G., B. & O., Mar., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1931.

SUNDAR SINGH, Sir, SARDAR BAHADUR SARDAR, MAJITHIA, Kt., C.I.E.—Apptd. member of govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, 3rd Jan., 1921; Kt., Jan., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; member, Indian franchise comee., Jan., 1932; minister of revenue, Apl., 1937.

SUNDAR SINGH, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 7th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 8th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Apl., 1932.

SUNDARA RAJ, B., M.A., Ph.D. (b. 25th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1912, as piscicultural asst., ditto expert, 12th Apl., 1917; asst. dir. of Fisheries (sub. pro tem.), 7th Feb., 1918; asst. dir. of Fisheries, inland, 1st Jan., 1919; acct. dir. of Fisheries, 7th Aug., 1923; Dir. of Fisheries (perm.), 1st Nov., 1924.

SUNDARACHARI, RANGASWAMI, Dewan Bahadur, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 7th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. examr., 29th Aug., 1906; govt. exam. of accts., M. & S.M. & S.I. Rlys., Feb., 1925; dep. auditor, E.B. Rly., May, 1925; offg. acctnt.-gen., rlys. (inspn.), April, 1926; offg. dep. auditor, E.I. Rly., Oct., 1928; dep. dir., rly. clearing accts. office, Apl., 1929; dep. ch. accts. officer, E.I. Rly., Apl., 1929; offg. dep. ch. auditor, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1929; ditto, Burma Rlys., Apl., 1930; ch. auditor, E.I. rly., and govt. exr. of accts., B.N. rly., Apl., 1932; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Nov., 1932; ditto, E.I. rly. and govt. examr. of accts., Bengal-Nagpur rly., Mar., 1933; ret'd. Oct., 1938.

SUNDARAM CHETTI, KRISHNAMA CHETTYAR, DIWAN BAHADUR, B.A., B.L., late Judl. dept., Madras (b. 18th Nov., 1875).—Joined the service as offg. dist. munsif, 2nd Aug., 1902; confd., Jan., 1903; sub. judge, Mar., 1919; sess. judge, Sept., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1929; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, Sept., 1926, Aug., 1929, and again, Apl., 1930; confd., July, 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

SUNDARAM, RAO BAHADUR GANGADARA, M.A., A.M.I.E.E. (suptg. engr., Electricity Dept., Madras) (b. 14th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1926, as electrical asst.; confd. as exec. engr., Apl., 1933; suptg. engr., Apl., 1937.

SUNDARAM, KUTHER VAIDYANATHA KALYAN, B.A. (Hons.) (Mad.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sessions judge, Cent. Provs.) (b. 1st Jan., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived 15th Dec., 1927, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr., sub judge, Dec., 1931; regr. judl. commr.'s court June, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1934; ditto (subs. provl.), Jan., 1936; offg. regr. high ct., Jan., 1936; attd. legis. dept., Govt. of India, May, 1936; on spec. duty, reforms office, Govt. of India, Sept., 1936 to Oct., 1938; addl. dep. secty. legis. dept. Oct., 1938.

SUNDARAM, RAMASWAMY MEENAKSHI, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 22nd May, 1905).—Educ. at University of Madras and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and

mag.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Feb., 1931; Diwan of Sivaganga, July, 1934.

SUNDARAM, VADUVUR SHRINIVAS, B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 24th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as probnr., 21st Nov., 1908; asst. acct.-genl., May, 1909; asst. comptr., India Treasuries, April, 1915; dep., ditto, March, 1916; examr., local fund accts., Nov., 1917; dep. acct.-genl., April, 1922; sec., central board of revenue, and under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., March, 1924; offg. commr. of inc. tax, Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1930; dep. contr. of rly. accts., Nov., 1930; offg. ch. accts. officer, N.W. rly., Nov., 1932; offg. dir. of finance, rly. bd., Jan., 1933; offg. ch. accts. officer, E.I. rly., Sept., 1933; contr. of rly. accts., Jan., 1935; jt. secty., Niemeyer finl. enquiry, Oct., 1935; acct. gen. Madras, May, 1939.

SUNDARARAMAN, SUBRAHMANYA, M.A., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 7th Nov., 1881).—Apptd. asst. mycologist, June, 1920; offg. govt. mycologist, July, 1920; confd., Oct., 1924; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

SUNDARESAN, KUPPUSWAMY, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 6th Jan., 1910).—Educ. at Hindu High School, Triplicane, Univ. of Madras and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934 and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Nov., 1936.

SUNDER LAL, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Sept., 1906; exec. engr., Sept., 1914; suptg. engr., Feb., 1925; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

SUR, HRISHIKESH, O.B.E., B.A., India Posts and Tels. (dep. dir.-genl., tels.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as apprentice asst. supt., 4th Oct., 1906; asst. supt., Dec., 1907; supt., April, 1917; asst. post-master-genl., July, 1918; dep. ditto, April, 1920; divl. engr., Oct., 1921; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan, Mar., 1931; offg. dep. dir.-genl., tels., June, 1933; offg. dir. tel. engrg., Bengal and Assam, Mar., 1934; dir. tel. engrg., Dec., 1934; elec. engr. in ch., Dec., 1935; O.B.E., May, 1937; dep. dir.-genl., tels., Apl., 1938.

SUR, NOLINI KANTA, D.Sc. Meteorological, Dept. (meteorologist) (b. June, 1891).—Joined the service as meteorologist on probn., 31st Oct., 1927; confd., Oct., 1929.

SUR, SACHINDRA NATH, M.B., D.P.H., Dept. of Public Health, Bengal (asst. dir. of public health) (b. 4th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as offg. dep. sany. commr., Dec., 1916; asst. dir. of public health, Dec., 1918.

SURATH NATH, CHANDHURI, RAI SAHIB, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar), (b. 30th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service 5th Feb., 1910, and held non-gazetted appt. prior to 12th Apl., 1924; asst. engr., Apl., 1924; apptd. to I.S.E., 1st Jan., 1930, as asst. exec. engr.; offg. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1930; confd., Apl., 1935.

SURENDRA NATH SEN, M.A., Ph.D., B.Litt. (Oxon.), F.R.Hist. S. (Keeper of the Records of the Government of India) (b. 29th July, 1890).—Educated Dacca College; Calcutta and Oxford Universities; professor of

history and English literature, Robertson College, Jubbulpore, 1916-17; lecturer in Maratha history and Marathi literature, Calcutta University, 1917-1931; Ashutosh professor of mediaeval and modern Indian History, Calcutta University, 1931; honorary adviser on records, Government of Bengal, 1931; keeper of the records of the Government of India, June, 1939.

SURENDRA NATH SEN, M.A., LL.D., Judicial Dept., United Provs. (judge, high ct., Rangoon).—Apptd. offg. judge, high ct., June, 1927; addl. judge, ditto, March, 1928; confd., Oct., 1930.

SURENDRA NATH SEN, Barr., late Judicial Dept., Burma.—Apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, 25th Oct., 1931; retd., Jan., 1938.

SURYAPRAKASA RAO, V., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th March, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bengal, 8th Nov., 1930.

SUTCLIFFE, FREDERICK COWAN, Indian Police (b. 7th June, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Nov., 1938.

SUTHERLAND, JOHN WILLIAM ROSS, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 11th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 17th Dec., 1922; lecturer in forestry, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, Nov., 1924, to April, 1926; dep. consvr., April, 1926.

SUTHERLAND, WILLIAM, C.S.I., V.D., M.I.E.E., late India Posts and Tel. Dept. (b. 16th Dec., 1870).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1891; supt., Aug., 1904; dir. of tel., Oct., 1913; postmr.-gen. and dir., telegraph engrg., Burma, Oct., 1914; dep. dir.-gen., telegraph traffic, India, Dec., 1916; on mil. service, June, 1918, to March, 1919; dir. of telegraphs and offg. postmr.-gen., United Provs., April, 1919; offg. chief engr. telegraphs, March, 1920; confd. as chief engr., May, 1922; C.S.I., June, 1924; asst. to dir.-in-chief, Indo-European Tel., Public Works dept., India Office, Dec., 1925, to Mar., 1931.

SUTTON, ERNEST WILLIAM, late India Office (b. 30th Sept., 1871).—Second divn. clerk, census office, 7th April, 1891; 2nd class clerk, India Office, 1st Sept., 1891; shorthand writer to sec. of state, Sept., 1896, to Nov., 1910; staff clerk, Dec., 1911; apptd. to organise and take charge of Central tel. section, July, 1920; supt., tel. and mails branch, Nov., 1923; retd., Sept., 1931.

SUTTON, NOEL DUDLEY TAYLER, Indian Police (asst. supt., Punjab) (b. 20th Dec., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Punjab, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

SUTTON, STANLEY CECIL M.Sc. (Econ.), India Office (asst. keeper, library) (b. 23rd Sept., 1907).—Educ. at Tottenham County Sch., London Sch. of Econ. (Graham Wallas prize-man), and Univ. Coll., London (Sir John Macalister Medallist); senr. asst., Brit. lib. of pol. and econ. science, 1931-35; asst. keeper, library, India Office, 17th June, 1935.

SUTTON, WARWICK EDWARD late Indian Police (b. 21st Mar., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1907; dep. supt., Jan., 1919; promoted to I.P. as supt., from 26th July, 1929; retd., Mar., 1937.

SVARUP, BISHUN (see Bishun Svarup).

SWAIN, WALTER, C.I.E., late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 17th Jan., 1876).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1895, as asst. supt. of police; dist. supt., Oct. 1905; addl. dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Dec., 1905, to Jan., 1906; princpl. pol. training coll., April, 1913; dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1919; offg. inspr.-gen., Nov., 1920; C.I.E., June, 1922; inspr.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1923; has recd. King's Police, Medal; retd., Jan., 1931.

SWAMINATHA AYYAR, NARAYANA DIWAN BAHADUR, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. Oct., 1878).—Joined the service, 10th April, 1901; asst. engr., April, 1902; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1925; confd., Sept., 1928; offg. ch. engr., Mar., 1929; confd., July, 1929; retd., Mar., 1934.

SWAMINATHAN, THINNIAM SUBRAHMANYA, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 23rd Feb., 1906).—Educ. at University of Madras and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1931.

SWAMINATHAN, THIRUMALRAYA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 18th June, 1912).—Educ. at Pachaiyappa's High School, Chidambaran, Madras University, University College, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, October, 1936, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

SWAN, JAMES GRAHAM GOODENOUGH, C.I.E., B.A., M.B., Ch.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 20th Oct., 1875).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1901; in mil. employ till June, 1905, when he was apptd. asst. plague medical officer, Punjab; civil surgeon, April, 1911; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to Dec., 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; retd., Oct., 1930.

SWANN, ROBERT SWINNEY, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 17th November, 1915).—Educated at George Watson's College, Edinburgh University, and Queen's College, Oxford; appointed on probation, 24th Sept., 1938; confirmed 16th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, October, 1939; and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

SWANZY, ROBERT EDWARD, B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 17th July, 1888).—Educ. at Rugby, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service, Oct., 1915, to April, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1924; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. commr., Mar., 1936.

SWEENEY, JAMES AUGUSTINE, C.I.E., B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th May, 1883).—Educ. at French Coll., Blackrock, and Univ. Coll., Blackrock, R.U.I.; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 10th Dec., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. offr.; transfd. to Bihar, April, 1912; settlt. offr., North Bihar, Sept., 1913; jt. mag. and dep. collr.

and ditto, Nov., 1913; sec., bd. of rev., Bihar and Orissa, from May, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1924; offg. supt. and remember. of legal affairs and sec. to govt., judl. dept., April, 1926; confd., Decr., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; retd., Apl., 1934.

SWEET, JACK McMURDO, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 20th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1923.

SWIFT, GERALD ARTHUR, Indian Police (b. 13th July, 1895) (supt., United Provs.).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Nov., 1920; offg. supt., Sept., 1923; confd., June, 1930.

SWINNERTON, ALBERT RICHARD, India Office (higher exec. officer, milty. dept.) (b. 6th Apl., 1896).—Apptd. clerical officer, 3rd Aug., 1921; exec. officer, Jan., 1929; actg. hr. ditto, Aug., to Oct., 1938; confd., July, 1939.

SWINSTEAD, NORMAN HILLYARD, M.A. (Cantab.), M.B.E. (Mil. div.), India Posts and Tels. (divl. engr., wireless) (b. 21st Mar., 1891).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1920; divl. engr., wireless, 3rd Oct., 1921; asst. dir.-genl., wireless, May, 1930; offg. dir., Aug., 1932; asst. dir.-genl., Oct., 1935; div. engr., Feb., 1936.

SWITHINBANK, BERNARD WINTHROP, C.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 4th Oct., 1884).—Educ. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dep. commr. (prov. subs.), Mar., 1924; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. secty., local govt. dept., Aug., 1929; offg. commr., Apl., 1933; perm., Jan., 1937; C.B.E., Jan., 1938.

SYED ABDUL VAHID, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr. and sylviculturist, Jan., 1927.

SYED FIDA HASSAN (see Hassan, Syed Fida.)

SYED ISKANDER ALI MIRZA, SAHIBZADA, O.B.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 13th Nov., 1899).—First commn., 16th July, 1920; asst. mag. and collr., Aligarh, Aug., 1926; supy. asst. commr., Hazara, Mar., 1927; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Apl., 1928; ditto, Tonk, May, 1928; ditto, Bannu, Apl., 1930; ditto, Nowshera, Apl., 1931; dep. commr., Hazara, May 1933; asst. commr., Marden, Oct., 1936; dep. commr., ditto, Jan., 1937; pol. agent, Khyber, Apl., 1938; O.B.E., June, 1939.

SYKES, The Right Hon. Sir FREDERICK HUGH, P.C. G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., G.B.E., K.C.B., C.M.G., Major-Gen. (retd.); govr. of Bombay, from 8th Dec., 1928, to Dec., 1933.

SYKES, JOHN FRANCIS GASKELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Wellington College and Oriel College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. asst. and comdt., Border Mily. Police, Apl., 1936; sub. div. officr., June, 1937.

SYKES, Sir PERCY MOLESWORTH, K.C.I.E., C.B., C.M.G., F.R.G.S., Lieut.-Col., temp. Brig.-Gen., Indian Army, (retd.).—Joined the service, 20th Feb., 1888; regtl. duty and staff duties in the intelligence dept., army headquarters, June, 1893, to May, 1901; pol. asst., Nov., 1902; pol.

agent, Feb., 1906; H.B.M.'s consul-gen. and agent to govr.-gen. in Khorasan, Dec., 1906, to Nov., 1913; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; employed as interpreter, headquarters, 3rd (Lahore) divn., in France, Sept. to Oct., 1914; offg. consul-gen. at Kashgar, April, 1915; on spl. duty, South Persia Rifles, Feb., 1916, to Dec., 1918; K.C.I.E., June, 1915; C.B., June, 1919; retd., Sept., 1920; served in S. African war with Imperial Yeomanry, slightly wounded, mentioned in despatches; author of 'Ten Thousand Miles in Persia,' 'A History of Persia,' and of papers read before various learned societies.

SYLVESTER, HAROLD FRASER, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. eng., Punjab) (b. 15th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 12th Jan. 1909; asst. engr., Jan., 1923; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1928; exec. engr., Jan., 1931.

SYMINGTON, DAVID, B.A., Indian C.S. (backward cl. officr.) (b. 4th July, 1904).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and supt. of stamps, May, 1929; offg. collr., Jan., 1933; backward class officr., Bombay presdy., Nov., 1937.

SYMNS, JOHN MONTFORT, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Jan., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1908, as inspr. of schools; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Jan., 1921, to Sept., 1922, and again, May, 1926; on depn. in connection with British Empire Exhibition, 1924; offg. dir. of public instrn., Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; retd., Feb., 1935.

SYMON, ALEXANDER COLIN BURLINGTON, India Office (asst. principal) (b. 13th May, 1902).—Apptd. clerical officer, 22nd Apl., 1920; asst. secty., Ind. deleg. to the disarmament, conf., 1932-33; exec. officer, Mar., 1933; asst. prinpl., Nov., 1934; secty., Ind. deleg. to London Naval conf., 1935-36; pte. secty. to permnt. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1938; acting principal, Feb., 1939; services loaned to the Ministry of Information, Sept., 1939 to Nov., 1939.

SYMONS, NOEL VICTOR HOUSMAN, M.C. Indian C.S. (secty., rev., etc., dept., Bengal) (b. 27th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at King Edward VI School, Bath; joined the service, 26th Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. mag., March, 1926; offg. magte. and collr., April, 1927; offg. dep. commr., March, 1929; offg. secty. to bd. of rev., Bengal; confd., May, 1932; offg. pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, Sept., 1934; mag. and collr., July, 1935; secty., rev., etc., dept., Bengal; confd., Aug., 1938.

SYMONS, RONALD STUART, Indian C.S. (dep. secty., fin. dept., govt. of India) (b. 24th July, 1904).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dept., Dec., 1937; dept. secty., defin. pt., Apl., 1938.

SYMONS, RUDOLPH VICTOR, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 18th Jan., 1873).—Joined the service, 5th March, 1897; asst. engr., May, 1900; resdt. engr., May, 1903; dist. engr. (temp.), Nov., 1911; suptg. engr.,

July, 1915; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; chief engr., July, 1919; ret'd., Jan., 1930.

SYMONS, SIR THOMAS HENRY, K.B.E., C.S.I., K.H.S., Major-Gen., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 17th May, 1872).—First commn., dated 29th Jan., 1896; in mil. employ to May, 1903, when he was apptd. physician, Madras gen. hosp.; ditto and prof. medl. coll., Nov., 1906; on mil. duty from Nov., 1914, to Feb., 1919; O.B.E., Dec., 1917; senr. medl. offr. and second surg., gen. hosp., Madras, also surg., 3rd dist.; offg. surg.-gen. with govt. of Madras, May, 1923; confd., Jan., 1924; C.S.I., June, 1925; dir.-gen., ind. medl. service, April, 1926; K.B.E., June, 1929; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

SYNGE, GEORGE BERNARD, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dy. collr., Bengal) (b. 11th Feb., 1903).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and apptd. depts., Jan., 1930; confd. Mar., 1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1933.

SYNNOTT, NEVILLE WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 31st July, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 14th July, 1909; supt., July, 1925; offg. dep. ch. operg. supt. (movement), Mar., 1935.

TAELA, PADAM CHAND, T.C.E., Coll., Roorkee, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr. (provl.), Mar., 1938.

TADULINGA MUDALIYAR, C., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 24th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1901; asst. in systematic botany, May, 1917; govt. lecturing botanist, Sept., 1923; principal, agric. coll., Coimbatore, May, 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

TAILYOUR, DOUGLAS STUART, M.C., late Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 30th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service as forest engr., Burma, 30th Nov., 1921; ret'd., Apl., 1934.

TAIT, DOUGLAS WALTER, India Office (suptg. exec. offcr., acctnl.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 12th Aug., 1889).—Served as 2nd divn. clerk (customs), from Nov., 1907; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 6th July, 1909, and attached to acct.-gen.'s dept.; transf. to library, March, 1913; on mily. service from March, 1917, to Nov., 1919; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher executive officer, Apl., 1926; assessor of income tax, Oct., 1936; actg. suptg. exec. offcr., Nov., 1937; confd., Jan., 1938.

TAJ MUHAMMAD, SHEIKH, M.A., M.Sc., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 13th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1907; asst. acct.-gen., Nov., 1911; dep. acct.-gen., central revenues, June, 1926; asst. acct.-gen., Punjab, Aug., 1928; examr., local fund accts., Punjab, Dec., 1928; offg. dep. dir. of army audit, E.C., March, 1929; comptr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1932.

TAJAL ISLAM MUHAMMAD NURANABI CHAUDHURI, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 8th Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Presidency Coll., Calcutta; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1927; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933.

TAJAMMUL ALI, KHAN BAHADUR B.A., late Assam Civil Service (b. July, 1878).—Joined the service 22nd July, 1901; sub. dep. collr., Feb., 1904; dep. magte. and dep. collr., Jan., 1908; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1912; offg. dep. commr., July, 1930; confd., Feb., 1932; ret'd., July, 1933.

TAKAKHAV, NILKANTH SADASHIV M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 13th Aug., 1881).—Joined the service as prof. of English, Karnatak Coll., Dharwar, Mar., 1922; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

TAKLE, GANESH GOVIND, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 14th Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 4th Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Feby., 1930.

TALBOT, Rev. ALFRED DIXON, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 28th Dec., 1881).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th Oct., 1914; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1917; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

TALEYARKHAN, KAIKHUSRO MANEKSHAH, Barr., late Judl. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service as offg. ct. receiver and liquidator, 26th Nov., 1934; confd., Mar., 1935; ret'd., Apl., 1938.

TALIA MUHAMMAD KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR, RANA, B.A., late Indian Police (b. 14th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th Feb., 1908; asst. supt., Aug., 1921; supt., Dec., 1922; dist. offcr., frontier constabulary, Apl., 1927; ret'd., 1937.

TALLENTS, PHILLIP CUBIT, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late India, C.S. (b. 13th April, 1886).—Educ. at Harrow Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transferred to Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1912; under-sec. to govt., fin. and munpl. depts., from Jan., 1914; acting sec. to board of rev., June, 1916, to Jan., 1917; offg. sec. to govt., fin. and munpl. depts., July to Aug., 1917; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1917; spl. duty under govt. of India, comm. and industry dept., Oct., 1917; temp. under-sec., comm. and industry dept., Nov., 1917; spl. duty, home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1918; supt. of census, Bihar and Orissa, March, 1920; mag. and collr., Sept., 1923; sec. to govt., fin. dept., April, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1929; pol. agent and commr., Orissa, Feud. States, April, 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Mar., 1932; confd., Jan., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1934; temp. jt. secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1934; offg. secty., ditto, Oct., 1934; confd., Nov., 1934; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Mar., 1936; to April, 1937; ret'd. Aug., 1939.

TALUKDAR, JATINDRA NATH, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India) (b. 22nd July, 1901).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Wadhams Coll., Oxf.; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 1st Dec., 1925, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1932; addl. secty. to govt., pol. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1935; dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India, 1938.

TAMBE, SHRIPAD BALWANT.—Apptd. member of govr.'s exec. council, Central Provs., 17th Dec., 1925; actg. govr., Central Provs., Nov., 1929; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1931; member, Indian franchise co. ee., Jan., 1932.

TAMBLING, Rev. FRANCIS GORDON HILL, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 5th Jan., 1885).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 2nd Nov., 1915; junr. chaplain, April, 1918; senr. chaplain, April, 1928; acting addl. chaplain, Lahore, Feb., 1933; ret'd. July, 1939.

TAMHANE, VINAYAK ATMARAM, M.Sc., M.Ag., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 4th May, 1884).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 3rd Jan., 1906, to 14th Dec., 1920; soil physicist, Dec., 1920; ret'd., May, 1939.

TAMPOE, ALFRED MCGOWAN COOMARASWAMI, late Indian C.S. (b. 25th June, 1881).—Educ. at St. Thomas's Coll., Colombo, Stevenage Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 13th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., March, 1913; mgr., Sivaganga estate, Jan., 1920, to May, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., June, 1924; confd. March, 1926; secty. to govt., development dept., Oct., 1927; inspr. of municipal councils and local boards, Mar., 1932 to May, 1937; ret'd. Sept., 1939.

TANDAN, BALWANT RAI, Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 8th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1933; and again, Feb., 1936; ditto (provl. subs.), Dec., 1936.

TANDAN, HARI RAM, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 31st Aug., 1879).—Joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1902; asst. engr., Sept., 1903; exec. engr., Sept., 1913; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

TANDON, PREM NARAIN, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Feb., 1909).—Educ. at S.S.M. Intermediate College, Chandhansi, Allahabad University and Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, asst. mag. and collr.; under secty., pol. and appts. dept., Mar., 1938.

TANDON, SUKHDEO PRASAD (see Sukhdeo, P. T.).

TANDY, Sir EDWARD ALDBOROUGH, Kt., Brigadier, R.E. (ret'd.) late Survey of India Dept. —First commn. dated 13th Feb., 1891; att'd. to mil. works dept., India, Oct., 1893; joined survey dept. as asst. supt., Jan., 1898; dept. supt., July, 1905; temply. reverted to mil. duty, June, 1916, to Sept., 1917; supt., Dec., 1922; surveyor-general, Sept., 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1928; Kt., June, 1929; war service, Tirah Expedy. Force, 1897-98, medal and two clasps; Mesopotamia, 1916-17.

TANDY, MAURICE O'CONNOR, D.S.O., O.B.E., Col., R.E. (ret'd.), late Survey of India Dept. —First commn. dated 25th July, 1893; att'd. to mil. works dept., Oct., 1898; joined survey dept. as asst. supt., Jan., 1900; dep. supt., March, 1910; on mil. duty from Oct., 1914, to Aug., 1919; supt., Oct., 1924; dir., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Dec., 1928; war service—operations in the interior, Aden, 1903-4; war, 1914-18 (mentd. in despatches, O.B.E., D.S.O.).

TANNAN, MOHAN LAL, M.Com. (Bir.), Barr., Indian Educl. Service (b. 2nd May, 1885).—Held various appts. in provl. service from 2nd Jan., 1915, to 31st Aug., 1920; apptd. prinpl. and prof. of banking, Sydenham Coll. of Com. and Economics, Bombay, Nov., 1920; secty., Indian accountancy bd., Mar., 1932.

TANNER, JOCELYN THEODORE, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 6th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1910, as asst. supt.; on milty. duty from July, 1917, to April, 1919; supt. of police, Jan., 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1932.

TANNER, MAURICE OSBORNE, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 3rd Dec., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 29th Nov., 1913; dist. supt., May, 1918; awarded King's police medal, March, 1929; O.B.E., Jan., 1935.

TANNER, RICHARD LIONEL, Indian State Rlys. (production engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 22nd Oct., 1888).—Apptd. production engr., E.I. rly., 1st Nov., 1927.

TAPENDRA KUMAR GHOSH CHAUD HURI (see Ghosh Chaudhuri, T. K.).

TAQI, MUHBMMAAD (see Muhammad Taqi).

TARAK, NATH GHOSH, Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Jan., 1937.

TARAKAD GOPALAKRISHNA NARAYANA AYYAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 15th Feb., 1902).—Educ. at University of Madras, and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 18th Oct., 1926; arrived, 7th Decr., 1926, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933.

TARAPORE, PHEROZE KHARSEDJI, Lt.-Col., Indian Medical Service, ret'd. (b. 12th Nov., 1879).—Arrived 26th Sept., 1907, and served in mil. dept.; supt., central jail, Mandalay, July, 1914; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to March, 1920; supt., central jail, Rangoon, April, 1920; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Burma, Sept., 1924; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

TARA SINGH, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st June, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

TARGETTI, ROBERT WILLIAM (ch. contr. of stores) (b. 23rd Feb., 1891).—Joined the service, 22nd May, 1918; asst. contr. clothing, Indian Munitions Bd. (tempy.); employed in I.M.B. and Surplus Stores Organsn., up to Dec., 1921; contr. of Leather and Textile Purchase, Indian Stores Dept. (which post was subsequently converted to Deputy Director of Purchase), Jan., 1922; ch. contr. of stores, April, 1939.

TARLOK, SINGH, B.A., B.Sc. (Econ.), Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 26th Feb., 1913).—Educ. at St. Vincent's High Sch., Poona Bombay Univ., and London School of Economics; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

TARR, WILLIAM, M.D., F.R.C.S., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 21st June, 1876).—First commn., 30th Jan., 1904; in mil. employ to May, 1907, when he was apptd. civil surgeon; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914, to June, 1918; offg. inspr. genl. of civil hosps., C.P., Mar., 1930; ret'd., June, 1931; re-employed as civil surgeon and supt. Robertson Med. School, Nagpur, June, 1931, to June, 1932.

TASKER, Sir THEODORE JAMES, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian C.S. (member, board of rev., Madras) (b. 20th Jan., 1884).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Trinity Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 24th Nov., 1908, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., Apl., 1913, to Mar., 1915; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1916; dist. mag. and collr., Bangalore, Nov., 1917; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; commr., Coorg, Dec., 1923; on for. service under Nizam's govt., Jan., 1927; collr., Mar., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; knighted, May, 1937; third member, board of rev. Madras, March, 1938.

TATE, GODFREY, C.I.E., M.B., Major-Gen., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 5th Mar., 1873).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1898; civil surg., Simla (East), temp., Oct., 1916; chief medl. offr., N.W.F. Prov., May, 1920; prof. of midwifery, King Edward medical coll., Lahore, Oct., 1920; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals, Nov., 1926; surg.-genl. with govt. of Bengal, March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1930; house govr. & medl. supt., King Edward VII. convalescent home, Osborne, Oct., 1931.

TATE, JOHN CREERY, Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 14th Aug., 1884).—First commn. 18th Jan., 1905; apptd. guardian to minor Chief of Miraj (junr.), Feb., 1909; asst. pol. agent, S.M. country, March, 1909; asst. resdt., Baroda, in Okhamandal dist., Nov., 1911; tutor and companion to minor Rana Sahib of Porbandar, May, 1913; to mil. duty, Dec., 1914. persl. asst. to agent to govr., Kathiawar, 1919; asst. resdt., Aden, Sept., 1920; pol. agent. Eastern Kathiawar States, Feb., 1923; on foreign service as admtr., Chhota Udepur State, Oct., 1923; pol. agent, W. K. states, Rajkot, Feb., 1929; offg. resdt., and A.G.G., States of Western India, May, 1933; offg. resdt., Hyderabad, May, 1934; offg. resdt., and A.G.G., Kolhapur, Nov., 1934; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

TATE, THOMAS BAILEY, C.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 11th Dec. 1882).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.) from Aug., 1916, to Dec., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1924; confd., Sept., 1927; secty. to govt., p.w.d. Punjab, irrigation bch., Nov., 1931; offg. ch. engr., and ditto, Nov., 1932; confd., Feb., 1933; C.S.I., Feb., 1937; ret'd., Dec., 1938.

TAUNTON, Ivon Hope, B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt. of Sind, home, legal, etc., depts.) (b. 19th Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Sind as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service, Nov., 1917, to April, 1919; offg. collr. and dis. mag., May, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1925; chairman,

cattle theft commn., Dec., 1925; offg. collr. and supt. of stamps, Jan., 1926; offg. dep. sec. to govt., home and eccl. depts., April, 1926; offg. dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., June, 1927; in foreign service as finl. and rev. member, Khairpur State Exec. Council, Oct., 1927; offg. collr., Sholapur and pol. agent, Akalkot, Nov., 1932; collr., Dec., 1932; municip. commr. for city at Bombay, July, 1934; ch. secty. to Govt. of Sind, home, legal, etc., depts.; member Sind rev. tribunal, Jan., 1939.

TAVENER, EDWARD SYDNEY BEAUMONT, M.A., Dip. of Forestry, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 29th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1923, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Jan., 1924.

TAYLER, HENRY FRANCIS TASKER, M.C. (b. 14th July, 1880).—Asst. consulting architect. Feb., 1920; spl. consulting architect, Dec., 1923; offg. ditto, March, 1925; confd., Oct., 1930.

TAYLER, HENRY HOLROYD, M.C., late Indian Police (b. 9th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 15th Mar., 1917; dist. supt., July, 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

TAYLOR, ALFRED GEORGE TUTTON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 1st Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

TAYLOR, BASIL CHURTON, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 19th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1910; dist. supt. and princpl., police training school, Saugor, Jan., 1923; on deputation to Indore as inspr.-gen. from April, 1926, to Oct., 1930; and again, Mar., 1932; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1936; on deputation to Jaipur State as inspr.-gen. of police June, 1939.

TAYLOR, BERTRAM FENWICK, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th Mar., 1881).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1905; asst. engr., Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1930; confd., Dec., 1932; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to govt., P.W.D. Assam, Feb., 1934; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

TAYLOR, BRUCE HENRY, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 18th Feb., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, 19th Nov., 1931; confd., Feb., 1934.

TAYLOR, CECIL GEORGE, late Impl. Customs Service (b. 11th Nov., 1881).—Joined the service, 10th May, 1909; asst. collr., Nov., 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

TAYLOR, ERNEST BERTRAM NEAVE, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 2nd Oct., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 20th Oct., 1910; exec. engr., May, 1919; offg. supt. of works, Dec., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1930, and again, Dec., 1932; offg. sent. govt. inspr., Oct., 1934; dep. ch. engr. (provl.), Nov., 1935; cnfd., Apl., 1938.

TAYLOR, E. MCKENZIE, M.B.E., Ph.D. (Cantab.) D.Sc. (Durham), F.I.C., Public Works Dept., Punjab (dir. irrign.).—Apptd. dir. irrign. research, Punjab, 17th Nov., 1930.

TAYLOR, ERNEST RIDLEY, late Indian Police Service (b. 16th Aug., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 18th April; 1920; offg. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, Dec., 1925; confd., May, 1927; supt., Mar., 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1931.

TAYLOR FRANCIS ERNEST ALFRED, B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 16th Sept., 1903).—Educ. at Glenalmond, Univ. of St. Andrews, and Merton Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927, arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1929.

TAYLOR, FRANK HERBERT GRAHAM, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 17th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., March, 1910; dist. supt., Nov., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. insp.-genl., Mar., 1936.

TAYLOR, HENRY JOHN BOSANQUET, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 23rd April, 1909).—Educated at the Northern Secondary School, Portsmouth, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

TAYLOR, Sir JAMES BRAID, K.C.I.E., M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 21st April, 1891).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1914, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; on mil. service from March, 1915, to April, 1919; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, commerce dept., Dec., 1920; confd., Jan., 1921; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dept., Jan., 1924; offg. dep. contr. of currency, Calcutta, April, 1924; offg. dep. contr. of currency, Bombay, Nov., 1925; confd. Sept., 1926; offg. contr. of the currency, Calcutta, April, 1927; confd., Oct., 1929; offg. sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, June, 1932, and again, July, 1933; temp. addl. secty., ditto, Sept., 1933; and again, Nov., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1935; dep. govr., Reserve Bank of India, Feb., 1935; knighted, June, 1935; govr., Reserve Bank of India, July, 1937; K.C.I.E., June, 1939.

TAYLOR, JOHN C.I.E., D.S.O., V.H.S., M.D., D.P.H., Col. I.M.S. (dir., Central research inst., Kasauli) (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1906; arrived in India, 26th Feb., 1907; in milty. employ from 26th Feb., 1907, to 31st Aug., 1909; in civil employ. in Bombay from 1st Sept., 1909, to 15th Oct., 1914; reverted to milty. duty from 16th Oct., 1914, to 5th Aug., 1920; offg. sanitary commr., Assam, Aug., 1920; offg. asst. dir., Pasteur Inst., Rangoon, March, 1923; offg. dir., ditto, March, 1925; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. dir., Haffkine inst., Feb., 1932; dir., central research inst., Kasauli, Oct., 1932; C.I.E., May, 1937.

TAYLOR, JOHN FREDERICK, A.T.C.L., Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 5th Jan., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 8th Aug., 1904; minor staff clerk, May, 1920; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922.

TAYLOR, LESLIE FERNANDES, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Burma) (b. 21st Aug., 1886).—Joined the service as prinpl., govt. high school, Bassein, 18th April, 1915; offg. as research lecturer in Indo-Chinese ethnology and linguistics, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, May, 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

TAYLOR, ROBERT WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 16th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., Mar., 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1936; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Oct., 1935; confd., Nov., 1937.

TAYLOR, SAMUEL, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd Feb., 1883).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1913; works manager, June, 1919; dist. loco. supt., Apl., 1927; offg. dep. ch. mechnl. engr., E.B. rly., June, 1935; ret'd., Feb., 1938.

TAYLOR, STANLEY GRISEWOOD, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 19th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 2nd Dec., 1913; addl. supt., March, 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1923; confd., Aug., 1925; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1934; prin., police training coll., Sardah, Jan., 1936.

TAYLOR, WALTER, India Office (asst. administrative officer, finl. dept.) (b. 29th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 5th Aug., 1914, and attached to acct.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from July, 1915, to Jan., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; jt. editor, India Office List, Nov., 1934; actg. hr. exec. officer, Sept., 1935; confd., Oct., 1936; attd. finl. dept., Dec., 1935; editor, India Office and Burma Office List, Nov., 1937; attd. Oct. 1938 to Expert Cttee. on the Defence of India, 1938-39; asst. administrative officer, June, 1939.

TAYLOR, WALTER, D.V.H., M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (prinpl. and profs. of medicine, vety. coll., Punjab) (b. 25th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as prinpl. and profs. of medicine, vety. coll., Punjab, 21st Mar., 1913; offg. dir., civil vety. dept., Punjab, Mar., 1929; offg. dir., vety. services, Punjab, Apl., 1932; offg. dir., impl. inst. of vety. research, Nov., 1933.

TAYLOR WILFRED REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 18th Mar., 1881).—Joined the service as dist. medl. officer, 10th Mar., 1910; offg. C.M.O., Burma rlys., Jan., 1918; confd., Sept., 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

TEAGUE, LIONEL ELLES STANLEY, late Indian Forest Service (Bengal) (b. 12th Feb., 1882).—Arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1905, and served as asst. consvr.; I.A.R.O., Nov., 1917, to April, 1919; dep. consr., Dec., 1919; ret'd., July, 1930.

TEDMAN, CYRIL, M.C., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 20th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th March, 1902; resdt. engr., March, 1905; senr. ditto, April, 1915; in milty. employ from 15th June, 1915, to 10th April, 1919; dist. engr., Nov., 1919; dep. chief engr., May, 1922; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

TEGART, Sir CHARLES AUGUSTUS, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.V.O., late Indian Police Service (Bengal) (b. 5th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, Nov., 1901, and served in Bengal as

asst. supt.; supt., Jan., 1908; awarded King's police medal, 1911; M.V.O., Jan., 1912; dep. commr. on special duty in addition, Feb., 1913; C.I.E., Jan., 1917; supt. and dep. inspr.-gen. in charge intell. divn. (temp.), April, 1918; offg. commr. of police, Calcutta March, 1923; confd., June, 1925; Kt. Jan., 1926; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1931; member of Council of India, Dec., 1931, to Dec., 1936; K.C.I.E., May, 1937.

TEJ NARAYAN MULLA, M.A., LL.B. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. July, 1887).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1921, as addl. dist. and sess. judge; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1923; offg. judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1936.

TEJA SINGH, SARDAR Bahadur, Sardar, B.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S., Judcl. Bch. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 1st Jan., 1889).—Joined the service, 8th July, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934.

TEJASWI PRASAD BHALLA, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (supl., United Provs.) (b. 11th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 24th May, 1923; offg. supt., July, 1926; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1928; supt., June, 1935; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D. (Gen.), Apl., 1936; O.S.D. at govt. hdqtrs., Nov., 1937.

TEK CHAND, BAKHSHI, M.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Punjab (judge, high ct., Lahore).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Lahore, 27th Jan., 1927.

TELFER, GEORGE HENRY, Indian State Railways (signal engr., Burma rlys.) (b. 11th May, 1883).—Joined the service, 15th June, 1904; asst. engr. (signals), Burma rlys., Oct., 1923; signal engr., Mar., 1931.

TEMPLE, JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th May, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 22nd Sept., 1905; resdt. engr., June, 1910; dist. engr., Mar., 1924; divl. engr., G.I.P. rly., June, 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

TEN-BROEKE, BERTRAM ST. LEGER, M.C., Indian Police (supl., Bihar and Orissa) (b. 8th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Mar., 1921; supt., July, 1925; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937.

TENNANT, WILLIAM ROBERT, M.A., Indian C.S. (b. 26th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ.; arrived, 30th Nov., 1919, and served in the United Provs. as asst. magr. and collr.; dep. supt. of census, March, 1921; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1923; depy. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1924; ditto, U.P., Nov., 1925; addl. dep. sec., govt. of India, finance dept., Nov., 1927; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Dec., 1929; confd., Mar., 1930; offg. financial adviser, milty. finance, Apl., 1933; acct.-genl., Bombay, Oct., 1934; dir. of audit, defence services, Oct., 1937.

TERRY, DOUGLAS EDGAR, India Office (hr. clerical officer) (b. 15th May, 1899).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st June, 1920, after previous service from 2nd May, 1917; hr. clerical officer, Nov., 1935.

TEULON, OLIVER HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 29th June, 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., Sept., 1923; confd., Mar.,

1927; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1928; offg. ch. engr., June, 1929; confd., July, 1933; ret'd., June, 1938.

TEW, GILBERT CHARLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th June, 1884).—Educ. at Warwick Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec., rev. dept., July, 1916; pte. sec. to the lieut.-govr., July, 1919, to March, 1920; dep. commr.
