(s.p.t.), May, 1923; confd., July, 1924; custodian of enemy property, Burma, from Oct., 1924; offg. excise commr., June, 1927; confd., June, 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

THA, MAUNG CHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 14th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at Government High School, Minbu, Rangoon University and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

THACKER, PITAMBERDAS LAVJI, B.A., LL.B., Bombay late Judicial Dept. (b. 31st July, 1881).—Joined the dept., 29th Mar., 1925; 6th presdy. mag., Mar., 1925; ret'd., July, 1936.

THACKWELL, ALBERT HENRY, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt., 19th June, 1920; asst. works manager, July, 1920; works manager, June, 1925; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., July, 1932; May, 1933, and again, Apl., 1935; confd., Jan., 1937.

THADANI, NENUMAL POHOOMAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Educ. in India and at Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to I.C.S. by nomination; arrived, 4th Jan., 1917, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. offr.; jt. mag., July, 1922; addl. dep. commr., June, 1925; magte. and collr. (provl. sub.), Dec., 1931; confd., Oct., 1933.

THAKOR, KRISHNALAL KIRPARAM, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service (b. 4th Feb., 1879).—Joined the service as offg. sub-judge, 2nd June, 1909; confd., Apl., 1911; asst. judge, Jan., 1927; judge and sess. judge, Mar., 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

THAKUR, KESHAR SADASHIV, Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (civil surg., Bengal) (b. 2nd Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 31st July, 1909; in milty. employ up to 22nd Feb., 1921; offg. civil surgeon, Bengal, Sept., 1921; ditto 24th Parganas and protector of emigrants, Calcutta, Feb., 1934.

THAMBAN, VENGANAD KOVILAGAM RAJAGOPALA (see Rajagopala Thamban, V. K.).

THAN, U BA, late Judl. Dept., Burma (b. 26th Mar., 1880).—Joined the service, 22nd July, 1908; asst. judge, July, 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1929; confd., Oct., 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

THAPAR, PREM NATH, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 13th April, 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1933; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), July, 1935.

THAVARDAS DAYARAM, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 8th Jan., 1875).—Joined the dept., 1st Dec., 1903; sub-judge, March, 1904; offg. asst. judge and asst. sessions judge, April, 1925; retd., Jan., 1930.

THAW, ALFRED BA, M.B., Major, I.M.S., Burma Medl. dept. (civil surg.) (b. 13th March, 1892).—First commn. dated 15th Jan., 1918; in milty. employ prior to 29th May, 1924; asst. medl. supt., Rangoon genl. hosp., June, 1924; civil surg., March, 1926.

THAW, U PO, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th July, 1875).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., 11th Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1928; retd., July, 1930.

THEIN, MAUNG KYAW, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. customs) (b. 10th June, 1910).—Educ. at Government High School, Akyab, Rangoon University and St. John's College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. collr. customs, Nov., 1937.

THEIN, U BA HLA, Burma Judl. Service (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 24th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 11th Jan., 1909; asst. judge, July, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; confd., Nov., 1937.

THI, U BA, B.A., Income Tax Dept., Burma (asst. commr.) (b. 22nd Sept., 1886).—Apptd. to dept., 9th July, 1923; inc. tax officer, Dec., 1923; offg. asst. commr., Aug., 1928; confd., Dec., 1930.

THICKETT, JOSEPH HEDLEY, M.A. (Cantab.) late Indian Educational Service (b. 3rd July, 1880).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1907, as inspr. of schools, Patna divn.; principal, training coll., Patna, Oct., 1908; retd., Mar., 1932.

THIN, U BA (AI), B.A., late Burma Civil Service (b. 6th Apl., 1876).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., May, 1910; dep. commr., April, 1926; retd., July, 1931.

THIRUMALAI IYENGAR, MANDAYAM ANNADHORAI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 6th Nov., 1907).—Educ. at Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934.

THIRUVEN KATA CHARI, R., Indian C.S., name changed to R. T. CHARI (q.v.).

THOM, HERBERT JAMES, M.C., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 24th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., May, 1923; confd., May, 1929; services lent to H.H. the Maharaja Bahadur of Benares from Feb., 1927; supt., Dec., 1931; princpl., police trng. sch., Apl., 1936.

THOM, Sir JOHN GIBB, D.S.O., M.C., M.A., LL.B., Judicial Dept., United Provs. (ch. justice, high ct., Allahabad).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, 17th Mar., 1931; offg. ch. justice, ditto, Mar., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937; knighted, May, 1937.

THOMAS, ALAN ROBERT, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. 26th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 22nd Dec., 1911; in mil. employ from 24th Sept., 1915, to 11th Oct., 1919; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; offg. consvr., Jan., 1933 to Feb., 1933.

THOMAS, ANTHONY RYLANDS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 8th Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1929.

THOMAS, ARNOLD NEWALL, D.S.O., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lon.), M.B., B.S. (Lon.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 7th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service, 27th July, 1907, and served in the mil. dept. prior to 15th Jan., 1920; personal asst. to surg.-gen., Jan., 1920; offg. supt. of Mahableswar, Oct., 1924; confd., May, 1926; presy. surg., Oct., 1927; retd., Aug., 1935.

THOMAS, BERNARD GORDON PROTHERO, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1906; supt., Apl., 1920; asst. inspr.-genl. rly. police, Mar., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1933; O.B.E., Jan., 1934; dep. inspr.-genl. Oct., 1936.

THOMAS, DAVID REES, O.B.E., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 12th April, 1889).—Apptd. chemical examiner to govt., Punjab, 1st March, 1924; O.B.E., June, 1932.

THOMAS, EDWARD FRANCIS, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th June, 1880).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; served in police dept., Feb., 1907, to April, 1908; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1912; ditto, and dep. commr., salt, abkari, etc., Nov., 1912; sec. to commr., salt, abkari and sep. rev., Oct., 1915; on mily. duty from May, 1917, to Jan., 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; offg. director of industries, Madras, Nov., 1922; collr. and dist. mag., March, 1925; collr. of Madras, Dec., 1926; collr. and dist. magte., June, 1928; offg. 3rd member, bd. of rev., Madras, July, 1931; ditto (provl. sub.), Sept., 1932; offg. 2nd member, bd. of rev., Madras, Mar., 1933; offg. 1st member, ditto, July, 1933; confd. as 3rd member, ditto, and 2nd member (provl. subs.), Aug., 1933; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, May, 1934; 1st member, bd. of rev. (provl.), Jan., 1935; subst., Feb., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1935; retd., Feb., 1938.

THOMAS, FREDERICK WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A., Hon. Ph.D. (Munich), late India Office.—Educ. at Birmingham, and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; elected Fellow of Trinity, 1892; apptd. asst. librarian, 1898; librarian, Dec., 1902; has (with Profr. Cowell) translated from the Sanskrit 'The Harsa-Carita of Bana' (Oriental Translation Fund, N.S.); 'India Office Library Publications' 'Catalogue of the Royal Society Collection' (in conjunction with Mr. Tawney), 1903; 'The Aufrecht Collection, 1908'; 'Catalogue of the Library of the India Office', Vol. I; Supplement 2, 1895-1909 (joint-author); other publications; retired, Sept., 1927 on appt. to Boden professorship of Sanskrit in Univ. of Oxford; C.I.E., Jan., 1928.

THOMAS, GEOFFREY BENION, M.B., Ch.B., M.Sc. (Sheffield), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), M.R.C.O.G. (Engl.), Captain, I.M.S., Madras Medical Dept. (b. 28th Jan., 1905).—First Commission dated Feb., 1933; in military employ until June, 1938; specialist in obstetrics with gynaecology, June, 1938.

THOMAS, GEOFFREY DAVID IVOR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 21st Aug., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. settlement offr., Oct., 1937.

THOMAS, GEORGE ARTHUR, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th May, 1877).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 30th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay (Imperial customs service), May, 1906; ditto, Madras, Jan., 1910; asst. collr., Bombay, Nov., 1911; temp. dep. sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Bombay, Nov., 1915; sec. to govt., genl., educ., marine and eccl. depts., Oct., 1916; also sec. to indigenous industries committee; collr. and mag., Feb., 1920; secty. to govt., rev. dept., April, 1922, and Dec., 1924; ditto, gen. educ., etc., depts., Dec., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. chief sec. to govt., rev. dept., Bombay, Jan., 1926; confd., April, 1926; collr. of Bombay and supt. of stamps, Jan., 1927; offg. commr. in Sind, April, 1928; confd., Sept., 1929; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, Apl., 1931; chairman, re-organisation comtee.; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

THOMAS, GEORGE HECTOR, Judicial Dept., United Provs. (chief judge, ch. ct., Oudh) (b. 23rd June, 1884).—Apptd. govt. advocate, ch. court, Lucknow, Nov., 1925; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, May, 1934; chief judge, ditto, July, 1938.

THOMAS, HERBERT PERCIVAL, C.I.E., B.Sc., M.A.I.E.E., Public Works Dept., Punjab (ch. engr., elec.) (b. 12th Dec., 1879).—Apptd. suptg. engr., engrg. circle, Lahore, 16th Oct., 1926; offg. ch. engr., hydro-elec., Apl., 1929; ch. engr., elec. bch., Apl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1933.

THOMAS, JOHN HERBERT, F.I.A., Govt. of India, Commerce Dept. (supt. of insur.) (b. 21st Feb., 1895).—Apptd. to home civil service, 1914; services loaned to govt. of India, dept. of commerce as supt. of insurance, June, 1938.

THOMAS, ROBERT HENRY, C.S.I., D.S.O., Brigadier, R.E., ret'd., late Survey of India (b. 23rd April, 1877).—First commn. dated 21st June, 1896; apptd. to survey of India dept. as asst. supt., 17th July, 1901; dep. supt., March, 1910; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to June, 1919; dir., Jan., 1926; surveyor-genl., Dec., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1933; C.S.I., Jan. 1934.

THOMAS, VINCENT EUSTACE, A.M.I.Struct E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 16th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as temp. engr., G.I.P. rly., 17th Aug., 1916; resdt. engr., May, 1922; exec. engr., G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1935.

THOMASSON, JAMES, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (resdt. elec. engr.).—Apptd. resdt. elec. engr. (temp.) 21st Sept., 1926; ditto (perm.), Dec., 1929.

THOMPSON, Miss AMY, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 5th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1915, as head mistress, govt girls' high school, Amraoti; inspectress of schools, Jan., 1923; ret'd., Nov., 1938.

THOMPSON, BRIAN BELL, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 16th June, 1898).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; supt. of police, Central India Agency, April, 1927; supt., Nov., 1932; awarded Indian Police medal, July, 1936.

THOMPSON, CLARENCE EDWARD, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 20th April, 1904; asst. engr., Oct., 1907; resdt. engr., Nov., 1911; dist. engr., June, 1924; divl. engr., Nov., 1926; offg. dep. ch. engr. (maintenance), G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

THOMPSON, CLAUDE MATTHEW, Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Survey of India (b. 21st Sept., 1883).—First commn. dated 21st Jan., 1903; apptd. asst. supt., trigl. survey, 1st Nov., 1907; 2nd asst. dir. of surveys, Bengal and Assam, Nov., 1913; temp. reverted to mil. duty from Jan., 1915, to Mar., 1919; dep. supt., Sept., 1922; asst. surveyor-gen., Jan., 1926; supt., Apl., 1928; offg. dir., eastern circle, May, 1932; offg. dir., variously from May, 1934; confd., Sept., 1937; ret'd., Sept., 1938.

THOMPSON, COLIN HUSTWICK, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 20th Mar., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

THOMPSON, EDWARD HENRY, M.C., Office of High Commr. for India (hr. executive officer, store dept.) (b. 30th Oct., 1889).—Apptd. second class clerk, 30th June, 1908; on mil. service from Jan., 1916; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; attached Indian del., Imp. econ. conf., July to Sept., 1932; hr. exec. officer, Dec., 1935.

THOMPSON, ERIC BERTRAM, M.B.E., Indian Posts and Tels. (dep. p.m.g.) (b. 21st July, 1887).—Joined as proby. supt., 25th Apl., 1908; confd., Dec., 1909; on depn. to I.E.F., Aug., 1914, to Apl., 1919; deputy p.m.g., July, 1935.

THOMPSON, JOSEPH HERBERT, M.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1923; arrived, 12th Dec., 1923, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1924; offg. asst. commr., D.I. Khan, July, 1926; city magte., Peshawar, Dec., 1926; dist. magte., ditto, Jan., 1928; asst. commr., Nowshera, Sept., 1929; divl. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Aug., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, Peshawar and Hazara, Aug., 1931; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1932; secty. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1934; resdt., Jaipur, Mar., 1938; pol. agent, E. Rajputana States, Dec., 1938; resdt. Jaipur (in addn.) May, 1939 to Oct., 1939.

THOMPSON, MALCOLM STUART HAUGHTON, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (princpl., govt. coll., Madras) (b. 20th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 6th Jan., 1910; asst. inspr. of schools, Dec., 1911; inspr., Sept., 1922; dist. educ. offcr., Feb., 1923; offg. princpl., govt. training coll., Rajahmundry, Nov., 1927;

offg. inspr. schools, Coorg and Bangalore, Feb., 1936; princpl., govt. Muhammadan Coll., Madras, Feb., 1937.

THOMPSON, SAMUEL PERCY, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 16th Dec., 1902).—Educ. at St. Olave's and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub. collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1928; asst. secty., Indian franchise comee., Jan., 1932.

THOMPSON, WILLIAM PERCY, M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Jan., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, Oct., 1905; asst. engr., Nov., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. supt. engr., May, 1932; ditto (prov. perm.), Jan., 1935; confd., July, 1935; retd. Jan., 1939.

THOMSON, ALEXANDER WELSH, O.B.E., India Office (ch. exec. offer., mil. dept.) (b. 26th Dec., 1878).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 12th Nov., 1900; temp. attached to mil. dept. from Dec., 1915; minor staff clerk, April, 1918; staff clerk (actg.), Nov., 1919; dep. reading clerk to council, Feb., 1920, to Feb., 1923; higher exec. officer, June, 1922; suptg. exec. officer, June, 1924; asst. acct.-genl., Dec., 1934; assessor of inc. tax, from Dec., 1934, to Dec., 1937; actg. chief exec. offer., mil. dept., Nov., 1937; confd., Jan., 1938; O.B.E., June, 1938.

THOMSON, DAVID, M.A., B.Sc., Ph.D., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 29th Mar., 1879).—Joined the service, 28th Feb., 1911, as profr., Cotton Coll., Gauhati; inspr. of schools, Oct., 1922; principal, Murarichand Coll., Sylhet, Nov., 1923; offg. dir. of public instrn., Assam, Feb., 1925; principal, Cotton Coll., Gauhati, Dec., 1926; retd., Mar., 1934.

THOMSON, ERIC SCOTT, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., April, 1926; confd., July, 1933.

THOMSON, GEORGE, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 3rd July, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 28th Feb., 1907; dist. loco. supt., Sept., 1913; offg. dep. loco. supt., Nov., 1923; confd., Feb., 1930; loco. and carr. and wagon supt., July, 1930; on spl. duty with rly. bd., Oct., 1930-Feb., 1931; dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. rly., Jan., 1932; offg. ch. mechl. engr., May, 1934; retd., Jan., 1938.

THOMSON, JAMES, India Office (hr. exec. offer., mil. dept.) (b. 1st Apl., 1895).—Apptd. exec. offer., 16th Aug., 1927; actg. hr. exec. offer., Mar., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936; actg. asst. information offer., Oct., 1936, and again, Dec., 1937 to Feb., 1938; actg. informn. offer., Oct., 1937, to Dec., 1937; sec. admin. side, mil. dept., Feb., to Oct., 1938; attached to Burma Office, Jan., 1939.

THOMSON, JOHN STUART, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Aug., 1888).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 18th Nov., 1912, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. asst. to commr., Lahore, March, 1914; on mily. duty from March, 1915; att. to civil admin., Mesopotamia, from June, 1917, to Feb., 1922; asst. pol. offr., Hillah, Feb., 1919; dep. commr. (prov.), April, 1925; development commr., N.W.F.P., May, 1931; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1931; secty.

to govt. (rev.), N.W.F.P., Apl., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1935; retd., July, 1937.

THOMSON, RONALD ORD CAMPBELL A.M.I.C.E., M.C., Indian State Railways (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 11th Dec., 1892).—Asst. exec. engr., 27th Nov., 1919; exec. engr., Apl., 1936.

THOMSON, The Rev. THOMAS ALBERT, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) Bengal (b. 6th July, 1884).—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1913; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1915; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1925.

THOMSON-GLOVER, Lt.-Col. J. W. (see Glover, Lt.-Col. J. W. T.).

THORNBURGH, DAVID GILL, Lieut., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 7th Feb., 1911).—First Commn., 27th Aug., 1931; under trng. in Rajputana, Oct., 1935; pers. asst. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Nov., 1935; under trng. in Punjab, Jan., 1936; under trng. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1936; under trng. in Punjab, Mar., 1937; asst. pol. agent, Mekram, Sept., 1937; 2nd asst. pol. agent, Mekram (in addn.), June, 1938; under sec. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, Oct., 1938; confd. in Indian Political Service, Feb., 1939; serving in N.W.F.P., Nov., 1939.

THORNE, HAROLD LIONEL, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. offer., N.W. rly.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Joined the service Sept., 1905; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1919; divl. transpn. offer., May, 1935.

THORNE, JOHN ANDERSON, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty. to govr.-genl., public dept.) (b. 17th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917, sec. to board of revenue, land revenue, March, 1920; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1922, ditto, May, 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1931; secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras) Nov., 1931; offg. jt. secty. to govt. of India, home dept., June, 1935; addl. ditto, Oct., 1935; jt. secty., ditto, Apl., 1936; again, Nov., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; secty. to govr.-genl. (public) Apl., 1938; temp. member, govr.-genl's exec. council, June, to October, 1939.

THORNTON, FRANCIS RUTHVEN, M.C., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 5th Aug., 1884).—First commn., 25th Feb., 1921; civil surg., Bombay, Feb., 1925; ditto, Dharwar, Nov., 1937; ditto, Poona, June, 1938; retd. Aug., 1939.

THORNTON, HUGH AYLMER, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 10th May, 1872).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 30th Nov., 1896, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; supt., Northern Shan States, Sept., 1909; dep. commr., and ditto, Nov., 1911; C.I.E., June, 1916; supt., and pol. officer, Southern Shan States, April, 1920; commr., Jan., 1922; C.S.I., Jan., 1930; retd., Nov., 1931.

THORNTON, NORMAN, M.I.E.E., Public Works Dept., Punjab (exec. engr.) (b. 5th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service, 22nd Feb., 1934; exec. engr., Mar., 1934; offg. dep. ch. engr., Mar., 1935.

THORPE, ARTHUR FANSHAWE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 13th Nov., 1878).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1902; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; under-sec. to govt., P.W. dept., Bombay, April, 1912, to Feb., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., Aug., 1924; confd., June, 1926; ret'd., July, 1930.

THOULESS, KENNETH JAMES, M.A. (Mech. Sc. Hons., Cantab.), Indian Posts and Tels. (director, S. and B. circle) (b. 13th May, 1900).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1923, as asst. div. engr., Tels.; div. engr., Oct., 1932; dir., S. and B. circle, Aug., 1935.

THRUPP, WILFRED GRAHAM, late Indian Police (b. 26th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 28th Nov., 1912; confd., Oct., 1915; supt., Jan., 1926; prin., police training coll., Sardah, Feb., 1934; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

THUBRON, JOHN BROWN SYDNEY, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Aug., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1901, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1909; chairman of the Aden Port Trust and of exec. cttee. of the Aden settlt. from May, 1912; C.I.E., Sept., 1918; chairman, Karachi Port Trust, Nov., 1919; actg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1921; confd. Sept., 1925; offg. chief engr. Dec., 1926; confd., Nov., 1928; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

THUILLIER, LESLIE CARDEW, Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Survey of India Dept. (b. 7th Oct., 1877).—First commn. dated 20th Jan., 1897; apptd. to survey of India, Sept., 1901; dep. supt., April, 1910; on mil. service in Mesopotamia, Nov., 1917 to March, 1919; offg. supt., northern circle, March 1924; war service, North West Frontier, 1897-98, Tirah Expedny. Force, medal, two clasps; supt. dir., Oct., 1925; dir. (temp.), Jan., 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

THURSFIELD, Rev GERALD ARTHUR RICHARD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt., Burma (b. 13th April, 1886).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, 18th Jan., 1917; on mil. service from 17th July, 1917, to 17th Oct., 1919; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1927; offg. archdeacon, June, 1935; ret'd. Feb., 1938.

TIDY, DOUGLAS RAMSAY, late Indian Police (b. 13th Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 16th Nov., 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

TIKA RAM MISRA, RAI BAHADUR, PANDIT, M.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., United Provs. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 1st Feb., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1906; sub-judge, Oct., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1934.

TILNEY-BASSETT, Rev. HUGH FRANCIS EMRA, M.A. (Oxon), Indian Eccles. Estabt (junr. chaplain) (b. 16th Jan., 1901).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, Bengal, 12th Aug., 1929; confd., Nov., 1931.

TIMMS, ARCHIBALD BARTON, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th June, 1883).—Joined as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

TIMS, BASIL DUNCAN, India Office (staff officer for currency duties, fin. dept.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1898).—War service: R.N. Air Service & R. Navy, 1916-1919; apptd. clerical officer, 19th Sept., 1921; on depn. to India in connection with Royal

Commn. on Agriculture in India, Oct. 1927 to May 1928; higher clerical officer, Apl., 1930; staff officer for currency duties, finl. dept., Dec., 1937.

TIN, MAUNG (A), Indian C.S. (dep. secty., agric. and for. dept., Burma) (b. 5th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ. and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., Burma, educn. dept., Dec., 1934; dep. secty., agric. and for. dept., Nov., 1937.

TIN, MAUNG SEIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 15th June, 1904).—Educ. at University of Rangoon and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Mar., 1939.

TIN TOON, MAUNG A. (see Toon, Maung Tin).

TIN TUT, MAUNG (see Tut, Maung Tin).

TIN, U PE MAUNG, M.A., B.Litt., Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th April, 1888).—Apptd. to the I.E.S. as lecturer in Oriental Studies, Univ. coll., Rangoon, from 1st Sept., 1924.

TINKER, HAROLD, B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 8th Dec., 1878).—Joined the service as prof., training coll., Allahabad, 12th Oct., 1915; princpl., training coll., Agra, July, 1920; offg. inspr. of schools, Feb., 1929; princpl. training coll., Allahabad, Sept., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

TINKLER, FREDERICK CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 3rd Apl., 1915).—Educ. at March Grammar Sch. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

TINSLEY, WILLIAM STEWART, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, Oct., 1914, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; ret'd. April, 1939.

TINT, MAUNG BA, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty., fin. dept., Burma) (b. 6th Nov., 1908).—Educ. at St. Peter's High Sch. and Kelly High Sch., Mandalay, Rangoon Univ., and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., fin. dept., Nov., 1937.

TINT, SOE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 5th Nov., 1910).—Educated at Govt. High School, Rangoon, Rangoon University and Wadham College, Oxford; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

TIPPER, GEORGE HOWLETT, M.A. (Cantab), F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 26th Feb., 1881).—Apptd. asst. supt., geological survey, 6th Dec., 1903; curator, geological museum, Calcutta, Aug., 1910; apptd. to I.A.R.O., May, 1915, to Dec., 1919; supt., Jan., 1920; ret'd., Sept., 1929; mineral adviser to H.C. for India, Nov., 1928; loaned for service under Min. of Economic Warfare, Sept., 1939.

TIPSON, KENNETH GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 29th May, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 15th Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Mar., 1933; services placed at disposal of Benares State, Apl., 1937.

TIRUMALAI AYYANGAR, MALUR SRINIVASA AYYANGAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 1st Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 1st June, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1921; exec. engr., Jan., 1930.

TODD, HERBERT JOHN, Indian Political Service (b. 15th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Burma, 4th Dec., 1913; confd., Nov., 1914; in milty. employ from 1st Mar., 1917, to 8th Jan., 1919; under civil administration, Bagdad, from Jan., 1919, to July, 1921; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Oct., 1921; ditto, Kalat, 5th July, 1922; pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1927; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states, Oct., 1935; presdt., Council of State, Bharatpur (in addn.), from May, 1936, to Aug., 1936; prime minister and vice-presdt., council of state, Jaipur, April, 1939.

TODD, JOHN, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 3rd May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Jan., 1921; dep. consvr., May, 1925; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

TODD, NORMAN ALBERT, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys (N.W. Riy.) (b. 6th Feb., 1877).—Joined the service, 24th June, 1899; officiated variously between July, 1909, and Dec., 1914, as asst. traffic supt.; confd. as ditto, Dec., 1914; O.B.E., Sept., 1921; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; transportation officer, July, 1927; senr. asst. comml., Mar., 1931; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

TODHUNTER, SIR CHARLES GEORGE, K.C.S.I., F.R.Hist.S., F.S.S., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Aldenham Gr. Sch. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. and head asst. collr., asst. sec. to govt., dep. registrar, and registrar, high court; dep. commr., salt, &c., rev., offg., Sept., 1896; confd., Nov., 1897; sec. to commr., salt, &c., rev., offg., Jan., 1898; confd., Oct., 1900; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1903; presdt., Central Prov. excise committee, Dec., 1903, to Nov., 1904; on special duty to advise on excise arrangements in the Central India states and the Malwa opium agency, Jan., 1905; sec., excise commn., Oct., 1905; collr. of customs, Madras (impl. customs service), Oct., 1906; employed in fin. dept., govt. of India, as inspr.-gen., excise and salt, Dec., 1909, to April, 1912; collr. and dist. mag., Apr., 1912; prest., life-saving appliances cttee., Oct., 1913, to Feb., 1914; protector of emigrants, June, 1915; sec. to govt., judl. dept., July, 1915; ditto, local and municipal, &c., depts., Jan., 1916; acting member, bd. of rev. Nov., 1916; confd., July, 1917; chief sec. to govt., Feb., 1919; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, April, 1919; member, ditto, Aug., 1919, to April, 1924; C.S.I., Jan., 1920; K.C.S.I., June, 1921; president, taxation enquiry cttee., Oct., 1924; ret'd., May, 1925.

TOLLEMACHE, HAROLD VINCENT, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b.

14th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Feb., 1927.

TOLLER, GEORGE GORDON TAYLOR, O.B.E., V.D., Indian State Rlys. (ch. engr., Burma Rlys.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 6th Jan., 1910; ditto (perm.), July, 1911; on milty. duty from 18th Dec., 1914, to 18th Dec., 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; dist. engr., April, 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1927; ditto, Irrawaddy bridge constr. Nov., 1932; exec. engr., ditto, July, 1933; offg. dep. ch. engr., Mar., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1936; ch. engr., July, 1937; presdt., rly. bd., Burma, Mar., 1938.

TOLLINTON, HUGH PHILLIPS, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 19th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Radley, Balliol Coll., Oxford, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932, and again, Apl., 1933; asst. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1934; ditto, Tank, Feb., 1935; ditto, Charsadda, Nov., 1936; home secty., govt., N.W.F.P., June, 1938; secty., ditto devel. depts., July, 1938; confd., Sept., 1938.

TOLSON, KENNETH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 11th Nov., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 3rd Dec., 1931; awarded Indian police medal, 1935; confd., Jan., 1934; a.d.c. to govr. of Bengal, May, 1938.

TOMKINS, THOMAS LESLIE, India Audit Office (exec. officer) (b. 4th Dec., 1889).—Apptd. clerical officer, 29th Mar., 1922; exec. officer, Oct., 1926.

TOMKINS, WILLIAM DOUGLAS, O.B.E., India Office (principal, economic and overseas dept.) (b. 29th July, 1882).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 24th Jan., 1902; transf. to rev. and stat. dept., Mar., 1906; minor staff clerk, May, 1914; staff clerk, June, 1919; sec., Indian delegations at Intl. Labour Conference Genoa, 1920, and Intl. Conference on Transit and Communications, Barcelona, 1921; administrative officer, April, 1922; pte. sec. to delegate for India at Intl. Economic Conference, Genoa, 1922, and Hague Conference, 1922; delegate for India at Intl. Conference on Customs Formalities, Geneva, Oct., 1923; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Aug., 1924; actg. principal, Dec., 1927; confd., July, 1929; secty. to Indian delegations to Assembly of League of Nations, 1934 to 1936; O.B.E., May, 1937.

TOMS, FRAZER WOODLAND, late Indian Police, Punjab (b. 22nd Mar., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1907; asst. supt., Oct., 1908; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1913; supt., Sept., 1915; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1928; confd. (provl. subst.), Sept., 1930; confd., July, 1931; ret'd., July, 1938.

TOOGOOD, GEORGE WATTS, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt.) (b. 20th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1920 as asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., E.B. rly (provl.), Aug., 1934.

TOOKE, COLIN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Burma) (b. 30th Sept., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Burma, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

TOON, J. CHAN (see Chan Toon, J.).

TOON, MAUNG TIN, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1866).—Educ. at Norris Coll., Rangoon, and Cambridge Univ.; joined the service, 1st July, 1922; arrived, 7th Sept., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1925; confd., May, 1932.

TOPLIS, ARTHUR ROBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Nov., 1885).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; on settlt. duty from Oct., 1915; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1917; mag. and collr., July, 1924; dep. commr., Oct., 1925; offg. commr., June, 1930; and again, May, 1932; retd., Jan., 1936.

TORICK, AMEER ALI, Judl. dept., Bengal (judge, high ct., Calcutta).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 28th Nov., 1931.

TOSTEVIN, FRED PERCY, O.B.E., M.A. (Oxon), Indian Ednel. Service (principal, King Edward Coll., Amraoti) (b. 29th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Jan., 1912; prof. of English, Morris Coll., Nagpur, Feb., 1912; dep. dir. of public instrn., Central Provs., July, 1921; principal, Morris Coll., Nagpur, April, 1922; principal, King Edward Coll., Amraoti, April, 1923; O.B.E., June, 1935.

TOTTENHAM, SIR ALEXANDER ROBERT LOFTUS, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st July, 1873).—Educ. at Clifton and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to commrs. of land rev., July, 1906, to April, 1911; sub. collr. and jt. mag., April, 1911; collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1916; temp. addl. sec. to govt., rev. dept., April, 1919; commr. of income tax, Nov., 1922; senior member, central board of revenue, Nov., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; Kt., Jan., 1931; retd., June, 1933; administrator, Pudukkottai State.

TOTTENHAM, CLIVE GORDON, Barr., late Indian Police Service (Madras) (b. 6th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1911; asst. supt., 2nd Nov., 1912; awarded King's police medal, Dec., 1921; offg. dist. supt., May, 1925; confd., July, 1926; retd., Jan., 1930.

TOTTENHAM, SIR GEORGE RICHARD FREDERICK, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 18th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Harrow and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1914, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. to resdt., Travancore and Cochin, Sept., 1917; sub. collr. and jt. mag., June, 1919; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, army dept., 1924; addl. ditto, Aug., 1926; offg. ditto, Sept., 1926; dep. sec. ditto, Nov., 1926; C.I.E., June, 1930; addl. secty. to govt., finance dept., Madras; secty. to govt. of India, Army dept., Apl., 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1936; member, council of state,

Oct., 1936; knighted, May, 1937; presdt. of council, Bharatpur, Mar., 1938.

TOWER, JOHN ALEXANDER, Indian State Rlys. (ch. transportation supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 10th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service, 22nd Dec., 1911; asst. traffic supt., Dec., 1914; dist. ditto, Feb., 1919; divl. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; offg. dep. agent, Sept., 1927; offg. dir. of fin., rly. board, March, 1929; dep. agent, July, 1929; ch. transportation supt. (prov.), July, 1932.

TOWLE, JOHN HOWARD, M.A., late Indian Ednel. Service (b. 2nd Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 12th March, 1919; dir. of public instrn., Feb., 1922; retd., Aug., 1933.

TOWNEND, HERBERT PATRICK VICTOR, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal) (b. March, 1887).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl. dept., Aug., 1917; jt. mag. and dep. collr. and dir. of civil supplies, Oct., 1918; offg. dir. gen. of comml. intellge., Jan., 1920; dep. sec. to govt. of Bengal, Finance, Commerce and Marine Depts., April, 1923; sec., Indian coal cttee., Oct., 1924, to April, 1925; mag. and collr., May, 1925; dep. commr., April, 1926; offg. secty., agri and indus. and pub. wks. depts., Bengal, Nov., 1930; offg. sec. local self-govt. dept., Apl., 1932; confd., Sept., 1932; offg. rural dev. commr., Feb., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; spec. offcr., home dept., govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1937; confd. as commr. divn., Mar., 1938.

TOWNSEND, CREWE ARMAND HAMILTON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 5th June, 1874).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 7th Dec., 1898 and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; offg. dir. of land records, May, 1911; dep. commr., Nov., 1914; dir. of agric. and industries, Nov., 1914; was also controller of munitions, Punjab circle; dir. of industries, May, 1918; ditto, and dir. of civil supplies, June, 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; offg. commr., May, 1923; offg. finl. commr. and secty. to govt. rev. dept., March, 1927; pres., commee. on co-operation, Madras, Aug., 1927; commr., Mar., 1928; finl. commr. (revenue), May, 1928; retd., Nov., 1931.

TOWNSEND, FREDERICK OWEN, D.S.O., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 1st Mar., 1878).—Joined the service, 5th Sept. 1905; asst. engr., Oct., 1910; on mil. duty from 25th March, 1915, to 4th May, 1919; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; retd., Sept., 1931.

TOWNSEND, REGINALD STEPHEN, M.C., M.D., Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 10th Nov., 1882).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1908; in mil. employ to 31st May, 1911, and again from 1st Oct., 1914, to 23rd Mar., 1921; civil surg., Apl., 1922; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., United Provs., Dec., 1934; inspr.-genl., civil hosp. and dir. p.h., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1937; retd., Nov., 1939.

TOWNSEND, EDMUND GEORGE, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt., Burma Rlys.) (b. 5th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt.,

N.W. Rly., 24th Jan., 1914; asst. rolling stock officer, Nov., 1926; divl. ditto., Sept., 1927; transferred to Burma Rlys., 29th Jan., 1930; dist. loco supt., Feb., 1930.

TOYNE, WILLIAM, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 14th Dec., 1905, as asst. loco. supt.; asst. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; divl. mechl. engr., N.W. rly. (provl.), Aug., 1934; perm., July, 1937; ret'd., July, 1937.

TREASURE, EDWARD SHEPPARD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 4th July, 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Madras, 22nd Nov., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937.

TREASURE, PATRICK MOORE, Indian Police (b. 21st Aug., 1916).—Asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, apptd. on probn., Dec., 1936; confd. April, 1938.

TREGENZA, EDWARD LESLIE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 15th May, 1868).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 11th March, 1922; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., March, 1928.

TRENCH, Rev. ALBERT CHARLES, M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 25th Jan., 1884).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Madras, 6th Oct., 1920; junr. chaplain, 6th Oct., 1922; senr. chaplain, 6th Oct., 1922; senr. chaplain, 29th Dec., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1938.

TRENCH, WILLIAM LAUNCELOT CROSBIE, C.I.E., B.A.I., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd July, 1881).—Joined, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; princpl. coll. of civil engineering, Poona, Nov., 1922; dep. secty. to govt. P.W.D. (irrgn. and rlys.), Bombay, Oct., 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; ch. engr., June, 1935; chief engr. and secty. to govt., p.w.d., Sind, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1936; ret'd., July, 1936.

TRENCH, CHENEVIX (see Chenevix-Trench).

TRESHAM, CECIL HENRY, V.D., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 3rd Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1903; extra asst. supt., June, 1911; supt., Oct., 1932.

TRESIDDER, ALFRED GEDDES, C.I.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 15th Dec., 1881).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1908; offg. surg. to govr., Bombay, March, 1924; reverted to milty. employ from 17th Dec., 1924, to 31st March, 1925; surg. to govr. of Bombay, April, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1927; offg. civil surg., May, 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

TRESTON, MAURICE LAWRENCE, F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Burma Medl. Dept. (civil surg.) (b. 9th Feb., 1891).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1914; civil surg., June, 1923; asst. medl. supt. and police surg., Rangoon gen. hosp., March, 1926; supt., Dufferin hosp., Rangoon, Feb., 1928; gynaecologist, genl. hosp., Rangoon, June, 1934.

TREVASKIS, HUGH KENNEDY, O.B.E., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 14th June, 1882).—Educ. at Marlborough Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge (honours degree in math., 1st class); apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 29th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. luty. from July, 1917, to Dec., 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; dir. of land records, Punjab, April, 1923; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

TREVELYAN, HUMPHREY, Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 27th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, October, 1929; arrived, November, 1929, and served as asst. coll. and mag., Madras; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Mar., 1931; under-secty. to A.G.G. in Central Indian, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. opium agent in Malwa, Apl., 1933; under-secty., pol. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1934; offg. dep. secty. ditto, Apl., 1935; guardian to Maharajah of Gwalior, Nov., 1935; secty. to Resident and C.C., Coorg, Mar., 1937.

TREVOR, SIR CHARLES GERALD, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 28th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1903; dep. consvr., Nov., 1911; offg.; asst. inspr.-gen. of forests, Nov., 1917, to March, 1918; consvr., March, 1924; vice-pres. and prof. of forestry, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1926; on depn. with internl. congress of forestry, Stockholm, July, 1929; chief consvr., Feb., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; offg. inspr.-genl., and presdt., Forest Research inst., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1933; confd., July, 1934; on depn. with fourth Brit. Empire forestry conf. S. Africa, Aug., 1935; knighted, Feb., 1937; ret'd., Apl., 1938.

TREVOR, DIGBY BRUCE, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Aug., 1881).—Apptd. asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1906; dist. traffic supt. N.W. Rly., April, 1912; dep. traffic mgr. ditto, April, 1918; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; divl. supt. (provl.), Oct., 1924; confd., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. comml. manager, April, 1928; and again, April, 1929; ret'd., May, 1933.

TRIBHUBAN PRASAD, SINGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 13th May, 1913).—Educ. at the Banka H.E. Sch., Bhagalpur, Patna Univ., and London School of Economics; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

TRIGG, CHARLES THOMAS, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 15th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; on depn. to Forest research institute, Dehra Dun, from 14th July, 1922; dep. consvr., Jan., 1932.

TRIMMING, GEORGE AUGUSTUS RODERICK, Indian State Rlys. (ch. mechl. engr. E.I. Rly.) (b. 24th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 8th Oct., 1913; works manager, April, 1923; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Sept., 1928; confd., Mar., 1934; offg. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. rly., Mar., 1935; confd., July, 1935.

TRIMURTI, NARAYANASWAMI, M.A., India n Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 15th July, 1900).—Arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1925; transferred from Central Provs., 27th June, 1926; asst. consvr., Madras, July, 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1929.

TRIPATHI, SHYAMA CHANDRA, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (princpl., Ravenshaw Coll.) (b. 1st Mar., 1893).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1920; prof. of physics, Feb., 1921; offg. dep. dir. of public instn., Oct., 1930; confd., Aug., 1933; offg. princpl., Ravenshaw Coll., Feb., 1936; confd., Aug., 1936.

TRIVEDI, CHANDULAL MADHAVLAL, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 2nd July, 1893).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and at St. John's Coll. Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, Dec., 1917, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1924; offg. regr. co-op. societies and dir. of industries, Central Provs., June, 1925; confd., Nov., 1926; dep. commr., March, 1927; O.B.E., Jan., 1931; dep. sec., home dept., govt. of India, May, 1932; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1935; offg. chief secty. to govt., C.P., Oct., 1937.

TRIVEDI, NARAYAN VISHWANATH, Rao Bahadur, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 5th Sept., 1886).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 18th Jan., 1909, to 26th Aug., 1914; dep. supt., Aug., 1914; employed under dir., intell. bureau, from Oct., 1923, to Jan., 1929; promoted to I.P.D. as supt. from 24th Jan., 1929; dep. commr. of police, C.I.D., Bombay, Mar., 1939.

TRIVEDI, RAM NARAYAN, M.A., late Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 9th Dec., 1878).—Joined the service as temp. extra asst. commr., July, 1901; confd., March, 1903; sub-judge, Jan., 1908; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

TRIVEDI, RAMANLAL SHANKERLAL, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 20th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Bombay University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1932.

TRIVEDI, RATILAL VITHAL DAS, M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 13th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 11th Apl., 1921; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Aug., 1922; asst. dir. of audit, United Provs., Sept., 1928; currency officer, Cawnpore, Dec., 1928; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Sept., 1931, and again June, 1937; examr., outside audit dept. Bombay, Mar., 1935; examr., local audit dept., Bombay, Feb., 1936; dep. acct.-genl., Central Rev., Oct., 1937.

TROTMAN EWART WASHINGTON, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 10th July, 1895).—Educ. at the Rich Sch., Gloucester; joined the service 1st Nov., 1923; arrived, 28th Oct., 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dep. secty. to govt., home and eccl. depts., June, 1927; offg. under secty. to ditto, Aug., 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; offg. secty. to ditto, June, 1929; offg. collr. and mag., April, 1931; confd., Nov., 1935.

TROTMAN, Rev. LIONEL WILLIAM, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 12th Aug., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, Bengal, 19th June, 1930; junr. chaplain, June, 1932.

TROTTER, HUGH, Indian Forest Service (asst. forest economist, forest research institute and coll., Dehra Dun) (b. 16th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 30th Dec., 1913; on mil. service from 28th Nov., 1914, to 9th March, 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1918; asst. forest economist, Sept. 1921.

TROUSDELL, HENRY FRANK, Printing and Stationery dept. (manager, govt. of India press) (b. 14th July, 1891).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Nov., 1916; asst. manager, govt. of India press, Jan., 1926; offg. dep. contr., forms, Nov., 1927; confd., Dec., 1930; offg. manager, govt. of India press, Nov., 1931; confd., Oct., 1932.

TRUSCOTT, ROBERT RODGER BATE, Indian Police (b. 12th Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 14th Nov., 1935.

TRUTWEIN, PERCY WALLACE, B.A., late Burma Commission (b. 30th Jan., 1883).—Apptd. offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; confd., Aug., 1923; ret'd., Jan., 1938.

TUBBS, Right Rev. NORMAN HENRY, D.D., M.A., late Indian Eccl. Establ. (b. 5th July, 1879).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge (hon. classical and theolog. triposes), curate, Whitechapel parish church, 1902-5; sub-warden, Oxford and Cambridge hostel, Allahabad, 1905-8; prinpl., St. John's Sch., Agra, 1908-17; prinpl., Bishops Coll., Calcutta, 1917-23; bishop in Tinnevelly, Madura and Ramnad, 1923-1928; bishop of Rangoon, August, 1928, to Nov., 1934.

TUCK, HOWELL ARTHUR, Indian State Rlys. (dist. signal engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 26th Feb., 1888).—Asst. signal engr., 1st Nov., 1917; dist. ditto, 1st July, 1937.

TUCKER, Rev. GORDON ELIJAH, B.Sc., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 8th Oct., 1896).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, 22nd Feb., 1929; junr. chaplain Feb., 1931.

TUCKER, OWEN RUFUS, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E. Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt.) (b. 27th Mar., 1897).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1923; asst. supt., E.I. rly., July, 1925, transpn. supt. (power), June, 1935.

TUCKER, WILLIAM ALFRED, late Central Provs. C.S. (b. 1st July, 1880).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr., 5th May, 1903; in milty. employ from 13th April, 1915, to 15th June, 1916; on foreign service as Diwan of Bastar state, May, 1923; dep. commr., March, 1927; ret'd., May, 1931.

TUCKETT, ROBERT HENRY, India Office (hr. exec. officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 17th Jan., 1891).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 1st July, 1913; on mil. service from Dec., 1916, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; actg. hr. ditto, Sept., 1938; confd., June, 1939; assessor of income tax, Mar., 1939.

TUCKEY, ARTHUR DAVYS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Oct., 1886).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1913; jt. mag. and dep. collr., and ditto, Oct., 1917; settl. offr., Chota Nagpur, Dec., 1920; offg. dir. of land records and survey, March, 1922, and again, Feb., 1925; mag. and collr., Oct., 1923; offg. commr., April, 1930, Apl., 1933, and Oct., 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

TUCKWELL, CHARLES ADOLPHUS, O.B.E., late India posts and tels. (b. 20th Jan., 1884).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 26th May,

1909; asst. supt., workshops, Oct., 1928; supt., ditto, Dec., 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1938; retired, May, 1938.

TUDOR-OWEN, WILLIAM COURTENAY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 20th Feb., 1877).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd., after exam. of 1900; arrived, 29th Nov., 1901; and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; administr., Palitana State, Aug., 1905; tutor to Nawab of Junagad, Jan., 1912; on dept. to England with minor chiefs of Junagad and Palitana, March, 1913, to June, 1914; pol. agent, Jhalavad Prant, Jan., 1915; ditto, Gohilwad Prant, and administrator, Palitana State, June, 1916; asst. to agent to govt., Kathiawar, Oct., 1918; administrator, Bhavnagar State, Aug., 1919, to Nov., 1920; collr., Oct., 1921; collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent., Nov., 1922; offg. commr. of excise, April, 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

TUFFIELD, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt. [senr. scale]), G.I.P. Rly. (b. 19th Mar., 1889).—Asst. transpn. supt., (junr. scale), July, 1926; ditto, (senr. scale), June, 1937.

TUFNELL-BARRETT, HUGH, Indian C.S. (dep. sect. to govt. of India, dept. of lab.) (b. 13th Jan., 1900).—Educ. at St. John's Sch., Leatherhead, and Wellington Coll., India; joined the service, 10th Sept., 1923; arrived, 16th Nov., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under secty. pol. and appt. dept., March, 1927; confd., July, 1927; 2nd. addl. dep. secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. dept., June, 1930; offg. mag., and collr., Nov., 1931; offg. addl. dep. secty., pol. dept., Bengal, Nov., 1933; K.I.H. medal, June, 1938; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of lab., Nov., 1939.

TULL, THLMAS STUART, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 11th October, 1914).—Educated at Rossall and Jesus College, Oxford; appointed on probation, 24th Sept., 1938; confirmed, 20th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, October, 1939, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

TULLIS, J., B.Sc., Dept. of Industries, Bihar (b. 20th May, 1888).—Joined the service as prof., Bihari coll. of engrg., 4th April, 1924; offg. prinpl., April, 1925.

TUN, MAUNG SEIN NYO, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 24th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Rangoon University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. comm.; dep. sect., finance dept., Feb., 1939.

TURNBULL, FRANCIS FEARON, B.A., India Office (prinpl., milty. dept.) (b. 30th Apr., 1905).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge (Scholar); asst. clerk, House of Commons, 1928-30; apptd. asst. prinpl., 12th Aug., 1930; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state, Jan., 1932; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, Sept., 1934; actg. prinpl., July, 1935; confd., Apr., 1937.

TURNER, ALEXANDER BERTRAM, late Indian State Rlys., A.M.I.M.E. (b. 15th May, 1882).—Joined the service as dist. C. and W. supt., 6th May, 1921; supt., Feb., 1925; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., Aug., 1935; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Jan., 1936; confd., Jan., 1937; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

TURNER, ALFRED CHARLES, M.B.E., M.A., Indian Civil Service (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 12th March, 1892).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 11th May, 1920, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag.; M.B.E., June, 1919; jt. mag., Sept., 1921; offg. mag. and coll. from Feb. to Oct., 1924, and from March, 1925; confd., Feb., 1932; provl. supt. of census opers., United Provs., Apr., 1930; offg. dep. secty., United Provs., fin. dept., Nov., 1933; offg. secty. to govt., U.P., rev. and p.w. depts., Feb., 1935; ditto, fin. dept., Apr., 1936, commr. of income tax, Bengal, Nov., 1938, ditto, Bombay Presidency, Sind and Baluchistan, April, 1939.

TURNER, ALFRED ELLIS, late Indian Police (b. 6th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., C.P., Nov., 1914; dist. supt., Apr., 1925; ret'd., July, 1938.

TURNER, ERNEST JAMES, C.B., C.B.E., late India Office (b. 26th Feb., 1877).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. junior clerk, mil. dept., India Office, 10th Oct., 1900; resdt. clerk, April, 1901; private sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Nov., 1902; senr. clerk, mil. dept., April, 1904; asst. sec., judl. and public dept., Oct., 1911; ditto, rev. and stat. dept., Oct., 1912; C.B.E., June, 1918; sec., commerce and rev. dept., April, 1921; sec., economic and overseas dept., April, 1924; seconded for duty with Imp. Econ. Conf., July to Sept., 1932; adviser, Ind. del., monetary and economic conf., June, 1933; C.B., Feb., 1937; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

TURNER, FREDERICK CHARLES, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Aug., 1872).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 19th Dec., 1895, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., July, 1908; commr., Dec., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; ret'd., May, 1931.

TURNER, JOHN TRENCH, M.A. (Cantab.) (b. 24th Dec., 1879).—Joined the service, 6th Sept., 1905, as head-master, High School, Poona; vice-principal, Rajkumar Coll., Rajkot, Aug., 1906; acting principal, ditto, Nov., 1912, to Nov., 1913; acting principal, Elphinstone High School, Bombay, Dec., 1914; on mil. service, April, 1915, to Jan., 1919; principal, Rajkumar Coll., Rajkot, Jan., 1923.

TURNER, JOSHUA EDGAR CARRINGTON, late Indian Forest Service (b. 15th Sept., 1883).—Joined the Imperial forest service, 1st March, 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1923; ret'd. Sept., 1938.

TURNER, LEONARD GEORGE HENRY, Indian State Rlys. (elec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 15th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as power stn. supt., G.I.P. rly., 28th Dec., 1928; electrical engr., June, 1932.

TURNER, SIDNEY, C.B.E., B.A., F.I.A., India Office (acct. gen.) (b. 18th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge (10th wrangler); apptd. junr. clerk, acct. gen.'s dept., 6th Oct., 1905; sec. to Lord Welby's commn. on Indian Mil. Service

Family Pension Regns., 1913; senr. clerk, July, 1915; accont.-gen., Dec., 1921; also controller of pension funds; C.B.E., June, 1927; commr. for inc. tax, Nov., 1930; apptd. chairman, applicn. ctee. for the Indo-Burma finl. award, Jan., 1936; apptd. commr. for the I.C.S. Family Pension Fund, Indian Military Service Family Pension Fund, Indian Military Widows' and Orphans' Fund, Superior Services (India) Family Pension Fund from Oct., 1937.

TURNER, WILLIAM HENRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 2nd Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., May, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; offg. exec. engr., April, 1925; confd., Oct., 1927.

TURNER, WILLIAM HOVELL, M.A., M.C., India Office (asst. to auditor) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Perse School and Christ's Coll., Camb.; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to Mar., 1919; apptd. junr. clerk, public wks. dept., 29th Aug., 1919; pte. sec. to parly. under sec. of state, Oct., 1920; actg. principal, acct.-genl.'s dept., Mar., 1922; transfed. to finl. dept., June, 1923; confd. as principal, Jan., 1924; posted to econ. and overseas dept., Apl., 1932; acting dy. clk. of council, Jan.-July, 1932; secty., Indian del. to Assembly of League of Nations, 1932 and 1933; asst. to auditor, Mar., 1934.

TUT, MAUNG TIN, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (secty., finance dept., Burma) (b. 1st Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Dulwich and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 16th Jan., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt. agric. dep., July, 1924; jt. regr., co-op. societies, June, 1927; under-secty., govt. of India dept. of industries and labour, Apl., 1928; dep.-secty., ditto, June, 1929; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1933; confd., Dec., 1935; dep. secty., fin. dept., govt. of Burma, May, 1935; secty., forest dept., Burma, Dec., 1935; ditto, fin. dept., June, 1938; author of essays on 'The Kingdom of Pagan in Legend and History,' 'Burma's part in the War,' 'Tales of Burma Labour Corps,' etc.

TWELLS, Miss HAZEL VIOLET, B.A. (Lond.) Indian Educl. Service (b. 7th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as inspector of girls' schools in Bombay 1st Oct., 1922.

TWINBERROW, JOHN DENIS, M.C., Indian Police (asst. supt., Punjab) (b. 14th Feb., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on probn., N.W.F.P., 22nd Feb., 1924; confd., Apl., 1927.

TWISS, CLIFFORD CHARLES HORACE, Lt.-Col., D.S.O., B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1905, as asst. master, Mayo Coll., Ajmer; offg. vice-principal, ditto, July, 1911, and from Oct., 1913, to Oct., 1914; on mil. service, Oct., 1915, to May, 1919; on foreign service in Jaipur State, July, 1927; retd., Sept., 1931.

TYWNAM, HENRY JOSEPH, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A. (Man.), Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal) (b. 24th April, 1887).—Educ. at Pensionat de la Salle, Rouen, Manchester, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr. and mag.; transfed. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag.

and dep. collr., March, 1916; I.A.R.O., June, 1915, to Jan., 1919; sec., Bengal employment and labour board, Jan., 1919; vice-presdt., Cooch Behar state, Feb., 1920, to April, 1921; mag. and collr., June, 1924; secty. to govt. of Bengal, rev. and irrign. depts., March, 1929; offg. ch. sec. to govt. of Bengal, July, 1932; offg. commr., Oct., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; temp. addl. member, bd. of rev., Bengal, June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, variously from Apl., 1936; C.S.I., May, 1937; actg. govr. of Assam, Feb., 1939 to Oct., 1939.

TYABJI, BADRUDDIN FAIZ HASAN BADRUDDIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (under sect. to Govt. of India, Defence dept.) (b. 12th Nov., 1907).—Educated at Bombay Univ. and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, November, 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. under-secty., home and pol. depts., Oct., 1936; secty., Punjab and N.W.F. Prov., jt. pub. serv. commn., May, 1937; under secty. to govt. of India, defence dept. (offg.), Dec., 1938; attache, defence dept., Mar., 1939; under secty. to govt. of India, defence dept., April, 1939.

TYABJI, SALMAN BADR-U-DIN, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th Nov., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E., Coll.; joined the service, 1st Jan., 1907, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; in foreign service as offg. chairman of Aden Port Trust, etc., March, 1928; to July, 1928; supt. engr., Bombay, June, 1935; retd., Nov., 1938.

TYAGARAJA, AYYAR, P.C., B.A., B.L., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. Jan., 1884).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1908; sub-judge, 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1936; retired, Jan., 1939.

TYDD, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian Police (asst. commr. of police, Rangoon) (b. 12th Nov., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 3rd Dec., 1929; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. supt., Dec., 1935; asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, May, 1938.

TYLER, Sir HENRY HEWEY FRANCIS MACDONALD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras (b. 13th Feb., 1877).—Educ. at Rossall Sch. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; manager, Vizianagaram Zamindari, Oct., 1909; sub. collr. and jt. mag., April, 1911; dep. commr., salt and abkari, July, 1911; sec. to commr., salt, abkari and sep. rev., Nov., 1912; on milty. duty from Oct., 1915; attd. to civil admin., Mesopotamia, from May, 1918; pol. offr., Hillah divn., Nov., 1918; C.I.E., June, 1919; collr. and dist. mag., May, 1922; offg. commr. of labour, Nov., 1926; secty., central commee. of the Indian Statutory Commn., Oct., 1928; on depn. as dep. secty. of ditto, April, 1929; Kt., June, 1930; retd., May, 1931.

TYMMS, FREDERICK, C.I.E., M.C., Civil Aviation Directorate (b. 4th Aug., 1889).—Apptd. director of civil aviation in India, 23rd Feb., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1935.

TYNDALE, ARTHUR CARRINGTON, O.B.E., M.A., Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (b. 20th Nov.,

1885).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1909, and employed in milty. accts. dept.; offg. dep. fnl. adviser, milty. finance, Mar., 1927; confd., May, 1927; confd. as Cl. I officr., Aug., 1929; confd. as Cmd. controller, Feb., 1934; O.B.E. June, 1938.

TYNDALE-BISCOE, HAROLD L'ESTRANGE, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 21st Oct., 1892).—Joined the army in the summer of 1915, and reported his arrival at Rangoon on 26th June, 1919; asst. consvr., Feb., 1916; dep. consvr., April, 1921; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

TYRRELL, JASPER ROBERT JOLY, C.I.E., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 14th June, 1876).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1902; in mil. employ till Dec., 1905; offg. agency surg., Nov., 1906; awarded K.-i.-H. gold medal, April, 1913; agency surg., Bunkelkhand, April, 1913; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914, to Dec., 1918; admn. medl. offr. in C.I. and residency surg., May, 1925; resdy. surg. in States of W.I., Sept., 1926; ch. medl. officer and resdy. surg., Indore, April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., June, 1931.

TYSON, JOHN DAWSON, C.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914; joined the service, 14th Feb., 1920; arrived 11th May, 1920, and posted to Bengal; undersec. to govt. of Bengal, fn. and commerce dept., Nov., 1922, to March, 1924; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; offg. chief presdy. mag., June, 1926; secty. to agent of govt. of India in South Africa, May, 1927; pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, June, 1930; C.B.E., Jan., 1933; mag. and collr., Sept., 1935; adviser to H.H. the Maharajah of Couch Behar, Mar., 1936.

TYSON, THOMAS (b. 14th Sept., 1879).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 14th Feb., 1907, to 1st Mar., 1914; supt., govt., printing, Mar., 14; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

U (see also Maung).

U BA AYE, B.A., Burma Police (asst. supt.). apptd. on probation, Jan., 1939.

U BA U, M.A., Burma Commn. (judge high ct., Rangoon).—Apptd. govt. prosecutor, April, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; confd., Jan., 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Rangoon, Feb., 1930; confd., Apl., 1932.

U BA ZAN, A.T.M., B.A., Burma Civil Service (dep. commr.) (b. 25th Feb., 1892).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., May, 1921; dep. commr., Aug., 1935.

U HLA, (1) B.A., Burma Civil Service (dep. commr.) (b. 14th Sept., 1892).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., June, 1922; dep. commr., April, 1938.

U KYAW (3), B.A., Burma Civil Service (dep. commr.) (b. 12th July, 1896).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., May, 1921; dep. commr., June, 1934; secty. to govt. education dept., Oct., 1937.

U KYAW DIN, A.T.M., B.A., Burma Civil Service (dep. commr.) (b. 7th March, 1894).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., April, 1922, under secty. to govt., judicial dept., Jan., 1930; ditto local govt., dept., March, 1934; dep. commr., April, 1938; addl. secty. to govt., education dept., April, 1938.

U KYAW U., Burma Judicial Dept. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 9th Nov., 1890); dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1934.

U MYA (3) A.T.M., Burma Judicial Dept. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 11th Mar., 1886).—Joined the service, Dec., 1909; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1936.

U PO HAN, Bar.-at.-Law, Member Public Service Commission, Feb., 1938.

UP PO SA, B.A., A.T.M., F.R.E.S., Burma Civil Service (dep. commr.) (b. 13th Aug., 1890).—Apptd. extra asst. commr. Dec., 1922; dep. commr., June, 1938.

U SEIN TUN, B.A., Burma Police (asst. supt.).—Apptd. on probation, Jan., 1939.

U THA TUN KHINE, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 22nd Apl., 1913).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Feb., 1937.

U THA ZAN U, B.A., B.L., late Burma Commission (b. 17th May, 1881).—Joined the service as myook, June, 1908; judl. extra asst. commr., May, 1913; asst. judge, July, 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1925; confd., July, 1926; ret'd. May, 1936.

U THAN TIN, B.A., Burma Civil Service (dep. commr.) (b. 1st Mar., 1893).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Jan., 1922; dep. commr., June, 1935; dep. secty. agric. and forests dept., Aug., 1937; secty. agric. and forests dept., Sept., 1937.

U TIN MRA U, A. T. M., Burma Police (Class I.) (supt.) (b. 8th June, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1914; promoted to Burma Police (class I.), June, 1937.

UBAIDULLAH, KHAWAJAH, Indian Railway Accts., service (asst. dir. of finance in the rly. bd.) (b. 30th Oct., 1900).—Joined the service, Feb., 1923; accts. officer, Aug., 1935; asst. dir. of finance in the rly. bd., Oct., 1936.

ULLAH, CHAUDHRI NIAMAT (see Niamat Ullah, Chaudhri).

ULLAH, H. M. SALAMAT (see Salamat Ullah, H. M.).

ULLAH, MUHAMMAD SANA ULLAH (see Muhammad, S.U.).

UMAR HAYAT KHAN, Hon. Maj.-Gen. NAWAB MALIK, Sir, Tiwana, of Kalra, G.B.E., K.C.I.E., M.V.O. (b. 1874).—Educ. at Aitchison Chief's Coll., Lahore; served in Somaliland; joined Tibet expedition; was attached to late Amir of Afghanistan; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; served in France and Mesopotamia in Great War; K.C.I.E., June, 1916; represented Punjab, Delhi War Conference, 1918; served in 3rd Kabul war; C.B.E., June, 1919; member, Esher committee, 1920; member, council of State, 1926; member of Council of India, June, 1929 to June, 1934; Hon. A.D.C. to King, Nov., 1930; G.B.E., June, 1934; hon.-col., 19th (K.G.O.) Lancers, Oct., 1935.

UNDERWOOD, FREDERICK LEWIS, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 16th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 10th April, 1923; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1934; cent. intell. officr., Madras presdy., Feb., 1936, to May, 1938.

UNGER, OSCAR RAMSEY, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bihar (inspr.-genl. of prisons) (b. 12th Sept., 1889).—First commn. dated 16th July, 1915; in milty. employ to 13th April, 1924; supt., central jail, Gaya, Jan., 1925; ditto, Hazaribagh, Feb., 1925; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933.

UNITT, ALFRED, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 22nd June, 1902).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1921; comdt., Baluch. levy, May, 1924; offg. supt., March, 1928; confd., Aug., 1932.

UNNITHAN, KRISHNAN MADHAVAN, Indian C.S. (Sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 13th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 17th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. magte. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Aug., 1930.

UNWIN, RICHARD, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 6th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 16th Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; lecturer in forestry, Univ. coll., Rangoon, from 1st March, 1923, to 10th Sept., 1924, and again from 26th Oct., 1925; offg. consvr., Apl., 1934; confd., Oct., 1936.

UPENDRA PAI, AMMEMBALA, late Madras Civil Service (b. 17th Dec., 1877).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 6th April, 1899; confd., Oct., 1903; offg. collr., Mar., 1920; confd., Nov., 1928; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

UPTON, SIR THOMAS EVERARD TICHBORNE, Kt., late Legislative dept., govt. of India.—Solicitor to the govt. of India, 26th March, 1927, to Apl., 1932; Kt., June, 1931.

UREN, CECIL WILLIAM EDWARD, Indian Police (b. 9th Nov., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 4th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Feby., 1932, Jan. and Sept., 1933; offg. dep. commr., Bombay, Feb., 1936.

URQUHART, DONALD WALLACE JOHNSTONE, Barr., late Burma Commn. (b. 5th Oct., 1879).—Apptd. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

USMAN, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Usman).

USMAN, SAIYID MISBAHUL (see Misbahul Usman, S.).

UTTAM SINGH, SARDAR SAHIB (exec. engr., central p.w.d.) (b. 24th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1922; exec. engr., 10th Jan., 1934.

UZIELLI, HERBERT REX, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 31st Aug., 1890).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 12th Dec., 1914, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1918; tempy. under sec. to govt., rev. spl. dept., Nov., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., Dec., 1925; offg. sec. to govt., local self-govt. dept., April, 1926; dep. sec. to ditto, May, 1926; offg. collr. and dist. magte., June, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; collr. (prov'l. subs.), Aug., 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; secty. to govt., Madras, rev. dept., Nov., 1934, to May, 1937; ditto, home dept., Aug., 1938.

VACHHA, JAMSHEDJI BEJANJI, Khan Bahadur, C.I.E., B.A., B.Sc., late Bombay C.S. (b. 26th May, 1879).—Joined the service 17th April, 1902; dep. collr. of income tax, Dec., 1909; 1st income tax collr., Bombay, 1910; asst. commr. of income tax, Bombay, April, 1922; commr., ditto, Bombay Presdy., Oct., 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; offg. member, central bd. of rev., variously from Dec., 1932; ret'd., May, 1939.

VAHID, SYED ABDUL (see Syed Abdul Vahid).

VAIDYA, JAGANNATH BALKRISHNA, M.R.C.S. Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., United Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—First commn., Jan., 1914; civil surg., United Provs., Apl., 1930.

VAIDYANATHAN, PERUVEMBA PARASURA MAIYER, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 1st Jan., 1915).—Educ. at Madras Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Sept., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

VAISH, RADHA KRISHNA, M.A., Imperial Customs Service (b. 28th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service, Apl., 1924; asst. coll. of customs, Apl., 1926.

VAKIL, KRISHNALAL SURAJRAM, B.A., M.Ed. (Leeds), late Indian Educl. Service) (b. 13th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service as educl. inspr., Bombay, 7th Sept., 1923; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

VAN WYCK, WILLIAM, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 12th Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ.; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1938, and served as asst. commr.

VAN DER BEEK, AUBREY GUILDFORD, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 22nd Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 4th Mar., 1914, as asst. engr. (tempy.); ditto (permt.), Mar., 1915; exec. engr., Burma rlys., Sept., 1932.

VALLURI KAMESWARA, RAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 15th July, 1914).—Educ. at the Board High Sch., Razole, Madras Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

VAN SOMERAN, LEONARD WILLIAM, V.D., Indian State Railways (traffic manager, E.B. Rly.) (b. 16th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1903; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1907; dist. ditto, May, 1915; offg. dep. traffic manager, May, 1927; confd., Mar., 1931; offg. traffic manager, variously from May, 1931; confd., Jan., 1937.

VANCE, ROBERT LANCELOT, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., (legation surg.).—First commn., 30th May, 1916; in milty. employ to 29th Apl., 1928; offg. C.M.O., W.I. States Agency and resdy. surg., Rajkot, May, 1928, and again, Mar., 1933; offg. civil surg., Quetta, Aug., 1928; confd., Apl., 1931; resdy. surg., Kashmir, May, 1935; legation surg., Nepal, Mar., 1937.

VARADACHARI, RAGHVA DIKSHIDAR, Income Tax Dept. (asst. commr. of income tax) (b. 23rd April, 1890).—Joined the service, April, 1923; income tax officer, April 1923 to Dec., 1936; asst. commr. of income tax, Dec., 1936.

VARADACHARI, Sir SRINAVASA, B.A., LL.B. (Judge federal ct. of India) (b. 20th June, 1881).—Apptd. Judge, high ct., Madras, 3rd March, 1934; Judge, federal ct. of India, Feb., 1939; Kt. April, 1939.

VARGHESE, THOPIL ANTHONY, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 17th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Madras University, University College, London, and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, November, 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Apl., 1932.

VARMA, BRIJ BIHARI, Indian State Ry. (exec. engr. E.I. Ry.) (b. 30th Nov., 1897).—Joined service, Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr. O. & R. Ry., Nov., 1921; exec. engr. provl. perm., E.I. Ry., July, 1938.

VARMA, BRIJ BHUSHAN SARAN (see Brij, B. S. V.).

VARMA, B. P. (see Bhagwati Prasad Varma).

VARMA, FATEH BAHADUR (see Fateh, B. V.).

VARMA, RAGHUNANDAN PRASAD, B.Sc., Indian State Ry. (exec. engr., N.W. Ry.) (b. 5th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921.

VARMA, RUP LALL, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th Dec., 1904).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Assam, 2nd Jan., 1929.

VARMA, SARADA PRASHAD, Babu, Indian Police (asst. supt., Orissa) (b. 15th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 2nd Jan., 1931; confd., Dec., 1931.

VARMA, S. P. (see Sidheshwari Prasad).

VARMA, SUKHEV PRASAD, Judi. Dept., Bihar (judge, high ct., Patna).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, 23rd Jan., 1934.

VAS, EDWIN THOMAS CHRISTIAN, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 25th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service, 20th July, 1921; asst. consvr., Aug., 1921; dep. consvr., Feb., 1927.

VASIST, SHYAM SUNDER, B.A. (Hons.) M.Sc., Indian State Ry. (asst. transportation supt., G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 1st Aug., 1902).—Joined service, Feb., 1926; asst. transportation supt., Feb., 1929; asst. transportation (traffic and commercial) supt. senior scale, provl., July, 1938.

VASUDEEV, KESHAVRAO BALKRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., Bombay C.S. (judge, high ct., Bombay) (b. 14th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, Oct., 1907; dep. collr., Aug., 1909; actg. asst. judge and sess. judge, April, 1913; asst. judge and jt. judge and addl. sess. judge, Oct., 1921; judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1936; confd., Nov., 1937.

VASUDEVA, BHANDARI, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 3rd August, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 8th Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Sept., 1933.

VASVANI TAHILRAM DHARAMDAS, RAI BAHADUR, Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 11th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service, 2nd July, 1906; King's police medal, 1923; promoted to I.P. as supt., Bombay, Sept., 1935.

VAZIFDAR, SOHRAB SHAPOORI, L.M. & S. (Bom.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), Lt.-Col. (ret'd.), late I.M.S. (b. 1st Aug., 1883).—Joined the service 1st Aug., 1908, and served in mil. dept. prior to 24th Jan., 1923, offg. as prof. of medicine &c., G.M. Coll., and physician and supt., J. J. Hosp., Bombay, June, 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1938.

VEALE, CHARLES JOHN, F.R.G.S., F.R.A.S. (b. 8th Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1896; prof. of surveying and drawing, Oct., 1908; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

VELLODI, MULLAT VIKKARA, Rao Bahadur Madras C.S. (sub. collr.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 12th Apl., 1910; dep. collr., Dec., 1928; sub-collr., Apl., 1936.

VELLODI, MULLATH KADINGI, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., govt. of Orissa, rev. and developments, depts.) (b. 14th Jan., 1896).—Educ. at Presidency Coll., Madras; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and mag., Jan., 1924; spl. asst. settl. offr., Jan., 1926; spl. settl. offr., Sept., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag. Mar., 1936; offg. dir. of land records, Madras, Apl., 1933; jt. secty., Bd. of rev., Madras, Nov., 1933; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Mar., 1937; secty., govt. of Orissa, educ., etc., depts., Dec., 1937; secty. govt. of Orissa, revenue and developments depts., Nov., 1938.

VENABLES, ARTHUR VINCENT, M.C., V.D., late Indian State Ry. (b. 16th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 30th Jan., 1903; junr. engr., Apl., 1903; on milty. duty from 23rd Nov., 1914, to 9th May, 1919; resdt. engr., June, 1917; divl. engr., April, 1923; divl. supt., Jan., 1926; offg. senr. dep. agent, June, 1929; offg. ch. engr., E.I. Ry., Dec., 1929; confd., July, 1932; offg. agent, E.I. rly., Apl., 1934; confd., July, 1934; ret'd., July, 1937.

VENGANAD KOVILAGAM RAJAGOPALA AHTMBAN (see Rajagopala Thamban, V. K.).

VENKATA ACHARYA, ATUR RANGASWAMI, Rao Sahib, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (dep. chief engr. for irrgn.) (b. 21st June, 1895).—Joined the service as appr. engr., Madras, 30th June, 1917; asst., engr., Mar., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1926; confd., July, 1928; dep. chief engr. for irrgn., Madras, Sept., 1936.

VENKATA RAO, GHANTA (sany. engr., Madras) (b. 18th Feb., 1886).—Apptd. dep. sany. engr., 3rd Mar., 1923; offg. sany. engr., Sept., 1931; confd., Feb., 1935.

VENKATA REDDI NAYUDU, RAI BAHADUR, Sir Kurma, K.C.I.E.—Member of govr.'s exec. council, Madras, 26th April, 1934 to March, 1937; actg. govr. of Madras, June, 1936; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

VENKATA, SUBRAMANYAN MINAKSHISUNDARAM (see Subramanyan Minakshisundaram, V.).

VENKATACHALA AYYAR, KUNNISSERI SUBRAHMANYA, B.A., B.L., late Judi. dept., Madras (b. 1st June, 1878).—Joined the service, 3rd July, 1905, as offg. dist. munisf.; confd., Jan., 1906; sub-judge, Aug., 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1932; ret'd., June, 1933.

VENKATACHAR, CADAMBI SHESHACHAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (agent to Govt. of India British Malaya) (b. 11th July, 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ., Univ. Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 15th Jan., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., 1925; offg. magte. and collr., March, 1927; under secty. to the agent to the govr.-genl. in States of W.I. Oct., 1927; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, Nov., 1927; under secty. to A.G.G. in States of W.I., Dec., 1928; census supt. C.I., Apl., 1930; offg. dep. commr., United Provs., Apl. 1933; agent to Govt. of India in British Malaya, Sept., 1937.

VENKATAKRISHNA AYYAR, LAKSHMINARAYANA AYYAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supptg. engr., Orissa) (b. 7th May, 1888).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 18th July, 1912; asst. engr., July, 1913; exec. engr. July, 1922; suptg. engr., Nov., 1939.
