Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; asst. supt. and judge, small cause ct., Dehra Dun, April, 1917, to May, 1918; jt. mag., April, 1917; on mil. duty, Aug. to Dec., 1918; dep. secy. to govt., and dep. leg. remembr., Oct., 1921; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1923; offg. ditto, Jan., 1924; confd., Nov., 1926; judl. secty. to govt., United Provs., July, 1934; retd., March, 1939.

WHITE, M. H. GLEESON, late Indian Forest Service (b. 13th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 20th Oct., 1930; retd., Dec., 1931.

WHITE, NICHOLAS, C.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 7th May, 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. examr., P.W. accts.; employed in Sibpur Eng. Coll., March to Aug., 1898; ast. engr., Nov., 1898; exec. engr., March, 1908; suptg. engr. (temp.), July, 1916; confirmed, Jan., 1919; chief engr. and sec. to govt., P.W.D. irrigation branch, March, 1925; C.B.E., June, 1929; retd., May, 1930.

WHITE, SYDNEY WILFRED, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 22nd June, 1884).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1921; resdnt. engr., Feb., 1924; exec. engr. (junr.), E.I. rly., July, 1927; retd., July, 1939.

WHITEHEAD, GEORGE SYDNEY, B.Sc. (Econ.), India Office (exec. offcr., milty. dept.) (b. 15th Nov., 1915).—Apptd. exec. offcr., 12th Dec., 1934.

WHITEHEAD, GEORGE UNSWORTH, late Indian C.S. (b. 5th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Bury Grammar Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Jan., 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1936; retd., April, 1937.

WHITEHEAD, THOMAS ALEC, C.I.E., Indian Forest Service (chief consvr., forests, Madras) (b. 8th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 30th Oct., 1907, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1912; offg. consvr., June, 1923; confd., Feb., 1926; chief consvr., June, 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938.

WHITEHOUSE, GEORGE EDWARD, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.) (b. 22nd July, 1911).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1930; asst. supt., Nov., 1932; offg. A.D.C. to govr., U.P., Dec., 1933; offg. supt., June, 1936.

WHITEHOUSE, VICTOR, Indian Police (b. 31st May, 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs. on probn., 1st Nov., 1935.

WHITEHURST, SQUIRE, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1878).—Joined the service, 31st Oct., 1913; asst. works manager, Feb., 1920; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., May, 1925; works manager, Dec., 1927; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Feb., 1929; retd., Nov., 1931.

WHITLEY, ROGER, B.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 21st June, 1879).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 16th Feb., 1906; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; dist. engr., Feb., 1925; retd., Mar., 1934.

WHITLOCK, PERCY ODDIE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 15th Mar., 1884).—Joined the service as prof., 23rd Dec., 1914; inspr. of schools, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1925; prof. of English, Ravenshaw Coll., Cuttack, Feb., 1926; dep. dir. public instrn., Dec., 1929; offg. prinpl., Patna Coll., Feb., 1932; prof. of English, ditto, Aug., 1933; retd., Apl., 1935.

WHITMORE, FRANK BELWORTHY, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 6th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as head master Patna collegiate school, 7th Dec., 1915; prinpl., training coll., Cuttack, June, 1923; retd., Mar., 1933.

WHITNEY, ALBERT, Indian State Railways (dist. signal engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 9th Mar., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th Jan., 1915; dist. signal engr., G.I.P. rly., Mar., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1936.

WHITNEY, WILLIAM WELCH, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 11th May, 1888).—Joined the service, 25th Feb., 1921, as asst. dist. loco. supt.; asst. supt., E.I. rly., Feb., 1925; transpn. supt., E.I. rly., May, 1934.

WHITTAKER, ARNOLD, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 27th July, 1900).—Educ. at London Sch. of Economics, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived, 9th Dec., 1924, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to govt. appt. and pol. depts., Dec., 1927; ditto home dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1930; served in Bengal from Feb., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; magte. and collr. (confd.), Apl., 1938.

WHITTAKER, HARRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., A.M.I.E.E. (b. 23rd Feb., 1879).—Prinpl. of technical and mechanical engrg. coll., Lahore, 2nd April, 1923, to Oct., 1934.

WHITTAKER, HERBERT, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 26th June, 1903).—Educ. at Colne Grammar School, School of Economics, London, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. judl. commr., Chota Nagpur, Mar., 1934; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1936; regr. high ct., Patna, 1938.

WHITTLE, GEORGE WILLIAM MURDOCH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 7th April, 1907).—Educ. at Secondary School, Washington Village, Durham, Durham University, and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, December, 1930, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.

WHITTY, SIR JOHN TARLTON, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Feb., 1876).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., New Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 29th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1908; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; mag. and collr., April, 1912; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; manager of the Bettiah Wards estate, Apr., 1916; offg. commr., April, 1925; confd., Nov., 1928; tempy. member of council, B. & O., April, 1929; confd., Dec., 1931; C.S.I., Jan., 1932; acting govr., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1934; retd., Feb., 1935; knighted, June, 1935.

WHITWORTH, CHARLES STANLEY, C.I.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th June, 1880).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1909, as asst. mining manager; senr. asst. coal supt., Dec., 1911; chief mining engr., April, 1922; C.I.E., June, 1927; retd., June, 1935.

WHITWORTH, HUGH HOPE ASTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 21st May, 1914).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

WHITWORTH, RUDGE, Indian State Rlys. (signal engr., Eastern Bengal Rly.) (b. 13th Oct., 1886).—Apptd. asst. signal engr., April, 1920; dist. signal engr., Aug., 1934; signal engr., Nov., 1938.

WHURR, LEONARD ERNEST, Indian Police (b. 1st Mar., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 7th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Mar., 1928.

WHYTE, SIR ALEXANDER FREDERICK, K.C.S.I., M.A.—Lecteur adjoint at the Sorbonne, 1905-6; special commr. on industrial insurance, Vienna and Budapest, 1908; Lieut., R.N.V.R., on special duty, 1914-17; M.P. for Perth City, 1910-18; president, Indian legve. assembly, 1920 to 1925; Kt., Jan., 1922; K.C.S.I., June, 1925; author of the Old Quadrangle (part), 1907, The Practice of Diplomacy (from the French of François de Callières).

WHYTE, ALEXANDER HORNBV, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Dec., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1901, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; offg.

suptg. engr., Feb., 1924; suptg. engr. (prov. perm.) Nov., 1928; confd., July, 1930; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

WHYTE, FREDERICK HAMILTON, M.B., B.Ch., M.Ch., F.R.C.S., Major, I.M.S. (civil surg.) (b. 1st Feb., 1893).—First commn. dated 22nd Feb., 1924, in milty. employ prior to 21st Jan., 1932, when he was transferred to civil employ, civil surg., Apl., 1939.

WHYTE, QUINTIN ALEXANDER, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Mar., 1909).—Educated at Morgan Academy, Dundee, St. Andrew's University and Downing College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933; and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collector; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

WICKENDEN, THOMAS DOUGLAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Central Provs.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1903).—Educ. at St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 5th Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1929; sub-judge, Oct., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1932, May, 1933, and again, May, 1934; confd. Jan., 1936.

WICKHAM, EDWARD THOMAS RUSCOMBE, M.V.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Dept. (b. 4th May, 1890).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1910; in mil. employ to 31st Dec., 1918; on special duty in Europe with H.I.M. the Shah of Persia, Aug.-Dec., 1919; asst. to consul, Ahwaz, Dec., 1921; sec., Kabul Legation, Feb., 1922; offg. H.B.M.'s consul and agent of govt. of India in Khorasan, March, 1925; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta Pishin, March, 1926; sec. to agent to gov. genl. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1926; counsellor, Kabul legation, March, 1927; M.V.O., March, 1928; pol. agent, Chagai, Nov., 1928; on spl. duty under A.G.G. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, May, 1929; sec. to agent to gov.-genl., Baluchistan, Nov., 1929; pol. agent and dep. commr. in Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1930; offg. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, for. and Pol. dept., May, 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

WICKHAM, HARRY TEMPLE, late India Police Dept.—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1904; supt. of police, Dec., 1912; dist. offr., frontier constabulary, Dec., 1913, to Oct., 1917; comdt. mily. police, N.W.F. Prov., Sept., 1919; ret'd., June, 1925; apptd. commr. for the Superior Services (India) Family Pension Fund, Oct., 1937.

WICKRAMASINGHE, PERCIVAL HERBERT, B.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Dec., 1909).—Educated at Royal College, Colombo, University of Ceylon and Trinity College, Cambridge; joined the service 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

WIGHTWICK, HERBERT MILNER, Lt.-Col. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Apl., 1885).—First commn., 16th Aug., 1905; apptd. asst. pol. agent and asst., Aden, Sept., 1909; controller, hostile trading concerns, 1917; 1st asst. to resdt., Baroda, Oct., 1925; pol. agent, Banas Kuntha, March, 1926; 1st asst. resdt., Aden, Mar., 1931; resdt., Kolhapur and pol. agent, S. Maratha country

states, Oct., 1932; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha, May, 1933; offg. ch. commr. and C. in C., Aden, Dec., 1933; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Feb., 1934; resdt., W. Rajputana states, Aug., 1934; offg. resdt., Jaipur, in addn., June, 1936; offg. resdt., Jaipur, in addn., Sept., 1936, to Feb., 1937; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar agency, Oct., 1938.

WILBERFORCE-BELL, Sir HAROLD, K.C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Nov., 1885).—First commn., 29th Nov., 1905; asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, Jan., 1910; on special duty in connection with the Delhi Durbar, 1911; pol. agent, Sorath (Kathiewar) Nov., 1912; tutor and guardian to H.H. Minor Nawab of Junagadh, Apl. to Dec., 1914; on mil. duty, 26th Dec., 1914, to 9th Dec., 1919; asst. mil. sec. to H.E. the C.-in-C. in India, May, 1918, to March, 1919; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Feb., 1920; dep. sec., pol. dept., Bombay, 1920; asst. resdt., Aden, March, 1922; pol. agent, Kathiawar, Jan., 1924; adviser to H.H. the Raja of Chamba, 1925; sec. to agent to gov.-genl. in C.I., Nov., 1926; pol. agent in Bhopal, May, 1927; ditto in Southern States of C.I. and in Malwa, July, 1927; offg. dep. secty. govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1928; confd., May, 1929; offg. pol. secty., ditto, July, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; pol. agent, Southern Maratha country states, Apl., 1932; on special duty under for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1932; A.G.G. Deccan states and resdt., Kolhapur, Apl., 1933; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Punjab states, Oct., 1934; resdt., 1st class, confd., Mar., 1937; resdt. for Punjab States, Apl., 1937; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938; author of "The History of Kathiawar," and other works.

WILBY, FREDERICK WILLIAM, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 22nd May, 1882).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1909; asst. auditor, O. & R. Rly., June, 1918; auditor, J.B. Rly., March, 1921; govt. examr. of accts., B. & N.W. Rly., June, 1922; dep. auditor, O. & R. Rly., March, 1922; dep. acctnt.-gen., rlys., Sept., 1925; asst. auditor, N.W. Rly., Dec., 1926; services placed at disposal of Jodhpur Durbar, Jan., 1927, to Feb., 1930; with finl. commr. for rlyws., Nov., 1930; with Burma Rlyws., Feb., 1931; offg. ch. accts. officer, Burma rlyws., June, 1933; addl. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts., (temp.), Oct., 1934; offg. ch. auditor, N.W. rly., June, 1935; ret'd., May, 1937.

WILCOCK, JOHN STEWART, O.B.E., B.A. (Cantab), Indian C.S. (secty. to govr., Orissa) (b. 6th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty. to govt., pol. and appts. dept., Mar., 1932; offg. secty. to A.G.G., Eastern States, Mar., 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1935; pte. secty. to govr., Orissa, Apl., 1936; secty., ditto, Apl., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1939.

WILCOX, NEVILLE CECIL, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 2nd Feb., 1876).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1896; joint opium officer Malwa, March, 1924; ret'd., Feb., 1931.

WILD, ARTHUR CLEMENT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th June, 1875.—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 24th Nov., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge; asst. membr. of legal affairs, and sec. to council of govr., March, 1908, to Feb., 1909; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1914; judge and membr. of legal affairs and secy. to govt., legal dept., Oct., 1919; agent for Sardars in the Deccan, Oct., 1923; addl. judl. commr. of Sind, Jan., 1929; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1929; judl. commr. of Sind, Nov., 1930; retd., Sept., 1933.

WILES, Sir GILBERT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt. (pol. and serv. dept., Bombay)) (b. 25th Mar., 1880).—Educ. at Perse Gr. Sch., and Sr. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of land records and regn., Nov., 1910; supt. of land records, Aug., 1915, to May, 1916; temp. dep. dir. of civil supplies, June, 1918; dep. sec. to govt., home dept., Nov., 1921; asst. collr. and sec. to govt., finance dept., Dec., 1923; collr. and ditto, Oct., 1925; member legis. council, 1924, 1927, 1930, 1931, and 1932; also finl. adviser to govt., dev. and P.W. depts., April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; member, tariff bd., Sept., 1933; presdnt., ditto, Oct., 1934; chairman, Bombay Port Trust, June, 1935; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938; ch. secty. to govt. (pol. and serv. dept.), Bombay, Jan., 1938.

WILKES, JOHN SEBASTIAN, Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Madras) (b. 31st Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1908; asst. supt., May, 1910; supt., Nov., 1922; dep. commr., Apl., 1929; offg. dep. inspr. genl., Mar., 1934; confd., June, 1936; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938.

WILKIE, HUGH GRAHAM, B.A., Indian C.S., b. (commr.) (b. 13th Mar., 1893).—Educ. at Uppingham and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to March, 1919; apptd., 7th Oct., 1919; arrived, 9th Dec., 1919, and served in Burma; colonization officer, Jan., 1922; headqrs. asst., May, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1924; confd., May, 1931; administrator of govt. estates, Nov., 1926; land officer, Rangoon dev. trust, Mar., 1928; offg. secty. to govt., educ. dept., Burma, Oct., 1930; secty., lands and rev. dept., May, 1937; commr., April, 1939.

WILKIE, RALPH SCUDAMORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 13th March, 1896).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 20th Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., June, 1924; asst. settl. officer, Nov., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1928; confd., Oct., 1934; offg. settlement officer, Mandalay, Nov., 1930.

WILKINS, PAUL BURGMAN, M.C., D.F.C., Indian Police (supi., Bombay) (b. 11th Aug., 1866).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 11th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., May,

1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1933; supt., Jan., 1936.

WILKINSON, Rev. ERNEST ROLAND, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 24th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 5th Nov., 1913; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1916; senr. Chaplain, Nov., 1926; retd., Mar., 1936.

WILKINSON, HECTOR RUSSELL, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th March, 1888).—Educ. at Clifton and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1917; pte. sec. to H.E. the govr. of Burma in addn., Feb., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, judl., pol. and appt. depts., April, 1918; pte. sec. to the govr. of Bengal, Nov., 1918, to Dec., 1919, and from March, 1922, to April, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; mag. and collr., Feb., 1927; offg. secty. to govt. of Bengal, dept. of educn., Oct., 1931; confd., Apl., 1932; retd., April, 1939.

WILLCOCKS, HAROLD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., central P.W.D.) (b. 11th May, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1921; exec. engr., Mar., 1927; offg. supt. engr., variously from Apl., 1932.

WILLFORD, GEORGE MARK, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service as extra asst. consvr., July, 1915; dep. consvr., July, 1930.

WILLIAMS, ALEXANDER THOMAS, India Office (prinp., finl. dept.), (b. 27th Feb., 1886).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 22nd Mar., 1907; asst. editor, 'India Office List,' Oct., 1918; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1919; asst. principal, Apl., 1921; accts. dept., June, 1921; finl. dept., Feb., 1926; joint editor, 'India Office List,' Apl., 1926, to Aug., 1927; reading clerk to Council, Aug., 1927; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, Sept., 1930, seconded to finl. dept., Jan. to May, 1932; actg. princpl. finl. dept., May, 1933; secty., Ind. staty. rly. bd. ctee., June, 1933; secty. Ind. Res. Bank ctee., July, 1933; princpl., Mar., 1936; apptd. to undertake duties of asst. dir. of Indian rly. companies, Jan., 1936.

WILLIAMS, ALFRED ARTHUR, B.A., Indian Civil Service (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th May, 1910).—Educated at Merchant Taylor's School, Crosby, and Selwyn College, Cambridge; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub divl. offcr., Oct., 1937.

WILLIAMS, ALFRED EDWARD, O.B.E., V.D., M.I.M.E., Indian State Ry's. (b. 13th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service, 3rd May, 1907; asst. works manager, Nov., 1912; works manager, Nov., 1918; dist. loco. supt., April, 1919; dist. mech. engr., Nov., 1922; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., April, 1923; confd., May, 1925; offg. ch. mech. engr., Oct., 1926; and again, Dec., 1931; confd. (provl.), Mar., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1927.

WILLIAMS, ARTHUR DE COETLOGAN, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty. to govr. genl. (def. co-ordination); secty., def. co-ordination dept., govt. of India; and secty., executive cl.) (b. 27th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Marlborough and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; on mil. duty in England, Aug. to Dec., 1914; arrived, 7th May, 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty in Rawalpindi, Quetta and E. Persia, Dec., 1916, to Mar., 1919; on mil. duty in Quetta, May to Sept., 1919; jt. mag., 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, 1921; attaché, legl. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1924; services lent as offg. dist. and sess. judge to govt. of Assam, Apl., 1925; offg. secty., legl. dept., govt. of Bengal and secty., legl. council, Feb., 1926; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, May, 1926; addl. dep. secty., legl. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1927; offg. dep. sec. ditto, Apl., 1929; on spl. duty, home office, govt. of India, Nov., 1931; secty., legl. dept., govt. of Bengal, Feb., 1934; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Bengal, July, 1934; offg. jt. sec. and draftsman, legl. dept., govt. of India, Mar., 1935; offg. sec., ditto, Oct., 1935; secty., council of state, Dec., 1935; dep. secty., legl. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1936; addl. jt. secty., ditto, Nov., 1936; offg. secty. to govt. genl's exec. cl., in addn., Nov., 1936; confd., Mar., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938; addl. secty., def. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1938; secty., gov. genl. def. co-ordn., Dec., 1938; secty., def. co-ordn. dept., govt. of India, in addn., Feb., 1939.

WILLIAMS, Rev. BERNARD RHYS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Establishment (United Provs.) (chaplain on probn.) (b. 11th July, 1908).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Nov., 1938.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES FREDERICK VICTOR, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 21st May, 1898).—Educ. at West Ham and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1923; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1923, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Feb., 1928; under secty. to govt., ch. secretariat, Madras, July, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round-table conf., London, Nov.-Dec., 1932; under secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1933; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Apl., 1934; secty. to agent of the govt. of India in South Africa, Sept., 1934; collr. and dep. magte., Aug., 1937; collr. civil and mil. station, Bangalore, Aug., 1937.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES ROBERT FARMER, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1922; prinpl., Madras forest coll., Coimbatore, July, 1924, to July, 1931.

WILLIAMS, CUTHBERT ARTHUR, late Indian Stores Dept. (b. 20th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., Indian State Rlys., 9th Oct., 1910; with Indian Munitions Board from 13th Oct., 1917, to 24th Feb., 1920; contr. of sales, Calcutta, Oct., 1920; offg. contr. of stores, O. & R. Rly., May, 1921; dep. dir. of purchase, June, 1923; offg. dir. of purchase, Mar., 1931; retd., July, 1935.

WILLIAMS, DAVID, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (transportation supt., G.I.P., Rly.) (b. 12th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as dist.

loco. supt., 25th March, 1920; asst. trans. supt., 1st Nov., 1922; supt., May, 1926; offg. divl. trans. supt., variously from Aug., 1926.

WILLIAMS, EDWIN OWEN, Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as forest engr., Punjab, 11th Nov., 1921; lecturer, forest coll., Delra Dun, Apl., 1938.

WILLIAMS, EMILY MATILDA, India Office (higher clerical officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 21st Feb., 1894).—Appointed clerical officer, 5th Sept., 1921; higher clerical officer, Sept., 1932.

WILLIAMS, FRANK ROBERT, India Office (higher clerical officer, fin. dept.) (b. 26th Nov., 1887).—Apptd. clerical officer, 15th Aug., 1921; actg. higher clerical officer, 1st Nov., 1926; confd., June, 1930.

WILLIAMS, GEOFFREY BAYNTON, M.C., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 19th Aug., 1896).—First commn. dated 13th Oct., 1914; in milty. employ prior to 27th July, 1925; asst. collr. and magte., Bareilly, July, 1925; under secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., March, 1926; ditto, in States of W.I., Dec., 1926; guardian and tutor to heir-apparent of Nawab of Junagadh, Dec., 1928; secty. to A.G.G. in the states of W.I., Nov., 1932; under secty. ditto, Oct., 1933; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha, Dec., 1933; secty. to A.G.G. Eastern states, Dec., 1935; pol. agent, Bhopal, Mar., 1936.

WILLIAMS, HAROLD VINITH, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th June, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 13th Oct., 1931; exec. engr., Oct., 1930; retd., Feb., 1935.

WILLIAMS, HARRY EDWIN, Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly.) (b. 15th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 23rd July, 1909 dist. ditto, Aug., 1922; retd., Dec., 1930.

WILLIAMS, Rev. HENRY FRANK FULFORD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 11th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 20th Feb., 1913; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1916; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1926; retd., Dec., 1936.

WILLIAMS, HERBERT ARMSTRONG, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.), (b. 11th Feb., 1875).—First commn. dated 27th Jan., 1900; in mil. emp. to June, 1903, when he was apptd. dep. sany. commr., Burma; resdt. medl. offr., Rangoon genl. hosp., Jan., 1905; plague medl. offr., Burma, Sept., 1906; supt., Rangoon lunatic asylum, Jan., 1907; resdt. medl. offr., Rangoon genl. hosp., Dec., 1907; D.S.O., Nov., 1900; resdt. medl. offr., Mandalay, Nov., 1912; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, April, 1915, to Jan., 1920; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Apl., 1929; retd., Jan., 1930.

WILLIAMS, JAMES HAROLD, late Survey of India (b. 14th Oct., 1879).—Joined the service as proby. sub-assst. supt., 20th Nov., 1899; confd., Nov., 1900; extra asst. supt., Feb., 1908; on milty. duty from 1st Oct., 1915, to 12th Aug., 1921; supt., Oct., 1929; retd., Apl., 1932.

WILLIAMS, JOHN HOWARD FRY, Indian Police (b. 6th Oct., 1914).—Appd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., 10th Nov., 1935; asst. to pol. officr., Sadiya, May, 1937; confd. as asst. supt. of police, July, 1937.

WILLIAMS, RICHARD AELWYN ELLIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 5th Dec., 1901).—Educ. at Univ. of Wales and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. pol. dept., March, 1930; offg. asst. pol. agent, Orissa feudatory states, Mar., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933.

WILLIAMS, RUSHBROOK—see RUSHBROOK WILLIAMS.

WILLIAMS, SEVERN, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—First commn., 27th Jan., 1904; in mil. employ to July, 1910; prob., pol. dept. and asst. pol. agent, Mekram, July, 1910; pol. agent, Tochi, Nov., 1920; city mag., Peshawar, May, 1921; under-sec. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1923; pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi, March, 1925; pol. agent, Zhop, Dec., 1927; ditto, Chagai and dep. commr., Shorajud, Nov., 1929; pol. agent, Loralai, Apl., 1931; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Apl., 1935; O.B.E., Nov., 1935; asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Apl., 1936; and again, Oct., 1938; resdt., ditto, July, 1938; ret'd., April, 1939.

WILLIAMS, THOMAS TALIESIN, M.A., B.Sc., F.S.S., late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 30th Aug., 1876).—Joined the service, 30th Dec., 1910; on mil. duty, Aug., 1918, to May, 1919; on special duty in office of collr. of income tax, Calcutta, May, 1919; collr. of income tax, Calcutta, Aug., 1919; asst. commr. of income tax, April, 1922; prinpl. Rajshahi Coll., Nov., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

WILLIAMS, Rev. WILLIAM PERCIVAL, B.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain).—Appd. chaplain-on-probation, 10th Mar., 1929; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1931; offg., archdeacon, Feb., 1936.

WILLIAMSON, ALEC, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 7th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at Kirkcudbright Academy, Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; appd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. officer; on mil. service, Jan., 1918, to April, 1919; dep. chairman, bd. of trustees for development of Rangoon, March, 1925; dep. commr. (prov. sub.), Feb., 1926; offg. land officer, Rangoon Development Trust, March, 1927; officer i/c., Mandalay municipal commee., Oct., 1929; offg. chairman, Rangoon dev. trust, July, 1933; excise commr., Mar., 1934; commr. of settlements and land records, Burma, Mar., 1936.

WILLIAMSON, HAROLD, O.B.E., M.D., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg.) (b. 26th Feb., 1891).—First commn., 3rd Dec., 1921;

offg. agency surg., Quetta, Apl., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; resdy. surg., Hyderabad, Apl., 1935; civil surg., Sibi and Loralai, Oct., 1935; offg. resdy. surg., Mysore, Mar., 1937.

WILLIAMSON, HERBERT STANSFIELD, M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Leeds Grammar Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; appd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 10th Dec., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. colln. officr., Chenab Canal Colony, July, 1906, to Dec., 1907; junior sec. to finl. commrs., Punjab, April, 1909; on sol. duty in office of finl. commr., Oct., 1910; junr. sec. to finl. commrs., Feb. to April, 1912; offg. dep. commr., April, 1912; settlt. officr., Oct., 1912; employed in finl. dept., India Office, from Jan. to Aug., 1917; on mil. duty from May to Aug., 1918; dep. commr., Feb., 1918; senior sec. to fin. commrs., Punjab, March 1920; ret'd., Oct., 1922; appd. sec.; Univ. Delegacy for Oriental Students, April, 1923.

WILLIAMSON, Sir HORACE, Kt., C.I.E., M.B.E., late Indian Police (adviser to sec. of state for India) (b. 16th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 8th Dec., 1900, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt., April, 1906; supt., March, 1913; asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, Mar., 1917; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; temply. employed in judl. and public dept., India Office, from Dec., 1920, to April, 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1923; confd., July, 1925; offg. inspr.-genl., May, 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; dir., intell. bureau, home dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1931; Knighted, Jan., 1934; ret'd., Sept., 1936; member, Council of India, Dec., 1936; adviser to sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937.

WILLIAMSON, RICHARD HARCOURT, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Dec., 1879).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; appd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Sept., 1913; mag. and collr., March, 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. commr., Sept., 1931; confd., Oct., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1938.

WILLINGDON, Most Hon. MARQUESS OF, FREEMAN FREEMAN-THOMAS, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., G.B.E., LL.D.—Govr. of Bombay, Apl., 1913, to Dec., 1918; Govr. of Madras, Apl., 1919, to Apl., 1924; Viceroy and govr.-genl. of India, Apl., 1931, to Apl. 1936.

WILLIS, Sir GEORGE HENRY, Kt., C.I.E., M.V.O., M.I.Mech.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), Col., R.E., ret'd. (b. 21st Oct., 1875).—First commn. dated 3rd Aug., 1895; appd. to mint dept., June, 1907; dep. mint master, Calcutta, Oct., 1909; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; dep. mint master, Bombay, July, 1912; offg. mint master, Calcutta, Jan., 1913, to Feb., 1915; junr. mint master, Bombay, Oct., 1915; C.I.E., June, 1918; mint master, Bombay, March 1921; senior mint master, May, 1923; master, security printing, India, Feb., 1926; Kt., June, 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

WILLMOTT, HENRY CLAUDE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 18th May, 1914).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

WILLMOTT, HERBERT, India Audit Office (higher exec. officer) (b. 9th June, 1887).—Appptd. clerical officer, 9th Nov., 1921; exec. officer, April 1922; hr. exec. officer, Mar. 1936.

WILLOUGHBY, VERE WILLIAM DIGBY, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Aug., 1901).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; in milty. employ to 20th June, Dec., 1921; in milty. employ to 20th June, 1927; vice-consul, Ahwaz, Dec., 1928; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan Nov., 1931; under secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Mar., 1932; secty. to ditto (temp.), July, 1934; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Aug., 1935; ditto, Zhob, Oct., 1935; vice-consul, Zabul, June, 1938; secty. to resdt. for Kolhapur and the Deccan States, Apl., 1939.

WILSON, ANDREW, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. agric., Ootacamund) (b. 27th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. economic botanist, United Provs., 27th Jan., 1912; dep. dir. of agric., I.A.S., on probn., June, 1916; confd., Mar., 1920; spec. offcr., Impl. Cl. of Agric. Research, Oct., 1937.

WILSON, Sir ARNOLD TALBOT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.M.G., D.S.O., M.P., Bt. Lieut.-Col. I.A. (ret'd.).—First commn. dated 19th Aug., 1903; was in mily. employ until Dec., 1907, when appptd. to special duty in S. Persia; H.B.M.'s consul for Arabistan, July, 1909; on special duty under pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Jan., 1911; C.M.G., June, 1912; 2nd asst. to ditto, July, 1912; addl. asst. to ditto for special duty in Khuristan, March, 1913; on special duty as dep. British commr., and later as British commr., Turko-Persian Frontier Boundary Commn., from Aug., 1913; on spl. duty, Persian Gulf, Jan., 1915; dep. chief pol. offr., Basrah, from Jan., 1916; dep. civil commr., Baghdad, from Oct., 1917; offg. civil commr., Baghdad, from March, 1918; C.S.I., June, 1919; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1919; pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Oct., 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1921; M.P., Hitchin, June, 1933.

WILSON, ARTHUR HENRY, B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept., (b. 12th July, 1893).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd Cl., July, 1920; D.M.A.G. (junr.), Dec., 1931; ditto (senr.), Nov., 1932; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Mar., 1934.

WILSON, ARTHUR NORMAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 4th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1935; vice-princl., govt. school of engrg., Rasul, Sept., 1936, to Nov., 1937.

WILSON, CECIL CLAUDE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consr., Madras) (b. 11th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr., dep. consvr., 1914; on milty. service, Oct., 1916, to Mar., 1919; actg. consr., Sept., 1924; confd., March, 1926; forest economist, Dehra Dun, Nov., 1925.

WILSON, CECIL HENRY ERSKINE, late Salt Dept., Bombay (b. 7th Dec., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 15th Aug., 1907, to 17th Aug., 1915; asst. collr. of salt rev., Aug., 1915; on milty. duty, 22nd Nov., 1914, to 20th Sept., 1919; supt. of salt and excise, Sept., 1920; asst. collr. of salt rev. (designation changed), Sept., 1926; on depn. to Jamnagara State, April, 1928 to Apl., 1931; ret'd., June, 1933.

WILSON, DENIS GREY, Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd. (b. 6th Jan., 1880).—First commn. dated 12th Aug., 1899; in mil. employ until July, 1906, when he was appptd. asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.; asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, Aug., 1907; asst. pol. agent, N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1907; asst. commr., Oct., 1908; ditto, and commdt., B.M. Police, May, 1909; city mag., Peshawar, Oct., 1912; asst. pol. agent, Dir and Swat, July, 1913; ditto Chitral, Aug., 1913; temply. to mil. duty, Oct., 1914; asst. commr., Peshawar, Oct., 1915; ditto, Thal sub.-divn., Nov., 1915; dep. commr., Hazara, March, 1919; asst. commr., Thal sub.-divn., May, 1916; dist. judge, Peshawar, Nov. 1917; first asst. resdt. at Hyderabad, Dec. 1920; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, June, 1923; pol. agent in Bhopal, May, 1928; resdt., Baroda, Nov. 1929; offg. resdt., Gwalior, Apl., 1931; offg., resdt., and A.G.G. in C.I., Apl., 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

WILSON, FRANCIS ARCHIBALD, late Excise and Salt Dept., Bengal (b. 17th May, 1875).—Held non-gazetted appt. prior to 1st April, 1915; supt., April, 1915; offg. dep. commr., April, 1924, July, 1925, and again, April, 1926; ret'd., May, 1930.

WILSON, FREDERICK ERNEST, M.B., Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Medl. Dept., Cent. Provs. (b. 21st Apl., 1879).—First commn. dated 26th Jan., 1902; in milty. employ to 5th Dec., 1907; offg. civil surg., Miranshah, Dec., 1907; ditto, Peshawar, Oct., 1909; offg. agency surg., Meshed, Dec., 1910; ditto, Sibi, April, 1914; offg. medl. offr., Mewar Bhil Corps, Sept., 1914; dir., medl. dept., Bikaner, March, 1915; in milty. employ from Aug. 1917, to Aug., 1921; resdy. surg. and C.M.O., in Baluchistan, Aug., 1921; civil surg., Quetta, Oct., 1923; resdy. surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Jan., 1928; ditto, W.I. States, Mar., 1930; inspr.-genl., civil hospitals, Cent. Provs., Feb., 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

WILSON, REV. GEORGE ALBERT, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 24th Apl., 1892).—Appointed chaplain on probation, Madras, 29th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1928.

WILSON, GEORGE, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Sept., 1877).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 6th Jan., 1896, to 16th July, 1913, and again from 14th Nov., 1913, to 30th June, 1914; offg. dist. loco. supt., July, 1913; confd., July, 1914; dist. loco. supt., June, 1923; supt., Feb., 1925; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

WILSON, GERALD SIDNEY, C.S.I., late Indian Police (b. 29th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1901; supt. of police, Sept., 1911; ditto, and dep. commr. of police for Port of

Bombay, June, 1915; awarded King's Police Medal, Jan., 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1921; confd., April, 1929; offg. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1928, and again, May, 1932; offg. commr. of police, Oct., 1930; C.S.I., June, 1931; inspr.-genl. of police, July, 1933; retd., Jan., 1934.

WILSON, Rt. Hon. Sir GUY DOUGLAS ARTHUR FLEETWOOD, P.C., G.C.I.E., K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—Member of govr.-genl.'s council, fin. dept., 9th Nov., 1908, to June, 1913; discharged functions on behalf of the govr.-genl. after the attempt on the latter's life in Dec., 1912; K.C.M.G., 1909; G.C.I.E., Dec., 1911; P.C., 1914.

WILSON, JAMES LEITCH, M.A., Edin., Punjab Educl. Service (supt. of educ., Baluchistan) (b. 31st Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as offg. inspr. of schools, 27th Mar., 1925; confd., Mar., 1930; vice-princl., Central Training Coll., Lahore, Nov., 1935; supt. of educ., Baluchistan, May, 1937.

WILSON, JOHN ANDREW MUIR, B.Sc., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 15th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1915; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1917; examr., local fund accts., ditto, Nov., 1923; divl. audit officer, E.I. Ely., Aug., 1927; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Apl., 1929; ditto, Madras, Jan., 1931; retd., Apl., 1933.

WILSON, JOHN CYRIL, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 24th May, 1902).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., Nov., 1930; asst. to inspr.-genl. dep. ocmmr., police, Bombay, Apl., 1938.

WILSON, JOSEPH ARNOLD, B.A., Indian Forest Services (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 12th Dec., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

WILSON, LEONARD, B.E., Indian State Ryis. (agent, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 12th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (tempy.), 24th April, 1911; confd., June, 1915; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; offg. divl. trans. supt., Sept., 1925; confd., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. trans. supt., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. agent, Dec., 1929; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1930; confd., Mar., 1933; offg. agent, G.I.P. rly., May, 1934; provl., confd., Feb., 1935.

WILSON, Rt. Hon. Sir LESLIE ORME, P.C., G.C.S.I. G.C.I.E., C.M.G., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., R.M.L.I. (retd.).—Educ. at St. Michaels, Westgate, and St. Paul's Sch.; 2nd lieut., R.M.L.I., 1895; capt., 1901; A.D.C. to Govr. of New South Wales, 1903 to 1909; C.M.G., 1916; parly. asst. sec. to the War Cabinet, 1918; chairman National Maritime Board, 1919; parly. sec. to Ministry of Shipping, 1919; jt. parly. sec. to Treasury and Chief Unionist Whip, 1921; P.C., 1922; govr. of Bombay from 10th Dec., 1923, to 8th Dec., 1928; G.C.I.E., Oct., 1923; G.C.S.I., March, 1929; served in S. Africa, 1899-1901 (mentd. in despatches, D.S.O.); served through operations in Gallipoli, 1914-15 (mentd. in despatches, C.M.G.), and in France, 1915-16; govr. of Queensland, Mar., 1932.

WILSON, LESLIE TALBOT, Indian Police (asst. inspr.-genl., Burma) (b. 11th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 5th Apl., 1921; offg. dist. supt. and asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, Mar., 1926; confd., Mar., 1932; asst. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1936.

WILSON, NORMAN METHUEN, C.I.E., O.B.E., Col., I.M.S. (surg.-genl. with govt. of Madras) (b. 23rd Feb., 1881).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1905; in mil. employ till June, 1912; plague medl. offr., Punjab, Nov., 1912; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to Sept., 1921; O.B.E., Aug., 1920; civil surgn., Punjab, Sept., 1921; war services, Waziristan operations, 1919-21 (mentioned in despatches); civil surg. and C.M.O., Delhi, Apl., 1932; inspr.-genl. of civil hosps., Central Provs., Jan., 1934; offg. dir. of public health, Central Provs., Mar., 1936; surg.-genl. with govt. of Madras, Sept., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1939.

WILSON, PERCY, A.R.I.B.A., late Indian State Ryis. (b. 4th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. architect, 22nd July, 1921; architect, G.I.P. Rly., Sept., 1930; retd., Sept., 1938.

WILSON, Sir ROGER COCHRANE, K.C.B., D.S.O., M.C., Lieut.-General, I.A. (b. 26th Dec., 1882).—First commn., 8th May, 1901; Indian Army, 1904 (114th Mahrattas); Brig. Major, India, Dec., 1914—April, 1915; DAA.G., Feb., 1916—May, 1916; A.A.G., Mesopotamian E.F. and Iraq E.F., Sept., 1917—Nov., 1921; Asst. Director, Auxiliary and Territorial Forces, India, Dec., 1921—Feb., 1922; G.S.O. I, India, Feb., 1922—Feb., 1926; Brig. Commander, India, Feb., 1926—Feb., 1930; C.B., June, 1930; Comdt., Staff College, Quetta, Sept., 1931—Oct., 1934; Commander, Rawalpindi District, Oct., 1934—May, 1936; Milty. Secty., India Office, May, 1936; K.C.B., May, 1937; Adj.-Genl., India, Oct., 1937; Colonel, 5th Royal Battalion, 5th Mahratta Light Infantry; War Services, European War, 1914-18, brevet of Lieut.-Col.; D.S.O., M.C.; six times mentioned in despatches.

WILSON, RONALD PATERSON, late Indian Police (b. 31st Oct., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Feb., 1920; offg. supt., Nov., 1923; confd., Apl., 1931; retd., Apl., 1935.

WILTON DAVIES, PERCY WILLIAM, V.D., Indian State Railways (exec. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 14th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1912; asst. engr., Dec., 1913; exec. engr., Mar., 1931.

WIMBERLEY, Capt. EDMUND ARABIN, R.E. (dep. mint master) (b. 16th Sept., 1906).—Under mil. dept. up to 14th Apl., 1937; deputy mint master (on probn.), Apl., 1937.

WIMBUSH, ANTHONY, late Indian Forest Service (b. 19th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 30th Oct., 1907, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1912; acting consvr., Aug., 1922; confd., July, 1925; offg. ch. consvr., Aug., 1928; confd., Nov., 1932; retd., June, 1937.

WIN, U. ZAW, Burma Police (class I) (b. ).—Apptd. asst. supt. of police, on probn., Dec., 1937.

WINDLE, OLIVER ETHELBERT, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 20th July, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Dec., 1905; supt., April, 1915; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1920; O.B.E., Jan., 1931; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1930; confd., Oct., 1930; commr. of police, Madras, Apl., 1933; dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1937; ret'd., July, 1939.

WINGATE, RONALD EVELYN LESLIE, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. and Indian Political Service (b. 30th Sept., 1889).—Educ. at Bradfield, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 8th Dec., 1913, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., transf. to Delhi as city mag., April to Sept., 1916; spl. duty on staff of lieut.-govr., Punjab, from April, 1917; spl. duty under civil commr. of occupied territories, Mesopotamia, from Oct., 1917; pol. agent and H.M.'s consul at Maskat, Oct., 1919; special asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Nov., 1921; pol. agent and H.M.'s Consul, Maskat, March, 1923; sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Sept., 1924; ditto, Baluchistan, March, 1927; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta-Pishin, March, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; pol. agent. Sibi, Oct., 1931; dep. secty. to the govt. of India, foreign and pol. dept., Mar., 1932; offg. secty., ditto, Oct., 1932; and May, 1934; jt. secty., ditto, June, 1933; temp. addl. ditto, Mar., 1935; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Dec., 1935; offg. A.G.G., and ch. commr., Baluchistan, Nov., 1936; rev. and judl. commr., ditto, Dec., 1936; offg. A.G.G., Resdt. C.C., ditto, June, 1937; ret'd., Apl., 1939.

WINGFIELD, DESMOND ERNEST, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 26th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. supt., Nov., 1935.

WINTERTON, EARL, RIGHT HON. EDWARD TURNOUR, P.C., M.P., J.P., D.L.—Parliamentary under-sec. of state for India, Mar., 1922, to Jan., 1924, and Nov., 1924, to June, 1929; delegate to Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; delegate to Indian round table conf., Ldn., Nov.-Dec., 1932.

WISE, JOHN HUMPHREY, C.B.E., Indian C.S. (b. 11th Mar., 1890) (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Oxford (Univ.); apptd. after exam. of 1913; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1914, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from April, 1915, to July, 1919; under-sec. to govt., Feb., 1921, to Aug., 1923; offg. sec. to finl. commr., Aug., 1923; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, health dept., from Nov., 1923, to Feb., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1924; secy., public service commn., Oct., 1926; dep. commr., Feb., 1930; secty. rev. dept., Burma, Nov., 1932, and again, Apl., 1934; secty., commerce and indus. dep., Mar., 1937; C.B.E., June, 1939.

WITHERS, FREDERICK WALTER, late Indian Forest Service (b. 16th June, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 12th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Aug., 1923; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

WITHINSHAW, LESLIE, late Indian Police (b. 27th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1906; asst. supt., July, 1907; supt., Oct., 1915; awarded King's police medal, 1916; on milty. duty from 12th Aug., 1918, to 28th Feb., 1919; dep. commr., March, 1919; on duty in central intell. office, Simla, Jan.-July, 1920; offg. dep. dir. central intelligence, Madras, Nov., 1921; asst. inspr.-gen., Jan., 1923; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, rly. bd., for appt. as supt., watch and ward dept., E.I. Rly., from 4th April, 1924; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

WOLFENDEN, RALPH, M.B.E., M.Sc., M.I.M.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), Barr., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 18th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as prof., civil engrg. coll., 16th Feb., 1921; M.B.E., Jan., 1918; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, Nov., 1928; inspr. of European sch., Dec., 1934; principl., Bengal Engrg. Coll., Nov., 1936; ret'd., Feb., 1939.

WOOD, ANTHONY ST. JOHN, Indian Police, (asst. supt.) (b. 19th Jan., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

WOOD, ARTHUR EDWARD FRANCIS, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 18th June, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Dec., 1910; supt., Nov., 1925.

WOOD, ARTHUR WESTMORELAND, Barr., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 1st Aug., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. examr., Oct., 1908; in naval service from 11th Aug. 1914, to 22nd April, 1919; field examr. of mil. works accts. Aug., 1919; dep. finl. adviser, May, 1920; dir. of army audit., Western Command, May, 1925; offg. ditto, N.C., March, 1926; ditto, E.C., May, 1927; with finl. commr., rlys., Apl., 1929; on foreign service under Jodhpur State from Feb., 1930; ch. auditor, E.B. rly. and Govt. exr. of accts., Assam Bengal rly., Oct., 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

WOOD, BERNARD READER, Indian Forest Service (b. 20th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Nov., 1914; dep. consvr., Oct., 1924; transferred to for. service as consvr. of forests in British Guiana, from 10th Oct., 1925.

WOOD, CHRISTOPHER FRANCIS, India C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 26th Aug., 1884).—Educ. at Grocers' Company's Sch., London, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1908; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and asst. commr.; on mil. duty, I.A.R.O., Sept., 1916, to Feb., 1919; jt. mag., March, 1918; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1920; jt. sec. to board of rev., Feb., 1921; sec., ditto, April, 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1924; confd., July, 1925; offg. commr., Dec., 1925.

WOOD, ERIC RAWLINSON, C.I.E., M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., govt. of Orissa educ. dept.) (b. 24th Mar., 1893).—Educ. at Denstone Coll., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1919; arrived, 1st Dec., 1919, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and

jt. mag., Jan., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; ditto (provl. subs.), Mar., 1935; collr., Mar., 1936; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Apl., 1936; secty. to govt. of Orissa, Educ. Dept., Mar., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1939.

WOOD, ERNEST CLEMENT, Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 12th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Cheltenham and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mily. duty from Oct., 1916, to May, 1919; asst. to resdt., Travancore and Cochin, June, 1919; sub-collr., March, 1922; offg. commr. of Coorg, May, 1926; offg. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1926; confd., March, 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1930; collr. (provl. subs.), May, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935.

WOOD, GEORGE HERBERT ANDERSON, Indian Finance Dept. (b. 31st Aug., 1894).—Joined the service, 14th June, 1919; asst. acctnt.-gen., Nov., 1920; currency officer, Madras, Oct., 1922; ditto, Lahore, May, 1925; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1926; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. Rly., June, 1932; services placed at disposal of finl. commr. of rlys., Feb., 1933; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts., June, 1935; dep. ch. accts. officer., E.B. rly., May, 1936; on for service under Jodhpur State, Jan., 1937.

WOOD, JOHN LUTHER, Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 25th Sept., 1908).—Educ. at Burnley Grammar School and Exeter College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931 and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub.-collr., Mar., 1934.

WOOD, LESLIE DERING, Indian Police (b. 5th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 24th Nov., 1928; offg. supt., April, 1935.

WOOD, PERCY, Indian Railway Accts. Service (chief accts. officer.) (b. 17th Aprl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acctnt., G.I.P. rly., 7th Nov., 1913; confd. as asst. auditor, G.I.P. rly., Dec., 1920; on depn. to Agency Dept. as supt. of statistics, G.I.P. rly., Feb., 1924, to Mar., 1929; served under finl. commr., rlys., Oct., 1929, and again Dec., 1935; dep. chief accts. officer., G.I.P. rly., Oct., 1929; served under agent, G.I.P. rly., July, 1932; confd. as deputy agent, Jan., 1933; dep. chief accts. officer., Nov., 1936; chief accts. officer., G.I.P. rly., Feb., 1938.

WOOD, WILLIAM GEORGE, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 27th July, 1897).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st Apl., 1927, after previous service from Apl., 1921; exec. officer, 1st Nov., 1932.

WOOD, WILLIAM OUTERSON, O.B.E., Indian Police (b. 11th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 20th Nov., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Mar., 1929; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; O.B.E., May, 1936.

WOODHEAD, SIR JOHN ACKROYD, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to sec. of state for India) (b. 19th June, 1881).—Educ.

at Bradford Gr. Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1911; re-transfd. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and collr., Jan., 1913; dist. and sess. judge, s.p.t., Jan., 1917; confd., March, 1917; 1st land acquisition offr., June, 1918; sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin., commerce and marine depts., Nov., 1924; jt. sec., dept. of commerce, govt. of India, Nov., 1927; sec., ditto, Mar., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s council, May, 1931; delegate to Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; member, exec. council govr. of Bengal, Apl., 1932; actg. govr. of Bengal, Aug., 1934 to Dec., 1934; K.C.S.I., Aug., 1934; govr. of Bengal (temp.), June, 1939 to Nov., 1939; ret'd., Dec., 1939; adviser to sec. of state for India, Dec., 1939.

WOODHOUSE, ALBERT CYRIL, C.I.E., B.A., Madras Civil Service (sub. collr.) (b. 5th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 30th Nov., 1912; offg. collr. and magte., July, 1926; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; collr. (provl. perm.), Apl., 1939.

WOODHOUSE, ARTHUR ALDERSON, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1874).—Joined the service, 16th April, 1898, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, April, 1906; supt., June, 1912; ret'd., Jan., 1930; author of 'New Revolver Manual of Police and Infantry Forces.'

WOODHOUSE, HAROLD LISTER, M.C., Col., R.E., Indian State Rlys. (chief engr.) (b. 30th Jan., 1887).—First commn. dated 25th July, 1906; asst. engr., 23rd March, 1911; on mil. service from 20th Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Jan., 1917; divl. supt., Sept., 1928; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1933; confd., May, 1935; offg. dir., rly. bd., July, 1934; confd., June, 1935; chief engr., Nov., 1937.

WOODHOUSE, SIDNEY, late Survey of India (b. 9th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 9th June, 1905 and held non-gaz. appts. to 25th Oct., 1919; officer in charge mathematical instrument office, Feb., 1923; supt., ditto, Mar., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

WOODS, DOUGLAS LESLIE, Indian Police (b. 12th Oct., 1909).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Punjab, 22nd Nov., 1929; confd., Feb., 1932; transfed. to N.W.F.P., Jan., 1933; asst. dist. offcr., frontier constab., Mar., 1937.

WOODS, GEORGE WILLIAM MURRAY, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 3rd June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Burma, Apl., 1928; O.B.E., June, 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

WOODS, PHILLIP DION, late Indian State Rlys, (N.W. Rly.) (b. 19th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 21st Oct., 1908; services placed at disposal of Indian Munitions Board, 6th Oct., 1917; dist. contr. of stores, Aug., 1919; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

WOODS-BALLARD, BASIL, M.B.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Sept., 1900).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1920; in milty. employ prior to 1st Jan., 1925; probnr. in pol. dept., May, 1925; asst. commr., D.I. Khan, Dec., 1926; city magte., Peshawar, Jan., 1928; supy. asst. commr., Hazara, June, 1929; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, July, 1919; undersecty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Apl., 1931; asst. pol. agent, Quetta, Pishin, Oct., 1934; M.B.E., Nov., 1935; pol. agent, Loralai, Feb., 1936; sec. to the resdt. for the Punjab States, Nov., 1939.

WOODTHORPE, Mrs. MARIA VAUGHAN, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service as inspectress of girl's schools, 20th Nov., 1915; ret'd., Apl., 1934.

WOODWARD, FRANK JAMES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Sept., 1884).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 27th Nov., 1909, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1914; sub-judge (provl.), June, 1919; confd., Dec., 1919; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1923; ret'd., June, 1934.

WOOLDRIDGE, LOVELL WILLIAM, Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Dec., 1901).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; in milty. employ to May, 1927; asst. pol. agent at Chilas, Nov., 1930; under secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Dec., 1934; asst. commr., Tank, Feb., 1937; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1937; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Oct., 1938.

WOOLLER, ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and sub div. offcr., Bengal) (b. 23rd May, 1912).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar Sch., and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and sub divl. offcr., Apl., 1937.

WORGAN, TREVOR HARTLAND, late Indian Finance Dept.—Joined the fin. dept., 19th Oct., 1901; asst. acct.-gen., United Provs., Nov., 1903; asst. comptr.-gen., forests, Jan., 1904; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, April, 1905; asst. comptr.-gen. in charge paper currency, Calcutta, Nov., 1905; asst. acct.-gen., United Provs., Nov., 1907; ditto, Bombay, Dec., 1908; examr., local fund accts., Bombay, May, 1912; examr., outside audits, Calcutta, Sept., 1916; offg. comptr., Assam, May, 1920; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1920; ditto, Madras, Oct., 1922; dep. auditor-gen., India inspection (civil), Apl., 1924; on spec. duty, Feb., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1928.

WORMALD, Rev. ROBERT LEONARD M.B.E., M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estab. (b. 17th April, 1882).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 30th Oct., 1914; junr. chaplain Oct. 1917; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

WORT, Sir ALFRED WILLIAM EWART Judl. dept., Bihar (b. 17th Mar., 1883).—Apptd judge, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1927; offg. ch justice, July, 1933; Apl., 1936; and July 1938; Kt., June, 1939.

WORTH, MEREDITH, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Repton, Grenoble, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse, and asst. to pol. officer in Sikkim, Apl., 1933; offg. Brit. trade agent, Yatung, Apl., 1933; asst. pol. agent, Bahrain, (temp.), May, 1934; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Oct., 1935; offg. pol. agent in addn., Mar., 1936; senior sub judge, Hazara, Dec., 1936; asst. commr., Hazara, Jan., 1937; ditto, in addn., Manshera, Apl., 1937; confd., Apl., 1937; Counsellor, Brit. Legation, Kabul, July, 1937; asst. commr., Hazara, Dec., 1937; secty. to resdt. for Baroda and Gujarat States, Mar., 1938, to Mar., 1939; on special duty as boundary settlement offr., Baroda, Oct., 1939; sec. to resdt. for the States of Western India, Dec., 1939.

WORTHY, GEORGE TOYNBEE, India Office (exec. officer, military dept.) (b. 9th Apl., 1882).—Educ. at Royal Gr. Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; apptd. 2nd div. clerk, War Office, 14th March, 1901; apptd. 2nd class clerk, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 13th Nov., 1905; transfid. to registry and record dept., April, 1906, and to military dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service, July, 1918, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; actg. higher clerical officer, April, 1922, to Sept., 1923.

WRENCH, JOHN CECIL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 13th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 31st Jan., 1922; dep. consvr., Jan., 1925; offg. princpl., Madras forest coll., Jan., 1930, to Apl., 1934.

WRENCH, JOHN MERVYN DALLAS, C.I.E. Indian State Railways (ch. contr. of standardisation, Rly. Bd.) (b. 15th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th Aug., 1906; dist. loco. supt., April, 1913; supt. of state workshops, Gwalior, Dec., 1914, to April, 1915; spl. duty with controller of stores, mil. rys., from Nov., 1916 (mentd. in despatch); chief mechanical engr., G.I.P. rly.; offg. dir. mechl. engrg., rly. dept., March, 1925; on spl. duty, Rly Board, Oct., 1926; ch. mech. engr., G.I.P. Rly., June, 1928; C.I.E., March, 1929; on spl. duty with Rly. Bd., Feb., 1929; offg. ch. contr. of standards, Rly. Bd., March, 1930; dir., mechl. engrg. in addn., Nov. 1932; ch. contr. of standardisation, Apl., 1935.

WRENICK, BERNARD CHARLES, late India Posts and Tels. (Madras) (b. 24th Nov., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1897; supt., April, 1910; div. engr., tels., Oct., 1920; dir., S.C., Nov., 1920; dep. dir.-genl., Nov., 1926; dir., telegraph engrg., Eastern circle, Nov., 1927; ditto, Northern circle, Dec., 1928; P.M.G., Madras, Jan., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

WRIGHT, ALBERT ERNEST, B.A. (Oxon.), Imperial Customs Service (b. 30th June, 1902).—Joined the service, Nov., 1926; asst. collr. of customs, Nov., 1926; dep. commr., cent. excises and salt dept., N. India, Feb., 1938.

WRIGHT, FRANCIS GERALD GRIBBLE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 2nd June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 4th Oct., 1921; offg. dist. supt., March, 1925; confd., Jan., 1929.

WRIGHT, HERBERT LAWRENCE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Punjab).—(b. 24th Jan., 1887) Joined the service, 24th Dec., 1909; served in the Punjab as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; on deputation to Kashmir as consvr. of forests from Jan., 1923; chief consvr., Kashmir, Aug., 1924; consvr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1932; apptd. officr. on special duty in Gilgit Agency, Aug. to Oct., 1937.

WRIGHT, JOHN MONCRIEFF, C.B.E., B.A. late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 31st July, 1884).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Jesus Coll. Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; supt., Chin Hills, Aug., 1913; C.B.E. July, 1920; dep. commr., June, 1922; offg. chief commr., Andaman and Nicobar Islands June, 1924, and again, Nov., 1927; spl. duty Rangoon, as dep. commr., Oct., 1924; ch. commr., Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Nov., 1927; retd., Mar., 1931.

WRIGHT, PHILIP VICTOR, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Sept., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 16th Dec., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937.

WRIGHT, RICHARD GANE, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Punjab) (b. 1st Jan., 1877).—Apptd. asst. prinpl., Aitchison Coll., Lahore, 15th Sept., 1915; offg. prinpl., ditto, April, 1926; retd., Jan., 1932.

WRIGHT, ROBERT ERNEST, C.I.E., M.D. Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 2nd Oct., 1884).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1907; in mil. employ to Nov., 1911; offg. asst. dir. Pasteur institute, Nov., 1911; offg. as prof. medl. coll., Madras, Apl., 1914; reverted to mil. duty from 20th Aug., 1914, to 9th Feb., 1920; offg. prof., medl. coll., Madras, May, 1920; confd., Jan., 1922; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; offg. surg.-genl. with Govt. of Madras, Oct., 1933 retd., Nov., 1938.

WRIGHT, ROBERT MARTIN, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Nov., 1908; supt., March, 1917; awarded King's police medal, 1918; prinpl., police training coll., Bengal, Feb., 1928; offg. dep. nspr.-genl., June, 1929; confd., Feb., 1932.

WRIGHT, ROBERT WALLACE, M.B.E., India Police (supt. of records) (b. 20th Spt., 1892).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 2nd July, 1912, and attd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.;

transfd. to registry and record dept., July, 1912, and to store dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from Nov., 1915; retransferred to registry and record dept., June, 1919; transfd. to comm. and rev. dept., Feb., 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; resident clerk, March, 1923; parly. clerk, April, 1926; asst. sec. Indian delegation to 7th and 8th assemblies of League of Nations, 1926 and 1927; M.B.E., June, 1932; asst. supt. of records, Nov., 1937 supt. of records, Apl., 1939.

WRIGHT, SYDNEY WILLIAM WALTER, Indian Police (b. 29th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 20th Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1931; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1933.

WRIGHT, WALTER HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 13th Sept., 1891).—Joined 22nd Nov., 1911; asst. supt., Nov., 1914; offg. supt., July, 1919, May, 1921, and Oct., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1918; supt. (provl. perm.), Jan., 1927; confd., Dec., 1928.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM ALAN, A.F.C., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 27th Nov., 1895).—Educ. at Oundle, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1921; arrived, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1924; confd., Mar., 1929.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM DUNDAS, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 9th Nov., 1880).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1905; in mil. employ to 23rd Dec., 1907, and again from 8th May to 14th Sept., 1908; on plague duty, Sept., 1908; civil surg., United Provs., March, 1921; retd., Dec., 1931.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM MCKENDREE, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th June, 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., United Provinces, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

WRIGHT, WILFRID THOMAS MERMOND, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1882).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905, arrived 28th Nov., 1906, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. A.G., United Provs., July, 1911; D.A.G. ditto Mar., 1912, jt. mag., May, 1915; dep. sec. govt. of I., legisl. dep., Nov., 1918; jt. sec., ditto, May, 1920; dep. sec., ditto, July, 1920; temp. jt. sec., ditto, Jan., 1921; confd., Nov. 1921; C.I.E. June, 1924; retd., Jan., 1932.

WRINCH, ROBERT CORNICK, India Posts and Tels. (dir. tels.) (b. 26th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. divl. engr., Mar., 1921; divl. engr., tels., Aug., 1928; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan, Mar., 1934; offg. dir., tels., Apl., 1936; confd., Dec., 1936.

WUNNAM, ESWARA VENKATA SITAPATI RAO, LL.B. (London Univ.), Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, accts. dept.) (b. 20th June, 1893).—Apptd. to India

Office, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 19th April, 1920; transferred to office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; clerical officer, Oct., 1921; exec. officer, 1st April, 1923; higher clerical officer (actg.), April, 1927; confd., June, 1927.

WYLIE, Sir FRANCIS VERNER, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Pol. Service (govr., Central Provs. and Berar) (b. 9th Aug., 1891).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 4th Dec., 1915, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from July, 1916, to March, 1919; temp. offg. as asst. commr., Charsadda, from May, 1919; pol. agent, Tochi, May, 1921; settlt. officer, Peshawar, Sept., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1929; offg. dep. sec. for and pol. dept., Dec., 1929 to June, 1930; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1930; dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1930; prime minister, Alwar, Mar., 1933; dep. agent to Govr.-Genl. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1935; jt. secty. to the govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Apl., 1936; resdt., Jaipur, Aug., 1936; on spl. duty in for. and pol. dept., Sept., 1936; resdt., Jaipur, Feb. to Dec., 1937; govr., Central Provs., and Berar, May, 1938; K.C.S.I., May, 1938.

WYNDHAM, GEORGE COLVILLE, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1916).—Educ. at Stowe, Corpus Christi College, Cambridge, and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 24th Sept., 1938; confd., 9th Sept., 1939; arrived in India, Dec., 1939, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

WYNNE, MARTIN GOYMOUR, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 26th May, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 29th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; asst. commr., Port Blair, Nov., 1934, to Feb., 1938.

WYNNE, Sir TREVREDYN RASHLEIGH, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., V.D., M.I.C.E., Col., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 18th Nov., 1874; transf. to Bombay, 1877 to 1882; exec. engr., May, 1883; resigned, April, 1887, and became agent and chief engr., Bengal Nagpur rly. co.; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; apptd. member of rly. board, Calcutta, Feb., 1905; presdt., May, 1908; K.C.I.F., 1st Jan., 1909; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1911; ret'd., May, 1914; apptd. govt. dir., Indian rly. cos., India Office, May, 1914; ret'd. from India Office, May, 1915.

YA (A), U TUN, late Burma Commission (b. 27th Oct., 1877).—Previous service from April, 1897; dep. commr., May, 1924; secty. to govt., judl. dept., Mar., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

YADAVA MOHAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as engr. student, 10th Oct., 1928; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1938.

YAHYA ALI SAHIB, KHAN BAHADUR' M.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 23rd Aug., 1893).—Joined the service June, 1936; apptd. dist. and sess. judge, on probn., June, 1936; confd., June, 1937.

YAKUB SHAH, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 2nd Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Jan., 1929; asst. contr., rly. accts., Mar., 1930; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, N.W. rly., Sept., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., Nov., 1933; exr., local fund accts., Punjab, May, 1935; offg. asst. secty., finance dept., Govt. of India, Nov., 1935; dep. dir., rly. bd. (temp.), May, 1936; offg. dep. dir., finance, rly. bd., Oct., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937.

YARNOLD, FREDERICK HERBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty., def. dept., Burma) (b. 18th Nov., 1911).—Educ. at the King's Sch., Worcester, and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., home dept., Nov., 1937; ditto, defence dept., Sept., 1938.

YATE, Sir CHARLES EDWARD, Bart., C.S.I., C.M.G., D.L., Col., Indian Army (ret'd.), late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commr. 9th Nov., 1867; arrived, 27th Feb., 1868; from Aug., 1871, served under the foreign dept.; on special duty, Sind border, 1872 and 1873; boundary settlt. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Sept., 1874; and Mewar-Tonk border, Dec., 1875; offd. for various periods as pol. asst., asst. to supt. for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, &c.; supt. of operations for control of Moghias, Sept., 1879; served in Afghanistan, 1880-81 (medal and clasp, and bronze star); subsequently served in the pol. dept. in Bhopal, Central India, and Bhopawar; attaché with Afghan boundary commn., July, 1884; on duty at Herat in connection with the fortifications, 1885; employed on various special services in connection with the Afghan boundary commn.; pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, March, 1890; offg. rev. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Zhob, March, 1893; on special duty on Russo-Afghan frontier, April, 1893; acting govr.-gen.'s agent and consul-gen., Khorassan and Seistan, Sept., 1893; confd. 1897; rest., Mewar, June, 1898; offg. rest., and agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Aug. to Oct., 1898; offg. resdt., Western States, Rajputana, April, 1899; agent to gov.-gen. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1900; ret'd., Oct., 1904; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; C.M.G., June, 1888; M.P. for Melton Dn., Leicestershire, from Dec., 1910, to Oct., 1924; Baronet, Jan., 1921; knight of justice of St. John of Jerusalem; member of Afghan Order of Hurmat; author of gazetteers of Mewar, Banswara and Partabgarh, 'Northern Afghanistan,' 'Khurasan and Seistan,' &c.

YATES, CLEMENT MERVYN SCOTT, Indian Police (b. 7th June, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 7th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., June, 1932, and again Feb., 1933; A.D.C. to govr., Bombay, Feb., 1936, to Sept., 1937.

YATES, ROWLAND WILLIAM PEEL, Major, R.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 22nd July, 1896).—Joined the service, Aug., 1916; asst. exec. engr., N.W. Rly., Jan., 1925; exec. engr., provl. perm., Apl., 1938.

YEAMAN, WILLIAM GEORGE, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1925, June, 1926, and again, Feb., 1932; retd., May, 1934.

YEATTS, WILLIAM WALTER MURRAY, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. to the Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands) (b. 30th Sept., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 28th Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; under-sec. to govt., chief sectt., April, 1926; census supt., Madras, Apl., 1930; dep. secty., reforms office, June, 1932; addl. dep. secty., to Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Apl., 1935; offg. dep. secty. ditto, June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. jt. secty., ditto, variously from Sept., 1935; and again, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

YELD, PERCIVAL HARLEY, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Sept., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th Oct., 1909; dist. ditto, Apl., 1918; on milty. duty from Sept., 1915, to Feb., 1919; offg. dep. traffic manager May, 1928; ditto (commr.), E.B. rly, Oct., 1932; offg. traffic manager, Mar., 1933; offg. dep. ch. manager, transptn., E.B. rly., Sept., 1935; retd. July, 1939.

YENNEMADI, DATTATRAYA VENKATRAO, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service, Judicial Branch (b. 23rd Apl., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Apl., 1908, to 31st Mar., 1914; sub-judge, Apl., 1914; offg. asst. judge, May 1927; confd., March, 1930; offg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1929; confd., Dec., 1934; retd., May, 1938.

YIN, KHIN MAUNG, B.Sc., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 19th Feb., 1897).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., educn. dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1931; confd., Jan., 1937.

YORKE, ROBERT LANGDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, Oudh Chief Court) (b. 2nd Feb., 1887).—Educ. at Haileybury, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1917; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., Oct., 1917; reverted to civil duty, Jan., 1919; actg. dist. and sess. judge from July, 1919; offg. dep. sec. to govt., judl. branch, March, 1926; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1926; judl. sec. to govt., May, 1927; apptd. judge, Oudh chief ct., Jan., 1938.

YOUNG, Rev. ERNEST JOSEPH, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (archdeacon of Calcutta) (b. 26th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 6th April, 1918; junr. chaplain, April, 1918; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1928; offg. archdeacon of Calcutta, Mar., 1935; confd., Sept., 1936.

YOUNG, FRANCIS WILLIAM WALLACE (assl. collr. of customs) (b. 26th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1906; held non-gaz. posts June, 1906, to Jan., 1926; pay and accts. officer, Jan., 1926, to July, 1931; ch. accts. officer, Apl., 1931; asst. collr. of customs, Mar., 1938.

YOUNG, FREDERICK, C.I.E., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 16th May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 16th Dec., 1909; supt., June, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1924; inspr.-genl. of police, Jaipur State, May, 1931.

YOUNG, GEORGE EDGAR, late India Office (b. 26th Aug., 1868).—Educ. at Westminster Gr. Sch., and King's Coll. Sch., London; apptd. 2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., 22nd May, 1888; transfed. to finl. dept., 14th May, 1901; resdt. clerk, Oct., 1907, to March, 1914; staff clerk, March, 1914; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; transferred to acct.-genl's. dept., May, 1922; suptg. exec. officer, July, 1922; retd., Jan., 1932.

YOUNG, GERARD MACKWORTH, C.I.E., M.A., F.R.G.S., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th April, 1884).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 4th Dec., 1908, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., April, 1913; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., July, 1916; to March, 1919; dep. sec., ditto, Feb., 1919; employed in mil. dept., India Office, from July, 1919, to Sept., 1920; dep. commr., Jan., 1921, to May, 1924; dep. sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Nov., 1924; offg. jt. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1926; jt. sec. army dept., Sept., 1926; sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Nov., 1926; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; retd., Aug., 1934.

YOUNG, HAROLD NORMAN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. offcr., N.W. rly.) (b. 1st Sept., 1890).—Asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1919; divl. transpn. offcr., May, 1935.

YOUNG, JAMES, Major, M.B.E., Indian Stores Dept. (dep. dir. of purchase) (b. 4th July, 1885).—In milty. employ from 20th May, 1915, to 15th September, 1924; M.B.E., Jan., 1920; asst. dir. of purchase, Sept., 1924; offg. dep. dir., ditto, May, 1925; confd., Feb., 1926.

YOUNG, JAMES WOLSTON, O.B.E., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 18th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1909; asst. acctnt.-gen., Sept., 1909; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; employed by Jodhpur Durbar from 10th March, 1923; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931.

YOUNG, Sir JOHN DOUGLAS, Judl. dept., United Provs. (ch. justice, high ct., Lahore) (b. 7th Apl., 1883).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, 15th Jan., 1929; ch. justice, high ct., Lahore, May, 1934; knighted, Jan., 1935.

YOUNG, Rev. PHILIP NORTON FRUSHARD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain). Apptd. chaplain on probation, Punjab, 2nd Nov., 1930; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1932.

YOUNG, THOMAS CHARLES McCOMBIE, M.D., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.).—First commn. dated 1st September, 1902; in mil. employ to Oct., 1909; dep. sanitary commr., Eastern Bengal and Assam, Nov., 1909; ditto, Assam, April, 1912; sanitary commr., Assam, July, 1915; offg. health officer, Port of Bombay, April, 1926; on foreign service under Indian Research Fund Assocn., Nov., 1926; retd., Sept., 1930.

YOUNG, WILLIAM HOARE HATCHELL, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (ch. operating supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 13th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service 28th April, 1904; asst. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., Jan., 1909; I.A.R.O., March, 1915; dist. traffic supt., Aug., 1917; offg. dep. chief operating supt., July, 1925; offg. divl. supt., Nov., 1926; confd., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. operating supt., E.I. rly., Aug., 1935; ch. comml. manager, E.I. rly., Nov., 1935; ch. operating supt., E.I. rly. Dec., 1937.

YOUNGSHUBAND, Sir FRANCIS EDWARD K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., LL.D. (Hon.), D.Sc. (Hon.), Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (retd.), late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 10th May, 1882; apptd. to the foreign dept., July, 1889; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, April, 1892; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Oct., 1892; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, Nov., 1897; pol. agent in Haraot and Tonk, Sept., 1898; K.-i-H. medal, Jan., 1901; offg. resdt., Indore, Feb., 1902; on depn. with Tibet Frontier commn. from June, 1903, to Nov., 1904; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1904; resdt. in Kashmir, June, 1906; retd., May, 1910; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1917; presdt., R. Geographical Society, 1919; author of 'The Relief of Chitral,' 'The Heart of a Continent,' and 'S. Africa of To-Day'; holds gold medal of R. Geographical Soc., and R. Scottish ditto, and silver medal of the R. Soc. of Arts.

YOUNGMAN, WILLIAM, B.Sc., Ph.D., late Indian Agric. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 6th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. economic botanist, 5th Aug., 1918; economic botanist, May, 1920; retd., Feb., 1930; dir. of agric., Ceylon, Mar., 1930; retd., Nov., 1935.

YOUNIE, JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 5th Apl., 1893).—Educ. at Elgin Acad. and Edinburgh University; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, Dec., 1916, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., Nov., 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1923; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1925; offg. ditto, April, 1926; confd., Mar., 1927.

YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 20th May, 1911).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 23rd Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

YULE, HARRY HOWARD, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 30th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Jan., 1901; dist. supt., March, 1914; supt., Feb., 1915; divl. supt., Jan., 1927; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., Nov., 1927; confd., Mar., 1932; offg. divl. supt., Mar., 1932; confd., June, 1934; offg. ch. comml. manager, E.I. rly., Feb., 1935; retd., July, 1937.

YUSAF, SALAHUDDIN AHMED (see Salahuddin A. Y.).

YUSUF ALI, A. (see Abdullah Ibn Yusuf Ali).

YUSUF ALI KHAN, AGHA, Indian Agricl. Service (b. Jan., 1886).—Joined the service 15th Apl., 1907, and held non-gazetted appts. to 31st Mar., 1919; extra asst. dir. of agric., Apl., 1919; offg. dep. dir. of agric., Mar., 1928.

YUSUF, SHEIKH MOHAMMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 13th Nov., 1913).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 19th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

ZACHARIAH, KURUVILA, M.A. (Oxon), Indian Educl. Service (prinpl. Hooghly Coll., Calcutta) (b. 24th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as prof., presdy. coll., 10th Jan., 1916; prinpl. Hooghly coll., July, 1930.
